_comment: The code is the source of truth for English phrases. They should be updated in the components directly, and additional plurals specified in this file.

add-permission.built-in-aria-label_description: A Role Picker in the Add Permission section within Access Control: This interactive element allows users to select from a list of predefined (built-in) roles. The string serves as an accessibility label for this component.
add-permission.level-aria-label_description: ARIA-label for a selection control in the 'Add Permission' dialog: This `aria-label` describes the field where users choose the specific level of permission (e.g., Viewer, Editor, Admin) to assign.
add-permission.permissions-aria-label_description: ARIA label for a slider in the 'Add Permission' section of the 'Access Control' feature: Provides an accessible name for a slider component used to configure permissions.
add-permission.role-label_description: Label in the Add Permission screen: Identifies the input field for selecting or specifying the user role to which permissions will be assigned.
add-permission.role-select-aria-label: Role to add new permission to
add-permission.role-select-aria-label_description: Accessibility label for a role selection field in the 'Add Permission' section of 'Access Control': It instructs the user to select the specific role to which new permissions will be granted.
add-permission.serviceaccount-label_description: Label in the 'Add Permission' section within Access Control: It identifies an input field or selection used to specify a service account. This service account will be granted the permissions being configured.
add-permission.team-label_description: Label in the 'Add Permission' form within Access Control: This identifies the field used to select or enter a team. Permissions will be granted to this specified team.
add-permission.title: Add permission for
add-permission.title_description: Title in the "Add Permission" dialog or section: This title introduces the process of granting a new permission. It serves as a prefix, indicating that the entity for which the permission is being granted will follow.
add-permission.user-label_description: Label in the 'Add Permission' section of Access Control: Identifies the input field or selection dropdown used to specify a user account when granting permissions.

permission-list.permission_description: Label in the Access Control > Permission List section: Identifies an individual access right or capability. This string represents a single entry within a list of defined permissions.

permission-list-item.inherited_description: Text label in an Access Control permission list item: This label indicates that the associated permission is inherited from the parent folder. It helps users understand the origin and management scope of the permission.
permission-list-item.locked-aria-label_description: ARIA label in a permission list item within the Access Control section: This label describes a visual indicator that signifies a permission is locked and cannot be modified. It provides an accessible description for screen readers.
permission-list-item.remove-aria-label_description: Accessibility label in a Permission list item within Access Control: Describes the action of removing a specific permission for the identified user, group, or role. This label is used by screen readers and other assistive technologies.
permission-list-item.tooltip-inherited-permission_description: Tooltip in a permission list item within the access control settings: This tooltip label indicates that the permission is inherited from a parent entity or higher level, rather than being explicitly set for the current item.
permission-list-item.tooltip-provisioned-permission_descripti: Tooltip in the Access Control permission list item: This tooltip appears on hover to label or clarify that the displayed permission is a "provisioned" permission.

permissions.add-label_description: Button in the Access Control Permissions screen: Initiates the process of adding a new permission to a user, group, or role.
permissions.no-permissions_description: Message in the Permissions management section within Access Control: Informs the user that no permissions are currently defined or assigned for the selected item or entity. It is displayed when the list of permissions is empty.
permissions.permissions-change-warning: This will change permissions for this folder and all its descendants. In total, this will affect:
permissions.permissions-change-warning_description: Warning message in `Access Control > Permissions`: This text informs the user that a permission change will apply to the current folder and all its descendants. It prepares the user for a summary of all items that will be affected by this change.
permissions.role_description: Label in the Access Control > Permissions section: Refers to a specific functional role or a predefined set of permissions. This role determines what actions a user or group can perform within the system.
permissions.serviceaccount_description: Label in the Access Control Permissions management section: Refers to a non-human account used by applications or services, for which specific access permissions can be configured or viewed.
permissions.team_description: Label in the Access Control > Permissions section: Refers to a group of users. This label likely serves as a heading or a selection option for managing permissions associated with a specific team.
permissions.user_description: Label or heading in the Access Control > Permissions management interface: Identifies a user whose permissions are being viewed, edited, or assigned.

style.background-color_description: Label in the button style settings within the action editor: Used to specify the background color of the button.

action-editor.label-headers_description: Label in the action editor: This label introduces a section where users can configure HTTP headers or similar key-value pairs for an action.

params-editor.placeholder-key_description: Placeholder text in the Parameters editor: Guides the user to input the name or identifier for a parameter.
params-editor.placeholder-value_description: Placeholder text in a parameter editor for actions: Indicates where the user should enter a specific value for a parameter.

variables-editor.placeholder-type_description: Placeholder text in the Variables Editor: Prompts the user to specify or select the data type for a variable.

add-to-org-modal.label-organization_description: Label in the 'Add to Organization' modal within the admin section: This label identifies the input field where an administrator selects or specifies an organization.
add-to-org-modal.label-role_description: Label in the "Add to Organization" modal within the administration section: Prompts the user to select or define a role for the entity being added to the organization.
add-to-org-modal.title-add-to-an-organization_description: Title in the Add to Organization modal window: Informs the user that they are about to add an entity (e.g., a user or resource) to an organization.

subTitle.manage-settings-roles-organization: Manage settings and user roles for an organization.
subTitle.manage-settings-roles-organization_descr: **Subtitle** in the page navigation of the 'Edit Organization' page within the admin section: It describes the primary function of this page or section, which is to manage the settings and user roles for a specific organization.

render-missing-permission-message.title-access-denied_description: Title in the Admin Edit Organization Page: This title is displayed within a message indicating that the current user lacks the necessary permissions to access or modify organization settings.

admin-orgs-table.aria-label-delete-org_description: ARIA label for an interactive element in the administrative organizations table: Describes the action of deleting an organization. This label is used for accessibility purposes.

anon-users.not-found_description: Message in the Anonymous Users section of the Admin settings: Informs the administrator that no anonymous user accounts are currently configured or found in the system.

base-user-sessions.body-force-logout-from-all-devices: Are you sure you want to force logout from all devices?
base-user-sessions.body-force-logout-from-all-devices_descr: Confirmation prompt in the Admin > User Sessions management section: Asks an administrator to confirm the action of forcing a selected user to log out from all their active sessions across all devices. This is typically used for security or user management purposes.
base-user-sessions.confirmText-confirm-logout_description: Confirmation prompt text in the Admin > Base User Sessions section: Asks the user to confirm their logout request, ensuring the action is intentional.
base-user-sessions.confirmText-force-logout_description: Confirmation button label in the Admin > Base User Sessions section: This label is displayed on a button within a confirmation dialog, used to immediately terminate a selected user's active session.
base-user-sessions.title-force-logout-from-all-devices_desc: Title in the Admin section, User Sessions management interface: This title describes an administrative action to forcibly terminate a specific user's active sessions across all devices.

edit-org.access-denied: You do not have permission to see users in this organization. To update this organization, contact your server administrator.
edit-org.heading: Edit Organization
edit-org.heading_description: Heading on the "Edit Organization" page in the Admin section: This heading introduces the functionality for users to modify an existing organization's details.
edit-org.users-heading_description: Heading in the 'Edit Organization' page of the admin interface: It introduces the section that lists or manages users associated with the specific organization being edited.

enterprise-auth-features-card.dismiss_description: Button on the Enterprise Authentication Features card in the Admin section: Allows the user to close or hide this informational or promotional card.
enterprise-auth-features-card.heading: Enterprise authentication
enterprise-auth-features-card.heading_description: Heading of a card in the Admin section: Labels the section or card that manages features for enterprise-level authentication methods.
enterprise-auth-features-card.learn-more-link_description: Link in the 'Enterprise Authentication Features' card within the Admin section: Provides additional information about enterprise authentication features.
enterprise-auth-features-card.text: Manage users, teams, and permissions automatically with SAML, SCIM, LDAP, and RBAC — available in Grafana Cloud and Enterprise.

feature-listing.title-auditing_description: Title in the Admin > Feature Listing section: This labels the 'Auditing' feature. It serves as the title for the section or entry point related to accessing auditing functionalities within the application.
feature-listing.title-custom-branding_description: Title in the admin section's feature listing: This is the heading for the feature that allows administrators to configure custom branding options for the dashboard.
feature-listing.title-dashboard-presence-indicators_desc: Feature Title on the Admin Feature Listing page: This title identifies a feature that allows users to see who else is currently viewing or active on a dashboard.
feature-listing.title-dashboard-usage-stats-drawer_descr: Drawer in the Admin > Feature Listing section: Displays usage statistics for dashboards. This drawer likely provides detailed information about how specific dashboards are being used.
feature-listing.title-data-source-permissions_descriptio: Title in the Admin feature listing section: Identifies the area where users can manage or view permissions related to data sources.
feature-listing.title-enhanced-ldap-integration_descript: Title on the Admin feature listing page: Identifies an enhanced version of LDAP integration available as a feature. This implies upgraded or expanded capabilities compared to a standard LDAP integration.
feature-listing.title-enterprise-plugins_description: Title in Admin > Feature Listing section: Identifies the area where users can view and manage enterprise-specific plugins.
feature-listing.title-find-unused-dashboards_description: Feature title in the Admin feature listing: Labels a utility or section dedicated to identifying dashboards that are no longer in use. This helps administrators manage and clean up resources.
feature-listing.title-grafana-usage-insights_description: Title in the Admin > Feature Listing section: This element introduces a feature or section displaying insights into Grafana usage.
feature-listing.title-reporting_description: Feature title in the Admin section's feature listing: This title labels the functionality or module for managing and viewing reports. It directs administrators to tools related to data reporting.
feature-listing.title-saml-authentication_description: Title in the admin feature listing page: Identifies the SAML authentication feature. This entry allows administrators to configure or manage SAML-based login.
feature-listing.title-settings-updates-at-runtime_descri: Title in the Admin > Feature Listing section: This title describes a feature that allows settings to be updated while the application is actively running, without requiring a system restart.
feature-listing.title-sort-dashboards-by-popularity-in-s: Sort dashboards by popularity in search
Title on the Admin feature listing page: This title describes an option or setting to sort dashboards by popularity in search results.
feature-listing.title-team-sync_description: Title in the 'Feature Listing' section of the Admin area: Identifies the feature that manages the synchronization of teams.

get-enterprise.contact-us: Contact us and get a free trial
get-enterprise.contact-us_description: Call-to-action link/button in the 'Get Enterprise' section of the admin interface: Prompts users to contact BMC to initiate a free trial of an enterprise-level product or service.
get-enterprise.description: You can use the trial version for free for 30 days. We will remind you about it five days before the trial period ends.
get-enterprise.features-heading_description: Heading on the 'Get Enterprise' page within the Admin section: It introduces a section detailing improved or advanced features available to the enterprise.
get-enterprise.included-description: Indemnification, working with Grafana Labs on future prioritization, and training from the core Grafana team.
get-enterprise.included-heading_description: Heading in the 'Get Enterprise' section within the admin interface: It introduces a list of additional features, benefits, or items that are included with the enterprise offering or plan.
get-enterprise.service-title_description: Header title on the Enterprise Services configuration or overview page in the Admin section: This title welcomes the administrator and signals the availability of services or support for the enterprise.
get-enterprise.team-sync-details: LDAP, GitHub OAuth, Auth Proxy, Okta
get-enterprise.team-sync-details_description: A list of supported authentication methods in the 'Team Synchronization Details' section of the 'Enterprise Settings' within the Admin UI: It enumerates the external identity providers available for team synchronization within BMC Helix Dashboards.
get-enterprise.title: Get Grafana Enterprise
get-enterprise.title_description: Page Title in the "Get Grafana Enterprise" section of the Admin area: This title introduces the content related to obtaining Grafana Enterprise.

ldap.debug-subtitle: Verify your LDAP and user mapping configuration.
ldap.debug-subtitle_description: Subtitle in the LDAP configuration page: This text serves as a subtitle for the LDAP debugging section. It instructs the user to verify their LDAP and user mapping settings.
ldap.debug-title_description: **Title** in the **LDAP configuration section within the Admin area**: **Displays diagnostic information for troubleshooting LDAP integration**. This section helps administrators verify and debug their LDAP setup.
ldap.test-mapping-heading_description: Heading in the LDAP configuration section of the Admin panel: Labels the functionality that allows administrators to test and verify how user attributes from an LDAP directory are mapped to internal user accounts and properties within BMC Helix Dashboards.
ldap.test-mapping-run-button_description: Button in the LDAP configuration section of the Admin panel: Initiates a test run for the configured LDAP mapping.

columns.content-connection-is-available_descript: Status message in a column of the LDAP Connection Status table within the Admin section: It confirms that a particular LDAP connection is currently functional and accessible.
columns.content-connection-is-not-available_desc: Status message in a table column on the LDAP Connection Status page: Informs the administrator that a specific LDAP connection is currently unavailable. This message indicates the status of an individual connection.

ldap-error-box.title-connection-error_description: Title in an LDAP error box in the admin section: Indicates a connection error, likely when attempting to connect to an LDAP server.

ldap-permissions.active_description: Count label in the Admin > LDAP Permissions section: Displays the number of active items or entities within the LDAP permissions configuration. The `<0></0>` placeholder is replaced by a numerical value.
ldap-permissions.admin_description: Selectable option text in the LDAP Permissions settings within the Admin panel: This option, labeled "Yes", allows an administrator to enable or confirm granting administrative privileges to LDAP users.
ldap-permissions.inactive_description: Label in the LDAP Permissions section within the Admin interface: This string forms part of a status indicator or filter option, displaying the count of inactive LDAP entities. The placeholder `<0></0>` will be replaced by a number representing the count.

ldap-settings-page.label-reset-to-default-values_descriptio: Button in the LDAP Settings page of the Admin section: This button allows the user to revert all configured LDAP settings back to their original, system-defined default values.
ldap-settings-page.title-invalid-configuration_description: Title in the LDAP Settings page (Admin section): This title is displayed when the configured or attempted LDAP settings are invalid, incorrect, or incomplete. It typically serves as a heading for an error message or a section detailing the problem.
ldap-settings-page.title-more-actions_description: Button in the Admin > LDAP Settings page: This button provides access to a dropdown menu containing additional actions or settings related to LDAP configuration.
ldap-settings-page.tooltip-more-actions_description: Tooltip in LDAP Settings page within the admin section: Explains that the associated UI element provides access to further actions related to LDAP configurations.

ldap-status.title: LDAP Connection
ldap-status.title_description: Page Title in the LDAP Status page within the Admin section: Identifies the page dedicated to displaying the status and configuration details of the LDAP connection.

ldap-sync.debug-button_description: Button in the LDAP synchronization settings within the admin section: Initiates a debugging process for the LDAP mapping configuration. This helps troubleshoot issues related to user and group synchronization from LDAP.
ldap-sync.external-sync-description: User synced via LDAP. Some changes must be done in LDAP or mappings.
ldap-sync.external-sync-label_description: Label in the LDAP Synchronization settings in the Admin section: Identifies the functionality for synchronizing user data with an external LDAP directory.
ldap-sync.next-sync-label_description: Label in the LDAP Sync settings within the Admin section: Indicates the date and time of the next scheduled synchronization with an LDAP directory. This informs administrators when the next automated user data update will occur.
ldap-sync.not-enabled_description: Status label in the LDAP Sync settings page (Admin section): Indicates that the LDAP Synchronization feature is currently not active.
ldap-sync.sync-button_description: Button in the LDAP Sync settings page within the Admin section: Triggers the synchronization of user accounts from the configured LDAP directory.
ldap-sync.title: LDAP Synchronisation
ldap-sync.title_description: Page title in the 'LDAP Synchronisation' section within the 'Admin' area: Identifies the page where administrators configure and manage the synchronization of user data from an LDAP directory.

ldap-sync-info.title: LDAP Synchronization
ldap-sync-info.title_description: Page Title in the LDAP Synchronization section within the Administration area: Identifies the content of the page, which is dedicated to managing or viewing details about LDAP synchronization.

columns.content-no-matching-organizations-found_: Informational message in the LDAP User Groups administration section, likely within an 'Organizations' column or search results display: Informs the user when no organizations are found that match their search or filter criteria.

ldap-user-groups.no-org-found_description: Status message in the LDAP User Groups management section: Informs the administrator that no matching organization was found during a search or lookup operation.

ldap-user-info.no-team_description: Status message in the LDAP user information section of the admin interface: Informs the administrator that no teams were found for a user when attempting to retrieve them from the configured LDAP server.

org-uers.last-seen-never_description: Status label in the 'Organization Users' list within the 'Admin' section: This value indicates that a user has never logged into the system or been active. It is displayed under a 'Last Seen' column for a user.

org-users.not-editable: This user's role is not editable because it is synchronized from your auth provider. Refer to the <1>Grafana authentication docs</1> for details.

org-users-table.body-delete: Are you sure you want to delete user {{user}}?
org-users-table.body-delete_description: Confirmation message in the Admin section's Organization Users table: This message is displayed in a confirmation dialog when an administrator attempts to delete a user. It asks for final confirmation before performing the deletion, with `{{user}}` replaced by the user's name.

columns.aria-label-role_description: Column Header in the 'Org Users' table within the administration section: Identifies the organizational role assigned to each user.

org-users-table.delete-aria-label_description: An `aria-label` for a delete button or icon in the organization users table within the admin section: It provides an accessible and specific label for the action of deleting a user. The `{{name}}` placeholder will be dynamically replaced with the user's actual name.

orgs.delete-body:
Are you sure you want to delete '{{deleteOrgName}}'?
<5>All dashboards for this organization will be removed!
orgs.new-org-button_description: Button in the Admin > Organizations section: Used to create a new organization.

server-settings.alerts-button_description: Button in the Admin Server Settings page: Navigates to a section where administrators can configure and manage system-wide alerts.
server-settings.dashboards-button_description: Button in Server Settings (Admin panel): Navigates to the dashboard management interface where administrators can view, edit, or delete dashboards.
server-settings.data-sources-button_description: Button in the Server Settings page of the Admin section: Allows users to access functionality for managing data sources.
server-settings.not-found_description: Informational message in the Server Settings section within the Admin area: Displayed when no statistics are found or available to display in this context.
server-settings.title: Instance statistics
server-settings.title_description: Page Title in Admin > Server Settings: This title designates a section or page where administrators can view statistics and performance metrics related to the BMC Helix Dashboards instance.
server-settings.users-button_description: Button in the Admin > Server Settings section: Allows administrators to access and manage user accounts for the BMC Helix Dashboards application.

service-info.title-critical-sla-hours_description: Display text in the Admin > Service Info section: Informs the user that the critical Service Level Agreement (SLA) threshold is set to 2 hours.
service-info.title-enterprise-plugins_description: Title in the Service Info section of the Administration area: Labels the section where users can view or manage enterprise-specific plugins. These plugins typically offer advanced functionalities tailored for enterprise environments.
service-info.title-handinhand-support-in-the-upgrade-: Hand-in-hand support in the upgrade process
Title in the Admin Service Information section: This title highlights the comprehensive and collaborative support BMC offers to users during the system upgrade process.
service-info.title-phone_description: Label in the "Service Info" section of the Admin settings: Identifies a field or section for displaying or entering a contact phone number for the service.
service-info.title-private-slack-channel_description: Title in the Admin > Service Info section: This title introduces a configuration option or field related to setting up or managing a private Slack channel, likely for notifications or integration purposes.
service-info.title-unlimited-expert-support_descripti: Title in the Admin > Service Info section: Indicates a feature or benefit of a service offering, specifically highlighting the availability of unlimited expert support.
service-info.year-round-support_description: Text label in the Service Information section of the Admin interface: Informs the user about the availability of continuous, year-round technical support. This phrase precedes a link or contact method that details how to access this support.

settings.info-description: These system settings are defined in grafana.ini or custom.ini (or overridden in ENV variables). To change these you currently need to restart Grafana.

un-themed-org-row.confirmText-confirm-removal_description: Button label in a confirmation dialog within the Admin section for managing organizations: Appears after a user initiates the removal of an organization (e.g., from a row in a list) to finalize the action.

upgrade-info.title: Enterprise license
upgrade-info.title_description: Title in the Upgrade Information section within the Admin area: This title introduces details about the current Enterprise license features, status, or upgrade options.

subTitle.manage-settings-for-an-individual-user: Manage settings for an individual user.
subTitle.manage-settings-for-an-individual-user_d: Subtitle on the User Administration page: This descriptive text indicates the purpose of the current section or view, which is to manage settings for a specific, individual user.

label.active-last-days_description: Label in the Admin User List page: This label specifies a filter or status for users who have been active within the last 30 days. It helps administrators identify recently active accounts.
label.all-users_description: Label or Filter Option in the admin user list page (specific variant 'un-connected'): This element is used to display or filter for all user accounts.

user-list-admin-page-un-connected.placeholder-search-login-email: Search user by login, email, or name.
user-list-admin-page-un-connected.placeholder-search-login-email_descripti: Placeholder text in a search input field on the Admin User List page: This text guides users on how to search for users, allowing input based on login, email, or name.

label.active-last-days_description: Label on the 'Unconnected Anonymous Devices' page in the Admin section: It indicates or filters for devices that have shown activity within the last 30 days.

user-list-anonymous-devices-page-un-conn.placeholder-search-devices-by-ip-address: Placeholder text in the search input field on the 'Unconnected Anonymous Devices' page in the admin section: Guides the user to search for devices using their IP address.

user-list-page.label-all-users_description: Label in the user list page within the admin section: Indicates a view or filter option to display all users in the system.
user-list-page.label-anonymous-devices_description: Label on the User List page within the Admin section: This label identifies a section or filter for devices not linked to a specific user account, often used for managing unassigned or guest devices.
user-list-page.label-organization-users_description: Label on the Admin User List Page: It designates or introduces a section displaying users associated with the current organization.
user-list-page.label-users_description: Label in the Admin User List Page: Identifies the main content of the page, which is a list of users.

user-orgs.add-button_description: Button in the User Organizations section within the Admin area: Used to initiate the process of adding a user to an organization.
user-orgs.change-role-button_description: Button on the Admin > User Organizations page: Allows an administrator to change the assigned role of a user within an organization.
user-orgs.external-user-tooltip: This user's built-in role is not editable because it is synchronized from your auth provider. Refer to the <1>Grafana authentication docs</1> for details.
user-orgs.remove-button_description: Button in the Admin > User Organizations section: Its purpose is to remove a selected user from an organization.
user-orgs.role-not-editable: This user's role is not editable because it is synchronized from your auth provider. Refer to the <1>Grafana authentication docs</1> for details.
user-orgs.title: Organizations
user-orgs.title_description: Page Title in the Admin > User Organizations section: This title identifies the page where administrators can view and manage the various organizations within the system.

user-orgs-modal.add-button_description: Button in the user organizations modal: Allows an administrator to add a selected user to an organization.

user-permissions.change-button_description: Button in the User Permissions section within the Admin settings: Allows an administrator to modify or update user permissions for a selected user or group.
user-permissions.confirmText-change_description: Button label in the User Permissions section of the Admin area: Confirms an action to update user permissions, roles, or group assignments.
user-permissions.grafana-admin-key_description: Label in the User Permissions section of the Admin settings: This indicates the highest administrative role within Grafana, granting full access and control. It is used for assigning or displaying this specific permission level to users.
user-permissions.title: Permissions
user-permissions.title_description: Title in the Admin > User Permissions section: This identifies the page or section where administrators can manage user access rights and authorizations within the system.

user-profile.body-delete: Are you sure you want to delete this user?
user-profile.body-delete_description: Confirmation prompt in a dialog for deleting a user in the Admin > User Profile section: It asks the administrator to confirm their intent to permanently remove a user from the system.
user-profile.body-disable: Are you sure you want to disable this user?
user-profile.body-disable_description: Confirmation Prompt in the User Profile section of the Admin panel: This message asks the administrator to confirm their intent to disable a selected user. It appears before the user account is actually deactivated.
user-profile.confirmText-delete-user_description: Text in a confirmation dialog in the 'User Profile' section (within the 'Admin' interface): This string is displayed to confirm the action of deleting a user account.
user-profile.confirmText-disable-user_description: Button label in the User Profile section of the Admin panel: This text is displayed on a button or as part of a confirmation message, prompting the administrator to disable the selected user's account.
user-profile.delete-button_description: Button in the admin user profile section: Initiates the deletion of the currently viewed user account.
user-profile.disable-button_description: Button in the User Profile section of the Admin interface: Used to disable a selected user's account. This action typically revokes the user's login access without deleting their profile.
user-profile.enable-button_description: Button in the user profile management screen within the Admin section: Used to activate a previously disabled user account.
user-profile.label-numerical-identifier_description: Label on the user profile page within the Admin section: It displays a unique numerical ID assigned to the user. This identifier is typically read-only and used for internal system reference.
user-profile.title: User information
user-profile.title_description: Page title in the User Profile page within the Admin section: Labels the page where users can view and manage their personal information.
user-profile.title-delete-user_description: Title in a dialog or screen within the Admin User Profile management section: This title identifies the interface for deleting a user account.
user-profile.title-disable-user_description: Action button in the User Profile screen within the Admin section: Allows an administrator to disable the selected user's account. This prevents the user from logging in or accessing the system.

user-sessions.browser-column_description: Column header in the User Sessions table on the Admin page: Displays information about the web browser and operating system used for a user session.
user-sessions.force-logout-all-button_description: Button in the Admin > User Sessions page: Allows an administrator to forcibly log out a selected user from all their active sessions across all devices. This action immediately terminates all active user sessions, often for security or management purposes.
user-sessions.force-logout-button_description: Button in the User Sessions management page: Allows an administrator to immediately terminate a selected user's active session. This action forces the user to log out of the system.
user-sessions.ip-column_description: Column header in the admin user sessions table: Displays the IP address from which a user session originated.
user-sessions.last-seen-column_description: Column header in the 'User Sessions' table in the admin section: Indicates the timestamp of the last recorded activity or interaction for a user session. This typically refers to the last login or system access time.
user-sessions.logged-on-column_description: Column header in the User Sessions table within the Admin section: Indicates the current logged-in status of a user session.
user-sessions.now_description: Filter option in the User Sessions section in the Admin area: Used to display user sessions that are currently active or occurring at the present moment.
user-sessions.title: Sessions
user-sessions.title_description: Page title in the Admin > User Sessions section: This title labels the area dedicated to displaying or managing user login sessions.

users-create.create-button_description: Button in the 'Create User' screen within the Admin section: Submits the entered details to create a new user account.

users-list.create-button_description: Button in the 'Users List' page within the Admin section: Initiates the process of creating a new user account.

columns.content-grafana-admin_description: Column header in the Users table within the Admin section: This header labels a column that indicates the Grafana administrative status or role of a user.
columns.title-edit-user_description: Title in the Admin > Users table: This string is the title for the dialog or page where an administrator can modify an existing user's details.

users-table.edit-aria-label_description: Accessibility label for an action button or link in the Admin > Users table: Provides a description for screen readers for the action of navigating to or managing a specific user's details. While the English string says "View", the key path and other translations consistently indicate an "Edit" functionality.
users-table.edit-tooltip_description: Tooltip for an action icon in the user table within the Admin section: Appears on hover to describe the user management action. The English value "View user" contradicts the German, Spanish, and French translations, which all indicate an "Edit user" action.
users-table.last-seen-never_description: Status indicator in the Users table in the Admin section: Indicates that a user has never logged in or been active. This value is typically displayed in the 'Last Seen' column.
users-table.no-licensed-roles_description: Text value in the Users table within the Admin section: This value indicates that a specific user has not been assigned any licensed roles. It is displayed in a column or field related to a user's role assignments.
users-table.tooltip-assigned-role: A licensed role will be assigned when this user signs in
users-table.tooltip-assigned-role_description: Tooltip in the Users Table within the Admin section: This tooltip explains that a licensed role for a user is assigned upon their first sign-in. It likely provides clarification regarding a user's role status or an assignment option.

home.irm-card-description: Unify on-call, alerting, and incident response with Grafana Cloud IRM.
home.irm-card-item-1: Manage on-call schedules with your calendar or Terraform.
home.irm-card-item-2: Respond to incidents via web, app, Slack, or other channels.
home.irm-card-item-3: Pinpoint root causes with AI-powered Grafana SIFT.
home.irm-card-item-4: Analyze past incidents to improve response and resilience.
home.synthetic-monitoring-card-description: Monitor critical user flows, websites, and APIs externally, from global locations.
home.synthetic-monitoring-card-item-1: Simulate end-to-end user journeys with browser checks.
home.synthetic-monitoring-card-item-2: Run ping, DNS, HTTP/S, and TCP checks at every network layer.
home.synthetic-monitoring-card-item-3: Use 20+ global probes or private probes behind your firewall.
home.synthetic-monitoring-card-item-4: Track SLOs with built-in Prometheus-style alerts — right from the UI.

mute-timing-time-interval.description: A time interval item is a definition for a moment in time. All fields are lists, and at least one list element must be satisfied to match the field. If a field is left blank, any moment of time will match the field. For an instant of time to match a complete time interval, all fields must match. A time interval can contain multiple time interval items.

mute-timing-time-interval.description-months: The months of the year in either numerical or the full calendar month

mute-timing-time-range.description-time-range: The time inclusive of the start and exclusive of the end time (in UTC if no location has been selected, otherwise local time)

receiver-form.body-attention: Because there is no default policy configured yet, this contact point will automatically be set as default.

Precompute expressions.
Should be combined with an alert rule.

recording-rules.target-data-source-required: Please select a target data source

recording-rules-name-space-and-group-ste.description-select-namespace-group-recor: Select the Namespace and Group for your recording rule.

redirect-to-clone-rule.body-evaluation-group: You will need to set a new evaluation group for the copied rule because the original one has been provisioned and cannot be used for rules created in the UI.
redirect-to-clone-rule.body-not-provisioned: The new rule will not be marked as a provisioned rule.

redirect-to-rule-viewer.several-rules-found: Several rules in <1>{{sourceName}}</1> matched the name <4>{{name}}</4>, please select the rule you want to view.

routing-option-description.manual: Notifications for firing alerts are routed to a selected contact point.
routing-option-description.matching-labels: Notifications for firing alerts are routed to contact points based on matching labels and the notification policy tree.

routing-settings.description-group-by: Combine multiple alerts into a single notification by grouping them by the same label values. If empty, it is inherited from the default notification policy.

rule-config-status.tooltip-min-interval: A minimum evaluation interval of <1>{{minInterval}}</1> has been configured in Grafana and will be used instead of the {{ruleInterval}} interval configured for the Rule Group.

get-content.sorry-allowed-create-rules: Sorry! You are not allowed to create rules.
get-content.sorry-allowed-rules: Sorry! You are not allowed to edit rules.

annotations.description1: Annotations add additional information to alerts, helping alert responders identify and address potential issues.
annotations.description2: For example, add a Summary annotation to tell you which value caused the alert to fire or which server it happened on.
annotations.description3: Annotations can contain a combination of text and template code, which is used to include data from queries.

evaluation.group-text: All rules in the selected group are evaluated every {{evaluateEvery}}.

pause.alerting: Turn on to pause evaluation for this alert rule.
pause.recording: Turn on to pause evaluation for this recording rule.

evaluation.select-folder-before: Select a folder before setting evaluation group and interval

description.text: Define how the alert rule is evaluated.

info-help.content: These settings can help mitigate temporary data source issues, preventing alerts from unintentionally firing due to lack of data, errors, or timeouts.
info-help.link-title: Configure no data and error handling
info-help.text: Define the alert behavior when the evaluation fails or the query returns no data.

info-help2.link-text: Read about evaluation and alert states

keep-firing-for.label-description: Period during which the alert will continue to show up as firing even though the threshold condition is no longer breached. Selecting "None" means the alert will be back to normal immediately.

rule-form.evaluation-behaviour-description1: Evaluation groups are containers for evaluating alert and recording rules.
rule-form.evaluation-behaviour-description2: An evaluation group defines an evaluation interval - how often a rule is evaluated. Alert rules within the same evaluation group are evaluated over the same evaluation interval.
rule-form.evaluation-behaviour-description3: Pending period specifies how long the threshold condition must be met before the alert starts firing. This option helps prevent alerts from being triggered by temporary issues.

evaluation-behaviour-for.validation: Pending period must be greater than or equal to the evaluation interval.

rule-form.folder-and-labels: Organize your alert rule with a folder and set of labels.

folders.help-info: Folders are used for storing alert rules. You can extend the access provided by a role to alert rules and assign permissions to individual folders.

labels.help-info: Labels are used to differentiate an alert from all other alerts.You can use them for searching, silencing, and routing notifications.

optional-settings.description: Configure how notifications for this alert rule are sent.
optional-settings.help-info1: Mute timings allows you to temporarily pause notifications for a specific recurring period, such as a regular maintenance window or weekends.
optional-settings.help-info2: Grouping and timing options combine multiple alerts within a specific period into a single notification, allowing you to customize default options.

recovery.stop-alerting-above: Stop alerting (or pending state) when above
recovery.stop-alerting-bellow: Stop alerting (or pending state) when below
recovery.stop-alerting-equal: Stop alerting (or pending state) when equal to
recovery.stop-alerting-inside-range: Stop alerting (or pending state) when inside range
recovery.stop-alerting-less: Stop alerting (or pending state) when less than
recovery.stop-alerting-more: Stop alerting (or pending state) when more than
recovery.stop-alerting-not-equal: Stop alerting (or pending state) when not equal to
recovery.stop-alerting-outside-range: Stop alerting (or pending state) when outside range
recovery.title: Custom recovery threshold

rule-modify-export.title-grafanamanaged-alert: This rule is not a Grafana-managed alert rule

rules-filter.configured-alert-rules: Data sources containing configured alert rules are Mimir or Loki data sources where alert rules are stored and evaluated in the data source itself.
rules-filter.contact-point-tooltip: Filters alert rules which route directly to the selected contact point. Alert rules routed to notification policies will not be displayed.

filter-options.placeholder-search-input: Search by name or enter filter query...

rules-filter.manage-alerts: In these data sources, you can select Manage alerts via Alerting UI to be able to manage these alert rules in the Grafana UI as well as in the data source where they were configured.

search-field-input.filter-instances-tooltip: Use label matcher expression or click on an instance label to filter instances, for example:

search-query-help.search-syntax: Search syntax allows to query alert rules by the parameters defined below.

selecting-data-source-tooltip.tooltip-content: Not finding the data source you want? Some data sources are not supported for alerting. Click on the icon for more information.

silenced-instances-preview.error-generating-preview: Error occurred when generating preview of affected alerts. Are your matchers valid?
silenced-instances-preview.valid-matcher-affected-alerts: Add a valid matcher to see affected alerts

silences.only-firing-instances: Only alert instances in the firing state are displayed.
silences.preview-affected-instances: Preview the alert instances affected by this silence.

silences-filter.search-by-matchers-tooltip: Filter silences by using a comma separated list of matchers, e.g.

silences-table.title-the-selected-alertmanager-has-no-c: The selected Alertmanager has no configuration

smart-alert-type-detector.data-sourcemanaged-alert-rules-descripti: Data source-managed alert rules can be used for Grafana Mimir or Grafana Loki data sources which have been configured to support rule creation. The use of expressions or multiple queries is not supported.
smart-alert-type-detector.grafanamanaged-alert-rules-description: Grafana-managed alert rules allow you to create alerts that can act on data from any of our supported data sources, including having multiple data sources in the same rule. You can also add expressions to transform your data and set alert conditions. Using images in alert notifications is also supported.
smart-alert-type-detector.rule-type-cannot-be-changed: The alert rule type cannot be changed for an existing rule.
smart-alert-type-detector.rule-type-grafana-managed: Based on the selected data sources this alert rule will be Grafana-managed.
smart-alert-type-detector.select-where-alert-managed: Select where the alert rule will be managed.
smart-alert-type-detector.switch-to-data-source-managed: The data source selected in your query supports alert rule management. Switch to data source-managed if you want the alert rule to be managed by the data source instead of Grafana.
smart-alert-type-detector.switch-to-grafana-managed: Switch to Grafana-managed to use expressions, multiple queries, images in notifications and various other features.

state-history.filter-group-tooltip: Filter each state history group either by exact match or a regular expression, for example:

template-data-docs.available-context-notification: Available in the context of a notification.

template-selector.tooltip-copy: Copy selected notification template to clipboard. You can use it in the custom tab.

editor.auto-complete: For auto-completion of common templating code, type the following keywords in the content editor:

help.intro:
Notification templates use Go templating language to create notification messages.

In Grafana, a template group can define multiple notification templates using {{ define "<NAME>" }}.
These templates can then be used in contact points and within other notification templates by calling {{ template "<NAME>" }}.
For detailed information about notification templates, refer to our documentation.

templates.misconfigured-warning-details: Templates must be defined in both the <2></2> and <5></5> sections of your alertmanager configuration.

templates-table.body-delete-template-group: Are you sure you want to delete template group "{{template}}"?

test-contact-point-modal.custom-notification-message: You will send a test notification that uses the annotations defined below. This is a good option if you use custom templates and messages.
test-contact-point-modal.predefined-notification-message: You will send a test notification that uses a predefined alert. If you have defined a custom template or message, for better results switch to custom notification message, from above.

time-intervals.description: Enter specific time intervals when not to send notifications or freeze notifications for recurring periods of time.

unused-contact-point-badge.tooltip-unused: This contact point is not used in any notification policy or alert rule

modal-element.deleting-contact-point-permanently-remov: Deleting this contact point will permanently remove it.

welcome-header.description-alert-rules: Define the condition that must be met before an alert rule fires
welcome-header.description-configure-firing-alert-insta: Configure how firing alert instances are routed to contact points
welcome-header.description-configure-receives-notificat: Configure who receives notifications and how they are sent

yaml-content-info.body:
The YAML content in the editor only contains alert rule configuration
To configure Prometheus, you need to provide the rest of the <4>configuration file content.

alertlist.descriptino-alerts-linked-to-dashboard: Only show alerts linked to this dashboard
alertlist.description-alert-instance-label: Filter alert instances using label querying, ex: {severity="critical", instance=~"cluster-us-.+"}
alertlist.description-alert-name: Filter for alerts containing this text
alertlist.description-folder: Filter for alerts in the selected folder (for Grafana-managed alert rules only)
alertlist.description-group-mode: How alert instances should be grouped
alertlist.description-show-inactive-alerts: Include alert rules which have 0 (zero) instances. Because these rules have no instances, they remain hidden if the Alert instance label filter is configured.
alertlist.description-sort-order: Sort order of alerts and alert instances
alertlist.description-view-mode: Toggle between list view and stat view

unified-alert-list-panel.body-permission-required: Sorry, you do not have the required permissions to read alert rules.

anno-list-panel.aria-label-remove-filter_description: ARIA label for a button or interactive element in the Annotation List Panel: This label informs screen reader users that activating the element will remove the specified filter. The `{{filterToRemove}}` placeholder provides the exact name of the filter.
anno-list-panel.filter_description: Label in Annotation List Panel: Indicates a control or input field used to filter the list of annotations displayed within the panel. It prompts the user to apply filtering criteria.
anno-list-panel.no-annotations-found_description: Message in Annotation List Panel: Informs the user when no annotations are found or available to display. This text is shown as a placeholder when the annotation list is empty.

annotation-list-item.tooltip-created-by_description: Text label in a Tooltip for an Annotation List Item: Displays the email address of the user who created the annotation. The email address appears on a new line below 'Created by:'.

annolist.category-annotation-query_description: Section title in the annotation configuration panel: Labels the area where users define or review the query for fetching annotations.
annolist.category-display_description: Section header or option label in the annotation list's category display settings: It identifies a control or section where users can configure the visual presentation or visibility of categories.
annolist.category-link-behaviour_description: Setting option in the Annotation List configuration: Determines how links within an annotation category function when interacted with.
annolist.description-tags_description: Label for a filter input field in the annotation list filter section: Allows users to specify tags to match against when filtering annotations.

link-target-options.label-dashboard_description: Label in the link target options of an annotation list: It represents the choice to link to a dashboard.
link-target-options.label-panel_description: Option label within the annotation list's link target options: It represents the choice to link to a specific dashboard panel.

annolist.name-limit_description: Label in Annotation List settings: Defines the maximum number of annotation entries or names that will be displayed or processed.
annolist.name-link-target_description: Label in the annotation list configuration: Specifies the destination or behavior (e.g., new tab, same window) for a link associated with an annotation.
annolist.name-query-filter_description: Label for a query filter control in the Annotation List: This allows users to define a query to filter the annotations displayed in the list.
annolist.name-show-tags_description: Button in the annotation list: Allows users to display tags associated with annotations.
annolist.name-show-time_description: Option in Annotation list settings: Controls the visibility of the time associated with annotations.
annolist.name-show-user_description: Action label in an annotation list: This label represents an action to display details for a user. It is typically found when interacting with a user's name within an annotation.
annolist.name-tags_description: Label in the Annotation List UI: Identifies the tags associated with an annotation, used for categorization or filtering.
annolist.name-time-after_description: **Input field label** in the **annotation editor or settings**: Defines a duration or an end point for an annotation, relative to its start time. It specifies a period of time that extends *after* the initial annotation mark.
annolist.name-time-before_description: Label in annotation properties: Prompts the user to specify a time duration that precedes an annotation's event or reference point, often used for defining a preceding time window.
annolist.name-time-range_description: Label in the annotation list settings: Identifies the time period for which annotations are displayed or managed.

query-filter-options.label-all-dashboards_description: Filter option in the annotation list's query filter options: This option allows users to apply a query or view information across all available dashboards.
query-filter-options.label-this-dashboard_description: Label in Annotation query filter options: Identifies the current dashboard as an option for filtering or scoping data within a query. It allows users to refer to the current dashboard in filter criteria.

time-range-options.label-none_description: Option in Time Range Options for the Annotation List: Allows the user to select no specific time range, effectively disabling time-based filtering for annotations.
time-range-options.label-this-dashboard_description: Label in the time range options menu within the annotation list: This option allows users to apply a time range specifically to the currently active dashboard. It helps filter annotations relevant to the active dashboard's time frame.

annotation-field-mapper.annotation_description: Label in the Annotation Field Mapper section: Refers to the definition or configuration of an annotation within the field mapping process.
annotation-field-mapper.first-value_description: Label in the Annotation Field Mapper: This label identifies an option or field for specifying the first value to be used when mapping data fields for annotations.
annotation-field-mapper.from_description: Label in the annotation field mapper configuration: Denotes the source field or property that provides the data for mapping. It prompts the user to specify the origin of the annotation's value.
annotation-field-mapper.noOptionsMessage-unknown-field-names_des: Informational message in the Annotation Field Mapper: This message is displayed when no options are available to select due to unrecognized or unknown field names.

empty-state.button-title_description: Button in the annotations empty state: Prompts the user to add a new query for annotations when no queries are currently configured.
empty-state.info-box-content: Annotations provide a way to integrate event data into your graphs. They are visualized as vertical lines and icons on all graph panels. When you hover over an annotation icon you can get event text & tags for the event. You can add annotation events directly from grafana by holding CTRL or CMD + click on graph (or drag region). These will be stored in Grafana's annotation database.
empty-state.info-box-content-2: Checkout the <2>Annotations documentation</2> for more information.
empty-state.info-box-content-2_description: Informational text in an empty state information box within the Annotations section: It prompts users to consult the documentation for more details about annotations when no annotations are currently displayed. The phrase "Annotations documentation" is a clickable link.
empty-state.title: There are no custom annotation queries added yet
empty-state.title_description: Heading in the empty state view for custom annotation queries: This text informs the user that no custom annotation queries have been configured or added yet, prompting them to create one.

help.annotation-treated-as-range: When this field is defined, the annotation will be treated as a range
help.annotation-treated-as-range_description: Help text in the Annotation Event Names configuration: It clarifies that when a specific field is set, the annotation will be treated as a time range. This informs users how to configure annotations to represent durations.
help.results-split-on-comma: The results will be split on comma (,)
help.results-split-on-comma_description: Help text in the 'Get Annotation Event Names' feature: Informs the user that the results, likely a list of annotation event names, will be processed or displayed with each item separated by a comma. This clarifies the expected delimiter for multiple values.

placeholder.text-or-the-first-field_description: Placeholder text in an input field for annotation event name configuration: This text guides the user on how event names are derived, either from a default field or the first available text field.
placeholder.time-or-the-first-field_description: Placeholder text in an input field for configuring annotation event properties: This text guides the user on how to specify the time field for annotation events. It suggests using a default field or selecting the first available time-related field.

standard-annotation-query-editor.events-found_description: Status message in the Standard Annotation Query Editor: Informs the user about the number of events found and the number of fields those events originated from after an annotation query is executed.
standard-annotation-query-editor.no-events-found_description: Informational message in the Standard Annotation Query Editor: This text is displayed when an annotation query or filter yields no matching events.
standard-annotation-query-editor.no-query-editor: Annotations are not supported. This datasource needs to export a QueryEditor
standard-annotation-query-editor.no-query-editor_description: Informational message in the standard annotation query editor: Informs the user that annotations cannot be used with the currently selected data source because the data source plugin does not provide the required QueryEditor component.
standard-annotation-query-editor.test-annotation-query_description: Button in the Standard Annotation Query Editor: Initiates a test run of the currently configured annotation query. This allows users to verify its results before saving or applying it.
standard-annotation-query-editor.title-query-result_description: Title in the Standard Annotation Query Editor: Labels the section that displays the data retrieved by the executed query.

app-chrome.skip-content-button_description: Button in the application's global header: Provides an accessibility feature to skip repetitive navigation and jump directly to the page's main content. This button is typically hidden until focused by keyboard navigation.

top-bar.sign-in_description: Button in the top bar of the application interface: Allows users to log into their account.

item.trace-id_description: Display text within an application notification item: Shows the unique identifier for a system trace. This ID is typically provided for debugging or tracking a specific operation or request.

auth-config-page-unconnected.auth-settings_description: Label or section heading on the Authentication Configuration page (for an unconfigured authentication system): It provides access to settings for configuring authentication methods. This appears when the authentication system is not yet fully set up or connected.

auth-drawer-unconneced.subtitle: Configure auth settings. Find out more in our <2>documentation</2> .

auth-drawer-unconnected.advanced-auth_description: Label in the 'Unconnected' section of the Authentication Configuration drawer: This label introduces or provides access to advanced authentication settings and options.
auth-drawer-unconnected.enable-insecure-email-lookup_description: Toggle in the Unconnected Authentication Configuration drawer: This control allows enabling the use of insecure methods for email lookups within the authentication process. This setting may be required for specific integrations but should be used with caution due to security implications.
auth-drawer-unconnected.enable-insecure-email-lookup-description: Allow users to use the same email address to log into Grafana with different identity providers.

Description for a configuration option in the 'Authentication Configuration' section related to identity providers: It explains an option that enables users to log into Grafana with the same email address across multiple identity providers. The key path indicates this feature might have security considerations due to relying on email for identity matching.
auth-drawer-unconnected.reset_description: Button in the authentication configuration drawer (for an unconnected method): Resets the currently entered configuration settings. This action typically reverts inputs to default values or clears the form.
auth-drawer-unconnected.reset-tooltip: This action will disregard any saved changes and load the configuration from the configuration file.
auth-drawer-unconnected.reset-tooltip_description: Tooltip on a 'Reset' action within the 'Unconnected' authentication configuration drawer. It explains that clicking 'Reset' will discard any unsaved changes made in the UI and reload the configuration from the backend file.
auth-drawer-unconnected.title-auth-settings_description: Title in the authentication configuration drawer: This title labels the section dedicated to managing authentication settings. It is displayed when the authentication connection is not yet established or configured.

configure-auth-cta.authentication-configuration-created-mom: You have no authentication configuration created at the moment.

Informational message in the Authentication Configuration section: This text informs the user that no authentication configurations have been created yet. It typically appears when the user first visits the authentication settings and no configurations are defined.
configure-auth-cta.configuration-required_description: Status indicator in the Authentication configuration section's Call-to-Action (CTA): This message informs the user that authentication settings are currently incomplete and require configuration to proceed.
configure-auth-cta.refer-documentation-configure-authentica: Refer to the documentation on how to configure authentication

Link text in the Authentication Configuration section, typically within a call-to-action element. Its purpose is to direct users to the official documentation for detailed instructions on configuring authentication.

label.none_description: Option label in the authentication configuration's field mapping settings: Used to indicate that no specific field is mapped or selected.

fields.allow-assign-grafana-admin-description: If enabled, it will automatically sync the Grafana server administrator role.
fields.allow-assign-grafana-admin-description_d: Description in the Authentication Configuration fields: This text describes the effect of enabling an associated setting, which is the automatic synchronization of the Grafana server administrator role.
fields.allow-assign-grafana-admin-label_descrip: Label for a checkbox or toggle in the Authentication Configuration settings: It describes an option to allow or restrict the assignment of the Grafana administrator role to users. This setting typically pertains to how external authentication sources manage Grafana roles.
fields.allow-sign-up-description: If not enabled, only existing Grafana users can log in using OAuth.
fields.allow-sign-up-description_description: Description in the OAuth authentication configuration settings: This text clarifies the login behavior for users attempting to authenticate via OAuth. It specifically details that only existing Grafana users can log in if the 'Allow Sign Up' option is disabled.
fields.allow-sign-up-label_description: Label for a setting in the Authentication configuration: This option controls whether new users are allowed to self-register and create accounts for the system.
fields.allowed-domains-description:
List of comma- or space-separated domains. The user should belong to at least 
one domain to log in.
fields.allowed-domains-label_description: Label in authentication configuration fields: This label indicates a field where administrators can define domains that are permitted for authentication or user provisioning processes.
fields.allowed-groups-description: List of comma- or space-separated groups. The user should be a member of at least one group to log in.
fields.allowed-groups-description-oauth: If you configure "{{ allowedGroupsLabel }}", you must also configure "{{ groupsAttributePathLabel }}".
fields.allowed-groups-label_description: Label in Authentication Configuration fields: Identifies the groups that are permitted access or certain actions within the system. This label is typically associated with an input field or a list displaying these groups.
fields.allowed-groups-object-ids_description: Validation message in the Auth Configuration fields: This message indicates a requirement or validation error where the attribute identified by `{{ allowedGroupsLabel }}` must exactly match or be defined as `{{ objectIDsField }}`.
fields.allowed-organizations-description:
List of comma- or space-separated organizations. The user should be a member 
of at least one organization to log in.
fields.allowed-organizations-label_description: Label in Authentication Configuration fields: This is a label for a setting that defines which organizations are permitted to authenticate or access the system. It helps administrators configure access control for different organizational entities.
fields.allowed-organizations-placeholder: Enter organizations (my-team, myteam...) and press Enter to add
fields.api-url-description: The user information endpoint of your OAuth2 provider. Information returned by this endpoint must be compatible with <2>OpenID UserInfo</2> .
fields.api-url-required: This field must be a valid URL if set.
fields.api-url-required_description: Validation message in an API URL input field within the Authentication Configuration settings: This message informs the user that if a value is entered into the API URL field, it must conform to a valid URL format. It clarifies a validation rule for a field that might be optional but requires valid input if used.
fields.auth-style-description: It determines how "{{ clientIDLabel }}" and "{{ clientSecretLabel }}" are sent to Oauth2 provider. Default is AutoDetect.
fields.auth-style-label_description: Label in the 'Authentication Configuration' section: Identifies a field where the user selects or views the specific method or type of authentication to be used.
fields.auth-url-description: The authorization endpoint of your OAuth2 provider.
fields.auth-url-description_description: Description text in an OAuth2 authentication configuration field: Explains that this field requires the authorization endpoint URL of the user's OAuth2 provider.
fields.auth-url-required: This field is required and must be a valid URL.
fields.auth-url-required_description: Validation message in the Auth URL field within the Authentication Configuration settings: This message appears when the user attempts to save or submit the configuration without providing a valid URL for the authentication URL field.
fields.auto-login-description: Log in automatically, skipping the login screen.
fields.auto-login-description_description: Description text in the Authentication configuration fields: Explains the functionality of the automatic login setting. This clarifies that users will bypass the login screen upon access.
fields.auto-login-label_description: Label in the authentication configuration settings: Indicates an option to enable automatic user login, allowing users to bypass manual login steps on subsequent visits.
fields.client-authentication-description: The client authentication method used to authenticate to the token endpoint.
fields.client-authentication-description_descri: Description text in the authentication configuration fields: Describes the client authentication method used for authenticating to the token endpoint.
fields.client-authentication-label_description: Label in authentication configuration settings: This label introduces a section or fields related to configuring how clients authenticate with the system.
fields.client-authentication-none_description: Option in the Client Authentication settings: Indicates that no client authentication method is to be used.
fields.client-id-description: The {{ clientIDLabel }} of your OAuth2 app.
fields.client-id-description_description: Helper text in the OAuth2 authentication configuration form: This text describes what value to provide for the client ID of the user's OAuth2 application. The `{{ clientIDLabel }}` is a dynamic placeholder for the actual field label (e.g., 'Client ID', 'App ID').
fields.client-secret-description: The {{ clientSecretLabel }} of your OAuth2 app.
fields.client-secret-description_description: Descriptive text in the Authentication Configuration screen, under fields related to OAuth2: Explains what the `{{ clientSecretLabel }}` field represents for an OAuth2 application. `{{ clientSecretLabel }}` is a dynamic placeholder for the actual secret label (e.g., "Client Secret").
fields.define-allowed-groups-label_description: Label in the Authentication Configuration fields: This label introduces an input area or section where administrators can specify which user groups are permitted access or certain privileges within the system.
fields.define-allowed-teams-ids-label_descripti: Label in the Authentication Configuration fields: It prompts the user to specify or configure the IDs of teams that are allowed to access or use the system.
fields.display-name-description: Will be displayed on the login page as "Sign in with ...". Helpful if you use more than one identity providers or SSO protocols.
fields.display-name-label_description: Label in authentication configuration settings: Refers to a field where users enter a public-facing name or alias, which may differ from their login ID or full legal name, for identification within the system.
fields.domain-hint-description: Parameter to indicate the realm of the user in the Entra ID tenant and streamline the login process.
fields.domain-hint-description_description: Description in the Authentication Configuration fields: Explains a parameter that indicates the user's realm within an Entra ID tenant to streamline the login process.
fields.domain-hint-label_description: Label in the Authentication Configuration fields: This label identifies a field used to provide a domain hint. This hint can help direct users to the appropriate identity provider during the authentication process.
fields.domain-hint-valid-domain: This field must be a valid domain.
fields.domain-hint-valid-domain_description: Validation message for an input field in the Authentication Configuration screen: It informs the user that the entered value must be a valid domain. This message appears when an invalid domain is entered into a field related to authentication settings (e.g., a domain hint).
fields.email-attribute-name-description: Name of the key to use for user email lookup within the attributes map of OAuth2 ID token.
fields.email-attribute-name-description_descrip: Description for an input field in the Authentication Configuration settings: This text explains that the field is used to specify the name of the attribute key within an OAuth2 ID token's attribute map that holds the user's email address.
fields.email-attribute-name-label_description: Label in the Authentication Configuration settings (Fields section): This field prompts the administrator to define the name of the attribute that holds the user's email address from an external authentication source. This is crucial for correctly mapping user email information.
fields.email-attribute-path-description: JMESPath expression to use for user email lookup from the user information.
fields.email-attribute-path-description_descrip: Input field in the authentication configuration screen: Defines the JMESPath expression used to extract a user's email address from their provided information. This ensures correct user identification during the authentication process.
fields.email-attribute-path-label_description: Label in the authentication configuration fields section: This field is used to specify the path or name of the attribute from an external identity provider (e.g., SAML, OIDC) that contains the user's email address. This path is used for mapping the external email attribute to the user's email within BMC Helix Dashboards.
fields.federated-credential-audience-descriptio: The audience of the federated identity credential of your OAuth2 app.
Description in the Auth Configuration screen: This text explains what the "audience" refers to for the federated identity credential of an OAuth2 application.
fields.federated-credential-audience-label_desc: A label in the Authentication Configuration fields section: It identifies the input field where the audience for Federated Identity Credentials (FIC) is specified. The audience refers to the intended recipient or consumer of the federated identity token.
fields.force-use-graph-api-description: If enabled, Grafana will fetch the users' groups using the Microsoft Graph API.
fields.force-use-graph-api-description_descript: Description text in the authentication configuration settings: This text describes an option that, when enabled, forces Grafana to use the Microsoft Graph API to retrieve user groups. This is typically found in configurations for Microsoft-based identity providers.
fields.force-use-graph-api-label_description: Label in Authentication configuration settings: This label indicates an option to explicitly force the system to use the Graph API for authentication or data access. This setting typically overrides any default or automatically detected API selection.
fields.groups-attribute-path-description:
JMESPath expression to use for user group lookup. If you configure "{{ allowedGroupsLabel }}", 
you must also configure "{{ groupsAttributePathLabel }}".
fields.groups-attribute-path-label_description: Label in the Authentication Configuration settings: Specifies the attribute path used to retrieve user group information from an external identity provider (e.g., LDAP, SAML). This allows mapping external groups to internal roles or permissions within BMC Helix Dashboards.
fields.hosted-domain-description: The domain under which Grafana is hosted and accessible.
fields.hosted-domain-description_description: Description text in the Authentication Configuration fields: Defines the domain under which Grafana is hosted and accessible. This text clarifies an associated input field for the hosted domain.
fields.hosted-domain-label_description: Label in the authentication configuration fields: This field prompts the user to enter or identify the domain that is hosted or managed, typically for single sign-on (SSO) or identity federation purposes.
fields.id-token-attribute-name-description: The name of the key used to extract the ID token from the returned OAuth2 token.
fields.id-token-attribute-name-description_desc: Configuration field label in the OAuth2 Authentication configuration settings: This describes a field where an administrator specifies the JSON key name that contains the ID token within an OAuth2 token response. This setting is essential for the system to correctly parse and retrieve the ID token during authentication.
fields.id-token-attribute-name-label_descriptio: Label in the Authentication Configuration fields section: Prompts the user to enter or view the name of a specific attribute (claim) that will be extracted from an ID token. This attribute is typically used for mapping user information from an identity provider.
fields.login-attribute-path-description: JMESPath expression to use for user login lookup from the user ID token.
fields.login-attribute-path-description_descrip: Explanatory text within the 'Authentication Configuration' section, associated with a field for defining a login attribute path: It clarifies that the specified JMESPath expression will be used to extract the user's login identifier from their ID token during authentication.
fields.login-attribute-path-label_description: Label in Authentication Configuration fields: Specifies the path used to locate the login attribute for a user within an external authentication system. This defines where to find the user's login identifier (e.g., username) in the external source response or directory.
fields.login-prompt-consent_description: Field label in Authentication configuration fields: This label identifies a setting or option concerning user consent required during a login prompt.
fields.login-prompt-description: Indicates the type of user interaction when the user logs in with the IdP.
fields.login-prompt-description_description: Description text in the Authentication Configuration settings: This text explains the purpose of a related setting that determines the type of user interaction during login via an Identity Provider (IdP).
fields.login-prompt-label_description: Label in the Authentication Configuration's Fields section: This text is a label for a configuration setting where an administrator can define the login prompt message shown to users.
