fields.login-prompt-login_description: Button on the Login screen: Initiates the user login process, allowing users to access the system.
fields.login-prompt-select-account_description: Text label in the login prompt within the Authentication Configuration screen: Prompts the user to choose one of their available accounts to proceed with authentication.
fields.managed-identity-client-id-description: The managed identity client ID of the federated identity credential of your OAuth2 app.
fields.managed-identity-client-id-description_d: Description text in the Authentication Configuration settings: This text explains the 'Managed Identity Client ID' field, which is used to specify the client ID for a federated identity credential of an OAuth2 application. It clarifies the type of ID required for secure OAuth2 app integration, often in cloud environments using managed identities.
fields.managed-identity-client-id-label_descrip: Label in the authentication configuration fields: Identifies the input field where users enter or view the client ID for a Federated Identity Client (FIC) managed identity.
fields.name-attribute-path-description:
JMESPath expression to use for user name lookup from the user ID token. 
This name will be used as the user's display name.
fields.name-attribute-path-label_description: Label for an input field in the Authentication Configuration screen: This field is used to define the path within an external identity provider's data (e.g., SAML assertion, LDAP entry) where the user's name attribute can be found. This path maps the external name to the application's user name for identification.
fields.org-mapping-description-azuread_descript: Descriptive text in the Azure AD authentication configuration settings: Explains the required format for defining Azure AD group-to-organization and role mappings. This guides users on how to correctly specify mapping values.
fields.org-mapping-description-generic_descript: Description text for a field in the authentication configuration: Explains the required format for organization-to-role mappings, linking external user identities to internal organizations and their assigned roles.
fields.org-mapping-description-github_descripti: Description text in the GitHub authentication configuration settings: This text explains the required format for defining GitHub team to organization and role mappings. It serves as a guide for users configuring these specific mappings.
fields.org-mapping-description-gitlab_descripti: Descriptive text in the GitLab organization mapping section of the Authentication Configuration. It explains the required format for defining GitLab group-to-organization and role mappings, specifying the colon-separated format.
fields.org-mapping-description-google_descripti: Description text in the Google Authentication Configuration screen: It explains the required format for defining mappings between Google Groups, BMC Helix Dashboards organizations, and user roles. This guides user provisioning based on Google group membership.
fields.organization-attribute-path-description: JMESPath expression to use for organization lookup. If you configure "{{ orgMappingLabel }}", you must also configure "{{ orgAttributePathLabel }}".
fields.organization-attribute-path-label_descri: Label for an input field in the authentication configuration fields. It indicates where to specify the path used to locate an organization-specific attribute, likely for mapping user properties from an external identity provider.
fields.organization-mapping-label_description: Label in Authentication configuration settings: This label introduces a section or field related to defining how organizations are mapped or assigned within the authentication setup.
fields.organization-mapping-placeholder: Enter mappings (my-team:1:Viewer...) and press Enter to add
fields.required_description: Validation message in an input field on the Authentication Configuration screen: Informs the user that the specified field requires a value and cannot be left blank.
fields.role-attribute-path-description: {{ jmesPathLabel }} expression to use for Grafana role lookup.
fields.role-attribute-path-description_descript: Help text for an input field on the Authentication Configuration screen. It describes that the field requires a JMESPath expression to look up Grafana roles from user attributes.
fields.role-attribute-path-label_description: Label in Authentication Configuration fields: Specifies the path or expression used to locate the user's role attribute within an external identity provider's response or directory. This attribute is essential for mapping external roles to internal system roles during authentication.
fields.role-attribute-strict-description: If enabled, denies user login if the Grafana role cannot be extracted using Role attribute path.
fields.role-attribute-strict-description_descri: Description text in the Authentication Configuration page, associated with the 'Strict Role Matching' setting. It explains that enabling this option will deny user login if the Grafana role cannot be extracted from the specified role attribute path.
fields.role-attribute-strict-label_description: Option in Auth Configuration fields: Controls the strictness of how role attributes are interpreted during user authentication. Enabling this mode typically enforces exact matches or specific handling for role values from external identity providers.
fields.scopes-description: List of comma- or space-separated OAuth2 {{ scopesLabel }}.
fields.scopes-description_description: Helper text in the Authentication Configuration fields: Explains the expected format for inputting OAuth2 scopes, specifying they should be separated by commas or spaces.
fields.section-extra-security_description: Section Title in Authentication Configuration settings: Labels a section that groups options for enhanced or additional security measures.
fields.section-general-settings_description: Section Title in the Authentication Configuration screen: It designates a section for general settings pertaining to the fields being configured for authentication.
fields.section-user-mapping_description: Section heading in the Authentication Configuration settings: This section allows administrators to define how external user identities or groups are associated with internal system roles, permissions, or attributes.
fields.server-discovery-url-description: The .well-known/openid-configuration endpoint for your IdP. The info extracted from this URL will be used to populate the "{{ authURLLabel }}", "{{ tokenURLLabel }}" and "{{ apiURLLabel }}" fields.
fields.signout-redirect-url-description: The URL to redirect the user to after signing out from Grafana.
fields.signout-redirect-url-description_descrip: Description for the 'Sign-out Redirect URL' field in the Authentication Configuration settings. It clarifies the destination URL for users after signing out of Grafana.
fields.signout-redirect-url-label_description: Label in the Authentication Configuration section: This label identifies a field where administrators can specify the URL to which users are redirected after successfully signing out of the system.
fields.skip-org-role-sync-description: Prevent synchronizing users' organization roles from your IdP.
fields.skip-org-role-sync-description_descripti: Description text in Authentication Configuration settings: This text explains an option that, when enabled, prevents the synchronization of users' organizational roles from an Identity Provider (IdP) to BMC Helix Dashboards.
fields.skip-org-role-sync-label_description: Label in Authentication Configuration fields: This option allows administrators to skip the automatic synchronization of organization roles during user authentication. It likely corresponds to a checkbox or toggle control.
fields.team-ids-attribute-path-description: The JMESPath expression to use for Grafana Team ID lookup within the results returned by the "{{ teamsURLLabel }}" endpoint.
fields.team-ids-attribute-path-label_descriptio: Label in the authentication configuration fields: This identifies the input field where an administrator specifies the attribute path for retrieving a user's team IDs from an external identity provider. This path is crucial for mapping users to teams within BMC Helix Dashboards.
fields.team-ids-attribute-path-required: This field must be set if "{{ teamIDsLabel }}" are configured.
fields.team-ids-attribute-path-required_descrip: Validation message for an authentication configuration form field: This message indicates that the field to which it applies is required to be filled in *only if* team ID configuration (referenced by `{{ teamIDsLabel }}`) is enabled or active. It ensures proper setup for mapping user attributes to teams during authentication.
fields.team-ids-description: If set, the user must be a member of one of the given teams to log in.
fields.team-ids-description_description: Description text in the Authentication Configuration section, next to a field for specifying team IDs: This text explains that if team IDs are configured in the associated field, user login will be restricted to members of those specified teams.
fields.team-ids-description-oauth: If you configure "{{ teamIDsLabel }}", you must also configure "{{ teamsURLLabel }}" and "{{ teamIDsAttributePathLabel }}".
fields.team-ids-github_description: Label for an input field in the Authentication Configuration section: This text describes an input field where users enter a list of integer IDs corresponding to GitHub teams. This configuration is used to define authentication or access permissions based on these specific GitHub teams.
fields.team-ids-label_description: Label in Authentication configuration fields: Identifies the unique identifiers for teams. It typically labels an input field where one or more team IDs are specified for authentication configuration.
fields.team-ids-numbers_description: Validation message in authentication configuration fields: Informs the user that the input for Team IDs must only contain numbers.
fields.team-ids-other_description: Field label in the Authentication configuration settings: This label describes an input field for entering a string list of additional team identifiers. These identifiers are used for authentication purposes.
fields.team-ids-placeholder: Enter Team IDs and press Enter to add
fields.team-ids-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in an input field within the Authentication Configuration settings: Instructs the user on how to enter multiple team IDs and the action required (pressing Enter) to add them.
fields.teams-url-description: The URL used to query for Team IDs. If not set, the default value is /teams.
fields.teams-url-description-oauth: If you configure "{{ teamsURLLabel }}", you must also configure "{{ teamIDsAttributePathLabel }}".
fields.teams-url-description-oauth_description: Instructional message in the Authentication Configuration section: This text informs the user about a mandatory configuration dependency when setting up Teams integration via OAuth. If the Teams URL is configured, the Team IDs Attribute Path field must also be set.
fields.teams-url-label_description: Label in Authentication configuration settings: Prompts the user to enter the URL required for integrating with Microsoft Teams.
fields.teams-url-required: This field must be set if "{{ teamIDsLabel }}" are configured and must be a valid URL.
fields.teams-url-required_description: Validation message in Auth Configuration form: This message indicates that a specific URL field is conditionally required and must be a valid URL if `{{ teamIDsLabel }}` are configured. It enforces a conditional validation rule for the URL field.
fields.tls-client-ca-description: The file path to the trusted certificate authority list. Is not applicable on Grafana Cloud.
fields.tls-client-ca-label_description: Label in authentication configuration settings: Identifies the input field for specifying the Certificate Authority (CA) used for TLS client authentication.
fields.tls-client-cert-description: The file path to the certificate. Is not applicable on Grafana Cloud.
fields.tls-client-cert-label_description: Label in the authentication configuration fields: Identifies the input field or setting where users specify or configure a TLS client certificate for authentication.
fields.tls-client-key-description: The file path to the key. Is not applicable on Grafana Cloud.
fields.tls-client-key-label_description: Label in Authentication configuration field: Identifies an input field where the user provides or uploads the TLS client key necessary for secure client authentication. This key is typically used in mutual TLS setups.
fields.tls-skip-verify-description:
If enabled, the client accepts any certificate presented by the server and any host 
name in that certificate. You should only use this for testing, because this mode leaves 
SSL/TLS susceptible to man-in-the-middle attacks.
fields.tls-skip-verify-label_description: Label in the authentication configuration fields: This option controls whether TLS certificate verification is skipped for connections. It is typically associated with a checkbox or toggle.
fields.token-url-description: The token endpoint of your OAuth2 provider.
fields.token-url-description_description: Description text in the OAuth2 authentication configuration section: This text clarifies that the associated input field is where the user should enter the token endpoint URL provided by their OAuth2 authentication service. It serves as help text for configuring OAuth2.
fields.token-url-required: This field is required and must be a valid URL.
fields.token-url-required_description: Validation message for the Token URL field in Authentication Configuration settings: This message informs the user that the field is mandatory and must contain a valid URL. It ensures proper authentication setup.
fields.use-pkce-description: If enabled, Grafana will use <2>Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE)</2> with the OAuth2 Authorization Code Grant.
fields.use-pkce-label_description: Checkbox label in the Authentication Configuration settings: This label indicates an option to enable or disable the use of Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) for secure authentication flows.
fields.use-refresh-token-description: If enabled, Grafana will fetch a new access token using the refresh token provided by the OAuth2 provider.
fields.use-refresh-token-label_description: Checkbox label in Authentication configuration fields: Indicates an option to enable or disable the use of refresh tokens for authentication. This setting allows for automatic renewal of access tokens.
fields.validate-hosted-domain-description: If enabled, Grafana will match the Hosted Domain retrieved from the Google ID Token against the "{{ allowedDomainsLabel }}" list specified by the user.
fields.validate-hosted-domain-label_description: Label for a checkbox or input field in the Authentication Configuration fields section: This text prompts the user about an option to validate a hosted domain. It likely pertains to verifying domain ownership or configuration for authentication-related services.
fields.workload-identity-token-file-description: The file path to the token file used to authenticate to the OAuth2 provider. This is only required when client authentication is set to "workload_identity". Defaults to /var/run/secrets/azure/tokens/azure-identity-token.
fields.workload-identity-token-file-label_descr: Label in the authentication configuration settings: This label indicates a field for specifying the path to a token file used for workload identity authentication. The `{{ workloadIdentityLabel }}` placeholder will be dynamically replaced by the specific identity provider (e.g., Google, AWS).
fields.workload-identity-token-file-required: This field must be set when client authentication is set to "Workload identity".
fields.workload-identity-token-file-required_de: Validation message for a workload identity token file field in the Client Authentication settings within the Authentication Configuration section: It indicates that this field is mandatory when 'Workload identity' is selected as the client authentication method.

subTitle.configure-authentication-providers_descr: Subtitle in the Authentication Configuration interface: It labels the section where administrators can configure and manage different authentication providers for BMC Helix Dashboards.

text.authentication_description: Navigation link in the Authentication Configuration section: Leads to the page where users can manage authentication settings.

provider-card.saml-learn-more_description: Title in an authentication provider configuration card: Describes the Single Sign-On (SSO) functionality using SAML. It likely heads a section or serves as a label for an informational link.
provider-card.scim-learn-more: Sync users and teams with SCIM.
provider-card.scim-learn-more_description: Descriptive text or link label in a provider configuration card within the Authentication Configuration section: Explains the purpose of SCIM integration for user and team synchronization. This text is associated with a "Learn More" action related to SCIM.
provider-card.text-badge-enabled_description: Text badge on an authentication provider card: Indicates the active status of an authentication provider within the authentication configuration settings.
provider-card.text-badge-not-enabled_description: Text badge in an authentication provider card within the Authentication Configuration section: Indicates that the specific authentication provider is currently disabled or inactive.

additional-actions-menu.label-reset-to-default-values_descriptio: Menu item in the 'Additional Actions' menu within the Authentication Provider Configuration form: Allows users to revert the configuration settings for the authentication provider to their original default values.

provider-config-form.confirmText-reset_description: Button label in a confirmation dialog within the authentication provider configuration form: Confirms the action to reset the configuration or form fields.
provider-config-form.disable_description: Button in the Authentication Provider Configuration Form: Used to deactivate the configured authentication provider or a related setting within the form.
provider-config-form.discard_description: Button in the authentication provider configuration form: To cancel any unsaved changes made in the form and revert to the previous state.
provider-config-form.enabled-label-enabled_description: Label in the authentication provider configuration form: Indicates the active state of a feature or provider.
provider-config-form.label-enabled_description: Label for a toggle or checkbox in the Authentication Provider Configuration Form: It allows users to enable or disable the configured authentication provider.
provider-config-form.reset-configuration: Are you sure you want to reset this configuration?
provider-config-form.reset-configuration_description: Confirmation dialog message in the authentication provider configuration form: Prompts the user to confirm the reset of the current authentication provider's configuration.
provider-config-form.reset-configuration-description: After resetting these settings Grafana will use the provider configuration from the system (config file/environment variables) if any.
provider-config-form.save-and-enable_description: Button in a provider configuration form within the authentication configuration section: Saves the current provider settings and immediately enables the configured provider.
provider-config-form.title-more-actions_description: Menu title in the Authentication Provider Configuration Form: Allows users to access additional or advanced actions for the configured authentication provider.
provider-config-form.title-reset_description: Button in the Provider Configuration Form (Authentication Configuration): Discards unsaved changes and reverts form fields to their default or last saved state.
provider-config-form.tooltip-more-actions_description: Label for an action button or menu item in the Provider Configuration Form within the Authentication Configuration section: This text indicates that additional actions or options are available for the configured provider. It typically triggers a dropdown menu or a list of related tasks.

provider-config-page.text-badge-enabled_description: Text badge in the provider configuration settings: Indicates that an authentication provider or a specific feature is active.
provider-config-page.text-badge-not-enabled_description: Text badge on the Authentication Provider Configuration Page: Indicates that a specific authentication provider is currently not enabled or active.

server-discovery-modal.label-the-wellknownopenidconfiguration-e: Label in Server Discovery Modal within Authentication Configuration: Informs the user about the specific `.well-known/openid-configuration` endpoint URL required for their Identity Provider (IdP). This endpoint is crucial for OpenID Connect automatic discovery.
server-discovery-modal.title-open-id-connect-discovery-url_desc: Field Label in the Server Discovery modal, part of the Authentication Configuration settings: Refers to the URL where an OpenID Connect provider publishes its configuration metadata for discovery.

auth-config-auth-config-page-unconnected.subtitle: Manage your auth settings and configure single sign-on. Find out more in our <2>documentation</2> .

warn.missing-numeric_description: Warning message in Bar Chart configuration: Informs the user that no numeric fields were detected in the data source or query, which are essential for plotting a bar chart.
warn.missing-series: Bar charts require a string or time field
warn.missing-series_description: Warning message in Bar Chart configuration: Informs the user that bar charts require either a string or time field to properly display data. This message is displayed when the necessary field type is missing for the series.

config.category-thresholds_description: Configuration setting in the bar chart configuration section: This enables users to define threshold values for categories, typically used to trigger visual styling changes or alerts based on data.
config.description-color-by-field: Use the color value for a sibling field to color each bar value.
config.description-color-by-field_description: Description in Bar Chart Configuration: This setting allows users to color individual bars based on the color value found in a sibling data field. It provides a data-driven method for assigning visual color to bars using an associated field's color value.
config.description-x-label-max-length: X-axis labels will be truncated to the length provided
config.description-x-label-max-length_descripti: Description in Bar chart configuration settings: Explains that X-axis labels will be truncated if they exceed the maximum length specified by the user.
config.name-bar-radius_description: Setting label in Bar chart configuration: Controls the radius used to round the corners of the bars, affecting their visual curvature.
config.name-bar-width_description: Setting in the configuration settings of a bar chart: This setting allows users to define the width of the bars displayed in the bar chart.
config.name-color-by-field_description: Label for a setting in Bar chart configuration: This option allows users to dynamically color the bars of the chart based on the values of a chosen data field.
config.name-fill-opacity_description: Property setting in the Bar chart configuration panel: Controls the transparency level of the filled areas (bars) within a bar chart.
config.name-full-highlight_description: Toggle option in the bar chart configuration panel: Configures whether the entire area of a bar chart segment is highlighted upon mouse hover. This setting affects the visual feedback on user interaction.
config.name-gradient-mode_description: Setting name in bar chart configuration options: This setting controls whether the bars in the chart are filled with a color gradient.
config.name-group-width_description: Setting label in Bar chart configuration: Specifies the width for groups of bars displayed in the bar chart visualization.
config.name-line-width_description: Setting in bar chart configuration: Controls the thickness of lines displayed within the bar chart.
config.name-orientation_description: Setting in the bar chart configuration panel: Determines whether the bars in the chart are displayed vertically or horizontally.
config.name-rotate-x-labels_description: Checkbox in the bar chart configuration panel: Toggles the rotation of x-axis tick labels. This feature helps prevent label overlap and improves readability.
config.name-show-thresholds_description: Checkbox in Bar Chart Configuration settings: Controls the visibility of defined threshold lines or markers on the bar chart.
config.name-show-values_description: Checkbox in the bar chart configuration panel: Controls whether data values are displayed on the bar chart.
config.name-stacking_description: Setting label in Bar Chart Configuration: This option allows users to define how multiple data series are displayed in the bar chart, typically as stacked segments or grouped bars.
config.name-transform_description: Option or button in Bar Chart Configuration settings: Used to apply or define transformations for the names of data elements or labels within the bar chart.
config.name-x-axis_description: Label for an input field in bar chart configuration options: Identifies the field where users can customize the name of the horizontal (X) axis.
config.name-x-label-max-length_description: Configuration setting in the bar chart configuration panel: Defines the maximum character length for labels displayed on the X-axis ticks. This helps prevent text overlap or undesirable truncation.
config.name-x-label-min-spacing_description: Configuration option in Bar Chart Configuration settings: Defines the minimum spacing between X-axis labels. This helps ensure readability by preventing label overlap.

orientation-options.label-auto_description: Option in Bar Chart Orientation settings: This selection automatically determines the orientation (vertical or horizontal) of the bar chart. It allows the system to decide the best display based on data or layout.
orientation-options.label-horizontal_description: Option label in the bar chart configuration settings, within the orientation options: This label allows users to select 'Horizontal' as the display orientation for the bar chart. Choosing this option will render the chart's bars horizontally.
orientation-options.label-line-vertical_description: Option label in the Bar Chart configuration's Orientation options: Used to select a vertical orientation for the bar chart display.

config.placeholder-x-axis_description: Placeholder text in the Bar Chart Configuration panel: This text indicates the expected data type (a string or time-based field) for the primary X-axis selection.
config.placeholder-x-label-max-length_descripti: Option or default value in the Bar Chart's X-axis label maximum length configuration: This indicates that no maximum character limit is applied to the X-axis labels.

show-values-options.label-always_description: Option label in the bar chart configuration's 'Show Values' options: This setting ensures data values are always displayed on the bar chart.
show-values-options.label-auto_description: Option in Show Values Options for Bar Chart Configuration: This option allows the system to automatically determine how values are displayed on the bar chart.
show-values-options.label-never_description: Option in the bar chart's 'Show values' configuration settings: This selection dictates that no values should ever be displayed on the bar chart.

transform-options.description-constant: The first value will be shown as a constant line
transform-options.description-constant_description: Description text in the 'Transform Options' section of a Barchart configuration: This text clarifies a setting that transforms data by displaying the first value of a series as a constant horizontal line on the chart.
transform-options.description-negative-y: Flip the results to negative values on the y axis
transform-options.description-negative-y_description: Description in Bar chart configuration > Transform options: Explains an option that allows users to invert bar chart data, displaying positive results as negative values on the Y-axis. This is a visual transformation feature.
transform-options.label-constant_description: Option label in Bar chart transformation options: This label identifies an option to use a constant value or display a constant line on the chart.
transform-options.label-negative-y_description: Option label in Bar Chart's Transform Options: This label identifies a setting related to the negative Y-axis. It controls its display, behavior, or how negative data values are transformed on the chart.

tick-spacing-editor.content-require-space-from-the-right-sid: Require space from the right side

Option in the Bar Chart's Tick Spacing Editor: This setting allows users to enforce additional space on the right side of the chart content, typically to prevent labels or data points from being cut off or to improve visual balance.

gaps-options.description-large_description: Descriptive text in the Gaps Options section of the Tick Spacing Editor for a Bar Chart: This text informs the user about a required minimum spacing between ticks, where `{{spacing}}` is a dynamic value.
gaps-options.description-medium_description: Descriptive text in the Bar Chart's tick spacing editor, within the gaps options section: This string serves as a label or instructional text for a setting that allows users to define or enforce a specific spacing value between ticks or bars on the chart.
gaps-options.description-none_description: Option label in the Barchart configuration's 'Tick Spacing Editor', within the 'Gaps Options' section: This option ensures all tick marks are visible on the chart axis. It signifies that no tick marks should be skipped, contrasting with other gap-related settings.
gaps-options.description-small_description: Description text in the Gaps Options section within the Tick Spacing Editor for a Bar Chart: This text informs the user that a specific spacing value for the gaps will be required or enforced.
gaps-options.label-large_description: Option in the Gaps Options section of the Tick Spacing Editor for a Bar Chart: This option allows the user to select "Large" as a predefined size for the gaps, likely affecting spacing between bars or axis ticks.
gaps-options.label-medium_description: Option label in the 'Gaps Options' section of the bar chart's tick spacing editor: This option allows users to select a medium level for the spacing between ticks or bars.
gaps-options.label-none_description: Option in the Gaps Options section of the Tick Spacing Editor for Bar Charts: This option allows the user to select 'no gaps' or disable a specific gap setting for the chart's ticks.
gaps-options.label-small_description: Option label in the "Gaps Options" section of the "Tick Spacing Editor" for bar charts: It represents a selection for a small gap size between elements (e.g., bars or axis ticks).

bar-size-options.label-auto_description: Label in Bar Size Options for Bar Gauge: This option allows the bar size to be automatically determined by the system.
bar-size-options.label-manual_description: Option in bar size options for a bar gauge: Allows the user to manually define the size of the bars.

bargauge.category-bar-gauge_description: Visualization type label in the dashboard editor: This string identifies the 'Bar gauge' visualization option, allowing users to select it for displaying data in a panel.
bargauge.description-max-height_description: Description text in Bar Gauge configuration settings: This text explains the 'Maximum row height' setting, clarifying that the height limit applies specifically to rows when the bar gauge visualization is oriented horizontally. It helps users understand how to control the vertical spacing of rows in this display mode.
bargauge.description-min-height_description: A setting in the Bar Gauge visualization configuration: Defines the minimum height for rows when the bar gauge is displayed with a horizontal orientation. This helps control the visual layout and spacing.
bargauge.description-min-width_description: Setting label in Bar Gauge visualization configuration: Defines the minimum width for the columns (bars) when the bar gauge is oriented vertically.
bargauge.description-show-unfilled-area: When enabled renders the unfilled region as gray
bargauge.description-show-unfilled-area_descripti: An option in the Bar Gauge visualization settings: This controls the visual rendering of the unfilled region of the bar gauge. When enabled, this region is displayed in gray.

display-mode-options.label-basic_description: Option name in Bar Gauge display mode options: Selects a basic or default display style for the bar gauge.
display-mode-options.label-gradient_description: Label in Bar Gauge Display Mode Options: This option allows users to select a gradient color fill for the bar gauge, providing a smooth color transition.
display-mode-options.label-retro_description: Label in the 'Display Mode Options' for a Bar Gauge: This option represents a visual style for the bar gauge, specifically a 'retro LCD' aesthetic.

bargauge.name-bar-size_description: Setting in Bar Gauge visualization options: Specifies the size of the bars displayed in the bar gauge.
bargauge.name-display-mode_description: Label in Bar Gauge panel settings: This option controls the visual presentation of the bar gauge's name or label.
bargauge.name-max-height_description: Label in Bar Gauge configuration: Specifies the maximum height allowed for the bar gauge visualization.
bargauge.name-min-height_description: Label in the bar gauge configuration panel: Defines the minimum height setting for the bar gauge visualization.
bargauge.name-min-width_description: Setting label in the Bar Gauge configuration panel: Defines the minimum width for the 'name' or label column associated with each bar in the visualization. This ensures the name text is always visible up to the specified width.
bargauge.name-name-placement_description: Setting in Bar Gauge visualization options: Defines where the name or label for the bar gauge or its series is positioned. This allows users to customize the visual layout.

name-placement-options.label-auto_description: Label option in Bar Gauge Name Placement options: Automatically determines the best placement for names or labels within the bar gauge visualization.
name-placement-options.label-hidden_description: A value for a label placement option in the Bar Gauge visualization's configuration settings: This selection hides the name or label of the bar gauge from being displayed.
name-placement-options.label-left_description: Option in Bar Gauge Name Placement Options: Specifies that the label or name for a bar gauge should be displayed on the left side.
name-placement-options.label-top_description: A placement option in the bar gauge's label configuration: Specifies that the label or name for the bar gauge should appear at the top of the visualization.

bargauge.name-show-unfilled-area_description: Checkbox or toggle switch in Bar Gauge visualization settings: Controls the visibility of the unfilled portion of a bar gauge. When enabled, it visually represents the remaining capacity of the gauge.
bargauge.name-value-display_description: Configuration option in Bar Gauge panel settings: Determines how the numerical value represented by the gauge is presented to the user.

value-display-options.label-hidden_description: Option in the value display options for a Bar Gauge visualization: Indicates that the label displaying the bar gauge's value should be hidden.
value-display-options.label-text-color_description: Setting label in the Value Display Options section of a Bar Gauge visualization: It allows users to choose the color for the text displaying the bar gauge's actual value.
value-display-options.label-value-color_description: Setting in the Value display options for a Bar Gauge: This option allows users to define the color of the numerical value displayed within the bar gauge.

users.search-label: Search user by login, email, or name
users.search-label_description: Search field label in the Users screen within the Admin section of BMC Helix Dashboards: Guides users on how to search for other users. It indicates the acceptable search criteria (login, email, or name) for the input field.

action.move-folder-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the Browse Dashboards section: Informs the user that folders cannot be moved. This message appears when a user attempts an action to move a folder, clarifying a system limitation.

bulk-operations.datsource-not-found: No data sources of type {{pluginName}} found
bulk-operations.datsource-not-found_description: Informational message in the Bulk Operations section of BMC Helix Dashboards: Informs the user that no data sources of the specified `{{pluginName}}` type could be found. This message typically appears when a bulk operation requires a data source of that particular type.

import.import-all-failed_description: Error Message in the Bulk Operations > Import section: Informs the user that the attempted bulk import of all dashboards was unsuccessful. This message is displayed when a bulk import operation fails to complete for all selected dashboards.
import.no-import_description: Status message in the Import section of the Bulk Operations feature: Informs the user that no dashboards were successfully imported during a bulk import operation. This message appears when an import attempt yields zero results.

bulk-operations.import-all_description: Button in the Bulk Operations section: Initiates the import of all available or selected items. This action is part of a larger bulk operation process.
bulk-operations.import-failed: Failed to import the following dashboards
bulk-operations.import-failed_description: Error message in the Bulk Operations section: Informs the user that an attempt to import multiple dashboards has failed. This message is typically followed by a list of the specific dashboards that could not be imported.
bulk-operations.import-success_description: Status message in the Bulk Operations feature: Confirms the successful import of all selected dashboards into BMC Helix Dashboards.
bulk-operations.options_description: Button in the Bulk Operations section: Allows users to configure settings or parameters for the bulk operation.
bulk-operations.select-datasource_description: Label in the Bulk Operations feature: Instructs the user to select a data source for the bulk operation. The `{{pluginName}}` placeholder will be replaced by the specific type of data source (e.g., Prometheus, Elasticsearch).
bulk-operations.set-parameters: set required parameters for the selected dashboards
bulk-operations.set-parameters_description: Instruction in the Bulk Operations feature: This string prompts the user to define the mandatory parameters for the selected dashboards. It functions as a heading or label for a step in the bulk operation workflow where these settings are configured.
bulk-operations.specify-constant_description: Label or placeholder text in the Bulk Operations feature: Prompts the user to enter a fixed string value that will be consistently applied or used as a criterion during a bulk operation.

calc-fields.a-z_description: Sorting option in Calculated Fields: Sorts displayed items alphabetically in ascending order.
calc-fields.aggregated_description: Option in the Calculated Fields configuration: Specifies that a calculated field's value is determined by an aggregation function. This setting indicates that data for the field will be summarized rather than displayed individually.
calc-fields.all_description: Option in the Calculated Fields section: Represents a selection or filter to display all available calculated fields without any specific criteria.
calc-fields.clone_description: Action in the Calculated Fields section: Used to create a duplicate copy of an existing calculated field.
calc-fields.confirm-delete: Do you want to delete the selected calculated field(s)
calc-fields.confirm-delete_description: Confirmation Message in the Calculated Fields management section: Prompts the user to confirm the deletion of one or more selected calculated fields.
calc-fields.connection-error: Connectivity to BMC Helix ITSM is not configured.
calc-fields.connection-error_description: Error message in the Calculated Fields configuration: Informs the user that the required connectivity to BMC Helix ITSM is not set up, preventing features related to calculated fields.
calc-fields.custom_description: Option in Calculated Fields: Allows users to define a custom, user-specified calculation.
calc-fields.details_description: Page title in the Calculated Fields section: Displays the details and properties of a selected calculated field.
calc-fields.edit-field_description: Page or dialog title in the Calculated Fields management area: Allows users to modify the properties or definition of an existing calculated field.
calc-fields.eg_description: Abbreviation in the Calculated Fields feature: This text is used to precede or introduce an example, often for field definitions or expressions. It stands for "for example".
calc-fields.failed_description: Error Message in the Calculated Fields section: This message is displayed when the system fails to retrieve the list or details of calculated fields, indicating a problem with data retrieval or the backend service.
calc-fields.failure_description: Indicator text in Calculated Fields: This term denotes an unsuccessful operation, validation, or outcome related to a calculated field.
calc-fields.filter-by-type_description: Filter control in the Calculated Fields management interface: This control allows users to filter the list of calculated fields by their type. By default, all calculated field types are displayed.
calc-fields.form-name_description: Label for an input field in the Calculated Fields section: Used to specify or display the name of a form. This form might be a data source or a configuration form for calculated fields.
calc-fields.is-aggregate_description: Checkbox/Toggle in Calculated Fields configuration: Specifies whether a calculated field's value is derived from an aggregation function. This property determines how the field is processed and displayed within dashboards.
calc-fields.is-aggregate-description: Enable this option if your query includes aggregation functions.
calc-fields.is-aggregate-description_description: Description for a checkbox/toggle in the Calculated Fields editor: It guides the user to enable the associated option if the query for their calculated field includes aggregation functions. This setting is important for the correct processing of the aggregate query.
calc-fields.module_description: Label in the Calculated Fields section: Indicates the module or functional area associated with a specific calculated field.
calc-fields.module-name_description: Label in the calculated fields editor: Used to identify or specify the name of the module where a calculated field is defined or managed.
calc-fields.name: Name
calc-fields.name-placeholder: Enter the name of the calculated field
calc-fields.name-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Calculated Fields creation/editing interface: Prompts the user to enter a name for the calculated field being defined.
calc-fields.new-field_description: Label in the Calculated Fields management section: Indicates an action to create a new calculated field.
calc-fields.no-calc-fields: You haven't created any calculated fields yet.
calc-fields.no-calc-fields_description: Message in the Calculated Fields section: Informs the user that no calculated fields have been defined. This message is displayed when the list of calculated fields is empty, prompting the user to create new ones.
calc-fields.not-found: No calculated fields matching your query were found.
calc-fields.not-found_description: **Message** in the **Calculated Fields section**: Informs the user that their search or filter query for calculated fields did not return any matching results. This message is displayed when a search operation yields no entries.
calc-fields.ootb_description: Label in Calculated Fields: This term identifies calculated fields that are provided as standard, pre-configured options within the product.
calc-fields.query-info: Query must be a single column query
calc-fields.query-info_description: Instruction/Validation message in the Calculated Fields editor: Informs the user that a query defined for a calculated field must return a single column of data. This constraint is crucial for the correct processing of the calculation.
calc-fields.query-not-validated_description: Error message in the calculated fields UI: Notifies the user that the query defined for a calculated field has failed validation. This indicates a potential issue with the query's syntax or logic, preventing its use.
calc-fields.search-by-name_description: Placeholder text for a search input field in the Calculated Fields section: Prompts users to enter a name to filter or find calculated fields.
calc-fields.search-items_description: Label or placeholder text for a search input field in the Calculated Fields section: Allows users to find specific calculated fields or related items by entering search terms.
calc-fields.select-form_description: Button in the Calculated Fields section: Allows the user to select a specific form. This action is typically part of configuring a calculated field's properties.
calc-fields.select-module_description: Label for a selection control in the Calculated Fields section: Prompts the user to choose a module. This selection is likely a prerequisite for defining or editing calculated fields.
calc-fields.sort-asc_description: Sorting option in the calculated fields editor: Allows users to arrange calculated fields in ascending alphabetical order (A-Z).
calc-fields.sort-asc-descripton: Sort results in an alphabetically ascending order
calc-fields.sort-asc-descripton_description: Description text in the Calculated Fields management section: Describes the action of sorting results alphabetically in ascending order. This text likely provides additional context or serves as a tooltip for a sorting control.
calc-fields.sort-dsc_description: Label for a sorting option in the Calculated Fields section: Indicates that items should be sorted in descending alphabetical order (Z-A).
calc-fields.sort-dsc-descripton: Sort results in an alphabetically descending order
calc-fields.sort-dsc-descripton_description: Description for a sorting option in the Calculated Fields editor: This text informs the user that the selected option will sort the results alphabetically in a descending order.
calc-fields.sort-options_description: Label in the Calculated Fields interface: Indicates a section or controls where users can configure the sorting criteria for calculated fields or data displayed using them.
calc-fields.sort-placeholder_description: Placeholder text for a sort selection control in the Calculated Fields section: This string indicates the default sorting option, displaying the name of the field that is currently used for default sorting.
calc-fields.sql-query_description: Label in the Calculated Fields configuration: This label identifies an input field where users define a SQL query. This query is used to calculate the value of a custom field.
calc-fields.subtitle: Create and manage calculated fields for ITSM service
calc-fields.subtitle_description: Subtitle on the Calculated Fields management page: Informs users that this section allows them to create and manage calculated fields specifically for ITSM services.
calc-fields.title: Calculated fields
calc-fields.title_description: Section Title in the Calculated Fields management area: This title identifies the section or page where users can create, view, and manage custom data fields derived from other data using formulas or expressions.
calc-fields.unknown-failure_description: Error message in Calculated Fields: Informs the user that an operation or process related to calculated fields has failed for an unknown reason. This message is displayed when the system cannot provide a specific cause for the failure.
calc-fields.validate_description: Button in Calculated Fields section: Allows users to check the syntax or correctness of a defined calculated field.
calc-fields.validation-success_description: Success message in the Calculated Fields Editor: Informs the user that the query defined for a calculated field has been successfully validated.

common.dashboards_description: Section Title in the BMC Helix Dashboards application: Designates the main area where users can access, create, and manage their monitoring and reporting dashboards.
common.download_description: Button in common UI components across the application: Initiates the download of a file or data.
common.name: Name
common.type_description: Field label in common forms or tables within BMC Helix Dashboards: Specifies the classification or category of an item or entity. This is a generic attribute used across various BMC modules.

bmc.content-localization_description: Error message in the language settings or content localization feature: Notifies the user when an invalid or unsupported language code has been entered or detected.

toolbar.manage-reports_description: Button in the dashboard toolbar: This button allows users to access the interface for viewing, editing, and managing reports that have been scheduled for automated generation and delivery.
toolbar.reset-filters_description: Button in the dashboard toolbar: Clears all applied filters on the dashboard, restoring the default view.
toolbar.reset-filters-failure_description: Error message in Dashboard toolbar: Informs the user that an attempt to reset the dashboard's filter values has failed. This message appears after a user initiates the "Reset Filters" action, but the operation could not be completed.
toolbar.save-filters_description: Button in the dashboard toolbar: Allows users to save the currently applied filter settings for later use.
toolbar.save-filters-failure_description: Error message in Dashboard Toolbar: Appears when a user tries to save filter values from the dashboard toolbar, but the operation fails due to an underlying issue.
toolbar.save-filters-success_description: Success message in the Dashboard toolbar: Confirms that filter values have been successfully saved. This message appears after a user attempts to save filter settings.

dashboard-import.import-text_description: Button in the Dashboard Import feature: Initiates the process of importing a dashboard.
dashboard-import.overwrite_description: Button in the Dashboard Import dialog: Allows the user to replace an existing dashboard with the imported version if a dashboard with the same name or ID already exists.
dashboard-import.panel-import_description: Label in the dashboard import feature: Denotes the functionality for importing individual panels into a dashboard.
dashboard-import.panel-import-alert: The import has a {{vqb}} panel. Please re-export it from the desired environment, or manual intervention will be required for view selection.
dashboard-import.sub-title: Import dashboard from file or via dashboard json
dashboard-import.sub-title_description: Subtitle in the Dashboard Import section: Informs the user about the two available methods for importing a dashboard. It clarifies that dashboards can be imported either by uploading a file or by providing its JSON configuration.

description.optimize-variable-description: Define an optimize variable, where users can select optimize query types
description.optimize-variable-description_descriptio: Optimize Variable description in the Editable Variables section of a BMC Helix Dashboard scene: Defines an optimize variable where users can select optimize query types.

name.datepicker_description: Label for a Date Picker component in the editable variables settings of a dashboard. It allows users to define or select a date range for a dashboard variable.
name.optimizepicker_description: Setting in the dashboard's variable editing interface: Configures optimization for the variable's value picker, impacting how its data is loaded or displayed.

text.discard-changes-to-dashboard: You have unsaved changes to this dashboard. Are you sure you want to discard them?
text.discard-changes-to-dashboard_description: Confirmation Message in the Dashboard Editor: Prompts the user to confirm discarding unsaved changes to a dashboard. This message appears when attempting to navigate away or close the dashboard without saving.

general.multilingual-pdf_description: Setting option in the Dashboard General settings: Controls the ability to generate PDF exports or reports that support multiple languages.
general.multilingual-pdf-description: Enable multilingual PDF generation for simple layout PDF reports
general.multilingual-pdf-description_description: Help text in the General settings section under Dashboard Settings: Describes a feature that allows users to enable multilingual PDF generation for PDF reports. This functionality is specifically for reports with a simple layout.

date-picker.description: Define a date range variable, where users can select any date range
date-picker.description_description: Description text in Date Picker configuration: Explains how to define a date range variable. This variable allows users to select any custom date range for data filtering.
date-picker.name: Date Range
date-picker.name_description: Label in a Date Picker component: Identifies the field or control where users can select a specific range of dates.

error-page.bmc-support_description: Link text in the BMC Helix Dashboards error page: Directs users to BMC's support resources for assistance when an error occurs.
error-page.description: This page might have been removed, had its name changed, or is temporarily unavailable.
error-page.description_description: Description text in an Error page: Informs the user about potential reasons why a requested page cannot be displayed. It provides common causes for a missing or inaccessible page.
error-page.go-back_description: Button or link text in an error page: Used to allow the user to navigate away from the current error page. This is a partial string and will be followed by the name of the destination page.
error-page.home-page_description: Label on the error page: Used for a link or button that allows the user to navigate back to the main home page of the application from an error state.
error-page.or-contact_description: Text segment in an Error Page: This conjunction connects a prior troubleshooting step with the alternative option to contact support. It guides the user on seeking further assistance when an error occurs.
error-page.title: Oops... we could not load that page.

explore.total-result-count-aria-live_one_descrip: Screen reader announcement in the Explore feature: Informs users that a query has finished loading. This string is specifically used when one query has been loaded.
explore.total-result-count-aria-live_other_descr: Accessibility announcement in the Explore section: Informs users that a specific number of queries have finished loading. This dynamic message is designed for screen readers to convey loading status updates.

export.csvdelimiter_description: Setting label in the CSV export options: Allows the user to select or specify the character used to separate values in an exported CSV file.
export.fail_description: Error message prefix in the Export functionality: Notifies the user that an export operation has failed. Note: The Spanish translation incorrectly uses 'importación' (import) instead of 'exportación' (export).
export.failed-dashboards_description: Section heading in the Export functionality: This identifies a list or category of dashboards that failed during an export process.
export.field-enclosure_description: Setting in export configuration: Defines the character used to enclose individual data fields when exporting data, commonly for formats like CSV.
export.generation-failed: Failed to generate report, try again later
export.generation-failed_description: Error message in the report export function: Informs the user that the attempt to generate a report has failed and suggests retrying the action later. This message is displayed when a technical issue prevents the successful creation of the report file.
export.grid_description: Option in export settings within BMC Helix Dashboards: Specifies to export data or content in a structured, tabular layout.
export.hide-header_description: Option in Export settings: Controls whether the header is visible in the exported file or document.
export.landscape_description: Option in Export settings: Allows users to select landscape orientation for exported documents. This sets the page layout where the width is greater than the height.
export.layout_description: Setting in export configuration: Allows users to define the visual arrangement or structure of the exported dashboard or report.
export.newline_description: Setting in the Export options: Defines the format for new line characters (e.g., Windows, Unix) used in exported files. This is important for ensuring data compatibility across different operating systems.
export.orientation_description: Label for a setting in the export options. It allows users to select the page orientation (e.g., portrait or landscape) for the exported file.
export.portrait_description: Option in the Export settings: Allows users to select 'Portrait' as the page orientation for the exported content.
export.reduce-selection: Please reduce selected variable values for {{variableQuery}} and try again later
export.reduce-selection_description: Error message in the Export functionality: Informs the user that the number of selected variable values for `{{variableQuery}}` exceeds export limits, requiring them to reduce the selection and retry.
export.save-as_description: Label in the export dialog: Indicates the operation to save a specified item, such as a dashboard or report, to a particular file format. The placeholders `{{target}}` and `{{type}}` are dynamically replaced with the item name and the chosen file type.
export.select-report-type-aria_description: Label for a selection control in the Export feature: Prompts the user to specify the type or format of the report being exported. This string likely also functions as an ARIA label for accessibility.
export.select-report-type-placeholder_descripti: Placeholder text in the Export feature/dialog: Prompts the user to choose the specific type of report they wish to export.
export.simple_description: Option label in Export dialog: Indicates a basic or standard export format or method. This option likely provides a less complex output compared to other export choices.
export.success_description: Success Message in the Report Export feature: Informs the user that a report generation or export operation has completed successfully.
export.successful_description: Status message in a notification or status area following an export operation: confirms to the user that an export action was successfully completed. The colon suggests it often precedes further details about the export.
export.tableScaling_description: Option in Export options: Specifies how tables are scaled for the exported output. This setting affects the visual size of tables in the generated file.
export.type_description: Label in the export settings: Identifies the format or category of the data or item to be exported.
export.view_description: Action button or link in the Export section: Allows users to display or open the generated export content or a preview of it.

features-list.date-function-resolution-for-service-man: Date function resolution for service management
Feature name in the Features list: This capability enhances how date functions are resolved or processed specifically for service management data within BMC Helix Dashboards.
features-list.enable-insight-finder_description: Toggle switch in the Features list section: Controls the activation of the Insight Finder feature. This determines if the Insight Finder functionality is available within BMC Helix Dashboards.
features-list.enable-ootb-views-for-visual-query-build: Enable OOTB Views for Visual Query Builder

Option in the Features List: This option controls the availability of pre-defined, Out-Of-The-Box (OOTB) views within the Visual Query Builder. Enabling this allows users to leverage ready-to-use views for creating queries.
features-list.export-complete-table-in-pdf_description: Menu item in a table's export or options menu within a dashboard: Allows users to export the entire displayed table's data into a PDF document.
features-list.skip-ootb-dashboards-during-upgrade_desc: Setting option in the Features list: This option allows users to prevent Out-Of-The-Box (OOTB) dashboards from being updated or overwritten during a system upgrade. Enabling it preserves custom modifications to these dashboards.

skip-ootb-dashboards-during-upgrade-conf.confirm-button_description: Button in a confirmation dialog shown when a user is interacting with the feature to skip out-of-the-box (OOTB) dashboards during an upgrade. Clicking this button confirms and activates the selected feature.
skip-ootb-dashboards-during-upgrade-conf.desc: Delete the folders of out-of-the-box dashboards that are not required before enabling this feature
skip-ootb-dashboards-during-upgrade-conf.desc_description: Confirmation dialog message in the Features list of BMC Helix Dashboards: This message instructs users to delete unneeded out-of-the-box dashboard folders as a prerequisite before enabling the feature to skip these dashboards during an upgrade.
skip-ootb-dashboards-during-upgrade-conf.title: Toggle Feature
skip-ootb-dashboards-during-upgrade-conf.title_description: Dialog title in the confirmation dialog shown when a user attempts to enable or disable a feature within the Features List. It informs the user that they are about to change the state of the "Skip OOTB Dashboards During Upgrade" feature.

features-list.snapshot_description: Feature in the Features list: Represents the ability to create a static, point-in-time capture of a dashboard or its data. This capture is typically used for sharing or historical comparison.

stats.statistics_description: Section title or tab label in the Inspector's 'Stats' panel: This element identifies the area where users can view detailed statistical data and performance metrics.
stats.value_description: Label in the Statistics section of the Inspector tool: Identifies a specific statistical value being displayed for an inspected element.

key-selector.no-results_description: Status message in the Key Selector component: Informs the user when a search or filter operation for keys yields no results. It prompts the user to revise their input.

load-blank-dashoard.no-data_description: Status message in a blank dashboard view: Informs the user that no data is available to display on the dashboard.
load-blank-dashoard.refresh-label_description: Text label in the context of a newly loaded or blank dashboard: Informs the user that they need to refresh the dashboard to fetch its data. This label guides the user on how to populate the dashboard with information.

manage-dashboards.drop-click: Drag and drop here or click to browse
manage-dashboards.drop-click_description: File upload area in the Manage Dashboards section: Prompts users to upload a dashboard file by dragging and dropping it, or by clicking to open a file browser.
manage-dashboards.import-failed_description: Error message in the Manage Dashboards section: Informs the user that an attempted dashboard import operation has failed. This message is displayed when the system encounters an issue during the import process.
manage-dashboards.serialization-failed_description: Error message in the Manage Dashboards section: Informs the user about a technical failure during the processing of dashboard data, specifically the conversion between JSON and JavaScript formats. This indicates an internal system error.
manage-dashboards.upload-json_description: Button in the 'Manage Dashboards' section: Allows users to upload one or more dashboard configuration files in JSON format.

dashboards.add-key_description: Button in the 'Manage Locales' screen for Dashboards: Allows users to add a new translation key for dashboard-related content.
dashboards.dashboard-name_description: Label in the dashboard management interface: It identifies the input field where users can enter or view the name of a dashboard.
dashboards.enter-key_description: Input field label in Dashboards locale management settings: Prompts the user to enter a localization or translation key.
dashboards.enter-value_description: Input field placeholder in the Dashboards section of the BMC Helix Dashboards' locale management interface: Instructs the user to input a value. This value likely pertains to locale-specific settings, properties, or data for dashboards.
dashboards.key-text_description: Label for a keyboard key in Dashboard localization management: This string names a specific keyboard key. It is likely used when defining or describing shortcuts or other interactive controls within dashboards.
dashboards.key-validation_description: Validation message in a dashboard key input field within the Dashboards management interface: Informs the user that only alphanumeric characters are allowed when defining or editing a dashboard key.
dashboards.no-empty-key_description: Validation message in the dashboard's locale management settings: Informs the user that a field for a 'key' identifier cannot be left blank, ensuring proper configuration or localization of dashboard elements.

validation.default-value-required_description: Validation message in dashboard configuration forms: Informs the user that a required default value for a setting, such as a variable or filter, has not been provided. This message appears when the user attempts to save or apply changes without fulfilling this requirement.
validation.key-required_description: Validation message in a form field within the Dashboard Locale Management section: Indicates that a required 'key' input is missing during form submission or data entry.

dashboards.value-text_description: Label in BMC Helix Dashboards: This text serves as a generic identifier for a data point, metric result, or a user-configurable setting displayed within a dashboard element.

manage-locales.export_description: Button in the Manage Locales page: Allows users to export locale data, such as translation files or locale configurations, from the system.

folders.folder-title_description: Label for a text input field in the folder management section: Prompts the user to enter the name of a folder. This folder likely organizes dashboards or other resources within BMC Helix Dashboards.

global.subtitle: Create and manage global keys for localization
global.subtitle_description: Subtitle in the Global Keys management screen: Clearly states the primary function of this section: to create and manage global keys used for application localization.
global.title: Localization management
global.title_description: Page Title in the Localization Management section: This title identifies the primary area where users can manage localization settings and content for the application.

manage-locales.global-keys-text_description: Label in the 'Manage Locales' section: Identifies a collection or management area for translation keys that are globally applicable across all supported locales.
manage-locales.import_description: Button in the 'Manage Locales' section: Triggers the import of locale data or translation files.
manage-locales.local-keys-text_description: Heading in the `Manage Locales` section: It serves as a title or label for a list or group of specific localization keys. This term distinguishes application-specific localization keys from global or other key types.
manage-locales.localization-title_description: Page Title in the 'Manage Locales' section: This title identifies the area dedicated to managing language and regional adaptations of the application.
manage-locales.new-key_description: Button label in the 'Manage Locales' section: Indicates the action to create a new localization key. This key will be used to identify a translatable string within the system.
manage-locales.title: Manage locales
manage-locales.title_description: Page Title in the 'Manage locales' configuration area: Designates the section for managing and configuring language, regional settings, and other localization preferences. This refers to locale definitions, not geographical locations.

insight-finder.configure-views-subtitle_description: Subtitle in the Configure Views section of the Insight Finder application: Describes the functionality available for viewing and managing user-created views or custom dashboards.
insight-finder.configure-views-title_description: Page Title in the Insight Finder application: It designates the section where users can set up and customize various data views.
insight-finder.home_description: Application Name in the applications section of the BMC Helix Dashboards navigation: This string identifies the main entry point for the Insight Finder application, which allows users to discover and analyze insights.
insight-finder.settings-title_description: Title in the Insight Finder application: This labels the section where users can adjust the configuration and preferences for the Insight Finder feature.

reports.history_description: Navigation item in the Reports section of the application navigation: Shows a record of past report activities, such as executions or modifications.
reports.manage-ownership_description: Link in the Reports section of the Applications navigation: Allows users to manage the ownership or access rights for reports.
reports.settings_description: Menu item in the Reports section of the Applications navigation: Allows users to access and configure options related to reports.

nav.reports-scheduler_description: Navigation menu item in the BMC Helix Dashboards application: This item allows users to access and manage the scheduling of various reports.

rms.subtitle: An application to create and modify views for Visual Query Builder
rms.subtitle_description: Subtitle in the navigation menu for the 'RMS' application: Describes an application used to create and modify views for the Visual Query Builder.
rms.title: Reporting Metadata Studio
rms.title_description: Navigation Item Title in the BMC Helix Dashboards main navigation: Identifies the "Reporting Metadata Studio" application. This studio is used for managing reporting-related metadata.

roles.subtitle: Manage roles across an organization
roles.subtitle_description: Subtitle in the Roles navigation area: Describes the functionality to manage user roles across the entire organization.
roles.title: Roles
roles.title_description: Title in the navigation menu: Labels the section dedicated to managing or viewing user roles and their associated permissions within BMC Helix Dashboards.

error.bhd-00004_description: Error message in an error notification within BMC Helix Dashboards: Informs the user that the application failed to retrieve "Calculated Fields." This typically occurs when the system attempts to load or display these fields during dashboard configuration or data operations.
error.bhd-00005_description: Error message in a notification area or pop-up within BMC Helix Dashboards: This message informs the user that the system was unable to successfully create a calculated field. It typically appears after a user attempts to define and save a new calculated field.
error.bhd-00006_description: Error message in a notification or toast: Informs the user that the system was unable to successfully delete a calculated field.
error.bhd-00007_description: Error message in a notification: Informs the user that the system was unable to successfully modify (edit or update) a calculated field. This typically appears when attempting to save changes to a field within dashboard or data panel configurations.
error.bhd-00008_description: Error Notification in BMC Helix Dashboards: Informs the user that an operation to update one or more dashboards has failed.
error.bhd-00009: Failed to delete field from Dashboard(s)
error.bhd-00009_description: Error Message in an error notification in BMC Helix Dashboards: Informs the user that an attempt to delete a field from one or more dashboards was unsuccessful. This message appears when a system or user action to remove a field from dashboard configurations or panel settings fails.
error.bhd-00010: bad request payload while creating calculated field
error.bhd-00010_description: Error message in a notification banner/pop-up within BMC Helix Dashboards: This message informs the user that the request to create a calculated field failed because the data payload sent to the server was invalid or malformed.
error.bhd-00011: bad request payload while deleting calculated field
error.bhd-00011_description: Error Message in BMC Helix Dashboards UI: Informs the user that an attempt to delete a calculated field failed because the system received an invalid or malformed request payload. This indicates a technical issue with the data sent during the deletion operation.
error.bhd-00012: bad request payload while modifying calculated field
error.bhd-00012_description: Error message in BMC Helix Dashboards: This notification appears when a user attempts to modify a calculated field, but the data sent to the server is malformed or invalid, preventing the update.
error.bhd-00013: Calculated field name is already taken. Please provide a different name.
error.bhd-00102: bad request data while setting IMS user info
error.bhd-00102_description: Error message in BMC Helix Dashboards: This message informs the user that the request to set IMS user information failed because the data provided was invalid or malformed. It indicates an issue with the format or content of the input when attempting to configure IMS user settings.
error.bhd-00103: bad request data while updating user preferences
error.bhd-00103_description: Error message in BMC Helix Dashboards notification: Informs the user that the data provided to update their preferences was invalid or malformed. This typically prevents the preferences from being saved successfully.
error.bhd-00104: bad request data while updating org. preferences
error.bhd-00104_description: Error message in an error notification: Informs the user that the data submitted to update organizational preferences was invalid or malformed, preventing the update.
error.bhd-00105_description: Error notification in BMC Helix Dashboards: This message is displayed when the system fails to parse or interpret the content (body) of a request, response, or other data structure. This typically indicates malformed or unexpected data format.
error.bhd-00106_description: Error message in User Preferences/Settings: Informs the user that the selected or entered UI theme is invalid when attempting to update their personal display preferences. This typically appears as a notification when a user tries to save an unsupported theme.
error.bhd-00107_description: Error message in an error notification: Informs the user that the requested dashboard could not be found or loaded.
error.bhd-00108_description: Error message in the system notification area: Notifies the user that an internal server error has occurred. This typically indicates a backend issue that prevents the requested operation from completing.
error.bhd-00109_description: Notification in BMC Helix Dashboards: This error message is displayed when the system fails to retrieve user or application preferences. It indicates a problem accessing stored settings.
error.bhd-00110_description: Error message in a notification area in BMC Helix Dashboards: Informs the user that an attempt to save their preferences (e.g., user settings, dashboard settings) was unsuccessful. This message appears after the user initiates a save action for preferences.
error.bhd-00111_description: Error message displayed as a notification in BMC Helix Dashboards: This message informs the user that their attempt to update personal settings or preferences could not be successfully saved. It typically appears due to a backend or network issue.
error.bhd-00201_description: Error message in an error notification within BMC Helix Dashboards: Informs the user that a submitted or referenced identifier (ID) is invalid, failing validation checks.
error.bhd-00202: bad request data while creating folder
error.bhd-00202_description: Error message in an error notification pop-up: This message informs the user that their attempt to create a folder failed because the data provided in the request was invalid or malformed.
error.bhd-00203: bad request data while updating folder
error.bhd-00203_description: Error notification in BMC Helix Dashboards: Informs the user that the data provided to update a folder was invalid or malformed. This message appears when a user attempts to modify an existing folder with incorrect data.
error.bhd-00204: bad request data when moving folder
error.bhd-00204_description: Error Message in a system notification: This message informs the user that an attempt to move a folder failed because the request contained invalid or malformed data.
error.bhd-00205: bad request data while updating folder permissions
error.bhd-00205_description: Error message in a notification banner or pop-up: Informs the user that the attempt to update folder permissions failed because the data sent in the request was invalid or malformed.
error.bhd-00206: team and user permissions cannot have an associated role
error.bhd-00206_description: Error Message in BMC Helix Dashboards permission management: Informs the user that team and user permissions cannot be assigned or have an associated role. This indicates an invalid configuration or action attempt.
error.bhd-00207: permissions cannot have both a user and team
error.bhd-00207_description: Error message in permission settings within BMC Helix Dashboards: Informs the user that a single permission entry cannot be assigned to both an individual user and a team simultaneously. This enforces a validation rule for permission assignments.
error.bhd-00208_description: Error notification in a folder management interface: Informs the user that the folder title field is mandatory and cannot be left blank when creating or updating a folder.
error.bhd-00209: Dashboard cannot be changed to a folder
error.bhd-00209_description: Error message in Dashboard management UI: Informs the user that an existing dashboard cannot be converted into a folder. This operation is not supported within the system.
error.bhd-00210_description: Error Message in System Notification: Informs the user that a provided Unique Identifier (UID) contains characters that are not permitted. This error typically appears during data entry or when processing an identifier.
error.bhd-00211_description: Error message in an error notification: Informs the user that an entered Unique Identifier (UID) exceeds the maximum allowed length of 40 characters.
