optimize.no-domain-selected_description: Status message in the Optimize Variables section: Informs the user that a domain selection is missing or required for the optimization feature.
optimize.no-items_description: Message in the 'Optimize' section of the 'Variables' configuration: Informs the user that no items are currently available for selection or optimization.
optimize.remove-item_description: Button in the Variables Optimization management screen: Enables users to remove a selected variable or configuration item from the optimization settings.
optimize.search_description: Search button or input field label in the Optimize Variables section: Allows users to find specific variables or related optimization settings.
optimize.selected_description: Label in the 'Optimize' section of 'Variables' within BMC Helix Dashboards: Indicates that a specific optimization variable or option has been chosen or is currently active.
optimize.selected-domains_description: Label in the BMC Helix Dashboards 'Optimize Variables' section: It indicates the domains that have been chosen or are available for selection for optimization within the variable settings.
optimize.type-to-search_description: Placeholder text in a search input field within the 'Optimize' section of variable management: prompts users to type to search for or filter available variables or options.

query-editor.continue_description: Button in the variable query editor: Allows the user to proceed to the next step or confirm the current configuration for a variable definition.
query-editor.enable-editor_description: Toggle in the variable query editor settings: This control allows users to activate or deactivate the dedicated interface for writing queries to define variables. It determines the visibility and usability of the query editor.
query-editor.enable-variable-caching_description: Option in the variable query editor: Controls whether caching is enabled for variable values. This feature can improve dashboard performance by storing frequently accessed variable data.
query-editor.modal-close-confirmation: The current query will be lost. Do you want to continue?
query-editor.modal-close-confirmation_description: Confirmation message in the Query Editor modal for Variables: Informs the user that closing the editor will discard the current, unsaved query, and asks for confirmation to proceed.
query-editor.modal-close-title_description: Modal Title in the Query Editor for Variables: Appears in a confirmation dialog when a user attempts to close the editor after making unsaved modifications. This title alerts the user to their pending changes.
query-editor.query-text_description: Label in the Query Editor for variables: Identifies the input field where users enter the text string for a data source query.
query-editor.service-management-note: Note: Applicable only to the Service Management query type.
query-editor.service-management-note_description: Informational text in the query editor for variables in BMC Helix Dashboards: This note clarifies that the feature or setting it accompanies is only applicable when the 'Service Management' query type has been selected.

variable-caching.dependant-error: Caching for time range dependent variables not allowed
variable-caching.dependant-error_description: Error message in the Variable Caching section of the Query Editor: Informs the user that caching cannot be enabled for variables whose values depend on the dashboard's time range. This prevents stale or incorrect data from being displayed.
variable-caching.modal-close-title_description: Modal title in the 'Variable Caching' settings modal within the Query Editor for dashboard variables: This title identifies the modal where users can configure caching options for dashboard variables.
variable-caching.modal-disable-confirmation: Do you want to disable caching for the variable?
variable-caching.modal-disable-confirmation_description: Confirmation message in the variable caching settings of the query editor: Prompts the user to confirm their intention to disable caching for a specific dashboard variable. This message appears in a modal dialog before the action is taken.
variable-caching.modal-enable-additional-text-1: Variable cache will be cleared for all users if you edit the query
variable-caching.modal-enable-additional-text-1_descripti: Informative text in a variable caching configuration modal within the Variable Query Editor: This text informs the user that editing a variable's query will cause the variable cache to be cleared for all users. It serves as a warning regarding the impact of query modifications on cached variable data.
variable-caching.modal-enable-confirmation: Do you want to enable caching for the variable?
variable-caching.modal-enable-confirmation_description: Confirmation modal in the variable caching settings within the query editor: Asks the user to confirm if they want to enable caching for a specific variable.
variable-caching.service-management-error: Caching is supported only for Service Management queries
variable-caching.service-management-error_description: Error message in the variable query editor's caching settings: Informs the user about a limitation when configuring caching for a variable. It specifies that caching is only supported for Service Management queries.
variable-caching.time-dependant-warning: Caching is not allowed for variables which are time dependant.
variable-caching.time-dependant-warning_description: Warning message in the variable caching settings within the query editor: This message informs the user that caching is automatically disabled or cannot be enabled for variables whose values are time-dependent, as caching would prevent them from updating correctly.

variables.refresh-aria_description: Accessible label for a button in the Variables section: Allows users to refresh a specific variable. This label is used for screen readers to describe the refresh action.
variables.refresh-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the Variables section: Explains the function of an associated UI element (e.g., a button or icon) to refresh a specific variable's data or values.
variables.refresh-variable-aria_description: ARIA label for an interactive element in the variables management area: Provides an accessibility description for a control that refreshes a specific dashboard variable. The `{{name}}` placeholder dynamically indicates which variable will be refreshed.

variable-caching.cache-delete-success_description: Success message in the variable management UI: Confirms that the cache for a specific variable has been successfully deleted. The `{{variableName}}` token is dynamically replaced with the actual variable's name.

welcome.dashboards_description: Link in the Welcome section: Navigates users to their personalized collection of data dashboards. This serves as a primary entry point for viewing data visualizations within BMC Helix Dashboards.

dashboard.panel-copied: Panel copied. Click **Add panel** icon to paste.

picker.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the dashboard picker: Guides the user to select a dashboard.

dashboard-import.datasource-validation_description: Validation message in the dashboard import process: It notifies the user that a data source must be selected or specified to proceed with the dashboard import.
dashboard-import.name-validation-text_description: Validation message in the dashboard import form: Informs the user that a name must be provided for the dashboard being imported. This message appears when the required 'Name' field is left empty.
dashboard-import.needs-value_description: Validation message in the Dashboard Import feature: This message is displayed when a user attempts to proceed without providing a value for a mandatory field during the dashboard import process.
dashboard-import.select-view_description: Label for a selection control in the dashboard import process: Prompts the user to choose a specific view or configuration for the dashboard being imported. This allows the user to define how the dashboard data or display will be structured post-import.
dashboard-import.view-selection-tooltip: Do not change the pre-selected view
dashboard-import.view-selection-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the Dashboard Import feature: Advises the user against changing a view that has been automatically pre-selected by the system. This instruction ensures the recommended configuration is maintained.
dashboard-import.view-selection-validation_description: Validation message in the Dashboard Import feature: Informs the user that a view selection is mandatory and must be made to proceed with the dashboard import process.

dashboard-preview-banner.not-yet-saved: The value is not saved in the BMC Helix Dashboard database
dashboard-preview-banner.not-yet-saved_description: Status message in the dashboard preview banner: Informs the user that the current value or state of the dashboard is not yet saved to the BMC Helix Dashboard database. This message appears when changes have been made but not explicitly saved.

help-wizard.troubleshooting-request-help: To request troubleshooting help, send a snapshot of this panel to BMC Helix Dashboards Technical Support. The snapshot contains query response data and panel settings.

save-provisioned-dashboard-form.cannot-be-saved: This dashboard cannot be saved from the BMC Helix Dashboards UI because it has been provisioned from another source. Copy the JSON or save it to a file below, then you can update your dashboard in the provisioning source.

edit-panel.actual_description: Label in the Edit Panel screen: Identifies a data series, field, or value that represents the actual, real-world, or observed performance/status. This is typically used in comparison with planned, target, or forecast values.
edit-panel.actual-description: Make same size as on the dashboard
edit-panel.actual-description_description: Option in the Edit Panel screen: Applies the panel's dimensions as they appear on the dashboard. This synchronizes the panel's size in the editor with its display on the dashboard.

grid-lines.auto-text_description: Text label in the grid lines settings for a dashboard panel's axis: This option allows for automatic configuration or display of grid lines.

edit-panel.discard_description: Button in the Edit Panel screen: Allows users to discard or cancel unsaved changes made to a dashboard panel.
edit-panel.fill_description: Setting label in the panel editing interface in BMC Helix Dashboards: This option determines how visual elements, such as areas in a graph or segments of a gauge, are filled with color or pattern.
edit-panel.fill-description_description: A checkbox or setting in the 'Edit Panel' section of a dashboard: This controls whether the panel's content (e.g., a visualization or text) expands to fill all available space within its container.

link-editor.url-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Link Editor when configuring panel options: It displays an example URL format, guiding users on how to link to a Grafana resource like a dashboard or its annotations.

query.empty-query-edit-warning: An empty query name is not allowed
query.empty-query-edit-warning_description: Warning message in the Query configuration section of the Edit Panel view in BMC Helix Dashboards: Displayed to indicate that the query name field is mandatory and cannot be left blank.
query.query-already-exist-warning_description: Warning message in the Query editor within the Edit Panel section of BMC Helix Dashboards: Informs the user that the query name they are attempting to use already exists. This prevents duplicate query names within a panel.

query-options.min-interval-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the 'Min interval' input field within the Query Options section when editing a dashboard panel. It indicates that no minimum time interval is set or enforced for data aggregation or sampling.

save-dashboard.error_description: Error message in the Save Dashboard functionality: Indicates that an attempt to save a dashboard has failed.

errors.dash-folder-same-name: Dashboard name cannot be the same as folder name
errors.dash-folder-same-name_description: Error message in the Save Dashboard dialog: Informs the user that the entered dashboard name cannot be identical to the name of the folder where it's being saved. This prevents naming conflicts and ensures unique identification.
errors.dash-name-invalid_description: Error message in the Save Dashboard dialog: Informs the user that the dashboard name they entered is invalid. This typically happens due to invalid characters, length restrictions, or a conflict with an existing dashboard name.

edit-form.new-annotation_description: Title in the Annotation Edit Form within Dashboard Settings: Labels the form or dialog for creating a new annotation.

links.description-edit-link: Edit a specific link of your dashboard
links.description-edit-link_description: Link in the Dashboard Settings > Links section: This link allows the user to modify the details and properties of a specific link associated with the dashboard.
links.description-new-link: Create a new link on your dashboard
links.description-new-link_description: Descriptive text in the 'Links' section of the Dashboard Settings: Explains the function of creating a new link that will be displayed on the dashboard.
links.title-edit-link_description: Title of a dialog/modal in the Dashboard Settings 'Links' section: This title appears when the user is configuring or modifying an existing link's properties.
links.title-new-link_description: Title in the Links section of the Dashboard Settings: Identifies the section or action for creating a new link configuration, typically for connecting to other dashboards or external URLs.

editor.exlude-variable_description: Option label in the Variable Editor for Dashboard Settings: Allows users to exclude the variable being configured. This typically prevents the variable from being active or displayed on the dashboard.
editor.exlude-variable-desc: Select to exclude the variable from the query
editor.exlude-variable-desc_description: **Checkbox/Toggle option description** in the **Variable Editor section of Dashboard Settings**: This option allows users to exclude a defined dashboard variable from the data queries. It enables testing or temporarily disabling a variable's influence on data retrieval without removing its definition.

ad-hoc-filters.name: Ad hoc filters
ad-hoc-filters.name_description: Selection option in the Dashboard variables editor: This identifies "Ad hoc filters" as a variable type, which allows users to dynamically add filters to dashboard panels.

constant.description: Define a hidden constant variable, useful for metric prefixes in dashboards you want to share.
constant.description_description: Description text for the 'Constant' variable type option in the BMC Helix Dashboards variable editor: Explains how to define a hidden constant variable. It specifies its utility for metric prefixes in dashboards meant for sharing.
constant.name: Constant
constant.name_description: Option label in the variable type selection dropdown within the Dashboard Variable Editor settings: It represents the "Constant" variable type, used for defining fixed, unchangeable values.

custom.name: Custom
custom.name_description: Option in the variable type selection dropdown in the BMC Helix Dashboards variable editor: This option allows users to designate a variable as a custom type.

data-source.description: Enables you to dynamically switch the data source for multiple panels.
data-source.description_description: Help text for the 'Data source' variable type option in the Dashboard Variables editor: It explains the functionality of this variable, enabling dynamic switching of data sources for multiple panels.
data-source.name: Data source
data-source.name_description: Option name in the dashboard variable editor, specifically when selecting a variable type. This option allows users to define a variable whose values are populated from a selected data source.

interval.name: Interval
interval.name_description: Option in the variable type selection within the BMC Helix Dashboards variable editor: This is a selectable variable type that allows users to define time intervals for dashboard data, such as aggregation periods or refresh rates.

query.name: Query
query.name_description: Selection option in the variable editor settings for BMC Helix Dashboards, under the 'Select Variable Type' section: This option allows users to define a variable whose values are fetched from a data source query.

textbox.description: Define a textbox variable, where users can enter any arbitrary string
textbox.description_description: Description in the dashboard variable editor's 'Select Variable Type' section for the 'Textbox' option: It explains that a textbox variable allows users to enter any arbitrary string.
textbox.name: Text box
textbox.name_description: Name of a variable type in the 'Select variable type' section of the variable editor in Dashboard settings: This option allows users to define a variable that accepts free-form text input.

ad-hoc.data-source-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the ad-hoc variable type editor for dashboard settings: Instructs the user to select a data source for the ad-hoc variable definition.

date-range.title: Select Time Range
date-range.title_description: Title in the BMC Helix Dashboards variable editor: This text labels the section where users define or select a time range for a 'date-range' type variable.

refresh-options.on-dash-load_description: Option in the Refresh Options section of the Query Variable Editor within Dashboard Settings: This option dictates that the variable's values are automatically refreshed whenever the dashboard loads.
refresh-options.on-time-change_description: Refresh option in the variable editor for a Query variable type in BMC Helix Dashboards settings: This option configures the variable to refresh its query and update its values whenever the dashboard's global time range is changed.

loading-msgs.changes_description: Loading message in Dashboard Settings, Versions section: Informs the user that the system is retrieving historical changes or updates related to dashboard versions. This message is displayed while the data is being fetched.
loading-msgs.history-list_description: Loading message in the Dashboard Settings > Versions section: Informs the user that the system is actively retrieving the list of historical versions for a dashboard.
loading-msgs.more-entries_description: Loading message in the 'Versions' section of 'Dashboard Settings': Informs the user that additional entries are being retrieved. This message appears during an ongoing process of loading more data, such as historical dashboard versions.

configuration.sort-asc_description: Button in the Data Source Configuration screen: Sorts a list of items or entries alphabetically in ascending order.
configuration.sort-desc_description: Sorting option in the data source configuration screen: This allows users to arrange a displayed list of items in descending alphabetical order (Z to A).

explore.no-data_description: Status message in the Explore feature of BMC Helix Dashboards: Informs the user that no data was found after executing a query or for the current visualization.
explore.query-added-announcement_description: Announcement message in the Explore view: This notification appears after a user adds a new query row, confirming the successful action. The `{{queryCount}}` variable provides the current total number of query rows.

rich-history-settings-tab.alert-info: BMC Helix Dashboards will keep entries up to {{optionLabel}}.Starred entries won't be deleted.

to-dashboard.panel-to-dashboard_description: Button in the Explore view: Allows users to add the currently configured panel or visualization from the Explore session to a dashboard.
to-dashboard.to-existing-dashboard-title_description: **Dialog Title** in the **Explore section of BMC Helix Dashboards, within the 'Add to Dashboard' workflow**: This title indicates that the user is about to add a panel to an existing dashboard.
to-dashboard.to-new-dashboard-title_description: Title in the Explore feature, within the workflow for saving a panel to a new dashboard: This title indicates that the user is about to add the current panel to a newly created dashboard.

file-drop-zone.accepted-file-types-prefix_description: Label in the file drop zone component: This text introduces or indicates the specific types of files that are allowed to be uploaded or processed by the system. It typically acts as a heading or a prefix to a list of file extensions.
file-drop-zone.file-invalid-type-prefix: File type must be one of
file-drop-zone.file-invalid-type-prefix_description: Error message prefix in a file drop zone: This text introduces a validation error indicating that the uploaded file's type is not permitted. It is followed by a list of acceptable file types.
file-drop-zone.max-file-size_description: Label in a file drop zone component within BMC Helix Dashboards: This label indicates the maximum size allowed for files that users can upload or drop into this area.

timezone.browser-time_description: Option label in the timezone settings or time display configuration: Indicates that data times should be displayed using the user's local browser's time zone.
timezone.coord-universal-time_description: Option in the timezone selection settings within BMC Helix Dashboards: Represents the Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) as a selectable global time reference.
timezone.local-browser-time_description: Option in Timezone settings within BMC Helix Dashboards (Grafana-based): Allows users to display timestamps and time-series data using the time zone configured on their local web browser.

formats.european_description: Value format option in the Grafana data settings under 'Miscellaneous' formats: This option allows users to display data using a European format. It is deprecated, meaning its use is no longer recommended.

date-time-pickers.time-range-input-text_description: Text label for an interactive input element in the date/time picker component: Prompts the user to select a specific time range for data filtering or visualization.

fieldValidationMessage.warning_description: Warning" label in field validation messages: It indicates a non-critical issue or potential problem with user input that requires attention.

file-dropzone.bulk-limit-error_description: Error message in the file dropzone: Informs the user about the maximum number of dashboards that can be selected or imported in a single operation.

icon.information_description: Information icon in the Grafana UI: Used to indicate the presence of supplementary details, help text, or explanatory content. Clicking or hovering often reveals this additional information.

inlineLabel.info_description: Label in an inline label component within the BMC Helix Dashboards UI: Indicates that the accompanying element or section provides informational details.

table.filter-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a filter input field within a table: Prompts users to enter text to filter the displayed data.

week-start-picker.weekStarts-label-browser_description: Label in the Week Start Picker component: This option allows users to configure the first day of the week to follow their web browser's locale settings.

import.view-label_description: Label in the import section of BMC Helix Dashboards: This label identifies a specific view during an import operation. The `{{index}}` token will be replaced by a dynamic identifier for that view.

delete.cannot-delete-text: This library panel can not be deleted because it is connected to {{message}} dashboard(s). Remove the library panel from the dashboards listed below and retry.

modal.select-aria-label: Start typing to search for dashboard
modal.select-aria-label_description: Search input field in a modal dialog for Library Panels: This ARIA label instructs users to start typing to search for available dashboards or panels.

manage-dashboards.specify-constant_description: Instruction text in the dashboard management interface: Guides the user to enter or define a fixed text value that acts as a constant, likely when configuring a dashboard variable or parameter.

config-general.subtitle: Manage default preferences and settings across BMC Helix Dashboards
config-general.subtitle_description: Subtitle in the "General Configuration" section within the BMC Helix Dashboards navigation: Indicates the area for managing default preferences and settings across the platform.

empty-state.description: Notifications you have received will appear here
empty-state.description_description: Empty state message in the Notifications section: Informs the user that received notifications will appear in this area once available. This text is displayed when the list of notifications is currently empty.
empty-state.title: You're all caught up!
empty-state.title_description: Title in the notifications empty state: This text informs the user that there are no new or pending notifications. It appears when the notification list is empty.

dismissed.multiple_one_description: Status message in the Live Notifications section: Displays a confirmation that a specific number of notifications have been dismissed. This specific key (`multiple_one`) is used when `{{count}}` is 1.
dismissed.multiple_other_description: Status message in the Live Notifications panel: Informs the user that a specific number of notifications have been dismissed. This message appears after a bulk dismiss action.
dismissed.single_description: Confirmation message in the Live Notifications section: Informs the user that exactly one notification has been successfully dismissed. This message is displayed after a user action to dismiss a single live notification.

stored-notifications.select-all_description: Selection control in the Stored Notifications section: Allows users to quickly select all listed notifications. This facilitates performing bulk actions on multiple notifications.

standard-field-condigs.category-data-links_description: Category in Standard Field Configurations: Groups settings for defining interactive links that connect dashboard data to external resources or detailed views.

page-loader.text_description: Status message in the page-loader component: Informs the user that content or data is currently being fetched or processed before displaying the main page content.

panel-message.refresh-dashboard_description: Message in a dashboard panel: Informs the user that refreshing the dashboard is necessary to retrieve the latest data. This message typically appears when data is stale or not yet loaded.

browse.subtitle: {{value}} Extend the Helix Dashboards experience with panel plugins and apps. To find more data sources go to <3>Connections</3>.

search-inputs.name-type_description: Placeholder text for a search input field in the `search-inputs` section of BMC Helix Dashboards: Guides users to search for items by their name or type. The Spanish translation uses 'email' instead of 'type', suggesting a potential context-specific meaning for 'type' or a localization choice.

team-permissions.add-member_description: Button in the Team Permissions page: Allows users to add a new member to the selected team.
team-permissions.no-members-message: There are no members in this team or you do not have the permissions to list the current members.
team-permissions.no-members-message_description: Informational message in the Team Permissions screen: Explains why the list of team members is empty, either because no members are assigned or the current user lacks viewing permissions.

headers.last-active-text_description: Column header in the "Users and Access" section: Displays the timestamp of a user's most recent activity or login. This helps administrators monitor user engagement.
headers.login-text_description: **Button/Link text** in the **Users and Access section's header**: Initiates or navigates users to the login process for account access.
headers.members-text_description: Header text in the Users and Access section: This label introduces a list or table of individuals or users who are members of an organization or team.

users-and-access.search-user-placeholder-text: Search user by login, email or name
users-and-access.search-user-placeholder-text_description: Placeholder text for a search field in the Users and Access management section of BMC Helix Dashboards: It instructs users on how to search for user accounts by their login, email address, or name.

empty.message: It looks like you haven’t created any bookmarks yet
empty.message_description: Empty state message in the Bookmarks page: Informs the user that they have not yet created any bookmarks. This message appears when the bookmarks list is empty.
empty.tip: Hover over any item in the nav menu and click on the bookmark icon to add it here.
empty.tip_description: Tip in the empty state of the Bookmarks page: Instructs users on how to add new bookmarks. It advises hovering over navigation items and clicking the bookmark icon to populate the page.

bouncing-loader.label: Loading
bouncing-loader.label_description: Text label in a bouncing loader component: Informs the user that content or data is currently being loaded or an operation is in progress.

action.bulk-delete-provisioned-resources_descri: Action in the Dashboards browsing interface: Allows users to perform a bulk deletion of multiple *provisioned* resources (e.g., dashboards, folders, data sources) that were created via configuration files rather than manually.
action.bulk-move-provisioned-resources_descript: **Menu item** in **the action menu of the dashboard browsing interface**: This option allows users to move multiple provisioned resources (e.g., dashboards or folders) simultaneously.
action.delete-modal-invalid-text: One or more folders contain library panels or alert rules. Delete these first in order to proceed.
action.delete-modal-invalid-title_description: Modal Title in the Delete Confirmation modal within the Browse Dashboards view: This title indicates that the selected folder cannot be deleted due to an invalid condition.
action.delete-modal-restore-dashboards-text: This action will delete the selected folders immediately. Deleted dashboards will be kept in the history for up to 12 months and can be restored by your organization administrator during that time. The history is limited to 1000 dashboards — older ones may be removed sooner if the limit is reached. Folders cannot be restored.
action.delete-modal-text: This action will delete the following content:
action.delete-modal-text_description: Confirmation message in the Delete confirmation modal on the Browse Dashboards screen: Informs the user that the action will delete specific content, typically followed by a list of items to be removed.
action.delete-provisioned-folder_description: Action in the dashboard browsing interface: Deletes a folder that has been provisioned. This typically refers to folders created via configuration files or APIs rather than manually.
action.manage-permissions-button_description: Button in the Browse Dashboards interface: Allows users to manage permissions for dashboards.
action.move-button_description: Button in the 'Browse Dashboards' action section: Initiates the process of relocating a selected dashboard or folder to a different location within the dashboard hierarchy.
action.move-modal-alert: Moving this item may change its permissions.
action.move-modal-alert_description: Alert message in a move confirmation modal within the 'Browse Dashboards' section: This message warns the user that moving the selected item (dashboard or folder) might change its associated permissions, prompting awareness before proceeding.
action.move-modal-field-label_description: Field Label in the 'Move Dashboard' modal: Labels the input field where the user specifies the name of the destination folder for moving a dashboard. This field is part of the dashboard browsing and management interface.
action.move-modal-text: This action will move the following content:
action.move-modal-text_description: Introductory text in a confirmation modal within the 'Browse Dashboards' section: It informs the user about the content that will be moved. This text precedes a list of the specific items affected by the move action.
action.move-modal-title_description: Modal Title in the 'Move' action modal in the Browse Dashboards section: It indicates that the user is about to relocate a dashboard or folder.
action.move-provisioned-folder_description: Action in Browse Dashboards section: Facilitates the relocation of a provisioned folder. This action is specifically for folders managed through provisioning processes.
action.new-folder-name-required-phrase_descript: Validation message in the 'Create New Folder' dialog within the Browse Dashboards section: Informs the user that the folder name field is mandatory. It appears if the user attempts to create a new folder without providing a name.
action.selected-mix-resources-modal-text: You have selected both provisioned and non-provisioned resources. These cannot be processed together. Please select only provisioned resources or only non-provisioned resources and try again.
action.selected-mix-resources-modal-title_descr: Modal title in the Browse Dashboards section: This title informs the user that they have selected multiple, potentially incompatible, resource types. It likely appears when an action has been initiated that requires a specific or compatible set of resource types.

actions.button-to-recently-deleted_description: Button in the actions menu on the Browse Dashboards page: Allows users to view recently deleted dashboards.

browse-folder-alerting-page.title-folder-not-found_description: Title in the 'Browse Folder Alerting' page: Informs the user that the requested folder could not be found. This message is displayed when a user attempts to access a non-existent or deleted folder.
browse-folder-alerting-page.title-ruler-namespace-error_description: Error message displayed as a title or prominent alert on the Alerting page within a dashboard folder. It informs the user that the system failed to load the associated alerting rules. The key path element 'ruler' should be interpreted as 'rule'.

browse-view.this-folder-is-empty_description: Status message in the 'Browse Dashboards' view when a folder is selected: Informs the user that the currently viewed folder contains no dashboards or subfolders.

bulk-delete-resources-form.delete-warning: This will delete selected folders and their descendants. In total, this will affect:
bulk-delete-resources-form.delete-warning_description: Warning message in the Bulk Delete Resources form in the Browse Dashboards section: It warns the user that selected folders and all their contents (descendants) will be permanently deleted. This message precedes a summary detailing the total number of items affected by the deletion.
bulk-delete-resources-form.error-deleting-resources_description: Error message in the bulk delete resources form within the Dashboards browser: This message indicates that an attempt to delete multiple selected resources has failed.

bulk-move-resources-form.button-move_description: Action button in the Bulk Move Resources form: Confirms and executes the action of moving selected resources to a new location. This form is accessed from the Browse Dashboards section.

error.read-only-message: If you have direct access to the target, please make modifications directly in the target repository.
error.read-only-saving-message: Repository is read-only and provisioned in git. {{readOnlyMessage}}
error.read-only-saving-message_description: Error message in the Bulk Move Resources form: Informs the user that the desired bulk move or save operation cannot be completed. This occurs because the target repository is in a read-only state, as it is provisioned through Git.
error.read-only-title_description: Error message title in the Bulk Move Resources form (within Dashboards): This title indicates that the user cannot complete the bulk move operation because the selected destination repository is read-only.
error.repository-not-found-message: The repository for the selected folder could not be found. Please ensure that the folder is provisioned correctly.
error.repository-not-found-title_description: Error title in the 'Bulk Move Resources' form on the Dashboards browsing page: This error informs the user that a specified repository, either as a source or destination for the bulk move operation, could not be found or accessed by the system.

bulk-move-resources-form.error-moving-resources_description: Error message in the Bulk Move Resources form within the Browse Dashboards section: Informs the user that the bulk movement of selected resources could not be completed.
bulk-move-resources-form.error-no-target-folder-path: Target folder path is invalid or empty, please select again.
bulk-move-resources-form.error-no-target-folder-path_description: Error message in the Bulk Move Resources form: Informs the user that the chosen target folder path for moving dashboards or other resources is either invalid or was not selected, prompting them to reselect a valid path.
bulk-move-resources-form.move-total_description: Informative text in the Bulk Move Resources Form: It introduces a summary displaying the total number of resources that will be affected by the bulk move operation.
bulk-move-resources-form.move-warning: This will move selected folders and their descendants. Available target folders depend on the selected resources.
bulk-move-resources-form.move-warning-tooltip: You can only move provisioned resources within their provisioned folder, and local resources to local folders.
bulk-move-resources-form.target-folder_description: Label for a selection field in the Bulk Move Resources form: Indicates where the selected dashboards or other resources will be moved. Users choose the destination folder here.

counts.alertRule_one_description: Text label in a count summary section on the Browse Dashboards page: Displays the singular count of alert rules. This specific string is used when there is exactly one alert rule.
counts.alertRule_other_description: Count display in the Counts section on the Browse Dashboards page: Shows the total number of alert rules available. The `{{count}}` placeholder will be dynamically replaced by the number, and this string is typically used for plural forms.
counts.dashboard_one_description: Text label in the Browse Dashboards section: Displays the total number of dashboards when there is exactly one dashboard. This string is specifically for the singular count.
counts.dashboard_other_description: Count display on the Browse Dashboards page: Shows the total number of dashboards. This string is used for plural (including zero) counts, as opposed to a singular count.
counts.folder_one_description: Count Label in the Browse Dashboards screen: Displays the singular count of folders. This string is used when the count is exactly one.
counts.folder_other_description: Display text in the dashboard browsing page's counts section: Indicates the total number of folders. This specific string is used for plural counts (when the folder count is greater than one).
counts.libraryPanel_one_description: Count label in the Dashboard browsing or management interface: Indicates the singular count of available library panels. This string is used specifically when the `{{count}}` placeholder value is one.
counts.libraryPanel_other_description: Count of library panels in the Browse Dashboards page: This string indicates the total number of library panels available to the user. The `{{count}}` placeholder is replaced by the actual numerical value.
counts.total_one_description: Count label in the "Counts" section on the "Browse Dashboards" page: Displays the singular total count of a specific item. This string is used when the count is exactly '1'.
counts.total_other_description: Count of items in the counts section of the Browse Dashboards page: Displays the total number of items. This specific count is likely used for a general or miscellaneous category of items.

checkbox.disabled-not-in-same-repo: This item is not in the same repository as the selected items.
checkbox.disabled-not-in-same-repo_description: **Checkbox** in **the Dashboards Tree (Browse Dashboards)**: Explains why a dashboard or folder checkbox is disabled. This message indicates that the item cannot be selected because it resides in a different repository than other items already chosen, enforcing a single-repository selection rule.

dashboards-tree.collapse-folder-button_description: Button in the Dashboards Tree component of the Browse Dashboards section: Collapses a specific dashboard folder, with `{{title}}` dynamically displaying the folder's name.
dashboards-tree.expand-folder-button_description: Button in the dashboard tree in the Browse Dashboards section: Expands a folder to show its contents. The `{{title}}` placeholder displays the name of the folder.
dashboards-tree.select-all-header-checkbox_description: Header checkbox in the Dashboards Tree (Browse Dashboards): Toggles the selection of all displayed dashboards or nodes within the tree view.
dashboards-tree.select-checkbox_description: Accessible label/tooltip for a checkbox in the Dashboards Tree within the "Browse Dashboards" view: It provides a descriptive name for a checkbox that allows users to select an individual dashboard or folder listed in the tree.
dashboards-tree.tags-column_description: Column header in the Dashboards tree view within the Browse Dashboards section: Indicates the column displaying tags associated with dashboards.

delete-folder.read-only-message: To delete this folder, please remove the folder from your repository.
delete-folder.read-only-message_description: Informational message in the Delete Folder dialog: Informs the user that a folder cannot be deleted directly from the UI as it is read-only and managed by an external repository. It instructs the user to remove the folder from their repository.

delete-provisioned-folder-form.api-error_description: Error message in the Delete Provisioned Folder form within the Browse Dashboards section: Informs the user that the attempt to delete a folder has failed. This message is displayed when an API error prevents the deletion from completing successfully.
delete-provisioned-folder-form.commit_description: Button in the 'Delete Provisioned Folder' confirmation dialog: Confirms and executes the deletion of a selected provisioned dashboard folder. This action is irreversible.
delete-provisioned-folder-form.delete-warning: This will delete this folder and all its descendants. In total, this will affect:
delete-provisioned-folder-form.delete-warning_description: A warning message within the 'Delete Provisioned Folder' confirmation form in BMC Helix Dashboards: It details the destructive impact of deleting a provisioned folder, indicating that the folder and all its contents (descendants) will be removed. This message precedes a summary of the affected items.
delete-provisioned-folder-form.success-message_description: Success message in the Delete Provisioned Folder form within the Browse Dashboards section: Confirms to the user that the selected folder was successfully deleted.

descendant-count.title-unable-to-retrieve-descendant-info: Error message in the Descendant Count section of the Browse Dashboards interface: Indicates that the system was unable to retrieve information about child dashboards or other hierarchical items linked to a dashboard. This message appears when the data cannot be fetched due to an issue.

empty-state.button-title_description: Button label in the empty state of the Browse Dashboards page: It prompts users to create a new dashboard when no dashboards have been created yet.
empty-state.pro-tip: Add/move dashboards to your folder at <2>Browse dashboards</2>
empty-state.pro-tip_description: Pro Tip in the empty state of a dashboard folder within the 'Browse Dashboards' section: Guides users on how to add or move dashboards into their currently empty folder. It provides a clickable link to the main 'Browse dashboards' page.
empty-state.title: You haven't created any dashboards yet
empty-state.title_description: Title in the empty state of the 'Browse Dashboards' page: Informs the user that they have not created any dashboards yet.
empty-state.title-folder: This folder doesn't have any dashboards yet
empty-state.title-folder_description: Title in the empty state for a folder within the 'Browse Dashboards' section: Informs the user that the selected folder currently contains no dashboards. This message appears when a user navigates into a folder that has not yet had any dashboards saved or moved into it.

folder-actions-button.delete-folder-error: Error deleting folder. Please try again later.
folder-actions-button.folder-actions_description: Label in the 'Browse Dashboards' interface: This designates a control (e.g., button, menu title) which, when activated, presents a list of actions that can be performed on a dashboard folder.
folder-actions-button.manage-permissions_description: Menu item within the 'Folder Actions' button menu on the 'Browse Dashboards' page: Allows users to configure access permissions for the selected dashboard folder.
folder-actions-button.move_description: Menu Item in the actions menu for a folder on the Browse Dashboards page: Allows users to relocate the selected folder to a different location.

folder-picker.accessible-label_description: Accessible label for a folder selection component within the 'Browse Dashboards' interface. It informs screen reader users about the currently selected folder and indicates the component's function to change the selection.
folder-picker.button-label_description: Button in the folder picker within the Browse Dashboards section: Allows the user to select a folder.
folder-picker.clear-selection_description: Button in the folder picker within the Browse Dashboards section: Allows users to clear any currently selected folders, resetting the selection.
folder-picker.empty-message_description: Empty state message in the folder picker within the Browse Dashboards view: Informs the user that no folders exist or could be retrieved. This message is displayed when the list of folders is empty.
folder-picker.error-title_description: Error message title in the folder picker component within the Browse Dashboards section: It informs the user that the system encountered an issue and failed to load the list of folders.
folder-picker.non-folder-item_description: Label in the folder picker within the dashboard browsing UI: This string identifies an item (e.g., a dashboard or alert) that is not a folder. It helps distinguish non-folder items from folder objects in a list or selection context.
folder-picker.root-title_description: Title in the folder picker within the "Browse Dashboards" section: It serves as the root or top-level title for the entire collection of dashboards.
folder-picker.search-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the folder picker component within the dashboard browsing interface: It prompts the user to type and search for specific folders.
folder-picker.unknown-error_description: Error message in the folder picker within the Browse Dashboards section: This message is displayed when an unexpected error occurs during operations related to folders, such as loading or selecting them. It serves as a generic fallback.

manage-folder-nav.alert-rules_description: Navigation link in the dashboard folder management navigation: Provides access to manage alert rules. Users can view, create, or modify alert conditions here.
manage-folder-nav.dashboards_description: Navigation link in the folder management navigation menu within the 'Browse Dashboards' section: It allows users to view or manage their dashboards.
manage-folder-nav.panels_description: Navigation item in the dashboard folder management interface: This item allows users to view or manage the individual panels that compose dashboards. Panels are the visual components displaying data.

name-cell.no-items_description: Status message in the dashboard browsing list: Informs the user that no dashboards are currently available to display, appearing when the list is empty.

new-folder.read-only-message: To create this folder, please add the resource in your repository directly.
new-folder.read-only-message_description: Informational message in the New Folder creation interface within the Browse Dashboards section: It instructs the user on how to create the folder when direct UI creation is not possible. This message indicates that the folder must be created by adding the resource directly to a repository.

new-folder-form.name-label_description: Label in the 'New Folder' form: Prompts the user to enter a name for the new folder being created.

new-provisioned-folder-form.alert-error-creating-folder_description: Alert message in the New Provisioned Folder form: Notifies the user that an error occurred while attempting to create a new provisioned folder.
new-provisioned-folder-form.error-invalid-characters: Folder name contains invalid characters. Only letters, numbers, spaces, underscores, and hyphens are allowed.
new-provisioned-folder-form.error-required_description: Error message in the New Provisioned Folder form within the Browse Dashboards section: Informs the user that the folder name field is mandatory.
new-provisioned-folder-form.folder-name-input-placeholder-enter-fold: Placeholder text in the 'New Provisioned Folder' form: Prompts the user to enter a name for the new folder they are creating.
new-provisioned-folder-form.label-folder-name_description: Label in the New Provisioned Folder Form within the Browse Dashboards section: Prompts the user to enter a name for the new folder being provisioned.
new-provisioned-folder-form.text-pull-request-created: A pull request has been created with changes to this folder:
new-provisioned-folder-form.text-pull-request-created_description: Confirmation message in the New Provisioned Folder form within the Browse Dashboards section: Notifies the user that a pull request has been successfully created to incorporate changes made to the provisioned folder. This message typically precedes details or a link to the newly created pull request, indicating completion of a GitOps-style action.
new-provisioned-folder-form.title-pull-request-created_description: Title in the success screen or dialog displayed after submitting the 'New Provisioned Folder' form: Indicates that a pull request has been successfully created as part of the folder provisioning process. This typically means the new folder configuration has been submitted for review.
new-provisioned-folder-form.title-this-repository-is-read-only_descr: Status message in the 'New Provisioned Folder' form within the 'Browse Dashboards' section: This message indicates that the repository selected for creating a new provisioned folder is read-only, preventing any write operations like folder creation.

no-results.clear_description: Button in the 'Browse Dashboards' section: Appears when a search or filter yields no results. It allows the user to clear all active search queries and filters to view all available dashboards or start a new search.
no-results.text: No results found for your query
no-results.text_description: Informational message in the Browse Dashboards interface: Informs the user that their search query or applied filters did not find any matching dashboards.

restore.all-failed_one_description: Notification message in the Dashboards Restore functionality: Informs the user that the restoration of a selected dashboard has failed. This message is displayed when only one dashboard could not be restored.
restore.all-failed_other_description: Error message in the Restore Dashboards feature in the Browse Dashboards section: Notifies the user when an attempt to restore multiple dashboards has failed, displaying the number of dashboards that could not be restored.
restore.failed-count_one_description: Notification message in the Browse Dashboards > Restore section: Informs the user that a single dashboard restoration attempt has failed. This string is specifically for a count of one.
restore.failed-count_other_description: Status message in the restore process summary within the Browse Dashboards section: Informs the user about the total number of dashboards that could not be successfully restored. This string is used for plural counts.
restore.success_description: Success message in the Browse Dashboards interface: Confirms to the user that the selected dashboards have been successfully restored. This message appears upon completion of a dashboard restore operation.
restore.success-count_one_description: Confirmation message in the Browse Dashboards section: Informs the user that a single dashboard has been successfully restored. This string is displayed when the count of restored dashboards is one.
restore.success-count_other_description: Success message in the Browse Dashboards section: Informs the user about the successful restoration of a specified number of dashboards after a restore operation.

browse-dashboards.text-this-repository-is-read-only: If you have direct access to the target, copy the JSON and paste it there.
browse-dashboards.text-this-repository-is-read-only_descri: Informational text in the Browse Dashboards section: This message provides instructions on how to manually transfer dashboard JSON when the current repository is read-only. It guides users to copy and paste the JSON if they have direct access to the target location.

label.alphabetically-az_description: Label in the Trash Manager section of Browse Dashboards: This text labels a sorting option to display items alphabetically (A-Z).
label.alphabetically-za_description: Sorting option label in the 'Trash' section of 'Browse Dashboards': This option allows users to sort the listed items in reverse alphabetical order (Z-A).
label.deleted-newest_description: Label in the trash state manager within the browse dashboards interface: It represents a sorting option to display deleted dashboards with the most recent deletions appearing first.
label.deleted-oldest_description: Label in the Trash State Manager within the Browse Dashboards section: This label represents a sorting option for viewing deleted dashboards. It orders the deleted items by their deletion date, showing the oldest entries first.

additional-fields-options.label-ignore_description: Option in Additional Fields settings for Candlestick charts: This selection allows users to ignore or exclude specific data fields from the chart's configuration.
additional-fields-options.label-include_description: Label in the Additional Fields Options section for a Candlestick chart: Indicates an option to include specific fields or data points.

candle-style-options.label-candles_description: Label in Candlestick Chart's Candle Style Options: This identifies the standard 'candlestick' visual style for displaying data points. It serves as an option for users to select this chart rendering.
candle-style-options.label-ohlc-bars_description: Label in the 'Candle Style Options' section: This option allows users to choose "OHLC Bars" as the display style for a candlestick chart, representing Open, High, Low, and Close values.

candlestick.category-candlestick_description: Category label in a chart type selection menu: Identifies the "Candlestick" chart type, commonly used for visualizing financial data.

color-strategy-options.label-since-open_description: Label in Candlestick color strategy options: This string labels an option to color candlesticks based on their price performance relative to the opening price of the selected time period. It indicates a comparison point for the color strategy.
color-strategy-options.label-since-prior-close_description: Label for an option in Candlestick chart color strategy options: This option allows users to color candlesticks based on whether the current closing price is higher or lower than the prior period's closing price.

candlestick.description-additional-fields: Use standard timeseries options to configure any fields not mapped above
candlestick.description-additional-fields_descriptio: Informational text in the Candlestick chart configuration panel: Instructs users on configuring additional fields not primarily mapped, by utilizing standard timeseries options.
candlestick.description-close: Value at the end of the period
candlestick.description-close_description: Tooltip description in a candlestick chart: Explains the 'close' value of a candlestick, representing the asset's value at the end of the specified period.
candlestick.description-high_description: Description text in a candlestick chart: Defines the 'High' value shown for a candlestick, which represents the maximum value recorded within the period it covers.
candlestick.description-low_description: Description text for the 'low' value in a Candlestick chart: This text explains that the displayed 'low' value indicates the minimum recorded metric within the period represented by that candlestick.
candlestick.description-open: Value at the start of the period
candlestick.description-open_description: Description text in a Candlestick chart legend or tooltip: Explains the 'open' value of a candlestick, representing the data value at the start of the period.
candlestick.description-volume_description: Description in Candlestick chart: Explains that the 'Volume' metric represents the sample count within the specified period.

mode-options.label-both_description: Option label in the candlestick chart's mode options: This allows users to select a display mode that combines or shows two distinct features or data representations.
mode-options.label-candles_description: Label in Candlestick Chart Mode Options: This option allows users to select "Candles" as the visual representation style for data on a candlestick chart. It is one of several available chart display modes.
mode-options.label-volume_description: A label for an option within the candlestick chart's mode settings: It allows users to control the display of volume data on the chart.

candlestick.name-additional-fields_description: Label in candlestick chart configuration: It identifies a section or option where users can define or select extra data fields to be displayed or utilized within the candlestick chart.
candlestick.name-candle-style_description: Setting label in the Candlestick chart visualization settings: Prompts the user to select the visual style of the candles displayed on the chart.
candlestick.name-color-strategy_description: Setting in Candlestick chart configuration: Defines the method or rule for applying colors to the elements within a candlestick chart. This allows users to customize the visual representation of price movements or other data points.
candlestick.name-down-color_description: Label for a color option in candlestick chart configuration: This allows users to set the color for candlesticks indicating a price decrease. These occur when the closing price is lower than the opening price.
candlestick.name-high_description: Label in a candlestick chart: Indicates the highest price or value achieved within the specified time period for a candlestick. This value is typically shown as the top of the upper wick.
candlestick.name-low_description: Label in a candlestick chart: Denotes the lowest value of a data point within a given period. It typically appears in a legend or tooltip.
candlestick.name-mode_description: Label for a control in Candlestick chart settings: Allows users to select a specific display or calculation mode for the chart.
candlestick.name-open_description: Label in a candlestick chart: Identifies the opening price of a security for a specific period.
candlestick.name-up-color_description: Color selection field in candlestick chart configuration: Defines the color used for candlesticks that represent a positive change (e.g., where the closing price is higher than the opening price).
candlestick.name-volume_description: Label in candlestick chart: Represents the trading volume associated with each candlestick.

apieditor.label-endpoint_description: Label in the API Editor on a canvas: Indicates the field where the user defines the URL or address for an API request. This is typically used to specify the target of an API call.
apieditor.label-header-parameters_description: Label in the API editor section on the canvas: Identifies the section where users can define or view parameters sent in the HTTP header of an API request.
apieditor.label-method_description: Label in the API editor on the canvas: Prompts the user to select or specify the HTTP method (e.g., GET, POST) for an API request.
apieditor.label-payload_description: Label in the API Editor within the Canvas: Identifies the section or field for defining or viewing the data (payload) exchanged in an API request or response.
apieditor.label-query-parameters_description: Label in the API Editor: Identifies the section for configuring parameters that will be included in an API query.

render-test-apibutton.test-api_description: Button in the API Editor on the Canvas: This button allows users to test an API configuration or definition they are currently working on.
render-test-apibutton.title-test-api_description: Button label in the API Editor within the canvas: This button triggers the execution of an API for testing purposes.

button-item.category-button_description: Button label in the canvas editor: This string identifies a generic button component that users can select to add to a dashboard canvas or configure its properties. It represents the basic type of button item available.

label.center_description: Button label on the canvas: This label identifies a button that performs an action to center content, elements, or the view within the canvas area.
label.left_description: Option in the text alignment settings for a button item's label on a canvas: This option allows users to align the label text to the left.
label.right_description: Label on a button item in the canvas: This text indicates a "right" option, typically for alignment or positioning.

button-item.name-align-text_description: Button in a button item on the canvas: This button allows users to adjust the alignment of text within the selected button item.
button-item.name-style_description: Label for a style configuration option within the button item settings on a canvas: Used to identify the controls for modifying the button's visual appearance.
button-item.name-text_description: The string "Text" in a Button Item on the Canvas: This represents the default or placeholder text displayed on a button.
button-item.name-text-color_description: Setting for the text color of a button item on a canvas: Defines the color of the text displayed on the button.
button-item.name-text-size_description: Text size setting in the properties of a button item on a canvas: Allows users to control the font size of the text displayed as the button's label.

placeholder.auto_description: Placeholder text in a button item on a dashboard canvas: This text 'Auto' suggests an automatic or system-determined setting for a button property, such as its size, position, or label.

button-style-editor.label-variant_description: Label in the Button Style Editor within the canvas: Identifies the option to choose a visual variant for the button. This allows users to select different pre-defined styles for the button's appearance.

canvas-context-menu.close-editor_description: Menu item in a context menu on the canvas: Closes the active editor that was opened for a canvas element. This action allows users to dismiss the editor and return to viewing the canvas.
canvas-context-menu.open-editor_description: Menu item in a context menu displayed on a canvas: This option allows the user to open an editor, likely for configuring the canvas itself or an element on it.

add-item-menu-item.label-add-item_description: Menu item in the canvas context menu: Allows users to add new components or elements to the dashboard canvas.

render-menu-items.label-bring-to-front_description: Menu item in the canvas context menu: Moves the currently selected dashboard panel or element to the forefront of the visual stack, ensuring it appears above any overlapping items.
render-menu-items.label-duplicate_description: Menu item label in the canvas context menu: This label indicates the action to create a copy of a selected dashboard element.
render-menu-items.label-send-to-back_description: Menu item in the canvas context menu: This option changes the layering of a selected dashboard element by moving it to the very back, underneath other overlapping elements.

set-background-menu-item.label-set-background_description: Menu item label in the canvas context menu: Allows users to set or change the background of the canvas.

canvas.category-arrow-direction_description: Setting label in the Canvas editor: Specifies the visual direction of an arrow element, which is typically used to represent relationships or flow between categories in a visualization.
canvas.category-background_description: Section label in Canvas settings: Indicates a group of options for customizing the canvas's background.
canvas.category-border_description: Label for a setting in the properties panel for a category on a canvas: This option controls the visual border of the category element.
canvas.category-canvas_description: A UI element label in BMC Helix Dashboards: It identifies the primary design area or a category section dedicated to building and arranging dashboard elements.
canvas.category-color_description: Label in the Canvas's styling options: Indicates the setting for selecting a color for a category. This is used for visual distinction of data categories within dashboard visualizations.
canvas.category-data-links_description: Section heading in the canvas configuration: This label introduces settings for defining data-driven links and interactive actions on dashboard elements.
canvas.category-line-style_description: Label for a selection control in the canvas customization panel: Configures the visual appearance (e.g., solid, dashed, dotted) of lines, especially for category lines within charts or visualizations.
canvas.category-metric-value_description: Label in a dashboard panel on the canvas: This string serves as a label to identify the specific numerical or textual value of a metric being displayed. It helps categorize and present performance data within the dashboard.
canvas.category-radius_description: Field label on the canvas, within category properties: Defines the radius for a visual element representing a data category.
canvas.category-selected-connection_description: Label in the canvas configuration panel: Indicates the data connection that has been chosen or is currently active for a specific category. This text helps users identify the currently linked data source.
canvas.category-selected-element_description: Selected element in canvas: This string serves as a label or title to identify a specific element that has been selected by the user on the dashboard canvas. The `{{element}}` placeholder is dynamically replaced with the name of the selected item.
canvas.category-size_description: Label in Canvas configuration for categories: This label refers to a setting that allows users to adjust the visual size of a category element displayed on the canvas.
canvas.category-tooltip_description: Label in canvas configuration: This string identifies the tooltip feature or configuration option for visual elements on a dashboard canvas.

cloud-item.category-cloud_description: Category label in a cloud-related item on the Canvas: Classifies or identifies the item as belonging to the 'Cloud' category.

label.bottom_description: Label positioning option for a cloud item on a dashboard canvas: Indicates the label should be placed at the bottom of the item.
label.center_description: Option in the label properties for a cloud item on the canvas: Aligns the text of the label horizontally to the center.
label.left_description: Option in horizontal alignment settings of a cloud item's label on the canvas: Positions the label to the left.
label.middle_description: A selection label in the configuration panel for a cloud item on the canvas: It defines the 'middle' option for alignment or position.
label.right_description: Label in alignment or positioning controls for a cloud item's label on a dashboard canvas: Indicates the right-hand side or alignment.
label.top_description: Label on a cloud-related item within a dashboard canvas: This text signifies a "top" position, alignment, or highest-ranked category.

cloud-item.name-align-text_description: Control in cloud item properties on the canvas: Used to adjust the alignment of the cloud item's display name or label. This option specifies how the text for the item's name should be positioned.
cloud-item.name-text_description: Label in a cloud item's property panel on the canvas: Labels an input field where users enter text for the item's name or a text-based property.
cloud-item.name-text-color_description: Control label in Cloud item properties on the dashboard canvas: This label identifies the control used to select the color for the name text of a cloud-related item displayed on the canvas.
cloud-item.name-text-size_description: Setting in cloud item properties on the canvas: Adjusts the font size of the displayed text for the cloud item's name.
cloud-item.name-vertical-align_description: Setting label in cloud item properties on the canvas: Controls the vertical alignment of the cloud item's name.

placeholder.auto_description: Configuration option in the placeholder settings for a Cloud Item on the Canvas: Allows for an automatic or system-determined behavior or value for the placeholder. This could relate to auto-sizing, auto-filling content, or other default behaviors.

direction-options.label-both_description: Option label in the connection direction settings on the canvas: Specifies that a connection is bidirectional.
direction-options.label-forward_description: **Option label** in the **direction options for a connection on a canvas**: This label allows users to specify a forward flow or orientation for the selected connection, typically indicating movement from an origin to a destination.
direction-options.label-none_description: Option label in Direction options for connections on the canvas: Allows the user to select no directional indicator for the connection. This choice ensures the connection line is displayed without arrows.
direction-options.label-reverse_description: Option label in the direction options for a connection on the canvas: Allows the user to reverse the direction of the selected connection.

canvas.description-experimental-types: Enable selection of experimental element types
canvas.description-experimental-types_descripti: Setting in Canvas configuration: Controls whether users can select and use experimental dashboard elements. This option enables access to new or unreleased features within the canvas.
canvas.description-inline-editing_description: Toggle or option in the Canvas settings: This enables users to directly edit dashboard panels on the canvas itself, bypassing a separate panel configuration interface.
canvas.description-pan-zoom_description: Option in Canvas Settings: Allows users to enable or disable the interactive panning and zooming of the dashboard content displayed on the canvas.

drone-front-item.category-drone-front_description: Category Title in a drone front item on a dashboard canvas: Labels information or a view representing the front side of a drone. This helps users distinguish specific drone perspectives.
drone-front-item.name-roll-angle_description: Label on a drone front item within the canvas: Identifies the displayed metric representing the drone's roll angle.

drone-side-item.category-drone-side_description: Category label in the 'drone-side-item' component on the canvas: This identifies a specific view or data category related to one side of a drone.
drone-side-item.name-pitch-angle_description: Label in the drone-side-item panel on the canvas: This text identifies the pitch angle, which represents the drone's rotational orientation around its lateral axis.

drone-top-item.category-drone-top_description: Category Label in the 'Drone Top Item' section on a dashboard canvas: This string labels a category or a specific view related to the top part of a drone. It helps organize or display information concerning the drone's upper components or a top-down perspective.
drone-top-item.name-back-left-rotor-rpm_description: **Label** in the **Drone Top Item section on a dashboard canvas**: This string identifies or displays the rotational speed (RPM) of the drone's back-left rotor. It is a specific metric for monitoring drone performance.
drone-top-item.name-back-right-rotor-rpm_description: Label in the drone's top-level information widget on a dashboard canvas: Displays the rotations per minute (RPM) of the back-right rotor.
drone-top-item.name-front-left-rotor-rpm_description: Label in the drone's top-level information display: Indicates the rotational speed (RPM) of the front-left rotor.
drone-top-item.name-front-right-rotor-rpm_description: Label in a drone monitoring panel on the dashboard canvas: Identifies the Revolutions Per Minute (RPM) of the front right rotor of a drone.
drone-top-item.name-yaw-angle_description: Label in a drone's top-level information section: This field displays the drone's yaw angle, indicating its rotation around the vertical axis.

element-editor.category-layout_description: Category label in Element Editor on the Canvas: This label identifies a section where users can configure the visual arrangement and positioning properties of a selected dashboard element.
element-editor.description-unsupported: Selected element type is not supported by current settings. Please enable advanced element types.
element-editor.name-quick-placement_description: Feature name in the Element Editor on the Canvas: This feature provides a fast and efficient way to position dashboard elements.

ellipse-item.category-ellipse_description: Ellipse in the canvas item category: This label identifies the graphical shape 'Ellipse' as a selectable element type for dashboard design.

label.bottom_description: Label alignment option in an ellipse item on a canvas: Specifies the label's vertical position at the bottom of the ellipse.
label.center_description: Alignment option for an ellipse item's label: Allows users to horizontally center the label's text within or relative to the ellipse on the dashboard canvas.
label.left_description: Option in the label alignment settings for an ellipse item on the canvas: Selects the left horizontal alignment for the ellipse's associated label.
label.middle_description: Label text for a positioning option within the settings for an ellipse item's label on the canvas: It specifies that the label should be centered.
label.right_description: An option in label positioning settings for an ellipse item on a canvas: Specifies that the label should be placed to the right side of the ellipse.
label.top_description: The string "Top" for an ellipse item's label on the canvas: It defines a vertical alignment or position setting, placing the label at the top of the ellipse or its container.

ellipse-item.name-align-text_description: Control in the properties panel for an ellipse item on the canvas: Used to adjust the alignment of text displayed within the ellipse.
ellipse-item.name-text_description: Label in the configuration properties for an ellipse item on the canvas: This label identifies the input field where users define the text content displayed within the ellipse shape.
ellipse-item.name-text-color_description: The 'Text color' label for the name of an ellipse item on the canvas: Identifies the control used to set the color of the text label displayed within the ellipse.
ellipse-item.name-text-size_description: Label for a font size setting in the properties of an ellipse item on a canvas. It controls the size of the name or label text displayed within that ellipse.
ellipse-item.name-vertical-align_description: Vertical alignment control in the properties of an ellipse item's name: This setting determines the vertical position of the text label displayed within the ellipse shape on the canvas.

placeholder.auto_description: Option 'Auto' for a placeholder property within an ellipse item's settings on the dashboard canvas: This setting configures a specific characteristic of the placeholder, such as its content or dimensions, to be automatically determined.

icon-item.category-icon_description: Text label in the configuration options for an icon item on a dashboard canvas: It identifies the setting for selecting or displaying an icon. This icon is potentially used for categorization.
icon-item.name-fill-color_description: Fill color" setting in an icon item on a canvas: Allows users to define the interior color of the icon.
icon-item.name-svg-path_description: Field label in an icon item's properties on a canvas: This label identifies the input field where users can define or view the SVG path data for the icon's visual representation.

inline-edit.canvas-inline-editor_description: Label for an inline editor on the dashboard canvas: This identifies an editor that allows users to directly modify elements within the canvas without opening a separate configuration window.
inline-edit.tooltip-close-inline-editor_description: Tooltip in an active inline editor on the BMC Helix Dashboards canvas: This text describes the action of closing the currently open inline editor.

inline-edit-body.category-selected-element_description: Label in the inline edit panel on the canvas: Displays the name of the element (e.g., from a list or category) that is currently selected for editing or configuration. The `{{element}}` placeholder will be replaced by the actual element's name.
inline-edit-body.label-add-item_description: Button label in an inline editing body within the canvas: Used to add a new item or element to the current editable section.
inline-edit-body.please-select-an-element_description: Instructional message in an inline editing panel on the canvas: This message appears when no element is selected, guiding the user to select one to proceed with an inline editing action.

canvas.label-actions_description: Button or menu label in a canvas: Provides access to various operations or commands related to the canvas or its components.
canvas.label-color_description: Label in canvas properties: This refers to the color setting for a label element displayed within the canvas area. It allows users to customize the color of such labels.
canvas.label-direction_description: Label in canvas settings: Identifies a control for specifying the orientation or flow of elements or content on the canvas.
canvas.label-image_description: Label in the canvas: This text identifies an image component or an option to insert an image onto the dashboard canvas.
canvas.label-image-size_description: Label in the image properties panel within the Canvas editor: Indicates the control for defining the dimensions (width and height) of an image element displayed on the canvas.
