canvas.label-line-style_description: Label in Canvas settings: Indicates the option to select or define the visual style of lines. This applies to lines used in visualizations or other elements drawn on the canvas.
canvas.label-links_description: Label in Canvas settings: Refers to the configuration options for various links associated with the dashboard or its individual elements.
canvas.label-radius_description: Input field label in the canvas editor: Allows users to specify the radius for visual elements or component properties, such as rounded corners.
canvas.label-size_description: Field label in canvas properties: Denotes the control for setting the visual size of a label. This typically refers to font size or the overall dimensions of the label element displayed on the canvas.
canvas.label-width_description: A property or input field in the canvas settings: Used to define the horizontal dimension of a label or other visual element on the dashboard canvas.

layer-editor.category-layer_description: Label in the Layer Editor within the Canvas: Identifies an individual graphical or data layer that can be managed or edited in the UI.
layer-editor.category-layout_description: Category label in the Layer Editor within the Canvas: Introduces a section of settings related to the arrangement and visual structure of elements on a layer.
layer-editor.name-constraints_description: Section heading in the canvas layer editor: It introduces a section where users can define rules or restrictions that apply to the selected layer.
layer-editor.name-elements_description: **Elements (section title) in the Layer Editor on the Canvas:** This string labels the section where individual visual components or objects within a selected layer are listed and managed. It allows users to interact with and configure the content of that specific layer.

line-style-editor.label-animate_description: Label in the Line Style Editor: This label is associated with an option to enable or disable animation for lines or line elements within a dashboard visualization.

line-style-options.label-solid_description: Label in the Line Style Options panel within the Canvas editor: Represents the 'Solid' option for line styles, indicating a continuous and unbroken line.

label.bottom_description: Positioning option for a label in a metric value item on the canvas: This option allows users to set the vertical alignment of the label to the bottom.
label.center_description: Label alignment option in a metric value item on a dashboard canvas: Used to horizontally center the text of a label.
label.left_description: Alignment option in the label settings of a metric value item: Specifies the horizontal alignment of the label to the left.
label.middle_description: Option within the label alignment settings for a metric value item on a dashboard canvas: Specifies the middle position for the label.
label.right_description: Alignment or position option in the settings for a metric-value item's label on the canvas: It defines that the label should be positioned or aligned to the right side.
label.top_description: Label in a metric value display item on a dashboard canvas: Specifies or indicates the top position or alignment for content or elements within that item.

placeholder.auto_description: Option "Auto" in the placeholder settings for a metric-value item on the dashboard canvas: This option automatically manages the display of placeholder content when a metric value is unavailable.

canvas.name-align-text_description: UI control in the canvas settings: Used to adjust the horizontal or vertical alignment of text within visual elements on the dashboard canvas.
canvas.name-color_description: Label in the canvas configuration panel: This option allows users to select the text color for elements displayed or configured within the canvas.
canvas.name-experimental-types_description: Experimental element types in Canvas settings: This refers to element types that are currently under development or being tested for use on a dashboard canvas. These features may not be stable or ready for production use.
canvas.name-inline-editing_description: Setting label in Canvas configuration: Indicates the option to allow users to directly edit elements, such as panel titles or text, on the dashboard canvas.
canvas.name-pan-zoom_description: Feature name on the canvas: This label identifies the functionality that allows users to pan and zoom the dashboard content, aiding in the navigation of large or detailed visualizations.
canvas.name-text_description: Label for a text element on the canvas: This identifies a selectable text component that users can add to their dashboard.
canvas.name-text-size_description: Setting label in the canvas properties: Used to define the font size for the name or title text of an element displayed on the canvas.
canvas.name-vertical-align_description: Option label in the Canvas properties panel: Controls the vertical alignment of elements or content displayed on the canvas.

not-found-display.not-found_description: Informational message in the canvas's not-found display: This text indicates that a specific configuration component or UI element could not be located. The `<config />` tag represents the embedded missing element.

pan-zoom-help.ctrl-right-mouse_description: A keyboard and mouse shortcut description in the help text for pan and zoom controls on a canvas: It specifies the key combination 'CTRL + right mouse' used for a particular canvas interaction.
pan-zoom-help.middle-mouse_description: Label in the pan/zoom help section on a canvas: Describes the middle mouse button as an input method for canvas interactions. This typically refers to the scroll wheel when pressed.
pan-zoom-help.pan-title_description: A title in the pan and zoom help section on a canvas: Introduces instructions or information on how to pan (move the view) within the canvas.
pan-zoom-help.reset-double-click_description: Instruction in the Pan/Zoom Help section of the canvas: Explains that double-clicking resets the pan and zoom state of the canvas view.
pan-zoom-help.title-pan-and-zoom-controls_description: **Title** in the **pan and zoom help section on a dashboard canvas**: This title labels the section that describes or provides instructions for using the pan and zoom controls.
pan-zoom-help.zoom-scroll-wheel_description: Help instruction in the pan-zoom help overlay on a canvas: Informs the user that zooming is performed by using the scroll wheel.

parallelogram-item.category-parallelogram_description: Shape category label in the shape selection menu on the dashboard canvas: Identifies the option to add or select a parallelogram shape.

label.bottom_description: A position option in the label settings for a parallelogram item on the canvas: Specifies to align the label to the bottom of the item.
label.center_description: Alignment option in the label properties of a parallelogram item: Aligns the associated text label horizontally to the center of the parallelogram.
label.left_description: Option label in the alignment settings for a parallelogram item's label on a canvas. This option allows users to set the label's horizontal position to the left.
label.middle_description: Setting value for label position in a parallelogram item on a dashboard canvas: Indicates that the label should be placed in the middle. This typically refers to vertical or horizontal centering.
label.right_description: Option value for label alignment or position in a parallelogram item on the canvas: Indicates the right-hand side or direction.
label.top_description: Positioning option for a label within a parallelogram item on the canvas: Specifies the top vertical alignment of the label.

parallelogram-item.name-align-text_description: Option in the properties or settings for a parallelogram item on a canvas: Allows users to adjust the alignment of the name text displayed within that parallelogram item.
parallelogram-item.name-text_description: Text label in a parallelogram item on the canvas: This string refers to the default or editable text content displayed inside a parallelogram shape.
parallelogram-item.name-text-color_description: Label in a parallelogram item on the canvas: Identifies the control used to select the color of the item's displayed name or label text.
parallelogram-item.name-text-size_description: Setting in the name of a parallelogram item on the canvas: Controls the display size of the item's name.
parallelogram-item.name-vertical-align_description: Setting option in the properties panel for a parallelogram item on the canvas: Allows users to adjust the vertical positioning of the item's name or label within or relative to the shape.

placeholder.auto_description: Auto" option in the placeholder settings for a parallelogram item on the canvas: This option allows the system to automatically manage the placeholder's content or properties.

params-editor.placeholder-key_description: Placeholder text in the parameter editor on the canvas: Guides the user to enter the name or identifier for a parameter.
params-editor.placeholder-value_description: Placeholder text in the parameter editor within the dashboard canvas: This string serves as a hint for users to input a value into the associated field.

horizontal-options.label-center_description: Label in horizontal alignment options within the placement editor: This option allows users to horizontally center a selected element on the canvas.
horizontal-options.label-left_description: Label in the horizontal placement options of the canvas editor: Indicates the 'Left' alignment for an element. This controls its horizontal position.
horizontal-options.label-left-and-right_description: Option label in the horizontal options section of the placement editor on a canvas: It represents a horizontal alignment or distribution setting that applies to both the left and right sides of elements.
horizontal-options.label-right_description: Option label in the horizontal placement options of the canvas placement editor: Designates the option to align an element to the right.
horizontal-options.label-scale_description: Label for a scaling control in the Canvas Placement Editor's 'Horizontal Options' section: This allows users to adjust the size or magnification of selected dashboard elements. Its placement suggests relevance to horizontal sizing or arrangement.

placement-editor.label-constraints_description: Label in the placement editor section of a canvas: Indicates an area where users can define rules or restrictions for element positioning, sizing, or alignment.
placement-editor.label-position_description: Label in the placement editor within the canvas: Indicates controls or settings for adjusting the location of an element.

vertical-options.label-bottom_description: UI option label in the Vertical Options section of the Placement Editor: Used to position a dashboard element vertically at the bottom of its allocated space.
vertical-options.label-center_description: Option in the 'Vertical Options' section of the 'Placement Editor': Sets the vertical alignment of a selected element or label to the center within its container.
vertical-options.label-scale_description: The 'Scale' option in the 'Vertical Options' panel of the placement editor: Allows users to adjust the vertical scaling of a selected element or label. This setting controls the size or proportion of an element along the vertical axis.
vertical-options.label-top_description: Label in the vertical alignment options of the placement editor: This option allows users to select 'Top' as the vertical alignment or position for an element on the canvas.
vertical-options.label-top-and-bottom_description: Option label in the vertical options section of the dashboard canvas's placement editor: Represents a vertical alignment choice that positions an element at both the top and bottom.

quick-positioning.tooltip-align-bottom_description: Button label in the quick-positioning controls on the canvas: This button aligns the bottom edges of selected dashboard elements.
quick-positioning.tooltip-align-horizontal-centers_descrip: Tooltip text for a button/icon in the Quick Positioning toolbar on the canvas: Describes the action of aligning the horizontal centers of selected visual elements. This feature helps users arrange dashboard panels or other components neatly.
quick-positioning.tooltip-align-left_description: **Option** in a **quick-positioning menu or pop-up on the dashboard canvas**: **Used to align selected dashboard elements horizontally to the left.** The `tooltip-` prefix in the key path suggests this quick-positioning menu might be a transient, tooltip-like interface.
quick-positioning.tooltip-align-right_description: Tooltip text for a button/icon in the quick-positioning section of the canvas: It describes the action of aligning selected dashboard elements or content to the right edge.
quick-positioning.tooltip-align-top_description: Action button in the Quick Positioning menu on the dashboard canvas: Aligns the top edges of selected visual elements.
quick-positioning.tooltip-align-vertical-centers_descripti: Tooltip for a button/menu item in the quick positioning tools on a canvas: Describes the function of aligning the vertical centers of selected dashboard elements. This feature helps users arrange multiple components evenly along their vertical axis.

rectangle-item.category-rectangle_description: Rectangle in the canvas editor: Represents a selectable or drawable rectangular shape on the canvas.

label.bottom_description: Alignment option for a label: This setting, used for a label within a rectangle item on the canvas, specifies that the label should be placed at the bottom of its container.
label.center_description: Label alignment option in a rectangle item: Aligns the label's text horizontally to the center.
label.left_description: Option in the horizontal alignment settings for a rectangle item's label on the canvas: This option allows users to align the label text to the left.
label.middle_description: Alignment option for a label within a rectangle item on a canvas: This option sets the label's position or content alignment to the middle.
label.right_description: Alignment option in the label properties for a rectangle item on a dashboard canvas: Specifies that the label should be aligned to the right. This setting controls the horizontal positioning of the label.
label.top_description: Positional option in the label settings of a rectangle item on a dashboard canvas: It indicates that the label should be positioned or vertically aligned to the top.

rectangle-item.name-align-text_description: Option in rectangle item properties on the canvas: Allows users to adjust the alignment of text displayed within the selected rectangle item.
rectangle-item.name-text_description: Text label in a rectangle item on the canvas: This string represents the default text displayed within a text element or a placeholder for text content inside a rectangle item on the dashboard canvas.
rectangle-item.name-text-color_description: Text color for the name of a rectangle item on a canvas: Used to define the color of the name's text.
rectangle-item.name-text-size_description: Label for a text size selection control or input field in the properties panel for a rectangle item on a canvas: Allows users to adjust the font size of text displayed within the rectangle item. This setting controls the size of text such as titles or other descriptive content.
rectangle-item.name-vertical-align_description: An alignment option for a rectangle item in the canvas: Configures the vertical positioning of content (e.g., text) displayed within the rectangle.

placeholder.auto_description: Auto" option in a placeholder setting for a rectangle item on the canvas: This option automatically determines or manages the placeholder's content, size, or behavior.

server-item.category-server_description: Label in a server item on a dashboard canvas: Designates a category for server-related items or data.
server-item.name-blink-rate_description: Setting in the configuration panel for a server item on a dashboard canvas: It defines the blinking frequency (in Hertz) for the server item's name. A value of 0 disables the blinking.
server-item.name-bulb-color_description: Label for a color selection field in the configuration settings of a server item on the canvas: It allows the user to define the color of a 'bulb' indicator, which visually represents the status or state of the server item.
server-item.name-status-color_description: Setting for a server item displayed on a dashboard canvas: It allows users to configure or view the color used to represent the server's operational status.
server-item.name-type_description: Field label in the properties panel for a server item on the canvas: Specifies the category or nature of the server item.

type-options.label-database_description: Label within the type options for a server item on the canvas: This text identifies "Database" as a choice for the type of server-related data source or connection being configured.
type-options.label-single_description: Option label in the type options for a server item: It represents a choice to configure or display the server item as a single entity or instance, as opposed to multiple or grouped.
type-options.label-stack_description: Option label in the server item's type options on the canvas: This label allows users to select or configure a stacked display mode for the server item's data visualization. It typically refers to displaying multiple data series on top of each other in a chart.
type-options.label-terminal_description: Label in type options for a server item on the canvas: Identifies or allows selection of 'Terminal' as a configuration type. This option likely distinguishes a specific server or connection type within the dashboard configuration.

text-display.double-click-to-set_description: Instruction in a text display element within the dashboard canvas: Informs the user to double-click the element to set or edit its text content.

text-item.category-text_description: Category label in a text item on the canvas: Identifies a section or option as being related to text content.

label.bottom_description: Label within the properties of a text item on a canvas: It represents the 'bottom' alignment or position option for the text or item itself.
label.center_description: Alignment option in the label settings of a text item on a canvas: Used to center the text horizontally within the label.
label.left_description: Label in text item alignment settings: Specifies the option to horizontally align the text content to the left.
label.middle_description: Option label in the text item settings on a dashboard canvas: It denotes the middle vertical alignment option for text within the item.
label.right_description: Option label in the settings for a text item's label on the canvas: This option allows users to set the alignment or positioning of the label content to the right.
label.top_description: Option label in text item configuration on a dashboard canvas: Used to select top vertical alignment for the content of a text item. This is typically found alongside other vertical alignment options.

text-item.name-align-text_description: Label in the properties panel for a text item within a canvas: Indicates the control to set the horizontal alignment of the text content.
text-item.name-text_description: Default label for a text item on a canvas: This string is the initial, generic name assigned to a text box component when it's placed on the dashboard canvas.
text-item.name-text-color_description: Label in Text Item properties on a Canvas: This label identifies the control used to select the display color for the text content within the item.
text-item.name-text-size_description: Control for text size in the properties panel of a text item on the dashboard canvas: This setting allows users to adjust the font size of the text within the selected text item.
text-item.name-vertical-align_description: **Setting label** in the **properties panel of a text item on the dashboard canvas**: This identifies an option that allows users to control the vertical positioning of text within the selected text item.

placeholder.auto_description: The 'Auto' option in a text item's placeholder settings: This specifies that the content or behavior of a text item's placeholder on the dashboard canvas is automatically determined by the system.

tooltip-options.label-disable-one-click_description: **Option label** in **canvas tooltip options**: This option allows users to disable tooltips specifically for elements on the dashboard canvas that trigger an action with a single click. This can prevent tooltips from interfering with or obscuring these interactive components.
tooltip-options.name-tooltip-mode_description: A setting in the Tooltip options panel for a dashboard canvas: It defines how tooltips are displayed or interact on the canvas.

tooltip-mode-options.label-disabled_description: Option label in the tooltip mode options for a dashboard canvas: This label indicates that a particular tooltip display mode or feature is currently inactive or turned off.
tooltip-mode-options.label-enabled_description: Label in Tooltip mode options settings on the dashboard canvas: It indicates that a specific tooltip display mode is currently active or selected.

tree-navigation-editor.clear-selection_description: Button in the tree navigation editor within the canvas: Clears any currently selected items or nodes in the tree structure.
tree-navigation-editor.frame-selection_description: Label in the Tree Navigation Editor within the Canvas: Indicates the feature for selecting a 'frame'. This 'frame' likely refers to a dashboard panel, view, or content element that will be included or referenced in the hierarchical navigation structure.
tree-navigation-editor.label-add-item_description: Button in the Tree Navigation Editor in the canvas: Used to add a new item or element to the tree-like navigation structure being edited.
tree-navigation-editor.missing-layer_description: Prompt in the tree navigation editor on the canvas: Asks if a layer, expected to be present within the navigation structure, is currently missing. It typically indicates a potential broken reference or configuration issue.
tree-navigation-editor.no-settings_description: Message in the tree navigation editor: Informs the user when there are no configurable settings or options available for the selected navigation item or the editor itself.

switcher-icon.title-node-icon_description: Label in the Tree Navigation Editor: This string identifies the input field or display for selecting or showing an icon specific to a navigation tree node's title. It is found within the icon management section, potentially as part of a component for switching between different icon options.

tree-node-title.title-duplicate_description: Action label in a tree node title within the canvas: This allows users to duplicate the associated tree node.
tree-node-title.tooltip-duplicate_description: Tooltip in a tree node title on the canvas: Indicates an action to duplicate the associated tree node.

triangle-item.category-triangle_description: Category label in the dashboard canvas: This text identifies a graphical item as a 'Triangle' shape.

label.bottom_description: Option for label positioning of a triangle item on the canvas: Indicates that the label will be placed at the bottom.
label.center_description: Label in the alignment settings for a triangle item on the canvas: It refers to an option to position or align the triangle item to the center.
label.left_description: Label text associated with a triangle item on the canvas: Represents the 'Left' option for its position, alignment, or direction.
label.middle_description: A label within the properties or options for a triangle-shaped item on the dashboard canvas: It indicates a selection for a middle position or alignment.
label.right_description: Label 'Right' within the configuration options for a triangle item on a canvas: This option is used to set the direction, orientation, or alignment of the triangle item or its associated elements.
label.top_description: Label for a positional or alignment option within a triangle item on the canvas: Denotes the 'Top' edge or boundary. This label is used to indicate the upper position or alignment setting.

triangle-item.name-align-text_description: A setting for text alignment in the properties of a triangle item on a canvas: Allows users to specify how text associated with the triangle item should be aligned.
triangle-item.name-text_description: Default text in a triangle item on the canvas: This is the default name or label displayed for the triangular graphical element.
triangle-item.name-text-color_description: The 'Text color' setting in the properties of a triangle item on the canvas: Used to specify the color of the name text displayed for that item.
triangle-item.name-text-size_description: Setting for the text of a triangle item's name on the canvas: Allows users to specify the font size for the name displayed within the triangle item.
triangle-item.name-vertical-align_description: Label in the properties panel for a triangle item on a dashboard canvas: This setting allows users to control the vertical alignment of content or text within or associated with the triangle item.

placeholder.auto_description: Option within the placeholder settings for a triangle item on the canvas: This selection enables an automatic behavior or configuration for a property (e.g., size, content) of the triangle item.

wind-turbine-item.category-wind-turbine_description: Category label in a wind turbine item on the canvas: Identifies the item or data as pertaining to a wind turbine. This string serves as a title or grouping label for a UI component representing a wind turbine.

carousel.error_description: Error message in an image carousel: Informs the user that images could not be loaded into the carousel.

inline-toast.success_description: Inline toast message displayed after clicking a clipboard button: Confirms that content has been successfully copied to the clipboard.

cloud-enterprise-feature-badge_descripti: A badge on a feature: Indicates that the associated feature or content is related to "Cloud & Enterprise" solutions or categories.
cloud-feature-badge_description: Feature Badge in BMC Helix Dashboards UI: Labels specific features or offerings as cloud-based, helping users quickly identify cloud-relevant capabilities.

async.error: An error occurred while loading options.
async.error_description: Combobox in asynchronous loading context: This error message is displayed when a combobox fails to retrieve its options dynamically.

clear.title: Clear value
clear.title_description: Title for a 'Clear' action in a combobox: This indicates the action of removing the currently selected or entered value from the combobox. It might appear as a tooltip or an accessible label for the clear control.

custom-value.description_description: Option in a combobox: This option allows the user to input a custom value not present in the predefined list. It enables free-form text entry for the combobox.

options.no-found_description: Message in a combobox's options list: Informs the user that no selectable options are found, perhaps due to filtering or an empty dataset.

action.change-theme_description: Action item in the Command Palette: Allows the user to change the visual theme (e.g., light or dark mode, color scheme) of the dashboard interface.
action.dark-theme_description: Option in the Command Palette's action menu: Allows the user to switch the application's visual theme to dark mode.
action.light-theme_description: Option in the Command Palette: Represents the "Light" theme choice for the user interface.
action.scopes_description: Action in the Command Palette: Allows users to define or select the specific entities, applications, or data sets (scopes) for their dashboard view or operation.

empty-state.message_description: Message in the empty state of the command palette: Informs the user that their search query or filtering action in the command palette did not yield any matching results.

scopes.apply-selected-scopes_description: Button in the 'scopes' section of the Command Palette: Used to confirm and apply the currently selected filters or data scopes.
scopes.selected-scopes-label_description: Label in the 'Scopes' section of the command palette: Indicates the area displaying or allowing selection of configured scopes.

section.actions_description: Section label in the command palette: Identifies a group of commands or operations the user can perform. This label serves as a header for a specific section within the command palette.
section.dashboard-search-results_description: Section heading in the Command Palette's dashboard search results: Labels the group of returned dashboard items.
section.folder-search-results_description: Section heading in the Command Palette's folder search results section: Labels the list of folders found during a search.
section.pages_description: Section heading in a command palette section: This label introduces a group of commands or items related to managing or navigating dashboard pages.
section.preferences_description: Section title in the Command Palette: This option allows users to access and configure application-specific settings and preferences.
section.recent-dashboards_description: Section heading in the command palette: Identifies a section that displays a list of recently accessed dashboards. This helps users quickly navigate to their most recently viewed dashboards.
section.recent-scopes_description: Section Header in the Command Palette: This header introduces a list of recently used data scopes, allowing users quick access to switch between different data contexts.

common.all_description: Option in selection controls or filters: Represents the choice to include every available item or category.
common.apply_description: Button in general UI contexts: Used to confirm and implement modifications, settings, or selections made by the user.
common.clear_description: Button or action in various UI contexts: Clears entered data, selections, or applied filters.
common.collapse_description: Action button in various common UI components: Used to hide or minimize an expanded section, panel, or view, making more space available.
common.disabled_description: Status label in various UI elements: Denotes that a component, feature, or option is currently inactive or unavailable for use.
common.help_description: Button or link in a common UI area (e.g., header, footer, or navigation menu): Provides access to product documentation, support, or help resources.

locale.default_description: Option in locale settings: Represents the default selection for the system's language or regional format.

common.search_description: Label or button text in common application areas: Used to initiate a search or identify an input field for searching. This string refers to a general search functionality.
common.view_description: Button, link, or menu item in common application areas: Initiates the display or opening of an item, such as a report, dashboard, or details.

config-card.complete_description: Status label on a Configuration Card: Indicates that a configuration or an associated task has been successfully completed. The existing translations confirm it describes a finished state.

label.all_description: Label in the 'Filter by options' section when adding a new connection: This option allows users to view all available items, effectively removing any active filter.
label.installed_description: Label in the "Filter by" options within the "Add New Connection" interface: This filter allows users to display only connection types or data sources that are already installed.
label.new-updates_description: Label in the 'Filter by' options section when adding a new connection: This option allows users to filter items to display those that are new or have received recent updates.

label.by-downloads_description: Option label in the Add New Connection section: It allows users to sort or filter the list of available connection types or plugins by their download count or popularity.
label.by-name-az_description: Option label in the 'Add new connection' interface: This label identifies a sorting option to order a list of items (such as connection types or available connections) alphabetically by name from A to Z.
label.by-name-za_description: Option label in the 'Add New Connection' dialog: This option allows users to sort a list of connections or connection types by name in descending alphabetical order.
label.by-published-date_description: Label in the "Add New Connection" screen: This option allows users to sort or filter the displayed items (e.g., available connection types or data sources) based on their published date.
label.by-updated-date_description: Label in the 'Add new connection' screen (Connections section): This label represents an option to sort or filter a list of items, such as available connection types or templates, based on their last updated date. It helps users organize and find specific options within the new connection creation workflow.

advisor-redirect-notice.aria-label-link-to-advisor_description: ARIA label for a link in the Advisor redirect notice within the Connections section: This label describes a link that navigates the user to the Advisor application.
advisor-redirect-notice.body: Try the new Advisor to uncover potential issues with your data sources and plugins.
advisor-redirect-notice.body_description: Message body in an Advisor redirect notice within the Connections section: Informs users about a new Advisor feature and encourages them to use it for identifying potential issues with data sources and plugins.
advisor-redirect-notice.go-to-advisor_description: Button in a redirect notice within the Connections section: Navigates the user to the Advisor tool or a related section. This button confirms the redirection.

cache-feature-highlight-page.header: Query caching can improve load times and reduce API costs by temporarily storing the results of data source queries. When you or other users submit the same query, the results will come back from the cache instead of from the data source.
cache-feature-highlight-page.item-1: Faster dashboard load times, especially for popular dashboards.
cache-feature-highlight-page.item-1_description: Feature highlight item in the cache feature highlight page within the Connections section: Explains the primary performance benefit of the caching feature: quicker loading of dashboards, particularly those that are frequently viewed.
cache-feature-highlight-page.item-2_description: Benefit statement in the Cache Feature Highlight page within the Connections section: It describes a key advantage of using the caching feature, specifically its ability to reduce API-related expenses.
cache-feature-highlight-page.item-3: Reduced likelihood that APIs will rate-limit or throttle requests.
cache-feature-highlight-page.item-3_description: Feature Highlight Text in Cache Feature Highlight Page (Connections): This text explains a core benefit of the cache feature by highlighting its ability to reduce the likelihood of APIs rate-limiting or throttling requests.
cache-feature-highlight-page.title: Optimize queries with Query Caching in Grafana Cloud
cache-feature-highlight-page.title_description: Page Title in the Query Caching Feature Highlight page: Introduces the capability to optimize queries using Query Caching in Grafana Cloud.

add-new-connection.subtitle: Connect data to Grafana through data sources, integrations and apps
add-new-connection.subtitle_description: Subtitle in the "Add New Connection" section on the Connections Home Page for Cloud connections: This text provides a brief explanation of how users can connect data to Grafana, listing the primary methods (data sources, integrations, and apps).
add-new-connection.title: Add new connection
add-new-connection.title_description: Title in the 'Add New Connection' page or modal, accessible from the Cloud Connections Home Page: It indicates that the user is on the screen to configure a new cloud connection.

collector.subtitle: Manage the configuration of Grafana Alloy, our distribution of the OpenTelemetry Collector
collector.subtitle_description: Subtitle in the Collector section on the Cloud Connections Home Page: Explains that users can manage the configuration of Grafana Alloy, BMC's distribution of the OpenTelemetry Collector.
collector.title: Collector
collector.title_description: Section title in the Cloud Connections Home Page: Identifies a dedicated section or component for managing and viewing data collectors.

data-sources.subtitle: Manage your existing data source connections
data-sources.subtitle_description: Subtitle in the Data Sources section of the Connections Home Page: It introduces the interface where users can manage (view, edit, or delete) their previously configured data source connections.
data-sources.title: Data sources
data-sources.title_description: Section Title in the 'Data Sources' section of the 'Connections Home Page' (under the 'Cloud' context). It labels the area where users can manage their data source connections within BMC Helix Dashboards.

integrations.subtitle: Manage your active integrations
integrations.subtitle_description: Subtitle in the Integrations section of the Cloud Connections Home Page: Indicates that this section allows users to manage their currently active integrations.
integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.title_description: Section Title on the Connections home page in BMC Helix Dashboards: Labels the dedicated area where users can manage or configure integrations with external systems.

private-data-source-connections.subtitle: Manage your private network connections for data sources
private-data-source-connections.subtitle_description: Subtitle in the "Private Data Source Connections" section within the Cloud Connections Home Page: This text describes the functionality for managing private network connections used by data sources.
private-data-source-connections.title: Private data source connect
private-data-source-connections.title_description: Section title in the 'Private Data Source Connections' area of the 'Connections Home Page' (within 'Cloud' settings): Identifies the section dedicated to managing and configuring connections to private data sources. The English value "connect" is used here as a noun, meaning "connection.

connections-home-page.subtitle: Connect your infrastructure to Grafana Cloud using data sources, integrations and apps. Use this page to add to manage everything from data ingestion to private connections and telemetry pipelines.

connect-data.apps-header_description: Header in the "Connect Data" section within Connections: It identifies a section where users can connect various applications as data sources for BMC Helix Dashboards.
connect-data.datasources-header_description: Header in the "Connect Data" section: Titles the area where users manage or view their data sources.
connect-data.empty-message: No results matching your query were found
connect-data.empty-message_description: Message in the Connect Data section within Connections: Informs the user that their search or filter query yielded no results. This message displays when no items match the user's input in a list or table.
connect-data.request-data-source_description: Link or Button in the 'Connect Data' section within 'Connections': Allows users to submit a request for a data source that is not currently available in the system. This option is used when a new data source needs to be added.
connect-data.roadmap_description: Link in the "Connect Data" section within the Connections area: This allows users to view the development roadmap, likely detailing future plans for data integration features or supported connectors within BMC Helix Dashboards.

connections-home-page.welcome-to-connections_description: Heading in Connections Home Page: Greets the user upon arrival at the Connections section. It introduces the main purpose of this area.

connections-redirect-notice.aria-label-link-to-connections_descripti: ARIA label for a link in a "Connections Redirect Notice": Provides an accessible name for a link that navigates the user to the main Connections section of the application.
connections-redirect-notice.body: Data sources have a new home! You can discover new data sources or manage existing ones in the Connections page, accessible from the main menu.
connections-redirect-notice.go-to-connections_description: Button in the connections redirect notice: Navigates the user to the Connections management page. It serves as a call to action within a system message.

subTitle.datasource-could-found: No datasource with this ID could be found.
subTitle.datasource-could-found_description: Status Message on the Data Source Details page within the Connections section: This message informs the user that a data source with the specified ID could not be found. It is displayed when the system fails to retrieve details for a non-existent or inaccessible data source.

text.unknown-datasource_description: Text label in the Data Source Details page within the Connections section: This message indicates that a data source cannot be identified or recognized by the system.

feature-highlight-page.foot-note: After creating an account, you can easily <2>migrate this instance to Grafana Cloud</2> with our Migration Assistant.
feature-highlight-page.footer: Create a Grafana Cloud Free account to start using data source permissions. This feature is also available with a Grafana Enterprise license.
feature-highlight-page.footer-link_description: Link in the footer of the Connections feature highlight page: Navigates users to information about the enterprise capabilities or edition of BMC Helix Dashboards. This helps users understand the benefits and features relevant to enterprise-level usage.
feature-highlight-page.link-button-label_description: Link button on the Feature Highlight page within the Connections section: This button allows users to initiate the account creation process.

insights-feature-highlight-page.header: Usage Insights provide detailed information about data source usage, like the number of views, queries, and errors users have experienced. You can use this to improve users’ experience and troubleshoot issues.
insights-feature-highlight-page.item-1: Demonstrate and improve the value of your observability service by keeping track of user engagement
insights-feature-highlight-page.item-1_description: Feature description on the Insights Feature Highlight page within the Connections section: It explains how tracking user engagement can demonstrate and improve the value of an observability service.
insights-feature-highlight-page.item-2: Keep Grafana performant and by identifying and fixing slow, error-prone data sources
insights-feature-highlight-page.item-2_description: Feature Highlight Item in the Insights feature highlight page (Connections section): This text highlights a key benefit of the feature, which is to ensure Grafana's optimal performance by identifying and resolving issues with data sources.
insights-feature-highlight-page.item-3: Clean up your instance by finding and removing unused data sources
insights-feature-highlight-page.item-3_description: Feature description on the Insights feature highlight page in the Connections section: Informs users about a key capability to clean up their instance. This feature allows users to find and remove unused data sources for better system optimization.
insights-feature-highlight-page.item-4: Review individual data source usage insights at a glance in the UI, sort search results by usage and errors, or dig into detailed usage logs
insights-feature-highlight-page.title: Understand usage of your data sources with Grafana Cloud
insights-feature-highlight-page.title_description: Page Title in the Connections > Insights Feature Highlight page: This title introduces a feature designed to help users understand the usage patterns of their data sources. It highlights the use of Grafana Cloud to achieve this understanding.

subTitle.choose-a-data-source-type_description: Subtitle in the New Data Source Page within the Connections section: This subtitle prompts the user to select from a list of available data source types when configuring a new connection.

text.add-data-source_description: Button on the 'New Data Source' page: Used to submit the configuration form and add a new data source to BMC Helix Dashboards.

no-access-modal.connection-contact-grafana-admin: To add a new connection, contact your Grafana admin.
no-access-modal.connection-contact-grafana-admin_descrip: Informational message in the 'No Access' modal within the Connections section: This message informs the user that they do not have the necessary permissions to add a new connection and advises them to contact their Grafana administrator for assistance.
no-access-modal.editor-warning: Editors cannot add new connections. You may check to see if it is already configured in <2>Data sources</2>.
no-access-modal.links_description: Section heading in the 'No Access' modal for Connections: This label identifies a list of links that provide further actions or information for the user experiencing access restrictions.
no-access-modal.okay_description: Button in the "No Access" modal within the Connections section: Allows the user to acknowledge the no-access message and close the modal.

not-found-datasource.body:
Maybe you mistyped the URL or the plugin with the id <1> is unavailable.
To see a list of available datasources please <5>click here.

add-new-connection.subtitle: Connect to a new data source
add-new-connection.subtitle_description: Subtitle in the 'Add New Connection' section of the Connections Home Page: This text guides the user to the process of setting up a connection to a new data source. It serves as an introductory prompt for the user.
add-new-connection.title: Add new connection
add-new-connection.title_description: Title in the Add New Connection page or dialog: It identifies the screen where users configure and add a new connection. This title appears when the user initiates the process of adding a new connection from the Connections Home Page.

data-sources.subtitle: Manage your existing data source connections
data-sources.subtitle_description: Subtitle in the Data Sources section of the Connections Home Page: Indicates where users can manage their existing data source connections.
data-sources.title: View configured data sources
data-sources.title_description: Title in the 'Data Sources' section of the 'Connections Home Page': It identifies the area where users can view and manage their configured data source connections.

connections-home-page.subtitle: Manage your data source connections in one place. Use this page to add a new data source or manage your existing connections.

permissions-feature-highlight-page.header: With data source permissions, you can protect sensitive data by limiting access to this data source to specific users, teams, and roles.
permissions-feature-highlight-page.item-1: Protect sensitive data, like security logs, production databases, and personally-identifiable information
permissions-feature-highlight-page.item-2: Clean up users’ experience by hiding data sources they don’t need to use
permissions-feature-highlight-page.item-2_description: Feature highlight on the Permissions Feature Highlight Page: Describes a new capability that allows administrators to improve the user experience by hiding data sources users don't need. This is the second highlight point on the page, emphasizing streamlined data access.
permissions-feature-highlight-page.item-3: Share Grafana access more freely, knowing that users will not unwittingly see sensitive data
permissions-feature-highlight-page.item-3_description: A feature highlight item in the Permissions feature highlight page (under Connections): This item explains how improved permissions allow for broader Grafana access sharing without exposing sensitive data.
permissions-feature-highlight-page.title: Secure access to data with data source permissions in Grafana Cloud
permissions-feature-highlight-page.title_description: Page Title in the Permissions Feature Highlight page (under Connections): Informs users about the ability to secure data access using data source permissions. This feature is available in Grafana Cloud.

search.aria-label-search-all_description: Accessible label for a search control in the 'Search' section within 'Connections': This label provides an accessible name for a control that performs a comprehensive search within the Connections area.
search.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the search input field within the Connections section: It guides the user to perform a comprehensive search across all available connections or items.

subTitle.data-source-error_description: Subtitle in a data source configuration node: It indicates an error state for the data source. This subtitle itself serves as the primary error message for that specific data source.

text.data-source-nav-node_description: Label in the page navigation for Data Source Settings: It represents a specific navigation node related to data sources.

header.hide-json-diff_description: Button in the header of the version comparison view: Allows users to hide the detailed JSON differences when comparing two versions of an item. This button is typically visible when the JSON diff is currently displayed.
header.show-json-diff_description: Button in the Version History Comparison header: Displays the JSON differences between the compared versions. This helps users understand the underlying data changes in the selected dashboard or panel version.
header.text_description: Header text in the version comparison feature: Displays details about a specific version. It includes the version number, the user who made the update, how long ago the update occurred, and an optional associated message.

comparison.select: Select two versions to start comparing
comparison.select_description: Instructional text in the Version Comparison interface: Guides the user to select two versions before starting a comparison.

versionHistory.no-properties-changed_description: Message in Version History view: Informs the user that no significant properties have changed between the selected versions being compared or in the current version being reviewed. It indicates that no relevant property differences were detected.

table.updated_description: Column header in the version history table: Indicates the date when a version or item was last updated.
table.updatedBy_description: Column header in the Version History table: Indicates the user who performed the last update on a specific version or entry.
table.version_description: Table Header in the Version History table: Labels the column that displays the version number of a dashboard, panel, or other configuration item.

versionHistory.view-json-diff: View JSON diff to see all changes
versionHistory.view-json-diff_description: Button or link in the Version History screen: Allows users to view the detailed JSON differences between two versions of an item. This helps them see all changes made.

correlations.add-new_description: Button in the Correlations section: Allows users to add a new correlation.

alert.error-message: An unknown error occurred while fetching correlation data. Please try again.
alert.title: Error fetching correlation data
alert.title_description: Error message title in an alert displayed within the Correlations feature: Informs the user that the system encountered an issue while trying to retrieve correlation data.

basic-info-form.description-description: Optional description with more information about the link
basic-info-form.description-description_description: Text field in the Basic Info Form for Correlations: Allows users to enter an optional description to provide more information about a link.
basic-info-form.label-description: This name will be used as the label for the correlation. This will show as button text, a menu item, or hover text on a link.
basic-info-form.label-label_description: Label for an input field in the 'Basic Info' form under 'Correlations': Prompts the user to provide a name or identifier for the item being configured.
basic-info-form.label-required_description: Validation message in the Basic Information Form within the Correlations feature: Informs the user that a specific input field must be completed before proceeding or submitting the form. This is a standard validation message for mandatory fields.
basic-info-form.sub-text: Define text that will describe the correlation.
basic-info-form.sub-text_description: Instructional text in the Basic Info form for Correlations: Guides the user to define a text description for the correlation being created or edited.
basic-info-form.title: Define correlation label (Step 1 of 3)
basic-info-form.title_description: Title in the Basic Information Form within the Correlations feature: Guides the user through the first step of a three-step process to define a label for a new correlation.

configure-correlation-target-form.aria-label-correlation-type_description: ARIA label for a form field in the 'Configure Correlation Target' form: It identifies the control where the user selects or inputs the desired type of correlation.

empty-correlations-cta.please-contact-administrator-create-corr: Please contact your administrator to create new correlations.

Instructional text in the empty state view for correlations: This message instructs users to contact their administrator to create new correlations, suggesting that correlation creation is an administrator-level task or requires specific permissions.
empty-correlations-cta.there-are-no-correlations-configured-yet: There are no correlations configured yet.

Informational message in the Correlations section: Informs the user that no correlations have been configured yet. It is displayed when the correlation list is empty, often preceding a call-to-action to create new correlations.

empty-state.button-title_description: Button in the correlations empty state: Allows the user to add a new correlation when no correlations are currently configured.
empty-state.pro-tip: You can also define correlations via datasource provisioning
empty-state.pro-tip_description: Pro-tip message in the Correlations empty state: Informs users of an alternative method to define correlations, specifically via data source provisioning, when no correlations have been configured yet.
empty-state.title: You haven't defined any correlations yet
empty-state.title_description: Title in the Correlations empty state: Informs the user that no correlations have been defined. This message encourages the user to create new correlations.

list.delete_description: Button label in the Correlations List: Used to initiate the deletion of a selected correlation from the system.
list.label: Label
list.label_description: Column header in the Correlations list: Used to display the descriptive name or identifier for each correlation.
list.read-only_description: State label in the correlations list: Indicates that the displayed correlations or the list view itself is in a non-editable state, allowing only viewing.
list.source_description: Label in the Correlations list: Identifies the origin or source of a correlation, indicating where it came from or what initiated it.
list.target_description: Column header in the correlations list: It represents the primary entity, metric, or object for which a correlation is being defined or displayed. This helps users quickly identify the subject of each correlation entry.

correlations.page-content: To enable Correlations, add it in the Grafana config:
correlations.page-content_description: Instructional message on the Correlations feature page: This message provides guidance to the user on how to enable the Correlations functionality by adding a setting to the Grafana configuration.
correlations.page-heading_description: Heading on the Correlations page: Informs the user that the correlation feature or functionality is currently turned off or unavailable in the system.

query-editor.control-rules: The selected target data source must export a query editor.
query-editor.control-rules_description: Validation message in the Query Editor for Correlations: This message indicates that the selected target data source must export a query editor to be compatible with the correlation rules.
query-editor.data-source-text: Please select a target data source first.
query-editor.data-source-text_description: Informational message in the Correlations Query Editor: It prompts the user to select a target data source. This selection is a prerequisite for defining or working with correlation queries.
query-editor.data-source-title_description: Status message in the Query Editor within the Correlations feature: Informs the user that no data source has been selected for the current query. This message guides the user to choose a data source.
query-editor.error-text: The selected data source could not be loaded.
query-editor.error-text_description: Error message in the Query Editor of the Correlations feature: Informs the user that the selected data source could not be loaded.
query-editor.error-title_description: Error title in Query Editor within the Correlations feature: This title indicates that an attempt to load data from a specified data source has failed. It provides a summary of the data loading error.
query-editor.query-description: Define the query that is run when the link is clicked. You can use <2>variables</2> to access specific field values.
query-editor.query-editor-title: Data source does not export a query editor.
query-editor.query-editor-title_description: Informational message in the Query Editor section of the Correlations feature: Informs the user that the selected data source lacks a built-in query editor. This explains why query editing functionality is unavailable for this data source.
query-editor.query-label_description: Label in the Query Editor for Correlations: It identifies the input field or section where users define the query string or parameters for correlation analysis.

source-form.control-required_description: Validation message in the source configuration form within the Correlations feature: Informs the user that a mandatory field requires input before proceeding with form submission.
source-form.description:
A data point needs to provide values to all variables as fields or as transformations output to make the correlation button appear in the visualization.
Note: Not every variable needs to be explicitly defined below. A transformation such as <4>logfmt will create variables for every key/value pair.
source-form.description-external-pre: You have used following variables in the target URL:
source-form.description-external-pre_description: Descriptive text in the Correlation Source Form: This text serves as a preamble, indicating to the user that the following list displays variables detected within the configured external target URL.
source-form.description-query-pre: You have used following variables in the target query:
source-form.description-query-pre_description: Informational message in the Correlations Source Form: It precedes and introduces a list of variables that the user has utilized in the target query within this form.
source-form.external-title: Configure the data source that will use the URL (Step 3 of 3)
source-form.external-title_description: Heading in the correlation data source configuration form (Step 3 of 3): Guides the user to configure the data source that will use a URL.
source-form.heading-external: Variables used in the target URL
source-form.heading-external_description: Heading in the correlation source configuration form: This heading introduces a section displaying or describing variables that can be embedded into a target URL for an external link. It guides users in configuring how data from the source can be passed to an external system.
source-form.heading-query: Variables used in the target query
source-form.heading-query_description: Heading in the correlation source form query configuration: It introduces a section dedicated to managing variables that will be utilized in the target query.
source-form.query-title: Configure the data source that will link to {{dataSourceName}} (Step 3 of 3)
source-form.query-title_description: Form title in the Correlations source configuration wizard: Guides the user to configure a data source that will link to a specified data source (`{{dataSourceName}}`). This is the final step (Step 3 of 3) of the data source linking process.
source-form.results-description: The link will be shown next to the value of this field
source-form.results-description_description: **Helper text** in the **Correlations Source Form**: **Informs the user that a link, resulting from the form's configuration, will be displayed directly beside the value of a specific field.**
source-form.results-label_description: Label in the source form within the Correlations feature: It prompts the user to specify which field from the data source should be identified as the 'results' for the correlation analysis. This field is likely used to extract specific output or outcome data.
source-form.results-required_description: Validation message in the source form within the Correlations feature: Informs the user that a mandatory input field has been left empty.
source-form.source-description: Results from selected source data source have links displayed in the panel
source-form.source-description_description: Informational text in the Source Form within the Correlations section: Describes that the results from the selected source data source will have links displayed in the panel.
source-form.source-label_description: Label in the source definition form within the Correlations feature: It identifies the field or selection used to specify the origin of data or events for correlation analysis.
source-form.sub-text: Define what data source will display the correlation, and what data will replace previously defined variables.

correlations.sub-title: Define how data living in different data sources relates to each other. Read more in the <2>documentation</2>

target-form.control-rules_description: Validation message for the 'Control Rules' field in the 'Target Form' within the 'Correlations' section. Its purpose is to inform the user that this field is mandatory and must be filled out to proceed.
target-form.sub-text: Define what the correlation will link to. With the query type, a query will run when the correlation is clicked. With the external type, clicking the correlation will open a URL.
target-form.target-description-external: Specify the URL that will open when the link is clicked
target-form.target-description-external_description: Description/Instruction in Correlations Target Form: Prompts the user to specify the URL that an external link will open when clicked. This defines the destination for a correlation link.
target-form.target-description-query: Specify which data source is queried when the link is clicked
target-form.target-description-query_description: Instruction text for a data source selection field in the target configuration form within the Correlations feature: It guides the user to specify which data source should be queried when the associated correlation link is activated.
target-form.target-label_description: Label in the Target form within the Correlations section: Identifies the input field for defining the object or entity to be correlated. This typically refers to a metric, service, or other data point.
target-form.target-type-description_description: Instructional text in the 'target form' within the 'correlations' section: This prompts the user to specify the type of correlation being configured. It likely accompanies a selection field where the correlation type is chosen.
target-form.title: Setup the target for the correlation (Step 2 of 3)
target-form.title_description: Title on a form or page within the Correlations feature: This title guides the user through the second step of a three-step wizard for setting up the target for a correlation.
target-form.type-label_description: Label in a form for defining targets within the correlations section: It prompts the user to specify the type or category of the target being configured.

trans-details.logfmt-description: Parse provided field with logfmt to get variables
trans-details.logfmt-description_description: Explanatory text in the Transaction Details section within the Correlations feature: This text describes the functionality to parse a specified log field using the `logfmt` format to extract variables. It enables users to structure raw log data for correlation and analysis within transaction details.
trans-details.logfmt-label_description: Label in the Transaction Details section of the Correlations feature: This identifies a display or input field related to the "logfmt" logging format.
trans-details.regex-description: Field will be parsed with regex. Use named capture groups to return multiple variables, or a single unnamed capture group to add variable to named map value. Regex is case insensitive.
trans-details.regex-expression: Use capture groups to extract a portion of the field.
trans-details.regex-expression_description: Guidance message in a Regular Expression input field within the Correlation's Transaction Details section: It instructs the user on how to use capture groups in their regular expression to extract a specific portion of the field's value.
trans-details.regex-label_description: Label in the Transaction Details section of the Correlations feature: This label identifies a field where users can input or view a regular expression.
trans-details.regex-map-values: Defines the name of the variable if the capture group is not named.
trans-details.regex-map-values_description: Description text in the 'Regex Map Values' section within the 'Transformation Details' of the 'Correlations' feature: This text describes the rule for automatically naming variables when a regular expression capture group is not explicitly named. It clarifies the system's default behavior for variable assignment in such cases.

transform.add-button_description: Button in the 'Transform' section of the Correlations feature: Allows users to add a new transformation to the correlation definition.
transform.heading: Transformations
transform.heading_description: Heading in the Transform section of Correlations: Identifies the area where users define or manage data transformations.
transform.no-transform_description: Message in the 'Transform' section of the 'Correlations' feature: It indicates that no data transformations have been configured or applied. This text is displayed when the list of transformations is empty.

transform-row.expression-label_description: Label in the Correlation Transform Row configuration: It identifies an input field where users define a data transformation expression.
transform-row.expression-required_description: Validation message in the 'Transform Row' configuration within the Correlations feature: This message appears when a required expression has not been defined, prompting the user to provide one.
transform-row.expression-tooltip: Required for regular expression. The expression the transformation will use. Logfmt does not use further specifications.
transform-row.field-input_description: Label in the 'Transform Row' section within the 'Correlations' feature: This label identifies an input where a data field or column name is specified for transformation or other operations.
transform-row.field-label_description: Label in the 'Transform Row' configuration within the 'Correlations' section: This label identifies an input or selection where the user specifies a data field to apply a transformation.
transform-row.field-tooltip: Optional. The field to transform. If not specified, the transformation will be applied to the results field.
transform-row.map-value-label_description: Label in the Transform Row configuration for correlations: This label denotes a field where users define the target value for a data transformation. It specifies what an original data value will be mapped to during the correlation process.
transform-row.map-value-tooltip: Optional. Defines the name of the variable. This is currently only valid for regular expressions with a single, unnamed capture group.
transform-row.remove-tooltip_description: Tooltip in a transformation row within the Correlations section: Explains the action of removing a data transformation.
transform-row.transform-required_description: Instructional message in the transformation configuration section within the Correlations feature: It prompts the user to select a specific data transformation type, appearing when no selection has been made yet.
transform-row.type-label_description: Label in the Transform Row configuration section within the Correlations feature: It identifies a control where users select or view the type of transformation applied to a row.
transform-row.type-tooltip: The type of transformation that will be applied to the source data.
transform-row.type-tooltip_description: Tooltip on the 'Type' field in a transformation row within the Correlations configuration: It explains the specific kind of transformation that will be applied to the source data.

variable-editor-form.run-query_description: Button in the variable editor form: Executes the query defined for the dashboard variable. This allows users to preview its results or update its values.

actions.open-settings_description: Menu item in the dashboard's actions menu: Opens the configuration settings for the current dashboard.

add-library-panel-widget.aria-label-close-add-panel-widget_descri: Close button in the 'Add Panel' widget: Closes the widget used to add new panels to a dashboard. This is an ARIA label, providing an accessible description for the close control.
add-library-panel-widget.tooltip-close-widget_description: Tooltip text in the 'Add library panel widget' interface: Describes the action of closing the widget that is currently being added or configured.

add-menu.import_description: Menu Item in the "Add" menu on a dashboard: This option allows users to import pre-existing dashboards or panels from a shared library.
add-menu.paste-panel_description: Menu item in the add-menu within the dashboard: Allows the user to paste a previously copied panel onto the current dashboard.
add-menu.row_description: Menu item in the 'Add' menu of a dashboard: Used to insert a new row into the dashboard layout, which acts as a container or separator for organizing panels.
add-menu.visualization_description: Menu item in the Add menu within a dashboard: Allows users to add a new visualization panel to the dashboard.

alert-rules-drawer.redirect-link_description: Link in the alert rules drawer of a dashboard: Navigates the user to the complete list of alert rules within the native Grafana Alerting interface.
alert-rules-drawer.subtitle: Alert rules related to this dashboard
alert-rules-drawer.subtitle_description: Subtitle in the alert rules drawer of a dashboard: It introduces the list of alert rules that are associated with the currently viewed dashboard.

annotation-settings-edit.apply_description: Button in the Annotation Settings Edit dialog: Confirms and applies the changes made to the annotation settings.
annotation-settings-edit.description-color-annotation-event-marke: Color to use for the annotation event markers
Color selection field in the Annotation Settings editor within a dashboard: Sets the color for the event markers associated with annotations.
annotation-settings-edit.description-enabled-annotation-query-iss: When enabled the annotation query is issued every dashboard refresh
Description text in the Annotation Settings edit screen of a dashboard: Informs the user that when the associated setting is enabled, the annotation query will execute with every dashboard refresh.
annotation-settings-edit.description-hidden: Annotation queries can be toggled on or off at the top of the dashboard. With this option checked this toggle will be hidden.
annotation-settings-edit.label-color_description: Label in the dashboard's annotation settings editor: Indicates the field or control for selecting the color of an annotation.
annotation-settings-edit.label-data-source_description: Label in the Annotation Settings Edit screen: Identifies the field where a user selects or views the data source for annotations. This specifies which data source to use when configuring or displaying annotations.
annotation-settings-edit.label-enabled_description: Label in the Annotation Settings Edit section of a Dashboard: Indicates whether annotations are currently enabled or disabled. This label is typically associated with a checkbox or toggle switch.
annotation-settings-edit.label-hidden_description: Label in the Annotation Settings Edit section of a Dashboard: This label represents the 'hidden' state for an annotation, meaning it is not visible.
annotation-settings-edit.label-show-in_description: Label in the Annotation Settings editor within a dashboard: This label introduces options for selecting where an annotation should be displayed.
annotation-settings-edit.placeholder-choose-panels_description: Placeholder text in the Dashboard's Annotation Settings Edit screen: It prompts the user to select which panels an annotation should be associated with or displayed on.
annotation-settings-edit.preview-in-dashboard_description: Button in the annotation settings edit panel: Allows the user to preview how the configured annotation will look directly on the dashboard.
annotation-settings-edit.query_description: **Section heading** in the **annotation settings edit screen within a dashboard**: It introduces the area where the user defines the data source query to retrieve events or data points that will be displayed as annotations.
annotation-settings-edit.title-annotation-support-source: No annotation support for this data source
annotation-settings-edit.title-annotation-support-source_descript: Informational Message in the Annotation Settings Edit section of a Dashboard: Informs the user that the selected data source lacks support for annotations. This message explains why annotation configuration options might be unavailable for that data source.

annotation-settings-list.aria-label-delete_description: Accessibility label for a delete button in the annotation settings list of a dashboard: It describes the action of deleting a specific annotation query, using its title for clarity. This label is primarily for screen reader users.
annotation-settings-list.built-in_description: Label for an annotation setting in the annotation settings list of a dashboard. It indicates that the specific annotation is a pre-defined, system-provided option that cannot be modified by the user.
annotation-settings-list.data-source_description: Label in the annotation settings list within a dashboard: Identifies the data source from which annotation data is retrieved or stored.
annotation-settings-list.disabled_description: List item label in the annotation settings list on a dashboard: It indicates that a specific annotation, identified by `{{name}}`, is currently disabled. Note that the placement of '(Disabled)' may vary by language.
annotation-settings-list.new-query_description: Button in the Annotation Settings list within a dashboard: Allows the user to add a new query definition for dashboard annotations.
annotation-settings-list.query-name_description: Label in the Annotation Settings List within a Dashboard: This field allows users to specify or view the name of a query being used for annotations.
annotation-settings-list.tooltip-move-down_description: Tooltip text in the annotation settings list within a dashboard: Indicates the action of moving a selected item to a lower position in the list order.
annotation-settings-list.tooltip-move-up_description: Tooltip in an annotation settings list within a dashboard: Indicates that clicking the associated element will reorder the selected annotation setting higher in the list.

auto-grid.description: Panels resize to fit and form uniform grids
auto-grid.description_description: Description for the Auto Grid setting in a dashboard: This text explains that panels will automatically resize to fit and form uniform grids.

repeat.title: Repeat options
repeat.title_description: Title in the repeat options for an item in an auto-grid on a dashboard: This title introduces settings for configuring how the selected dashboard item should repeat.

variable.description: Repeat this panel for each value in the selected variable. This is not visible while in edit mode. You need to go back to dashboard and then update the variable or reload the dashboard.
variable.title: Repeat by variable
variable.title_description: Title in the repeat options for an item within an auto-grid dashboard layout: This title labels the functionality that allows a dashboard item (e.g., a panel) to be duplicated for each value of a selected variable, enabling dynamic content generation.

auto-grid.name: Auto grid
auto-grid.name_description: Name in the Dashboard auto-grid feature: Identifies the 'Auto grid' layout option, which automatically arranges elements on a dashboard.

custom-min-height.clear_description: Button in the Custom Minimum Height options for the Auto Grid in a Dashboard: Used to remove or reset the configured custom minimum height value.

custom-min-width.clear_description: Button in the custom minimum width settings for auto-grid options within a dashboard: Clears any custom minimum width value that has been set. This action typically reverts the minimum width to its default or automatic behavior.

options.height-fill_description: Option in auto-grid settings on a dashboard: This allows the user to configure the auto-grid to expand and fill the available screen height.
options.max-columns_description: Setting label in Auto-grid options within a dashboard: Specifies the maximum number of columns the auto-grid layout can utilize. This controls the horizontal arrangement of items within the grid.
options.min-height_description: Label for an input field in the Auto Grid Options section of a dashboard: Defines the minimum height for rows within the auto-grid layout.
options.min-height-custom_description: Setting label in Dashboard auto-grid options: Allows users to specify a custom height for rows within the auto-grid layout.
options.min-height-error: A number between 50 and 2000 is required
options.min-height-error_description: Error message in the Auto Grid options within a dashboard: This message validates the input for the minimum height setting. It informs the user that the value must be between 50 and 2000.
options.min-width_description: Label in the dashboard auto-grid options: Defines the minimum width for columns within an auto-grid layout. This setting ensures columns do not shrink below a specified size.
