options.min-width-custom_description: Label in the auto-grid options within a dashboard: This setting allows users to specify a custom minimum width for elements or columns displayed in the auto-grid. It provides control over the layout's minimum sizing.
options.min-width-custom-clear: Back to standard min column width
options.min-width-custom-clear_description: Button or link in the Auto-grid Options within a Dashboard: Resets a previously configured custom minimum column width back to the standard or default setting.
options.min-width-error: A number between 50 and 2000 is required
options.min-width-error_description: Error message in the auto-grid layout options for a dashboard: It informs the user that the entered value for a minimum width setting must be between 50 and 2000.

canvas-actions.add-panel_description: Button in Dashboard canvas actions: Allows the user to add a new data visualization or content panel to the dashboard.
canvas-actions.disabled-nested-grouping_description: Informational message in Dashboard Canvas Actions: This message informs the user that grouping operations are limited to a maximum of two nested levels. It explains why deeper grouping options may be unavailable or disabled.
canvas-actions.disabled-nested-tabs: Tabs cannot be nested inside other tabs
canvas-actions.disabled-nested-tabs_description: Rule Description in Dashboard Canvas Actions: Explains a structural constraint preventing tabs from being nested within other tabs on the dashboard canvas. This ensures a clear and manageable dashboard layout.
canvas-actions.group-into-row_description: Action in Dashboard Canvas Actions: This allows users to group multiple selected visual elements or widgets on the dashboard canvas into a single horizontal row. It is used for organizing the layout and presentation of dashboard content.
canvas-actions.group-into-tab_description: Action in dashboard canvas actions menu: Allows users to group multiple selected dashboard panels or widgets into a new or existing tab for organizational purposes.
canvas-actions.group-panels_description: Action in the Canvas Actions menu: Enables users to group selected panels on the dashboard canvas. This allows for unified manipulation, such as moving or resizing, of the grouped panels.
canvas-actions.new-row_description: Button or menu item in the dashboard canvas actions: Used to add a new row to the dashboard layout.
canvas-actions.new-tab_description: Action button in the dashboard canvas actions menu: Allows users to create a new tab on the dashboard, typically for organizing different sets of panels or views.
canvas-actions.paste-panel_description: Menu item in the dashboard's canvas actions: Allows users to insert a previously copied or cut panel onto the dashboard.
canvas-actions.paste-row_description: Menu item in the dashboard canvas actions: Allows users to paste a previously copied or cut dashboard row onto the canvas.
canvas-actions.paste-tab_description: Menu item in the dashboard canvas actions: Allows users to paste a previously copied dashboard tab into the current dashboard.
canvas-actions.un-group-panels_description: Action in Dashboard Canvas Actions: Allows users to separate panels that were previously grouped together on the dashboard.
canvas-actions.ungroup-rows_description: Action in the Canvas Actions menu of a dashboard: Reverts the grouping of rows within a visual element on the dashboard canvas. This action is the counterpart to 'Group rows'.
canvas-actions.ungroup-tabs_description: Button or menu item in Dashboard canvas actions: Allows users to separate previously grouped tabs.

data.disable_description: Label in the data conditions for conditional rendering within a dashboard: It represents a condition indicating that no data is available. This condition likely leads to disabling a UI element when met.
data.enable_description: Label for a toggle or checkbox in the data conditions section of conditional rendering settings: This label indicates an option to enable a condition that checks if data exists.
data.info: Show or hide the {{type}} based on query results.
data.info_description: Informational text in the 'Data' condition type settings under Conditional Rendering: It describes that the configured conditional rule will show or hide a specified dashboard element based on data query results. The `{{type}}` placeholder will be replaced by the name of the element being controlled (e.g., panel, row).
data.label: Query result
data.label_description: Label in Dashboard Conditional Rendering conditions data settings: It identifies an option to use the outcome of a data query as the basis for a conditional rendering rule.

add.button_description: Button in the Conditional Rendering conditions group within a dashboard: Allows the user to add a new conditional rendering rule to control element visibility or behavior.
add.data_description: Option label in the 'Add condition' section within Conditional Rendering settings of a Dashboard: This option allows users to define a conditional rendering rule based on the outcome of a data query.
add.time-range-size_description: Label or option in the 'Add Condition' section for conditional rendering in a dashboard. This string defines a condition based on the duration of the current dashboard time range, specifically when it is less than a user-defined value.
add.variable_description: Option in the 'Add' section for defining conditions within a conditional rendering group in a dashboard: Allows users to select or specify a template variable for use in the condition.

condition.all_description: Option in a condition group for conditional rendering: Specifies that all conditions within that group must be met for the group to evaluate as true. This defines an 'AND' logical relationship between grouped conditions.
condition.any_description: Selection option in Conditional Rendering settings within a dashboard, specifically for a condition group: This option determines how multiple conditions within a group are evaluated. When selected, the group condition is considered true if at least one of its defined sub-conditions is met.
condition.label: Match rules
condition.label_description: **Label** in **Conditional Rendering settings within a dashboard**: This label identifies the section where users define the rules that must be met for content to be conditionally displayed or hidden. It specifies the criteria for matching conditions.

visibility.hide_description: Option in the conditional rendering visibility settings for a group: Defines the action to hide a group of elements when specified conditions are met.
visibility.label: {{type}} visibility
visibility.label_description: Label in the Conditional Rendering settings for a group's visibility within a dashboard. This string describes the visibility setting for a specific type of element, with `{{type}}` replaced dynamically (e.g., 'Row', 'Panel').
visibility.show_description: Label in conditional rendering group visibility settings: This label indicates that the associated group of dashboard elements will be displayed when the configured conditions are met.

time-range-size.info: Show or hide the {{type}} if the dashboard time range is shorter than the selected time frame.
time-range-size.info_description: Description in Dashboard's Conditional Rendering settings for Time Range Size condition: This text explains the logic for showing or hiding a dashboard element (represented by `{{type}}`) if the dashboard's current time range is shorter than a configured time frame.
time-range-size.invalid-message_description: Validation message in the time range size condition settings for conditional rendering: It appears when the user provides an invalid interval value, such as an incorrectly formatted duration or an out-of-range number.
time-range-size.label: Time range less than
time-range-size.label_description: Label in the dashboard's conditional rendering settings, within the time range size condition configuration: This label introduces an input field where the user specifies a maximum time duration. The condition will be met if the dashboard's currently selected time range is shorter than the specified duration.

values.days_description: Display format in Conditional Rendering condition configuration for time range size: This string is used to present a numeric duration in days. The `{{value}}` placeholder will be replaced by the actual number of days.
values.hour_description: Unit label in dashboard conditional rendering conditions: Specifies 'hour' as a unit when defining a time range size condition. This string is displayed with a numerical input or as a selection option.
values.hours_description: Time unit label in Conditional Rendering settings for time range size on a Dashboard: This label indicates 'hours' as the unit for a specified time range duration. The `{{value}}` placeholder will be replaced by a numeric value.
values.minutes_description: Time unit label in Conditional Rendering settings for a Dashboard: Specifies the 'minutes' unit for a numerical value defining the size of a time range. This is used when configuring conditions based on time range duration.
values.months_description: Value display format in Dashboard Conditional Rendering conditions for time range size: This string is used to specify or display a time duration in months, typically for conditional logic based on time range size.
values.year_description: Value for a time range unit in Conditional Rendering conditions: Represents 'year' as a unit when specifying the size of a time range. This string is displayed with a preceding numerical value, such as '5 years'.
values.years_description: Duration unit option in Conditional Rendering settings: This string is used to define a time range size condition in years within a dashboard. The `{{value}}` placeholder will be replaced by a numeric value.

error.invalid-regex_description: Error message in the conditional rendering configuration for a dashboard variable: Informs the user that the regular expression entered for a variable's condition is syntactically incorrect.

variable.info: Show or hide the {{type}} dynamically based on the variable value.
variable.info_description: Help text in the Conditional Rendering settings for variable-based conditions: This explains that the visibility of a dashboard element (e.g., a panel or row, represented by the `{{type}}` placeholder) can be dynamically controlled based on the current value of a dashboard variable.
variable.label: Template variable
variable.label_description: Label in Dashboard Conditional Rendering conditions: Identifies a template variable that can be selected or referenced when defining a condition for rendering dashboard elements dynamically.
variable.name: Name

operator.equals_description: Operator option in the variable condition settings for conditional rendering within a dashboard: Defines that a variable's value must be equal to a specified value.
operator.matches_description: Operator option in the conditional rendering conditions for a dashboard variable: This option defines a comparison where the variable's value must match a specified pattern or value.
operator.not-equals_description: Operator option in the conditional rendering settings for a dashboard variable: It is used to specify a condition where the variable's value should not be equal to a given value.
operator.not-matches_description: Operator option in the conditional rendering settings for a dashboard variable: This option defines a condition where a variable's value does not match a specified pattern or value. It is used to apply actions (like showing or hiding elements) when a negative match occurs.

variable.value_description: Label 'Value' in conditional rendering variable conditions: Indicates the field for specifying the exact value a dashboard variable must have to activate a conditional rendering rule.

wrapper.delete-condition_description: Button in a condition wrapper within the Conditional Rendering section of a dashboard: This button allows users to remove a specific conditional rendering rule from the dashboard.
wrapper.unsupported-object-type: This condition is not supported by the element, hence it will be ignored.
wrapper.unsupported-object-type_description: Conditional Rendering Condition in the Dashboard's Conditional Rendering settings for a wrapper element: Informs the user that a configured conditional rendering condition is not compatible with the selected dashboard element. The system will ignore this unsupported condition.

editor.info: Set rules to control {{type}} visibility by matching any or all rules.
editor.info_description: Instructional text in Conditional Rendering Editor (Dashboard): This text explains how to set rules for controlling the visibility of various dashboard element types. It clarifies that rules can be configured to match any or all specified conditions.
editor.learn-more_description: Link in the Conditional Rendering Editor within a dashboard: Allows users to access additional documentation or help regarding conditional rendering.
editor.not-supported-for-custom-grid: Show/hide rules aren't supported for panels in the Custom panel layout. Change the panel layout to Auto grid to enable this feature.
editor.unsupported-object-type: Conditional rendering not supported for this item type
editor.unsupported-object-type_description: Informational message in the Conditional Rendering editor within a Dashboard: Informs the user that the selected dashboard item type does not support conditional rendering. This message guides users by indicating limitations when configuring conditional visibility for certain dashboard elements.

overlay.tooltip: Element is hidden by show/hide rules.
overlay.tooltip_description: **Tooltip in a Dashboard's Conditional Rendering Overlay**: Informs the user that a dashboard element is currently hidden due to the application of show/hide rules.

root.title: Show / hide rules
root.title_description: Title in the Conditional Rendering section of a dashboard: This title introduces the area where users can define and manage rules for showing or hiding dashboard elements based on conditions.

confirm-plugin-dashboard-save-modal.body-plugin-dashboard:
Your changes will be lost when you update the plugin.
<2>Use Save As to create custom version.
confirm-plugin-dashboard-save-modal.overwrite_description: Button label in the 'Confirm Plugin Dashboard Save' modal: This button allows the user to confirm replacing an existing plugin dashboard with the current version being saved. It is displayed when a dashboard with the same identifier already exists.
confirm-plugin-dashboard-save-modal.title-plugin-dashboard_description: Title in the 'Confirm Plugin Dashboard Save' modal: Serves as the main title of the modal, indicating that the operation pertains to a dashboard provided by a plugin.

menu.aria-label_description: ARIA label for a menu control in the dashboard: Provides an accessible name for the menu element for screen readers. The empty English value indicates the label is likely dynamically generated or a placeholder.
menu.title_description: Title in a menu within the controls section of a dashboard: It serves to label or identify the menu's purpose or content. The empty value indicates it might be dynamically populated or an internal identifier.

confirmText.proceed_description: Button label in a confirmation dialog displayed when a user attempts to open an original dashboard snapshot. It allows the user to confirm and proceed with loading the original version of the dashboard.

title.proceed-to-external-site_description: Confirmation dialog title in a dialog displayed when a user attempts to navigate to an external site from a dashboard snapshot or original resource. It prompts the user to confirm their action of leaving the current application.

render-left-actions.tooltip-view-as-scene_description: Tooltip in the left actions area of the dashboard navigation: This tooltip describes an action button that allows users to view the current dashboard in a 'Scene' display mode. It appears when hovering over the corresponding button or icon.

dashboard-links-menu.aria-label-dashboard-name_description: ARIA label in the dashboard links menu: Provides an accessible name for a link or menu item that navigates to a specific dashboard. It helps screen reader users identify which dashboard the link points to.
dashboard-links-menu.label-no-dashboards-found_description: Label in the Dashboard Links Menu: This message informs the user that no dashboards were found or are available for display within the menu. It serves as an empty state message when a list of dashboards is expected.

dashboard-loading.cancel-loading-dashboard_description: Button in the dashboard loading screen: Allows users to interrupt the process of a dashboard being loaded. This is useful if the loading is taking too long or was initiated by mistake.

text.not-found_description: Error message in a Dashboard Page: Informs the user that the requested dashboard or its content could not be found.

title.dashboard-init-failed_description: Error title displayed as the title of a dashboard or dashboard section: Informs the user that the dashboard or a component within it failed to initialize or load correctly.

message.edit-dashboard-json_description: Menu item label on a dashboard's settings or management page: Prompts the user to open an interface for directly editing the raw JSON configuration of the current dashboard. This feature is typically used by advanced users for fine-tuning or troubleshooting.

dashboard-validation.body-dashboard-failed-schema-validation: Validation is provided for development purposes and should be safe to ignore. If you are a Grafana developer, consider checking and updating the dashboard schema
dashboard-validation.title-checking-dashboard-validity_descri: Title in a dashboard validation process: It indicates to the user that the system is currently checking the validity of the dashboard.
dashboard-validation.title-dashboard-failed-schema-validation: Title in a Dashboard Validation dialog or alert: Informs the user that the dashboard's structure does not conform to the expected schema. This message indicates a failure during the validation process, preventing the dashboard from being saved or used correctly.
dashboard-validation.title-error-checking-dashboard-validity_: **Title** in **dashboard validation**: This text indicates that an error occurred while the system was attempting to check the validity of the dashboard. It typically serves as a heading for an error message or a status indicator.

default-layout.description: Position and size each panel individually
default-layout.description_description: Description text in the 'Default Layout' section of a dashboard: Explains that panels can be individually positioned and sized within this layout.

direction.horizontal_description: Direction option in repeat settings for a dashboard item: Specifies that dynamically repeated panels or rows will be arranged horizontally across the dashboard.
direction.title: Repeat direction
direction.title_description: Label in the Repeat options for a dashboard item: Specifies the layout orientation for dynamically repeated elements. This controls whether duplicated items are arranged horizontally or vertically.
direction.vertical_description: Option in the repeat options for dashboard panels: Defines the vertical orientation for arranging repeated dashboard panels.

repeat.max_description: Setting label in the 'Repeat' options for a dashboard item's layout: This specifies the maximum number of instances of a repeated item that will be displayed horizontally in a single row.
repeat.title: Repeat options
repeat.title_description: Section title in the 'Repeat options' settings for a dashboard panel: This title introduces controls for configuring dynamic repetition of the panel. These options allow the panel to be duplicated based on variable values.

variable.description: Repeat this panel for each value in the selected variable. This is not visible while in edit mode. You need to go back to dashboard and then update the variable or reload the dashboard.
variable.title: Repeat by variable
variable.title_description: Title for a setting in the 'Repeat options' for a dashboard item. This setting allows users to duplicate a dashboard panel or row for each value of a selected dashboard variable.

default-layout.name: Custom
default-layout.name_description: Name in the Default Layout section of a Dashboard: Identifies a user-defined or custom layout configuration. This allows users to create their own unique dashboard arrangement.

row-actions.delete_description: Button in the row actions menu within a dashboard's default layout: Allows users to delete a specific structural row. This removes a layout element (e.g., a section containing panels) from the dashboard.

modal.alt-action_description: Button or link in a modal dialog: This option allows the user to delete only the selected row within the dashboard's default layout. It specifies deleting just the row, likely distinguishing it from other deletion options that might include associated content or panels.
modal.text: Are you sure you want to remove this row and all its panels?
modal.text_description: Confirmation message in a modal dialog for row actions within a dashboard's default layout: It asks the user to confirm the irreversible removal of a selected row and all its associated panels.
modal.title: Delete row
modal.title_description: Modal title in the dashboard's default layout, for row actions: Indicates that the associated modal dialog is for deleting a row. This title appears at the top of a confirmation or action dialog.

button.label: Row options
button.label_description: Button in the default layout of a dashboard: Opens settings and configuration options for a specific row. This allows users to manage row-specific properties.

repeat-for.label: Repeat for
repeat-for.label_description: Label in the Row Options form for a dashboard's default layout: This label introduces the setting to dynamically repeat a dashboard row. It is typically followed by a selection field for the variable or data source to repeat for.
repeat-for.learn-more_description: Link in the 'Repeat for' section of the row options form on a dashboard: This link provides users with additional information or documentation about the 'Repeat for' functionality for dashboard rows.

warning.text: Panels in this row use the {{SHARED_DASHBOARD_QUERY}} data source. These panels will reference the panel in the original row, not the ones in the repeated rows.

modal.title: Row options
modal.title_description: Title of a modal dialog in the dashboard's default layout: It labels the dialog where users configure settings or properties for a specific dashboard row.

repeat.title: Repeat options
repeat.title_description: **Title** in **the 'Repeat' section of a dashboard row's options**: This title introduces the settings available for configuring how a dashboard row can be repeated dynamically. This feature allows for generating multiple instances of a row based on variables or query results.

variable.title: Variable
variable.title_description: Label in the 'Repeat' section of Row Options for a dashboard row: Designates the input field for selecting a variable. This variable is used to dynamically repeat the row based on its values.

row-options.title: Row options
row-options.title_description: Title in the "Row options" section of a dashboard's default layout: Labels the settings or configurations available for a dashboard row.

description.action_description: Action in the dashboard description area: Allows users to initiate the modification of the current dashboard's descriptive text.

render-label.tooltip-remove-property_description: Tooltip in the Dynamic Configuration Value Editor's property rendering area: It describes the action of removing a property, appearing when the user hovers over an interactive element that triggers this action.

edit-actions.add_description: Action label in the 'Edit Actions' section of a dashboard: Allows users to add a new item of a specified type. The `{{typeName}}` placeholder is dynamically replaced by the actual type of item being added (e.g., 'panel', 'row', 'variable').
edit-actions.add-conditional-rule_description: Button in the 'Edit Actions' section of a dashboard: Allows users to add a new rule of a specific type to a dashboard element. The `{{ruleDescription}}` placeholder will be replaced by the actual type of rule being added (e.g., 'conditional formatting').
edit-actions.duplicate-panel_description: Menu item or button in the dashboard's edit actions menu: Allows users to create an exact copy of the currently selected panel within the dashboard. This action helps quickly reproduce a panel with all its configurations.
edit-actions.edit-query-result-rule_description: Menu item in the 'Edit Actions' menu on a dashboard: Allows users to modify the rule that dictates how the results of a query are handled or interpreted.
edit-actions.edit-template-variable-rule_description: Menu item in the dashboard's edit actions menu: Allows users to modify the rules configured for a template variable. This option enables customization of variable behavior or data sourcing.
edit-actions.edit-time-range-rule_description: Menu item or button in the 'Edit actions' section of a dashboard: Used to modify the configured rule that determines the dashboard's time range.
edit-actions.move_description: Menu item in the 'Edit actions' menu of a dashboard: Used to change the position or location of a specific dashboard element. `{{typeName}}` is a placeholder for the type of element (e.g., panel, row) being moved.
edit-actions.panel-background_description: Menu option in the edit actions menu for a dashboard panel: Allows users to customize the background style (e.g., color, image) of the selected panel.
edit-actions.panel-description_description: Action within the dashboard's 'Edit actions' menu: Allows users to modify the descriptive text associated with a dashboard panel.
edit-actions.panel-edit_description: Label in the Edit actions section of a dashboard: This string signifies actions or information related to modifications made to dashboard panels.
edit-actions.panel-max-repeats-per-row_description: Setting label in the Dashboard's 'Edit Actions' section: This field allows users to specify the maximum number of times a repeating panel or element can be displayed horizontally in a single row. It controls the layout and wrapping of panels generated by template variables.
edit-actions.panel-repeat-direction_description: Setting label in panel edit actions: This setting determines the layout direction for dynamically repeated dashboard panels. It controls whether panels are arranged horizontally or vertically when a repeat-for variable is used.
edit-actions.panel-repeat-variable_description: Label for a setting in the panel edit actions of a dashboard. This allows users to configure a panel to be repeated dynamically for each value of a chosen variable.
edit-actions.panel-title_description: Menu item in the dashboard's edit actions menu: Allows the user to modify the title of a selected dashboard panel.
edit-actions.paste-panel_description: Action in the 'Edit actions' menu on a dashboard: Allows users to paste a previously copied dashboard panel onto the current dashboard.
edit-actions.remove_description: Button or menu item in the dashboard's edit actions: Allows the user to remove a specific type of dashboard element. The `{{typeName}}` placeholder is dynamically replaced by the name of the item being removed (e.g., "Panel", "Row").
edit-actions.row-title_description: Action item in the edit actions menu for a dashboard row: Allows users to change the title of the selected row.
edit-actions.switch-layout_description: Button in dashboard edit actions: Allows users to change the overall arrangement and design of the dashboard.
edit-actions.tab-title_description: Menu item in the 'edit actions' section of a dashboard: Allows users to modify the title of a dashboard tab.

elements.dashboard_description: Label in the dashboard's edit pane: Identifies the main dashboard entity or serves as a heading for its global configuration options.
elements.element_description: Dashboard Element in the 'Elements' section of the dashboard edit pane: This refers to an individual component that can be added, configured, or manipulated on the dashboard.
elements.local-variable_description: A Local variable in the edit pane of a dashboard: Refers to a variable whose scope is limited to the specific dashboard panel or element it is defined within, enabling dynamic configuration.
elements.multiple-elements_description: Label/Header in the dashboard edit pane, within the elements section: Indicates that multiple dashboard elements are currently selected or being managed simultaneously.
elements.multiple-elements-delete-text: Are you sure you want to delete these elements?
elements.multiple-elements-delete-text_descriptio: Confirmation message in the Dashboard Edit Pane: Asks for user confirmation before deleting multiple selected elements from the dashboard.
elements.multiple-panels_description: Selection indicator in the dashboard edit pane's elements section: It signifies that multiple dashboard panels are currently selected. This state often enables bulk operations or displays grouped properties.
elements.multiple-panels-delete-text: Are you sure you want to delete these panels? All queries will be removed.
elements.multiple-panels-delete-text_description: Confirmation message in the Dashboard edit pane: Prompts the user to confirm the deletion of multiple selected panels, explicitly stating that all associated queries will be removed.
elements.objects_description: The label 'Objects' in the 'Elements' section of the dashboard's 'Edit Pane': It categorizes and provides access to various configurable components or entities that can be added to the dashboard.
elements.panel_description: Panel in the dashboard edit pane: Refers to a customizable display component that visualizes data. This is an option for adding elements to a dashboard.
elements.panels_description: Panels in the 'Elements' section of the dashboard edit pane: Represents the individual display units (e.g., charts, tables, text) that compose the dashboard. Users interact with this section to add, configure, or arrange these components.
elements.row_description: A 'Row' element in the dashboard's edit pane: Used to organize and group dashboard panels or other elements for layout purposes. It acts as a horizontal container within the dashboard structure.
elements.rows_description: UI element in the dashboard edit pane: Refers to the structural components used to organize and group dashboard panels. They act as containers for other dashboard elements within the layout.
elements.tab_description: Tab element in the 'elements' section of the dashboard's edit pane: This refers to a UI component that allows users to organize dashboard content into distinct sections or views.
elements.tabs_description: Label "Tabs" in the dashboard edit pane's elements section: This refers to a configurable dashboard element that allows users to organize and display other dashboard content within multiple, selectable tabs.
elements.variable_description: Variable in the dashboard edit pane's elements section: Labels a variable by its specific type. The `{{type}}` placeholder is replaced with the actual variable kind (e.g., "Text variable", "Query variable").
elements.variable-set_description: Variables section in the dashboard's edit pane: Where users define and manage dynamic variables for filtering or customizing dashboard content.

edit-pane.open_description: Button in the Edit Pane of a Dashboard: Opens an additional pane to reveal configuration options or settings for the dashboard element being edited.

header.hide_description: Action in the header of a row within the dashboard edit pane: Hides the row's header.
header.title: Row header
header.title_description: Label for a row header in the dashboard edit pane: This text identifies the header of a dashboard row within the dashboard's editing interface.

custom-options.modal-title_description: Modal Title in the custom options configuration for a dashboard variable: It names the dialog where users define or edit a custom variable.
custom-options.values_description: Instruction text for an input field in the custom options section of a variable within the dashboard edit pane: It clarifies the required format for entering multiple values by separating them with commas.

datasource-options.name-filter_description: Label for an input field in the 'Datasource options' section when editing a dashboard variable: It enables users to filter a list of available data sources or related entities by their name.
datasource-options.name-filter-description: Regex filter for which data source instances to include. Leave empty for all.
datasource-options.name-filter-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Data Source Options section for a variable in the dashboard edit pane: This example (`/^prod/`) guides users on how to filter data source names using regular expressions. It clarifies the expected input format for the name filter field.
datasource-options.type_description: Label in the data source options section for a variable in the dashboard edit pane: Identifies the selection or input field for choosing the type of data source.
datasource-options.type-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the data source options for a variable in the dashboard edit pane: It prompts the user to select the specific type of data source for the variable being configured.

variable.description-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the description input field for a variable within the dashboard edit pane: It prompts the user to enter a descriptive text for the variable.
variable.label: Label
variable.label_description: A label field in the variable configuration area within the dashboard's edit pane: This identifies the display name for a dashboard variable.
variable.label-description_description: Text label in the variable configuration section of the Dashboard's edit pane: This describes an input field where users can define an optional, user-friendly display name for a dashboard variable.
variable.name: Name
variable.open-editor_description: Button in the variable section of the dashboard edit pane: Used to launch the dedicated editor for creating, modifying, or managing dashboard variables.
variable.open-editor-tooltip: For more variable options open variable editor
variable.open-editor-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the variable section of the Dashboard Edit Pane: This tooltip indicates that opening the dedicated variable editor will provide access to additional options for managing variables.

query-options.modal-title_description: Modal Title in the Query Options modal for a Variable in the Dashboard Edit Pane: This title identifies the modal where users configure or edit a variable that retrieves its values via a query.
query-options.preview_description: Button within the 'Query Options' section of a variable's configuration in the Dashboard Edit Pane: Allows the user to view the results or output of the configured query for the variable.

selection-options.allow-custom-values_description: Checkbox label in the variable selection options within the dashboard edit pane: This option controls whether users can enter values for a dashboard variable that are not present in its predefined list of choices.
selection-options.allow-custom-values-description_descript: Description text for the 'Allow custom values' setting, found within the 'Selection Options' for a dashboard variable in the dashboard edit pane. It clarifies that this setting enables users to manually input values for the variable.
selection-options.category_description: Section heading in the dashboard variable editor: This label introduces the configurable options for how a variable's values are selected and displayed.
selection-options.custom-all-value_description: Setting label in the variable selection options of the Dashboard Edit Pane: This allows users to define a custom string that represents the selection of 'all' values for a variable. This custom string is displayed when the 'All' option is chosen.
selection-options.custom-all-value-description: A wildcard regex or other value to represent All
selection-options.custom-all-value-description_description: Description in variable selection options within the dashboard edit pane: This text explains that a wildcard regex or other custom value can be entered to represent the "All" option for a variable.
selection-options.include-all_description: Setting label in the variable selection options within the dashboard edit pane: This controls whether an 'All' value can be included as a selectable option for a dashboard variable. This 'All' option allows users to apply the variable filter to all possible values.
selection-options.include-all-description: Enables a single option that represent all values
selection-options.include-all-description_description: Description in the 'Selection Options' section when configuring a dashboard variable: This text explains a setting that, when enabled, adds a single 'All' option to the variable's dropdown list, representing all possible values.
selection-options.multi-value_description: Checkbox label in Variable selection options within the dashboard edit pane: Enables the ability for the associated dashboard variable to accept multiple selected values.

variable.type-category_description: Section heading in the variable configuration section of the dashboard's edit pane: It labels the configuration settings available for a specific variable type. The `{{type}}` placeholder dynamically displays the name of the variable type (e.g., "Query", "Text").

variables.add-variable_description: Button in the 'Variables' section within the dashboard's edit pane: Allows users to add a new dashboard variable.
variables.select-type_description: Prompt in the 'Variables' section of the Dashboard Edit Pane: Instructs the user to select the type for a new or existing dashboard variable. This prompt appears before a selection of available variable types (e.g., Query, Custom, Textbox).
variables.select-type-card-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the Variables section of the Dashboard's Edit Pane: This tooltip appears on a UI element (likely a card) to instruct the user to click it to choose a specific type for a variable.
variables.select-variable_description: Action label in the variables section of the dashboard's edit pane: Prompts the user to choose a variable. This string indicates the action of selecting a specific variable for configuration.

empty.add-library-panel-body: Add visualizations that are shared with other dashboards.
empty.add-library-panel-body_description: Body text in an empty dashboard state: Describes the purpose of adding library panels, which are visualizations shared across multiple dashboards, prompting the user to add them.
empty.add-library-panel-button_description: Button in an empty dashboard screen: Allows users to add a pre-configured panel from the shared library.
empty.add-library-panel-header_description: Header in the initial view of an empty dashboard: It serves as a title for the section or option allowing users to import an existing panel, possibly from a library.
empty.add-visualization-body: Select a data source and then query and visualize your data with charts, stats and tables or create lists, markdowns and other widgets.
empty.add-visualization-button_description: Button on an empty dashboard: Prompts the user to add a new visualization to begin populating the dashboard. This is likely the primary call to action when a dashboard has no content yet.
empty.add-visualization-header: Start your new dashboard by adding a visualization
empty.add-visualization-header_description: Header in an empty dashboard screen: Guides users to begin populating their new dashboard by adding a visualization.
empty.import-a-dashboard-header_description: Header on the empty dashboard screen: This text serves as a prominent title, prompting users to import a dashboard.
empty.import-dashboard-button_description: Button in an empty dashboard state: Allows users to initiate the process of importing an existing dashboard.
empty.show-less-dashboards_description: Button in a dashboard list view: Used to collapse an expanded list of dashboards, showing fewer dashboards. This option typically appears after a 'Show more' action has been performed, reverting the display to a more concise or default view.
empty.show-more-dashboards_description: Link in the empty dashboard view: To navigate to a page where users can discover or create additional dashboards.
empty.start-with-suggested-dashboards: Start with a pre-made dashboard from your data source
empty.start-with-suggested-dashboards_descript: Button in Empty Dashboard screen: Allows users to quickly create a new dashboard by selecting a pre-made template. These templates are suggested based on the user's connected data sources.
empty.use-template-button_description: Button in an Empty Dashboard View: Allows users to adopt or activate a displayed dashboard template. This button is presented when a dashboard is empty, offering a quick way to populate it.

errors.failed-to-load_description: Error message displayed when attempting to load a dashboard: It notifies the user that the dashboard could not be retrieved or rendered due to an underlying issue.

button.label: Export dashboard
button.label_description: Button within the export functionality of a dashboard: Initiates the process to export the current dashboard.

menu.label: Export dashboard menu
menu.label_description: Menu label in the dashboard's export functionality: This identifies the menu containing options to export the current dashboard.

message.failed-to-fetch-dashboard_description: Error Message in the dashboard loading sequence: Informs the user that the system was unable to retrieve the requested dashboard. This message indicates a failure during the dashboard fetching operation.

gen-aihistory.aria-label-send-custom-feedback_descript: Button in the Generative AI History section of a dashboard: Allows users to submit personalized feedback about the generative AI features. This string serves as an ARIA label for accessibility.
gen-aihistory.footer-text: This content is AI-generated using the <2>Grafana LLM plugin</2>
gen-aihistory.footer-text_description: Footer text in the Gen-AI History section of a dashboard: Informs the user that the displayed content was generated by AI using the specified Grafana LLM plugin. This serves as an attribution and disclosure statement for the AI-generated content.
gen-aihistory.placeholder-tell-ai-what-to-do-next: Tell AI what to do next...

label.editable_description: Label in the dashboard's general settings, under 'editable options': This text indicates that a particular dashboard element or setting is capable of being modified.
label.readonly_description: Label in the 'Editable Options' section within the 'General Settings (Unconnected)' of a dashboard: This label identifies an option that, when selected, sets the dashboard or its associated settings to a read-only state, preventing edits.

label.default_description: Label for an option in graph tooltip settings within dashboard general settings: This option allows users to set or revert the display behavior of graph tooltips to their default configuration.
label.shared-crosshair_description: Setting label in the graph tooltip options within dashboard general settings: Enables or disables a shared crosshair feature, which displays a synchronized vertical line across all graphs when hovering to facilitate data comparison.
label.shared-tooltip_description: Label in the 'Graph tooltip options' section of the Dashboard's General Settings: This option controls whether a single tooltip is displayed for multiple data series or graphs upon hover.

title.annotations_description: Title in the Debug Dashboard: Labels the section or feature dedicated to displaying or managing annotations, which are markers or notes on a timeline or graph.

message.error-loading-data_description: Error message on a dashboard: Notifies the user that the system encountered an issue and was unable to load data for display.

get-field-override-categories.label-add-field-override_description: Button in the dashboard's field override categories section: Initiates the process of adding a new override for a data field.
get-field-override-categories.label-add-override-property_description: Button or link in the field override categories section within a dashboard: It allows users to add a new property for customizing or overriding field settings.
get-field-override-categories.override-name_description: Label in the Field Override Categories section in BMC Helix Dashboards: This serves as the title or identifier for an individual field override configuration. The `{{overrideNum}}` placeholder is dynamically replaced by a sequential number to distinguish multiple overrides.

title.add-button_description: Button in the title area of the 'Get Field Override Categories' interface: Allows users to add a new field override category or a related configuration.

title.library-panel-options_description: Section Title in the Dashboard's library panel configuration: This title labels the category or group of settings related to managing reusable library panels.

title.information_description: Category Title in the Library Panel Options: Identifies a section for general information or metadata related to the library panel. This category likely groups descriptive details or settings.
title.name: Name

title.library-panel-options_description: Title in the Dashboard: This label identifies a category or section dedicated to configuring various settings and options for panels sourced from the library.

title.information_description: Category Title in the visual panel library options category on a dashboard: It labels a section containing general information or details about the selected visual panel.
title.name: Name

text.alert_description: A tab label in the dashboard panel editor: This tab provides access to settings for configuring and managing alerts associated with the panel.
text.query_description: Tab label in Panel Editor tabs: This tab allows users to define the data source and specific queries (e.g., SQL, PromQL) that power the visualizations within a dashboard panel.
text.transform-data_description: Tab in the panel editor: This tab allows users to configure transformations and manipulations for the data displayed within a dashboard panel.

description.annotation-following-panels: Do not send annotation data to the following panels
description.annotation-following-panels_description: Setting description in the 'Get Panel Filters' configuration of a dashboard: This text describes an option that prevents annotation data from being sent to a subsequent selection of panels. It controls the sharing of annotation information between dashboard panels.
description.annotation-panels-support-annotations: Send the annotation data to all panels that support annotations
description.annotation-panels-support-annotations_de: Description text in the Dashboard's panel filter configuration: It explains an option to distribute annotation data to all panels within the dashboard that are configured to display annotations.
description.annotations-explicitly-listed-panels: Send the annotations to the explicitly listed panels
description.annotations-explicitly-listed-panels_des: Description text in the Dashboard's panel filter or annotation settings: This string explains an option where annotations are specifically directed and displayed only on panels that have been explicitly designated by the user. This ensures targeted annotation delivery.

label.all-panels_description: Label in a filter application setting for dashboard panels: This option allows the user to select all panels on a dashboard as the target for a filter or action.
label.all-panels-except_description: Label in a dashboard's panel filtering configuration: This option indicates a selection method to apply a filter to all panels except specific ones chosen by the user.
label.selected-panels_description: Label in a dashboard's panel filter configuration interface: It identifies the group of dashboard panels that have been chosen or are currently selected for actions related to filtering.

title.panel-options_description: Section title in the panel configuration interface: This title introduces the available settings and configurations for a specific dashboard panel.

label.horizontal_description: Label in the direction options for a panel frame category: Represents the choice for horizontal alignment or direction when configuring a panel's display.
label.vertical_description: Label in Panel Direction Options: This string represents the "Vertical" choice for a setting that controls the direction or orientation of elements within a panel frame category in a dashboard.

title.max-per-row_description: Configuration option in the 'Title' settings of a 'Panel Frame Category' within a dashboard: Specifies the maximum number of items or panels to display horizontally on a single row.
title.panel-links_description: Category title in a dashboard panel's configuration settings: This identifies the section where users manage links associated with the panel.
title.repeat-by-variable_description: An option in the panel configuration settings: This allows a dashboard panel to be dynamically duplicated for each value of a selected variable.
title.repeat-direction_description: **Setting label** in **Dashboard Panel Configuration**: This label allows users to specify the layout direction for repeated panels or elements. It determines whether dynamically generated panel instances are arranged horizontally or vertically.
title.repeat-options_description: **Title** in **panel configuration settings**: This title introduces options for configuring the dynamic repetition of dashboard panels based on selected variables or data.
title.title: Title
title.transparent-background_description: A checkbox or toggle option in the dashboard panel's title settings: Allows users to set the background of the panel title as transparent.

text.extensions_description: Menu item in a dashboard panel's menu: This option allows users to access or manage additional functionalities or integrations for the specific panel.

help-wizard.copy-to-clipboard_description: Button in the Help Wizard of a Dashboard: Copies information, such as text or a link, to the user's clipboard for easy pasting elsewhere.
help-wizard.description-obfuscate-data: Modify the original data to hide sensitve information.  Note the lengths will stay the same, and duplicate values will be equal.
help-wizard.description-support-snapshot_description: Description text in the Help Wizard's support snapshot feature within a Dashboard: Identifies a specific panel using its title. The `{{panelTitle}}` is a placeholder for the actual panel name.
help-wizard.download-snapshot_description: Button in the Help Wizard within a dashboard: Allows the user to download a dashboard snapshot. The displayed `{{snapshotSize}}` indicates the size of the file to be downloaded.
help-wizard.label-obfuscate-data_description: A label in the help wizard of a dashboard: It marks an option or control for obfuscating (masking or hiding) sensitive data, typically to enhance data privacy.
help-wizard.label-support-snapshot_description: Label in the Help Wizard of a dashboard: Identifies a feature that generates a snapshot of the environment for troubleshooting and support assistance.
help-wizard.label-template_description: Label in the dashboard's help wizard: Identifies an option or section for selecting or creating from a pre-defined template.
help-wizard.randomize-field-names-label-field-names_: Label in the 'Randomize Field Names' section of the Help Wizard on a Dashboard: This identifies the input field or display area for field names that are to be randomized or have been randomized.
help-wizard.randomize-labels-label-labels_descriptio: Label in the Dashboard Help Wizard: This string identifies a section, category, or input field related to managing or randomizing 'Labels'.
help-wizard.randomize-string-values-label-string-val: Label in the Help Wizard's 'Randomize String Values' feature: This label identifies the input field or section where users define the string values to be randomized.

label.data_description: Tab label in the Help Wizard of a dashboard: Identifies a section that guides the user through configuring or managing the data sources and settings for their dashboard.
label.snapshot_description: A tab label in the Help Wizard of a dashboard: This tab is used to access or manage dashboard snapshots.

help-wizard.title-complete-git-hub-comment-clipboard: Copy a complete GitHub comment to the clipboard
Instruction title in the Help Wizard within the Dashboard: Guides the user to copy a complete GitHub comment to their clipboard, likely as a step in a setup or configuration workflow.
help-wizard.title-get-help-with-this-panel_descripti: Title in the Help Wizard within a Dashboard: Indicates that the user is about to receive help or guidance specifically for the currently viewed dashboard panel.
help-wizard.troubleshooting-docs_description: Link in the Help wizard of a BMC Helix Dashboard: Directs users to documentation for troubleshooting common issues.

message.failed-create-dashboard-model_descriptio: Error message during the dashboard initialization process: Informs the user that the system failed to create the dashboard's underlying data model or configuration. This usually indicates a technical issue.

inspect.data-tab_description: A tab in the "Inspect" view of a dashboard: Displays the underlying data for the currently selected panel or visualization.
inspect.error-tab_description: Tab title in the Inspect panel of a dashboard: This tab displays error information or logs related to the inspected dashboard panel or query.
inspect.meta-tab_description: Tab label in the Inspect section of a dashboard: This tab displays metadata or properties related to the dashboard itself, a specific panel, or its underlying data source.
inspect.query-tab_description: Tab in the Inspect panel within a dashboard: Allows users to view and analyze the data queries associated with a dashboard panel.
inspect.stats-tab_description: Tab in the Inspect section of a dashboard: This tab is used to display statistical information. It provides metrics and numerical data about the dashboard or a selected panel.
inspect.subtitle: {{queryCount}} queries with total query time of {{formatted}}
inspect.subtitle_description: Status label in the 'Inspect' view of a Dashboard: Summarizes the total number of queries executed and their combined execution time. It provides performance context for the dashboard being inspected.
inspect.title: Inspect: {{panelTitle}}
inspect.title_description: Title in the Inspect view of a dashboard: Identifies the specific dashboard panel currently being inspected. The `{{panelTitle}}` placeholder is dynamically replaced by the title of the panel.

inspect-data.data-options_description: Section heading in the 'Inspect Data' view of a dashboard: Allows users to configure settings related to data processing and display for the inspected data.
inspect-data.dataframe-aria-label_description: Accessible label for a control in the 'Inspect Data' section of a dashboard: Guides users to select a specific dataframe for detailed analysis. A dataframe represents a structured data set, typically tabular.
inspect-data.dataframe-label_description: Button label in the 'Inspect Data' panel of a dashboard: Toggles the visibility of the raw tabular data (data frame) associated with a dashboard panel.
inspect-data.download-csv_description: Button in the "Inspect Data" view of a dashboard: Allows users to download the currently displayed data in CSV format.
inspect-data.download-logs_description: Button in the Inspect Data section of a dashboard: Allows the user to download log files. These logs typically provide details for troubleshooting data-related issues or inspecting dashboard operations.
inspect-data.download-service_description: Button in the Inspect Data view of a dashboard: Triggers the download of a service-specific graph. This graph typically visualizes aspects like service performance, dependencies, or topology.
inspect-data.download-traces_description: Action in the Inspect Data section of a dashboard: Allows users to download detailed trace data. This function exports performance or execution path information for further analysis.
inspect-data.excel-compatibility-mode-description: Generates a CSV file that's compatible with most Excel versions
inspect-data.excel-compatibility-mode-description_des: Description in the Inspect data section of a dashboard: This text explains the purpose of an Excel compatibility mode option. It clarifies that this option generates a CSV file that works with most Excel versions.
inspect-data.excel-compatibility-mode-label_descripti: Button in the Inspect Data section of a dashboard: Triggers the download of the currently inspected data, formatted specifically for compatibility with Microsoft Excel.
inspect-data.formatted_description: Toggle option in the Inspect Data panel: Allows users to display dashboard data in a human-readable, formatted representation, contrasting with its raw, underlying values.
inspect-data.formatted-data-description: Table data is formatted with options defined in the Field and Override tabs.
inspect-data.formatted-data-description_description: Informational text in the "Inspect Data" view of a dashboard: Explains how the displayed table data is formatted. It clarifies that these formatting options are defined in the "Field" and "Override" tabs.
inspect-data.formatted-data-label_description: Label in the Inspect Data section of a dashboard: Indicates a view or section displaying data after it has been processed and presented according to the dashboard's display formatting rules.
inspect-data.panel-transforms_description: Section title in the 'Inspect Data' view of a dashboard: This section allows users to apply various data transformation operations to the data of a specific panel. These transformations modify the raw data before it is visualized.
inspect-data.series-to-columns_description: Option label in the Inspect Data feature of a Dashboard: This option indicates that data series are aligned and presented based on their common time points. It defines how multiple series are displayed when converted into columns for inspection.
inspect-data.transformation_description: Transformation type in the Inspect Data section of a dashboard: This option describes a data transformation method where multiple data series are combined or aligned based on their time values. It is used to link and display series along a common timeline.
inspect-data.transformations-description: Table data is displayed with transformations defined in the panel Transform tab.
inspect-data.transformations-description_description: Informational text in the "Inspect Data" section of a dashboard: This text explains that the table data currently being viewed has already been processed by the transformations configured in the panel's "Transform" tab.
inspect-data.transformations-label_description: Section heading in the 'Inspect Data' section of a dashboard: This heading introduces the area where users can configure and apply various operations to transform the data displayed in the current panel.

inspect-json.dataframe-description: Raw data without transformations and field config applied.
inspect-json.dataframe-description_description: Descriptive text in the 'Inspect JSON' view of a dashboard: This text describes that the associated data frame shows the raw data as it was received, before any dashboard transformations or field configurations were applied.
inspect-json.dataframe-label_description: Label in the 'Inspect JSON' view of a dashboard: This text identifies a section displaying the raw JSON data of a DataFrame, specifically as it was returned by the underlying data query.
inspect-json.panel-data-description: The raw model passed to the panel visualization
inspect-json.panel-data-description_description: Description in the 'Inspect JSON' view of a dashboard: This text explains that the displayed content represents the raw data model that is directly consumed by the panel's visualization component.
inspect-json.panel-data-label_description: Label in the "Inspect JSON" view of a dashboard: Identifies the section displaying the raw JSON data associated with a specific dashboard panel. This allows users to examine the underlying data structure powering the panel.
inspect-json.panel-json-description: The model saved in the dashboard JSON that configures how everything works.
inspect-json.panel-json-description_description: Description in the 'Inspect JSON' view of a dashboard panel: This text explains that the displayed JSON is the underlying configuration model that dictates how the panel functions.
inspect-json.panel-json-label_description: Label in the 'Inspect JSON' section of a dashboard: Identifies the JSON configuration data for a selected panel.
inspect-json.select-source_description: Label or button in the Inspect JSON view within a dashboard: Allows the user to choose the data source or specific component whose JSON data will be displayed for inspection.
inspect-json.unknown_description: Error message in the Dashboard JSON inspector: This message is displayed when an object within the dashboard's underlying JSON structure is not recognized or has an unexpected format. The `{{show}}` placeholder will be replaced with details about the unknown object.

inspect-meta.no-inspector_description: Message in the Inspect Metadata panel of a dashboard: Informs the user that the metadata inspector tool is not available or active.

inspect-stats.data-title_description: Title in the Inspect Stats section of a dashboard: Identifies a section displaying statistics about data sources.
inspect-stats.data-traceids_description: Header or label in the 'Inspect Stats' section of a dashboard: This text identifies unique identifiers used for tracking distributed operations or requests. It helps users trace specific events or transactions within the system.
inspect-stats.processing-time_description: Label in the Inspect Stats section of a dashboard: Indicates the time required for data processing. This metric helps users assess dashboard performance.
inspect-stats.queries_description: Text label in the 'Inspect Stats' section of a dashboard: Indicates the total number of data queries executed. This statistic helps users understand the query load or performance of the dashboard.
inspect-stats.request-time_description: Text label in the "Inspect stats" section of a dashboard: Displays the total duration of requests made.
inspect-stats.rows_description: Label in the 'Inspect Stats' section of a dashboard: This indicates the total count of data rows returned or processed within the inspected data. It provides a summary metric of the dataset's volume.
inspect-stats.table-title_description: Table title in the 'Inspect Stats' section of a dashboard: Labels a table that displays statistical data or metrics. This table provides detailed information for analysis.

title.deleted_description: Status label in a Kubernetes API dashboard title: Indicates that a specific Kubernetes resource or item has been deleted or is no longer available.
Status indicator in a Kubernetes Dashboard (v2api), likely associated with a resource's title or header. It signifies that the corresponding resource or entity has been removed.

common.copy_description: Button or menu item in common dashboard layout elements: Allows users to duplicate a selected dashboard component, panel, or other configurable item.
common.copy-or-duplicate_description: Action label in the dashboard's common layout management options: Provides the functionality to create an identical copy or duplicate of a selected dashboard element (e.g., a panel, row, or the entire dashboard).
common.duplicate_description: Action label within a dashboard's common layout options: Provides an option to create a duplicate of an existing dashboard element.
common.group-layout_description: UI label in the common layout settings of a dashboard: This label refers to the setting or option for defining how groups of dashboard components or panels are visually arranged. It controls the organization of grouped elements within the dashboard layout.
common.group-layout-disabled: No groups exists on this level
common.group-layout-disabled_description: Informational message in a dashboard's layout configuration or display: Informs the user that no groups have been defined or are present at the current level. This message explains why group-related layout options or displays might appear empty or inactive.
common.panel-layout_description: Section heading or label in the common layout settings for a dashboard: It identifies the area where users can define or modify the arrangement and positioning of panels on the dashboard.
common.panel-layout-disabled: Select a row or tab to change panel layout options
common.panel-layout-disabled_description: Instructional text in the dashboard's layout options: Informs the user that panel layout options are currently disabled and prompts them to select a specific row or tab to enable and modify these settings. This message appears when no row or tab is active.

layout.continue_description: Button in BMC Helix Dashboards Layout: This button allows the user to proceed to the next step or confirm changes within a dashboard configuration wizard or layout editor.
layout.ungroup-nested-text: This will ungroup all nested groups.
layout.ungroup-nested-text_description: Action description text in the dashboard layout editor: This text informs the user that executing the associated action will dismantle all nested groups within the dashboard's layout. This means any groups containing other groups will be flattened.
layout.ungroup-nested-title_description: Confirmation Dialog Title in Dashboard Layout settings: Prompts the user to confirm the ungrouping of nested groups within the dashboard's layout. This action flattens the hierarchy of grouped elements.

message.dashboard-not-found_description: Error message in Dashboard UI: Informs the user that the specified dashboard could not be found, typically during a fetch operation using the legacy API.

make-editable.dashboard-not-editable_description: Status message on a dashboard or within an edit-related panel: Informs the user that the current dashboard cannot be modified. This typically indicates insufficient permissions or that the dashboard is in a read-only state.
make-editable.make-editable_description: Button or Menu Item in a dashboard's interface: Changes the dashboard's state from view-only to editable. This allows users to modify its content or layout.

minimalistic-pagination.page-count_description: Pagination indicator in the minimalistic pagination component of a dashboard: Displays the current page number relative to the total number of available pages. This helps users understand their position within a paginated list or data set.

new-dashboard-with-ds.not-found: Data source with UID "{{datasourceUid}}" not found.
new-dashboard-with-ds.not-found_description: Error message in the 'New Dashboard with Data Source' creation process: Informs the user that a specified data source, identified by its UID, could not be found. This message typically appears when the system fails to locate or access the intended data source.

new-panel.configure-button_description: Button in the new panel creation workflow within a dashboard: Allows the user to open the configuration settings for the newly added panel.
new-panel.configure-button-menu_description: Menu item in a configuration menu for a new panel on a dashboard: Used to show or hide an associated sub-menu or options panel.
new-panel.menu-open-panel-editor_description: Menu item in the new panel menu within a dashboard: Allows the user to open the panel editor to configure the new panel's settings and content.
new-panel.menu-use-library-panel_description: Menu item in the New Panel menu on a dashboard: Allows the user to add a pre-configured panel from the panel library to the current dashboard.

dashboard.new-panel-title_description: Title in the dashboard interface: Appears as a heading or a default name when a user is adding or configuring a new panel to a dashboard.

title.row-title_description: Label in the 'Create New Row' interface within the Dashboard: This text identifies the input field where the user specifies the title for the new row being created.

title.widget-title_description: Label in the 'New Panel' for creating a widget: Guides the user to enter a title for the widget being created.

options-pane-category.aria-label-collapse_description: Aria-label for a collapse button in a dashboard's options pane category. It provides an accessibility description for a control that, when activated, hides the contents of the specified `{{title}}` category.
options-pane-category.aria-label-expand_description: ARIA label for an interactive element in a category section within the dashboard's options pane: This label describes the action of expanding a specific category to reveal its content or sub-options. The `{{title}}` placeholder is replaced by the category's name.

options-pane-options.placeholder-search-options_description: Placeholder text in a search input field within the dashboard's options pane: Guides the user to search for available dashboard options.
options-pane-options.Recent options-title-recent-options_desc: Title in the Options pane of a dashboard: This title heads a section that lists recently used options. It provides quick access to previously selected settings or configurations.
options-pane-options.title-matched_one_description: A **status label** in the **options pane of a dashboard**: It displays the number of options that currently match a specified criterion (e.g., a filter or search) compared to the total number of available options.
options-pane-options.title-matched_other_description: Label in the options pane within a dashboard: Indicates how many options satisfy a current condition (e.g., filtering, search) out of the total available options. This provides a summary of the current selection or filtered results.

outline.repeated-item_description: Option in the Dashboard Outline: Configures a dashboard item (such as a panel or row) to be repeated dynamically based on a variable.

tree-item.empty_description: Label in a tree item within the dashboard outline: This placeholder indicates that the specific item's content, value, or children are currently empty or undefined.
tree-item.no-title_description: Placeholder text in the Dashboard Outline tree view: This text is displayed for a tree item when it does not have a defined title. It indicates that the item's title is missing.

override-category-title.aria-label-remove-override_description: Button in the category title override section of a dashboard: Removes a custom or overridden title, restoring the default category title. This is an accessibility label.
override-category-title.tooltip-remove-override_description: Tooltip for an interactive element in the Dashboard's category title override section: Indicates that clicking this element will remove an existing override applied to a category title.

time-range-settings.hide-time-info_description: Setting in the time range settings for a dashboard panel: Allows users to hide the visual display of the time range configured for that specific panel.
time-range-settings.hide-time-info-description: Do not show the panel time range in the panel header
time-range-settings.hide-time-info-description_description: Descriptive text for a setting in the time range configuration for a dashboard panel: Explains an option that, when enabled, prevents the panel's specific time range from being displayed in its header.
time-range-settings.time-from_description: Label in the time range settings for a dashboard panel: Identifies the option to configure the data's time period specific to this panel. This allows a panel to display data from a different time range than the overall dashboard.
time-range-settings.time-from-description: Overrides the dashboard time range. Use one of the preset values or enter a custom value like 5m or 2h.
time-range-settings.time-shift_description: Configuration option in Time Range Settings for a Dashboard Panel: Configures an offset to the time range displayed, typically used for comparing current data with past data.
time-range-settings.time-shift-description: Adds a time shift relative to the dashboard or panel time range. Use one of the preset values or enter a custom value like 5m or 2h.
time-range-settings.time-window-compare_description: Feature label in the Time Range Settings within a dashboard panel: Indicates the option to enable and configure comparison of data across different time windows. This functionality allows users to analyze trends by comparing current data with a previous period.
time-range-settings.time-window-compare-description: Compare data between two time ranges
time-range-settings.time-window-compare-description_descript: Explanatory text in the time-range settings section of a dashboard panel: Describes the function of comparing data between two specified time ranges.
time-range-settings.title: Panel time settings
time-range-settings.title_description: Title in the time range settings for a dashboard panel: This title identifies the configuration area where users can adjust time-related parameters specific to a dashboard panel.

alerting-tab.dashboard-not-saved: Dashboard must be saved before alerts can be added.
alerting-tab.dashboard-not-saved_description: Warning message in the Alerting tab within the panel editor: Informs the user that the dashboard must be saved before they can add or configure alerts for a panel. This message appears to prevent setting up alerts on an unsaved dashboard configuration.
alerting-tab.no-rules: There are no alert rules linked to this panel.
alerting-tab.no-rules_description: Informational message in the Alerting tab of the Panel Edit settings: This text is displayed when there are no alert rules configured or linked to the specific dashboard panel being edited. It informs the user about the absence of alerts for that panel.

panel-edit.only-overrides-button-tooltip_descriptio: Tooltip on a button within the Panel Edit section of a Dashboard: Informs the user that clicking the button will filter the view to show only the panel's overridden settings.
panel-edit.placeholder-search-options_description: Placeholder text in a search input field within the panel editing view of a dashboard: Guides the user to search for relevant options or settings.
panel-edit.visualization-button-label_description: Button label in the panel editing interface of a dashboard: This button allows users to access or configure the visualization settings for the current panel. It is used to define how the panel's data is displayed (e.g., chart type, appearance).
panel-edit.visualization-button-tooltip_description: Tooltip on a button in the panel editor: Indicates that the button provides access to search-related options for the visualization.

panel-editor-table-view.title-raw-data_description: Title in the panel editor's table view: It identifies a section or an option to display the underlying, unprocessed data for a panel.

editor-actions.apply_description: Button in the panel editor's actions section within a Dashboard: Applies the configured settings or changes to the panel being edited. This typically updates a panel preview or temporary state before it is fully integrated or saved to a dashboard.
editor-actions.discard_description: Button in the editor actions section of an unconnected panel editor: Allows users to discard unsaved changes made to the panel.
editor-actions.save-library-panel_description: Button in the Panel Editor: Saves the currently configured panel as a reusable library panel. This action is used when creating or modifying a panel independently from a specific dashboard.
editor-actions.title-apply-changes-and-save-dashboard_d: Button label in the Editor Actions section of the Panel Editor for an unconnected panel within BMC Helix Dashboards: This button confirms applying changes made to the panel and saves the entire dashboard. It finalizes modifications from the panel editor and persists them to the overall dashboard.
editor-actions.title-apply-changes-and-save-library-pan: Apply changes and save library panel

Button label in the panel editor's action section for library panels: Allows the user to apply changes made to the panel configuration and save the updated definition to the library. This action is used when managing the library panel definition itself, not an instance connected to a dashboard.
editor-actions.title-apply-changes-dashboard: Apply changes and go back to dashboard
editor-actions.title-apply-changes-dashboard_descriptio: Button in the Panel Editor for an unconnected panel: This button saves the changes made within the panel editor and navigates the user back to the main dashboard view. This action is specific to editing panels that are "unconnected.
editor-actions.title-undo-all-changes_description: Action button in the panel editor's action bar: Reverts all modifications made to the panel during the current editing session.

panel-editor-unconnected.table-view-label-table-view_description: Label in the Panel editor: This label identifies an option to display data within a panel in a table format.
panel-editor-unconnected.title-unlink: Disconnects this panel from the library panel so that you can edit it regularly.
panel-editor-unconnected.title-unlink_description: Title in the Panel Editor: This title indicates an action that will disconnect the current panel from its library source, allowing it to be edited independently within the dashboard.
panel-editor-unconnected.unlink_description: Button in the Panel Editor: Allows the user to break the association of the current panel with a library panel or shared template, making it independent.

panel-links.aria-label-panel-links_description: Accessibility label for the 'Panel links' feature in a dashboard panel: Provides a descriptive label for the group of links associated with a panel, used by assistive technologies.

panel-not-supported.go-back-to-queries_description: Button or link in a dashboard's "Panel Not Supported" view: Allows the user to navigate back to the query editor or list of queries to modify the panel. This action is presented when a dashboard panel cannot be displayed due to an unsupported configuration or type.

public-dashboard-not-available.does-not-exist: The dashboard you are trying to access does not exist
public-dashboard-not-available.does-not-exist_description: Informational message in the public dashboard access interface: Informs the user that the public dashboard they are trying to access does not exist.
