This message is displayed when a user attempts to open a public dashboard via a URL that leads to a non-existent dashboard.
public-dashboard-not-available.paused: This dashboard has been paused by the administrator
public-dashboard-not-available.paused_description: Status message on a public dashboard view: Informs the user that the specific public dashboard they are trying to access has been suspended by an administrator. It explains why the dashboard is currently inaccessible.
public-dashboard-not-available.try-again-later_description: Instructional text or button label on the "Public Dashboard Not Available" screen: Advises the user to try accessing the dashboard again later, indicating a temporary issue.

text.remove-panel: Are you sure you want to remove this panel?
text.remove-panel_description: Confirmation prompt in the dashboard's panel removal workflow: Asks the user to confirm their intent before deleting a selected panel.

title.remove-panel_description: Title in the 'Remove Panel' dialog: Informs the user about the action of removing a dashboard panel.

label.disable-repeating_description: Label in Variable options for Repeat row selection: This option allows users to disable the repeating behavior of dashboard elements (rows or panels) that are driven by variable selections.
label.no-template-variables-found_description: Label in the variable options section of the repeat row selection within a dashboard: Informs the user that no template variables are currently available or found for selection.

repeat-row-select2.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a dashboard's repeat-row select2 dropdown: It instructs the user to choose an item from the list to control the repetition of dashboard rows.

label.disable-repeating_description: Label in the variable options for repeat row configuration within a dashboard: It allows users to disable the dynamic repetition of rows or panels based on variable selections.
label.no-template-variables-found_description: Label in the template variable options section of the Repeat Row Select2 component within a dashboard: It informs the user when no template variables are available or found for selection.

revert-dashboard-modal.body-restore-version: Are you sure you want to restore the dashboard to version {{version}}? All unsaved changes will be lost.
revert-dashboard-modal.confirmText-restore-version_description: Confirmation button text in the Revert Dashboard modal: Confirms the user's decision to restore the current dashboard to a selected previous version. The `{{version}}` placeholder dynamically displays the target version number.
revert-dashboard-modal.title-restore-version_description: Title in the "Revert Dashboard" modal: This is the heading that informs the user they are about to restore a previous version of the dashboard.

row-options-button.aria-label-row-options_description: ARIA label for a button in a dashboard: This label identifies a button that, when clicked, opens a menu with options for a specific dashboard row.

row-options-form.label-repeat-for_description: Label in the row options form of a dashboard: Indicates an option to configure how the current row should be duplicated. This typically allows users to repeat the row for each value of a selected variable.

row-options-modal.title-row-options_description: Title in the Row Options modal on a dashboard: Indicates that the modal contains settings and configuration options for a selected dashboard row.

rows-layout.convert-to_description: Option in the rows layout configuration of a dashboard: Facilitates changing the type of a rows layout element. The `{{name}}` placeholder indicates the new layout type (e.g., section, tab).
rows-layout.delete-row-text: Deleting this row will also remove all panels. Are you sure you want to continue?
rows-layout.delete-row-text_description: Confirmation message in the Dashboard Rows Layout: Warns the user that deleting a dashboard row will also remove all associated panels and prompts for confirmation.
rows-layout.delete-row-title_description: **Dialog title** in the dashboard's row layout management: Prompts the user to confirm their intent to delete a selected row.
rows-layout.description_description: Description of a layout option in the Rows Layout configuration of a dashboard: It explains that this layout allows for collapsible panel groups with headings.

edit.ungroup-rows_description: Action in the row layout editing interface of a dashboard: Separates rows that were previously grouped together. This allows for individual management and reordering of dashboard elements.

rows-layout.header-hidden-tooltip: Row header only visible in edit mode
rows-layout.header-hidden-tooltip_description: Tooltip message in the Dashboard's row layout configuration: It clarifies that row headers are only visible when the dashboard is in edit mode.
rows-layout.name: Rows
rows-layout.name_description: Label in Dashboard layout settings: Identifies a horizontal layout component used to organize and group dashboard panels.

row.collapse_description: Button or link in a row within a dashboard's layout: Allows the user to hide the contents of that specific row, making the dashboard more compact.
row.expand_description: Button in a dashboard row: Allows the user to reveal the full content of a collapsed row. This action toggles the row's state between expanded and collapsed, managing screen space.
row.new_description: Action label in the dashboard's rows layout: Triggers the addition of a new horizontal section to organize content within the dashboard.

repeat.learn-more_description: Link in the 'Repeat' configuration section for a dashboard row: Provides access to documentation or further details about how to use this feature. This helps users understand how to dynamically duplicate rows based on selected variables.
repeat.warning: Panels in this row use the {{SHARED_DASHBOARD_QUERY}} data source. These panels will reference the panel in the original row, not the ones in the repeated rows.

repeat.title: Repeat options
repeat.title_description: Heading in the "Repeat" section under "Row Options" within a dashboard's rows layout: It labels the configuration settings for repeating dashboard rows or their content.

variable.description: Repeat this row for each value in the selected variable.
variable.description_description: Description in the dashboard's row repetition variable selection: Informs the user that the current row will be repeated (duplicated) for every value in the selected variable.
variable.title: Repeat by variable
variable.title_description: Title in the row options of a dashboard: This option enables repeating a dashboard row for each value of a selected variable. It is used to dynamically display content based on variable selections.

row.fill-screen_description: Option in the row options menu for a dashboard row: This setting allows the user to expand the selected row to fill the available screen space for focused viewing.
row.hide-header_description: Setting in row options for a dashboard row: Controls the visibility of the header (title or label) for that specific row.
row.title: Title

row-options.title-not-unique_description: Validation message in the Row Options for a Dashboard layout: This message is displayed when a user attempts to set a row title that is not unique, prompting them to enter a distinct title.

row-warning.title-not-unique_description: Warning message in Dashboard Rows Layout: Informs the user that the entered title for a dashboard row is not unique. This prompt appears when a user attempts to save a row with a title that already exists.

rows-layout.ungroup-convert-text: All grids must be converted to the same type and positions will be lost.
rows-layout.ungroup-convert-text_description: Warning message in Dashboard Rows Layout: Informs the user that converting all grids to a single type will result in the loss of their current positions. This message likely appears when an ungroup or conversion action is initiated for grids.
rows-layout.ungroup-convert-title_description: Confirmation Dialog Title in Dashboard Rows Layout settings: Asks the user to confirm if they want to convert a row layout that contains mixed grids. This prompt appears when a user attempts an action that would modify or simplify such a layout.

save-dashboard.message-dashboard-saved_description: Confirmation message in the dashboard save process: Informs the user that their dashboard has been successfully saved after a save action.

save-dashboard-as-button.save-as_description: Button label in the Save Dashboard As interface: Allows the user to save the current dashboard as a new, separate copy, typically prompting for a new name or location.

save-dashboard-as-form.aria-label-save-dashboard-button_descrip: Button in the 'Save Dashboard As' form: This button saves the dashboard. It serves as an ARIA label for accessibility.
save-dashboard-as-form.aria-label-save-dashboard-description-fi: Aria-label for a text field in the 'Save Dashboard As' form: This label describes an input field where users can enter a description for the dashboard they are saving.
save-dashboard-as-form.aria-label-save-dashboard-title-field_de: Text input field in the 'Save Dashboard As' form: An ARIA label for the field where users enter or edit the title of the dashboard they are saving. This string helps screen readers identify the purpose of the input field.
save-dashboard-as-form.label-copy-tags_description: Label in the 'Save Dashboard As' form: This option allows users to copy existing tags from the original dashboard to the new one being saved. It is typically a checkbox.
save-dashboard-as-form.label-folder_description: Label in the Save Dashboard As form: Indicates the field where the user can select or enter the folder for saving the dashboard.
save-dashboard-as-form.title: Title

save-dashboard-button.save-dashboard_description: Button in the dashboard editor's save functionality: Confirms and saves the current dashboard configuration and any changes made.

save-dashboard-diff.full-json-diff_description: Label in the Save Dashboard Differences functionality: This option allows users to view the complete JSON representation of the differences between the current and previous versions of a dashboard before saving.
save-dashboard-diff.no-changes-in-the-dashboard-json: No changes in the dashboard JSON
save-dashboard-diff.no-changes-in-the-dashboard-json_descrip: Status message in the dashboard editor's save or version comparison dialog: Informs the user that no modifications have been detected in the dashboard's JSON configuration. This message is displayed when the current dashboard state is identical to a reference state (e.g., the last saved version).
save-dashboard-diff.title-because-dashboard-migrated-grafana: The diff is hard to read because the dashboard has been migrated to the new dashboard format

Title in the save dashboard diff screen: Explains to the user why the displayed differences between dashboard versions are difficult to interpret. This specific message is shown when the dashboard has been migrated to the new Grafana dashboard format.
save-dashboard-diff.title-folder_description: Section title in the Save Dashboard Difference dialog: Labels the component for selecting or viewing the dashboard's destination folder.

save-dashboard-drawer.label-changes_description: Label in the Save Dashboard drawer: Identifies a section or option related to the modifications made to the dashboard that are about to be saved.
save-dashboard-drawer.label-details_description: Section title in the Save Dashboard drawer: It identifies the section where users can input or review the primary attributes and settings for the dashboard being saved.
save-dashboard-drawer.title: Save dashboard
save-dashboard-drawer.title_description: Title in the 'Save dashboard' drawer/modal: This text labels the drawer where users configure and confirm saving their dashboard.
save-dashboard-drawer.title-copy_description: Title in the "Save dashboard" drawer: This indicates the action of saving a copy of the current dashboard. It is displayed when the user chooses to duplicate an existing dashboard.
save-dashboard-drawer.title-provisioned_description: Title in the 'Save Dashboard' drawer: This title identifies the dashboard as a provisioned dashboard, indicating it is managed externally rather than through the UI.

save-dashboard-error-proxy.body-name-exists:
A dashboard with the same name in selected folder already exists.
<2>Would you still like to save this dashboard?
save-dashboard-error-proxy.body-version-mismatch:
Someone else has updated this dashboard
<2>Would you still like to save this dashboard?
save-dashboard-error-proxy.body-version-mismatch_description: Message body in Save Dashboard Error dialog: Informs the user that another user has updated the dashboard since it was loaded, and prompts them to confirm if they still wish to save, potentially overwriting newer changes.
save-dashboard-error-proxy.confirmText-save-and-overwrite_descripti: Button label in a confirmation dialog when saving a dashboard: This option allows the user to save the current dashboard by overwriting an existing one. This typically appears when a naming conflict or proxy issue is detected.
save-dashboard-error-proxy.title-name-exists_description: Title in an error dialog when saving a dashboard: Indicates that the save operation failed due to a conflict. This typically occurs when a dashboard with the same name already exists.
save-dashboard-error-proxy.title-version-mismatch_description: Title in the Save Dashboard Error dialog: This title indicates a conflict, specifically a version mismatch, that occurred when trying to save a dashboard.

save-dashboard-form.label-current-range-dashboard-default: Save current time range as dashboard default
save-dashboard-form.label-current-range-dashboard-default_de: Checkbox label in the dashboard save form: This option allows the user to save the time range currently applied to the dashboard as its default setting. When this dashboard is opened later, it will automatically load with this saved default time range.
save-dashboard-form.label-current-variable-values-dashboard-: Save current variable values as dashboard default

Checkbox label in the Save Dashboard Form: This option allows users to save the currently selected values for dashboard variables as the new default. If checked, these values will be pre-applied every time the dashboard is loaded.
save-dashboard-form.no-changes-to-save_description: Message in the Save Dashboard form on a dashboard: Informs the user that no modifications have been made to the dashboard content, so there is nothing to save. It typically appears when a user attempts to save an unchanged dashboard.
save-dashboard-form.placeholder-describe-changes: Add a note to describe your changes.
save-dashboard-form.placeholder-describe-changes_description: Placeholder text in the Save Dashboard form: Guides the user to add a note describing the changes they have made to the dashboard before saving.

save-provisioned-dashboard-form.cannot-be-saved: This dashboard cannot be saved from the BMC Helix Dashboards because it has been provisioned from another source. Copy the JSON or save it to a file below, then you can update your dashboard in the provisioning source.
save-provisioned-dashboard-form.copy-json-to-clipboard_description: Button in the Save Provisioned Dashboard form: This button allows users to copy the dashboard's JSON configuration to their clipboard. This enables easy sharing, backup, or programmatic deployment of the dashboard definition.
save-provisioned-dashboard-form.file-path_description: **Label** in **Save Provisioned Dashboard Form**: Displays the file path where the provisioned dashboard will be saved or is located. The `{{filePath}}` placeholder will be dynamically replaced with the actual path.
save-provisioned-dashboard-form.save-json-to-file_description: Button in the 'Save Provisioned Dashboard' form: Allows the user to download the JSON definition of the current provisioned dashboard to a local file.

search-panel.no-match_description: Status message in the dashboard's search panel: Informs the user that no panels were found matching their search criteria after a search operation.

share-public-dashboard-loader.loading-configuration_description: Status message in the Public Dashboard Share component: Informs the user that the configuration settings required to share a public dashboard are currently being loaded.

solo-panel.loading-initializing-dashboard_descripti: Loading indicator text in a solo panel on a dashboard: Informs the user that the dashboard's content is actively being loaded and initialized. This message appears during the brief period before the panel's data is fully rendered.
solo-panel.title-not-found_description: Error message in the solo panel view within a dashboard: Informs the user that the requested panel could not be found. This typically occurs when a panel ID is invalid or the panel has been removed.

sub-menu-un-connected.aria-label-template-variables_descriptio: ARIA label in a sub-menu within the Dashboard UI: Provides an accessible name for an interactive element (e.g., button or menu item) related to managing or viewing template variables.

description.dashboard-troubleshoot-visualization-iss: Dashboard JSON used to help troubleshoot visualization issues
Description in the Support Snapshot Service: This text explains that the Dashboard JSON is used to help troubleshoot visualization issues within the system.

label.git-hub-comment_description: Label in the Support Snapshot Service section of a dashboard: Identifies a field or display area related to GitHub comments. This field likely allows users to input or view comments associated with GitHub for a specific support snapshot.
label.panel-support-snapshot_description: **Label** in the **Support Snapshot Service of a Dashboard**: This text identifies a feature or section for capturing a diagnostic snapshot of a panel to assist with support or troubleshooting.

title.copy-to-clipboard-failed_description: Title of an error notification in the Dashboard's Support Snapshot Service: Informs the user that an attempt to copy content to the clipboard has failed. This typically appears after a user interaction to copy data.

tabs-layout.delete-tab-text: Deleting this tab will also remove all panels. Are you sure you want to continue?
tabs-layout.delete-tab-text_description: Confirmation message in the Tabs Layout section of a Dashboard: This message warns the user that deleting a tab will also remove all associated panels and prompts for confirmation before proceeding with the action.
tabs-layout.delete-tab-title_description: Confirmation dialog title in the tabs layout within a dashboard: Prompts the user to confirm the deletion of a dashboard tab.
tabs-layout.description_description: Description text in the dashboard's 'Tabs layout' section: This text explains that the tabs layout feature enables users to organize dashboard panels into horizontal tabs.

edit.ungroup-tabs_description: Action in the Dashboard's Tabs Layout Edit section: This action allows users to ungroup previously grouped tabs, restoring them to individual tabs within the dashboard layout.

tabs-layout.name: Tabs
tabs-layout.name_description: Label in dashboard layout settings: This identifies the 'Tabs' layout feature, which organizes dashboard content into distinct, selectable tabs.

tab.new_description: Button in a tabs layout within a dashboard: Allows the user to add a new tab to the dashboard.

repeat.learn-more_description: Link in the configuration settings for a tab's repeat feature: Allows users to access documentation or help content about how to use or configure tab repetition.
repeat.loading_description: Status message in the Dashboard's tab layout: Informs the user that repeated tabs, dynamically generated based on variable values, are currently being loaded.
repeat.warning: Panels in this tab use the {{SHARED_DASHBOARD_QUERY}} data source. These panels will reference the panel in the original tab, not the ones in the repeated tabs.

repeat.title: Repeat options
repeat.title_description: Section title in the tab options panel for a dashboard tab: This title introduces a set of configurations related to repeating the tab's content or the tab itself.

variable.description: Repeat this tab for each value in the selected variable.
variable.description_description: Description text in tab configuration settings for repeating tabs by variable: Describes the functionality of duplicating a dashboard tab for each value of a selected variable.
variable.title: Repeat by variable
variable.title_description: Title in Dashboard Tab Options: This option labels the functionality that allows a dashboard tab to be repeated dynamically based on the selected values of a variable.

tab-options.title-not-unique_description: Validation message in the Tab Options panel within the Dashboard's Tabs Layout: Informs the user that the entered tab title is a duplicate and must be unique. This ensures distinct identification for each dashboard tab.

tab-warning.title-not-unique_description: Warning message in the Tabs Layout of a Dashboard: Informs the user that the title they are attempting to assign to a tab is already in use by another tab, necessitating a unique title.

title.action_description: Action in the dashboard header: Allows the user to modify the title of the current dashboard.

toolbar.alert-rules_description: Button in the dashboard's toolbar: Provides access to the section for managing alert rules.
toolbar.back-to-dashboard_description: Button in the toolbar on a dashboard page: Allows users to navigate back to the main dashboard view, typically after drilling down into a panel or viewing a sub-panel.

dashboard-settings.label: Settings
dashboard-settings.label_description: Button in the toolbar on the dashboard: Opens the settings panel or page for the current dashboard.
dashboard-settings.tooltip_description: Tooltip for the Dashboard settings button in the dashboard toolbar: Informs the user that this button provides access to the settings for the current dashboard.

toolbar.discard-library-panel-changes_descriptio: Button in Dashboard toolbar: Allows users to discard unsaved changes made to a library panel. This option is available when editing a library panel.
toolbar.discard-panel_description: Button in the Dashboard Toolbar: Allows the user to discard any unsaved modifications made to a panel. This action reverts the panel to its previous saved state.
toolbar.discard-panel-new_description: Button in the dashboard toolbar: Allows the user to discard a panel. This action is typically used when a user is creating or editing a panel and wants to cancel changes or remove the panel.

edit.label: Edit
edit.tooltip_description: Tooltip for an 'Edit' button/icon in the dashboard toolbar: Explains that selecting this option will put the dashboard into edit mode, allowing for modifications.

enter-edit-mode.label: Make editable
enter-edit-mode.label_description: Button in the dashboard toolbar: Allows users to switch the dashboard into an editable state, enabling modifications to its content and layout.
enter-edit-mode.tooltip: This dashboard was marked as read only
enter-edit-mode.tooltip_description: Tooltip on the 'Enter Edit Mode' control in the dashboard toolbar: This message informs the user that the dashboard is currently marked as read-only. It explains why the user cannot proceed to edit the dashboard.

exit-edit-mode.label: Exit edit
exit-edit-mode.label_description: Button in the toolbar on a dashboard: Allows the user to exit the current editing mode for the dashboard. This typically returns the user to the standard viewing state.
exit-edit-mode.tooltip: Exits edit mode and discards unsaved changes
exit-edit-mode.tooltip_description: Tooltip for an 'Exit Edit Mode' button in the dashboard toolbar: This describes the action of leaving the dashboard's edit mode, informing the user that any unsaved changes will be discarded.

toolbar.mark-favorite_description: Action item in Dashboard toolbar: Allows the user to mark the currently viewed dashboard as a favorite for easy access.
toolbar.more-save-options_description: Button in the dashboard toolbar: Provides access to additional or advanced options for saving the current dashboard. This typically reveals a dropdown menu with actions like "Save as", "Export", or other sharing/saving functionalities.

new.back-to-dashboard_description: Button or link label in the 'New' section of the dashboard toolbar: Allows users to navigate back to the main dashboard view. This typically appears after an action related to creating or configuring new content.

dashboard-settings.tooltip_description: Tooltip for a UI element (e.g., button, menu item) in the 'New' section of the dashboard toolbar: It indicates that this element allows users to access and configure the settings for the current dashboard.

new.discard-library-panel-changes_descriptio: Menu item in the 'New' menu within the Dashboard toolbar: Allows users to discard unsaved modifications made to a library panel. This action reverts the panel to its last saved state from the library.
new.discard-panel_description: Menu item in the "New" dropdown menu within the dashboard toolbar: Allows users to discard unsaved changes made to a panel that is currently being edited or configured. This action reverts any modifications without saving them.
new.discard-panel-new_description: Button in the dashboard toolbar when adding a new panel: Allows the user to discard the currently configured or unsaved new panel. This action removes the panel from the dashboard.

edit-dashboard-v2-schema.tooltip_description: **Tooltip** for an option found within the 'New' section of the Dashboard toolbar: It describes the action of editing the schema for a version 2 dashboard. This allows users to configure the underlying structure of their dashboard.

enter.label: Enter edit mode
enter.label_description: Button in the Dashboard Toolbar: Allows the user to enter edit mode for the current dashboard. This action enables modification of the dashboard's content and layout.

exit.label: Exit edit mode
exit.label_description: Button in the dashboard toolbar's section for creation/modification actions: Allows the user to exit the dashboard editing mode and return to viewing the dashboard.

enter-edit-mode.label: Make editable
enter-edit-mode.label_description: Menu item or button in the dashboard toolbar, located within a 'New' or related section: This action allows users to switch the current dashboard from view-only mode to an editable state, enabling content and layout modifications.
enter-edit-mode.tooltip: This dashboard was marked as read only
enter-edit-mode.tooltip_description: Tooltip on the 'Enter Edit Mode' button/icon in the dashboard toolbar: It explains that the dashboard is read-only, which prevents the user from entering edit mode.

export.arrow_description: Button in the dashboard toolbar: This button opens a dropdown menu offering various options to export the current dashboard or its data.
export.title: Export
export.title_description: Menu item in the 'New' submenu within the dashboard toolbar: Initiates the export of the current dashboard or its displayed content.

tooltip.as-code_description: Tooltip content for an export option within the 'New' menu on the Dashboard toolbar: Informs the user that the associated action allows exporting the current dashboard's definition in a machine-readable code format (e.g., JSON or YAML).
tooltip.json_description: Tooltip for the 'Export as JSON' option in the dashboard toolbar's 'New' menu: This text describes that selecting this option will export the current dashboard's configuration or data into a JSON file.

new.more-save-options_description: Menu item in the 'New' button's dropdown menu in the dashboard toolbar: This option provides access to advanced or alternative saving functionalities for the current dashboard.
new.playlist-next_description: Button in the 'New' section of the Dashboard Toolbar: Navigates to the next dashboard within an active playlist sequence.
new.playlist-previous_description: Button in the "New" menu within the dashboard toolbar: Allows users to navigate to the previous dashboard in a playlist.
new.playlist-stop_description: Menu item in the 'New' dropdown menu on the dashboard toolbar: Stops the currently active playlist.
new.public-dashboard_description: Menu item in the 'New' submenu of the dashboard toolbar: Allows users to create a new publicly accessible dashboard.

save-dashboard.label: Save
save-dashboard.tooltip_description: Tooltip for the 'Save Dashboard' control, located within a 'New' or similar section of the dashboard toolbar: It indicates that activating this control will save any unsaved changes to the current dashboard. This applies to both newly created dashboards and modifications to existing ones.

save-dashboard-copy.label: Save as copy
save-dashboard-copy.label_description: Button label in the 'New' menu within the dashboard toolbar: Allows users to create a duplicate of the currently open dashboard, saving it independently from the original.
save-dashboard-copy.tooltip_description: Tooltip for the "Save as copy" option within the Dashboard Toolbar's "New" menu: It explains that selecting this option will create a duplicate of the current dashboard.

new.save-library-panel_description: Menu item in the 'New' dropdown menu within the dashboard toolbar: Allows users to save the currently created or edited panel as a reusable library panel.

share.arrow_description: Share dropdown button in the Dashboard toolbar, within the 'new' section: Opens a menu with various options for sharing the current dashboard.
share.title: Share
share.title_description: Button label in the 'New' section of the dashboard toolbar: Indicates an action to share the current dashboard or its content with other users or via a link.
share.tooltip_description: Tooltip for a 'Copy link' action or icon, within the sharing features accessed via a 'New' option in the Dashboard toolbar: It indicates that selecting this option will copy a sharable URL of the current dashboard to the clipboard.

modal.noText_description: Button in the 'Share or Export' modal dialog: Allows the user to cancel the current sharing or exporting operation or to close the modal without saving any changes.
modal.text: You have unsaved changes to this dashboard. You need to save them before you can share it.
modal.title: Save changes to dashboard?
modal.title_description: Modal Title in the Share/Export modal: Asks the user if they want to save unsaved changes to the dashboard. This prompt appears before proceeding with sharing or exporting the dashboard.

new.unlink-library-panel_description: Menu item in the 'New' submenu of the dashboard toolbar: Provides the option to convert a panel that was previously linked from the panel library into a standalone, local panel specific to the current dashboard.

toolbar.open-original_description: Button in the dashboard toolbar: Allows users to open the original, unmodified version of a dashboard. This is typically used when the user is viewing a customized or copied version of a dashboard.
toolbar.playlist-next_description: Button in the dashboard toolbar: Allows the user to manually advance to the next dashboard when viewing a dashboard playlist. This control is part of the playlist navigation features.
toolbar.playlist-previous_description: Button in the Toolbar of the Dashboard: Navigates to the previous dashboard within a configured playlist.
toolbar.playlist-stop_description: Button in the Dashboard Toolbar: Halts the automatic rotation of dashboards that are part of an active playlist.
toolbar.read-only_description: Status indicator in the dashboard toolbar: Indicates that the current dashboard is in a read-only mode, preventing any modifications to its content or configuration.
toolbar.refresh_description: Button in the dashboard toolbar: Reloads the current dashboard's data and display.
toolbar.save_description: Button in the dashboard toolbar: Allows the user to save the current dashboard and any changes made to it.

save-dashboard.label: Save dashboard
save-dashboard.label_description: Button label in the dashboard toolbar: This label identifies the button used to save the current dashboard configuration and any changes made to it.
save-dashboard.tooltip_description: Tooltip for a button in the dashboard toolbar: Describes the action of saving any modifications made to the current dashboard.

save-dashboard-copy.label: Save as copy
save-dashboard-copy.label_description: Button label in the dashboard toolbar: Allows users to save the currently viewed or edited dashboard as a new, independent copy. This is typically used to create a duplicate without altering the original.
save-dashboard-copy.tooltip_description: Tooltip in the dashboard toolbar: Appears when hovering over the "Save as copy" button. It indicates that clicking the button will save the current dashboard as a new, distinct copy without affecting the original.

toolbar.save-library-panel_description: Button in the dashboard toolbar: Saves the currently selected panel to the library, making it available for reuse across multiple dashboards.
toolbar.settings_description: Button on the dashboard toolbar: Allows users to access and modify the current dashboard's settings.
toolbar.share-button_description: Button in the toolbar on a dashboard: Allows users to share the dashboard or its content with others.
toolbar.star-add-error_description: Error message in Dashboard Toolbar: Informs the user that the action to add a dashboard to their starred collection was unsuccessful. This indicates a failure when trying to mark a dashboard as a favorite.
toolbar.star-added_description: Notification message in the dashboard toolbar: Confirms that the current dashboard has been successfully added to the user's starred list.
toolbar.star-remove-error_description: Error message in the Dashboard toolbar: Informs the user that an attempt to remove a dashboard from their starred list was unsuccessful. This message would typically appear as a transient notification.
toolbar.star-removed_description: Notification in the dashboard toolbar: Confirms that the dashboard has been removed from the user's starred items.
toolbar.unlink-library-panel_description: Button/Menu item in the Dashboard toolbar: Allows users to disconnect a dashboard panel from its linked library panel definition. This action converts the panel into a standalone entity within the dashboard, meaning it will no longer inherit updates from the library panel.
toolbar.unmark-favorite_description: Button on the dashboard toolbar: Allows the user to remove the currently viewed dashboard from their personal list of favorite dashboards.

transformation-editor.input-data_description: Section header in the transformation editor within a dashboard: Identifies the section displaying or configuring the data that serves as input for the transformation. This is the raw data manipulated by the transformation.
transformation-editor.output-data_description: Label in Transformation Editor within a dashboard: Identifies the section displaying the data that results from applying one or more transformations to input data.
transformation-editor.title-debug-transformation_description: Title in the transformation editor within a dashboard: Indicates an action or section for debugging a data transformation.

transformation-filter.label-apply-transformation-to_descriptio: Label in transformation filter settings within a dashboard: This label introduces a control where the user specifies which data or series a transformation should be applied to. It typically precedes a dropdown or input field for selection.

transformation-operation-row.body-delete: Note that removing one transformation may break others. If there is only a single transformation, you will go back to the main selection screen.

render-actions.title-debug_description: Action in the rendering actions of a dashboard transformation operation row: Provides an option to debug or inspect the transformation's output. This assists users in troubleshooting problems.
render-actions.title-disable-transformation_description: Menu item in the actions menu for a data transformation row on a dashboard: Allows the user to disable a specific data transformation operation, preventing it from being applied to the data.
render-actions.title-filter_description: Label in the render actions for a transformation operation row on a dashboard: Allows users to apply a filter to the data. This label likely identifies a button or menu item to configure or enable filtering for that specific transformation's output.
render-actions.title-show-transform-help_description: Title for a button or link in the actions section of a data transformation operation row: It allows users to view help documentation about data transformations within the dashboard.

transformation-operation-row.title-delete_description: Dialog title in a transformation operation row on a dashboard: Prompts the user to confirm the deletion of a specific transformation operation. The `{{name}}` placeholder indicates the item being deleted.

transformation-picker.info: Transformations allow you to join, calculate, re-order, hide, and rename your query results before they are visualized.
transformation-picker.info-graph-not-suitable: Many transforms are not suitable if you're using the Graph visualization, as it currently only supports time series data.
transformation-picker.info-switch-to-table: It can help to switch to the Table visualization to understand what a transformation is doing.
transformation-picker.info-switch-to-table_description: Informational message in the Transformation picker in a dashboard: It advises users that switching to the Table visualization can help them understand the effects of a data transformation.
transformation-picker.placeholder-search-for-transformation_de: Placeholder text in the transformation picker within the dashboard: It prompts the user to type and search for available data transformation options.
transformation-picker.read-more_description: Link in Transformation Picker within a Dashboard: This link allows users to access additional information or documentation about the data transformations available.
transformation-picker.title-transformations_description: Title in the transformation picker in a dashboard: Labels the section where users can configure data transformations for the displayed data.

transformation-picker-ng.placeholder-search-for-transformation_de: Placeholder text in a search input field within the 'Transformation Picker' component of a dashboard: guides users to search for available data transformations.
transformation-picker-ng.show-images_description: Option in the dashboard's transformation picker: Controls whether images are displayed in the transformed data or visualization.
transformation-picker-ng.sql-expressions-message-description: A new way to manipulate and transform the results of data source queries using MySQL-like syntax.
transformation-picker-ng.sql-expressions-message-description_desc: Message description in the transformation picker within a dashboard: It informs users about a new feature that enables data manipulation and transformation using MySQL-like SQL expressions.
transformation-picker-ng.sql-expressions-message-link_description: Link in the Transformation Picker NG component: This link allows users to access more information or documentation related to SQL expressions.
transformation-picker-ng.sql-expressions-title_description: Title in the transformation picker settings on a dashboard: This title introduces a section where users can define or manage SQL-based expressions for data transformation.
transformation-picker-ng.title-add-another-transformation_descrip: Button or link in the transformation picker on a dashboard: Allows the user to add an additional data transformation to the displayed data.
transformation-picker-ng.view-all_description: Link in the Transformation Picker on a Dashboard: Allows the user to view all available transformation options.

un-theme-transformations-editor.body-delete-all-transformations: By deleting all transformations, you will go back to the main selection screen.
un-theme-transformations-editor.body-delete-all-transformations_descript: Confirmation message body text in a confirmation dialog within the Transformations Editor: It informs the user about the consequence of deleting all transformations, specifically that this action will revert them to the main selection screen.

actions.add-another-transformation_description: Button in the transformations editor within a dashboard: Allows users to add an additional data transformation to a dataset or query.

un-themed-transformations-editor.confirmText-delete-all_description: Confirmation button text in the un-themed transformations editor: Confirms the action to delete all applied transformations or data items.
un-themed-transformations-editor.delete-all-transformations_description: Button in the un-themed transformations editor within a dashboard: Allows the user to remove all currently applied data transformations.
un-themed-transformations-editor.title-delete-all-transformations_descrip: Confirmation dialog title in the un-themed transformations editor within a dashboard: Prompts the user to confirm deleting all transformations applied to the data.
un-themed-transformations-editor.tooltip-clear-search_description: Tooltip in the un-themed transformations editor within a dashboard: Describes the function of an element that clears currently applied search filters or terms.
un-themed-transformations-editor.tooltip-close-picker_description: Tooltip in the un-themed transformations editor: Displayed on hover over an icon or button to indicate that it closes the active picker component used for selection.

unsaved-changes-modal.changes: Do you want to save your changes?
unsaved-changes-modal.changes_description: Question in Unsaved Changes Modal on a Dashboard: This prompt asks the user if they want to save any unsaved modifications made to the dashboard. It appears when the user attempts to close the dashboard or navigate away after making changes.
unsaved-changes-modal.discard_description: Button in the 'Unsaved Changes' modal on a dashboard: Allows the user to discard all unsaved changes and proceed without saving the current modifications.
unsaved-changes-modal.title-unsaved-changes_description: Title in the unsaved changes modal dialog: This title informs the user that there are unsaved modifications on the current dashboard before they proceed with an action that might discard them.

untheme-dashboard-row.dashboard-datasource: Panels in this row use the {{SHARED_DASHBOARD_QUERY}} data source. These panels will reference the panel in the original row, not the ones in the repeated rows.

unthemed-dashboard-row.aria-label-delete-row_description: ARIA label for a delete action in an unthemed dashboard row: Specifies that the associated button or icon will delete the current row within the dashboard. This label improves accessibility.
unthemed-dashboard-row.learn-more_description: Text link in an unthemed dashboard row: Allows users to access additional information or a detailed explanation related to the content presented in that row.

title.delete-row_description: Button or menu item located within the title section of an unthemed dashboard row: Allows the user to delete the entire dashboard row. This action typically removes all panels and content within that row.

validation.invalid-dashboard-id: Could not find a valid Grafana.com ID
validation.invalid-dashboard-id_description: Error message in dashboard validation: Informs the user that the system could not find or validate the provided Grafana.com ID. This message typically appears when attempting an operation that requires referencing a valid Grafana.com ID for a dashboard.
validation.invalid-json_description: Validation message during dashboard configuration or import: Informs the user that the submitted content (e.g., dashboard definition, panel configuration) does not conform to a valid JSON structure.
validation.tags-expected-array_description: Message component in Dashboard validation settings: Describes that tags are expected to be provided in an array format for a validation rule.
validation.tags-expected-strings_description: Validation message in Dashboard validation settings: This message specifies that tags must be provided as an array of strings.

description.action_description: Action in the dashboard's variable settings: Allows users to edit or update the description associated with a specific dashboard variable.

hide.action_description: Action within the variable 'hide' settings in a dashboard: This allows the user to modify the hide option for a specific dashboard variable.

label.action_description: Action in the Variable settings within a Dashboard: Allows users to modify the display label for a dashboard variable.

name.action_description: Action button or menu item for a dashboard variable's name: Enables the user to modify the display name of that specific dashboard variable.

version-history-comparison.button-restore_description: Button in the Dashboard Version History Comparison view: Allows the user to restore the dashboard to a selected historical version. The `{{version}}` placeholder will be replaced with the specific version number.
version-history-comparison.label-view-json-diff_description: Button text in the Dashboard Version History Comparison view: Displays the differences in the underlying JSON configuration between two compared dashboard versions.
version-history-comparison.new-updated-by_description: Text label in the Version History Comparison view of a dashboard: It displays detailed information for a specific dashboard version, indicating who performed the update and how long ago. This label uses dynamic placeholders for the version number, editor's name, and the time elapsed since the update.
version-history-comparison.old-updated-by_description: Text label in Dashboard Version History Comparison: Displays information about an older dashboard version, including its specific version number, the user who made the update, and how long ago the update occurred. This string is typically used to identify one of the versions being compared in the history view.

version-history-table.aria-label-toggle-selection_description: Aria-label for a selection control in the Version History table on a dashboard: Describes the action of selecting or deselecting a specific dashboard version. The `{{version}}` placeholder will be dynamically replaced with the version number.
version-history-table.date_description: Column header in the Version History table within a dashboard: Indicates the creation or modification date of a specific version of the dashboard.
version-history-table.name-latest_description: Label in the dashboard version history table: It identifies the most recent version of a dashboard. This label helps users quickly distinguish the newest iteration from older ones.
version-history-table.notes_description: Column Header in the version history table of a dashboard: Labels the column that displays explanatory text, comments, or remarks associated with each dashboard version.
version-history-table.restore_description: Button in the Version History table within a dashboard: Allows the user to restore a previously saved version of the dashboard, making it the current active version.
version-history-table.updated-by_description: Column Header in the version history table on a dashboard: Identifies the user who performed a specific update or created a particular version of the dashboard.
version-history-table.version_description: Column header in the version history table of a dashboard: It identifies the unique version number or identifier of a dashboard entry.

view-panel.not-found_description: Message in a dashboard panel: Informs the user that the requested or expected panel could not be found or loaded. This typically indicates an error or a missing configuration for the panel.

visualization-button.aria-label-change-visualization_descript: Button in the dashboard's visualization section: Allows users to change the type or display of the current data visualization. This is an accessibility label.
visualization-button.aria-label-close_description: ARIA label for a button in a visualization's options pane on a dashboard: Its purpose is to close the options pane that displays settings or configurations for the visualization.
visualization-button.aria-label-show_description: Button on a dashboard's visualization: This ARIA label describes a button that, when activated, displays the options pane for configuring the visualization.
visualization-button.tooltip-click-to-change-visualization_de: Tooltip in a visualization button on a dashboard: Informs the user that clicking this button will open options to change the type of visualization displayed.
visualization-button.tooltip-close_description: Tooltip message displayed when hovering over the close control of an options pane, which is associated with a visualization button on a dashboard: It informs the user that clicking the control will close the options pane.
visualization-button.tooltip-show_description: Tooltip in the Visualization button on a dashboard: Informs the user that clicking this button will open the configuration options pane for the visualization.

description.reusable-panels-share-between-multiple-d: Reusable panels you can share between multiple dashboards.

Description text in the Visualization Select pane's radio options: This text describes the functionality of reusable panels, stating that they can be shared across multiple dashboards. It likely provides additional information for a radio button choice related to panel types.

label.library-panels_description: Radio button label in the 'Visualization Select' pane of a dashboard: This label identifies an option for users to select or manage reusable panels stored in a library.
label.suggestions_description: Label in Visualization Select Pane radio options: Identifies a group or category of suggested choices for visualization configuration.
label.visualizations_description: Label in the visualization selection pane of a dashboard: This heading introduces a section or group of radio button options related to selecting different types of data visualizations.

viz-panel.delete-panel-text: Deleting this panel will also remove all queries. Are you sure you want to continue?
viz-panel.delete-panel-text_description: Confirmation prompt in a visualization panel within a dashboard: It warns the user that deleting the panel will also remove all its associated queries and asks for confirmation.
viz-panel.delete-panel-title_description: Title in a confirmation dialog that appears when a user attempts to delete a visualization panel from a dashboard: Prompts the user to confirm the deletion action.

options.configure-button-tooltip_description: Tooltip for a Configure button in the visualization panel options on a dashboard: It informs users that they can edit the data queries and visualization settings for the panel by clicking this button.
options.transparent-background_description: Option in the visualization panel options on a dashboard: Controls whether the background of the visualization panel is transparent.

file-dropzone.primary-text_description: Primary text in the file dropzone on the dashboard import page: Instructs the user to upload a dashboard configuration file in JSON format.
file-dropzone.secondary-text: Drag and drop here or click to browse
file-dropzone.secondary-text_description: Instructional text in the file dropzone within the dashboard import feature: Guides the user on how to upload a file for import, either by dragging and dropping or by clicking to browse. This text provides user interaction options for file selection.

form-actions.load_description: Button in the dashboard import form: Initiates the loading of a dashboard, typically from a selected source or file.

gcom-field.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the "Grafana.com field" within the Dashboard Import feature: It instructs users to enter either a URL or an ID for a dashboard to be imported from Grafana.com.
gcom-field.validation-required: A Grafana dashboard URL or ID is required
gcom-field.validation-required_description: Validation message in the Grafana dashboard URL/ID input field on the Dashboard Import page: This message appears when the field is left empty, informing the user that a Grafana dashboard URL or ID is required to proceed with the import.

json-field.label: Import via dashboard JSON model
json-field.label_description: Label in the Dashboard Import screen: This text describes the method of importing a dashboard by providing its JSON model. It likely labels an input field for pasting or uploading the JSON.
json-field.validation-required_description: Validation message in the dashboard import interface, specifically for the JSON input field: This message informs the user that a dashboard JSON model is a required input for the import process. It appears when the field is left empty or no model is provided.

empty-state.button-title_description: Button in the empty state of the Dashboard Links section: Prompts the user to add a new dashboard link when no links have been configured yet.
empty-state.info-box-content: Dashboard links allow you to place links to other dashboards and web sites directly below the dashboard header. <2>Learn more</2>
empty-state.title: There are no dashboard links added yet
empty-state.title_description: Title in the empty state of the dashboard links section: This message informs the user that no dashboard links have been added yet to the current dashboard.

ad-hoc-variable-form.adhoc-options_description: Section title in the Ad-hoc variable form: This label introduces a group of settings for configuring or managing ad-hoc variables. It provides parameters for defining specific, temporary variables within the dashboard.
ad-hoc-variable-form.alert-not-supported: This data source does not support ad hoc filters
ad-hoc-variable-form.alert-not-supported_description: Alert message in the ad-hoc variable form within a dashboard scene: Informs the user that the currently selected data source does not support ad hoc filters. This message prevents users from attempting to configure ad-hoc filtering options for an incompatible data source.
ad-hoc-variable-form.description-enables-users-custom-values: Enables users to add custom values to the list
ad-hoc-variable-form.description-enables-users-custom-values_: Description in the ad-hoc variable form: This text explains that the associated feature or control enables users to add custom values to a list.
ad-hoc-variable-form.description-provide-dimensions-as-csv-di: Instruction text in the Ad-hoc variable form within the Dashboard scene: This text provides an example of the required CSV format for entering dimensions, indicating that each dimension should be specified as "name, value".
ad-hoc-variable-form.label-data-source_description: Label in an ad-hoc variable configuration form within a dashboard scene: It prompts the user to select the data source for the ad-hoc variable.
ad-hoc-variable-form.label-use-static-key-dimensions_descript: Label in the Ad-hoc Variable Form: This label indicates an option to use static key dimensions when defining an ad-hoc variable.
ad-hoc-variable-form.name-allow-custom-values_description: Checkbox in an ad-hoc variable form within a dashboard scene: This control, when checked, allows users to manually input values for the ad-hoc variable that are not available in its predefined options.

message.could-navigate-selected-dashboard-please: Could not navigate to the selected dashboard. Please try again.
message.could-panel-dashboard-please-again: Could not add panel to dashboard. Please try again.

add-to-dashboard-form.description-choose-where-to-add-the-pane: Choose where to add the panel.
Instructional text in the 'Add to Dashboard' form: This text guides the user to select the specific location or target dashboard for the panel they are currently adding.
add-to-dashboard-form.description-select-which-dashboard-panel: Select in which dashboard the panel will be created.

Instruction in Add to Dashboard form: This text guides the user to choose a specific dashboard where a new panel will be created. It appears as a prompt above a selection control (e.g., dropdown, radio buttons) to pick the destination dashboard.

label.existing-dashboard_description: Label in the "Add to Dashboard" form within the Dashboard Scene: This label identifies an option allowing the user to select an already-created dashboard for adding new content. It distinguishes this choice from creating a new dashboard.
label.new-dashboard_description: Label in the 'Add to Dashboard' form: This text labels an option or field for creating a brand new dashboard. It likely differentiates from choosing an existing dashboard.

add-to-dashboard-form.label-dashboard_description: Label in the 'Add to Dashboard' form: This text labels a field where the user selects or specifies the target dashboard to which an item will be added.
add-to-dashboard-form.label-target-dashboard_description: Label in the "Add to Dashboard" form: It identifies the input field where the user specifies the dashboard to which the current item (e.g., a panel) will be added.

message.this-field-is-required_description: Error Message in Add to Dashboard form: Informs the user that a mandatory input field must be filled out before proceeding. This validation message appears when a required form field is left empty.

add-to-dashboard-form.open-dashboard_description: Button in the Add to Dashboard form: Allows the user to open or navigate to a dashboard. This button likely appears after an item has been successfully added to a dashboard, providing a quick way to view the updated dashboard.
add-to-dashboard-form.open-in-new-tab_description: Checkbox label in the 'Add to Dashboard' form: This option controls whether the item being added to the dashboard, or any related link/action, will open in a new browser tab.
add-to-dashboard-form.title-error-adding-the-panel_description: Error message title in the 'Add to Dashboard' form within the Dashboard scene: This title is displayed to indicate that an error occurred while attempting to add a new panel to the dashboard.

title.new-panel_description: Title in the 'Add to Dashboard' form: This title designates the section where a user can create a new dashboard panel.

annotation-settings-edit.back-to-list_description: Button in the annotation settings edit screen: Allows users to navigate back to the main list of annotations.
annotation-settings-edit.description-color-annotation-event-marke: Color to use for the annotation event markers
Label for a color selection field in the Annotation Settings Edit view: Specifies the color for event markers associated with annotations on a dashboard.
annotation-settings-edit.description-enabled-annotation-query-iss: When enabled the annotation query is issued every dashboard refresh
Help text in Annotation settings editor: This text describes the behavior of an enabled annotation query, stating that it will be executed every time the dashboard refreshes.
annotation-settings-edit.description-hidden: Annotation queries can be toggled on or off at the top of the dashboard. With this option checked this toggle will be hidden.
annotation-settings-edit.label-color_description: Label in the "Edit Annotation Settings" section of a dashboard: Prompts the user to select the color for an annotation.
annotation-settings-edit.label-data-source_description: Label in the annotation settings editor within a dashboard: Specifies the source from which annotation data is retrieved.
annotation-settings-edit.label-enabled_description: Label in the Annotation Settings Edit section of a Dashboard Scene: Indicates whether annotations are enabled or disabled for the dashboard.
annotation-settings-edit.label-hidden_description: Label in the dashboard's annotation settings edit view: This label indicates that an annotation's visibility is set to hidden, or serves as an option to hide an annotation.
annotation-settings-edit.label-show-in_description: Label in the annotation settings edit panel within a dashboard: Prompts the user to select where the annotation should be displayed.
annotation-settings-edit.placeholder-choose-panels_description: Placeholder text in the annotation settings edit screen within a dashboard: Guides the user to select one or more dashboard panels to which an annotation will apply.
annotation-settings-edit.query_description: Label in the annotation settings edit screen: Denotes the data query used for fetching and displaying annotations on the dashboard.
annotation-settings-edit.title-annotation-support-source: No annotation support for this data source
annotation-settings-edit.title-annotation-support-source_descript: Informational message in the annotation settings edit screen within a dashboard: Informs the user that the currently selected data source does not support annotations.

annotation-settings-list.built-in_description: Label for an annotation in the Annotation settings list in the dashboard scene: Identifies a specific annotation as a built-in, system-provided type. This helps distinguish it from user-defined annotations.
annotation-settings-list.data-source_description: Field label in the annotation settings list of a dashboard scene: Used to specify the data source for annotations.
annotation-settings-list.delete-aria-label_description: ARIA label for a delete button in the annotation settings list within a dashboard. This label provides assistive technology users with a clear description of the action, indicating which specific annotation query, by its title, will be deleted.
annotation-settings-list.disabled_description: Label in the Annotation Settings List on a Dashboard: This label displays the name of an annotation that is currently disabled. The `{{annoName}}` placeholder is dynamically replaced with the annotation's actual name.
annotation-settings-list.new-query_description: Button in the Annotation Settings list on a dashboard: Allows users to add a new data query. This query defines the data source and conditions for new annotations displayed on the dashboard.
annotation-settings-list.query-name_description: Label in the Annotation Settings list within a Dashboard: Identifies the name given to a data source query that provides data for annotations.
annotation-settings-list.tooltip-move-down_description: Tooltip in the annotation settings list: Indicates the action to move a selected annotation item downwards, changing its display order.
annotation-settings-list.tooltip-move-up_description: Tooltip in the annotation settings list within a dashboard: Indicates the action to move an item (e.g., an annotation setting) up in the list order.

branch-validation-error.cannot-contain-invalid-characters: It cannot contain invalid characters: '~', '^', ':', '?', '*', '[', '\\', or ']'.
branch-validation-error.cannot-contain-invalid-characters_descri: Validation error message in a dashboard's branch name/identifier field: Informs the user that the entered value contains characters that are not permitted.
branch-validation-error.cannot-start-with: It cannot start with '/' or end with '/', '.', or whitespace.
branch-validation-error.invalid-branch-name_description: Validation error message in the Dashboard Scene: Informs the user that the entered branch name does not meet the system's requirements. This message appears when attempting to create or modify a dashboard branch.
branch-validation-error.least-valid-character: It must have at least one valid character.
branch-validation-error.least-valid-character_description: Validation error message in a dashboard scene: This message is displayed when input for a branch name or identifier fails validation, informing the user that it must contain at least one valid character.

title.new-dashboard_description: Title in the 'New Dashboard' creation/save dialog: This text serves as the title for the dialog or screen where users configure and save a new dashboard.
Title in the 'Save New Dashboard' dialog: This title indicates that the user is currently creating and saving a new dashboard.

constant-variable-form.constant-options_description: Section heading in the constant variable configuration form: It labels the group of settings or choices available for defining a constant variable.
constant-variable-form.label-value_description: Label in the constant variable form within the dashboard scene: This label identifies the input field where users define or view the static value of a constant variable.
constant-variable-form.placeholder-your-metric-prefix_descripti: Placeholder text in the Constant Variable Form on the Dashboard Scene: It prompts the user to enter a prefix for a metric. This placeholder guides the user on what kind of value to input when defining a constant variable.

custom-variable-form.custom-options_description: Section heading/Label in the Custom Variable Form: This indicates a section or field group where users can define specific custom settings or choices for the variable.
custom-variable-form.name-values-separated-comma_description: Instructional text in the Custom Variable Form on the Dashboard Scene: It guides the user on how to enter multiple values for a custom variable, specifying that they should be separated by a comma. This indicates the expected input format for list-like variable values.
custom-variable-form.selection-options_description: Section Header in the Custom Variable Configuration Form within a dashboard: This label introduces settings for defining how values for a custom variable are selected or presented.

dashboard-edit-pane-renderer.outline_description: Section title in the dashboard edit pane: Displays a structural overview or hierarchical representation of the dashboard's components, allowing users to manage its layout.

dashboard-link-form.back-to-list_description: Button in a dashboard link form: Allows the user to navigate back from the current form (e.g., for creating or editing a dashboard link) to the main list of dashboard links.
dashboard-link-form.label-icon_description: Label in the Dashboard Link Form: Used to identify the field where a user can select an icon to represent a dashboard link. This allows for visual identification of the link's purpose.
dashboard-link-form.label-include-current-template-variable-: Label in Dashboard link form: Specifies whether the current values of template variables should be included when generating a dashboard link. This option helps maintain the dashboard's current state when shared or navigated.
dashboard-link-form.label-include-current-time-range_descrip: Label in Dashboard Link Form: This option allows users to include the currently selected time range when generating a shareable link for the dashboard.
dashboard-link-form.label-open-link-in-new-tab_description: Label for a checkbox in the dashboard link configuration form: This option allows users to specify if a configured link should open in a new browser tab.
dashboard-link-form.label-options_description: Label in the Dashboard Link Form within the Dashboard Scene: This label designates a section where users can configure various settings or choices related to the dashboard link being created or edited.
dashboard-link-form.label-show-as-dropdown_description: Option in the dashboard link form: This setting controls whether a link or a group of links is displayed as a dropdown menu on the dashboard. It provides a compact way to present multiple navigation options.
dashboard-link-form.label-tooltip_description: Label in the dashboard link configuration form: It identifies the input field where users can define the text that will appear as a tooltip when hovering over the configured dashboard link.
dashboard-link-form.label-type_description: Label in dashboard link form: Identifies the kind of link being configured for a dashboard.
dashboard-link-form.label-with-tags_description: Label in the Dashboard Link Form: This label identifies an option or setting related to including or managing tags for the dashboard link being created or edited.

label.dashboards_description: Option label in the link type selection within the Dashboard Link Form: Indicates that the link should point to another dashboard.
label.link_description: Label in the 'Link Type Options' section of the Dashboard Link Form: Used to identify a specific type of hyperlink or connection, often selectable among other link types.

dashboard-link-form.placeholder-open-dashboard_description: Placeholder text in the Dashboard Link Form: This text guides the user to specify or select a dashboard to be opened when configuring a link.

dashboard-link-list.delete-aria-label_description: ARIA label for a delete button or icon in the dashboard link list: This label describes the action of deleting a specific dashboard link. It includes the link's title for clarity and accessibility purposes.
dashboard-link-list.info_description: Link in the dashboard-link-list: Provides access to general information about the dashboard or the listed items.
dashboard-link-list.new-link_description: Button in the dashboard link list: This button allows the user to create a new link within the dashboard scene. It typically opens a form or dialog to configure the new link's properties.
dashboard-link-list.tooltip-copy-link_description: Action text in a tooltip displayed for an item within the dashboard link list: This action allows the user to copy the associated link to their clipboard.
dashboard-link-list.tooltip-move-link-down_description: Tooltip on an interactive element in a dashboard link list: This tooltip indicates that interacting with the element will move the associated link one position down in the list, thereby changing its display order.
dashboard-link-list.tooltip-move-link-up_description: Tooltip text in the dashboard link list: Describes the action of moving a selected link to an earlier position in the list, typically used for reordering.
dashboard-link-list.type_description: Column Header in the Dashboard Link List: Indicates the category or nature of each listed dashboard link.

dashboard-preview-banner.title-dashboard-loaded-external-reposito: This dashboard is loaded from an external repository
Title text in the dashboard preview banner: Informs the user that the dashboard being viewed has been loaded from an external repository. This message provides context about the dashboard's origin.
dashboard-preview-banner.title-error-loading-dashboard_descriptio: Title of an error message in a banner within the dashboard scene: Informs the user that the dashboard could not be loaded.

text.edit-panel_description: Button or menu item in Dashboard Scene: Initiates the process to modify the settings or content of a dashboard panel.
text.view-panel_description: Action label in a dashboard scene: Prompts the user to view the detailed content of a specific dashboard panel. This label is typically found on a button, link, or as a menu item.

title.dashboard_description: Title in a dashboard scene: Identifies the current page or view as 'Dashboard'.

message.dashboard-not-found_description: Message in Dashboard Scene: Displayed to the user when a requested dashboard cannot be found or loaded.
Status message in the dashboard scene: Informs the user that the requested dashboard could not be found or loaded.

data-source-variable-form.data-source-options_description: Section title in the data source variable configuration form: It labels the section where users define configurable settings for the data source linked to the variable.
data-source-variable-form.description-instance-name-filter: Regex filter for which data source instances to choose from in the variable value list. Leave empty for all.
data-source-variable-form.example-instance-name-filter_description: Hint text in the Data Source Variable Form: Provides an example for how to format an instance name filter. The `<codeExample />` placeholder will be dynamically replaced with a specific example string.
data-source-variable-form.name-instance-name-filter_description: Label for a filter field in the data source variable configuration form on a dashboard: It allows users to filter available instances by their name.
data-source-variable-form.name-type_description: Label in the Data Source Variable Form within the Dashboard Scene: This label identifies a field where the user specifies the type of the data source variable being configured.
data-source-variable-form.selection-options_description: Label in the data source variable configuration form: Designates a section where users configure options for selecting values for a dashboard variable. These options typically control user interaction, such as single vs. multi-selection.

title.row-title_description: UI label in the row configuration settings of a dashboard's grid layout: This text identifies the field where users can define or view the title of a specific dashboard row. It is used for naming and organizing dashboard sections.

delete-provisioned-dashboard-form.api-error_description: Error message in the 'Delete Provisioned Dashboard' form: Informs the user that the deletion of the dashboard was unsuccessful. This message is displayed when the underlying API call to delete the dashboard fails.
delete-provisioned-dashboard-form.delete-action_description: Button in the 'Delete provisioned dashboard' form: This button confirms and executes the deletion of a provisioned dashboard.
delete-provisioned-dashboard-form.drawer-title_description: Drawer Title in the 'Delete Provisioned Dashboard' form: This title identifies the purpose of the drawer, which is to allow users to delete a dashboard that was provisioned through configuration.
delete-provisioned-dashboard-form.success-message_description: Success message in the delete provisioned dashboard form: Informs the user that a provisioned dashboard has been successfully deleted.

text.edit-link_description: Button text in the 'Edit Link' page navigation within a dashboard: Initiates the process of modifying an existing link.

email-list.aria-label-emailmenu_description: Aria-label for a button in the email list section of the dashboard: This button toggles the visibility of a menu containing email-related actions or settings.

subTitle.showing-dashboard-panel-tests-pie-chart: Showing dashboard: Panel Tests - Pie chart
subTitle.showing-dashboard-panel-tests-pie-chart_: Display text in the embedded dashboard test page: Informs the user which dashboard and specific panel or view (e.g., 'Panel Tests - Pie chart') is currently being displayed.

text.embedding-dashboard_description: A heading or title on the embedded dashboard test page: It labels a section or describes the functionality for embedding dashboards, likely for testing or demonstration purposes.

empty-transformations-message.add-transformation_description: Button in the empty transformations message on a Dashboard scene: Allows the user to add a new transformation. This button appears when no transformations are currently configured for a panel or query.

label.editable_description: Label in the General Settings Edit View: This label signifies an option that controls whether the associated dashboard or its elements can be edited.
label.readonly_description: **Label** in the **General Settings Edit View of a Dashboard**: This label identifies an option to configure the dashboard or a related setting as read-only, preventing any modifications.

label.default_description: Option label in the Graph tooltip options section of the general settings edit view: This option allows users to select the default, standard, or pre-configured display settings for graph tooltips.
label.shared-crosshair_description: Label in the 'Graph Tooltip Options' section of the Dashboard General Settings: Identifies an option to enable a synchronized crosshair across multiple graphs on the dashboard. This feature helps users compare data points across different visualizations simultaneously.
label.shared-tooltip_description: Label in the General Settings edit view of a dashboard, under Graph Tooltip Options: This identifies the setting for enabling shared tooltips. A shared tooltip synchronizes tooltip display across multiple series or panels within a graph.

title.column-options_description: Heading in the dashboard's edit options: Labels the section containing settings for configuring columns, likely within a table or similar visualization panel.
title.row-height-options_description: Section heading in the dashboard's 'Edit Options' panel: Introduces settings for configuring the height of rows within the dashboard.

description.add-keyvalue-filters-on-the-fly: Add key/value filters on the fly
description.add-keyvalue-filters-on-the-fly_descript: Descriptive text within the editable variables feature on a dashboard: It describes the functionality to dynamically add key/value filters. This enables quick and flexible data filtering.
description.dynamically-switch-source-multiple-panel: Dynamically switch the data source for multiple panels
Description text in the dashboard variable configuration settings: This text explains a feature that allows users to dynamically switch the data source for multiple panels on a dashboard.
description.group: Add keys to group by on the fly
description.group_description: Helper text in the Dashboard Variable Editor: This describes the functionality for users to dynamically add keys to group data within the dashboard.
description.hidden-constant-variable: A hidden constant variable, useful for metric prefixes in dashboards you want to share
description.hidden-constant-variable_description: Description for a hidden constant variable in the dashboard's variable management interface: It explains the utility of this variable type for setting metric prefixes, especially in dashboards intended for sharing.
description.users-enter-arbitrary-strings-switch: A variable that can be toggled on and off
description.users-enter-arbitrary-strings-switch_des: Description in the editable variable settings of a Dashboard Scene: This text describes a variable type that functions as a simple on/off toggle switch.
description.users-enter-arbitrary-strings-textbox: Users can enter any arbitrary strings in a textbox
description.users-enter-arbitrary-strings-textbox_de: Description in the 'Get Editable Variables' settings of a dashboard: It informs users that a particular textbox input is designed to accept any free-form string they enter.
description.values-are-static-and-defined-manually: Values are static and defined manually
description.values-are-static-and-defined-manually_d: Description text in the variable configuration options within a dashboard: It explains that the values for a variable are static and manually defined by the user.
description.values-fetched-source-query: Values are fetched from a data source query
description.values-fetched-source-query_description: Informational text in the dashboard variable configuration section: Describes that the variable's possible values are populated by a query to a data source.
