description.values-timespans_description: Hint text in the configuration interface for an editable dashboard variable: It informs the user that the expected values for the variable are timespans, providing examples like '1m', '1h', '1d'.

name.ad-hoc-filters_description: **Label** in the **dashboard variable management section**: Identifies the option or category for creating and managing **ad hoc filters**. These filters are defined on-the-fly by users for temporary analysis.
name.constant_description: Option in the dashboard variable definition interface: Designates a variable as having a fixed, unchangeable value. This option is selected when configuring a variable that should not change its value.
name.custom_description: Option label in the editable variables configuration for a dashboard: It designates a user-defined or non-standard choice for the variable's name or its naming method.
name.data-source_description: Label in the dashboard variable configuration panel: Prompts the user to select or specify the data source from which a dashboard variable will retrieve its values.
name.group-by_description: Label for a selection control in the dashboard variable management interface: It allows users to choose how to organize or categorize the displayed list of editable variables.
name.interval_description: Label for a variable in the Editable Variables section of a dashboard: This variable allows users to define a time interval or refresh frequency for the dashboard content.
name.query_description: Label or option in the Editable Variables configuration settings: It identifies the method or input field for defining a data source query that populates the values for an editable dashboard variable.
name.switch_description: Switch button in the dashboard's editable variables section, associated with a variable's name: Allows users to change the active variable name or toggle a related setting. This provides a quick way to modify the variable's identity or behavior.
name.textbox_description: Textbox in the editable variables configuration for a dashboard scene: It allows users to input the name for a variable.

description.annotation-following-panels: Do not send annotation data to the following panels
description.annotation-following-panels_description: Description text in dashboard panel configuration settings: This text describes an option that prevents annotation data from being sent to or displayed on specific other panels within the dashboard.
description.annotation-panels-support-annotations: Send the annotation data to all panels that support annotations
description.annotation-panels-support-annotations_de: Setting description in Dashboard Panel Configuration: Describes an option that controls the distribution of annotation data. If enabled, annotation data will be sent to all panels on the dashboard that support displaying annotations.
description.annotations-explicitly-listed-panels: Send the annotations to the explicitly listed panels
description.annotations-explicitly-listed-panels_des: Description in the dashboard's panel filtering options for annotations: This text explains that annotations will only be sent to and displayed on the panels explicitly selected by the user.

label.all-panels_description: Label in the panel filter selection area of a dashboard: It represents an option to apply a chosen filter across all available panels.
label.all-panels-except_description: Option label in the panel filter configuration: It indicates a choice to apply a filter to all panels except those explicitly excluded.
label.selected-panels_description: Label in the panel filter configuration interface: Identifies a section or list displaying panels that have been selected, typically for applying filters to them.

title.panel-options_description: Section Title in the dashboard panel's configuration interface: It labels the group of settings where users can customize a specific dashboard panel.

title.panel-links_description: Section title in the Panel Frame Options: This label identifies the settings area for managing links associated with a specific dashboard panel. Users can configure drill-down or external navigation links here.
title.title: Title
title.transparent-background_description: Label for a checkbox option in the panel frame display options: Allows users to set the background of a dashboard panel to be transparent.

group-by-variable-form.alert-not-supported: This data source does not support group by variables
group-by-variable-form.alert-not-supported_description: Alert message in the Group By Variable form within the Dashboard scene: Informs the user that the selected data source does not support the "group by variables" functionality they are attempting to configure.
group-by-variable-form.description-enables-users-custom-values: Enables users to add custom values to the list
group-by-variable-form.description-enables-users-custom-values_: Description in the Group By Variable Form: This text informs users about the capability to add custom, user-defined values to the list of available options for the variable being configured.
group-by-variable-form.description-provide-dimensions-as-csv-di: Instructional text in the "Group by variable" configuration form: It guides users on the required CSV format for providing dimension values. This clarifies the expected input structure as `dimension name, dimension ID/value`.
group-by-variable-form.group-by-options_description: Section heading in the 'Group by variable' form within the dashboard scene: It labels the set of configurable options that determine how data or variables are grouped.
group-by-variable-form.label-data-source_description: Label in the 'Group by variable' form within a dashboard configuration: Indicates the field where the user selects the data source for the variable being defined.
group-by-variable-form.label-use-static-group-by-dimensions_des: Label in Group By Variable Form: This label describes an option to enable or disable the use of predefined, non-dynamic dimensions for grouping data within the dashboard.
group-by-variable-form.name-allow-custom-values_description: Checkbox in the Group-By Variable configuration form on a dashboard: Allows users to manually enter custom values for the variable, in addition to or instead of predefined options.

help-wizard.copy-to-clipboard_description: Button in Help Wizard within a Dashboard Scene: Allows users to copy specific help-related information or a link to their clipboard for easy transfer.
help-wizard.description-randomize-data: Modify the original data to hide sensitive information. Note the lengths will stay the same, and duplicate values will be equal.
help-wizard.description-support-snapshot_description: Descriptive label in the dashboard help wizard: This string identifies a specific panel by displaying its title, likely as part of the information provided for a support snapshot.
help-wizard.download-dashboard_description: Label in the Help Wizard within a dashboard scene: Indicates an option to download the dashboard, showing its snapshot size. The `{{snapshotSize}}` placeholder will be replaced with the actual size.
help-wizard.download-snapshot_description: Button in the Help Wizard of a Dashboard scene: Allows users to download a snapshot of the current dashboard, with its size indicated in the button label.
help-wizard.label-randomize-data_description: Label in the Help Wizard within a dashboard scene: This text provides an option to randomize data, typically for demonstration or testing purposes. It allows users to explore dashboard functionalities without affecting real data.
help-wizard.label-support-snapshot_description: Label in the Help Wizard on a Dashboard scene: This label identifies a feature or action to create a diagnostic 'snapshot' of the current dashboard or system configuration. This snapshot is intended to aid in troubleshooting and support cases.
help-wizard.label-template_description: Label in the Help Wizard within a Dashboard scene: Refers to a pre-defined layout or structure that the user can select or apply. This label likely identifies an option or field for choosing a template.
help-wizard.randomize-field-names-label-field-names_: Label in the Dashboard help wizard: Identifies the concept of 'Field names' when configuring or explaining options for randomizing them.
help-wizard.randomize-labels-label-labels_descriptio: Text label in the 'Randomize Labels' section of the Help Wizard: This identifies the labels or settings related to labels that are available for randomization.
help-wizard.randomize-string-values-label-string-val: Label in the dashboard's help wizard: Identifies the input or configuration area where string values are defined or managed for randomization.

label.data_description: Tab label in the Help Wizard on a dashboard: This label identifies a section within the help wizard that focuses on data-related information or functionalities.
label.snapshot_description: Tab Label in the 'Help Wizard' section of the Dashboard Scene: This tab allows users to access or manage dashboard snapshots. A snapshot typically represents a saved state or point-in-time view of a dashboard's configuration or data.

help-wizard.title-complete-git-hub-comment-clipboard: Copy a complete GitHub comment to the clipboard

Title in the Help Wizard within the Dashboard Scene: This title instructs the user to copy a complete GitHub comment to their clipboard. It describes an action required as part of a guided help process.
help-wizard.title-get-help-with-this-panel_descripti: Title in the help wizard displayed on a dashboard: It identifies a section or dialog that provides contextual assistance for a specific dashboard panel.

import-dashboard-form-v2.label-folder_description: Label in Import Dashboard Form (V2): Identifies the input field where users select or specify the destination folder for the dashboard being imported.

inspect-data-tab.no-data-found_description: Message in the Inspect Data tab of a dashboard: Informs the user that no data was found or retrieved for the current data inspection. It appears when a data query yields no results.

inspect-json-tab.apply_description: Button in the Inspect JSON tab of a dashboard: Used to confirm and apply any changes or settings made within that tab, typically after editing the underlying JSON data.

interval-variable-form.description-auto-option: Dynamically calculates interval by dividing time range by the count specified
interval-variable-form.description-auto-option_description: Descriptive text in the Interval Variable form: This text explains the dynamic calculation method for the 'Auto' option, detailing how the interval is determined by dividing the time range by a specified count.
interval-variable-form.description-calculated-value-below-thres: The calculated value will not go below this threshold

Descriptive text in the interval variable form on a dashboard scene: This message clarifies that the automatically calculated value for an interval variable will not be set below a specific minimum threshold. It likely provides context for a field where this minimum threshold can be defined.
interval-variable-form.description-step-count: How many times the current time range should be divided to calculate the value
interval-variable-form.description-step-count_description: Description text in the Interval Variable Form in the Dashboard Scene: It explains how to set the number of divisions for a time range to calculate values. This clarifies how to configure the granularity of an interval variable.
interval-variable-form.interval-options_description: Section heading in the interval variable form on a dashboard: It introduces the configuration settings available for an interval variable, allowing users to define its behavior and choices.
interval-variable-form.name-auto-option_description: Label for an option in the Interval Variable configuration form: This option allows users to select an automatic setting for the interval variable.
interval-variable-form.name-min-interval_description: Label in the interval variable configuration form within a dashboard scene: It identifies the input field where users specify the minimum time interval.
interval-variable-form.name-step-count_description: Label in Interval variable form in the dashboard scene: This label identifies an input field where users define the number of increments or steps for an interval variable.
interval-variable-form.name-values_description: Label in the interval variable configuration form: This indicates the input field or section where users define the specific selectable values for an interval dashboard variable.

render-save-button-and-error.body-plugin-dashboard: Your changes will be lost when you update the plugin. Use Save as to create custom version.
render-save-button-and-error.title-failed-to-save-dashboard_descripti: Error message title in the dashboard's JSON model edit view: Informs the user that the attempt to save the dashboard was unsuccessful, appearing typically after clicking a save button.
render-save-button-and-error.title-plugin-dashboard_description: Label in the dashboard's JSON model edit view: Identifies the dashboard currently being edited or saved as a 'Plugin dashboard'.
render-save-button-and-error.title-someone-else-has-updated-this-dash: Someone else has updated this dashboard

Title of an error message or notification in a dashboard save conflict dialog: This message indicates that another user has updated the dashboard, preventing the current user's changes from being saved to avoid overwriting newer modifications. It typically prompts the user to refresh their view.
render-save-button-and-error.would-still-dashboard: Would you still like to save this dashboard?
render-save-button-and-error.would-still-dashboard_description: Confirmation dialog message in the dashboard's JSON model editor: Prompts the user to confirm saving the dashboard, typically after a warning or error has been displayed regarding the dashboard's configuration.

json-model-edit-view.save-and-overwrite_description: Button label in the JSON model edit view of a dashboard scene: Allows the user to save their current modifications to the dashboard's JSON model, replacing the previously saved version.

library-viz-panel-info.last-edited_description: Label in the library visualization panel information section within a dashboard: Displays the last modification time and user of a library visualization panel. This provides context on the recency and authorship of shared dashboard components.
library-viz-panel-info.usage-count_one_description: Usage counter text in the Library Visualization Panel details within the Dashboard Scene: Informs the user how many dashboards a specific library visualization panel is currently used on. This string is used when the count is exactly '1'.
library-viz-panel-info.usage-count_other_description: Text label in the library visualization panel information section: This displays the total number of dashboards that currently utilize the selected library visualization panel. This string is used for plural counts (when {{count}} is not 1).

managed-badge.kubectl_description: A Badge in the Dashboard: This text indicates that the associated resource or component is managed by Kubectl.
managed-badge.plugin_description: Label in a Managed Badge within the Dashboard Scene: Displays the plugin responsible for managing the dashboard or its content. The `{{id}}` is a placeholder for the plugin's unique identifier.
managed-badge.provisioned_description: Status label on a Managed Badge within a Dashboard Scene: Indicates that the specific managed item, such as a dashboard or resource, has been successfully provisioned and is available.
managed-badge.repository_description: Text label within a "Managed by" badge on a dashboard scene: This label displays the name of the repository that manages the current dashboard. It helps users identify the source and ownership of the dashboard content.
managed-badge.terraform_description: Text within a managed badge on a dashboard: Identifies that the dashboard's configuration or underlying resources are managed by Terraform. This indicates the automation tool responsible for its provisioning or updates.

move-provisioned-dashboard-form.api-error_description: Error message in Move Provisioned Dashboard form: Informs the user that the attempt to move a dashboard failed. This typically indicates an issue with the underlying API call.
move-provisioned-dashboard-form.current-file-not-found: Current dashboard file could not be found
move-provisioned-dashboard-form.current-file-not-found_description: Error message in the Move Provisioned Dashboard Form: This message informs the user that the file associated with the currently selected provisioned dashboard could not be located, preventing the intended move operation. It indicates a missing or inaccessible source file for the dashboard.
move-provisioned-dashboard-form.drawer-title_description: Drawer title in the form for moving provisioned dashboards: This is the heading for the user interface panel that allows users to relocate a dashboard created through a provisioning process.
move-provisioned-dashboard-form.file-load-error_description: Error message in the 'Move Provisioned Dashboard' form: Informs the user that a dashboard file failed to load during the process of moving or provisioning a dashboard.
move-provisioned-dashboard-form.loading-dashboard-data_description: Status Message in the Move Provisioned Dashboard Form: Indicates that dashboard data is currently being loaded. This message is displayed while the system fetches information necessary for moving or provisioning a dashboard.
move-provisioned-dashboard-form.move-action_description: Button in the "Move Provisioned Dashboard Form": This button initiates the action of moving a selected provisioned dashboard to a new folder or location.
move-provisioned-dashboard-form.move-read-only-message: This dashboard cannot be moved directly from Grafana because the repository is read-only. To move this dashboard, please move the file in your Git repository.
move-provisioned-dashboard-form.success-message_description: Success message in the 'Move Provisioned Dashboard' form: Informs the user that the dashboard was successfully moved to a new location.
move-provisioned-dashboard-form.target-path-label_description: Label in the 'Move Provisioned Dashboard' form: It indicates the destination path or new location where the selected provisioned dashboard will be moved. This field likely requires user input or displays the proposed target location.
move-provisioned-dashboard-form.title-this-repository-is-read-only_descr: Message in the "Move Provisioned Dashboard" form: Informs the user that the selected or target repository is read-only, preventing certain operations.

name-already-exists-error.body-name-already-exists: A dashboard with the same name in selected folder already exists. Would you still like to save this dashboard?
name-already-exists-error.title-name-already-exists_description: Error message in the dashboard creation or editing interface: Informs the user that the dashboard name they entered is already taken, preventing the creation or renaming of a dashboard with a duplicate name.

confirmText.proceed_description: Button label in a confirmation dialog: Confirms the user's decision to navigate from a dashboard snapshot to the original, live dashboard.

title.proceed-to-external-site_description: Dialog title in a confirmation prompt: Appears when a user attempts to access the original dashboard from a snapshot, and this action involves navigating to an external website. It asks for user confirmation before proceeding.

text.remove-panel: Are you sure you want to remove this panel?
text.remove-panel_description: Confirmation Dialog Message in Dashboard Scene: Asks the user for confirmation before permanently deleting a panel. This prompt helps prevent accidental data loss.

title.remove-panel_description: Confirmation dialog title in a dashboard scene: This title appears when a user attempts to remove a panel, clearly identifying the purpose of the confirmation prompt.

panel-data-alerting-tab.tab-label_description: Tab label in a dashboard panel's 'Data Alerting' section: Identifies the tab dedicated to configuring or displaying alerts for the panel's data.

alert.title-errors-loading-rules_description: Alert Title in the Alerting tab of a data panel on the Dashboard: This title informs the user that an error occurred while attempting to load rules for the panel.

panel-data-alerting-tab-rendered.error-failed-to-load: Failed to load Grafana rules state: {{errorToDisplay}}
panel-data-alerting-tab-rendered.error-failed-to-load_description: Error message in the Data Alerting tab of a dashboard panel: Informs the user when the system fails to load the current state of Grafana alert rules. The `{{errorToDisplay}}` placeholder provides the specific reason for the failure.
panel-data-alerting-tab-rendered.title-dashboard-not-saved_description: A status title in the Alerting or Data tab of a dashboard panel: It indicates that the dashboard currently has unsaved changes or has not been saved, which is often a prerequisite for configuring alerts or related data settings.

panel-data-queries-tab.tab-label_description: Tab label in the data queries section of a dashboard panel: This tab allows users to define and manage data queries for the panel.

panel-data-queries-tab-rendered.add-query_description: Button in the Data Queries tab of a dashboard panel: Allows users to add a new data source query to fetch and display data for the panel.
panel-data-queries-tab-rendered.expression_description: Label for a data input field within the Data Queries tab of a dashboard panel: It prompts the user to define an expression for data calculations or transformations.

panel-data-transformations-tab.tab-label_description: Tab Label in the Panel Data Transformations section of a dashboard: This label identifies the tab where users can configure data transformations applied to the data displayed within a panel.

panel-data-transformations-tab-rendered.add-another-transformation_description: Button in the Data Transformations tab of a panel: Allows users to add an additional data transformation step to the panel's data processing pipeline.
panel-data-transformations-tab-rendered.body-delete-all-transformations: By deleting all transformations, you will go back to the main selection screen.
panel-data-transformations-tab-rendered.body-delete-all-transformations_descript: Informational message in the Data Transformations tab of a dashboard panel: It informs the user that deleting all data transformations will revert the panel to its initial data source selection screen.
panel-data-transformations-tab-rendered.confirmText-delete-all_description: Button label in the Data Transformations tab within a Dashboard Panel: Confirms the action to delete all data transformations or related items.
panel-data-transformations-tab-rendered.delete-all-transformations_description: Button in the data transformations tab of a panel within a dashboard: Allows users to delete all data transformations configured for that specific panel.
panel-data-transformations-tab-rendered.title-delete-all-transformations_descrip: Confirmation prompt title in the Panel Data Transformations tab of the Dashboard scene: Asks the user to confirm the deletion of all data transformations.

panel-edit-controls.table-view-aria-label-toggletableview_de: Toggle button in panel edit controls within a dashboard: Allows users to switch between different display states or views of a table. This string serves as an ARIA label for accessibility.
panel-edit-controls.table-view-label-table-view_description: Selection option in panel edit controls: This option allows users to choose a display mode that presents data in a tabular format for a dashboard panel.

text.edit-panel_description: Button in the panel editor: Opens the configuration or content editing interface for a specific dashboard panel.

panel-editor-renderer.aria-label-open-options-pane_description: Button in the panel editor: Allows the user to open a pane containing various configuration options for the current panel. This string serves as an ARIA label for accessibility.
panel-editor-renderer.tooltip-open-options-pane_description: Tooltip in the Panel Editor: This text appears when hovering over an icon or button that opens the configuration options pane for the current dashboard panel.

panel-inspect-renderer.body-panel-plugin-not-loaded: Make sure the panel you want to inspect is visible and has been displayed before opening inspect.
panel-inspect-renderer.body-panel-plugin-not-loaded_description: Instruction message in the Panel Inspect Renderer within a Dashboard: Advises the user that the panel they wish to inspect must be visible and fully rendered before the inspection function can be accessed. This message appears when the panel is not yet ready for inspection.
panel-inspect-renderer.title-panel-plugin-not-loaded_descriptio: Error message title in the Panel Inspect Renderer within a dashboard: Informs the user that the plugin required to display or function for the selected panel could not be loaded, preventing its proper rendering.

text.extensions_description: Menu item in a dashboard panel's behavior settings, found within the section for asynchronous functions: This option allows users to manage or configure add-ons that extend the panel's functionality.
text.metrics-drilldown_description: Menu item in a panel's context menu on a dashboard: This option allows users to perform a drilldown action to view more granular details for the selected metrics.

label.suggestions_description: Label in the Panel Visualization Type Picker: This text labels a group of suggested visualization types presented as radio button options for a panel.
label.visualizations_description: Label in the Panel Visualization Type Picker: This label heads a section of radio button options where users choose the type of visualization for their dashboard panel.

provisioned-delete-modal.cannot-be-deleted: This dashboard is managed by Grafana provisioning and cannot be deleted. Remove the dashboard from the config file to delete it.
provisioned-delete-modal.file-path_description: Label in the Provisioned Dashboard Delete Modal: Displays the file path associated with the provisioned dashboard the user is intending to delete. This helps the user confirm which specific file will be affected by the deletion.
provisioned-delete-modal.see-docs: See <2>documentation</2> for more information about provisioning.
provisioned-delete-modal.see-docs_description: Link text in the provisioned delete modal: This text directs users to external documentation for more information about provisioning, which is relevant when considering the deletion of provisioned items.
provisioned-delete-modal.title-cannot-delete-provisioned-dashboar: Title in the "Provisioned Dashboard Delete" modal: Informs the user that a provisioned dashboard cannot be deleted directly through the UI. This message appears when a user attempts to delete a dashboard managed by provisioning.

query-editor.query_description: Label in the query editor: This text identifies the section or input field where users define the data retrieval query for a dashboard panel. It signifies the primary input for data source interaction.

query-variable-editor-form.description-examples: Named capture groups can be used to separate the display text and value ( <1>see examples</1> ).
query-variable-editor-form.description-examples_description: Explanatory text in the Query Variable Editor Form: This provides guidance on utilizing named capture groups to separate the display text and value for a query variable. It includes a link to examples for further detail.
query-variable-editor-form.description-optional: Optional, if you want to extract part of a series name or metric node segment.
query-variable-editor-form.description-optional_description: Description in the Query Variable Editor form in the Dashboard Scene: Explains the purpose of an optional field or setting that enables users to extract specific parts of series names or metric node segments.
query-variable-editor-form.label-data-source_description: Label in the Query Variable Editor Form: Identifies the input field where users select or specify the data source for a query variable within a dashboard scene.
query-variable-editor-form.label-static-options-sort_description: Label in the Query Variable Editor Form: This label introduces a control for setting the sort order of static options associated with a dashboard variable.
query-variable-editor-form.label-target-data-source_description: Label in the query variable editor form within a dashboard scene: This label identifies the input field where users specify the data source from which a query variable will fetch its values.
query-variable-editor-form.label-use-static-options_description: Label (e.g., for a toggle or checkbox) in the query variable editor form: This option allows the user to define a static list of values for a dashboard variable, rather than fetching them dynamically via a query.
query-variable-editor-form.name-regex_description: Label in the Query Variable Editor form in the Dashboard scene: This field label indicates an input for defining a regular expression.
query-variable-editor-form.query-options_description: Section heading in the Query Variable Editor Form within a Dashboard Scene: Identifies a group of settings that control how data queries for the variable are configured and executed.
query-variable-editor-form.selection-options_description: Section heading in the Query Variable Editor form: This heading introduces settings that control how users can select values for the variable, such as enabling multi-select or an 'All' option.
query-variable-editor-form.static-options-legend_description: Section label in the query variable editor form: It indicates the area for manually defining options for a dashboard variable, as opposed to options generated by a query.

label.classic_description: Label in the dashboard's resource export settings: This option designates a 'Classic' export format or method. It distinguishes it from other available export types.
label.v1-resource_description: Label in the resource export section of a dashboard: This identifies "V1 Resource" as a specific type of resource available for export.
label.v2-resource_description: Label in the Resource Export feature of a dashboard scene: This label identifies "V2 Resource" as an available option or type of resource that can be exported. It likely distinguishes it from other resource versions.

revert-dashboard-modal.body-restore-version: Are you sure you want to restore the dashboard to version {{version}}? All unsaved changes will be lost.
revert-dashboard-modal.confirmText-restore-version_description: Confirmation button text in the 'Revert Dashboard' modal: Confirms the user's action to restore the dashboard to a specific previous version. The `{{version}}` placeholder will display the version number.
revert-dashboard-modal.title-restore-version_description: Title in the 'Revert Dashboard' modal: This heading identifies the dialog where a user can restore a previous version of a dashboard.

save-button.save-and-overwrite_description: Option text in the Save dropdown menu within the Dashboard scene: This option allows the user to save the current dashboard, replacing any previously saved version.

save-dashboard-as-form.aria-label-save-dashboard-description-fi: Text input field in the 'Save Dashboard As' form: Allows users to enter a description for the dashboard they are saving. This string serves as the accessibility label (aria-label) for this field.
save-dashboard-as-form.aria-label-save-dashboard-title-field_de: An ARIA label for a text input field in the 'Save Dashboard As' form on the Dashboard Scene: It indicates where users should enter the title for the new dashboard they are saving. This label provides accessible information for screen readers.
save-dashboard-as-form.label-copy-tags_description: Label in the 'Save Dashboard As' form: This option allows users to copy the tags from the original dashboard when saving it as a new one.
save-dashboard-as-form.label-folder_description: Label in the "Save Dashboard As" form: This label prompts the user to select or specify the folder where the new dashboard will be saved. It helps organize dashboards within the system.

render-footer.title-failed-to-save-dashboard_descripti: Error message in the footer of the 'Save Dashboard As' form: Notifies the user when an attempt to save the dashboard has failed.

save-dashboard-as-form.required_description: Validation message in the "Save Dashboard As" form: This message indicates that a mandatory field, such as the dashboard name, must be filled before the dashboard can be saved.

tabs.label-changes_description: Tab label in the 'Save dashboard' drawer: This tab is used to display the modifications made to the dashboard, allowing users to review them before saving.
tabs.label-details_description: Tab label in the "Save Dashboard" drawer: Identifies a tab within the save workflow where users can view or edit the details (e.g., name, description, tags) of the dashboard being saved.
tabs.title: Save dashboard
tabs.title_description: Title of a tab in the 'Save dashboard' drawer: Identifies the tab dedicated to saving the current dashboard. This drawer is part of the dashboard scene.
tabs.title-copy_description: Title in the 'Save Dashboard' drawer: This title indicates the user is saving a duplicate copy of the dashboard, likely appearing in a tab or section dedicated to this action.
tabs.title-provisioned_description: Tab title in the "Save dashboard" drawer: It identifies a tab that displays or manages dashboards that have been provisioned (i.e., configured through files rather than the UI).

save-dashboard-form.aria-label-message_description: ARIA label for a text input field in the 'Save Dashboard' form: This label provides an accessible name for a field where the user can enter a message, such as a description or comment, when saving the dashboard.
save-dashboard-form.body-dashboard-drastically-changed: The dashboard will be saved using the new experimental Grafana dashboard schema. This action can’t be reverted and could result in the irreversible loss of data. We recommend that you save this dashboard as a copy instead. If you’re seeing this message in a production environment, contact Support to have the feature disabled.
save-dashboard-form.label-message_description: Label in the "Save Dashboard" form: This labels an input field where users can add a descriptive message or comment when saving a dashboard. This message is often used for version control or to provide context for the saved changes.
save-dashboard-form.placeholder-describe-changes-optional: Add a note to describe your changes (optional).
save-dashboard-form.placeholder-describe-changes-optional_de: Placeholder text in the 'Save Dashboard' form: It prompts the user to optionally add a note describing the changes made to the dashboard before saving.

render-footer.body-plugin-dashboard: Your changes will be lost when you update the plugin. Use Save as to create custom version.
render-footer.no-changes-to-save_description: Status message in the footer of the Save Dashboard form: Informs the user that no modifications have been made to the dashboard since its last save or opening. This indicates there are no changes currently needing to be saved.
render-footer.title-failed-to-save-dashboard_descripti: Error message title in the footer of the 'Save Dashboard' form: It informs the user that the attempt to save the dashboard was unsuccessful.
render-footer.title-plugin-dashboard_description: Title in the footer of the 'Save Dashboard' form: This title identifies the dashboard being saved or referenced as a 'plugin dashboard', distinguishing it from other dashboard types.
render-footer.title-someone-else-has-updated-this-dash: Someone else has updated this dashboard

Message in the Save Dashboard form footer: Informs the user about a version conflict when attempting to save changes to a dashboard that has been updated by another user since it was last loaded or edited by the current user.
render-footer.would-still-dashboard: Would you still like to save this dashboard?
render-footer.would-still-dashboard_description: Confirmation question in the footer of the Save Dashboard form: Prompts the user to confirm if they still want to save the dashboard despite a previous warning or potential issue that may have been presented.

save-dashboard-form.schema-v2-library-panels-export: Due to limitations in the new dashboard schema (V2), library panels will be converted to regular panels with embedded content during external export.
save-dashboard-form.schema-v2-library-panels-export-title: Library panels will be converted to regular panels
save-dashboard-form.schema-v2-library-panels-export-title_de: Informational title in the Save Dashboard Form: It explains that library panels within the dashboard will be converted into regular, standalone panels. This message informs the user that saving or exporting the dashboard will sever the link to the original library panel.
save-dashboard-form.title-dashboard-drastically-changed_desc: Title in the 'Save Dashboard' form: This title is displayed to inform the user that the dashboard has undergone irreversible changes. It likely appears in a warning or confirmation dialog when saving a significantly modified dashboard.

save-dashboard-form-common-options.save-refresh-description-current-refresh: Will make the current refresh the new default

Description in the 'Common Options' section of the 'Save Dashboard' form: This text explains that an associated option will make the dashboard's currently configured refresh setting the new default. It likely accompanies a checkbox or radio button for this action.
save-dashboard-form-common-options.save-refresh-label-update-default-refres: Label in the Common Options section of the Save Dashboard form: This label describes an option to update the system's default refresh interval for dashboards. It likely accompanies a checkbox or toggle that, when enabled, applies the current dashboard's refresh setting as the new default.
save-dashboard-form-common-options.save-timerange-description-current-range: Will make current time range the new default
Informational text in the 'Save Dashboard' form, under 'Common Options': This text describes the effect of an option that saves the currently active time range as the new default for the dashboard.
save-dashboard-form-common-options.save-timerange-label-update-default-time: Label in the common options section of the 'Save Dashboard' form: This option allows users to update the dashboard's default time range to reflect the currently selected time range when saving.
save-dashboard-form-common-options.save-variables-description-current-value: Will make the current values the new default

Description text in the 'Common Options' section of the 'Save Dashboard' form: This text describes an option that, when enabled, will save the dashboard's currently selected variable values as their new default settings for future loads.
save-dashboard-form-common-options.save-variables-label-update-default-vari: Option label in the 'Common Options' section of the 'Save Dashboard' form: This option allows users to update the default values of variables configured in the dashboard to their current selections when the dashboard is saved.
save-dashboard-form-common-options.show-variables-warning-alert-body: Some variables failed to load. If you keep “Update default variable values” checked, the current (failed) values will become the dashboard defaults. You can save anyway or uncheck the option to avoid storing those (failed) values.
save-dashboard-form-common-options.show-variables-warning-alert-title_descr: Alert Title in the Save Dashboard form's common options section: It notifies the user that one or more variable queries associated with the dashboard failed to execute. This warning is displayed when attempting to save the dashboard.

save-library-viz-panel-modal.affected-dashboards_one: This update will affect <1>{{count}} dashboards.</1> The following dashboards using the panel will be affected:
save-library-viz-panel-modal.affected-dashboards_other: This update will affect <1>{{count}} dashboards.</1> The following dashboards using the panel will be affected:
save-library-viz-panel-modal.dashboard-name_description: Label in the 'Save Library Visualization Panel' modal: Specifies the input field where the user provides the name of the dashboard that will contain the library visualization panel.
save-library-viz-panel-modal.discard_description: Button in the 'Save library visualization panel' modal dialog on the dashboard scene: Allows the user to cancel the operation and discard any unsaved changes or selections made for the library panel.
save-library-viz-panel-modal.placeholder-search-affected-dashboards_d: Placeholder text in the "Save Library Visualization Panel" modal: It prompts the user to search for dashboards that are affected by changes to the library visualization panel being saved.
save-library-viz-panel-modal.update-all_description: Button in the Save Library Visualization Panel modal: This button updates all instances of the modified library visualization panel across all dashboards where it is used.

save-provisioned-dashboard-form.api-error_description: Error message in the Save Provisioned Dashboard form: Informs the user that the operation to save a provisioned dashboard failed due to an underlying API error.
save-provisioned-dashboard-form.copy-json-to-clipboard_description: Button in the Save Provisioned Dashboard Form: Allows users to copy the dashboard's JSON definition to their clipboard. This is useful for programmatic management, backup, or sharing of dashboard configurations.
save-provisioned-dashboard-form.file-path_description: Label in the 'Save Provisioned Dashboard' form: Displays the file path associated with the provisioned dashboard. This helps users identify or confirm the location of the dashboard's configuration file.
save-provisioned-dashboard-form.label-target-folder_description: Label in the 'Save Provisioned Dashboard' form: It indicates the input field where the user specifies the destination folder for the provisioned dashboard. This helps organize where the dashboard will be stored within the system.
save-provisioned-dashboard-form.save-json-to-file_description: Button in the 'Save Provisioned Dashboard' form: Allows the user to save the current dashboard's JSON configuration to a local file.
save-provisioned-dashboard-form.title-required_description: Validation message in the 'Save Provisioned Dashboard' form: Informs the user that the dashboard title field is mandatory when attempting to save a provisioned dashboard.
save-provisioned-dashboard-form.title-same-as-folder: Dashboard name cannot be the same as the folder name
save-provisioned-dashboard-form.title-same-as-folder_description: Validation message in the Save Provisioned Dashboard Form within the Dashboard Scene: Informs the user that a dashboard's name cannot be identical to its containing folder. This validation rule is displayed when attempting to save a provisioned dashboard.
save-provisioned-dashboard-form.title-validation-failed_description: Error message in the "Save Provisioned Dashboard" form: Informs the user that the title entered for the provisioned dashboard failed to meet validation requirements. This prevents the dashboard from being saved.

scenes-new-rule-from-panel-button.body-no-alerting-capable-query-found: Cannot create alerts from this panel because no query to an alerting capable datasource is found.
scenes-new-rule-from-panel-button.body-no-alerting-capable-query-found_des: Informational message in a dialog or notification: Explains to the user why an alert rule cannot be created from a dashboard panel. This occurs when the panel lacks queries to an alerting-capable datasource.
scenes-new-rule-from-panel-button.new-alert-rule_description: Button label in a dashboard scene, likely accessible from a panel's options or a context menu. This button allows users to initiate the creation of a new alert rule.
scenes-new-rule-from-panel-button.title-no-alerting-capable-query-found_de: Informational message: Displayed when a user attempts to create a new alerting rule from a dashboard panel, indicating that the selected panel's query is not capable of being used for alerts.

selection-options-form.description-enables-multiple-values-sele: Enables multiple values to be selected at the same time
Description in the selection options form within a dashboard: This text clarifies that the associated control allows users to select multiple values simultaneously.
selection-options-form.description-enables-option-include-varia: Enables an option to include all values

Descriptive text in the Dashboard Scene's Selection Options Form: This text explains an option that, when enabled, allows the inclusion of all values for dashboard variables. It clarifies the purpose of a setting related to variable selection.
selection-options-form.description-enables-users-custom-values: Enables users to add custom values to the list
selection-options-form.description-enables-users-custom-values_: Description text in the selection options form of a dashboard scene: This text explains that users can add custom, unique values to the list of selectable options for that form.
selection-options-form.name-allow-custom-values_description: Checkbox in the Selection Options form within the Dashboard editor: Specifies whether users are permitted to manually enter values for a selection field (e.g., a variable or filter) that are not part of a predefined list. When enabled, users can input their own custom selections instead of being restricted to predefined choices.
selection-options-form.name-custom-all-value_description: Label for an input field in the selection options form of a dashboard scene: This allows users to define a custom display text or identifier for the 'All' option in a filter or selector.
selection-options-form.name-include-all-option_description: Name of an option in Selection Options Form within the Dashboard Scene: This name identifies a user choice that, when selected, includes all available items in a selection.
selection-options-form.name-multi-value_description: Label in the selection options form within a dashboard scene: It identifies a setting or capability where multiple values can be selected or processed.

share-button.aria-label-sharedropdownmenu_description: ARIA label for a button in the Share button component within the dashboard scene: This label provides an accessible description for the button, indicating that it toggles (opens/closes) the share menu.

description.dashboard-troubleshoot-visualization-iss: Dashboard JSON used to help troubleshoot visualization issues
Description text in the Support Snapshot Service feature for a dashboard: Explains that the Dashboard JSON included in the snapshot is used to help troubleshoot visualization issues.

label.git-hub-comment_description: Label in the Support Snapshot Service section of a Dashboard Scene: Identifies a field or section for entering or displaying comments intended for GitHub, typically when creating a support snapshot.
label.panel-support-snapshot_description: Label in the dashboard's support snapshot service: This text names the feature or action used to generate a diagnostic snapshot of a specific dashboard panel for support purposes.

title.copy-to-clipboard-failed_description: Error message in the Support Snapshot service within a dashboard scene: It notifies the user that an attempt to copy content, likely a snapshot detail or related information, to the clipboard was unsuccessful.

switch-variable-form.disabled-value_description: Label in the Switch Variable form within a Dashboard scene: Identifies an option or value that is currently disabled or unavailable for selection.
switch-variable-form.disabled-value-description_description: Description text in the Switch Variable form on a dashboard: This text clarifies that the associated input field is used to set the value for a switch variable when it is disabled.
switch-variable-form.enabled-value_description: Label in the Switch Variable form: Indicates the specific value associated with the 'enabled' state of a switch variable.
switch-variable-form.enabled-value-description_description: Description in the Switch variable configuration form: This text labels an input field that defines the value used when the associated switch is enabled. It clarifies what setting an adjacent input field controls.
switch-variable-form.same-values-error: Enabled and disabled values cannot be the same
switch-variable-form.same-values-error_description: Error message in the Switch Variable configuration form: This message indicates that the values configured for the 'enabled' and 'disabled' states of a switch variable must be distinct. It ensures the switch can clearly differentiate between its two states.
switch-variable-form.switch-options_description: Section title in the Switch Variable configuration form: This title introduces a set of configurable options related to a switch variable's behavior or display on a dashboard.
switch-variable-form.value-pair-type_description: Label in the Switch Variable Form within a Dashboard Scene: This field allows users to specify the type or format of value pairs used for the switch variable.
switch-variable-form.value-pair-type-description: Choose the type of values for the switch states
switch-variable-form.value-pair-type-description_description: **Helper Text** in the **Switch Variable Form within the Dashboard Scene**: This text guides the user to select the data type or format for the values associated with each state of a switch variable. It clarifies how these variable states will be defined and interpreted.

text-box-variable.name-default-value_description: Label in Text box variable configuration within a dashboard: This label identifies the input field where the user defines the initial or pre-selected value for a text input variable.

text-box-variable-form.placeholder-default-value-if-any_descrip: Placeholder text in the Text Box Variable Form on a dashboard: It indicates that providing a default value for the text box variable is optional.
text-box-variable-form.text-options_description: Section header in the text box variable configuration form: This header introduces settings for customizing the text properties of a text box variable within a dashboard.

textbox-variable-form.label-value_description: Label in a textbox variable form on a dashboard: Identifies the input field where users define or select the specific value for a dashboard variable.

title.discard-changes-to-dashboard_description: Confirmation Dialog Title in the Dashboard scene: Prompts the user to confirm discarding unsaved changes to the dashboard. This dialog appears when the user attempts to leave or close a dashboard with unsaved modifications.

label.dashboard_description: Label in the results section of the 'Transform Usages to Network' feature: This string identifies a dashboard object or category within the generated output.

search-box-suffix.tooltip-clear-search_description: Tooltip in a search box within the transformations drawer on a dashboard scene: It explains that the associated button or icon will clear the current search query.

unlink-modal.body-unlink-panel: If you unlink this panel, you will be able to edit it without affecting any other dashboards. However, once you make a change you will not be able to revert to its original reusable panel.
unlink-modal.confirmText-yes-unlink_description: Confirmation button in the unlink confirmation modal: This button allows the user to confirm their decision to unlink an item or entity. It serves as the affirmative option in a confirmation prompt.
unlink-modal.title-really-unlink-panel: Do you really want to unlink this panel?
unlink-modal.title-really-unlink-panel_description: Confirmation Question in Unlink Modal within a Dashboard Scene: Prompts the user to confirm their intention to unlink a specific panel, indicating this action might be significant or irreversible.

unsaved-changes-modal.changes: Do you want to save your changes?
unsaved-changes-modal.changes_description: Question prompt in the unsaved changes modal on the dashboard scene: This asks the user if they want to save their modifications to the dashboard. It typically appears when the user attempts to close or navigate away without saving.
unsaved-changes-modal.discard_description: Button in the Unsaved Changes modal on a dashboard: Allows the user to abandon any unsaved changes made to the dashboard. Selecting this will close the modal without saving the changes.
unsaved-changes-modal.save-dashboard_description: Button in the 'Unsaved Changes' modal on the dashboard scene: Allows the user to save the current dashboard's modifications. This confirms the user's intent to apply unsaved changes before proceeding.
unsaved-changes-modal.title-unsaved-changes_description: Title in Unsaved Changes Modal within the Dashboard scene: Informs the user that modifications have been made to the dashboard that have not yet been saved.

text.settings_description: Navigation item label in the dashboard's edit page navigation: It allows users to access and configure settings for the dashboard.

use-save-dashboard.message-dashboard-saved_description: Confirmation message in the dashboard view: Indicates that the user's changes to the dashboard have been successfully saved.

variable-check-indicator.aria-label-variable-referenced-dashboard: This variable is not referenced by any variable or dashboard.
An accessibility label for a variable check indicator in the dashboard scene. This label informs users that the associated dashboard variable is not referenced by any other variable or dashboard.
variable-check-indicator.aria-label-variable-referenced-other-var: This variable is referenced by other variables or dashboard.

ARIA label for an indicator in the dashboard variable management UI: This label describes an indicator that signifies a specific dashboard variable is referenced by other variables or the dashboard itself. This helps users understand dependencies when managing variables.
variable-check-indicator.content-variable-not-referenced-other-va: This variable is not referenced by other variables or dashboard.

Informational message in a variable check indicator: It informs the user that a specific dashboard variable lacks references from other variables or the dashboard itself. This helps in identifying potentially unused or redundant variables.
variable-check-indicator.content-variable-referenced-other-variab: This variable is referenced by other variables or dashboard.

Status message in a variable's status indicator within the dashboard's variable editor: It informs the user that the current variable is referenced by other variables or by the dashboard itself. This highlights its dependencies.

variable-editor-form.aria-label-variable-editor-form_descript: Aria-label for a form in the Variable Editor within the Dashboard Scene: This label provides an accessible name for the form where users can create, edit, and manage dashboard variables.
variable-editor-form.back-to-list_description: Button in the variable editor form: Allows the user to navigate back to the list of dashboard variables.

confirmText.delete-variable_description: Confirmation button in a dialog within the variable editor form: Confirms the deletion of a dashboard variable.

variable-editor-form.description-optional-display-name_descri: Label in the variable editor form within a dashboard: This label identifies an optional input field for users to provide a display name for a dashboard variable. This name determines how the variable is presented in the UI.
variable-editor-form.description-template-variable-characters: The name of the template variable. (Max. 50 characters)
variable-editor-form.general_description: Section label in the variable editor form: Designates the area containing general or foundational settings for the dashboard variable being edited.
variable-editor-form.name-label_description: Label in the variable editor form: Specifies the user-facing display name for a variable. This is the human-readable title displayed in the dashboard, distinct from the variable's internal name or ID.
variable-editor-form.placeholder-descriptive-text_description: Placeholder text in an input field within the variable editor form on a dashboard scene: It prompts the user to enter a descriptive text for the variable being configured.
variable-editor-form.placeholder-label-name_description: Placeholder text in the Variable Editor Form in the Dashboard Scene: Guides the user to input the display name (label) for a variable or a variable option.
variable-editor-form.placeholder-variable-name_description: Placeholder text in a variable editor form: Prompts the user to enter a name for the variable being created or edited within a dashboard.

title.delete-variable_description: Title in the variable editor form: This string serves as the heading for a dialog or section dedicated to deleting a dashboard variable.

variable-editor-list.definition_description: Heading in the variable editor list within a dashboard scene: It labels the section where the properties and characteristics of a dashboard variable are defined.
variable-editor-list.new-variable_description: Button in the variable editor list within a dashboard: Allows the user to create a new dashboard variable. This button typically opens a form or dialog for defining the variable's properties.
variable-editor-list.variable_description: Label in the Variable Editor List within the Dashboard Scene: This string identifies a dashboard variable or serves as a general label related to variables. It likely functions as a column header or an item in a list for managing dashboard variables.

variable-editor-list-row.body-delete-variable: Are you sure you want to delete: {{variable}}?
variable-editor-list-row.body-delete-variable_description: Confirmation message in the Dashboard's Variable Editor: Prompts the user to confirm the deletion of a specific dashboard variable.
variable-editor-list-row.confirmText-delete-variable_description: Confirmation text in a dialog for deleting a variable: Prompts the user to confirm the permanent removal of a dashboard variable. This text appears when a user clicks to delete a variable from the list in the variable editor.
variable-editor-list-row.title-delete-variable_description: Button text in a row of the variable editor list: Initiates the deletion of the corresponding dashboard variable.
variable-editor-list-row.tooltip-duplicate-variable_description: Tooltip in a variable editor list row within the dashboard scene: Appears on hover over an icon or button, indicating that it will duplicate the selected variable.
variable-editor-list-row.tooltip-remove-variable_description: Tooltip in a variable editor list row within the dashboard scene: Appears when hovering over an icon or button used to remove a specific dashboard variable.

label.label: Label
label.label_description: Option Label in the variable hide options within the dashboard scene: This label identifies an option that controls the visibility of a dashboard variable's display label.
label.nothing_description: Option Label in the variable hide options selection on a dashboard: It indicates that no specific hiding behavior should be applied to variables.
label.variable_description: Label in the variable hide options section: This label identifies an option or control related to configuring the visibility of dashboard variables.

variable-hide-select.label: Hide
variable-hide-select.label_description: Label for an option in the variable hide selection component of a dashboard: It indicates the action to hide a dashboard variable. This allows users to control variable visibility within the dashboard.

variable-type-select.name-variable-type_description: Label in the variable type selection component on a Dashboard configuration screen: This label prompts the user to select the specific type for a dashboard variable being created or edited. This determines how the variable's values are populated (e.g., Query, Custom, Textbox).

variable-usages-button.title-show-usages_description: Title in the variable usages panel: This title indicates which specific dashboard variable's usages are currently being displayed to the user. The `{{variableId}}` placeholder is dynamically replaced with the variable's identifier.
variable-usages-button.tooltip-show-usages_description: Tooltip on a button within a dashboard: This tooltip indicates that the button displays where variables are used.

variable-values-preview.preview-of-values_description: Heading in the Variable Values Preview section: This indicates that the displayed content is a preliminary view of the possible values for a dashboard variable.
variable-values-preview.show-more_description: Button in the variable values preview section of a dashboard: Displays additional variable values that are currently hidden or truncated.

comparison.button-restore_description: Button in the version history comparison view for a dashboard: Allows the user to restore the current dashboard to a selected historical version.

version-history-comparison.label-view-json-diff_description: Button in the Version History Comparison feature of a dashboard: It allows users to view the JSON differences between two selected dashboard versions.
version-history-comparison.new-version-updated_description: Message in the Version History Comparison view of a Dashboard Scene: It informs the user that a dashboard version has been updated, specifying the version number, the editor, and the time since the update.
version-history-comparison.old-version-updated_description: History entry text in the Version History Comparison screen: It describes a specific older version of a dashboard, detailing its version number, the user who updated it, and when the update occurred. This text aids in understanding the modification history of a selected old version.

version-history-header.compare-versions_description: Header in the Version History header of the Dashboard scene: This header displays which two specific versions of a dashboard are currently being compared. The `<3></3>` tag acts as a non-translatable separator between the two version placeholders.
version-history-header.latest_description: Label in the version history header within a dashboard view: Identifies the most current or recently saved version of the dashboard.
version-history-header.tooltip-reset-version_description: Tooltip in the dashboard's version history header: Describes an action to revert the dashboard to a previous saved version or discard current unsaved changes.

version-history-table.aria-label-toggle-selection_description: ARIA label for a toggle or checkbox in the Version History table within the Dashboard scene. This label is used for accessibility, describing the action of selecting or deselecting a specific dashboard version to screen reader users.
version-history-table.date_description: Table header in the Version History table on the dashboard scene: It labels the column indicating the date of a specific dashboard version.
version-history-table.name-latest_description: Label in the version history table: Identifies the most recent or current version of a dashboard listed in the table. This helps users quickly spot the active version.
version-history-table.notes_description: Column header in the version history table of the dashboard scene: It displays descriptive comments or remarks associated with each dashboard version.
version-history-table.restore_description: Button in the Version History table on the Dashboard view: Allows users to revert the dashboard to a selected previous version.
version-history-table.updated-by_description: Column header in the version history table within the Dashboard scene: This label indicates the user who performed a specific update or change to a dashboard version.
version-history-table.version_description: Column header in the Version History table: This labels the column that displays the version number of a dashboard.

versions-history-buttons.compare-versions_description: Button in the Versions History section within a dashboard: Allows users to initiate a comparison between different versions of the dashboard.
versions-history-buttons.content-select-two-versions-to-start-com: Select two versions to start comparing
Instructional text in the Versions History section of a dashboard: It guides the user to select two specific dashboard versions before they can proceed with comparing them.
versions-history-buttons.show-more-versions_description: Button in the dashboard's version history section: Allows users to expand and view more available historical versions of the dashboard. It typically appears when a truncated list of versions is initially displayed.

visualization-button.aria-label-change-visualization_descript: An ARIA label for the Visualization button in a dashboard scene: It describes the button's function to change the type of visualization being displayed.
visualization-button.tooltip-click-to-change-visualization_de: Tooltip on a visualization button in the dashboard scene: Informs the user that clicking the button will change the current visualization.

viz-and-data-pane.aria-label-open-query-pane_description: Button in the Visualization and Data Pane within a Dashboard Scene: Used to open or display the query editor section for a visualization.
viz-and-data-pane.tooltip-open-query-pane_description: Tooltip text in the Viz and Data pane on a dashboard: This text appears on hover over a control, indicating that clicking it will open the query configuration pane for the visualization.

viz-panel-links-renderer.aria-label-panel-links_description: Label in a dashboard visualization panel: Identifies a collection of navigational links associated with that panel. This string serves as an ARIA label for accessibility purposes.

dashboard-scene-help-wizard.support-bundle: You can also retrieve a support bundle containing information concerning your Grafana instance and configured datasources in the <1>support bundles section</1>.

annotations.title: Annotations
annotations.title_description: Section title in the dashboard settings: This labels the section where users configure and manage annotations for the dashboard.

dashboard-settings.dashboard-delete-button_description: Button in Dashboard settings: Allows the user to delete the current dashboard.
dashboard-settings.delete-modal-restore-dashboards-text: This action will mark the dashboard for deletion in 30 days. Your organization administrator can restore it anytime before the 30 days expire.
dashboard-settings.delete-modal-text: Do you want to delete this dashboard?
dashboard-settings.delete-modal-text_description: Prompt in the delete confirmation modal within Dashboard Settings: Asks the user to confirm their intention to delete the dashboard.

general.auto-refresh-description: Define the auto refresh intervals that should be available in the auto refresh list. Use the format '5s' for seconds, '1m' for minutes, '1h' for hours, and '1d' for days (e.g.: '5s,10s,30s,1m,5m,15m,30m,1h,2h,1d').
general.auto-refresh-label_description: Label in the General section of Dashboard Settings: This label identifies the control that enables or disables automatic data refreshing for the dashboard.
general.editable-description: Set to read-only to disable all editing. Reload the dashboard for changes to take effect
general.editable-description_description: Descriptive text in the General settings section of the Dashboard settings: This text explains that setting a dashboard to read-only will disable all editing capabilities. It also instructs the user to reload the dashboard for these changes to take effect.
general.editable-label_description: Label for a setting in the 'General' section of the Dashboard Settings: This setting controls whether the dashboard can be edited by users with appropriate permissions. It likely accompanies a toggle or checkbox.
general.folder-label_description: Label in the General settings section of dashboard settings: Identifies the organizational unit for the dashboard, typically indicating where the dashboard is located or will be saved.
general.panel-options-graph-tooltip-description: Controls tooltip and hover highlight behavior across different panels. Reload the dashboard for changes to take effect
general.panel-options-graph-tooltip-label_descri: Label in the General settings section of a dashboard, under Panel Options: This identifies the pop-up box that displays information when a user hovers over a data point on a graph.
general.panel-options-label_description: Label in the General section of Dashboard Settings: This heading introduces configuration settings specifically for individual panels on the dashboard.
general.panels-preload-description: When enabled all panels will start loading as soon as the dashboard has been loaded.
general.panels-preload-description_description: Description for a setting in the General section of Dashboard Settings: This text explains that enabling the associated option will cause all dashboard panels to start loading immediately after the dashboard itself has finished loading.
general.panels-preload-label_description: Label in the General settings section of dashboard settings: This text identifies an option allowing users to configure the preloading behavior of dashboard panels.
general.tags-label_description: Label in the General settings section of Dashboard settings: This indicates the field where users can input or view tags for the dashboard, used for categorization.
general.title: General
general.title_description: Section header in Dashboard Settings: Groups common or basic configuration options for the dashboard.

json-editor.save-button_description: Button in the JSON Editor within Dashboard Settings: Saves any modifications made to the dashboard's JSON configuration.
json-editor.subtitle: The JSON model below is the data structure that defines the dashboard. This includes dashboard settings, panel settings, layout, queries, and so on.
json-editor.title: JSON Model
json-editor.title_description: Title in the JSON editor section of the dashboard settings: It identifies the section where users can view or directly edit the dashboard's underlying JSON model.

links.title: Links
links.title_description: Title in Dashboard Settings: Identifies the section where users can view or configure links related to the dashboard.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.title_description: Title in the Permissions section within the Dashboard Settings: This title identifies the area where users can manage access rights and privileges for the specific dashboard.

provisioned-delete-modal.text-1: This dashboard is managed by Grafana provisioning and cannot be deleted. Remove the dashboard from the config file to delete it.
provisioned-delete-modal.text-2: See grafana documentation for more information about provisioning.
provisioned-delete-modal.text-2_description: Informational text in the provisioned delete modal within Dashboard Settings: This text provides a helpful reference to Grafana documentation for users seeking more information about dashboard provisioning. It appears when a user is interacting with provisioned dashboards, specifically regarding deletion.
provisioned-delete-modal.text-3_description: Text label in the provisioned dashboard delete confirmation modal within Dashboard Settings: Displays the file path of the provisioned dashboard that is about to be deleted. This helps the user confirm they are deleting the correct item.
provisioned-delete-modal.text-link_description: Text link in the modal for deleting provisioned dashboards within Dashboard Settings: Navigates the user to the official documentation page. This link likely provides more information or guidance related to provisioned dashboards or the deletion process.
provisioned-delete-modal.title: Cannot delete provisioned dashboard
provisioned-delete-modal.title_description: Title in a modal dialog within Dashboard Settings: Informs the user that they cannot delete a provisioned dashboard through the UI. Provisioned dashboards are managed by configuration files.

settings.title: Settings
settings.title_description: Section title in the Dashboard Settings section: Labels the page or tab where users can configure various settings related to the current dashboard.

time-picker.hide-time-picker_description: Checkbox/Toggle in the Time Picker section of the Dashboard Settings: This option controls the visibility of the time picker UI element on the dashboard.
time-picker.now-delay-description: Exclude recent data that may be incomplete.
time-picker.now-delay-description_description: Help text in the 'Now delay' setting within the Time Picker configuration in Dashboard Settings: It explains the purpose of the 'Now delay' option. This option allows users to exclude very recent data that might be incomplete or still processing.
time-picker.now-delay-label_description: Label in the time-picker section within Dashboard Settings: This label identifies a setting used to configure a delay for the "now" timestamp in dashboard time ranges. This delay helps account for data ingestion or processing latency.
time-picker.refresh-live-dashboards-description: Continuously update panels when the time range includes the current time
time-picker.refresh-live-dashboards-description_desc: Description text in the Time Picker section of the Dashboard Settings: Explains that dashboard panels will continuously update when the selected time range includes the current time, enabling a live data view.
time-picker.refresh-live-dashboards-label_descriptio: Label in the dashboard's time picker settings: This text identifies an option or action to refresh dashboards that display live or real-time data.
time-picker.time-options-label_description: Label in the Time Picker settings within Dashboard Settings: This label introduces a section containing various configurations related to time range selection and display for the dashboard.
time-picker.time-zone-label_description: Label in the Time Picker settings within the Dashboard Settings: Identifies the field where users select the time zone for the dashboard.
time-picker.week-start-label_description: Label in the Time Picker section of the Dashboard Settings: It prompts the user to define which day should be considered the start of the week. This setting affects how weeks are displayed and calculated within the dashboard.

variables.title: Variables
variables.title_description: Section Title in the Variables section within Dashboard Settings: Identifies the area for defining and managing dashboard variables.

versions.title: Versions
versions.title_description: Section Title in the Dashboard Settings: This title identifies the area dedicated to managing different versions of a dashboard.

panel-queries.add-from-saved-queries_description: Button in the Panel Queries section of a dashboard: Used to add pre-existing, saved queries as data sources for the current panel. This streamlines the process of configuring data by leveraging reusable queries.

dependencies.button_description: Button in the Dependencies section within the Variables settings of a dashboard: Allows the user to view or display the dependencies related to the dashboard variables.
dependencies.title: Dependencies
dependencies.title_description: Section Title in the Dashboard Variables settings: It introduces a section where users can define or view relationships and conditions between dashboard variables.

dashlist.category-dashboard-list_description: Title in the Dashboard List view: Labels the main area that displays a list of dashboards.
dashlist.name-folder_description: Label in Dashlist: Designates a folder item within the dashboard list. It serves as a container for organizing dashboards.
dashlist.name-include-current-template-variables_: **Checkbox** in **Dashboard sharing or linking options**: This option allows users to include the currently selected template variable values when generating a shareable link or saving a dashboard. It ensures the shared or saved dashboard reflects a specific data context.
dashlist.name-include-current-time-range_descript: Checkbox in a Dashlist save or share dialog: Determines if the currently applied time range for the dashboard is included when saving its configuration or generating a shareable link.
dashlist.name-max-items_description: Setting in dashboard list configuration: Defines the maximum number of dashboards or items allowed to be displayed in a dashboard list.
dashlist.name-query_description: Label for an input field in the Dashboard List's search/filter section: It prompts users to enter a search term to find dashboards by their name.
dashlist.name-recently-viewed_description: Section title in Dashboard list view: Identifies a section or filter that displays a list of dashboards the user has recently viewed.
dashlist.name-search_description: Placeholder text or label for a search input field in the Dashboards List: Enables users to search for dashboards, typically by their name.
dashlist.name-show-folder-names_description: Option in the Dashlist: Controls the visibility of folder names. This allows users to see the hierarchical structure of dashboards.
dashlist.name-show-headings_description: Toggle button in the Dashboard list view settings: Controls whether category headings are displayed in the dashboard list.
dashlist.name-starred_description: Label in the Dashboard list: Identifies a filter or category used to display dashboards that the user has marked as favorites.
dashlist.name-tags_description: Column header or filter label in the Dashboard List: Used to display or filter dashboards based on their associated tags.

empty-state.button-title_description: Button in the empty state of the data source list: Allows users to add a new data source when no data sources have been configured yet.
empty-state.pro-tip: You can also define data sources through configuration files. <2>Learn more</2>
empty-state.pro-tip_description: Pro-tip message in the empty state of the data source list page: Informs users of an alternative method to define data sources via configuration files, including a link to learn more.
empty-state.title: No data sources defined
empty-state.title_description: Title in the empty state of the data source list: Informs the user that no data sources have been configured. This message prompts the user to add new data sources.

data-source-picker.add-new-data-source_description: Button or link in the data source picker component: Allows users to initiate the configuration process for adding a new data source.

built-in-list.description-dashboard: Reuse query results from other visualizations
