built-in-list.description-dashboard_description: Description text in the built-in data source list for the 'Dashboard' option: This text explains that selecting 'Dashboard' as a data source allows users to reuse query results from other visualizations on the same dashboard, improving performance and data consistency.
built-in-list.description-grafana_description: Description text in the list of built-in data sources within the data source picker: It describes an option, likely related to Grafana's built-in features, that allows users to explore visualizations using mock data. This is intended for demonstration or testing purposes.
built-in-list.description-mixed_description: Description text in the built-in list section of the data source picker: It explains the functionality or option to use multiple data sources. This typically refers to combining data from various sources.

list.no-data-source-message_description: Message in the data source picker list: Informs the user that no data sources were found or are currently available for selection in the list.

modal.configure-new-data-source: Open a new tab and configure a data source
modal.configure-new-data-source_description: Button label in a modal dialog within the data source picker: Guides the user to open a new browser tab to configure a new data source.
modal.input-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a data source picker modal: It prompts the user to select or search for a data source within the input field.
modal.title: Select data source
modal.title_description: Title in the modal of the data source picker: Prompts the user to select a data source.

data-source-picker.open-advanced-button_description: Button in Data Source Picker: Opens an advanced version of the data source selection interface. This provides additional or more complex options for selecting data sources.

data-source-testing-status-page.error-more-details-link: Click <2>here</2> to learn more about this error.
data-source-testing-status-page.error-more-details-link_description: Link in the Data Source Testing Status Page: Provides additional details about a specific error encountered during data source testing. The word "here" is the clickable element.
data-source-testing-status-page.success-more-details-links: Next, you can start to visualize data by <2>building a dashboard</2> , or by querying data in the <5>Explore view</5> .

datasource-add-button.label: Add new data source
datasource-add-button.label_description: Button label on the Data Sources management page: Used to initiate the process of adding a new data source to BMC Helix Dashboards.

empty-state.message_description: Message in the data sources empty state: Informs the user that no data sources are currently configured or available. This message is displayed when the list of data sources is empty.

add-column.placeholder-column-name_description: Placeholder text in an input field for adding a new column to a datagrid: It prompts the user to enter the desired name for the column.

render-context-menu-items.label-clear-column_description: Context menu item in a datagrid: This action clears any filters, sorting, or selections applied to a specific column. It does not remove the column itself from the datagrid.
render-context-menu-items.label-clear-row_description: Menu item in a data grid's context menu: Used to clear the contents or reset the values of the selected row. This action typically empties the row without deleting the row structure itself.
render-context-menu-items.label-remove-all-data_description: Menu item in the context menu of a datagrid: Allows the user to clear or delete all data displayed within or associated with the grid.

render-header-menu-items.label-clear-column_description: Menu item in the context menu of a data grid column header: Allows the user to clear filters or other applied settings for that specific column.
render-header-menu-items.label-delete-column_description: Menu item label in the header context menu of a datagrid: This option allows users to delete a column from the datagrid.
render-header-menu-items.label-rename-column_description: Menu item in the header context menu of a data grid: Allows users to change the name of a selected column.
render-header-menu-items.label-set-field-type_description: Menu item in the context menu of a data grid column header: Allows users to define or change the data type for the selected column.

add-new-data-source-button.tooltip-no-permission: You do not have permission to configure new data sources
add-new-data-source-button.tooltip-no-permission_description: Tooltip on the "Add new data source" button in the Data Sources section: Explains that the user lacks the necessary permissions to configure new data sources. This tooltip appears when the button is disabled due to insufficient privileges.

alert-success-message.aria-label-create-a-dashboard_descriptio: ARIA label for an interactive element (e.g., button or link) in a success alert message within the Data Sources section. It provides an accessible name for the action to create a new dashboard, likely offered as a follow-up after a successful data source operation.
alert-success-message.aria-label-explore-data_description: Action button's `aria-label` in a datasource success alert message: Allows users to immediately navigate to explore data from the newly configured or updated datasource.

alerting-enabled.text-alerting-not-supported_description: Text in Data Source Settings: Informs the user that the current data source does not support alerting functionality. It appears when the system attempts to display or configure alerting capabilities for that data source.
alerting-enabled.text-alerting-supported_description: Text label in a data source's alerting settings: This string indicates that the configured data source possesses the capability to be used for creating and managing alerts.

basic-serttings.tooltip-name: The name is used when you select the data source in panels. The default data source is preselected in new panels.

basic-settings.aria-label-datasource-settings-page-basi: ARIA label for a section on the Datasource settings page: It provides an accessible description for screen readers, indicating the fundamental configuration options for a datasource.
basic-settings.label-default_description: Label in the Basic Settings for Datasources: It identifies or allows the selection of a primary or standard option. This might designate a default datasource or a default value for a setting.

title.cloud_description: Category title in the Data Sources 'Build Categories' section: It labels a category specifically for cloud-based data sources.
title.distributed-tracing_description: Category Name in the Data Sources build categories: Labels a group of data source types that provide distributed tracing capabilities, allowing users to filter and select relevant data sources.
title.enterprise-plugins_description: Category title in the Data Sources configuration interface: Labels a category specifically grouping data source plugins designed for enterprise use, helping users find relevant integrations.
title.industrial-io-t_description: Category title in the Data Sources category builder: Identifies a category for grouping data sources related to industrial and Internet of Things (IoT) applications.
title.logging-document-databases_description: Category title in the Data Sources > Build Categories section: Identifies a group of data sources categorized as logging systems or document databases.
title.others_description: Category Title in Data Source Category Management: Designates a generic category for data sources or dashboard elements that do not fit into other specific, predefined categories.
title.profiling_description: Title for a category in the data source category builder: This title defines a category focused on profiling characteristics or behavior of data.
title.time-series-databases_description: Category title in the categories list within the 'Build Data Sources' section: This labels a group of data source types that are time series databases.

text.insights_description: Navigation label in the Analytics section of the data source navigation model: It provides access to a feature or view that displays derived observations or valuable information from the data.

text.cache_description: Label in the Caching section within Data Source configuration for building the navigation model: Identifies a setting or option related to data caching.

text.permissions_description: Label in the Data Source Permissions section: This text identifies the area or tab within the Data Source configuration interface where users can manage access rights and permissions for a specific data source.

text.settings_description: Navigation item in the Data Sources navigation menu: Allows users to access configuration options or preferences for managing data sources.

text.dashboards_description: Label in the Datasources navigation model builder: This text identifies an option or link that can be added to the navigation for accessing or managing dashboards.

button-row.save-and-test_description: Button in the Data Sources configuration page: Saves the configured data source settings and tests its connection. This ensures the data source is properly set up and functional.
button-row.test_description: Button in the button row of the datasources section: Triggers a test or validation process for a data source.

cloud-info-box.body-alert: Or skip the effort and get {{mainDS}} (and {{extraDS}}) as fully-managed, scalable, and hosted data sources from Grafana Labs with the <6>free-forever Grafana Cloud plan</6> .
cloud-info-box.title-alert_description: Alert title in a cloud information box on the datasources page: Informs the user that a specific data source (`{{mainDS}}`) requires configuration and directs them to the relevant settings located further down the current page. It serves as a call to action for initial setup.

dashboards-table.aria-label-delete-dashboard_description: ARIA label for a button or icon in the dashboards table within the data sources section: Provides an accessible name for the control used to delete a dashboard.
dashboards-table.import_description: Button on the Dashboards table within the Data Sources section: Used to import new dashboards or dashboard configurations into the system.

data-source-load-error.not-found_description: Error message when loading or referencing a data source: Informs the user that the specified data source does not exist. This typically occurs if the data source has been deleted or its identifier is incorrect.

data-source-missing-rights-message.title-missing-rights_description: Title in a message related to data sources: Informs the user that they lack the necessary permissions or rights to perform an action or access specific data.

data-source-picker.aria-label-select-a-data-source_descript: Aria label for a selection control in the Data Source Picker component: It instructs users to choose a data source from available options. This label provides accessibility context for screen readers.

data-source-plugin-config-page.page-not-found_description: Error message in the Data Source Plugin Configuration page: Informs the user that a specific page they attempted to access or navigate to within this configuration context could not be found. It includes a placeholder for the name of the missing page.

data-source-plugin-state.plugin-state_description: Label in the Data Source Plugin State section: Indicates the current operational status or health of a specific data source plugin.

data-source-read-only-message.title-provisioned-data-source_descriptio: Title in the Data Sources settings: This string serves as the title or heading for a read-only message, indicating that the data source is provisioned externally (e.g., via configuration files) and thus cannot be modified directly in the UI.

message.testing-could-couple-minutes: Testing... this could take up to a couple of minutes

data-source-type-card.aria-label-learn-more_description: ARIA label for a 'Learn more' link/button in a data source type card: This label provides an accessible description for an interactive element that directs users to more information about the specific data source plugin displayed on the card.

data-sources-list-card.build-a-dashboard_description: Button or link in the Data Sources List Card: Allows users to initiate the creation of a new dashboard. This element provides a direct call to action to start building visualizations from available data sources.
data-sources-list-card.explore_description: Button in a data sources list card: Allows users to explore or query data associated with that specific data source.

edit-data-source-actions.add-favorite_description: Button or menu item in the data source editing actions: This allows the user to add the currently viewed or selected data source to their list of favorites.
edit-data-source-actions.build-a-dashboard_description: Menu item in the data source editing actions: This option allows users to create a new dashboard, often pre-configured to utilize the currently selected data source.
edit-data-source-actions.explore-data_description: Button or menu item in the data source editor: This action allows users to interactively query, browse, or preview the data provided by the configured data source.
edit-data-source-actions.open-in-explore_description: Button in the 'Edit Data Source Actions' menu: This button allows users to open the currently configured data source directly in the 'Explore View' for ad-hoc data querying and visualization.
edit-data-source-actions.remove-favorite_description: Action button in the "Edit Data Source Actions" section: Allows the user to remove the currently selected data source from their list of favorite data sources.

error-details-link.aria-label-more-details-about-the-error_: aria-label` for a link in an error message displayed for a datasource: Its purpose is to inform users (especially screen reader users) that clicking this link will reveal additional details about the error.

footer.add-csv-or-spreadsheet_description: Button in the footer of the Data Sources management page: Allows users to add a new data source by uploading a CSV or spreadsheet file.

description.adobe-analytics-datasource_description: Label in the datasource selection or configuration screen: Identifies the Adobe Analytics data source as an available option for data connection. It is listed among other enterprise plugin options.
description.app-dynamics-integration-and-data-source: Description within the 'Enterprise Phantom Plugins' configuration area of the Data Sources management section: This string identifies a specific integration type. It refers to the AppDynamics integration and its associated data source functionality.
description.atlassian-statuspage-datasource_descript: Name in the list of available enterprise data source plugins: It identifies the Atlassian Statuspage integration as an option for connecting to external data.
description.aurora-data-source_description: Description in the list of available data source types: Identifies the Aurora data source as a selectable option for integration.
description.azure-cosmos-db-datasource_description: Description text in the data source selection interface for enterprise plugins: It identifies the Azure CosmosDB data source type that can be added or configured.
description.azure-devops-datasource_description: Datasource name in the Datasources configuration UI: This string identifies the Azure DevOps data source. It is displayed when managing or selecting 'enterprise phantom plugins' within BMC Helix Dashboards.
description.catchpoint-datasource_description: Label in the Data Sources management interface: Identifies 'Catchpoint' as a specific data source type. This label is presented when configuring or viewing available enterprise phantom plugins.
description.cloudflare-datasource_description: Label in the Data Source configuration or selection interface: It identifies the Cloudflare data source as an available option for integration. This label is used when managing enterprise-specific or pre-defined "phantom" data source plugins.
description.cockroach-db-datasource_description: Label in the data source selection or configuration interface for enterprise phantom plugins: Identifies CockroachDB as an available data source type.
description.data-dog-integration-and-data-source_des: Description text in the Enterprise Phantom Plugins section within the Data Sources configuration: It identifies and describes the DataDog integration as an available data source option.
description.drone-datasource_description: Name in the Datasource selection or configuration screen under enterprise phantom plugins: This string identifies a specific 'Drone datasource' type available for integration.
description.git-lab-integration-and-datasource_descr: Descriptive label in the Datasources configuration for enterprise phantom plugins: It identifies a specific plugin that offers integration with GitLab and serves as a data source.
description.honeycomb-integration-and-datasource_des: Descriptive label in the Data Sources management section for enterprise phantom plugins: Identifies or describes the option to integrate with Honeycomb as a data source.
description.jira-integration-and-datasource_descript: Description in the Data Sources configuration section: Identifies the specific integration that allows Jira to be connected and used as a data source within BMC Helix Dashboards.
description.logic-monitor-devices-datasource_descrip: **Datasource name** in the **datasource selection interface**: This string identifies the LogicMonitor Devices datasource, used for integrating monitoring data from LogicMonitor devices. This option is likely found under 'enterprise phantom plugins' in the datasource list.
description.mongo-db-integration-and-data-source_des: Description in the Data Sources section, under the Enterprise Phantom Plugins feature: This text identifies the option for integrating with MongoDB and using it as a data source.
description.netlify-datasource_description: **Label** in the **data sources configuration interface**: This string identifies the Netlify data source as an available option for integration within BMC Helix Dashboards. It is displayed when selecting from a list of enterprise data source plugins or types.
description.new-relic-integration-and-data-source: New Relic integration and data source
description.new-relic-integration-and-data-source_de: Description text in the Data Sources configuration area: Identifies and describes the New Relic integration which serves as a data source. This string enables users to select the appropriate pre-configured monitoring option.
description.pager-duty-datasource_description: Description text in the section for managing or adding enterprise phantom data source plugins: Identifies the PagerDuty data source. This string serves as the name for the PagerDuty integration.
description.salesforce-integration-and-datasource_de: Description label in the Enterprise Plugin Selection for Data Sources: It specifies the Salesforce integration that functions as a data source option.
description.sap-hana-integration-and-data-source: SAP HANA® integration and data source
description.sap-hana-integration-and-data-source_des: Description text in the Data Sources configuration interface: This text identifies and describes the integration capabilities and data source type for SAP HANA that can be configured or selected.
description.service-now-integration-and-data-source_: Option Title in the Data Sources configuration, under the 'Enterprise Phantom Plugins' section: Identifies the integration type that connects to ServiceNow to function as a data source.
description.signal-fx-integration-and-datasource_des: Description text in the Data Sources configuration: This string identifies and briefly describes the SignalFx data source integration. It appears as an option within the list of available enterprise plugins.
description.snowflake-integration-and-data-source_de: Description in the Datasources configuration section: Identifies the option or plugin for integrating Snowflake as a data source for dashboards.
description.solarwinds-datasource_description: Label in the 'Enterprise Phantom Plugins' description section within Data Sources configuration: Identifies the SolarWinds data source option. This label is used to name or categorize this specific data source integration.
description.sumo-logic-integration-and-datasource_de: Label in the Data Sources configuration section, specifically when listing or selecting 'Enterprise Phantom Plugins'. It identifies the SumoLogic integration, highlighting its function as a data source.
description.visualize-and-explore-dynatrace-data_des: Descriptive text for a Dynatrace data source plugin or integration: It informs users about the capability to visualize and explore data sourced from Dynatrace.
description.visualize-and-explore-oracle-sql_descrip: Description text in the Enterprise Phantom Plugin details within the Data Sources configuration: It informs the user that this plugin allows them to visualize and explore Oracle SQL data.
description.visualize-and-explore-splunk-logs_descri: Description in the Enterprise Phantom Plugins section of Data Sources configuration: This string informs users about the primary capability of a specific plugin or integration, enabling the visualization and exploration of Splunk logs.
description.wavefront-integration-and-data-source_de: Description for a Data Source Plugin: This string identifies and briefly describes the Wavefront integration and data source option. It is displayed in the data source configuration, likely within a list of available enterprise plugins.
description.zendesk-datasource_description: Label in the description of enterprise data source plugins: It identifies the Zendesk data source.

description.hosted-graphite-prometheus-and-loki_desc: Description text in the Grafana Cloud plugin's data source configuration: It lists the specific hosted monitoring and logging services available through this plugin.

list.starred_description: Category label in the list of data sources: Indicates a section or filter for data sources that the user has marked as favorites.

new-data-source-view.placeholder-filter-by-name-or-type_descr: Placeholder text in the New Data Source View: It prompts users to enter text to filter the list of available data sources by their name or type.

message.an-unexpected-error-occurred_description: Error message in the Data Source update process: Informs the user that an unexpected issue occurred while attempting to save changes to a data source. This is a generic fallback message for unhandled errors.

use-data-source-info.badge-text-not-supported_description: Badge text in the 'Use Data Source Info' section: Informs the user that a particular aspect or capability related to the data source is not supported. It functions as a visual status indicator.
use-data-source-info.badge-text-supported_description: Badge text in the 'Use Data Source Info' section for a specific data source: This text indicates that the data source is officially supported and compatible with the system.

label.alerting_description: Label in Data Source Information section: Denotes a section or setting where users can configure or view alerting functionalities specific to the selected data source.
label.type_description: Label in the "Use Data Source Info" section: It indicates the specific type or category of the data source.

cursor-view.no-events-yet_description: Informational message in the debug cursor-view: This message is displayed when no events are found or available to show in the view.

render-info-viewer.data-counter_description: Data counter in the Debug Render Information Viewer: Displays the total number of data changes that have occurred.
render-info-viewer.elapsed-time_description: Display field in the render information viewer within the debug panel: Shows the duration of a rendering process. The `{{elapsed}}` placeholder represents the time in milliseconds.
render-info-viewer.field_description: Label or Column Header in the Render Info Viewer within the Debug interface: Identifies a specific data attribute or property being displayed about a rendered element for inspection.
render-info-viewer.last_description: Button label in the render info viewer debug panel: Allows users to view or navigate to the most recent rendering data or log entries. This is likely used for displaying the latest debug information.
render-info-viewer.render-counter_description: Counter label in the Render Info Viewer within the Debug panel: Displays the number of times a UI component or element has been rendered. This is used for performance monitoring and debugging.
render-info-viewer.schema-counter_description: Counter label in the Render Info Viewer (Debug section): Displays the current count of schema changes. This metric is useful for debugging and monitoring data structure updates during rendering.
render-info-viewer.title-reset-counters_description: Button label in the "Render Info Viewer" within the Debug tools: It allows a user to reset performance or rendering-related counters. This is typically used by developers for diagnostic purposes.
render-info-viewer.tooltip-step-back_description: Tooltip text in the Debug Render Info Viewer: Indicates an action to revert to a previous state or step in the rendering process being debugged.
render-info-viewer.type_description: Label in the Render Info Viewer within the Debug section: This label identifies the category or classification of a displayed item, such as the type of rendering component or data.

state-view.current-value_description: Label in the Debug State View: Displays the current value of a specific state or variable. This assists in debugging by showing real-time data.
state-view.label-state-name_description: Label in the State View within the Debug section: Identifies the name of a specific state. This likely refers to a system, application, or data state being displayed for debugging.

options.label-contain_description: Label in Background Size Editor options: This label represents an option for scaling a background image within its container. Selecting "Contain" ensures the entire image is visible, scaling it down if needed without cropping, potentially leaving empty space.
options.label-cover_description: Option label in the Background size editor: This option scales a background image to completely fill its container without distortion, potentially cropping parts of the image.
options.label-fill_description: An option label in the background size editor: Represents a display mode where the background image is scaled to fill the entire container area, potentially cropping parts of the image.
options.label-original_description: Option label in the background size editor options: Represents the choice to display a background image at its original size.
options.label-tile_description: Label for a background sizing option in the dimensions editor: This option dictates that a background image will be repeated, or 'tiled,' to cover the available area.

color-dimension-editor.label-fixed-color_description: Label in the Color Dimension Editor within the Dimensions settings: This label identifies an option to assign a single, static color to a dimension's values, rather than dynamically coloring them based on other criteria.
color-dimension-editor.noOptionsMessage-no-fields-found_descrip: Informational message in the color dimension editor: displayed when no data fields are available for selection or configuration. It informs the user that there are no options to define the color dimension.

direction-dimension-editor.description-field_description: Description in the Direction Dimension Editor: Explains that the direction property for a dimension is determined by the value of a specific field. This clarifies the method used for assigning direction.
direction-dimension-editor.description-fixed_description: Option description in the Direction Dimension Editor: This text describes a configuration where a direction dimension is set to a static, unchangeable value.
direction-dimension-editor.label-direction_description: Label in the "Direction Dimension Editor": It identifies a field or setting where the user defines or selects a directional aspect for a data dimension.
direction-dimension-editor.label-field_description: Label in the 'Direction Dimension Editor': This label identifies the input or selection element where the user defines which data field will be used for the dimension.
direction-dimension-editor.label-fixed_description: Option label in the Direction Dimension Editor: Indicates that a dimension property or value is set to a constant or unchangeable state, rather than being dynamic or variable.
direction-dimension-editor.label-source_description: Label in the Direction Dimension Editor: Identifies the origin or input for a dimension's data or value.

file-dropzone-custom-children.upload_description: Button in a file dropzone component: Initiates the upload of files. These files are typically for custom configurations or elements within the 'dimensions' context.

preview.label-preview_description: Label in the File Uploader's Preview section: Indicates or initiates a preview of the uploaded file or its data before final processing.

folder-picker-tab.placeholder-search_description: Placeholder text in the Folder Picker tab within the Dimensions section: Prompts users to type a query to search for folders.

resource-dimension-editor.description-field_description: Instructional text within a dimension configuration field in the Resource Dimension Editor: Informs the user to define the dimension's value by using the result of a string field. This guides the user on the expected data source or method for the dimension.
resource-dimension-editor.description-fixed_description: Option label in Resource Dimension Editor: This option allows users to set a dimension to a specific, unchangeable constant value. It distinguishes this setting from dynamic or variable value options.
resource-dimension-editor.label-field_description: Label in Resource Dimension Editor: Identifies an input where the user defines or selects a data attribute or property for a resource dimension. This field typically specifies which data column or attribute to use for the dimension.
resource-dimension-editor.label-fixed_description: Label in the Resource Dimension Editor: Indicates that a resource dimension's setting or value is static, predefined, and not modifiable.
resource-dimension-editor.label-mappings_description: Label in the Resource Dimension Editor: This refers to the section where users define how resource attributes are mapped to specific values or labels for display or analysis.
resource-dimension-editor.label-source_description: Label in Resource Dimension Editor: Indicates the origin or definition of the resource dimension being configured.

resource-picker.aria-label-clear-value_description: Accessibility label for a button in the resource picker component: It enables users to clear the currently selected or entered value.

render-small-resource-picker.set-icon_description: Button in the Small Resource Picker within the Dimensions section: Allows the user to associate or assign an icon to a selected resource or dimension.

resource-picker-popover.folder_description: Label in the Resource Picker Popover: It denotes a control, section, or column related to folders for organizing and selecting resources.
resource-picker-popover.select_description: Button in the Resource Picker Popover within the Dimensions section: Used to confirm and finalize the selection of resources within the popover.

fixed-value-options.label-fixed-values_description: Label in the fixed-value options section of the scalar dimension editor: This label identifies the input field or selection where a user can specify a static, unchanging value for the dimension.

scalar-dimension-editor.label-limit_description: Label in the scalar dimension editor: This label indicates a setting that allows users to define a maximum number of values or elements to display for the scalar dimension.
scalar-dimension-editor.label-value_description: Label in the scalar dimension editor: It identifies the field where a user enters or views the specific data point for a scalar dimension.
scalar-dimension-editor.noOptionsMessage-no-fields-found_descrip: Status message in the Scalar Dimension Editor: Informs the user that no data fields were found for selection when configuring a dimension. This message indicates an absence of selectable options.

scalar-options.description-clamped: Use field values, clamped to max and min
scalar-options.description-clamped_description: Description in the Scalar Options section of the Scalar Dimension Editor: This text explains that the field values for the scalar dimension will be constrained to a defined maximum and minimum range. This describes the effect of enabling a clamping function for dimension values.
scalar-options.description-mod: Use field values, mod from max
scalar-options.description-mod_description: Option description in the scalar dimension editor options: This explains a method for processing dimension field values, where a modulo operation is applied relative to a maximum value. This likely ensures values wrap around or stay within a defined range.
scalar-options.label-clamped_description: Label in the Scalar Options section of the Scalar Dimension Editor: Indicates an option to restrict or limit the range of a scalar dimension's values or display.
scalar-options.label-mod_description: Label in the scalar options of the scalar dimension editor: Represents the "Modulo" mathematical operation. This option allows users to apply a modulo function to scalar dimension values.

label.fixed-value_description: Label in the Scale Dimension Editor: This label identifies an option or input field for setting a specific, unchanging value for a dimension's scale.

scale-dimension-editor.label-max_description: Label in Scale Dimension Editor: Marks the input field or display for the maximum value of a scale dimension.
scale-dimension-editor.label-min_description: Label in the Scale Dimension Editor (within the Dimensions section): This label indicates the minimum value or lower bound for a scale or range.
scale-dimension-editor.label-value_description: Label in the Scale Dimension Editor: This label prompts the user to input or select a specific value for a dimension's scale.
scale-dimension-editor.noOptionsMessage-no-fields-found_descrip: Informational message in the Scale Dimension Editor: Displayed when no data fields are found or available for selection within this editor.

text-dimension-editor.aria-label-clear-value_description: ARIA label for a button or icon in the text dimension editor: This label describes the action to clear the currently selected or entered value for a dimension field.
text-dimension-editor.description-field_description: Checkbox label in the Text Dimension Editor within BMC Helix Dashboards dimensions configuration: This option controls whether the value of the configured description field is displayed for the text dimension.
text-dimension-editor.description-fixed_description: Option label in the Text Dimension Editor: It indicates that the dimension's value is static and pre-defined, rather than dynamic.
text-dimension-editor.label-field_description: Label in Text Dimension Editor: Prompts the user to specify or select a data field or column to be used for the text dimension.
text-dimension-editor.label-fixed_description: Label in the Text Dimension Editor: This label or option denotes a dimension value that is constant or predetermined, rather than dynamic.
text-dimension-editor.label-source_description: Label in the text dimension editor: This field allows users to specify the origin or data source for the text dimension being configured.
text-dimension-editor.label-template_description: Label in the Text Dimension Editor: This string is a label for a field or section where users can define a template or pattern for text dimension values.
text-dimension-editor.label-value_description: Label for an input field in the Text Dimension Editor: This label prompts the user to enter the specific text value for a dimension.

threshold-editor.tooltip-remove-threshold_description: Tooltip in the Threshold Editor: This tooltip appears when hovering over an interactive element, explaining that clicking it will remove the specified threshold. The `{{thresholdNumber}}` placeholder indicates the specific threshold to be removed.

thresholds-editor.add-threshold_description: Button in the Thresholds Editor within the Dimensions section: Used to initiate the creation of a new threshold.
thresholds-editor.aria-label-threshold_description: ARIA label in the Thresholds Editor: Provides an accessible name for an individual threshold. The `{{thresholdNumber}}` token helps differentiate between multiple thresholds for accessibility users.
thresholds-editor.description-percentage-means-thresholds-: Percentage means thresholds relative to min & max
Informative text in the Thresholds Editor: This explains that when thresholds are defined as a percentage, they are calculated relative to the minimum and maximum values within the data range.

description.threshold-based-percent-between-minmax: Pick threshold based on the percent between min/max
description.threshold-based-percent-between-minmax_d: Description in Thresholds Editor: This label explains a mode for setting a threshold. It indicates that the threshold value will be determined by a percentage of the range between the minimum and maximum possible values.
description.thresholds-based-absolute-values: Pick thresholds based on the absolute values
description.thresholds-based-absolute-values_descrip: Description of a threshold mode in the dimensions threshold editor: This text explains a setting or mode where thresholds are defined by absolute values, rather than relative or percentage-based values.

label.absolute_description: Label in the Thresholds Editor (Modes section): This label identifies an option for setting a threshold using an absolute, fixed numerical value.
label.percentage_description: Label in Thresholds editor's mode selection: This label identifies a mode for defining threshold values as a percentage.

thresholds-editor.thresholds-mode_description: Label in Thresholds Editor: This label introduces a control where users select the desired mode for how thresholds are configured or applied.
thresholds-editor.value-base_description: Label in the Thresholds Editor within Dimensions settings: Indicates the fundamental reference value used for defining thresholds. This value serves as the baseline for comparison.

urlpicker-tab.label-preview_description: Label in the URL picker tab within the dimensions settings: Indicates an option or section to preview the configured URL or its content.

value-mapping-edit-row.duplicate-value-mapping-aria-label-dupli: ARIA label for an action in the Value Mapping Edit Row within the Dimensions section: Describes an interactive element that duplicates an existing value mapping.
value-mapping-edit-row.duplicate-value-mapping-tooltip-duplicat: Action label within a tooltip in a Value Mapping Edit Row: This label provides the option to duplicate the current value mapping.
value-mapping-edit-row.placeholder-exact-value-to-match_descrip: Placeholder text in an input field within the 'Value Mapping Edit Row' section: This text prompts the user to enter a specific value that should be exactly matched when defining a value mapping rule for a dimension.
value-mapping-edit-row.placeholder-from_description: Placeholder text in the value mapping edit row for dimensions: It prompts the user to enter the starting value for a mapping rule, such as the lower bound of a range.
value-mapping-edit-row.placeholder-optional-display-text_descri: Placeholder text in an input field within a value mapping edit row in the dimensions section: It suggests where the user can enter an optional custom text to be displayed instead of the actual mapped value.
value-mapping-edit-row.placeholder-regular-expression_descripti: Placeholder text in an input field within the Value Mapping Edit Row for dimensions: It prompts the user to enter a regular expression used for pattern matching in value mapping definitions.
value-mapping-edit-row.placeholder-to_description: Placeholder text in an input field within a value mapping edit row in the Dimensions settings: It prompts the user to enter the upper bound of a value range.
value-mapping-edit-row.remove-value-mapping-aria-label-delete-v: Button in the value mapping edit row within the dimensions section: Allows users to delete a specific value mapping. This is an ARIA label for an interactive element.
value-mapping-edit-row.set-color_description: Button in the value mapping edit row for dimensions: Allows the user to set a color for a specific mapped value. This color is used for visual representation within the dashboard.

description.boolean-false-values_description: Label in the 'Special Match Options' section of the Value Mapping editor for dimensions: It indicates the setting or input area where users define specific data values to be interpreted as 'false' when mapping boolean fields.
description.boolean-true-values_description: Label in the Special Match Options section of the Value Mapping Edit Row: This identifies the input field or section where users can specify which values should be recognized or mapped as "true" for boolean comparisons.
description.empty-string_description: Option in the special match options for a value mapping edit row: This option allows users to configure how to match or represent empty string values found in the data.
description.matches-against-number-na-n-not-a-number: Description in the 'Special Match Options' section of a value mapping edit row: This text clarifies that a specific matching rule or option will apply to data values identified as Number.NaN (Not a Number).
description.matches-null-and-undefined-values_descri: Description text in the 'Special match options' section of a value mapping edit row: It clarifies that the associated match condition or setting will apply to data values that are explicitly `null` or `undefined`.
description.matches-null-undefined-and-na-n_descript: Description text in the 'Special match options' section of a value mapping edit row: It clarifies that the associated special match option will apply when data values are null, undefined, or NaN. This helps users understand the behavior of this special matching rule.

label.empty_description: Option label in the special match options within the value mapping editor for dashboard dimensions. This option allows users to define a rule that applies specifically when a data value for a dimension is empty (e.g., null, undefined, or a blank string).

value-mapping-edit-row.tooltip-remove-color_description: Tooltip in a value mapping edit row within the Dimensions settings: This tooltip appears when hovering over an option to remove a color that has been assigned to a specific value mapping entry. It allows the user to clear the color assignment for that value.
value-mapping-edit-row.tooltip-remove-icon_description: Tooltip text on a 'remove' icon within a value mapping edit row: Informs the user that clicking the icon will remove an associated item.

value-mappings-editor.add-value-mappings_description: Button in the Value Mappings Editor for Dimensions: Allows users to add new value mappings for specific dimension values.
value-mappings-editor.edit-value-mappings_description: Button or link in the Value Mappings editor within the Dimensions configuration: Allows users to modify the rules that transform raw data values into specific display values, colors, or categories.
value-mappings-editor.title-value-mappings_description: Title in the Value Mappings Editor: This section allows users to define rules for transforming or interpreting raw data values, such as assigning text, colors, or ranges for display.

value-mappings-editor-modal.color_description: Label in the Value Mappings Editor modal for a dimension: Specifies the color associated with a particular mapped value.
value-mappings-editor-modal.condition_description: Label in the Value Mappings Editor modal within the Dimensions section: This label introduces a field or section where users define a specific criterion or rule. This condition determines when a particular value mapping should be applied.
value-mappings-editor-modal.display-text_description: Label in the Value Mappings Editor Modal: This field allows users to specify the text that will be displayed in the dashboard for a specific mapped value.
value-mappings-editor-modal.icon_description: Label in the Value Mappings Editor Modal: Identifies the control for selecting an icon to be associated with a specific value mapping. This icon provides a visual representation for the mapped value within the dimension.
value-mappings-editor-modal.label-add-a-new-mapping_description: Button in the Value Mappings Editor Modal for dimensions: Allows users to add a new value mapping to configure how specific dimension values are displayed.

description.match-a-numerical-range-of-values: Match a numerical range of values
description.match-a-numerical-range-of-values_descri: Description in the Value Mappings Editor Modal: This string explains a mapping type option that allows users to match incoming data against a specified numerical range of values when configuring dimensions.
description.match-a-regular-expression-with-replacem: Match a regular expression with replacement
Description text in the Value Mappings Editor modal: Explains a mapping type option for dimensions where data values are matched using a regular expression, followed by a replacement.
description.match-a-specific-text-value_description: Description text in the Value Mappings Editor modal: This string describes a mapping type option that allows users to configure a value mapping based on an exact match of a specific text value. It distinguishes this mapping type from others like range-based or regex matching.
description.match-boolean-empty-values: Match on null, NaN, boolean and empty values
description.match-boolean-empty-values_description: Description text in the Value Mappings Editor modal: Describes the data states (null, NaN, boolean, empty values) that a specific mapping type will match. This helps users understand the criteria for this mapping.

label.range_description: Label in Value Mappings Editor Modal: Identifies a type of value mapping based on a numerical or value range. This option allows users to define how values within a specified interval are represented or transformed.
label.regex_description: Label in the "Value Mappings Editor" modal: This label identifies the "Regex" mapping type, allowing users to define value mappings using regular expressions.
label.special_description: Label in the Value Mappings Editor Modal: Identifies a distinct type of value mapping configuration. This option is used for values requiring unique or advanced handling rules.
label.value_description: Label in the Value Mappings Editor Modal: This label identifies an input field where the user specifies a particular value for a mapping type definition. It refers to the literal value being defined or mapped.

share.time-range-description: Change the current relative time range to an absolute time range
share.time-range-description_description: Description text in the Share dialog/panel of an embedded dashboard: It informs the user about the option to convert the dashboard's current relative time range (e.g., "Last 5 minutes") to an absolute time range (e.g., "Oct 26, 2023 10:00 AM - 10:05 AM"). This ensures the shared dashboard always displays data for a fixed period.
share.time-range-label_description: Label for a checkbox or toggle in the 'Share' settings for embedded dashboards: It allows users to lock the current time range of the dashboard when it is shared or embedded, preventing viewers from changing it.

empty-list-cta.pro-tip_description: Text label in an empty list Call to Action (CTA): This label introduces a helpful professional tip displayed to users when a list or table is empty. The `{{proTip}}` placeholder dynamically inserts the specific tip content.

entity-not-found.description: We're looking but can't seem to find this {{lowerCaseEntity}}. Try returning <4>home</4> or seeking help on the <7>community site.</7>

annotations.datasource: The selected data source does not support annotations. Please select a different data source.

exemplar-tooltip-header_description: Tooltip header in data visualizations: Identifies a tooltip section displaying information about an exemplar trace. This label indicates the tooltip content refers to a specific instance of data.

accordian-logs.events_description: Section label in the 'Logs' accordion within the Explore interface: Identifies a panel or view dedicated to displaying system events from logs.
accordian-logs.footer: Event timestamps are relative to the start time of the full trace.
accordian-logs.footer_description: Informational text in the footer of the Logs accordion within the Explore section: Explains that event timestamps are displayed relative to the start time of the full trace. This provides context for interpreting the displayed log data.

accordian-references.references_description: Section Title within the 'References' accordion on the Explore page: This title labels a collapsible section that provides access to related documentation, external links, or other referenced materials.
accordian-references.view-linked-span_description: Button in the "References" accordion section on the Explore page: Allows users to navigate to and view the details of a linked trace span.

explore.add-to-dashboard_description: Button in the Explore view: Enables users to add the currently explored data or visualization to an existing or new dashboard.

title.new-panel_description: Title in the dashboard panel creation process: This string labels a new panel being built, or the dialog/wizard used to configure it, when initiated from the Explore view.

explore.clear_description: Button in the Explore view: Used to clear or reset active filters, search criteria, or other selections within the exploration area.

confirm-navigation-modal.new-tab: Do you want to proceed in the current tab or open a new tab?
confirm-navigation-modal.new-tab_description: Confirmation Prompt in the Confirm Navigation Modal within the Explore section: This prompt asks the user to choose whether to navigate to a new location within the current browser tab or to open a new tab for the destination.
confirm-navigation-modal.open_description: Button in a confirmation modal in the Explore view: Confirms the user's decision to proceed with opening a new view or resource. This action typically discards any unsaved changes on the current page.
confirm-navigation-modal.open-in-new-tab_description: Button label in a confirmation modal within the Explore view: Offers the user the choice to open the navigation target in a new browser tab. This option is presented when confirming a navigation action that could otherwise occur in the current tab.

content-outline.tooltip-collapse-outline_description: Tooltip in the Content Outline section within the Explore page: Describes a button or icon that, when clicked, collapses or hides the hierarchical content outline.
content-outline.tooltip-expand-outline_description: Tooltip for an element in the Content Outline within the Explore section: It indicates that clicking the associated UI element will expand a collapsed hierarchical outline or tree view.

body.aria-label-content-outline-item-collapse: Button in the 'Explore' section's content outline: This ARIA label describes a button used to collapse an individual item within the content outline structure.
body.aria-label-content-outline-item-delete-b: Delete button for a content outline item in the Explore section: This button allows users to remove the associated content item.

correlation-editor-mode-bar.content-correlations-editor-explore-expe: Correlations editor in Explore is an experimental feature.
Informational message in the 'Explore' section: This text informs users that the Correlations editor is an experimental feature. It is displayed within the Correlation Editor Mode Bar.
correlation-editor-mode-bar.exit-correlation-editor_description: Button in the correlation editor mode bar in the Explore feature: Allows users to exit the correlation editor.

correlation-helper.add-transformation_description: Button in the correlation helper section of the Explore view: Used to add a data transformation for correlation analysis.
correlation-helper.aria-label-delete-transformation_descrip: Aria-label in the Correlation Helper section of the Explore view: Describes a button or action used to delete a transformation. This label provides an accessible name for screen readers.
correlation-helper.aria-label-edit-transformation_descripti: Action label in the Correlation Helper section within the Explore interface: Provides an accessible name for an interactive element that allows users to modify an existing data transformation.
correlation-helper.body-correlation-details: The correlation link will appear by the <1>{{resultField}}</1> field. You can use the following variables to set up your correlations:
correlation-helper.expression_description: Label in Correlation Helper within the Explore feature: This string labels and displays a dynamic expression relevant to correlation analysis, often an equation or formula used to define the correlation.
correlation-helper.label-description-header_description: Column Header in the Correlation Helper section of BMC Helix Dashboards' Explore view: It designates columns or input fields for specifying both a short identifier (Label) and a detailed explanation (Description) for correlation-related elements.
correlation-helper.label-label_description: Field label in the Correlation Helper within the Explore section: Prompts users to enter a descriptive name or identifier for a correlation metric, query, or data point.
correlation-helper.title-correlation-details_description: Title in the correlation helper section within the Explore view: Displays comprehensive information regarding a specific data correlation.
correlation-helper.tooltip-transformations: A transformation extracts one or more variables out of a single field.
correlation-helper.tooltip-transformations_description: Tooltip in the Correlation Helper feature of the Explore section: Defines a 'transformation' as a process that extracts one or more variables from a single field.
correlation-helper.transformations_description: Section title in the Correlation Helper section of the Explore feature: This refers to the area where users can define or apply data transformations. These transformations prepare the data for correlation analysis.

correlation-transformation-add-modal.add-transformation_description: Button in the 'Add Transformation to Correlation' modal within the Explore section: Confirms and applies the selected data transformation to the correlation.
correlation-transformation-add-modal.added-variables: This transformation will add the following variables:
correlation-transformation-add-modal.added-variables_description: Text label in the 'Add Correlation Transformation' modal within the Explore section: This label precedes a list of variables that are about to be added by the correlation transformation being configured. It informs the user about the outcome of applying the transformation.
correlation-transformation-add-modal.aria-label-field_description: Label for an input element in the 'Add Correlation Transformation' modal: It prompts the user to enter or select a data field to be used in the correlation transformation.
correlation-transformation-add-modal.aria-label-type_description: Label in the 'Add Correlation Transformation' modal in the Explore section: This label identifies the input or selection field for choosing the type of correlation transformation.
correlation-transformation-add-modal.body: A transformation extracts variables out of a single field. These variables will be available along with your field variables.
correlation-transformation-add-modal.edit-transformation_description: Button in the Correlation Transformation Add modal (within the Explore section): This button allows the user to modify an existing data transformation.
correlation-transformation-add-modal.label-expression_description: Label in the 'Add Correlation Transformation' modal: This label identifies an input field for defining a data transformation expression (a formula or rule).
correlation-transformation-add-modal.label-expression-without-tooltip_descrip: Label in the "Add Correlation Transformation" modal within the Explore section: It identifies an input field or area where users define a data expression or formula.
correlation-transformation-add-modal.label-field_description: Input field label in the Add Correlation Transformation modal: Prompts the user to specify a data field. This field defines the data column or attribute relevant to the correlation transformation.
correlation-transformation-add-modal.label-type_description: Label in the 'Add Correlation Transformation' modal within the Explore section: It labels a field for selecting or specifying the type of correlation transformation to be added.
correlation-transformation-add-modal.label-variable-name_description: Label in the 'Add Correlation Transformation' modal within the Explore section: Identifies the input field where the user specifies the name for a variable.
correlation-transformation-add-modal.label-variable-name-without-tooltip_desc: Label in the "Add Correlation Transformation" modal within the Explore section: This label prompts the user to enter a name for a new variable being defined.
correlation-transformation-add-modal.title-add_description: Title of a modal dialog in the Explore section: This modal allows users to add a new data transformation, likely for correlation purposes.
correlation-transformation-add-modal.title-edit_description: Title in the 'Edit Correlation Transformation' modal: This title indicates the user is modifying an existing correlation transformation within the 'Explore' section.

correlation-unsaved-changes-modal.continue-without-saving_description: Button in the 'Unsaved Changes' modal within the Correlation feature of the Explore section: This allows the user to proceed with an action, discarding any unsaved changes made to the correlation configuration.
correlation-unsaved-changes-modal.save-correlation_description: Button in the 'Unsaved Correlation Changes' modal: Prompts the user to save unsaved changes made to a correlation. Clicking this button will confirm and save the current correlation configuration.
correlation-unsaved-changes-modal.title-unsaved-changes-to-correlation_des: Modal title in the "Unsaved changes to correlation" modal within the Explore feature: It informs the user about unsaved modifications to a correlation definition or settings.

draggable-manager-demo.click-and-drag-gray-divider: Click and drag the gray divider in the colored area, below.
draggable-manager-demo.click-and-drag-gray-divider_description: **Draggable gray divider** in a colored area below the instruction within the `draggable-manager-demo` section: This instruction guides the user to click and drag the divider. Its purpose is to demonstrate and allow interaction with the draggable manager functionality on the page.
draggable-manager-demo.click-horizontally-somewhere-colored-bel: Click and drag horizontally somewhere in the colored area, below.

Instructional text in the 'draggable-manager-demo' section: This text guides the user to click and drag horizontally within a colored area located below it, demonstrating horizontal dragging functionality.
draggable-manager-demo.drag-value_description: Text label in the draggable manager demonstration within the Explore section: displays the current value that is being dragged or managed. This string is part of a UI component used for demonstrating draggable functionality.
draggable-manager-demo.draggable-manager-demo_description: Title in the DraggableManager demo feature within the Explore area: Labels a demonstration of draggable UI components.
draggable-manager-demo.dragging-a-divider_description: A divider in the 'Draggable Manager Demo' within the 'Explore' section: This string describes the active user action of dragging a UI divider. It likely indicates a state or action where a user is moving a divider to adjust layout or resize sections.
draggable-manager-demo.dragging-a-sub-region_description: Status message in the draggable manager demo within the explore section: It indicates that a user is currently dragging a sub-region. This text is displayed as part of a demonstration for draggable UI elements.
draggable-manager-demo.value_description: Label in the Draggable Manager Demo within the Explore section: Displays the current numerical value of the `dividerPos` variable. This value likely represents the position of a draggable divider or element in the demo.

drop-down-menu.aria-label-links_description: Menu item in the 'Explore' drop-down menu: This option provides access to a collection of various navigation links relevant to the dashboard's explore functionality.
drop-down-menu.links_description: Menu item in the Explore drop-down menu: This option allows users to navigate to related dashboards, external URLs, or other linked content.

explore.title-flame-graph_description: Title in the Explore section: Identifies the 'Flame graph' visualization type, which is used for performance analysis and tracing.
explore.title-graph_description: Title in the Explore view: Labels a panel or section displaying data as a graph.
explore.title-logs_description: Title in the log exploration view within the Explore feature: Indicates the section dedicated to viewing and analyzing log data.
explore.title-logs-sample_description: Title in the Explore feature: Labels a section or view dedicated to displaying sample log data.
explore.title-node-graph_description: Title in the Explore section: Labels a visualization type that displays data points as nodes and their relationships as edges. This visualization is used for exploring interconnected data or networks.
explore.title-queries_description: Label in the Explore feature: Identifies the section or tab where users define and execute data queries.
explore.title-raw-prometheus_description: Title for a data source tab or view in the Explore feature: This indicates that the user is currently viewing or querying data directly from a Prometheus data source, in its raw, unprocessed form.
explore.title-table_description: Label or Option in the Explore feature: Identifies or allows selection of a table visualization or data display format within the Explore section.
explore.title-traces_description: Title in the Explore feature: It labels a section or view dedicated to displaying and analyzing distributed traces.

label.data_description: Tab label in the Query Inspector (within the Explore section): Identifies the tab where users can view the raw data or results of a query.

label.error_description: Label in the 'Error' tab of the 'Query Inspector' within the 'Explore' feature: It serves as a general indicator or title for content related to an error.

label.query_description: Label in the Query tab of the Explore Query Inspector: Identifies the section or input field where the user defines or views the data query.

label.stats_description: Label in the Stats tab of the Query Inspector within the Explore section: It identifies the tab that displays statistical information related to the executed query.

run-button.aria-label-run-query-options_description: ARIA label on a button in the Explore section: This label describes a button that, when clicked, reveals various options for how to run a query.

explore-toolbar.outline_description: Button in the Explore toolbar: Displays a structured outline or summary of the dashboard's content or components. This helps users navigate or understand the overall organization.
explore-toolbar.tooltip-content-outline_description: Tooltip in the Explore toolbar in the Explore section: This tooltip describes an icon or button that, when activated, displays a structured overview or summary of the dashboard or data content being viewed.

feature-toggle-page.description-explore-disabled: To enable Explore, enable it in the Grafana config:
feature-toggle-page.description-explore-disabled_description: Instructional text on the 'Explore' feature toggle page: Informs the user how to enable the 'Explore' feature by configuring it in Grafana. This message is displayed when the 'Explore' feature is currently disabled.
feature-toggle-page.title-explore-disabled_description: Page Title in the Explore feature toggle page: Informs the user that the 'Explore' functionality is currently disabled.

text.data_description: Label in the BMC Helix Dashboards Explore feature, within the 'get field links' functionality: It designates a section, tab, or option related to the actual data content.
text.raw-value_description: Option label in the Explore feature's field link options: Represents the choice to display or use the original, unformatted value of a data field.

get-matches-metadata.matches_description: Count label in the Explore search results: Informs the user that no items were found after a search or filter operation. This specific string is displayed when the count of matches is zero.

text.tags_description: A label in the 'Legacy Create Span Link Factory' interface within the Explore section: This identifies a field or section where users can add or view metadata tags for the span links being created.

title.related-logs_description: Title in the Explore feature, within the trace or span exploration view: It labels a section or a link that provides access to logs associated with the currently viewed span or trace.
title.session-for-this-span_description: Title in the Explore section, specifically within a legacy feature designed for creating or managing links from trace spans: This title labels a section or action that allows users to identify or view the user session associated with the currently selected trace span.

legacy-create-span-link-factory.title-explore-metrics-for-this-span_desc: Link title in the Explore section: This title is displayed for a link that allows users to navigate to a dedicated view for exploring metrics related to a specific trace span.
legacy-create-span-link-factory.title-explore-split: Explore the logs for this in split view
legacy-create-span-link-factory.title-explore-split_description: Link label in the Explore section, likely displayed alongside a specific data entity (e.g., a trace span or log entry). Its purpose is to navigate to a new, split-view interface that shows the logs associated with that specific entity.
legacy-create-span-link-factory.title-session-for-this-span_description: **Title** in the **Explore view's legacy span link creation feature**: This title identifies or introduces the tracing **session** that is associated with the currently selected **span**. It helps users understand or navigate to session-specific data related to a span.

live-logs.clear-logs_description: Button in the Live Logs view within the Explore section: Clears the log entries currently displayed in the live stream.
live-logs.exit-live-mode_description: Button or link in the Live Logs view within the Explore feature: Allows users to exit the real-time log streaming mode.
live-logs.last-line-received_description: Status label in the Live Logs section of the Explore view: This indicates the time elapsed since the last log line was received, informing the user about the recency of the log stream data.
live-logs.pause_description: Button in the Live Logs section of the Explore view: Pauses the real-time streaming of log data.
live-logs.resume_description: Button in the Live Logs section of the Explore page: Resumes the streaming of live logs after they have been paused.

live-tail-button.live_description: Button label in the 'Live Tail' button within the Explore section: This label indicates or activates real-time data streaming or log tailing functionality.
live-tail-button.pause-the-live-stream_description: Button label in the Explore section, within the live tail feature: This text instructs the user to pause the real-time data stream being displayed.
live-tail-button.paused_description: Button text on the "Live Tail" button in the Explore view: It indicates that the live streaming of data or logs is currently paused. Users can click this button to resume the stream.
live-tail-button.start-live-stream-your-logs_description: Button label within the Live Tail feature section on the Explore page: Instructs the user to begin a real-time, continuous display of their logs.
live-tail-button.stop-and-exit-the-live-stream: Stop and exit the live stream
live-tail-button.stop-and-exit-the-live-stream_descriptio: Button in the Live Tail feature on the Explore page: Stops the active real-time data stream and exits the live tail view.

logs-volume.add-filters: Add more labels to your query to narrow down your search.
logs-volume.add-filters_description: Hint text in the Logs Volume view: Explains how to narrow down log search results. It advises users to add more labels (filters) to their query.
logs-volume.decrease-timerange: Decrease the time range of your query.
logs-volume.decrease-timerange_description: Button in the Logs Volume view in the Explore section: Used to reduce the duration covered by the current log query, effectively shortening the displayed time frame.
logs-volume.much-data: The query is trying to access too much data. Try one or more of the following:
logs-volume.much-data_description: Warning message in the Logs Volume section of the Explore feature: This message informs the user that their log query is trying to access too much data and suggests corrective actions.

logs.maximum-pinned-logs: Maximum of {{PINNED_LOGS_LIMIT}} pinned logs reached. Unpin a log to add another.
logs.no-logs-found_description: Informational message in the Logs viewer within the Explore section: Appears when no logs are found for the applied search queries or filters.
logs.scan-for-older-logs_description: Button in the Logs view within the Explore section: This button allows users to retrieve and display log entries that precede the currently loaded timeframe.
logs.stop-scan_description: Button in the Logs view of the Explore feature: Allows the user to terminate an active log scanning or searching process. This is typically used to stop a long-running or unwanted log scan.

logs-column-search.placeholder-search-fields-by-name_descri: Placeholder text in the Logs Column Search section within the Explore feature: This text is displayed inside an input field to prompt the user to type the name of a log field they wish to find.

logs-container.label-logs_description: Section title or tab label in the 'Logs' container of the 'Explore' section: Identifies the interface area dedicated to displaying and managing system and application logs.

logs-feedback.give-feedback_description: Button in the Logs Feedback section of the Explore feature: Allows users to provide feedback on logs.

logs-meta-row.download_description: Download button in a log metadata row within the Explore section: Allows users to download the log data or metadata associated with that row.

label.deduplication-count_description: Label in the Logs Explore view, specifically within a metadata row (`logs-meta-row`): It identifies the number of log entries that have been deduplicated. This count is displayed to the user.
label.showing-only-selected-fields_description: Status message in the logs meta row within the Explore section: This label informs the user that only a specific subset of log fields, chosen by the user, is currently being displayed. It indicates a filtered view of the log data.

logs-meta-row.show-original-line_description: Button or link in the Explore section, within a log entry's metadata row: Displays the complete, unformatted original version of a log line. This is typically used when a log line has been truncated or formatted for display.

logs-sample-panel.label: Logs sample
logs-sample-panel.label_description: Panel title in the Explore feature: Labels a panel designed to display sample log data.
logs-sample-panel.no-logs-sample-data_description: Informational message in the Logs Sample Panel within the Explore section: It indicates that no sample log data is currently available to be displayed. This message appears when the system cannot retrieve or generate any sample logs based on current criteria.

open-in-split-view-button.open-logs-in-split-view_description: Button label in the Logs sample panel within the Explore view: This button allows users to open the currently displayed logs in a split view. This facilitates side-by-side comparison or concurrent analysis of log data.

logs-sample-panel.title-failed-sample-query: Failed to load logs sample for this query
logs-sample-panel.title-failed-sample-query_description: Error message in the Logs Sample Panel within the Explore section: Notifies the user that the system was unable to load a sample of logs for the executed query, likely due to a backend issue or invalid query.
logs-sample-panel.tooltip: Show log lines that contributed to visualized metrics
logs-sample-panel.tooltip_description: Tooltip text for an interactive element in the Logs Sample Panel within the Explore section: This text explains that activating the element will display the log lines that contributed to the visualized metrics.

logs-table-empty-fields.no-fields_description: Status message in the Explore section's logs table: Indicates that no fields are currently present or selected for display within the table.

logs-table-multi-select.fields_description: Label in the multi-select interface for log table columns within the Explore section: Indicates the list or group of data fields (columns) available for selection or display in the log table.
logs-table-multi-select.reset_description: Button in the multi-select logs table in the Explore section: Resets or clears the current selections made by the user in the table.
logs-table-multi-select.selected-fields_description: Label in the logs table multi-select view of the Explore section: This identifies the fields that the user has selected.
logs-table-multi-select.suggested-fields_description: Section title in the logs table multi-select feature within the Explore view: Labels a list of automatically proposed fields relevant to the log data. This helps users quickly identify and select common or useful fields.

logs-table-wrap.aria-label-select-query-by-name_descript: ARIA label for a selection control in the logs table within the Explore section: Instructs users to choose an existing or saved query by its name.
logs-table-wrap.label-select-query_description: Label in the logs table wrap section within the Explore feature: Prompts the user to select or choose a query. This label is likely associated with a dropdown or input field where the user can define or pick a log query.
logs-table-wrap.tooltip-select-query-visualize-table: Select a query to visualize in the table
logs-table-wrap.tooltip-select-query-visualize-table_des: Tooltip in the logs table wrapper within the Explore section: Guides the user to select a query whose results will be displayed in the table.

logs-volume-panel.content-streaming_description: Option in the Logs Volume panel within the Explore section: This enables or indicates the real-time, continuous display of incoming log data.

logs-volume-panel-list.aria-label-reload-log-volume_description: Button or icon in the Logs Volume panel within the Explore section: Allows users to refresh or reload the displayed log volume data.
logs-volume-panel-list.label-reload-log-volume_description: Button label in the Logs Volume panel list within the Explore section: Allows the user to refresh or reload the displayed log volume data.
logs-volume-panel-list.title-failed-volume-query: Failed to load log volume for this query
logs-volume-panel-list.title-failed-volume-query_description: Error message in the Logs volume panel list within the Explore section: Informs the user that the system failed to retrieve or display log volume data for a specific search query.
logs-volume-panel-list.title-no-logs-volume-available_descripti: Title in the Logs Volume panel list within the Explore section: Informs the user that no log volume data could be found or is available for display.
logs-volume-panel-list.title-showing-partial-data_description: Status indicator in a Logs Volume panel within the Explore section: Informs the user that the displayed data is incomplete. This message appears when the full dataset cannot be loaded or retrieved for the selected time range or query.
logs-volume-panel-list.title-unable-to-show-log-volume_descript: Title in the Logs Volume Panel List within the Explore section: Informs the user that the system was unable to retrieve or display the log volume data.

logs-volumne-panel-list.body-no-logs-volume-available: No volume information available for the current queries and time range.
logs-volumne-panel-list.body-no-logs-volume-available_descriptio: Status message in the Logs Volume panel within the Explore section: Indicates that no logs volume data is available based on the currently applied queries and time range.

next-prev-result.aria-label-next_description: Button in the Next/Previous Result navigation section within the Explore feature: Allows users to move to the subsequent item in a sequence of results. This is an ARIA label for accessibility purposes.
next-prev-result.aria-label-prev_description: ARIA label for a button in the Explore view, within the result navigation controls: Its purpose is to allow users to navigate to the previous search or query result. This text provides context for screen readers.
next-prev-result.depth_description: Display field in the Next/Previous Result navigation within the Explore section: It displays the current hierarchical depth of the data or result being viewed.
next-prev-result.depth-span-filter-matches_description: Label in Next/Previous Result navigation in the Explore view: Displays the hierarchical depth of a matched trace span or item when reviewing filter results.
next-prev-result.services_description: Display text in the 'Next/Previous Result' section of the Explore view: Indicates the services associated with the currently displayed item or result. The `{{services}}` placeholder will show the actual service names or a count.
next-prev-result.services-span-filter-matches_description: Status indicator in the next/previous result navigation within the Explore section: It shows the number of services ({{total}}) that match the current filter or are displayed, out of the total services ({{services}}) in the current view or span.

cta-element.add-data-source_description: Button in the Explore page's 'no data source' call-to-action section: This button allows users to initiate the process of adding a new data source when none are currently configured.
