footer.learn-more_description: Link in the footer of the 'No Data Source' call-to-action: This link provides users with additional information or guidance on configuring data sources in the Explore section.
footer.pro-tip-define-sources-through-configura: ProTip: You can also define data sources through configuration files.

ProTip message in the footer of the "no data source call-to-action" area within the Explore feature: Advises users that data sources can also be defined through configuration files, offering an advanced alternative when initially setting up data connections.

explore.prev_description: Button in the Explore section: Navigates to the previous item, page, or state within the current exploration view.

queryless-apps-extensions.aria-label-go-queryless_description: ARIA label for an interactive element in the 'Explore' section, within the 'Queryless Apps Extensions' area: It describes the action of proceeding with an application or extension without requiring a specific query. This option allows users to switch to a 'queryless' mode.

raw-list-container.item-count_description: Label in Raw List Container within the Explore section: Displays the total number of data series returned from a query. "Series" in this context refers to distinct sets of data.
raw-list-container.label-expand-results_description: Button in the Raw List Container within the Explore view: This button allows users to display additional raw data entries or logs that were initially truncated.

raw-list-item.tooltip-copy-to-clipboard_description: Tooltip in a raw list item within the Explore feature: This text is displayed when hovering over an icon or button that, when clicked, copies the specific raw list item's content to the user's clipboard.

references.label-attributes_description: Label in the References section of the Explore feature: It indicates a list or section displaying the characteristics or properties associated with a reference.

rich-history.close-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the Explore section's rich history panel: Indicates that clicking the associated icon or button will close the query history view.
rich-history.datasource-a-z_description: A sorting option in the 'Rich History' section of the 'Explore' feature: This option allows users to sort the displayed rich history entries alphabetically by their associated data source name (A-Z).
rich-history.datasource-z-a_description: Sorting option in the Explore > Rich History section: Allows users to sort the list of historical data sources in descending alphabetical order (Z-A).
rich-history.newest-first_description: Sorting option in the rich history panel within the Explore section: Allows users to sort the displayed history entries by date, with the newest entries appearing at the top.
rich-history.oldest-first_description: Sort option in the "Rich History" feature of the "Explore" section: Used to order a list of historical entries (e.g., queries, dashboards) by their creation or usage time, displaying the oldest items first.
rich-history.query-history_description: Section title in the 'Rich History' area within the 'Explore' page: Displays a list of all queries that have been previously executed by the user.
rich-history.settings_description: Button in the Rich History feature within the Explore section: To access configuration options for the Rich History feature.
rich-history.starred_description: Label in the rich history section within the Explore feature: Indicates or filters items that the user has marked as important or favorited for quick access.

rich-history-card.add-comment-form_description: Form in the rich history card within the Explore section: This string functions as the title or label for the input form where users can add comments.
rich-history-card.add-comment-tooltip_description: Tooltip text in the Rich History Card within the Explore section: It indicates that the associated action allows the user to add a comment to the displayed history item.
rich-history-card.add-to-library_description: Button in the rich history card within the Explore section: Allows users to save a previously executed or constructed query. This action adds the query to a personal library for future reuse.
rich-history-card.copy-query-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the rich history card within the Explore feature: Indicates that clicking the associated icon or button will copy the displayed query to the user's clipboard. This allows users to easily reuse or share past queries.
rich-history-card.copy-shortened-link-tooltip_description: Tooltip text for an action in the Rich History Card within the Explore section: It indicates that hovering over or clicking the associated icon/button will copy a shortened link to the user's clipboard.
rich-history-card.datasource-icon-label_description: Label for an icon in a rich history card within the Explore section: This text provides an accessible label or tooltip for an icon that visually represents a data source.
rich-history-card.datasource-name-label_description: Label in the Rich History card within the Explore section: Identifies the name of a data source associated with a historical query or action displayed in the card.
rich-history-card.datasource-not-exist: Data source does not exist anymore
rich-history-card.datasource-not-exist_description: Notification message in a rich history card within the Explore view: Informs the user that a data source previously associated with a historical query or dashboard entry no longer exists. This explains why the historical item might not be fully functional or accessible.
rich-history-card.delete-query-title_description: Title of a confirmation dialog in the rich history card within the Explore section: This title is displayed when a user attempts to delete a saved or historical query.
rich-history-card.delete-query-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the rich history card within the Explore section: Appears on hover over an icon or button, indicating that clicking it will delete the associated query from the history.
rich-history-card.delete-starred-query-confirmation-text: Are you sure you want to permanently delete your starred query?
rich-history-card.delete-starred-query-confirmation-text_d: Confirmation message in the rich history card within the Explore section: Asks the user to confirm the permanent deletion of a starred query. This action cannot be undone.
rich-history-card.edit-comment-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the rich history card in the Explore section: Appears when hovering over an icon or button to indicate the action of editing an associated comment.
rich-history-card.optional-description: An optional description of what the query does.
rich-history-card.optional-description_description: Description field in the rich history card of the Explore section: Allows users to add an optional explanation detailing the purpose or function of a specific query recorded in the history.
rich-history-card.query-comment-label_description: Label in the rich history card within the Explore section: Indicates a field where a user can view or add a comment pertaining to a saved or executed query. This allows users to add notes for future reference or context.
rich-history-card.query-text-label_description: Label in the Rich History Card within the Explore section: Identifies and displays the actual text content of a previously executed data query.
rich-history-card.save-comment_description: Button in the rich history card within the Explore section: Saves a comment related to the displayed history entry.
rich-history-card.star-query-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the Rich History Card within the Explore section: This text appears when hovering over the 'star' icon/button, indicating that clicking it will mark the associated query as a favorite or important.
rich-history-card.unstar-query-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the Rich History Card within the Explore section: This text appears when hovering over an icon and indicates that clicking it will remove the associated query from the user's favorites.
rich-history-card.update-comment-form_description: Form in the rich history card within the Explore section: This form allows users to update an existing comment.

rich-history-notification.query-copied_description: Notification message in the Explore feature: Informs the user that a query has been successfully copied to their clipboard, typically after interacting with a rich history or query management panel.
rich-history-notification.query-deleted_description: Notification message in the rich history notification area within the Explore section: Informs the user that a query has been successfully deleted. This message confirms the user's action.

rich-history-queries-tab.displaying-partial-queries_one_descripti: Status message in the Rich History Queries tab of the Explore section: This text informs the user about the current number of historical queries being displayed. It serves as a visual indicator of the filtered or loaded query results.
rich-history-queries-tab.displaying-partial-queries_other_descrip: Status message in the Rich History Queries tab within the Explore section: This message informs the user about the number of queries currently being displayed. It indicates that the displayed list might be a partial subset of available queries.
rich-history-queries-tab.displaying-queries_one_description: Count display in the Rich History queries tab within the Explore section: Indicates the number of historical queries currently shown or available in the list. The `{{ count }}` placeholder will be dynamically replaced with the actual numerical value.
rich-history-queries-tab.displaying-queries_other_description: Label in the Rich History Queries tab within the Explore feature: Displays the total number of queries being shown. This specific string is used when the count of queries is plural (not one).
rich-history-queries-tab.filter-aria-label_description: ARIA label for a filter control in the Rich History Queries tab of the Explore feature: It describes a filter that enables users to narrow down the displayed queries by selecting one or more data sources.
rich-history-queries-tab.filter-history_description: A filter control in the rich history queries tab within the Explore section: Used to narrow down the displayed list of rich history queries.
rich-history-queries-tab.filter-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a filter input field on the 'Queries' tab within the 'Explore' section. It guides users to input text to filter the displayed queries by their associated data source(s).
rich-history-queries-tab.history-local: The history is local to your browser and is not shared with others.
rich-history-queries-tab.history-local_description: Informational text in the Rich History Queries tab of the Explore section: Informs the user that the displayed query history is stored locally within their browser and is not shared with others.
rich-history-queries-tab.search-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Rich history queries tab of the Explore section: Prompts the user to type keywords to search through historical or previously executed queries.
rich-history-queries-tab.showing-queries_description: Status message with a 'Load more' link in the Rich History Queries tab within the Explore section: It informs the user about the number of rich history queries currently displayed out of the total available, and provides an option to load more. `{{ shown }}` and `{{ total }}` are numerical placeholders for the count.
rich-history-queries-tab.sort-aria-label_description: ARIA label for a button in the Rich History Queries tab within the Explore section: This label describes the action of sorting the displayed list of historical queries. It is primarily used for accessibility, providing context to screen readers.
rich-history-queries-tab.sort-placeholder_description: Placeholder text for a sort control in the Rich History Queries tab of the Explore section: Prompts the user to select a criterion for sorting the displayed queries.

rich-history-settings-tab.change-default-tab: Change the default active tab from “Query history” to “Starred”
rich-history-settings-tab.change-default-tab_description: Setting label in the Rich History Settings tab of the Explore section: This option allows users to configure the default active tab for the rich history view, choosing between 'Query history' and 'Starred'.
rich-history-settings-tab.clear-history-info: Delete all of your query history, permanently.
rich-history-settings-tab.clear-history-info_description: Descriptive text in the Rich History Settings tab of the Explore feature: This string informs the user about the irreversible action of permanently deleting all their stored query history. It likely accompanies a button or is part of a confirmation dialog.
rich-history-settings-tab.clear-query-history_description: Button in the "Rich History" settings tab within the "Explore" section: Allows the user to clear their saved history of executed queries.
rich-history-settings-tab.clear-query-history-button_description: Button in the Rich History Settings tab within the Explore section: Clears the user's previously executed query history.
rich-history-settings-tab.delete-confirm-text: Are you sure you want to permanently delete your query history?
rich-history-settings-tab.delete-confirm-text_description: Confirmation message in the Rich History Settings tab of the Explore section: Prompts the user to confirm the permanent and irreversible deletion of their stored query history.
rich-history-settings-tab.history-time-span_description: Setting name in the Rich History Settings tab within the Explore section: This label indicates a setting where the user can define the duration for which historical data or events are retained or displayed.
rich-history-settings-tab.history-time-span-description: Select the period of time for which Grafana will save your query history. Up to {{MAX_HISTORY_ITEMS}} entries will be stored.
rich-history-settings-tab.only-show-active-datasource: Only show queries for data source currently active in Explore
rich-history-settings-tab.only-show-active-datasource_description: Checkbox in the Rich History Settings tab within the Explore feature: Filters the displayed query history. When checked, only queries executed against the currently active data source in the Explore view are shown.
rich-history-settings-tab.query-history-deleted_description: Status message in the rich-history-settings-tab within the Explore section: Notifies the user about the successful deletion of their query history.

retention-period.1-week_description: A selectable duration option in the Rich History Settings tab within the Explore section: Represents a one-week retention period for historical data or events.
retention-period.2-days_description: Option in the Retention Period setting on the Rich History Settings tab in the Explore section: This option allows users to set the rich history data retention to two days.
retention-period.2-weeks_description: Option value in the Retention Period setting within the Rich History Settings tab: Specifies that rich history data will be retained for two weeks. This is one of the selectable options for setting the data retention duration.
retention-period.5-days_description: Option in the 'Retention Period' setting on the 'Rich History Settings' tab within the 'Explore' section: This value specifies a duration of 5 days for which rich history data will be retained.

rich-history-starred-tab.filter-queries-aria-label_description: Accessibility label for an input field in the "Starred" tab of the Rich History section on the Explore page: This label describes an input field used to filter the displayed queries by their associated data sources. It is primarily intended for screen readers.
rich-history-starred-tab.filter-queries-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a filter/search input field on the 'Rich History' or 'Starred' tab within the 'Explore' section. It prompts the user to enter text to filter the displayed queries by their associated data source(s).
rich-history-starred-tab.local-history-message: The history is local to your browser and is not shared with others.
rich-history-starred-tab.local-history-message_description: Informational message in the 'Local History' feature within the 'Rich History & Starred' tab of the 'Explore' view: This message informs the user about the privacy and storage scope of the displayed history. It clarifies that this history is stored locally in their browser and is not shared with other users.
rich-history-starred-tab.search-queries-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the search input field within the 'Starred' tab of the 'Rich History' section on the 'Explore' page: It prompts users to enter text to search for saved or starred queries.
rich-history-starred-tab.showing-queries_description: Status message with an embedded action link in the 'Rich History Starred' tab of the Explore section: This message displays the current number of starred queries shown out of the total, with a clickable link to load more results.
rich-history-starred-tab.sort-queries-aria-label_description: An ARIA label for a sort control in the 'Starred' tab of the 'Rich History' feature on the Explore page: Users can activate this control to rearrange the order of their starred queries. It provides an accessible name for the sorting functionality.
rich-history-starred-tab.sort-queries-placeholder_description: Label in the Starred tab of the Rich History feature in the Explore section: This text introduces a control (e.g., dropdown, menu) that allows users to sort the list of starred queries.

rich-history-utils.a-week-ago_description: A time range option within the Explore section's rich history utilities: It specifies a predefined time period covering the last week, relative to the current moment.
rich-history-utils.days-ago_description: Time display label in the Explore section, within rich history utilities: Indicates how many days ago an event or data point occurred. The `{{num}}` placeholder will be replaced by the actual number of days.
rich-history-utils.today_description: Time selection option in the Explore section, within a rich history utility: This option allows users to quickly filter or view data specifically for the current day.
rich-history-utils.two-weeks-ago_description: Relative time option in the Explore section's rich history utilities: Allows users to quickly select or refer to a time period starting two weeks prior to the current date for data filtering or display.
rich-history-utils.yesterday_description: Time range option in the Explore section's rich history utilities: Represents the previous day for data selection. This option is typically found within a time range selector or filter.

rich-history-utils-notification.saving-failed_description: Notification message in the Explore page's rich history notification area: Informs the user that an attempt to save the rich history has failed.
rich-history-utils-notification.update-failed_description: Notification message in the Explore section: Informs the user that an update operation for the 'Rich History' feature has failed. This message indicates an issue with synchronizing or refreshing historical data.

run-query.left-pane_description: Pane in the 'Run Query' section of the 'Explore' feature: It typically contains controls, query builders, or data selection options for building and executing queries.
run-query.right-pane_description: Pane in the Run Query section of the Explore feature: This string labels the UI panel located on the right side of the screen. It serves as a title or identifier for this specific area.
run-query.run-query-button_description: Button in the Explore page, within the query editor section: Triggers the execution of the entered query.
run-query.switch-datasource-button: Switch data source and run query
run-query.switch-datasource-button_description: **Button** in the "Run Query" section of the "Explore" view: This button allows users to change the currently selected data source and then immediately execute the query against the newly chosen source. It implies that a query is already defined or being edited, and this action applies that query to a different data source.

text.span_description: Label in the Explore feature's 'scoped variables from span' settings: Identifies a trace span, a fundamental unit of work or duration in distributed tracing, for the purpose of variable extraction. The French translation 'Durée' emphasizes its temporal aspect.

text.tags_description: Label in the 'Scoped Variables from Tags' configuration within the Explore section: Designates an area for managing tags used to create scoped variables.

text.trace_description: Text label in the 'Explore' section, within the 'scoped variables from trace' feature: It identifies or represents a distributed trace.

suffix.tooltip-clear-input_description: Tooltip in the search bar input's suffix within the 'Explore' section: This tooltip appears when hovering over an icon that, when clicked, clears the text currently entered into the search bar.

secondary-actions.add-from-query-library_description: Menu item in the secondary actions section of the Explore page: Allows users to add a query to their current view by selecting from a library of previously saved queries.
secondary-actions.query-add-button_description: Button in the secondary actions section of the Explore view: Allows users to add a new data source query to retrieve additional data.
secondary-actions.query-add-button-aria-label_description: Button in the secondary actions section of the Explore view: Used to add a new query to the current dashboard or panel.
secondary-actions.query-history-button_description: Button in the secondary actions area of the Explore view: Allows users to view a list of their past queries. It typically opens a panel or page displaying previously executed data queries.
secondary-actions.query-history-button-aria-label_descript: Button in the secondary actions menu within the Explore view: Opens a panel or dialog displaying a list of previously executed queries.
secondary-actions.query-inspector-button_description: Button in the secondary actions section of the Explore page: Allows users to inspect the details, performance, and raw results of the data query being executed or edited. This tool aids in debugging and understanding data retrieval for dashboard panels.
secondary-actions.query-inspector-button-aria-label_descri: Button in the Explore secondary actions menu: Used to open a tool for inspecting and debugging data queries. This string provides an accessibility label for the button.

explore.show-critical-path-only_description: Toggle in the Explore feature: This option filters the displayed view to show only the critical path. It helps users focus on the most essential elements or dependencies within a complex system or timeline.
explore.show-matches-only_description: A checkbox or toggle switch in the Explore view: It filters the displayed results, showing only items that match a specific search query or set criteria.

span-bar.tooltip-critical-path: A segment on the <1>critical path</1> of the overall trace / request / workflow.
span-bar.tooltip-critical-path_description: Tooltip in the Explore view's span bar: Explains that a specific segment is part of the 'critical path' within an overall trace, request, or workflow. It clarifies the significance of such a segment for the user.

span-bar-section.description-link: Add additional info next to the service and operation on a span bar row in the trace view.
span-bar-section.description-link_description: Link in the Span Bar section of the Explore view: Provides functionality to add additional information next to the service and operation on a span bar row in the trace view.
span-bar-section.title-span-bar_description: Title in the span-bar-section within the Explore feature: This element identifies a UI control used to define or display a specific range or interval, often a time span.

span-bar-settings.aria-label-selectlabelname_description: ARIA label for a selection control in the Span Bar Settings within the Explore section: This label instructs users to choose a specific label name. The selected label name likely customizes the display or organization of span bars.
span-bar-settings.label-label_description: Field label in the 'Span Bar Settings' section of the 'Explore' view: This identifies the option or input field for defining a display label for a span bar.
span-bar-settings.label-tag-key_description: Label in the Span bar settings within the Explore view: This identifies the input field for specifying a tag key. This tag key is used to configure how spans are displayed or grouped.
span-bar-settings.placeholder-duration_description: Placeholder text in an input field within the 'Span Bar Settings' section of the 'Explore' feature: It prompts the user to enter a time duration.
span-bar-settings.placeholder-enter-tag-key_description: Placeholder text in an input field within the Span Bar Settings in the Explore view: This prompt guides the user to enter a key for a tag. It is likely used for filtering or defining criteria for data displayed in span bars.
span-bar-settings.tooltip-default-duration_description: Label in span bar settings within the Explore section: Informs the user about the default duration that will be applied to tooltips.
span-bar-settings.tooltip-tag-key: Tag key which will be used to get the tag value. A span's attributes and resources will be searched for the tag key

label.kind_description: Label in the Span Detail view within the Explore section: This label identifies and displays the kind or type of the trace span being viewed.
label.library-name_description: Label in the span detail view within the Explore section: Displays the name of the software library associated with a specific span. This helps users understand the origin of an operation within a trace.
label.library-version_description: Label in the span detail view within the Explore section: Identifies the version of a library. This informs the user about the specific software library version associated with the detailed span being examined.
label.status_description: Label in the Span Details view within the Explore section: Identifies the current status of the displayed span. It is typically followed by a dynamic status value.
label.status-message_description: Label in the Span Detail view within the Explore section: Identifies the message describing the status of a specific span or operation.
label.trace-state_description: Label in the Span Details view within the Explore section: This label identifies and describes the current state or status of the overall trace associated with the displayed span. It is typically followed by the actual trace state value.

span-detail.label-resource-attributes_description: Section header in the Span Detail view within the Explore section: This header introduces a list or section displaying attributes that describe the resource which generated the currently viewed span.
span-detail.label-span-attributes_description: Label in the Span Detail view within the Explore section: This label identifies a section that displays various attributes and properties associated with a selected trace span. These attributes provide detailed metadata about the span, such as its name, duration, tags, or other relevant contextual information.
span-detail.label-stack-trace_description: Label in the Span Detail view within the Explore section: This label identifies the section that displays the call stack for the selected span. It is crucial for debugging and understanding the execution flow of the operation.

label.child-count_description: Label in the overview items section of a span detail view: This label precedes the numerical value representing the total number of child spans for the currently selected span.
label.duration_description: Label in the overview section of a span's detail view within the "Explore" functionality: It indicates the time taken for the specific operation or event represented by the span.
label.service_description: Label in the Span Details view's overview items section: Identifies the service name associated with the displayed span.
label.start-time_description: Label in the 'Overview Items' section of the 'Span Detail' view within the 'Explore' feature: Identifies the start time of the displayed span.

span-detail.share-span_description: Button in the Span Detail page within the Explore section: Enables users to share the details of the currently viewed span.
span-detail.warnings_description: Label in the Span Detail view within the Explore section: Identifies a section presenting non-critical issues or anomalies detected within the selected trace span. This section typically lists messages that are not errors but may indicate potential problems.

span-filters.aria-label-select-max-span-operator_desc: Aria-label for a dropdown menu in the Span Filters section of the Explore view: This label describes a control where users can choose an operator to apply to the maximum span duration filter. It enhances accessibility for screen reader users.
span-filters.aria-label-select-min-span-operator_desc: Label for a selection control in the Span Filters section of the Explore view: It prompts users to choose an operator (e.g., "greater than") for specifying the minimum span duration in a filter.
span-filters.aria-label-select-service-name_descripti: Dropdown label in the Explore view's Span Filters section: Allows users to select a service name for filtering. This is an accessibility label for the dropdown.
span-filters.aria-label-select-service-name-operator_: ARIA label for a selection control in the Span filters section of the Explore view: It guides the user to choose a logical operator (e.g., equals, contains) for applying a service name filter.
span-filters.aria-label-select-span-name_description: Accessibility label for a dropdown or selection input in the Span Filters section of the Explore view. It guides users to select a span name for filtering tracing data.
span-filters.aria-label-select-span-name-operator_des: ARIA label for a selection field in the Span Filters section of the Explore view: Instructs the user to choose an operator to apply when filtering spans by their name.
span-filters.ariaLabel-select-max-span-duration_descr: ARIA label for a selection control in the Span Filters section of the Explore view: This label prompts users to choose the maximum allowed duration for a trace span when filtering tracing data.
span-filters.ariaLabel-select-min-span-duration_descr: This is an accessibility label for a selection control within the "Span Filters" section of the "Explore" view. Its purpose is to allow users to specify the minimum duration that spans must meet to be included in the results.
span-filters.label-collapse_description: Collapsible section label in the Explore page, specifically within the span filters area: This label identifies a section that allows users to apply filters based on 'spans' or time intervals to refine data exploration. It indicates the section can be expanded and collapsed.
span-filters.label-duration_description: Label in the span filters section of the Explore page: Specifies a filter option for defining the time duration of spans. Users can set a value here to filter traces based on the execution length of individual spans.
span-filters.label-service-name_description: Label in the Span Filters section within the Explore view: Used for identifying or filtering by the name of a service.
span-filters.label-span-name_description: Label in the Span Filters section within the Explore view: This identifies the input field for specifying or displaying the name of a tracing span. It is primarily used for filtering spans.
span-filters.label-tags_description: Label in the Span Filters section of the Explore view: It identifies the input field or section where users specify or select tags to filter tracing spans. This allows for narrowing down trace data based on associated key-value pairs.
span-filters.placeholder-all-service-names_descriptio: Placeholder text in a service name filter field within the Span Filters section of the Explore page: It indicates that all service names are currently considered in the filter, or guides the user to select specific service names.
span-filters.placeholder-all-span-names_description: Placeholder text in the span filters section of the Explore view: It indicates that the filter is currently set to include all span names, rather than specific ones, or serves as a default option to view all.
span-filters.tooltip-collapse: Filter your spans below. You can continue to apply filters until you have narrowed down your resulting spans to the select few you are most interested in.
span-filters.tooltip-duration: Filter by duration. Accepted units are {{units}}
span-filters.tooltip-duration_description: Tooltip for a duration input field in the Explore section's span filters: It explains how to filter data by duration and specifies the accepted units. The `{{units}}` placeholder is replaced with a list of valid time units.
span-filters.tooltip-tags: Filter by tags, process tags or log fields in your spans.
span-filters.tooltip-tags_description: Tooltip in the span filters section within the explore view: Explains that users can filter their spans using various data types such as tags, process tags, or log fields.

span-filters-tags.aria-label-add-tag_description: Button in the Span Filters/Tags component of the Explore view: Adds a new tag to filter spans. This text serves as an accessibility label.
span-filters-tags.aria-label-input-tag-value_description: Input field in the Span Filters/Tags section of the Explore page: Users enter a specific value for a tag to apply a filter.
span-filters-tags.aria-label-remove-tag_description: ARIA label for an interactive element in the span filters tags section of the Explore page: Describes the action of removing a selected tag.
span-filters-tags.aria-label-select-tag-key_description: Aria-label for a dropdown or selection field in the Span Filters Tags section of the Explore feature: Guides the user to select a specific tag key. This label is primarily for accessibility.
span-filters-tags.aria-label-select-tag-operator_descripti: ARIA label for a dropdown in the Span Filters Tags section within the Explore interface: It prompts users to select an operator for defining how tag values should be compared when filtering spans.
span-filters-tags.aria-label-select-tag-value_description: ARIA label for a dropdown in the Span Filters and Tags section of the Explore feature: It instructs users to select a specific value for a tag when filtering spans.
span-filters-tags.placeholder-select-tag_description: Placeholder text in the "Span Filters & Tags" section within the "Explore" view: This text guides the user to select or input a tag to filter the displayed spans.
span-filters-tags.placeholder-select-value_description: Placeholder text in an input field for span filter tags within the Explore section: It prompts the user to select a value to apply a filter.
span-filters-tags.placeholder-tag-value_description: Placeholder text in the Span filters/tags section of the Explore view: It prompts the user to enter the specific value for a tag when defining filters for spans.
span-filters-tags.tooltip-add-tag_description: Tooltip in the Span Filters & Tags section of the Explore page: Identifies an action to add a new tag. Tags are used for filtering or categorizing spans.
span-filters-tags.tooltip-remove-tag_description: Tooltip in the Explore section's span filters and tags area: This tooltip text is displayed when hovering over an element that removes a selected tag used for filtering spans.

span-flame-graph.flame-graph_description: Label in the Explore section's Span Flame Graph view: Identifies the interactive visual representation of trace spans. This visualization is used for performance analysis and understanding call hierarchies.

supplementary-result-error.show-details_description: Button or Link in an error message concerning supplementary results within the Explore view: It allows users to view more detailed information about the specific error.

table.no-data_description: Informational message in a table within the Explore section: It indicates that the executed data query or request returned zero series to be displayed.
table.title: Table
table.title_description: Title within the 'table' view of the Explore section: This identifies a data visualization that displays information in a tabular format.
table.title-with-name_description: Table Title in the Explore section: Identifies a specific table, including its user-defined name. The `{{name}}` placeholder will be replaced by the table's actual name.

time-sync-button.aria-label-synced_description: Accessibility label for the time synchronization button in the Explore view: This label informs screen reader users that time ranges across relevant panels or dashboards are currently synchronized.
time-sync-button.aria-label-unsynced_description: ARIA label for a time synchronization button in the Explore feature: It indicates that the current time settings are not synchronized, providing an accessible name for screen reader users when the button is in an unsynced state.

timeline-collapser.tooltip-collapse_description: Tooltip in the Explore section's Timeline Collapser component: This tooltip indicates an action to collapse one additional item or section within the timeline view. The "+1" signifies collapsing one more item that is currently expanded.
timeline-collapser.tooltip-collapse-all_description: Tooltip in the timeline collapser component of the Explore section: Informs the user that clicking the associated button will collapse all displayed elements.
timeline-collapser.tooltip-expand_description: Tooltip in Explore page, within the timeline collapser: This tooltip describes an action to incrementally expand the timeline view by one additional unit or segment. The '+1' explicitly indicates this specific expansion amount.
timeline-collapser.tooltip-expand-all_description: Tooltip in the timeline collapser component within the Explore section: Appears when hovering over a button that expands all collapsed elements in a timeline view.

timeline-header-row.title: Service & Operation
timeline-header-row.title_description: Title in the timeline header row within the Explore section: This title identifies the specific view or data displayed in the timeline, indicating its focus on services and their operational status or events.

toolbar.add-to-queryless-extensions_description: Button in the Explore toolbar: Allows the user to switch to a "queryless" mode or view. This mode enables interaction with data using pre-configured options rather than direct queries.
toolbar.aria-label_description: Toolbar in the Explore section: This accessibility label identifies the toolbar located within the 'Explore' interface. It provides users with various tools and controls for data exploration and query building.
toolbar.copy-link_description: Button in the Toolbar within the Explore view: Copies the URL of the currently displayed content or state.
toolbar.copy-link-abs-time_description: Button in the Explore view's toolbar: Allows users to copy the absolute URL of the currently displayed content or dashboard. This URL includes the specific absolute time range selected by the user.
toolbar.copy-links-absolute-category: Time-sync URL links (share with time range intact)
toolbar.copy-links-absolute-category_description: Option in the Explore feature's toolbar: Allows users to generate and copy a URL link for the current dashboard or panel view that includes and preserves the selected time range. This ensures the shared link always displays the data for the exact same period.
toolbar.copy-links-normal-category_description: Menu item or category label in the 'Copy Links' section of the Explore toolbar: This option allows users to copy a standard, non-time-bound URL to the current dashboard or panel. It distinguishes normal links from other link types (e.g., with time ranges or snapshots).
toolbar.copy-shortened-link_description: Button in the Explore toolbar: Allows users to copy a shortened version of the current Explore view's URL to their clipboard for easy sharing.
toolbar.copy-shortened-link-abs-time_description: Button in the Explore toolbar: Allows users to copy a shortened URL for the current Explore view that includes the selected *absolute* time range. This ensures the shared link displays data for a fixed, specific period.
toolbar.copy-shortened-link-label_description: Button in the toolbar of the Explore section: Initiates the sharing functionality for the current dashboard or data view, which typically includes options to copy a shortened link.
toolbar.copy-shortened-link-menu_description: Menu item in the Toolbar within the Explore section: This item, when clicked, displays a menu or pop-over with various options for copying a shortened link.
toolbar.refresh-picker-run_description: Button in the Explore toolbar: Initiates the execution of the currently defined query.
toolbar.split-close-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the Explore toolbar: Informs the user that clicking the associated button will close an active split pane, reverting the display to a single pane view.
toolbar.split-narrow_description: Button in the toolbar within the Explore section: Used to adjust the layout of display panes, specifically to make a pane narrower. This option is typically found in split-view configurations.
toolbar.split-title_description: Button in the Explore toolbar: Used to split the current panel or visualization into multiple instances. This allows users to compare different time ranges or query results side-by-side.
toolbar.split-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the Toolbar of the Explore section: Describes a button that splits the current display pane into multiple sections. This allows users to view multiple queries or data sources simultaneously.
toolbar.split-widen_description: Button in the toolbar in the Explore view: This button allows users to increase the width of a specific pane, particularly when the view is split into multiple panels, providing more horizontal space to that pane.

trace-page-header.aria-label-share-dropdown_description: Button in the trace page header of the Explore section: This button opens a dropdown menu with options for sharing the current trace. This is an accessibility label for screen readers.
trace-page-header.duration_description: Label in the trace page header within the Explore section: Indicates the total time taken for a trace or a specific operation displayed on the page. It provides a key performance metric for understanding trace execution.
trace-page-header.export-started_description: Status message in Trace page header: Informs the user that an export operation has successfully started. This message provides immediate feedback after initiating an export.
trace-page-header.give-feedback_description: Button in the trace page header within the explore section: Allows users to provide feedback related to the trace page or its functionality.
trace-page-header.link-copied_description: Confirmation message in the header of a trace-specific page within the Explore section: Notifies the user that a link to the current trace view has been successfully copied to their clipboard.
trace-page-header.path_description: Label in the Trace page header: Identifies the section or field that displays the execution path of a trace. This path outlines the sequence of services or operations involved in a transaction.
trace-page-header.route_description: Section title or tab in the header of the trace details page within the Explore section: It indicates the area where users can view the execution path or flow of a specific trace.
trace-page-header.services_description: Section header in the trace page header within the Explore section: Labels a view or tab dedicated to displaying information about the various services involved in the traced transactions.
trace-page-header.share_description: Button in the trace page header within the Explore section: This button allows users to share the currently displayed trace or its view with others.
trace-page-header.share-copy-link_description: Button in the trace page header within the explore section: Allows users to copy a shareable link to the currently displayed trace.
trace-page-header.share-export-json_description: Button in the trace page header within the Explore section: Allows the user to export the current trace data or its configuration in JSON format.
trace-page-header.share-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the trace page header: This tooltip appears when hovering over the share icon or button for a trace, indicating that users can share the currently displayed trace.
trace-page-header.start-time_description: Label in the trace page header within the Explore section: Designates the start time for the displayed trace data.
trace-page-header.target_description: Label in Trace Page Header in the Explore section: Refers to the specific service, application, or endpoint that is the subject of the trace.
trace-page-header.title-share-thoughts-about-tracing-grafa: Share your thoughts about tracing in Grafana.
Title in the header of the Trace page within the Explore section: Prompts users to provide feedback on tracing features integrated with Grafana.
trace-page-header.tooltip-url_description: Tooltip message in the trace page header of the Explore section: It indicates the various ways a location identifier (such as a route, URL, target, or path) related to the trace can be presented.
trace-page-header.trace-id_description: A label in the header of the trace page within the 'Explore' section: It identifies the unique ID of a distributed trace, which is typically displayed for user reference.

trace-page-search-bar.aria-label-clear-filters_description: Button in the trace page search bar within the Explore section: This ARIA label describes a button that, when clicked, clears all currently applied filters.
trace-page-search-bar.label-show-matches_description: Toggle switch in the search bar on the Trace Page within the Explore section. This switch allows users to filter search results to display only items that match their current search query.
trace-page-search-bar.label-show-paths_description: Switch in the trace page search bar within the Explore section: This switch controls whether the trace view displays all paths or is filtered to show only the critical path.

trace-view.aria-label-copy_description: Accessibility label in the trace view of the Explore section: Describes the action of copying specific trace-related data (e.g., a trace ID or span ID) to the user's clipboard. This label provides context for screen reader users interacting with a copy button or icon.
trace-view.no-data_description: Status message in Trace View within the Explore section: Informs the user that no trace data is available to display.
trace-view.tooltip-copy-icon_description: Feedback message in Trace View of the Explore section: This temporary message appears to confirm that content has been successfully copied to the clipboard after clicking a copy icon.

trace-view-container.title-trace_description: Title in the Trace view container within the Explore section: Labels the area dedicated to displaying the details of a specific trace. This trace typically represents an observed execution path or log sequence.

unconnected-node-graph-container.count-warning: ({{numNodes}} nodes, can be slow to load)
unconnected-node-graph-container.count-warning_description: Warning text in the Unconnected Node Graph container on the Explore page: Informs the user about the number of unconnected nodes being displayed and warns that a high count may result in slow loading times for the graph.
unconnected-node-graph-container.title-node-graph_description: Title in the 'Unconnected Node Graph' container within the Explore section: Labels the node graph visualization displayed in this container.

description.show-results-in-logs-visualisation_descr: Option description in the Explore section, within the unthemed logs settings: Describes an option to display query results in a dedicated logs visualization format, likely for logs without a specific parsing theme applied.
description.show-results-in-table-visualisation_desc: Option text in Explore > Unthemed Logs section: This text labels an option to display the results of a log query or search in a tabular visualization.
description.show-results-newest-to-oldest_descriptio: Label for a sorting option in the BMC Helix Dashboards Explore section for unthemed logs. This option allows users to display log entries in reverse chronological order, showing the newest results first.
description.show-results-oldest-to-newest_descriptio: Sorting option in the Explore section for unthemed logs: Sorts the displayed log results chronologically, with the oldest entries appearing first. This option controls the order in which log data is presented.

label.logs_description: Label in the "Un-themed Logs" section of the "Explore" feature: Identifies a view or content related to raw or un-categorized log data.
label.newest-first_description: Sorting option label in the Unthemed Logs view within the Explore section: This label describes a sorting preference that displays log entries with the most recent ones at the top.
label.oldest-first_description: Label in the 'Explore' section for unthemed logs: This text represents a sorting option that arranges log entries with the oldest entries appearing first. It is used to specify a chronological display order for logs.
label.table_description: A label for a display option in the Explore section for unthemed logs: Allows users to view log data in a tabular format.

unthemed-logs.label-deduplication_description: Label in the "Unthemed Logs" section within the "Explore" view: This label identifies a feature or setting that removes duplicate log entries from the display to improve readability and analysis.
unthemed-logs.label-display-results_description: Button label in the 'Explore' section for unthemed logs: This text is used on a button that triggers the display of log search results or generated data.
unthemed-logs.label-prettify-json_description: Button in the Explore section for unthemed logs: Allows users to format raw JSON content within log entries for improved readability.
unthemed-logs.label-time_description: Label in the Explore > Unthemed Logs section: This text identifies a field or column related to time, typically a timestamp for log entries or a selection for a time range.
unthemed-logs.label-unique-labels_description: Heading or label in the 'unthemed logs' view within the 'Explore' section: This refers to a feature or display that shows the distinct labels present in the unthemed log data, often used for filtering or analysis.
unthemed-logs.label-wrap-lines_description: Label in the Explore section for unthemed logs: This label is for a control that toggles whether long log lines are wrapped. This feature enhances readability by displaying long lines across multiple rows instead of a single scrollable line.
unthemed-logs.title-feedback_description: Title in the unthemed logs view within the Explore section: Prompts users to provide feedback or suggestions related to the unthemed logs feature.
unthemed-logs.title-logs_description: Title in the Explore section's unthemed logs viewer: Labels the main section or viewer dedicated to displaying raw or unsorted log data.
unthemed-logs.title-logs-volume_description: Title in the 'Unthemed Logs' section of the 'Explore' view: This title indicates a display or metric showing the total volume of log data.

unthemed-viewing-layer.reset-selection_description: Button in the Explore feature's unthemed viewing layer: Allows users to clear any active filters or data selections made. This resets the immediate view to its default state.

unthemed-virtualized-trace-view.title-scroll-to-top_description: Tooltip text in the unthemed virtualized trace view within the Explore section: Displayed when hovering over a UI element that, when activated, scrolls the user's view back to the top of the trace content.

title.deep-link-to-this-span_description: Link Text in the Explore section, within the options for creating a focus span link: This text labels a link or button that allows users to generate a deep link to the currently selected data span or time interval.

json.copy-button_description: Button in the JSON export section: Copies the displayed JSON data to the user's clipboard.
json.download-button_description: Button in the JSON export section: Initiates the download of the generated JSON file.
json.download-successful_toast_message_descri: Toast message after a JSON export: Informs the user that their JSON file has been successfully downloaded.
json.export-externally-label: Export the dashboard to use in another instance
json.export-externally-label_description: Descriptive text in the dashboard export settings for JSON format: Informs the user that the exported dashboard can be used in a different BMC Helix Dashboards instance. This facilitates sharing or migrating dashboard configurations between environments.
json.export-format_description: Field label in the JSON export options: Allows the user to specify the desired format for the exported JSON data.
json.export-mode_description: Export mode option in the JSON export settings: This option allows users to export the structural definition or configuration (the 'model') of a dashboard or report.
json.export-remove-ds-refs_description: Option in the Export JSON settings: Allows the user to exclude environment-specific deployment details from the generated JSON export. This makes the exported configuration more portable.
json.info-text: Copy or download a file containing the definition of your dashboard
json.info-text_description: Informational text in the "Export Dashboard as JSON" section: It explains to the user the functionality of copying or downloading the definition of their dashboard. This text guides the user on how to retrieve the dashboard's JSON configuration.
json.title: Export dashboard
json.title_description: Title for a dialog or section within the dashboard export feature: This text labels the action of exporting the current dashboard, specifically when the export format is JSON.

menu.export-as-json-label_description: Menu Item in the Export menu: This option allows users to export the current dashboard or data. Although the label is generic, the key path implies this specific action exports the data in JSON format.
menu.export-as-json-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the export menu: Displayed when hovering over an option to export data in JSON format.

math.description-abs: Returns the absolute value of its argument which can be a number or a series
math.description-abs_description: Description string in the math functions section of the expression editor: Provides a concise explanation of the 'absolute value' mathematical function, detailing its behavior and accepted argument types.
math.description-ceil: Rounds the number up to the nearest integer value. It's able to operate on series or escalar values.
math.description-floor: Rounds the number down to the nearest integer value. It's able to operate on series or escalar values.
math.description-inf-nan-null: The inf for infinity positive, infn for infinity negative, nan, and null functions all return a single scalar value that matches its name.
math.description-is-inf: Returns 1 for Inf values (negative or positive) and 0 for other values. It's able to operate on series or scalar values.
math.description-is-nan: Returns 1 for NaN values and 0 for other values. It's able to operate on series or scalar values.
math.description-is-null: Returns 1 for null values and 0 for other values. It's able to operate on series or scalar values.
math.description-is-number: Returns 1 for all real number values and 0 for non-number. It's able to operate on series or scalar values.
math.description-log: Returns the natural logarithm of its argument, which can be a number or a series
math.description-log_description: Description of a mathematical function in the expression builder: Explains the 'log' function, detailing that it returns the natural logarithm of its argument, which can be a number or a series.
math.description-round: Returns a rounded integer value. It's able to operate on series or escalar values.

classic-conditions.label-conditions_description: Label in the Classic Conditions section, under Expressions: Refers to the criteria or rules that can be set for a dashboard element or calculation.

expression-query-editor.helper-text-sql: Run MySQL-dialect SQL against the tables returned from your data sources. Data source queries (ie "A", "B") are available as tables and referenced by query-name. Fields are available as columns, as returned from the data source.
expression-query-editor.label-operation_description: Label in Expression Query Editor: This label identifies the field or control where a user selects or defines an operation (e.g., mathematical, logical, aggregation) to be applied within an expression or query.

math.available-math-functions_description: Label or Section Title in the Math Expressions section: This string indicates a list or selection of mathematical functions that users can apply or choose from.
math.run-math-operations:
Run math operations on one or more queries. You reference the query by {{refExample}} ie. {{ref1}}, {{ref2}}, {{ref3}} etc.
Example: <12>{{example}}
math.tooltip-footer: See our additional documentation on <2>Math expressions</2> .
math.tooltip-footer_description: Link in a tooltip footer for Math expressions: Provides a clickable link to additional documentation about math expressions.
math.tooltip-title_description: Tooltip title in the Math expression configuration: Identifies the element as a mathematical operator. This tooltip likely appears when hovering over a field or selection related to defining mathematical operations within an expression.
math.tooltip-trigger_description: Tooltip trigger label in the Math section of expression settings: Identifies a mathematical expression or field. Interacting with this label reveals a tooltip containing more information.

query-toolbox.tooltip-collapse-editor_description: Tooltip for a button/icon in the Query Toolbox within the Expressions feature: This tooltip describes an action to collapse an active editor panel, optimizing screen space.
query-toolbox.tooltip-copy-query_description: Tooltip in the Query Toolbox within the Expressions editor: Allows users to copy a query.
query-toolbox.tooltip-expand-editor_description: Tooltip in the Query Toolbox within the Expressions editor: Used to indicate that clicking the associated icon or button will expand the editor area.
query-toolbox.tooltip-format-query_description: Tooltip in the Query Toolbox: Explains the function of a button or icon that formats the currently displayed query text for improved readability.
query-toolbox.tooltip-run-query_description: Tooltip in the Query Toolbox within the Expressions editor: Informs users that they can run the query by pressing Ctrl/Cmd+Enter.

reduce.label-function_description: Label in the Reduce transformation settings: It identifies the field for selecting the aggregation function (e.g., sum, average, count) to be applied to data within an expression.
reduce.label-input_description: Label for an input field in the 'Reduce' configuration section of expressions: It prompts the user to provide a value or select an option required for the reduction operation.
reduce.label-mode_description: Label in Reduce settings for expressions: Refers to the method or type of reduction operation to be applied. It labels the control that allows users to select the desired reduction function (e.g., sum, average, min, max).

replace-with-number.label-replace-with_description: Label in the expressions > reduce > replace-with-number configuration: This label prompts the user to specify the numeric value that will be used for replacement during a data reduction operation.

resample.label-downsample_description: Action label within the data resampling options of an expression definition: Refers to the operation of reducing the number of data points or the granularity of a dataset. This is typically used to optimize performance and simplify data visualization.
resample.label-input_description: Label for an input field in the resample configuration within expressions: This field is used to specify the data or a parameter for the resampling operation.
resample.label-resample-to_description: Label in the 'Resample' section of the 'Expressions' editor: This label prompts the user to specify the desired time interval or frequency to which the data should be resampled.
resample.label-upsample_description: Label in the data expression's resampling options: This option allows users to increase the data sampling rate. It interpolates new data points for a higher frequency.
resample.tooltip-s-m-h_description: Tooltip in the resample configuration for expressions: It provides examples of valid time units (seconds, minutes, hours) that can be used to specify resampling intervals.

sql-expr.button-run-query_description: Button in SQL Expression Editor: Executes the SQL query defined within the editor to fetch data.
sql-expr.modal-title_description: Modal Title in the SQL Expression feature: Identifies a modal window where users can write and edit SQL queries.
sql-expr.tooltip-experimental: SQL Expressions LLM integration is experimental. Please report any issues to the Grafana team.

threshold.label-input_description: Label for an input field in the threshold configuration section of expressions. It prompts the user to enter a specific value for the threshold.

folder-filter.select-aria-label_description: ARIA label for a select field in the folder filtering section: This label provides an accessible name for the control that allows users to filter dashboards or other content by folder.
folder-filter.select-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a folder filter dropdown: This text prompts the user to select a folder for filtering data.

folder-repo.provisioned-badge_description: Badge in the folder repository: Identifies a folder that has been successfully provisioned, meaning it has been set up and made ready for use.
folder-repo.read-only-badge_description: Badge on a folder within the folder repository: Indicates that the folder is read-only, preventing users from modifying its contents.

api.folder-delete-error-provisioned: Cannot delete provisioned folder. To remove it, delete it from the repository and synchronise to apply the changes.
api.folder-deleted-success_description: Success notification in the folder management UI: Confirms to the user that a folder has been successfully deleted.
api.folder-move-error-provisioned: Cannot move provisioned folder. To move it, move it in the repository and synchronise to apply the changes.
api.folder-moved-success_description: Success notification in the folder management interface: Informs the user that a folder has been successfully relocated. This message is displayed upon a successful API response for a folder move operation.

title.loading_description: Title in the main folders navigation component: Informs the user that the navigation content for folders is currently being loaded or retrieved.

forgot-password.back-button_description: Button in the forgot password screen: Allows users to navigate back to the login page from the forgot password process.

change-password.confirm-label_description: Label in the "Change Password" screen of the "Forgot Password" process: Prompts the user to re-enter their desired new password for confirmation.
change-password.default-password-alert: Continuing to use the default password exposes you to security risks.
change-password.default-password-alert_description: **Warning message** in the **Change Password screen**: It informs the user about the security vulnerabilities of continuing to use a default password, prompting them to create a stronger one.
change-password.new-password-label_description: Label in the 'Change Password' screen (part of the 'Forgot Password' flow): Prompts the user to enter their desired new password.
change-password.skip-button_description: Button in the Change Password screen within the Forgot Password flow: Allows the user to bypass the password change step.
change-password.submit-button_description: Button in the 'Change Password' screen of the 'Forgot Password' flow: Used to submit the newly entered password and finalize the password change.
change-password.tooltip-skip-button: If you skip you will be prompted to change password next time you log in.
change-password.tooltip-skip-button_description: Tooltip on a "Skip" button in the "Change Password" screen: Informs the user that skipping the password change now will result in a prompt to change it upon their next login. This message appears after a user has initiated a password reset or recovery.

forgot-password.contact-admin: Did you forget your username or email? Contact your Grafana administrator.
forgot-password.contact-admin_description: Instructional text on the Forgot Password screen: Guides users who have forgotten their username or email to contact their Grafana administrator for assistance.
forgot-password.email-sent: An email with a reset link has been sent to the email address. You should receive it shortly.
forgot-password.reset-password-header_description: Header in the 'Forgot Password' or 'Reset Password' flow: This is the main title displayed on the screen where users are prompted to set a new password.
forgot-password.send-email-button_description: Button on the Forgot Password page: Initiates the process of sending a password reset email to the user's registered email address.
forgot-password.user-field-description: Enter your information to get a reset link sent to you
forgot-password.user-field-description_description: Descriptive text for an input field on the Forgot Password page: Instructs the user to enter their information to receive a password reset link.
forgot-password.user-field-label_description: Input field label on the Forgot Password screen: Prompts the user to enter their username or user ID to initiate the password reset process.
forgot-password.user-field-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Forgot Password screen: Guides the user to enter their email or username to initiate the password reset process.

form-prompt.continue-button_description: Button in a form prompt or dialog: Allows the user to continue editing a form or data. This button is presented when the user is given an option to resume their current editing session.
form-prompt.description: Changes that you made may not be saved.
form-prompt.description_description: Message in a prompt dialog: This message warns the user that their unsaved changes on a form might be lost. It typically appears when the user attempts to close or navigate away from a form with unsaved modifications.
form-prompt.discard-button_description: Button in a form prompt dialog: Allows the user to discard any unsaved changes made in the form. This action will typically result in the loss of those changes and close the dialog.
form-prompt.title: Leave page?
form-prompt.title_description: Title of a confirmation prompt within a form: This prompt appears when a user attempts to navigate away from a page, typically to confirm if they want to abandon unsaved changes or an ongoing action.

gauge.category-gauge_description: Visualization category label in the chart type selection menu: Identifies the 'Gauge' visualization type. This string is used as the label for the option to create a gauge chart.
gauge.category-radial-bar_description: Gauge visualization in the gauge type selector for radial bar charts: This label identifies a basic or standard gauge display. It is used when configuring or selecting a gauge that displays data in a radial bar format.
gauge.description-min-height_description: Setting description in Gauge panel configuration: This text explains the 'Minimum row height' option, which controls the minimum height of rows within the gauge display when configured for horizontal orientation.
gauge.description-min-width_description: Configuration setting in Gauge visualization display options: Specifies the minimum width for columns when the gauge is rendered with a vertical orientation.
gauge.description-neutral: Leave empty to use Min as neutral point
gauge.description-neutral_description: Instructional text in the Gauge configuration panel: Guides the user on how to set the neutral point for the gauge visualization. It indicates that leaving an associated input field empty will default the neutral point to the gauge's minimum value.
gauge.description-show-threshold-labels: Render the threshold values around the gauge bar
gauge.description-show-threshold-labels_descri: Description text in Gauge panel configuration settings: This text describes the functionality of an option that, when enabled, displays the numeric threshold values directly on or around the gauge bar in a gauge visualization.
gauge.description-show-threshold-markers: Renders the thresholds as an outer bar
gauge.description-show-threshold-markers_descr: Description in Gauge panel configuration: Clarifies the visual effect of an option that displays threshold markers. It explains that the thresholds will be rendered as an outer bar around the gauge, providing context for the visual representation.

gauge-size-options.label-auto_description: Option label in Gauge size options: This allows the gauge visualization to automatically adjust its dimensions. It contrasts with fixed or manually set sizing.
gauge-size-options.label-manual_description: Label in Gauge size options: This indicates an option for users to manually configure the size settings for a gauge.

gauge.name-gauge-size_description: Setting in Gauge panel configuration options: Allows users to adjust the visual dimensions of the gauge component.
gauge.name-min-height_description: Label in the gauge visualization's configuration options: It indicates the setting to define the minimum height for the gauge component.
gauge.name-min-width_description: Label in Gauge panel configuration settings: Defines the minimum width for the gauge visualization.
gauge.name-neutral_description: Label in a gauge visualization: Represents the "neutral" state or value range, typically used when configuring thresholds or color bands.
gauge.name-show-threshold-labels_description: Checkbox/Toggle in Gauge visualization settings: Controls the visibility of numeric labels for configured thresholds on the gauge visualization.
gauge.name-show-threshold-markers_description: Checkbox in the configuration settings for a gauge visualization: Controls the display of visual markers that indicate defined thresholds on the gauge.
gauge.placeholder-neutral_description: Text placeholder in a gauge's configuration field: This value indicates that a setting, such as the gauge's minimum or maximum range, will be automatically determined by the system. It serves as a neutral default option when no specific value is provided.
gauge.threshold_description: Label in Gauge visualization settings: Displays a configured threshold value for the gauge. The `{{value}}` placeholder dynamically shows the numeric threshold.

gen-ai.apply-suggestion_description: Button in a Generative AI feature: Allows the user to apply a suggestion generated by the Gen AI system. This confirms and implements the proposed change or action.
gen-ai.incomplete-request-error: Sorry, I was unable to complete your request. Please try again.
gen-ai.send-custom-feedback_description: Button in the Gen-AI custom feedback interface: Submits the user's custom feedback.

get-geometry-field.warning-gazetteer-not-found_description: A warning message in the geographical geometry field retrieval process: It informs the user that a required gazetteer (geographical data source) could not be found. This prevents successful completion of geo-related operations.
get-geometry-field.warning-no-geometry_description: Warning Message in a geo-related configuration or a field selection component: It informs the user that the system failed to retrieve the expected geometry data. This typically means the geographical data cannot be displayed or processed.
get-geometry-field.warning-select-geohash_description: Instructional text in the geographic data configuration section: It prompts the user to select a geohash field when defining geometry, likely appearing as a warning if the selection is required but missing.
get-geometry-field.warning-select-lat-long_description: Warning message in the geographic data configuration interface: Alerts the user that they need to select specific latitude and longitude fields. This is essential for correctly defining a geometry field.
get-geometry-field.warning-select-lookup_description: Prompt message in the 'Get Geometry Field' section of the Geo configuration: This message instructs the user to select a data field that will be used to look up or match geographical geometry. It typically appears as a warning when this selection is mandatory and not yet made.
get-geometry-field.warning-unable-to-find_description: Warning message in the Geolocation configuration or map visualization panel: Informs the user that the system could not identify suitable fields representing location data. This prevents the display or configuration of geo-spatial information.

geohash-field.no-fields-message_description: Informational message in the Geohash field configuration section of the Location Editor: Informs the user that no string fields are available for selection or mapping in the current context.

latitude-field.no-fields-message_description: Message in the Latitude field of the Location Editor: Informs the user that no numeric fields are available to select as a source for the latitude value when configuring a geographic location.

longitude-field.no-fields-message_description: Informational message in the Longitude field selector within the Location Editor: This message indicates that no numeric data fields are available for selection to define the longitude value.

lookup-field.no-fields-message_description: Informational message in a lookup field within the geographical location editor: Informs the user that no string-type fields could be found during a lookup operation.

location-editor.name-gazetteer_description: Label or section title in the geographic location editor: Refers to a geographical directory of place names. It is used for searching, defining, or managing locations.
location-editor.name-geohash-field_description: Input field in the geolocation editor: Where users can specify or view a geohash value.
location-editor.name-latitude-field_description: Label in the Geo Location Editor: This label identifies the input field where users enter or view the latitude coordinate of a geographical point.
location-editor.name-location-mode_description: Setting in the Geo Location Editor: This determines the method or approach for defining or managing a geographical location.
location-editor.name-longitude-field_description: Input field in the geolocation editor: This field allows users to input the longitude coordinate for a geographical location.
location-editor.name-lookup-field_description: Input field in the geo location editor: Allows users to search for and select a geographical name or entity.

mode-options.description-auto: Automatically identify location data based on default field names
mode-options.description-auto_description: Description for a mode option in the Geo Location Editor's 'Mode Options' section: Explains the capability to automatically identify location data. It specifies that this automatic identification relies on default field names.
mode-options.description-coords_description: Description text in the Geo Location Editor's "More Options" section. It instructs the user to specify which data fields correspond to latitude and longitude values for geographical data configuration.
mode-options.description-geohash_description: **Label** in the **"More options" section of the Geo location editor**: **Instructs the user to specify or select the geohash field.** This is used for defining geographical data based on geohash values.
mode-options.description-lookup_description: Description in the Geographic Location Editor's "More Options" section: This text guides the user to specify a Gazetteer (a geographical dictionary) and the field to be used for looking up corresponding geographic data.
mode-options.label-auto_description: Label in the mode options section of the geo-location editor: Allows users to select an automatic setting for location configuration.
mode-options.label-coords_description: Label in the geographical location editor's mode options: Represents geographical coordinates as a selection or input field.
mode-options.label-geohash_description: Option label in the mode options of the geographical location editor: This label allows users to select 'Geohash' as a method for encoding or representing geographical data.
mode-options.label-lookup_description: Label in the "More Options" section within the Geo Location Editor: This option triggers a lookup or search function for geographical data.

geomap.category-basemap-layer_description: Category label in GeoMap configuration: This string identifies a section or group of options related to selecting or configuring the foundational map layer for a geomap panel.
geomap.category-map-controls_description: A set of UI controls in the geomap interface: Allows users to interact with and adjust the map's view and displayed data.
geomap.category-map-layers_description: Section heading in a GeoMap panel: Labels a category or section where users can manage or configure different data layers displayed on the map.
geomap.category-map-view_description: Display option in geomap visualization settings: Allows the user to view data presented on a geographical map.

coordinates-map-view-editor.label-latitude_description: Label in the GeoMap's coordinates map view editor: Identifies the input field where users enter or view the latitude value for a geographical point.
coordinates-map-view-editor.label-longitude_description: Label in the geomap coordinates map view editor: This label identifies the input or display field for the geographical longitude.

debug-overlay.center_description: Label in the debug overlay on a geomap: Indicates the geographical center coordinates being displayed for debugging purposes.
debug-overlay.zoom_description: Text label in the GeoMap debug overlay: Displays the current zoom level or scale of the map. This information is intended for debugging purposes.

geomap.description-initial-view: This location will show when the panel first loads.
geomap.description-initial-view_description: Description for the 'Initial View' setting in GeoMap panel configuration: This text explains that the selected geographic location will be the first view displayed when the GeoMap panel loads.
geomap.description-mouse-wheel-zoom: Enable zoom control via mouse wheel
