formats.name-petahashes-sec_description: Unit label in the value formatting options for hash rate metrics: Represents the 'petahashes per second' unit used when displaying hash rate values in data visualization panels.
formats.name-terahashes-sec_description: Unit of measurement in value format settings for hash rate metrics: This specifies the display unit for hash rate values, representing terahashes per second.

hash-rate.name: Hash rate
hash-rate.name_description: Label in Value Format Categories: This string names the 'Hash rate' category, used for applying specific formatting to numerical values representing hash rates in dashboards.

formats.name-feet_description: **Display name** in the **`length` category of `valueFormats` settings**: This string represents the selectable unit 'feet (ft)' for data formatting in BMC Helix Dashboards.
formats.name-inch_description: Unit name in the length category within value format settings: This string identifies the 'inch' unit of measurement. It is used when selecting a display format for length-related data.
formats.name-kilometer_description: Unit format option in the length category within value format settings: Allows users to select 'kilometer' as the unit for displaying length values in dashboards.
formats.name-meter_description: Format name in value formatting options for length measurements: Identifies the 'meter' unit, including its abbreviation, for display in dashboards.
formats.name-mile_description: Unit name in the value format selection menu for length units: Identifies the 'mile' unit, including its abbreviation, for displaying numerical data in dashboards.
formats.name-millimeter_description: Option label in value format selection for length units: This string is the display name for the "millimeter" unit, used when users choose how to format length values in dashboards. It includes the common abbreviation for clarity.

length.name: Length
length.name_description: Category name in Value Formats configuration: Identifies a data formatting option where values are interpreted and displayed as units of length.

formats.name-gram_description: Display name for the 'gram' unit within the value formatting options for mass categories. Users select this to format dashboard data representing mass.
formats.name-kilogram_description: Value format option in the value format settings under the 'mass' category: This string displays the name 'kilogram' for users to select as a unit of measurement for data. It includes the standard abbreviation "(kg)" for clarity.
formats.name-metric-ton_description: Unit format label in the value formatting options for mass units: This label identifies "metric ton (t)" as a selectable unit option for displaying mass data in BMC Helix Dashboards.
formats.name-milligram_description: Format option in mass value format settings: This is the display name for the 'milligram' unit, used when selecting how mass data should be presented.
formats.name-pound_description: Unit display name in the value formatting options for mass measurements: Represents the 'pound' unit (lb) that users can select to format and display numerical data related to mass. It helps users identify and choose this specific unit for data visualization.

mass.name: Mass
mass.name_description: Category name in value format categories within BMC Helix Dashboards: It identifies the grouping for units of measurement related to mass. Users select this category to format data displays with mass-specific units.

formats.name-candala_description: Format option in the miscellaneous category within the value formats settings for Grafana data: This option allows users to select 'Candela (cd)' as a unit for displaying data values. It represents a unit of luminous intensity.
formats.name-decibel_description: Format name in the list of available value format options under the 'Miscellaneous' category: It represents the "Decibel" unit, allowing users to format numerical data for display as decibels.
formats.name-hexadecimal_description: Label in data formatting settings: Represents the option to format a displayed data value as a hexadecimal number.
formats.name-hexadecimal-0x_description: Format option name in value formatting settings: This option allows users to select 'Hexadecimal (0x)' as the display format for numeric values, showing them with a '0x' prefix.
formats.name-humidity_description: **Label** in **value format options**: This string identifies a specific format for displaying humidity measurements, where `(%H)` represents the unit or type of humidity value.
formats.name-locale_description: Label in the data value formatting options: Identifies a formatting option within the 'Miscellaneous formats' category that displays data values based on the user's locale settings.
formats.name-number_description: Format option in value formatting settings: This option allows users to select a basic numeric display format for data. It indicates that the data should be presented as a simple number.
formats.name-percent-100_description: Format option name in the value formatting settings: It defines how a numeric value is displayed as a percentage on a scale of 0 to 100. This option is typically found within a dropdown or list of data display formats under the 'Miscellaneous' category.
formats.name-pixels_description: Label in Value format selection: Identifies a unit format option for displaying numerical data as "Pixels". This format is used for measurements related to screen dimensions or image sizes.
formats.name-scientific_description: A format option label in the value formatting settings: This option allows users to display numerical values using scientific notation.
formats.name-short_description: Label in the value formatting options under miscellaneous categories: This identifies the "short" format, which applies a concise, abbreviated display style to numerical values. It is used to present data compactly (e.g., 1000 as 1k).
formats.name-si-short_description: Display name in Value Formatting settings, under the Miscellaneous category: This option identifies a short SI unit format that can be applied to numerical data. It displays numbers using standard SI prefixes (e.g., k, M, G) for brevity.
formats.name-string_description: Format option name in the miscellaneous value format categories for Grafana data: This option allows users to display a field's value as a plain string.

misc.name: Misc
misc.name_description: Category name in the Value Formats configuration: Labels a collection of miscellaneous or general data formatting options that do not fit into other specific categories.

formats.name-bars_description: Option in value formatting settings for pressure data: It labels a display format type that renders data visually as bars. This option is part of a list of predefined format choices.
formats.name-hectopascals_description: Label in value formatting settings for pressure measurements: Identifies 'Hectopascals' as a selectable unit for displaying pressure data.
formats.name-inches-mercury_description: Unit name in the pressure value format settings: This label represents the 'Inches of mercury' measurement unit that users can select for displaying pressure data in BMC Helix Dashboards.
formats.name-kilobars_description: Unit name in the pressure value format category: This label identifies 'Kilobars' as a unit option for displaying pressure measurements in dashboards. It is selectable within value formatting settings.
formats.name-kilopascals_description: Unit Label in Data Formatting Options for Pressure Measurements: Represents the 'Kilopascals' unit for displaying pressure values in dashboards.
formats.name-millibars_description: Unit Name in the unit selection dropdown for Pressure data formatting: This unit name identifies 'Millibars' as a selectable option for displaying pressure measurements.
formats.name-pascals_description: Unit display name in the value formatting options for pressure measurements: Represents "Pascals" as a selectable unit for displaying pressure values.

pressure.name: Pressure
pressure.name_description: Display Name in value formats categories: Identifies a category for formatting values related to pressure measurements. Users select this to apply pressure-specific units.

formats.name-becquerel_description: Unit format option name in the data value formatting settings, under the 'Radiation' category: This string is the display name for the 'Becquerel (Bq)' unit. It allows users to select this unit to format numerical data related to radioactivity.
formats.name-curie_description: Unit format display name in the data formatting options for radiation units: It specifies the 'curie' unit of radioactivity along with its abbreviation.
formats.name-exposure_description: Display name in the radiation value format category: This label identifies a specific unit format option for displaying radiation exposure, measured in Coulombs per kilogram (C/kg).
formats.name-gray_description: Format name in Value format settings for radiation measurements: Specifies 'Gray (Gy)' as a display option for radiation dose units.
formats.name-microsievert_description: Display option in the 'Radiation' category of value formats: This name represents the microSievert unit for formatting and displaying radiation data. It includes the standard unit symbol (µSv) for user recognition.
formats.name-microsievert-hour_description: Unit format display name in the 'Radiation' category of value formatting options: This string provides the user-facing label for the 'microSievert per hour' (µSv/h) unit, used when displaying radiation dose rate measurements.
formats.name-millisievert_description: Unit format label in the radiation category of value format settings: Specifies the display name for the millisievert (mSv) unit when formatting radiation dosage values.
formats.name-millisievert-hour_description: Value format option name in the 'Radiation' category of unit format settings: This string identifies the 'milliSievert per hour' unit (mSv/h) for displaying radiation dose rates. Users select this option to apply this specific unit format to relevant data.
formats.name-rad_description: Unit label in value formatting settings for radiation data: Provides the display name for the 'rad' unit of radiation.
formats.name-rem_description: Unit label in Radiation measurement value formatting within BMC Helix Dashboards: Represents the 'rem' (Roentgen equivalent man) unit for displaying radiation values.
formats.name-roentgen_description: Display name in Grafana's radiation value format options: This label identifies the 'roentgen' unit of radiation measurement. It is used when configuring how radiation values are displayed in dashboards.
formats.name-sievert_description: The 'Sievert (Sv)' unit label in the Radiation category of Value Formats: Defines the display text for the Sievert unit when formatting radiation-related data.
formats.name-sievert-hour_description: Unit format name in the Radiation category of value formats: This string identifies and displays the "Sievert/hour" unit (Sv/h) as an option for formatting radiation dose rate values in charts and panels. It represents a specific measurement unit.

radiation.name: Radiation
radiation.name_description: Category name in value format categories: Represents a predefined grouping for applying specific display formatting to data values related to radiation.

formats.name-degrees-sec_description: Value format label in the rotational speed unit category: This label identifies the "Degrees per second" unit option. It is used by dashboard designers to select the appropriate display unit for angular velocity metrics.
formats.name-gigahertz_description: Format option name in value format settings under the 'rotational-speed' category: Displays the unit 'Gigahertz (GHz)' for data visualization. This unit is typically associated with frequency, despite its placement within the 'rotational-speed' category.
formats.name-hertz_description: Unit Name in Rotational Speed Value Format Options: Display name for the 'Hertz (Hz)' unit, used for formatting rotational speed values. It includes the standard abbreviation.
formats.name-kilohertz_description: Unit option in the 'Rotational Speed' category of value formats: It represents the display name for the Kilohertz (kHz) unit, used when formatting frequency values in dashboards. This unit is likely applied to frequency measurements associated with rotational equipment.
formats.name-megahertz_description: Format option name in the 'Rotational Speed' category of data value formats: This string identifies the 'Megahertz (MHz)' unit for displaying numerical values related to frequency or high-frequency rotational rates in dashboards.
formats.name-radians-sec_description: Display name in the value format dropdown for rotational speed: This option allows users to format numerical data as "Radians per second (rad/s)" in a dashboard panel. It specifies a unit for angular velocity.
formats.name-rpm_description: Display name for a unit in the rotational speed value format options: This string identifies the 'Revolutions per minute (rpm)' unit. It is used to format and label rotational speed measurements in dashboards.

rotational-speed.name: Rotational Speed
rotational-speed.name_description: Category name in value format settings: Identifies the category used for formatting and displaying data values that represent rotational speed.

formats.name-celsius_description: Label for a value format option in the temperature category: Defines the display name for the Celsius temperature unit. Users see this string when selecting a temperature format for data.
formats.name-fahrenheit_description: Display Name in Value Formats > Temperature category: This string provides the display text for the Fahrenheit temperature unit, including its standard symbol (°F). It is used when users select a unit for temperature data visualization.
formats.name-kelvin_description: Unit format option name in temperature unit formatting settings: This option allows users to select "Kelvin (K)" as the unit for displaying temperature values in dashboards.

temperature.name: Temperature
temperature.name_description: Category name in value format settings: Identifies the 'Temperature' category, used for grouping temperature-related unit and display options.

formats.name-counts-min_description: Label in the value format options for throughput metrics: This string represents a unit of measurement for data displayed as counts per minute.
formats.name-counts-sec_description: Format option name within the throughput category of value formats: It defines a display unit for metrics measured in counts per second.
formats.name-events-min_description: Value format option in data formatting options for throughput metrics: This unit label represents the rate of events processed per minute. The 'epm' abbreviation stands for 'events per minute'.
formats.name-events-sec_description: This format name in the throughput value format category within Grafana data settings: Defines the display label for the 'events per second' (eps) unit, used when visualizing data related to event processing rates.
formats.name-io-ops-sec_description: This format name in the throughput category of value formats: Specifies the display unit for Input/Output Operations Per Second (IOPS). It is used to label performance metrics related to disk or network activity.
formats.name-messages-min_description: Format option name in the throughput category of value format options: This unit name is used to display data measured in messages per minute. It includes the common abbreviation 'mpm'.
formats.name-messages-sec_description: Format name in the throughput category of value formats: It is used to display metrics measuring the rate of messages processed per second. This option provides 'messages/sec (mps)' as a unit choice for dashboard visualizations.
formats.name-ops-min_description: Value format name in the Throughput category of value formats: This string is the display name for the "operations per minute" unit. It is used to label data or axes representing throughput measurements.
formats.name-ops-sec_description: Unit label in the throughput category of value formats: This string represents the display name for the "operations per second" unit. It is used to format metrics showing processing rates or system activity, with "(ops)" providing a common abbreviation.
formats.name-reads-min_description: Value Format Option in the Throughput category of Grafana data value formats: This option allows users to display metrics related to read operations as "reads per minute" (rpm).
formats.name-reads-sec_description: Format option in the throughput category of value formats: Specifies the display unit for metrics measuring read operations per second.
formats.name-records-min_description: Value format option in the throughput category within the value formatting settings: This unit is used to display data representing the rate of records processed per minute in dashboards.
formats.name-records-sec_description: Value format option name in Throughput value format settings: This string serves as a display name for a unit representing data throughput. It indicates the rate of 'records' processed per second, with 'rps' as its abbreviation.
formats.name-requests-min_description: Format name in the 'Throughput' category of value format options: It represents the unit 'requests per minute' (rpm) for displaying throughput data.
formats.name-requests-sec_description: Unit format option in the Value Formats selection for Throughput metrics: This option allows users to display throughput data as 'requests per second (rps)'.
formats.name-rows-min_description: Value format option in the Throughput category: This option defines a display unit for throughput metrics, representing the number of rows processed per minute. The abbreviation 'rpm' is used to denote 'rows per minute'.
formats.name-rows-sec_description: Format name in the throughput value format category: This option represents a unit for displaying data processing rates as rows per second (rps).
formats.name-writes-min_description: A display unit string in the Throughput category of Value Formats settings: This string provides a unit for metrics representing write operations per minute, abbreviated as 'wpm'.
formats.name-writes-sec_description: Value format name in the 'Throughput' category: This label defines a display unit for data representing write operations per second. It includes the common abbreviation 'wps' for clarity.

throughput.name: Throughput
throughput.name_description: Category Name in Value Format Categories: Identifies a predefined category for formatting metrics related to throughput. This includes measurements like data transfer rates or transaction processing rates.

formats.name-clock-ms_description: Format option in time formatting settings in BMC Helix Dashboards: This option allows users to display time values in a clock format that includes milliseconds.
formats.name-clock-s_description: Label in time value format settings: This identifies a specific format option for displaying time data, representing values in seconds with a clock-like presentation.
formats.name-days_description: This display name in the value format settings for time categories: Represents the 'days' unit, allowing users to configure how time-based data is presented.
formats.name-duration-dhms_description: Option label in the value format selection menu for time-based data. This label identifies a specific duration format that displays values in days, hours, minutes, and seconds.
formats.name-duration-hms_description: Format option label in the time value format selection: This label identifies a predefined format for displaying durations using hours, minutes, and seconds (hh:mm:ss).
formats.name-duration-ms_description: Display name in a dropdown menu for value formatting settings within the time category: It specifies that numerical values will be formatted and shown as a duration, with milliseconds as the unit.
formats.name-duration-s_description: Format name in time value formatting settings: This name identifies a format option that displays time values as a duration, specifically measured in seconds.
formats.name-hertz_description: Format name in the value format options: This label represents the Hertz (frequency) unit for displaying data in dashboards. It is categorized under 'Time' formats because Hertz is defined as cycles per second (1/s), which is the reciprocal of time.
formats.name-hours_description: Display name in the time value format selection menu: This string identifies the 'hours' unit as an option for formatting time-based data in dashboard visualizations.
formats.name-microseconds_description: Unit label in the Time format options: Represents the 'microseconds' unit for formatting and displaying time values. This option includes the standard symbol (µs).
formats.name-milliseconds_description: Format option name in the time category of value format settings: Specifies 'milliseconds (ms)' as a unit for displaying time values in dashboards.
formats.name-minutes_description: Label in time value formatting settings: Represents the 'minutes' unit option for displaying time-based data. Users select this to format how time values appear in dashboards or panels.
formats.name-nanoseconds_description: Display name in the time unit formatting options: Identifies 'nanoseconds' as a selectable unit for displaying time values in dashboards. This label helps users choose the appropriate precision for time series data.
formats.name-seconds_description: List item in time unit selection: This string is the display name for the 'seconds' time unit. It allows users to format numerical values to be displayed in seconds.
formats.name-timeticks_description: Format option label within the time category of value formats: It allows users to display time values as 'timeticks', with each tick representing 1/100th of a second.

time.name: Time
time.name_description: Category name in value format categories: This label represents the group of options for formatting time-based values in data displays.

formats.name-kilometers-hour_description: A display unit name in the velocity unit formatting options: Represents a selectable unit for formatting velocity values in dashboards as kilometers per hour (km/h).
formats.name-knot_description: Unit format option label in velocity value formatting settings: This label identifies the 'knot' (kn) unit of speed available for displaying velocity data in dashboards.
formats.name-meters-second_description: Format name in the 'Velocity' category within Value Format settings: Specifies a unit option for displaying velocity values as 'meters/second (m/s)'.
formats.name-miles-hour_description: Unit format name in the velocity category of value formatting options: This string represents the display name for the "miles per hour" unit. It is presented as a selection option for users to choose how velocity values are displayed in dashboards.

velocity.name: Velocity
velocity.name_description: Category name in value formatting settings: This label represents a category for metrics or data values that measure speed or a rate of change.

formats.name-cubic-decimeter_description: A unit name in the volume category of value formatting options: This string provides "cubic decimeter" as a selectable unit for users to format and display volume-related data in dashboards.
formats.name-cubic-meter_description: Unit Name in the Volume category within the Value Formats settings: Identifies "cubic meter" as a specific option for displaying volume measurements in dashboards. This string appears in unit selection dropdowns or configuration fields for volume-related data.
formats.name-gallons_description: Unit name in value format options for volume metrics in BMC Helix Dashboards: Represents the "gallons" unit for displaying volume measurements.
formats.name-litre_description: Value format option display name in the volume category settings: This string is the display text for 'litre' as a unit to format volume-related data. It includes the common abbreviation (L) for clarity when users select a unit.
formats.name-millilitre_description: Unit format name in the volume category of value formatting options: This string represents the display name for the 'millilitre' unit. It is presented to users when choosing how to format volume-related data in dashboards.
formats.name-normal-cubic-meter_description: **Unit format label** in the **volume category of value formatting options**: This label represents the 'Normal cubic meter' (Nm³) unit that users can select to display volume measurements. It is typically used for reporting gas volumes under standard conditions of temperature and pressure.

volume.name: Volume
volume.name_description: Category name in the value format settings: This label identifies a category of predefined units and display options specifically for metrics representing 'volume' (e.g., storage volume, data volume).

button.action-variables-title_description: Button title in the action editor interface: Labels a control for managing or accessing variables that can be used when defining or configuring actions.
button.confirm-action_description: Button in the action editor: Used to finalize or approve a pending action or configuration change within the editor.
button.style_description: Setting label in the Action Editor's button configuration section: Identifies the options for customizing the visual appearance of a button.
button.variable-value-placeholder_description: Button in the Action Editor: This button serves as a placeholder for a variable's value. It prompts the user to interact with it to define or select the specific value for a variable.

action-editor.continue_description: Button in the Action Editor: Used to advance to the next step in a multi-stage process or confirm a current selection within the action editor.

inline.add-action_description: Button in an inline action editor: Allows the user to add a new action or define a new behavior.
inline.edit-action_description: Button in the inline action editor: Initiates the editing process for an existing action. It likely opens an inline form or modal to make modifications.

modal.action-body_description: Field label in the Action Editor modal: Refers to the main content or payload area for configuring an action. This is where users define the request body, message content, or other textual data for the action.
modal.action-method_description: Label in the action editor modal: This identifies the specific method or type of action that is being configured or selected.
modal.action-query-params_description: Label in the action editor modal: This identifies a section or field for defining parameters that customize a data query executed by an action. Users configure these to influence the query results.
modal.action-title-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in an input field in the Action Editor modal: Guides the user to enter a title for an action.
modal.action-variables_description: Section header in the action editor modal: This label identifies a section where users can define or manage variables associated with the action being edited.
modal.connection_description: Label/Heading in the Action Editor modal: Indicates the area for selecting or configuring a connection that the action will utilize.
modal.connection-direct-description_descriptio: Descriptive text for a connection option in the 'Action Editor' modal dialog: It clarifies that if selected, data requests for the action will be performed directly from the user's web browser, potentially bypassing a server-side proxy.
modal.connection-direct-label_description: Label in the action editor modal: This label identifies an option for a data source connection method that fetches data directly from the user's browser. It differentiates this client-side connection from other server-side or proxied methods.
modal.connection-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the action editor modal: Prompts the user to select a connection. It appears in a dropdown or input field before a specific connection is chosen.
modal.one-click-description: Only one link or action can have one click enabled at a time
modal.one-click-description_description: Informational message in the Action Editor modal: This text explains a constraint regarding the 'one-click' feature, clarifying that it can only be enabled for one link or action at a time.

alert.close-button_description: Button in an alert message or dialog: Closes or dismisses the displayed alert notification.

auto-save-field.saved_description: Status message in an auto-save field: Indicates that the user's input or changes have been automatically saved.
auto-save-field.saving_description: Status message in an auto-save enabled field or component: Informs the user that content is currently being saved automatically. The placeholder `<1></1>` is for the name of the item being saved.

axis-builder.linear-threshold_description: Label in Axis Builder settings: This identifies a type of threshold that defines boundaries based on linear values along an axis. It is typically used for simple, direct value comparisons.
axis-builder.log-base_description: Input field label in the Axis builder: Allows users to specify the base for a logarithmic axis scale on a dashboard panel.

axis.category-axis_description: Label in the axis configuration section of a visualization builder: Identifies a setting or section for configuring an axis. It is specifically used when configuring a category axis.
axis.color-label_description: Label in the axis configuration section of the dashboard builder: Identifies the input field or control for selecting a color, likely for the axis itself or its components.

color-options.label-series_description: Label in the axis color options within the dashboard builder: This label identifies an option or setting related to configuring the color of a specific data series displayed on the axis.
color-options.label-text_description: Label in the Axis color options section of the dashboard builder: This string identifies the setting used to choose the color for the axis's text elements (e.g., tick labels, axis title).

grid-line-options.label-auto_description: This setting value in the Grid Line Options section of the Axis Builder automatically determines how labels are displayed for grid lines on a chart axis.
grid-line-options.label-off_description: The label 'Off' in the Grid Line Options for an axis within the dashboard builder: It allows users to disable or turn off a specific grid line feature or setting.
grid-line-options.label-on_description: Label in the Grid Line Options section within the Axis Builder: Indicates that a particular grid line setting or feature is enabled. This typically functions as an 'On' state for a toggle or selection.

axis.name-centered-zero_description: A configuration option in the Axis builder: This setting ensures the axis displays zero in the middle of its range, ideal for visualizing data with both positive and negative values symmetrically.
axis.name-grid-lines_description: A checkbox or toggle control in the dashboard builder's axis configuration settings: Allows users to enable or disable the display of grid lines on a chart's axis.
axis.name-label_description: Input field label in the axis configuration within the dashboard builder: It prompts the user to define the display name or title for a dashboard axis.
axis.name-placement_description: Setting in Axis builder configuration: Specifies the position of the axis name or label within the dashboard's visualization.
axis.name-scale_description: Setting label in the axis configuration builder: This allows users to define the scaling method (e.g., linear, logarithmic) for an axis.
axis.name-show-border_description: Toggle option in Axis configuration settings: Controls the visibility of a border around the axis name. This allows users to visually frame the axis title or label.
axis.name-soft-max_description: Setting name in the Axis configuration panel: Defines a flexible or suggested maximum value for an axis. It acts as a preferred upper bound that can be automatically extended if data necessitates.
axis.name-soft-min_description: Setting name in the Axis configuration section of a dashboard or panel builder: Defines a preferred minimum value for the axis scale. This 'soft' limit allows the axis to automatically extend below it if the data points require a lower boundary.
axis.name-width_description: A configuration setting in axis properties: Defines the width for an element associated with the axis. This typically controls the space allocated for the axis name or labels.
axis.placeholder-label_description: Placeholder text in the Axis configuration builder: It guides users to an optional input field where they can define a custom label for the axis.
axis.placeholder-soft-max_description: Placeholder text in the axis configuration section of the dashboard builder: It instructs the user to refer to the 'Max' option under 'Standard options' for setting the axis's upper bound. This string likely appears in an input field related to a 'soft maximum', guiding where the primary maximum value is configured.
axis.placeholder-soft-min_description: Placeholder text for a soft minimum input field in the axis configuration builder. It directs users to the primary 'Min' setting found under 'Standard options' for the axis.
axis.placeholder-width_description: Value in axis configuration settings in the Dashboard Builder: Indicates that an axis's width is automatically determined. This option allows for dynamic adjustment of axis spacing.

distribution-options.label-linear_description: Label in the Scale Distribution Editor for an Axis: This option selects a linear scale distribution, meaning values are evenly spaced along the axis.
distribution-options.label-log_description: Option label in the Axis Scale Distribution Editor: Indicates the choice to apply a logarithmic scale to a graph axis.
distribution-options.label-symlog_description: Option Label in the Axis Scale Distribution Editor: This label represents the "Symlog" scale distribution option for an axis. It allows users to apply a symmetrical logarithmic scale, which is useful for data with both positive and negative values, including those near zero.

legend.category_description: Label for a UI section in the dashboard builder: This string identifies the 'Legend' category or settings area for a visualization within the configuration interface.
legend.description-values: Select values or calculations to show in legend
legend.description-values_description: Instruction text in the legend configuration section of the dashboard builder: Guides the user to select specific values or calculations to be displayed as entries in the dashboard's legend.

mode-options.label-list_description: Option label within the legend mode options settings: for displaying legend items as a simple list. This option dictates how the legend data is presented, typically contrasting with table or hidden modes.
mode-options.label-table_description: Option label in Legend display mode options: This option allows users to display the chart legend items in a tabular format.

legend.name-mode_description: Label for a setting in the Legend builder: This option allows users to select a specific mode for displaying or generating legend names.
legend.name-placement_description: Option label in Legend configuration settings: Allows users to specify the position of the legend on a dashboard panel.
legend.name-values_description: Label in the legend configuration panel: Identifies options or a section related to displaying data values within the dashboard legend.
legend.name-visibility_description: Option label in the legend configuration builder: Controls the display of elements within the legend. This specific option likely governs the visibility of names associated with series or data points in the legend.
legend.name-width_description: Label in Legend builder settings: Controls the width allocated for displaying the names or labels of legend items (e.g., series names).

placement-options.label-bottom_description: Option label in the legend placement options within the dashboard builder: Indicates the choice to position the legend at the bottom of a visualization.
placement-options.label-right_description: Option label in Legend placement settings within the dashboard or panel builder: This option, when selected, positions the legend to the right side of the visualization.

stacking.name-stack-series_description: Option in the panel builder's stacking configuration section: This allows users to enable or disable the stacking of multiple data series within a chart or graph. Stacking displays series values cumulatively on top of each other.

text.category-text-size_description: Label or category heading in the text customization settings: This option allows users to adjust the font size of text displayed within a dashboard panel or widget.
text.name-percent-change_description: Field label in the Dashboard Builder's text configuration: This label identifies a setting or display option for configuring or showing values as a percentage change.
text.name-value_description: Label in a text builder or configuration section: It identifies the input field or display area for the actual content or setting. This label represents the 'value' part of a name-value pair, typically for text-related properties.
text.placeholder-percent-change_description: Placeholder text in a text input field within the dashboard builder's 'percent change' setting: It indicates that 'Auto' is the default or an available option for automatically calculating or displaying percentage changes.
text.placeholder-title_description: Placeholder text for a title input field in the Text builder section of the Grafana UI: It indicates that the title will be automatically generated if not explicitly provided by the user.
text.placeholder-value_description: Placeholder text in a text input field within a builder component: It suggests an automatic value ("Auto") for the field. This option implies the system will handle the value automatically if no specific input is provided.

tooltip.category_description: Category label in the dashboard builder's tooltip configuration: This heading marks the section dedicated to defining and customizing tooltip properties for dashboard elements.
tooltip.description-hover-proximity: How close the cursor must be to a point to trigger the tooltip, in pixels
tooltip.description-hover-proximity_description: Configuration setting description in the dashboard builder's tooltip configuration: It describes the maximum pixel distance the cursor can be from a data point to trigger its tooltip. This setting controls the hover sensitivity for tooltips.

modeOptions.label-all_description: Option Label in Tooltip Mode Options within the Dashboard Builder: This option allows users to select a tooltip display mode that shows data for all applicable series or points. It's used to configure how tooltips present information.
modeOptions.label-hidden_description: Option label in the Tooltip mode options of the UI builder: This option represents a display mode where the tooltip is completely hidden and not shown to the user.
modeOptions.label-single_description: Option label in the tooltip mode options within the dashboard builder: This option configures the tooltip to display information for a single hovered data point or element.

tooltip.name-hide-zeros_description: Toggle label in a tooltip within the Grafana UI builder: This option allows users to hide data values that are zero in visualizations or tables. This helps improve readability by focusing on non-zero data.
tooltip.name-hover-proximity_description: Setting in the tooltip configuration of the panel builder: This option defines the distance or area around a data point that triggers the display of its associated tooltip when hovered over.
tooltip.name-max-height_description: Setting label in the tooltip configuration within the dashboard builder: Specifies the maximum vertical height allowed for a tooltip. This setting helps prevent tooltips from becoming excessively long and obscuring content.
tooltip.name-max-width_description: Configuration option label in the tooltip configuration panel: Defines the maximum width for the name component displayed within a tooltip. This setting helps manage the layout and readability of tooltip content.
tooltip.name-tooltip-mode_description: Setting in the dashboard builder's tooltip configuration: Allows users to select the display mode or behavior for tooltips on visualizations.
tooltip.name-values-sort-order_description: Label in tooltip configuration settings within the dashboard builder: This option allows users to define the sort order for values displayed in the tooltip.

sortOptions.label-ascending_description: Label in a sort options tooltip in a builder interface: Indicates sorting data in ascending order (e.g., A-Z, 1-9).
sortOptions.label-descending_description: Label in the sort options of a builder's tooltip: It indicates the option to sort data in descending order.
sortOptions.label-none_description: Option label in a menu or list of sorting options found within a tooltip in the builder: This label indicates the choice to apply no specific sorting order.

card.option_description: Label in a card component: This text identifies a selectable choice or a configuration setting available within the card.

carousel.aria-label-open-image_description: ARIA label in a carousel: This label describes a clickable element that, when activated, opens a larger preview of an image displayed within the carousel. This is used for accessibility purposes.

cascader.clear-button_description: Button in a Cascader component: This button allows users to clear any selected items within the cascader, effectively resetting its current selection.

color-picker-popover.palette-tab_description: Tab label in the color picker popover: Identifies the tab that allows users to select colors from a palette.
color-picker-popover.spectrum-tab_description: Tab label in the color picker popover: This tab allows users to switch to a mode where they can select or define a custom color, as opposed to choosing from predefined palettes.

color-swatch.aria-label-default_description: Color swatch in the Grafana UI: This ARIA label provides an accessible default description for screen readers, prompting users to pick a color. It is associated with the interactive element used for color selection.
color-swatch.aria-label-selected-color_description: ARIA label in a color swatch component: Describes the currently selected color for accessibility purposes. The `{{colorLabel}}` placeholder will be replaced with the actual color name.

confirm-content.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in an input field within a confirmation dialog: Instructs the user to type a specific phrase, indicated by `{{confirmPromptText}}`, to confirm a potentially destructive or irreversible action.

data-link-editor.info: With data links you can reference data variables like series name, labels and values. Type CMD+Space, CTRL+Space, or $ to open variable suggestions.
data-link-editor.new-tab-label_description: Checkbox label in the data link editor: This option allows users to configure a data link to open its target URL in a new browser tab. It specifies the behavior for opening the linked content.
data-link-editor.title-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Data Link Editor: It suggests a descriptive title for a data link, guiding the user on its purpose (e.g., "Show details" for a link that reveals more information).

data-link-editor-modal.one-click-description: Only one link can have one click enabled at a time
data-link-editor-modal.one-click-description_description: Informational text in the Data Link Editor modal: Explains that only one data link can be configured for a 'one-click' action simultaneously. This ensures a clear primary action for interactive elements.

data-link-inline-editor.one-click_description: Setting label in the Data Link Inline Editor: This text designates an option or mode where a data link is activated by a single click.

data-links-inline-editor.add-link_description: Button in Data Links Inline Editor: Used to add a new data link definition.
data-links-inline-editor.edit-link_description: Button in the data links inline editor: Allows users to modify an existing data link's configuration.
data-links-inline-editor.one-click_description: Setting label in the Data Links inline editor: This option enables a mode where a single click on a data link directly triggers its action, simplifying the interaction.
data-links-inline-editor.one-click-enabled_description: Label in Data Links Inline Editor: Indicates that the 'one-click' functionality for data links is enabled. This means clicking a data link will directly trigger its action (e.g., navigate to a URL) without requiring further selection or confirmation.
data-links-inline-editor.title-not-provided_description: Status message in the Data Links inline editor: Informs the user that a title for a data link is missing or has not been specified.
data-links-inline-editor.url-not-provided_description: Validation message in the data links inline editor: Informs the user that the URL field, which is required for a data link, has not been provided.

data-source-basic-auth-settings.user-label_description: Label in Data Source Basic Auth Settings: Identifies the input field where a user enters their username for basic authentication.
data-source-basic-auth-settings.user-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in Data Source Basic Authentication Settings: Guides the user to enter their username in the corresponding input field.

data-source-http-proxy-settings.oauth-identity-label_description: Label for a checkbox/toggle in the Data Source HTTP Proxy Settings. This option controls whether the user's OAuth identity is forwarded when making HTTP requests through a proxy.
data-source-http-proxy-settings.oauth-identity-tooltip: Forward the user's upstream OAuth identity to the data source (Their access token gets passed along).
data-source-http-proxy-settings.skip-tls-verify-label_description: Label in Data Source HTTP Proxy Settings: This label describes an option that, when enabled, bypasses the verification of TLS certificates for HTTP proxy connections. This setting is typically used for development or testing and should be enabled with caution as it bypasses a security check.
data-source-http-proxy-settings.ts-client-auth-label_description: Label in the Data Source HTTP Proxy Settings: This indicates a setting to enable or configure TLS client authentication for connections made through an HTTP proxy.
data-source-http-proxy-settings.with-ca-cert-label_description: Checkbox label in Data Source HTTP Proxy Settings: This option allows the user to specify whether a Custom Certificate Authority (CA) certificate should be used for the HTTP proxy connection. It is typically checked to enable secure communication with a proxy using a non-standard CA.
data-source-http-proxy-settings.with-ca-cert-tooltip: Needed for verifying self-signed TLS Certs
data-source-http-proxy-settings.with-ca-cert-tooltip_description: Tooltip for the 'With CA Cert' option in the data source HTTP proxy settings: This explains that a CA certificate is required for verifying self-signed TLS certificates when connecting to a data source or proxy.

data-source-http-settings.access-help_description: Link in HTTP data source settings: Provides access to help documentation or guidance specifically about configuring HTTP data source access.
data-source-http-settings.access-help-details: Access mode controls how requests to the data source will be handled. Server should be the preferred way if nothing else is stated.
data-source-http-settings.access-help-title_description: Link label in the HTTP Data Source settings: Provides users with a clickable option to access help or documentation related to configuring HTTP data sources.
data-source-http-settings.access-label_description: Label in HTTP Data Source Settings: This label introduces a configuration option that determines the access method or mode for the data source, such as direct or via a proxy.
data-source-http-settings.allowed-cookies_description: Setting label in Data Source HTTP Settings: Specifies which cookies are permitted to be sent along with HTTP requests made by the data source.
data-source-http-settings.allowed-cookies-description: Grafana proxy deletes forwarded cookies by default. Specify cookies by name that should be forwarded to the data source.
data-source-http-settings.auth_description: Label in the HTTP data source settings panel: Refers to the authentication configuration section or settings for the data source. This is an abbreviation for 'Authentication'.
data-source-http-settings.azure-auth-label_description: Label in the Data Source HTTP Settings configuration: It introduces or identifies the settings related to Azure authentication. This option is used when configuring a data source that needs to authenticate with Azure services.
data-source-http-settings.azure-auth-tooltip: Use Azure authentication for Azure endpoint.
data-source-http-settings.azure-auth-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the data source's HTTP settings: Explains that Azure authentication should be used when connecting to an Azure endpoint.
data-source-http-settings.basic-auth_description: Section header in the HTTP data source settings configuration: Designates the input fields where users can provide basic authentication credentials. This typically includes fields for username and password.
data-source-http-settings.basic-auth-label_description: Label in the HTTP data source settings: This identifies the option or section for configuring Basic Authentication for the data source.
data-source-http-settings.browser-mode-description: All requests will be made from the browser directly to the data source and may be subject to Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) requirements. The URL needs to be accessible from the browser if you select this access mode.
data-source-http-settings.browser-mode-label_description: Label in Data Source HTTP Settings: This label identifies an option or mode for an HTTP data source where the user's browser directly makes the requests. This is typically an alternative to a server-side proxy mode.
data-source-http-settings.browser-mode-title_description: Label in the data source HTTP settings configuration: This label introduces the setting for configuring how a data source is accessed when queries originate from the browser. It relates to options like direct browser access versus server-side proxying.
data-source-http-settings.default-url-tooltip: Specify a complete HTTP URL (for example http://your_server:8080)
data-source-http-settings.default-url-tooltip_description: Tooltip for the 'Default URL' input field in the HTTP Data Source settings: It provides guidance and an example for specifying a complete HTTP URL for the data source.
data-source-http-settings.direct-url-tooltip: Your access method is <1>Browser</1>, this means the URL needs to be accessible from the browser.
data-source-http-settings.direct-url-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the HTTP Data Source Settings: Clarifies that the configured URL must be directly accessible from the user's browser when the 'Browser' access method is selected for the data source.
data-source-http-settings.heading: HTTP
data-source-http-settings.invalid-url-error_description: Error message in Data Source HTTP Settings: Informs the user that the URL entered for an HTTP-based data source is not valid.
data-source-http-settings.proxy-url-tooltip: Your access method is <1>Server</1>, this means the URL needs to be accessible from the grafana backend/server.
data-source-http-settings.server-mode-description: All requests will be made from the browser to Grafana backend/server which in turn will forward the requests to the data source and by that circumvent possible Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) requirements. The URL needs to be accessible from the grafana backend/server if you select this access mode.
data-source-http-settings.server-mode-label_description: Label in HTTP Data Source Settings: This label indicates that the "Server" connection mode is the default choice for proxying HTTP requests to the data source.
data-source-http-settings.server-mode-title_description: Label in the data source HTTP settings: Introduces the configuration option for the server access mode. This setting determines whether requests to the data source's server are proxied through the Grafana server (default) or made directly from the user's browser.
data-source-http-settings.sigv4-auth-label_description: Label in Data Source HTTP Settings: Identifies the SigV4 authentication method option for configuring HTTP data source connections.
data-source-http-settings.timeout-description_description: Description text in the HTTP Data Source Settings section: Clarifies the meaning of the HTTP request timeout setting, which specifies how long an HTTP request will wait before timing out.
data-source-http-settings.timeout-label_description: Label in the HTTP settings for a data source: Indicates the maximum duration the system will wait for a response from the data source before an operation is aborted.
data-source-http-settings.timeout-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the HTTP settings section of a data source configuration: Indicates the unit (seconds) for the HTTP request timeout value. This guides the user on the expected input format for the timeout duration.
data-source-http-settings.with-credentials-label_description: Toggle Label in the HTTP data source settings screen: This option allows users to enable or disable sending authentication credentials with HTTP requests made by the data source.
data-source-http-settings.with-credentials-tooltip: Whether credentials such as cookies or auth headers should be sent with cross-site requests.
data-source-http-settings.with-credentials-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the HTTP settings section of a data source configuration: Explains the option that controls whether credentials (such as cookies or authentication headers) are sent with cross-site requests.

data-source-settings.alerting-settings-heading_description: Heading in Data Source Settings: Labels the section where users configure alert-related settings for the specific data source.
data-source-settings.alerting-settings-label: Manage alert rules in Alerting UI
data-source-settings.alerting-settings-label_description: Text label in the data source settings section: Informs the user that alert rules related to this data source are managed in the dedicated Alerting UI. This text often appears next to a link or button that navigates to the Alerting UI.
data-source-settings.alerting-settings-tooltip: Manage alert rules for this data source. To manage other alerting resources, add an Alertmanager data source.
data-source-settings.cert-key-reset_description: Button in Data Source Settings: Used to reset the certificate and/or key configurations for a data source.
data-source-settings.custom-headers-add_description: Button in Data Source Settings: Allows users to add a new custom HTTP header for configuring data source connections.
data-source-settings.custom-headers-header_description: Label in the Custom Headers section of Data Source Settings: Prompts the user to enter the name of a custom HTTP header. This is used to define the key part of a header-value pair.
data-source-settings.custom-headers-header-placeholder_descri: Placeholder text for an input field in the Data Source Settings screen: Guides the user to enter the value for a custom HTTP header. This value is part of a custom header sent with data source requests.
data-source-settings.custom-headers-header-remove_description: Button in Data Source Settings: Allows users to remove a custom HTTP header from the list of headers configured for a data source.
data-source-settings.custom-headers-header-value_description: Input field label in the data source settings, under custom HTTP headers. It prompts the user to enter the value for a custom HTTP header.
data-source-settings.custom-headers-title_description: Title in Data Source Settings: This heading introduces a section where users can define custom HTTP headers. These headers are typically used for advanced authentication or specific API request requirements when configuring a data source.
data-source-settings.secure-socks-heading_description: Heading in Data Source Settings: Introduces the configuration section where users can set up a secure SOCKS proxy for their data source's network connection.
data-source-settings.secure-socks-label_description: Status label in Data Source Settings: Indicates that the Secure SOCKS feature is currently active or enabled.
data-source-settings.secure-socks-tooltip: Connect to this datasource via the secure socks proxy.
data-source-settings.secure-socks-tooltip_description: Tooltip in Data Source Settings: Explains that enabling the associated option allows the data source to connect via a secure SOCKS proxy. This tooltip clarifies the function of the "Secure SOCKS Proxy" setting.
data-source-settings.tls-certification-label_description: Label in Data Source Settings: Indicates the input field or section where the user provides a Certificate Authority (CA) certificate. This certificate is used for establishing secure TLS/SSL connections to the data source.
data-source-settings.tls-certification-placeholder_descriptio: Placeholder text in data source settings: This string shows a truncated prefix of a TLS certificate. Its purpose is to help users identify or confirm the certificate being used without displaying its full sensitive content.
data-source-settings.tls-client-certification-label_descripti: Label in Data Source settings for TLS client certification: This identifies the input field or section where the client certificate for secure connections is specified.
data-source-settings.tls-client-key-label_description: Label in Data Source Settings: This field identifies where the private key for client-side TLS authentication is entered or uploaded. It's essential for establishing a secure connection to the data source, paired with a client certificate.
data-source-settings.tls-client-key-placeholder_description: Informational text in the TLS Client Key section of Data Source Settings: It displays a masked representation of the configured private key by showing only its initial characters. This helps confirm the presence of a key without revealing the full secret.
data-source-settings.tls-heading_description: Heading in the Data Source Settings page: This heading introduces the section where users configure the details for TLS/SSL authentication to establish a secure connection to a data source.
data-source-settings.tls-server-name-label_description: Label in Data Source Settings: This label identifies an input field where the user specifies the TLS server name, often for Server Name Indication (SNI) during secure data source connections.
data-source-settings.tls-tooltip: TLS/SSL Certs are encrypted and stored in the Grafana database.
data-source-settings.tls-tooltip_description: Tooltip in Data Source Settings: Informs users that TLS/SSL certificates are encrypted and stored securely in the Grafana database. This provides transparency regarding certificate handling.

date-time-picker.apply_description: Button in the date-time picker: Used to confirm and apply the selected date and time range.
date-time-picker.calendar-icon-label_description: Accessible label for a calendar icon in the date/time picker component: This label identifies the control used to open the time selection interface.
date-time-picker.next-label_description: Button label in the date-time picker: Allows users to advance the displayed calendar view to the next month.
date-time-picker.previous-label_description: Button label in the date-time picker component: This label allows the user to navigate to the previous month. It is typically found next to an arrow icon, enabling a backward step in time.
date-time-picker.select-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a date/time picker component: Prompts the user to select a date and/or time from the available options.

month-options.label-april_description: Option label in the date-time picker's month options: Represents the month of April for user selection.
month-options.label-august_description: Month option label in Date/Time Pickers: Represents "August" as a selectable choice within a list of months.
month-options.label-december_description: Label in a date-time picker's month options: Identifies December as a selectable month.
month-options.label-february_description: Label in the month selection options of a date/time picker: Identifies the month of February for user selection.
month-options.label-january_description: Label in date-time picker month options: Represents the month 'January' as a selectable option for date input.
month-options.label-july_description: Month option in date-time picker components: Represents the month of July as a selectable option within month selection dropdowns or lists.
month-options.label-june_description: Label in month selection options within a date-time picker: Identifies the month of June as a selectable option.
month-options.label-march_description: Label in a date/time picker's month selection options: Identifies the month of March for user selection. This is a standard month name.
month-options.label-may_description: Label in Date Time Pickers' month options: Identifies the month of May for selection within a date or time range component. This label is part of a list of month options available to the user.
month-options.label-november_description: Label in date-time pickers: Represents November as an option for month selection.
month-options.label-october_description: Month option label in a date-time picker: Represents the month of October for user selection from a list of available months.
month-options.label-september_description: Month Option in Date/Time Pickers: Allows users to select September as a month.

quick-options.day-before-yesterday_description: A selectable option in the 'Quick options' section of a date/time picker: This allows users to quickly select the day before yesterday as a relative time range. It simplifies choosing common historical periods for data display.
quick-options.last-1-hour_description: Menu option in the date-time picker's quick options list: Allows the user to set the dashboard's displayed time range to the past hour.
quick-options.last-1-year_description: Quick time range option in the quick selection menu of a date and time picker: This option allows users to set the displayed data's time frame to the preceding 12 months. It refers to a rolling period (e.g., from today back one year) rather than a specific calendar year.
quick-options.last-12-hours_description: A quick time range option within a date-time picker component: It allows users to rapidly select the last 12 hours as the data display period.
quick-options.last-15-mins_description: Quick time range option in a date and time picker's 'quick options' menu: Allows users to quickly select a predefined relative time range to display data from the past 15 minutes. This is a common shortcut for filtering dashboard data.
quick-options.last-2-days_description: Quick option in a date/time range picker: Allows users to quickly select a predefined time range, setting the data display period to the last two days relative to the current time.
quick-options.last-2-years_description: Option label in the 'Quick Options' section of a date/time picker: This label allows users to quickly select a predefined time range that displays data from the past two years.
quick-options.last-24-hours_description: A quick time range option in a date and time picker: Enables users to select the previous 24 hours as the data display period for dashboards or panels.
quick-options.last-3-hours_description: Quick-select option in a date/time picker: This option allows users to quickly set the displayed time range for a dashboard or panel to the last three hours.
quick-options.last-30-days_description: Quick option in a date range quick selection menu: This option allows users to quickly set the time frame for displayed dashboard data to the past 30 days. It is one of several predefined quick range selections.
quick-options.last-30-mins_description: Quick option in the date-time picker component: This option allows users to quickly select a predefined time range for data display, specifically showing data from the last 30 minutes relative to the current time.
quick-options.last-5-mins_description: Quick option in a date-time picker's quick options menu: This option allows users to quickly set the data display time range to the most recent 5 minutes.
quick-options.last-5-years_description: Option in the quick options menu of a date-time picker: Allows users to quickly select a relative time range for data, spanning the last five years from the current moment.
quick-options.last-6-hours_description: Quick option button in a date-time picker's quick options menu: Allows users to quickly set the dashboard's time range to the last 6 hours.
quick-options.last-6-months_description: Quick option in a date-time picker: Allows users to select a predefined time range covering the last six months. This option is part of a list of common, quick time range selections.
quick-options.last-7-days_description: Label for an option in the 'Quick options' section of a date-time picker: This option allows users to quickly set the time range for displayed data to the last 7 days.
quick-options.last-90-days_description: Option in the quick options menu within a date/time picker: This option allows users to quickly select the time range for displayed data to be the last 90 days from the current moment.
quick-options.previous-fiscal-quarter_description: Quick selection option in a date-time picker's quick options menu: This option allows users to set the dashboard's time range to the immediately preceding fiscal quarter.
quick-options.previous-fiscal-year_description: An option in a date-time picker's quick options menu: This sets the time range to the immediately preceding fiscal year. This refers to the financial year, not a standard calendar year.
quick-options.previous-month_description: Option in the quick options section of a date-time picker: Allows users to quickly select the previous month as a time range.
quick-options.previous-week_description: Option label in a quick options menu within a date and time picker: Allows users to quickly set the displayed time range to cover the previous week, relative to the current date.
quick-options.previous-year_description: Quick option in the quick options section of a date-time picker: This option allows users to quickly set the selected time range to the entire previous calendar year.
quick-options.this-day-last-week_description: Date option in a quick options menu within a date-time picker: Allows users to quickly select the entire day from exactly one week ago.
quick-options.this-fiscal-quarter_description: Selectable option in the Quick options section of a date-time picker: Provides a predefined date range preset for setting the displayed data to cover the current fiscal quarter.
quick-options.this-fiscal-quarter-so-far_description: Date range option in a Date/Time picker's 'Quick options' section: Sets the displayed data's time range to span from the beginning of the current fiscal quarter up to the present moment.
quick-options.this-fiscal-year_description: Option in a date/time picker's quick options menu: Allows users to select the current fiscal year as a predefined time range. This option provides a quick selection for organizational financial periods.
quick-options.this-fiscal-year-so-far_description: Quick option in a date range picker: This preset allows users to select a time range covering the period from the beginning of the current fiscal year up to the present moment for data display.
quick-options.this-month_description: Option label in a date/time range picker's 'Quick options' menu: This option allows users to quickly select the current month as the time range for the displayed data.
quick-options.this-month-so-far_description: Quick Option in the Date/Time Picker's "Quick Options" section: This option sets the data display period from the beginning of the current calendar month until the current time.
quick-options.this-week_description: Quick option label in a date-time picker's quick options section: This label allows users to instantly set the current week as the selected time range.
quick-options.this-week-so-far_description: A predefined time range option in the Quick Options section of a date/time picker: It allows users to select data from the beginning of the current calendar week up to the present moment.
quick-options.this-year_description: Quick option in a date-time picker's quick options menu: Allows users to quickly set the time range to the entire current calendar year.
quick-options.this-year-so-far_description: Option in a Date/Time Picker's quick options menu: This option allows users to set the selected time range from the beginning of the current calendar year up to the current date and time.
quick-options.today_description: Option in the quick options menu of the date-time picker: Allows users to quickly set the selected date to the current day.
quick-options.today-so-far_description: A quick option in a date-time picker: It sets the time range to cover the current day from its start up to the present moment.
quick-options.yesterday_description: Option in a date/time picker's quick options menu: Allows users to quickly select the time range covering the entire previous day.

error-boundary.title: An unexpected error happened
error-boundary.title_description: Title in the Error Boundary component: Informs the user that an unforeseen error has occurred. This message is displayed when a UI component encounters an unhandled exception.

feature-badge.experimental_description: Text in a feature badge: Informs users that the associated feature or functionality is experimental, indicating it may not be stable or fully released.
feature-badge.new_description: Text label in a feature badge: Signals that the associated feature or UI element is new. This helps users quickly identify recently added functionalities.
feature-badge.preview_description: Badge text in a feature badge: Indicates that the associated feature is currently in a preview or experimental state, meaning it may not be fully released or stable.
feature-badge.private-preview_description: Badge in a feature's UI element: Indicates that the associated feature is currently in a private, early access, or limited release phase. This informs users that the feature might not be generally available.

field-link-list.external-links-heading_description: Heading in the field link list component: It labels a section containing links that direct users to external websites or resources.

field-name-by-regex-matcher.input-placeholder_description: Input field placeholder in the 'Field name by regex matcher' component: Prompts the user to enter a regular expression for matching field names.

field-value-matcher.operator-label_description: Label in the field value matcher component: Prompts the user to select a comparison operator (e.g., 'equals', 'greater than') for evaluating field values.
field-value-matcher.select-field-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a dropdown or selection input within the Field Value Matcher configuration: Prompts the user to select a method for aggregating or transforming field values.

file-dropzone.cancel-upload_description: Button in the file dropzone component: Allows the user to cancel an ongoing file upload process.
file-dropzone.error-title_description: Error Title in the File Dropzone component: Informs the user that a file upload operation has failed.
file-dropzone.file-too-large_description: Error message in the file dropzone component: Informs the user that the file they are attempting to upload or drop exceeds the maximum allowed size. The `{{size}}` placeholder indicates the specific size limit.
file-dropzone.item-retry_description: Button in a file dropzone: Allows a user to reattempt a failed operation (e.g., upload or processing) for a specific item within the dropzone.

file-upload.file-name_description: Label in a file upload component: Identifies the name of a file being handled within the upload process.

filter-input.clear_description: Button in a filter input field: Clears the text or selections currently entered into that specific filter.

interactive-table.aria-label-collapse-all_description: An ARIA label for a button or icon in an interactive table: This label describes a control that collapses all currently expanded rows within the table, primarily for accessibility purposes.
interactive-table.aria-label-expand-all_description: ARIA label in an interactive table: This label describes a control that, when activated, expands all expandable rows within the table. It is used for accessibility purposes.
interactive-table.expand-row-tooltip_description: Tooltip in an interactive table: Informs the user that clicking the associated element will toggle the expanded state of a table row.
interactive-table.tooltip-collapse-all_description: Tooltip in an interactive table: Describes the action of collapsing all expandable rows within the table. This tooltip appears when hovering over a UI element that performs this bulk action.
interactive-table.tooltip-expand-all_description: Tooltip in an interactive table: Describes the action of expanding all collapsible rows in the table.

matchers-ui.description-field-name-by-regex-matcher: Set properties for fields with names matching a regex
matchers-ui.description-field-name-by-regex-matcher_: Description in the Matchers UI's field name matcher configuration: Explains that this matcher allows setting properties for fields whose names match a regular expression.
matchers-ui.description-fields-by-query: Set properties for fields from a specific query
matchers-ui.description-fields-by-query_description: Instruction in the Matchers UI configuration panel within BMC Helix Dashboards: This text guides users on how to set specific properties for data fields originating from a particular query. It clarifies the customization options available for field processing or display.
matchers-ui.description-fields-with-name: Set properties for a specific field
matchers-ui.description-fields-with-name_description: Description text in the Matchers UI: This text explains that the current section is for configuring properties for a specific data field. It appears within a panel or section dedicated to field-level settings or transformations.
matchers-ui.description-fields-with-type: Set properties for fields of a specific type (number, string, boolean)
matchers-ui.description-fields-with-type_description: Description in Matchers UI: This text explains that users can configure properties for data fields by specifying their type (number, string, boolean). This allows for type-specific adjustments within the matching or filtering logic.
matchers-ui.description-fields-with-value: Set properties for fields with reducer condition
matchers-ui.description-fields-with-value_descriptio: Label in the Matchers UI: This string describes an option or setting to configure properties for data fields that satisfy a specified 'reducer condition'. This typically refers to setting rules for fields that are subject to data aggregation or transformation.

field-name-picker.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the field name picker component within the matchers UI: Prompts the user to select a field. This text appears when no field has been chosen yet.

get-all-field-type-icon-options.label-boolean_description: Label in the Matchers UI's field type selection: Identifies 'Boolean' as a specific data type. This option is available for users to select when defining field properties or matching criteria.
get-all-field-type-icon-options.label-enum_description: Label in Field Type Icon Options for Matchers UI: This option represents the 'Enumeration' data type for a field. It is used to identify fields whose values are restricted to a predefined set.
get-all-field-type-icon-options.label-number_description: Label in the field type icon options of the Matchers UI: Identifies a data field as having a numerical data type for matching or filtering operations.
get-all-field-type-icon-options.label-other_description: Label in the list of field type icon options within the matchers UI: It serves as a generic or catch-all option for field types.
get-all-field-type-icon-options.label-string_description: Field type option in the Matchers UI: This label represents the 'String' data type, allowing users to select it when defining field-based matching criteria.
get-all-field-type-icon-options.label-time_description: Option label in the Matchers UI's field type selection: It identifies "Time" as a data type option for configuring matchers. This allows users to categorize fields that store time-based values.
get-all-field-type-icon-options.label-traces_description: Label in Matchers UI field type options: Represents the "Traces" field type, used for selecting or filtering data related to distributed traces within dashboards.

get-list-of-query-ref-ids.description_description: Display label in the Matchers UI when listing query reference IDs: This string summarizes the data frames, showing their total count and names. It helps users identify the data frames associated with the query reference IDs.
get-list-of-query-ref-ids.label: Query: {{refId}}
get-list-of-query-ref-ids.label_description: Label in the Query Matchers UI: This label identifies a specific query by its unique reference ID (`refId`). It is displayed when presenting or allowing selection of individual queries, such as in alert rule configurations or data linking.
get-list-of-query-ref-ids.label-missing-ref-id_description: A label in the query selection or matcher configuration UI: It informs the user that a specific query's reference ID (refId) is missing or undefined.

matchers-ui.name-field-name-by-regex-matcher_descrip: Filter option label in the Matchers UI: Indicates a configuration option for selecting or filtering fields whose names match a specified regular expression.
matchers-ui.name-fields-by-query_description: **Label** in the **Matchers UI section**: It indicates the data fields that are returned by a data query, making them available for selection or configuration within the matching rules.
matchers-ui.name-fields-with-name_description: Label in a matching or filtering UI: Indicates a section or option where users can specify a name to select or group fields.
matchers-ui.name-fields-with-type_description: Section title in Matchers UI: This title introduces a list or selection of fields available for defining matching criteria, presented along with their associated data types. It helps users understand and select the appropriate fields for their match expressions.
