matchers-ui.name-fields-with-value_description: Label in the Matchers UI: Identifies a filter criterion to select fields that contain any value, excluding empty or null fields.

menu-item.keyboard-shortcut-label_description: Label in a menu item: Identifies a keyboard shortcut associated with the menu item's action. This label informs users how to perform the action using the keyboard.

monaco.error-label: React Monaco Editor failed to load
monaco.error-label_description: Error message in the Monaco editor component: Informs the user that the React Monaco Editor component failed to load, preventing code editing functionality.
monaco.loading-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Monaco editor component: Informs the user that the editor is currently loading. This message is displayed temporarily while the editor initializes.

named-colors-palette.text-color-swatch_description: Label in the named colors palette: This identifies the color swatch or input control used to select or define the color of text. It allows users to set the text color for various UI elements.
named-colors-palette.transparent-swatch_description: Label for a color swatch in the named colors palette: Identifies the option to select a transparent color for an element or background.

page-toolbar.go-back_description: Button in the page toolbar: Allows users to navigate back to the previous page or view. It indicates that the action can also be triggered by pressing the Escape key.
page-toolbar.search-dashboard-name_description: Placeholder text in an input field in the page toolbar: Indicates where the user can type a dashboard name to search for it.
page-toolbar.search-links_description: Text for a button or menu item in the page toolbar: Enables users to search for and filter various types of links, including navigation or dashboard links.
page-toolbar.search-parent-folder_description: Placeholder text in the Page Toolbar: Guides the user to search for dashboards specifically within a designated parent folder. The `{{parent}}` variable will be replaced by the actual folder name.

pagination.next-page_description: Button or link in a pagination component: Navigates the user to the next page of results or data.
pagination.previous-page_description: Button in a pagination component: Navigates the user to the previous page of content, data, or results within a paginated view.

panel-chrome.aria-label-toggle-collapse_description: Accessibility label for a button in the header of a dashboard panel: It describes the action of toggling the panel's collapsed or expanded state.
panel-chrome.ariaLabel-panel-loading_description: ARIA label in the panel chrome: Describes the visual loading bar that appears while a dashboard panel is fetching data, providing an accessible indication for screen reader users.
panel-chrome.ariaLabel-panel-status_description: ARIA label in the panel chrome: Describes the current status or state of a dashboard panel, primarily for accessibility purposes.
panel-chrome.tooltip-cancel_description: Tooltip in Grafana panel chrome: Explains that clicking the associated button or icon will stop an ongoing data query.
panel-chrome.tooltip-cancel-loading_description: Tooltip in a dashboard panel's chrome: This text appears when hovering over an element allowing users to cancel an ongoing data query that is loading.
panel-chrome.tooltip-stop-streaming_description: Tooltip in Grafana panel controls: Explains the action of stopping a data streaming process displayed within the panel.
panel-chrome.tooltip-streaming_description: Tooltip label in a dashboard panel's header or controls: Indicates that the data within the panel is being streamed or refreshed continuously.

panel-menu.label: Menu for panel {{ title }}
panel-menu.label_description: Label for the panel header menu: This string provides an accessible description for the menu button or icon associated with a specific dashboard panel. It dynamically includes the panel's title for clear context.
panel-menu.title: Menu
panel-menu.title_description: Label for a menu button/icon in the panel header: Identifies the control used to access a dropdown menu containing options for the specific dashboard panel.

password-field.tooltip-hide_description: Tooltip for an icon in a password field: This tooltip appears when hovering over an icon that, when clicked, hides the visible characters of the entered password.
password-field.tooltip-show_description: Tooltip in a password field: This text is displayed when hovering over the icon or button that allows the user to temporarily reveal the entered password characters.

range-slider.drag-handle-aria-label: Use arrow keys to change the value
range-slider.drag-handle-aria-label_description: Drag handle in a range slider component: This accessibility label provides instructions for users to change the value of the drag handle using arrow keys. It assists with keyboard navigation for the range slider.

row-expander.aria-label-expand_description: Accessibility label for an expand control in a table or list row: It indicates to screen readers that activating this control will expand the row to reveal additional content.
row-expander.collapse_description: Button in the row expander component: Allows users to hide the details or sub-items of an expanded table row. This action is available when a row is currently expanded.
row-expander.expand_description: Button or icon in a table or list row: Allows the user to expand a collapsed row to reveal hidden details or nested content.

row-expander-ng.aria-label-collapse_description: ARIA label for a button or icon in a row expander component: Describes the action of collapsing an expanded row for screen reader users.

secret-form-field.reset_description: Button in a secret form field: Clears the currently entered or displayed value of the secret, often allowing for re-entry or regeneration.

segment-async.error_description: Error message in an asynchronous option selector: displayed when the system fails to load the available options for a user selection, such as in a dropdown or filter.
segment-async.no-options_description: Status message in an asynchronous segment selector: Informs the user when no options are found for selection. This message is displayed when the component fails to load or filter any available choices.

select.clear-value_description: Icon or button in a select component: Clears the currently selected value(s) from the selection field.
select.default-create-label_description: Instructional label in a multi-select or tag input field: This text guides the user to press Enter to add a custom or newly typed item to the selection.
select.empty-options_description: **Select input** in **a Grafana UI dropdown component**: **Informs the user that no options are available for selection.** This occurs when the list of choices for the input is empty.
select.menu-label_description: Menu label in a select/dropdown component: Labels the dropdown menu from which users can choose options.
select.no-options-label_description: Informational message in a dropdown or selection component: Informs the user when no selectable options are currently available to choose from. This message appears when a search or filter yields no results for the selection.
select.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a select component in the Grafana UI: Prompts the user to make a selection from the available options.

series-color-picker-popover.y-axis-usage_description: Option in Series Color Picker Popover: This option allows users to assign the selected data series to be displayed on the right y-axis of a visualization.

slider.drag-handle-aria-label: Use arrow keys to change the value
slider.drag-handle-aria-label_description: Drag handle in a Slider component: This ARIA label provides instructions for keyboard users on how to adjust the slider's value using arrow keys.

spinner.aria-label_description: ARIA label in a loading spinner: Indicates to assistive technologies (like screen readers) that content is being loaded. This text is not visually displayed.

stacking-builder.group_description: Label or header in the Stacking Builder UI: Designates a setting or configuration for grouping data series or values, especially for creating stacked visualizations or aggregating data.
stacking-builder.group-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the Stacking Builder UI: This tooltip clarifies that the associated input field is for entering the name of a stacking group.

table.cell-filter-on_description: Context menu item in a table cell: Allows the user to apply a filter to the table based on the specific value within that cell.
table.cell-filter-out_description: Context menu item in a table cell: Allows the user to exclude the specific value of that cell from the table's displayed data.
table.cell-inspect_description: Action within a table cell: Allows users to inspect or view detailed information about the value displayed in that cell.
table.cell-inspect-tooltip_description: Tooltip text in a table cell: This text provides an option for users to inspect or view the detailed value of the cell when interacting with it.
table.copy_description: Button or menu item in a table: Allows users to copy selected table data to the system clipboard for pasting elsewhere.
table.csv-counts_description: Information label in a table component: Displays the total number of rows and columns present in the table data.
table.filter-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a table component's filter input field: Guides users to type in values to filter the table data.
table.filter-popup-aria-label-match-case_descr: Checkbox option in a filter popup for a table: Allows users to enable or disable case-sensitive filtering. This is an ARIA label to improve accessibility for this option.
table.filter-popup-clear_description: Button in a filter popup within a table: Clears all applied filters in that specific filter popup.
table.filter-popup-heading_description: **Heading** in the **filter popup for a table component**: It labels the section where users can select values to filter the table data.
table.filter-popup-input-placeholder_descripti: Placeholder text in a table's filter input field: Prompts the user to enter values to filter the displayed table data.
table.filter-popup-match-case_description: Checkbox in the table filter pop-up: Controls whether the filter operation is case-sensitive. When enabled, the filter will only match values with identical casing.

reducer.count_description: Table aggregation option in Table Footer: This option allows users to select 'Count' to display the total number of entries or rows for a specific table column. It appears among other aggregation functions available in the table's summary footer.

table.inspect-drawer-title_description: Title in an inspect drawer within a table: This title indicates that the drawer displays detailed information for a selected value from the table.

nested-table.no-data_description: Status message in a nested table: Informs the user that no data is available to be displayed within the nested table.

table.no-values-label_description: Label in a table component: This text is displayed when the table has no data or values to show.
table.pagination-summary_description: Pagination summary text in a table footer: Indicates the range of rows currently displayed on the page and the total number of rows in the table.

sparkline.no-data_description: Text label in a sparkline chart within a table: Indicates that no data is available to display for the sparkline.

tags.list-label_description: Label in a list or section displaying tags: Identifies the collection of tags used for organizing and filtering dashboards or panels.

tags-input.placeholder-new-tag_description: Placeholder text in a tags input field: It prompts the user to enter a new tag and instructs them to press the Enter key to add it to a list of tags.
tags-input.remove_description: Action label in the tags input component: Informs the user about the action of removing a specific tag. The `{{name}}` placeholder displays the name of the tag being removed.

time-sync-button.aria-label-sync_description: Aria-label for a button in the time synchronization controls: This button allows users to synchronize time ranges across dashboard panels. It provides an accessibility description for screen readers.
time-sync-button.aria-label-unsync_description: Aria-label for a button in the Grafana UI's time synchronization controls: This button, when clicked, desynchronizes the time ranges across various dashboard panels. It is used for accessibility purposes.

toolbar-button-row.show-more_description: Button in a toolbar button row: This button allows users to expand a truncated list or set of options to show additional items that are currently hidden.

axis-placement.description-auto: First field on the left, everything else on the right
axis-placement.description-auto_description: Description for the 'Auto' axis placement option in graph field settings: It explains that the first data field will be placed on the left Y-axis, and all subsequent fields on the right Y-axis.
axis-placement.label-auto_description: A selection option in the graph configuration settings for axis placement: Sets the axis placement to be automatically determined by the system.
axis-placement.label-hidden_description: Option value in axis placement settings within graph field options: It signifies that the chosen axis or its associated labels will not be displayed on the graph.
axis-placement.label-left_description: Selection label in axis placement settings within graph configuration: Indicates the left side as the desired position for an axis on a graph.
axis-placement.label-right_description: Option label in the axis placement configuration for graph fields: Represents the choice to position an axis on the right side of the graph.

bar-alignment.description-after_description: Option label in Bar alignment settings for u-Plot graphs: This option specifies that a bar should be positioned *after* its corresponding data point or tick mark. It defines one of the possible alignment choices for graph bars.
bar-alignment.description-before_description: Option label in the bar alignment settings for a u-plot graph field: This option positions a bar to start before its associated data point or tick on the axis.
bar-alignment.description-center_description: Label in bar alignment settings for graph field options: This option allows users to align the bars in a u-plot graph to the center.

draw-style.label-bars_description: Option label in the graph field options for draw style: Selects 'Bars' as the visualization type for a specific data series on a graph.
draw-style.label-lines_description: Option label in drawing style settings for graph fields: This option allows users to display graph data using connecting lines.
draw-style.label-points_description: Option in draw-style settings for graph field options in a uPlot visualization: Allows users to select 'Points' as a visual rendering style for data series on a graph.

fill-gradient.description-hue_description: Description in the fill gradient configuration options for a graph panel: This describes a subtle fill gradient that features a small or light change in color hue. It indicates a gentle rather than a dramatic color transition across the filled area.
fill-gradient.description-opacity_description: **Setting** within **the fill gradient configuration for a graph or chart field**: **Allows the user to activate a visual effect where the filled area of the graph transitions in opacity, creating a gradient effect.**
fill-gradient.description-scheme: Use color scheme to define gradient
fill-gradient.description-scheme_description: Checkbox label in the fill gradient configuration settings for u-plot graphs: Allows users to enable or disable the use of the dashboard's chosen color scheme to automatically define the gradient's colors.
fill-gradient.label-hue_description: Label in the fill gradient configuration settings for u-plot graphs: Denotes the hue component of a color used in gradient fills.
fill-gradient.label-none_description: Option label in the 'Fill Gradient' setting for graph field options: This allows users to select no gradient for the graph's fill effect.
fill-gradient.label-opacity_description: Setting in the gradient fill options for a graph field: Controls the transparency level of a label associated with the gradient fill.
fill-gradient.label-scheme_description: Label in the 'Fill Gradient' configuration section for graph field options: This allows users to select a predefined color or gradient scheme to apply to the fill. The French translation "Palette" suggests it refers to a color palette.

line-interpolation.description-linear_description: Value in graph field configuration's line interpolation options: Represents the "Linear" method for drawing lines between data points in a graph, connecting them with straight segments.
line-interpolation.description-smooth_description: Label in Line Interpolation options within graph configuration: Indicates a drawing style where lines between data points are rendered with a smooth curve on a graph.
line-interpolation.description-step-after_description: Option label in the line interpolation settings for a uPlot graph's field options: This defines a line interpolation method where the line steps horizontally *after* each data point. It's used to create a stair-step graph where the step occurs at the end of the interval.
line-interpolation.description-step-before_description: Line interpolation option in the graph field configuration for u-plot: This option specifies a step-style line interpolation where the vertical change occurs before the data point. It determines the visual representation of stepped lines on a chart.

show-points.description-auto: Show points when the density is low
show-points.description-auto_description: Description for the 'Show points' graph field option in the uPlot configuration: This text clarifies that points are automatically displayed on the graph only when data density is low.
show-points.label-always_description: Selection option in the 'Show Points' setting for graph field options: This option ensures that data points are always visible on the graph, regardless of zoom level or data density.
show-points.label-auto_description: Option label in graph field options under the 'Show points' configuration for u-plot graphs: This value represents the 'Auto' setting for automatically determining when to display data points.
show-points.label-never_description: An option label in graph visualization settings: This choice configures the 'Show Points' setting to ensure data points are never displayed on the graph.

stacking.label-100_description: Label in graph stacking configuration: This option sets the stacking mode to '100%', displaying series as proportions of the total.
stacking.label-normal_description: Option Label in Graph Stacking Configuration: This option sets the graph series stacking mode to 'Normal', accumulating values on top of each other.
stacking.label-off_description: Option label in the graph field stacking configuration: Selects the option to disable data series stacking on a graph.

thresholds-display-mode.label-dashed-lines_description: Option label in the Thresholds Display Mode settings for graph field options: This option allows users to visualize thresholds on a u-plot graph as dashed lines.
thresholds-display-mode.label-filled-regions_description: Option label in graph threshold display mode settings: This label describes a method for visualizing thresholds on a graph by filling the regions that meet the threshold condition.
thresholds-display-mode.label-filled-regions-and-dashed-lines_de: Option label in the Thresholds display mode settings: This option allows users to visualize graph thresholds as filled regions and dashed lines.
thresholds-display-mode.label-filled-regions-and-lines_descripti: Label in Graph Threshold Display Mode settings: Describes a visualization option where graph thresholds are displayed with both the regions between the threshold lines filled and the threshold lines themselves visible.
thresholds-display-mode.label-lines_description: Option in the `Thresholds display mode` setting within graph field options: This option allows users to configure thresholds to be displayed as lines on the graph.
thresholds-display-mode.label-off_description: Option label in the Thresholds Display Mode setting for graph field options: This selection disables the display of thresholds on a graph.

unit-picker.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the unit picker component: This text appears in an input or dropdown field, instructing the user to choose a unit from the available options.

user-icon.active-text_description: Status text near a user icon in the Grafana UI: It indicates that the associated user has been active within the last 15 minutes, serving as a real-time presence or activity indicator.
user-icon.label: {{name}} icon
user-icon.label_description: Accessible label in the user icon component: Provides a descriptive label for the icon, indicating which user it represents. The `{{name}}` placeholder will be replaced with the user's name.

users-indicator.container-label_description: Container in the Users Indicator component: This is the main visual container for the elements that display user-related indicators.

value-pill.remove-button_description: Button in a Value Pill component: Allows users to remove the item or value displayed within that pill. The `{{children}}` placeholder represents the specific content of the pill being removed.

viz-legend.right-axis-indicator_description: Indicator in Visualization Legend: This text clarifies that a specific data series uses the right Y-axis on the chart. It typically appears in parentheses next to the series name in the legend.

viz-tooltip.actions-confirmation-input-placeholder: Are you sure you want to {{ actionTitle }}?
viz-tooltip.actions-confirmation-input-placeholder_d: Confirmation prompt in a visualization tooltip: This message asks the user to confirm a specific action (indicated by `actionTitle`) before it is executed, ensuring intentional user interaction.
viz-tooltip.actions-confirmation-label_description: Label in a visualization tooltip: This label introduces or is the content of a confirmation message. It is displayed after a user performs an action from within the tooltip.
viz-tooltip.actions-confirmation-message: Provide a descriptive prompt to confirm or cancel the action.
viz-tooltip.actions-confirmation-message_description: Instructional text for an input field in a confirmation dialog within a visualization tooltip: It guides the user to provide a descriptive message to confirm or cancel a specific action. This often applies to actions requiring explicit textual acknowledgment before proceeding.
viz-tooltip.footer-add-annotation_description: Button in the visualization tooltip footer: Allows users to add a new annotation to the dashboard, typically at the time or data point indicated by the hovered element.
viz-tooltip.footer-click-to-action_description: Clickable text in the footer of a visualization tooltip: Prompts the user to click to perform a specific action related to the visualized data. The `{{actionTitle}}` placeholder is replaced with the name of the action.
viz-tooltip.footer-click-to-navigate_description: Instructional text in the footer of a visualization tooltip: Informs the user that clicking this element will open the linked item specified by `{{linkTitle}}`. This text prompts interaction to navigate to a related resource.
viz-tooltip.footer-filter-for-value_description: Clickable action in the footer of a visualization tooltip: This allows users to filter the dashboard or current visualization by the specific data `{{value}}` displayed. The `{{value}}` placeholder is dynamically replaced with the actual data point from the visualization.
viz-tooltip.timestamp_description: Label in a visualization tooltip: It identifies the time associated with the data point currently being hovered over in a visualization.

week-start-picker.weekStarts-label-default_description: Option label in the week start picker: Represents the option to use the system's or application's default setting for the start day of the week, rather than a specific day.
week-start-picker.weekStarts-label-monday_description: Label in the week start day picker: Identifies "Monday" as a selectable option for the first day of the week.
week-start-picker.weekStarts-label-saturday_description: Option label in the week start picker: Represents "Saturday" as a selectable day for when the week should start.
week-start-picker.weekStarts-label-sunday_description: Option label in the Week Start Picker component: Represents Sunday as a selectable choice for the beginning of the week.

container.content: Rendering too many series in a single panel may impact performance and make data harder to read. Consider refining your queries.
container.show-all-series_description: Button in Graph Container: This button allows users to display all data series within a graph when some are currently hidden or truncated. The `{{length}}` placeholder will be replaced by the total number of series available.
container.show-only-series_description: Status message in a graph container: Informs the user that the number of time series displayed on the graph is currently limited to a specific maximum. The `{{MAX_NUMBER_OF_TIME_SERIES}}` placeholder represents this limit.
container.title: Graph
container.title_description: Default title for a graph container in a dashboard: Identifies the panel's content as a graph visualization.

heatmap.category-cell-display_description: Setting label in heatmap display configuration: This label introduces options for customizing the visual representation and content of individual cells within a heatmap.
heatmap.category-colors_description: Section Title in the Heatmap configuration panel: This title introduces the options for customizing the colors displayed in the heatmap.
heatmap.category-exemplars_description: Label in a Heatmap: Identifies specific examples or representative data points associated with a category displayed on the heatmap. These exemplars provide detailed instances for aggregated metrics.
heatmap.category-heatmap_description: Chart type name in visualization options: Identifies the Heatmap chart, a data visualization technique that displays magnitude using colors.
heatmap.category-legend_description: Legend label in a heatmap visualization: Identifying the key that explains the color mapping for the heatmap data.
heatmap.category-tooltip_description: Label in heatmap visualization settings: This string is a label for a setting or section dedicated to configuring the display and content of tooltips, especially for categories, within a heatmap visualization.
heatmap.category-y-axis_description: Label in Heatmap panel settings: Identifies the vertical Y-axis of the heatmap visualization. This axis is specifically used for displaying or configuring categorical data.

mode-options.label-opacity_description: Label for a setting in the Heatmap Mode Options panel: Controls the transparency of labels displayed on the heatmap.
mode-options.label-scheme_description: Label in the Mode Options section of a heatmap configuration: This allows users to select the scheme for categorizing or visually representing data on the heatmap.

heatmap.name-axis-label_description: Configuration option label in the heatmap panel's configuration settings: It identifies the field where users can define the text to be displayed as an axis label on the heatmap.
heatmap.name-axis-width_description: Configuration option in Heatmap panel settings: Defines the visual width of an axis. This typically controls the space allocated for the axis labels and ticks.
heatmap.name-calculate-from-data_description: Option in Heatmap panel configuration: This setting allows a specific property or value, such as a dimension's label or name, to be dynamically calculated and derived directly from the underlying data for the heatmap.
heatmap.name-cell-gap_description: Setting in Heatmap configuration: Defines the visual spacing between individual cells in a heatmap chart. This adjusts the appearance and density of the grid.
heatmap.name-color_description: Label in heatmap configuration: Identifies the control for selecting a color used within the heatmap.
heatmap.name-decimals_description: Label in heatmap configuration settings: Specifies the number of decimal places to display for values shown on the heatmap. This controls the precision of the visualized data.
heatmap.name-end-color-at-value_description: Setting label in Heatmap configuration settings: This label indicates where users can specify the maximum data value that corresponds to the end of the color scale. It allows users to define the upper bound for the color mapping used in the heatmap visualization.
heatmap.name-exponent_description: Label in Heatmap configuration: This text identifies a field where a numerical exponent can be entered to adjust data scaling or color mapping for the heatmap visualization.
heatmap.name-hide-cells-gt_description: Option label in Heatmap Visualization Settings: This option, typically accompanied by an input field, allows users to hide cells in the heatmap visualization whose numerical values are greater than or equal to a user-defined threshold.
heatmap.name-hide-cells-lt_description: Label in the heatmap configuration options: This string introduces a setting that allows users to hide data cells in the heatmap where their value is less than or equal to a user-defined number.
heatmap.name-max-height_description: Label in Heatmap display options: Allows users to set the maximum vertical dimension for the heatmap panel. This ensures the heatmap does not exceed a specified height on the dashboard.
heatmap.name-max-value_description: Label for an input field in heatmap configuration: Specifies the maximum data value for the heatmap's color scale or data range.
heatmap.name-max-width_description: Label in Heatmap panel configuration: Specifies the maximum display width for names within the heatmap, such as series names or axis labels.
heatmap.name-min-value_description: Label in the heatmap configuration settings: Specifies the lower bound for the data range displayed on the heatmap.
heatmap.name-mode_description: Label in heatmap configuration settings: Allows selecting a specific operational or display mode for the heatmap, influencing how data is processed, visualized, or interacted with.
heatmap.name-placement_description: Option in Heatmap panel settings: Defines the display position for the name or label associated with a heatmap series or element.
heatmap.name-reverse_description: Option in Heatmap panel settings: Reverses the display order of names or labels displayed on a heatmap's axis or in its legend. This allows users to invert the default sequence of categories or data points.
heatmap.name-scale_description: Label in Heatmap panel settings: Denotes the section or setting related to configuring the value scale (e.g., min, max values, units, color mapping) for the heatmap visualization.
heatmap.name-scheme_description: Label for a selection field in the heatmap configuration panel: Users can choose a predefined visual scheme, commonly a color scheme, to control how data is represented on the heatmap.
heatmap.name-show-color-scale_description: Toggle/Checkbox option in heatmap visualization settings: Controls the visibility of the color legend that maps values to colors on the heatmap.
heatmap.name-show-histogram_description: Option in Heatmap panel settings: Controls the display of a histogram along the Y-axis of the heatmap visualization.
heatmap.name-show-legend_description: Option in heatmap settings: Controls the visibility of the heatmap's color legend.
heatmap.name-start-color-from-value_description: Label in Heatmap panel options: This label for an input field allows users to specify the data value from which the color scale should begin. It defines the lower bound for the color gradient mapping in the heatmap.
heatmap.name-steps_description: Label in Heatmap configuration: This setting specifies the number of distinct intervals or color steps used to represent data values on the heatmap.
heatmap.name-tick-alignment_description: Option in Heatmap panel settings: Defines the alignment of tick marks on an axis within a heatmap visualization.
heatmap.name-tooltip-mode_description: Setting label in heatmap visualization settings: This option defines the display behavior or interaction mode for tooltips shown on the heatmap. It likely presents different choices for how tooltips appear or are triggered.
heatmap.name-unit_description: Field label in Heatmap configuration settings: Defines or displays the unit of measurement for the data values presented in the heatmap.
heatmap.name-value-name_description: Label in the heatmap configuration panel: Prompts the user to enter a descriptive name for a data value or range within the heatmap visualization.
heatmap.name-y-axis-scale_description: Setting label in Heatmap panel configuration: Defines the scaling options for the Y-axis of the heatmap. This allows users to control how values are displayed on the vertical axis.
heatmap.placeholder-axis-label_description: Placeholder text in an axis label input field within the heatmap visualization configuration: It suggests that the axis label will be automatically determined if no custom value is provided.
heatmap.placeholder-axis-width_description: Option in Heatmap axis width setting: This placeholder value indicates that the axis width should be automatically determined by the system. It is used when a specific width is not manually configured.
heatmap.placeholder-decimals_description: Placeholder text in Heatmap decimal display settings: This text indicates that the number of decimal places for displayed values will be automatically determined. It guides the user on the default behavior for decimal precision.
heatmap.placeholder-end-color-at-value_descripti: Option in the Heatmap panel configuration: This option automatically sets the upper bound of the heatmap's color scale to the maximum value present in the visualized data.
heatmap.placeholder-hide-cells-gt_description: A selectable option in Heatmap configuration settings, specifically for the 'hide cells greater than' filter: It signifies that no threshold is applied for hiding cells whose values are greater than a specified amount, effectively disabling this specific filter.
heatmap.placeholder-hide-cells-lt_description: Option in Heatmap cell visibility settings: It allows the user to choose to not hide any cells based on a "less than" threshold value.
heatmap.placeholder-max-value_description: Placeholder text in a heatmap's maximum value input field: Signifies that the system will automatically calculate the upper limit for the heatmap's scale when no manual value is provided.
heatmap.placeholder-min-value_description: Placeholder text in the minimum value input field for a Heatmap configuration. It informs the user that the heatmap's minimum value will be automatically calculated if left unspecified.
heatmap.placeholder-start-color-from-value_descr: Placeholder text in the heatmap color settings: This indicates that the starting color of the heatmap gradient is automatically derived from the minimum data value.
heatmap.placeholder-value-name_description: Default name in heatmap configuration: This string is the generic or default identifier for a data value or metric represented in the heatmap cells.

placement-options.label-hidden_description: Option label in Heatmap placement options: This label represents a setting to hide labels within the heatmap visualization.
placement-options.label-left_description: Option label in Heatmap Placement Options: Specifies the 'left' alignment or position for a configurable element (e.g., axis label, legend) within the heatmap visualization.
placement-options.label-right_description: Option label in heatmap placement options: Specifies the right-hand side for the placement or alignment of a heatmap element (e.g., legend or labels).

scale-options.label-exponential_description: Label in Scale options for a Heatmap visualization: This option allows users to select an exponential scale for the data displayed in the heatmap.
scale-options.label-linear_description: Option label in the Heatmap scale options: It represents the choice to use a linear scale for mapping data values to colors on the heatmap.

tick-alignment-options.label-auto_description: Auto" option in the heatmap's tick alignment settings: This option allows the system to automatically determine the optimal positioning and scaling of tick marks on the heatmap's axes.
tick-alignment-options.label-bottom_description: Option in Heatmap tick alignment settings: Aligns tick labels to the bottom edge of the heatmap grid. The "(GE)" clarifies this alignment is specifically relative to the grid edge.
tick-alignment-options.label-middle_description: Option in heatmap tick alignment settings: Specifies that ticks on the heatmap scale or axis should be aligned to the middle.
tick-alignment-options.label-top_description: Option in the tick alignment options for a heatmap visualization: It sets the vertical position of labels to the top. The '(LE)' is likely an internal abbreviation indicating a specific alignment variant.

tooltip-mode-options.label-all_description: Option label in Heatmap tooltip mode options: This option allows users to select a tooltip display mode that applies to all data points or shows all available information.
tooltip-mode-options.label-hidden_description: Option label in heatmap tooltip mode options: This selection sets the tooltip display mode to hidden, preventing it from appearing on the heatmap.
tooltip-mode-options.label-single_description: Option label in Heatmap tooltip mode options: Specifies a tooltip display mode where only a single tooltip is shown.

column-headers.keys_description: Column Header in Help Modal: Labels a column that lists specific keyboard keys or key combinations. This is typically used when explaining keyboard shortcuts or key-related functionalities.

shortcuts-category.dashboard_description: Category name in the Help Modal's shortcuts category list: Labels a group of keyboard shortcuts that are specific to dashboard operations.
shortcuts-category.focused-panel_description: Shortcut category title in the Shortcuts section of the Help modal: This title introduces a group of keyboard shortcuts that are specific to the currently active or selected panel in the dashboard.
shortcuts-category.global_description: Category title in the Help modal's shortcuts category section: Identifies a group of shortcuts that are globally applicable within the application.
shortcuts-category.time-range_description: Category title in the Shortcuts section of the Help modal: Labels the group of keyboard shortcuts used for managing the dashboard's time range.

shortcuts-description.change-theme_description: Label in the Shortcuts Description section of the Help modal: Describes the action to change the application's visual theme. This label likely corresponds to a shortcut or an instruction within the help content.
shortcuts-description.collapse-all-rows_description: Description text in the shortcuts description section within a help modal: It explains the action performed by a keyboard shortcut, which is to collapse all expandable rows in a data table or list.
shortcuts-description.copy-time-range_description: Shortcut description in the Help modal: This describes a keyboard shortcut action that copies the currently selected time range within the dashboard. This allows users to easily reuse the time range settings.
shortcuts-description.dashboard-settings_description: Label in the Shortcuts description section of the Help modal: Identifies the Dashboard settings. This string likely refers to the configuration area for dashboards or an action that opens it, often in the context of explaining shortcuts.
shortcuts-description.duplicate-panel_description: Shortcut description in the 'Shortcuts' section of a help modal: Describes the action that creates a duplicate of an existing dashboard panel.
shortcuts-description.exit-edit/setting-views_description: Text description in the help modal's shortcuts section: This describes a keyboard shortcut used to exit from either an editing view or a view where settings and configurations are adjusted.
shortcuts-description.expand-all-rows_description: Text in the Help modal's Shortcuts description section: Describes a keyboard shortcut used to expand all rows in a collapsible data display, such as a table or tree.
shortcuts-description.explore-panel_description: Text label within the 'Shortcuts' description section of the help modal: This label identifies the 'Explore panel,' a dedicated area for data investigation. It likely serves to describe a keyboard shortcut that interacts with or navigates to this specific panel.
shortcuts-description.go-to-dashboards_description: Text describing a shortcut action in the Shortcuts section of the Help modal: This text explains that a specific keyboard shortcut or command allows the user to navigate directly to the Dashboards page.
shortcuts-description.go-to-explore_description: Shortcut description in Help modal > Shortcuts description section: Explains the function of a shortcut that navigates the user to the 'Explore' feature.
shortcuts-description.go-to-home-dashboard_description: Shortcut action description in the help modal's shortcuts description section: Describes the action of a shortcut that navigates the user to the main Home Dashboard.
shortcuts-description.go-to-profile_description: Link in the shortcuts description section of the help modal: Navigates the user to their profile page.
shortcuts-description.inspect-panel_description: Shortcut description in the Shortcuts section of a Help modal: Describes the action of inspecting a panel, likely explaining the function of a corresponding keyboard shortcut.
shortcuts-description.make-time-range-permanent_description: Shortcut description in the Help modal: Explains how to make the currently selected time range within the dashboard absolute/permanent. This prevents the time range from changing dynamically or updating automatically.
shortcuts-description.move-time-range-back_description: Text label in the 'Shortcuts' section of the Help modal: Describes the keyboard shortcut used to shift the displayed time range back in time (to an earlier period).
shortcuts-description.move-time-range-forward_description: Label in the Shortcuts section of the Help Modal: Describes the action of a keyboard shortcut that shifts the currently displayed time range in a dashboard to a later period.
shortcuts-description.open-alerting_description: Link in the shortcuts description section of the help modal: Navigates the user to the Alerting feature or page.
shortcuts-description.open-assistant_description: Button in the Help Modal's Shortcuts Description: Initiates the opening of the application's assistant. This element likely serves as a shortcut to quickly access AI-powered help or a guided tour within the application.
shortcuts-description.open-search_description: Shortcut description in the Help modal's shortcuts list: Explains a keyboard shortcut that opens the search functionality.
shortcuts-description.open-share-embed-drawer_description: Shortcut description label in the Shortcuts description section of the Help modal: Describes the action of opening the drawer where users can generate and share embed code for a specific dashboard panel. This label indicates a shortcut related to sharing the embedded view of a panel.
shortcuts-description.open-share-link-drawer_description: Shortcut description in the Help modal's shortcuts section: Describes the shortcut or action that allows users to open the interface for sharing a link to a specific dashboard panel.
shortcuts-description.open-shared-modal_description: Action label in the "Shortcuts Description" section of the Help Modal: This allows users to open a dedicated modal for sharing a snapshot of a dashboard panel.
shortcuts-description.paste-time-range_description: Shortcut action description in the keyboard shortcuts section of the Help modal: This text describes the keyboard shortcut that allows users to paste a previously copied time range. It enables applying a consistent time range across different dashboards or panels.
shortcuts-description.refresh-all-panels_description: Description string in the Help modal, under the 'Shortcuts description' section: Explains the function of a keyboard shortcut that refreshes all panels on the dashboard.
shortcuts-description.remove-panel_description: Shortcut action label in the help modal's shortcuts description section: Describes the action of removing a panel from a dashboard using a keyboard shortcut.
shortcuts-description.save-dashboard_description: Shortcut description in the help modal: Explains the keyboard shortcut used to save the current dashboard.
shortcuts-description.show-all-shortcuts_description: Link in the help modal's shortcuts description section: This link allows users to view a complete list of all available keyboard shortcuts.
shortcuts-description.toggle-active-mode_description: Toggle button in Help modal (Shortcuts description): Allows switching between an 'in-active' state and a 'view mode'. This typically controls whether a dashboard or component is in an interactive/editing mode or a read-only display mode.
shortcuts-description.toggle-all-panel-legends_description: Description of a keyboard shortcut in the Help modal: Explains the shortcut that allows users to quickly show or hide all legends on dashboard panels.
shortcuts-description.toggle-auto-fit_description: Keyboard shortcut in the Help modal: Toggles the automatic fitting of panels. This is an experimental feature.
shortcuts-description.toggle-exemplars_description: Shortcut description in the Help modal, within the shortcuts section: Explains a keyboard shortcut used to toggle the display of exemplars (specific data points) across all dashboard panels.
shortcuts-description.toggle-graph-crosshair_description: Keyboard shortcut description in the Help Modal's Shortcuts section: This shortcut allows users to toggle the shared crosshair feature for graphs. When enabled, a synchronized vertical line appears across multiple graph panels, aiding in comparative data analysis at a specific time point.
shortcuts-description.toggle-kiosk_description: Keyboard Shortcut in the Help Modal: Explains the function of a keyboard shortcut that toggles kiosk mode, which hides the top navigation bar for a cleaner display.
shortcuts-description.toggle-panel-edit_description: Action description for a keyboard shortcut in the Help modal's "Keyboard Shortcuts" section: This text describes the shortcut's function to switch a dashboard panel between its display and editing modes.
shortcuts-description.toggle-panel-fullscreen_description: Keyboard shortcut description in the Help modal's shortcuts section: Explains the keyboard shortcut used to switch a dashboard panel between its normal and fullscreen display modes.
shortcuts-description.toggle-panel-legend_description: **Keyboard shortcut** in the **Shortcuts section of the Help modal**: This command allows users to show or hide the legend associated with a dashboard panel. It describes a keyboard shortcut.
shortcuts-description.zoom-out-time-range_description: Shortcut action description in the Shortcuts section of a help modal: This text describes the function of a keyboard shortcut that expands the visible time range displayed in a dashboard or chart.

help-modal.title: Shortcuts
help-modal.title_description: Title in a help modal: Identifies the content of the modal as a list or explanation of keyboard shortcuts available within the application.

help-wizard.download-snapshot_description: Button in the help wizard: Triggers the download of a data snapshot. The `{{snapshotSize}}` variable dynamically displays the size of the snapshot being downloaded.
help-wizard.github-comment_description: Button in the help wizard: Enables copying a pre-defined message or template to the clipboard, intended for use as a Github comment.
help-wizard.support-bundle: You can also retrieve a support bundle containing information concerning your Grafana instance and configured datasources in the <1>support bundles section</1>.
help-wizard.troubleshooting-help: To request troubleshooting help, send a snapshot of this panel to Grafana Labs Technical Support. The snapshot contains query response data and panel settings.

histogram.category-histogram_description: Label in a data visualization or chart configuration context: Identifies the Histogram chart type. This string likely serves as a title for a histogram panel or an option in a chart selection menu.

histogram-panel.no-histogram-found-in-response_descripti: Error message in Histogram panel: Informs the user that the data response from the data source did not contain the necessary histogram data. This message is displayed when the panel cannot render a histogram due to missing data.

histogram.name-fill-opacity_description: A setting in the Histogram visualization: Controls the transparency level of the filled areas (e.g., bars or bins) within the histogram chart.
histogram.name-gradient-mode_description: A toggle or selection option in the histogram panel's display settings. It allows users to enable a gradient coloring mode for the histogram bars.
histogram.name-line-width_description: Label in the styling options for a histogram panel: This controls the thickness of a line used in the histogram visualization.
histogram.name-stacking_description: Label in histogram panel settings: Controls the display mode for multiple data series, allowing them to be stacked on top of each other.

main-settings.view-manage-config_description: Link in the Main Settings section of Insight Finder: Allows users to view and manage the application's configuration. This feature provides control over Insight Finder's operational settings.

inspect-data-tab.loading_description: Status message in the Inspect Data tab of the Inspector panel: Informs the user that data is currently being retrieved or processed for display within this tab.
inspect-data-tab.no-data_description: Message in the Inspect Data tab of the Inspector panel: Indicates that no data is available to display in the current view or selection.

inspect-error-tab.error-status-message_description: Label in the Error tab within the Inspector panel: Displays the status of an error and introduces its accompanying message. The `{{errorStatus}}` placeholder will be replaced with the specific error status.
inspect-error-tab.error-status-no-message_description: Label in the Error tab within the Inspector panel: Displays the status code or brief status of an error. This specific key might be used when no detailed error message accompanies the status.
inspect-error-tab.error-trace-message_description: Informational text in the Inspect Error tab within the Inspector panel: Displays the unique Trace ID associated with an error. This ID is used for debugging and tracking the error within the system.

inspect-jsontab.apply_description: Button in the JSON tab of the inspector panel: This button is used to apply any modifications made within the JSON configuration editor. It confirms and saves the changes.
inspect-jsontab.support_description: Button/Link in the JSON tab of the Inspector panel: Provides access to help or support resources.

query.collapse-all_description: Button in the Query section of the Inspector panel: Collapses all expandable query rows, sections, or settings to their minimized state.
query.copy-to-clipboard_description: Button in the Query section of the Inspector panel: Allows the user to copy the displayed query or its definition to the system clipboard.
query.description: Query inspector allows you to view raw request and response. To collect this data Grafana needs to issue a new query. Click refresh button below to trigger a new query.
query.expand-all_description: Button in the Query section of the Inspector panel: To expand all collapsed query elements or configuration details, revealing their full view.
query.no-data: No request and response collected yet. Hit refresh button
query.no-data_description: Informational message in the Query tab of the Inspector panel: It indicates that no query request or response data has been collected yet for the current query. Users are prompted to click the refresh button to attempt data retrieval.
query.refresh_description: Button in the query section within the inspector panel: Triggers a re-execution of the current query to display updated results.

query-inspector.count-frames_one_description: Text label in the Inspector > Query Inspector panel: This string displays the number of data frames returned by a query. It is typically followed by additional information about the frames or query results.
query-inspector.count-frames_other_description: Text label in the Query Inspector panel: Displays the number of data frames found or processed by a query. This string is used for plural counts (e.g., when `{{count}}` is 0, 2, or more), typically as part of a larger status message.
query-inspector.count-rows_one_description: Text label in the Query Inspector panel: Displays the total number of rows returned by the executed query.
query-inspector.count-rows_other_description: Text Display in the Query Inspector panel: Shows the total number of rows retrieved by the executed query. This template represents the 'other' plural form based on i18n pluralization rules.
query-inspector.query-inspector_description: Panel title in the Inspector view: This title identifies the dedicated section for examining and troubleshooting data queries.

invitee-row.aria-label-revoke-invite_description: ARIA label for a button in an invitee row within the Invites section: Indicates the action to revoke a sent invitation for that specific invitee.

signup-invited-page.complete-following: Please complete the following and choose a password to accept your invitation and continue:
signup-invited-page.complete-following_description: Instructional text in the Signup Invited Page: Prompts an invited user to complete their registration by filling out required fields and setting a password. This text appears above the form where additional details are to be entered.
signup-invited-page.custom-has-invited-you: <0>{{invitedBy}}</0> has invited you to join Grafana and the organization <3>{{orgName}}</3>
signup-invited-page.custom-has-invited-you_description: Invitation message on the sign-up invited page: This message informs a user that they have been invited by a specific person to join Grafana and a particular organization. It includes dynamic placeholders for the inviter's name and the organization's name.
signup-invited-page.default-has-invited-you: <0>Someone</0> has invited you to join Grafana and the organization <3>{{orgName}}</3>
signup-invited-page.default-has-invited-you_description: Main message on the Signup Invited Page: Informs the user that they have been invited to join Grafana and a specific organization. It includes placeholders for the inviting entity (defaults to "Someone") and the organization's name (`{{orgName}}`).
signup-invited-page.greeting-custom_description: Greeting message on the invited user signup page: Welcomes an invited user to the signup process. The `{{greeting}}` placeholder is dynamically replaced with a personalized greeting, such as the user's name.
signup-invited-page.greeting-default_description: Greeting text on the signup invited page: This is a default, general greeting displayed to a user who has landed on the page after being invited to sign up.

message.email-is-invalid_description: Error message in the Sign-up/Invitation Page: Informs an invited user that the email address they entered during sign-up is invalid.

signup-invited-page.placeholder-name-optional_description: Placeholder text in an input field on the signup invited page: Prompts the invited user to enter their name, clarifying that this input is optional.
signup-invited-page.sign-up_description: Button in the Sign-up invited page: Allows an invited user to complete their account registration.

label-dropdown-info: Can't find your label? Enter it manually
label-dropdown-info_description: Info message associated with a label selection dropdown: It guides users who cannot find their desired label in the dropdown list to manually enter it as an alternative.

layer-drag-drop-list.draggable-aria-label_description: ARIA label on a draggable item in the Layer Drag-and-Drop List: It provides an accessibility instruction that the item can be reordered by dragging and dropping.
layer-drag-drop-list.duplicate-tooltip_description: Tooltip in Layers drag-and-drop list: Indicates the action to create a duplicate of a layer.

layer-name.edit-layer-title_description: Title in a layer editing dialog: This text labels the dialog or section where a user can change the name of a specific layer.

attributes-section.description: Specify the LDAP attributes that map to the user's given name, surname, and email address, ensuring the application correctly retrieves and displays user information.
attributes-section.label: Attributes
attributes-section.label_description: Section Label in the Attributes section of the LDAP drawer: This label identifies the area where users can view or configure LDAP attributes.
attributes-section.member-of-label_description: Label in the Attributes section of the LDAP Drawer: This label identifies the attribute or field that displays the groups or roles an LDAP user or entity is a member of. It prompts the user to view or configure group memberships.
attributes-section.surname-label_description: Label in the Attributes section within the LDAP drawer: Identifies the input field or display area for a user's surname (family name). This typically facilitates mapping user attributes from an LDAP directory.

extra-security-section.client-cert-label_description: Label in the 'Extra Security' section of the LDAP configuration drawer: It identifies the field where the user enters or views the file path for the client certificate used for secure LDAP connections.
extra-security-section.client-cert-value-label_description: Label in the 'Extra Security' section of the LDAP configuration drawer: Identifies the field where the content of the client certificate is entered or displayed. This certificate is used for enhanced security during LDAP communication.
extra-security-section.client-cert-value-placeholder_descriptio: Input field placeholder in the 'Extra Security' section of the LDAP configuration drawer: This prompts the user to enter the base64 encoded content of a client certificate for enhanced security.
extra-security-section.client-key-label_description: Label in the 'Extra Security' section of the LDAP configuration drawer: Identifies an input field where the user specifies the file path to a client key. This key is typically used for secure communication, such as SSL/TLS, with the LDAP server.
extra-security-section.client-key-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Extra Security section of the LDAP configuration drawer: This string provides an example path for the client key file required for securing LDAP connections.
extra-security-section.client-key-value-label_description: Label in the 'Extra Security' section of the LDAP Configuration drawer: It identifies the field where the content of a client key (e.g., a certificate or private key) is entered or displayed. This key is used for secure communication or authentication with the LDAP server.
extra-security-section.client-key-value-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Extra Security section of the LDAP configuration drawer: This field guides the user to input the base64-encoded content of a client key. It is used for configuring secure LDAP connections.
extra-security-section.encryption-provider-base-64_description: Label in the Extra Security section of the LDAP Drawer: This text indicates that content expected or displayed in an associated field is Base64-encoded, likely for an encryption provider.
extra-security-section.encryption-provider-description: X.509 certificate provides the public part, while the private key issued in a PKCS#8 format provides the private part of the asymmetric encryption.
extra-security-section.encryption-provider-file-path_descriptio: Label for an input field in the 'Extra Security' section of the LDAP Drawer: Used to specify the file path for an encryption provider.
extra-security-section.encryption-provider-label: Encryption key and certificate provision specification.
extra-security-section.encryption-provider-label_description: Label in the 'Extra Security' section of the LDAP configuration drawer: It identifies the settings or field where users specify or review the provisioning details for encryption keys and certificates. This defines how these security components are made available for secure communication within the LDAP setup.
extra-security-section.label: Extra security measures
extra-security-section.label_description: Section Heading in the 'Extra Security' section of the LDAP configuration drawer: It labels a group of advanced security settings for LDAP integration.
extra-security-section.min-tls-version-description: This is the minimum TLS version allowed. Accepted values are: TLS1.2, TLS1.3.
extra-security-section.min-tls-version-label_description: Label in the Extra Security Section of the LDAP Drawer: This label indicates or allows the user to configure the minimum acceptable Transport Layer Security (TLS) protocol version for LDAP communication.
extra-security-section.root-ca-cert-label_description: Label in the "Extra Security" section of the LDAP configuration drawer: It indicates where to enter the file path for the Root CA certificate. This certificate is used to establish trust for secure LDAP connections.
extra-security-section.root-ca-cert-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the 'Extra Security' section of the LDAP configuration drawer: This value indicates the expected format for the Root CA Certificate file path. It serves as an example for users configuring secure LDAP connections.
extra-security-section.root-ca-cert-value-label_description: Label in the Extra Security Section of the LDAP Drawer: This label indicates where the content of the Root CA certificate, necessary for securing LDAP communication, should be entered or displayed.
extra-security-section.root-ca-cert-value-placeholder_descripti: Placeholder text in the Extra Security section of the LDAP configuration drawer: It provides an example of the Root CA certificate value. This guides users on the expected base64 encoded format for secure LDAP communication.
extra-security-section.start-tls-description: If set to true, use LDAP with STARTTLS instead of LDAPS
extra-security-section.start-tls-description_description: Description text in the 'Extra Security' section of the LDAP configuration drawer: Explains the behavior of enabling the STARTTLS option for LDAP connections, clarifying that it will use LDAP with STARTTLS instead of LDAPS for secure communication.
extra-security-section.start-tls-label_description: Label in the Extra Security section of the LDAP drawer: This text identifies a control (likely a checkbox or toggle) that, when enabled, initiates Transport Layer Security (TLS) for secure LDAP communication.
extra-security-section.tls-ciphers-label_description: Label in the 'Extra Security' section within the LDAP drawer: This identifies the setting or field for configuring the TLS cipher suites used for secure LDAP communication.
extra-security-section.tls-ciphers-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in an input field for TLS ciphers within the 'Extra Security' section of the LDAP configuration drawer. It provides an example of a valid TLS cipher string format that the user should enter.
extra-security-section.use-ssl-description: Set to true if LDAP server should use TLS connection (either with STARTTLS or LDAPS)
extra-security-section.use-ssl-description_description: Description in the 'Extra Security' section of the LDAP configuration drawer: Describes the condition for enabling a secure TLS connection to the LDAP server. It clarifies that this setting covers both STARTTLS and LDAPS protocols.
extra-security-section.use-ssl-label_description: Label in the 'Extra Security' section of the LDAP configuration drawer: Indicates an option to enable or disable the use of SSL for secure LDAP communication.
extra-security-section.use-ssl-tooltip: For a complete list of supported ciphers and TLS versions, refer to:
extra-security-section.use-ssl-tooltip_description: Tooltip text in the 'Extra Security' section of the LDAP configuration drawer: This text informs the user where to find a complete list of supported ciphers and TLS versions. It is displayed as part of a tooltip related to SSL/TLS configuration settings.

grafana-admin.description: If enabled, all users from this group will be Grafana Admins
grafana-admin.description_description: Description in LDAP Group Mapping settings for the Grafana Admin option: This description explains the effect of enabling the "Grafana Admin" setting for an LDAP group. It indicates that all users belonging to that group will be granted Grafana Administrator privileges.
grafana-admin.label: Grafana Admin
grafana-admin.label_description: Label in the LDAP Group Mapping section of the LDAP configuration drawer: Identifies the option used to map an LDAP group to the Grafana Administrator role.

group-dn.description: The name of the key used to extract the ID token from the returned OAuth2 token.
group-dn.description_description: Description for a configuration field in the 'Group Mapping' section of the 'LDAP Drawer' settings: This field specifies the key name used to extract the ID token from an OAuth2 token. This ID token is likely utilized to identify and map users to groups in LDAP.
group-dn.label: Group DN
group-dn.label_description: Label in the LDAP configuration drawer, within the group mapping section: It identifies the input field or display for an LDAP group's Distinguished Name used in the mapping process. This field expects the unique identifier of a group in the LDAP directory.

org-id.description: The Grafana organization database id. Default org (ID 1) will be used if left out
org-id.description_description: Description for an input field in the 'Org ID' setting within the 'Group Mapping' section of the 'LDAP Drawer': It explains the function of the Grafana organization database ID. It clarifies that the default organization (ID 1) is used if the field is left empty.
org-id.label: Org ID
org-id.label_description: Label in the Group Mapping section of the LDAP Drawer: This field is used to specify the Organization ID. It associates mapped LDAP groups with a particular organization within BMC Helix Dashboards.

org-role.label: Org role *
org-role.label_description: Label in the LDAP Drawer > Group Mapping section: This label identifies a mandatory input field where an administrator assigns an organizational role to users based on their LDAP group membership. The asterisk indicates it is a required field.

remove.button_description: Button in the LDAP Drawer's Group Mapping section: Allows the user to remove a specific, selected group mapping.

add.button_description: Button in the 'Group Mapping' section of the LDAP configuration drawer: Initiates the process of adding a new LDAP group mapping.

group-mapping-section.description: Map LDAP groups to Grafana org roles
group-mapping-section.description_description: Description in the LDAP Group Mapping section: Explains the functionality for mapping LDAP groups to Grafana organization roles.
group-mapping-section.group-search-base-dns-label_description: Label in the Group Mapping section of the LDAP drawer: This label identifies the input field where administrators define the base Distinguished Name (DN) in the LDAP directory for searching groups. It specifies the starting point for group lookups.
group-mapping-section.group-search-base-dns-placeholder_descri: Placeholder text in the Group Search Base DN input field within the LDAP group mapping configuration section: This placeholder text provides an example of the Distinguished Name (DN) format required for specifying the base location to search for groups in LDAP. It guides users on the expected input syntax.
group-mapping-section.group-search-filter-description: Used to filter and identify group entries within the directory
group-mapping-section.group-search-filter-description_descript: Description text for the Group Search Filter in the 'Group Mapping' section of the LDAP configuration drawer: It explains that this filter is used to identify and filter group entries from the LDAP directory.
group-mapping-section.group-search-filter-label_description: Label in the Group mapping section within the LDAP drawer: It prompts the user to define criteria for searching groups in the LDAP directory. This filter is used to narrow down the groups displayed or available for mapping.
group-mapping-section.group-search-filter-user-attribute-descr: Identifies users within group entries for filtering purposes

Explanatory text in the Group Mapping section of the LDAP configuration drawer: It describes the function of a user attribute that identifies members within group entries, enabling their inclusion or exclusion through filtering.
group-mapping-section.group-search-filter-user-attribute-label: Label in the Group Mapping section of the LDAP configuration drawer: It prompts the user to specify the LDAP attribute that contains the name of a group. This attribute is used to identify groups for mapping or search filters.
group-mapping-section.label: Group mapping
group-mapping-section.label_description: Section title in the 'Group mapping' section of the LDAP drawer: This label denotes the dedicated area for configuring how groups from an LDAP directory are mapped.
group-mapping-section.skip-org-role-sync-description: Prevent synchronizing users’ organization roles from your IdP
group-mapping-section.skip-org-role-sync-description_descripti: Description text in the Group Mapping section of the LDAP drawer: This string describes a setting that allows administrators to prevent the synchronization of users’ organization roles from their Identity Provider (IdP).
group-mapping-section.skip-org-role-sync-label_description: Label in the LDAP Drawer's Group Mapping section: This option allows administrators to skip or disable the synchronization of organization roles when configuring LDAP group mappings. It is likely associated with a checkbox or toggle.

misc-section.allow-sign-up-descrition: If not enabled, only existing Grafana users can log in using LDAP
misc-section.allow-sign-up-descrition_description: Informational text in the 'Miscellaneous' section of the LDAP Configuration drawer: This text clarifies the login behavior for LDAP users if the "Allow sign up" (or auto-provisioning) option is disabled, stating that only existing Grafana users can log in via LDAP.
misc-section.allow-sign-up-label_description: Checkbox label in the "Miscellaneous" section of the LDAP configuration drawer: This option controls whether new users can self-register or sign up for an account in the system when LDAP authentication is configured.
misc-section.label: Misc
misc-section.label_description: Label in the LDAP configuration drawer: This identifies the 'Miscellaneous' section, which groups various or unclassified settings and options related to LDAP configuration.
misc-section.port-description: Default port is 389 without SSL or 636 with SSL
misc-section.port-description_description: Description text in the LDAP configuration drawer's 'Miscellaneous' section: This text provides guidance on the default port numbers used for LDAP connections, differentiating between secure (SSL) and non-secure configurations.
misc-section.timeout-description: Timeout in seconds for the connection to the LDAP server
misc-section.timeout-description_description: Description in the LDAP configuration drawer, within the Miscellaneous section: Clarifies the 'Timeout' setting, specifying that it defines the connection timeout to the LDAP server in seconds.
misc-section.timeout-label_description: Label in the miscellaneous section of the LDAP configuration drawer: This label indicates a setting for the maximum duration an LDAP operation should wait before timing out. It is typically associated with an input field where the timeout value can be specified.

ldap-drawer.title: Advanced settings
ldap-drawer.title_description: Title in an LDAP configuration drawer/side panel: Labels the section where users can configure advanced settings for LDAP integration.

advanced-settings-section.subtitle: Mappings, extra security measures, and more.
advanced-settings-section.subtitle_description: Subtitle in the Advanced Settings section of the LDAP Settings page: It summarizes the types of configurations and options available within this section, such as user/group mappings and additional security measures.
advanced-settings-section.title: Advanced Settings
advanced-settings-section.title_description: Section Title in the Advanced Settings section on the LDAP Settings page: Labels the area where users can configure additional, more specialized parameters for LDAP integration.

alert.discard-success_description: Alert message in the LDAP settings page: Confirms that unsaved changes to LDAP settings have been successfully discarded.
alert.error-fetching_description: Alert message in the LDAP Settings page: Informs the user that an error occurred while attempting to retrieve or load the current LDAP configuration settings.
alert.error-saving_description: Error message in the LDAP settings page: Informs the user when an attempt to save changes to the LDAP settings has failed.
alert.error-validate-form_description: Error message in the alert section on the LDAP Settings page: Informs the user that an error occurred while attempting to validate the provided LDAP settings.
alert.feature-flag-disabled: This page is only accessible by enabling the ssoSettingsLDAP feature flag.
alert.feature-flag-disabled_description: Alert message in the LDAP Settings page: Informs the user that the page is currently inaccessible. It specifies that the 'ssoSettingsLDAP' feature flag must be enabled to view and interact with the page content.
alert.saved_description: Alert message in the LDAP Settings page: Informs the user that their changes to the LDAP settings have been successfully saved.

bind-dn.description: Distinguished name of the account used to bind and authenticate to the LDAP server.
bind-dn.description_description: Description for an input field on the LDAP Settings page, specifically for the 'Bind DN' field: This text defines the distinguished name (DN) of the account that will be used to establish and authenticate the connection to the LDAP server.
bind-dn.label: Bind DN
bind-dn.label_description: Label in the LDAP settings page: Identifies the Distinguished Name (DN) used by BMC Helix Dashboards to authenticate to the LDAP server. This account typically needs permissions to read user and group information.
bind-dn.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Bind DN input field on the LDAP settings page: Provides an example of the distinguished name (DN) format required for the Bind DN.

bind-password.label: Bind password
bind-password.label_description: Label in the LDAP Settings page: This label identifies the input field where the password for authenticating the dashboard to the LDAP server is entered.

buttons-section.disable-button_description: Button in the buttons section of the LDAP Settings page: This button allows the user to disable the LDAP integration or specific LDAP settings within the system.
buttons-section.discard-button_description: Button in the buttons section on the LDAP settings page: Discards any unsaved changes made to the LDAP settings, reverting to the previous configuration.
buttons-section.save-and-enable-button_description: Button in the buttons section on the LDAP settings page: Saves the current LDAP configuration settings and immediately activates them.
buttons-section.test-button_description: Button in the buttons section of the LDAP settings page: Used to test the configured LDAP connection or settings.

ldap-settings-page.documentation_description: A link or button in the LDAP settings page: Provides access to documentation or help related to configuring LDAP.

host.description: Hostname or IP address of the LDAP server you wish to connect to.
host.description_description: Instructional text for an input field on the LDAP Settings page: It prompts the user to enter the hostname or IP address of the LDAP server they intend to connect to.
host.error: Server host is a required field
host.error_description: Error message for the 'Host' field on the LDAP Settings page: This message is displayed when the user attempts to save settings without entering a server host, indicating it's a required field.
host.label: Server host
host.label_description: Label in the LDAP Settings page: Prompts the user to enter the hostname or IP address of the LDAP server. This field is crucial for establishing a connection to the LDAP directory.

login-form-alert.description: Your LDAP configuration is not working because the basic login form is currently disabled. Please enable the login form to use LDAP authentication. You can enable it on the Authentication page under “Auth settings”.
login-form-alert.title: Basic login disabled
login-form-alert.title_description: Alert title in the LDAP settings page: This title appears in an alert informing the user that basic login (e.g., username/password without LDAP) is currently disabled.

search_filter.description: LDAP search filter used to locate specific entries within the directory.
search_filter.description_description: Description text in the 'Search Filter' section on the LDAP Settings page: It explains that the LDAP search filter is used to locate specific entries within the directory.
search_filter.error: Search filter is a required field
search_filter.error_description: Error message for the 'Search filter' field on the LDAP settings page: Informs the user that this field is mandatory and must be populated. This message appears when the user attempts to save or proceed without entering a value.
search_filter.label: Search filter
search_filter.label_description: Label in the LDAP Settings Page: Refers to a field or section where administrators define the criteria for searching the LDAP directory. This filter is used to specify which users or groups should be retrieved.
search_filter.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the LDAP Settings page, for the search filter input field. It provides an example of a valid LDAP search filter, showing the expected format including the variable placeholder (`%s`).

search-base-dns.description: An array of base dns to search through.
