search-base-dns.description_description: Description text in the LDAP Settings page, for the 'Search Base DNs' field: Clarifies that this field is used to provide an array of distinguished names (DNs) that serve as base points for LDAP searches.
search-base-dns.error: Search base DNS is a required field
search-base-dns.error_description: Error message for the 'Search base DNS' input field on the LDAP Settings page: Informs the user that this field is mandatory. This message appears if the user attempts to save or proceed without entering a value.
search-base-dns.label: Search base DNS
search-base-dns.label_description: Label on the LDAP settings page: This field is used to specify the search base DNS. It defines the starting point for directory searches within the configured LDAP system.
search-base-dns.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Search Base DNs field on the LDAP Settings page: It provides an example of the expected format for an LDAP Search Base DN. This guides users on how to specify the starting point for LDAP directory searches.

ldap-settings-page.subtitle: The LDAP integration in Grafana allows your Grafana users to log in with their LDAP credentials. Find out more in our <2><0>documentation</0></2> .
ldap-settings-page.title: Basic Settings
ldap-settings-page.title_description: Page Title in the LDAP Settings page: Identifies the section containing fundamental configuration options for LDAP integration.

add-modal.error: Library panel with this name already exists
add-modal.error_description: Error message in Add Library Panel modal: Informs the user that the chosen name for the new library panel is already in use, preventing the creation of a duplicate panel.
add-modal.folder_description: Label in the "Add" modal within the Library Panel: This text prompts the user to select or specify a folder where the new library item will be saved.
add-modal.folder-description: Library panel permissions are derived from the folder permissions
add-modal.folder-description_description: Informational text in the "Add Library Panel" modal: Informs the user that library panel permissions are inherited from the permissions of the folder where the panel is located.
add-modal.name: Library panel name
add-modal.name_description: Label in the 'Add Library Panel' modal dialog: It prompts the user to enter a name for the new library panel being created.

add-widget.title: Add panel from panel library
add-widget.title_description: Modal Title in the 'Add widget' workflow: It informs the user that they are in the process of adding a new panel by selecting an existing one from the panel library.

empty-state.message: You haven't created any library panels yet
empty-state.message_description: Message in the Library Panel's empty state: Informs the user that they have not yet created any library panels, typically prompting them to create new ones.
empty-state.more-info: Create a library panel from any existing dashboard panel through the panel context menu. <2>Learn more</2>

add-library-panel-modal.title-create-library-panel_description: Title in the Add Library Panel modal: This title indicates the user's action of creating a new library panel.

change-library-panel-modal.confirmText-change_description: Button label in the "Change Library Panel" modal: Confirms and applies the modifications made to a library panel.
change-library-panel-modal.confirmText-replace_description: Button in the 'Change Library Panel' modal: Confirms the user's decision to replace the selected library panel or its contents.

confirm.delete-panel: Do you want to delete this panel?
confirm.delete-panel_description: Confirmation message in a deletion dialog: Prompts the user to confirm the deletion of a library panel.

delete-library-panel-modal.title-delete-library-panel_description: Modal Title in the Delete Library Panel modal: This title indicates the purpose of the modal, which is to confirm or initiate the deletion of a library panel. It appears when a user attempts to remove a reusable dashboard component.

empty-state.message_description: Message in the empty state for Library Panels: Informs the user that there are currently no library panels to display.

folder-link.dashboards_description: Label in a folder link within the Library Panels section: Refers to the section or option for viewing and managing dashboards. This label helps users locate dashboards associated with that specific folder.

has-connected-dashboards.dashboard-name_description: Label in the 'Connected Dashboards' section of a Library Panel: Identifies the name of a dashboard that is connected to the library panel.

library-panel-info.last-edited_description: An informational text in the Library Panel Information section: It displays the date and user responsible for the last edit of a library panel. This helps in tracking changes and understanding the panel's history.
library-panel-info.usage-count_one_description: Information label in the Library Panel Information section: It displays the number of dashboards that are currently using the selected library panel. The `_one` suffix in the key indicates this string is intended for the singular count case (i.e., when `count` is 1).
library-panel-info.usage-count_other_description: Text label in the Library Panel Information view: Displays the total number of dashboards where a specific library panel is currently in use. This string is for plural counts (e.g., 2, 3, ... dashboards).

library-panels-search.placeholder-search-by-name-or-descriptio: Search by name, description or folder name

Placeholder text in the search field within the Library Panels search interface: This text prompts users to search for library panels by their name, description, or the name of their containing folder.

library-panels.loading-panel-text_description: Loading indicator text displayed when interacting with library panels: Informs the user that a library panel's content is currently being loaded.

modal.body_one: This panel is being used in {{count}} dashboard. Please choose which dashboard to view the panel in:
modal.body_one_description: Message in a modal dialog for library panels: Informs the user that a library panel is being used across multiple dashboards and prompts them to select a specific dashboard to view the panel within its context. The `{{count}}` placeholder indicates the number of dashboards using the panel.
modal.body_other: This panel is being used in {{count}} dashboard. Please choose which dashboard to view the panel in:
modal.body_other_description: Body message in a modal dialog for Library Panels: This message informs the user that a selected library panel is used in multiple dashboards and prompts them to choose one specific dashboard to view it within. The `{{count}}` variable indicates the number of dashboards.
modal.panel-not-linked: Panel is not linked to a dashboard. Add the panel to a dashboard and retry.
modal.select-no-options-message_description: Informational message in a modal within the Library Panels section: This message is displayed when no dashboards are found or available for selection within the modal's context.
modal.select-placeholder: Start typing to search for dashboard
modal.select-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a search input field within a modal window for library panels: It prompts the user to start typing to search for an existing dashboard.
modal.title: View panel in dashboard
modal.title_description: Modal Title in a Library Panels modal: Describes the action of viewing a library panel in a dashboard.

panel-library-options-group.create-new-library-panel_description: Button in the Panel Library Options group: Initiates the process of creating a new dashboard panel that can be saved to the central library for reuse across multiple dashboards. This option allows users to standardize and manage their panel components efficiently.

save.error_description: Error message in the Library Panels feature: Informs the user that an attempt to save a library panel has failed. It includes a placeholder for the specific error details.
save.success_description: Success message in the Library Panels section: Notifies the user that a library panel has been successfully saved.

save-library-panel-modal.affected-dashboards: The following dashboards using the panel will be affected:
save-library-panel-modal.affected-dashboards_description: Label in Save Library Panel modal: This label introduces a list of dashboards that will be affected by saving changes to the library panel. It informs the user about the wider impact of their action.
save-library-panel-modal.dashboard-name_description: Label in the 'Save Library Panel' modal: Identifies the field for entering or displaying the name of a dashboard. This field is presented during the process of saving a panel as a reusable library panel.
save-library-panel-modal.discard_description: Button in the Save Library Panel modal: Allows the user to abandon any unsaved changes or cancel the creation of a new library panel without saving.
save-library-panel-modal.num-affected_one_description: Informational message in Save Library Panel modal: Informs the user that their update will affect a single dashboard. This specific key is used when the count of affected dashboards is exactly one.
save-library-panel-modal.num-affected_other_description: Informative message in the Save Library Panel modal: Notifies the user about the total number of dashboards that will be affected by saving changes to a library panel. This variant is used when the count of affected dashboards is greater than one.
save-library-panel-modal.placeholder-search-affected-dashboards_d: Placeholder text in a search input field within the 'Save Library Panel' modal: It prompts users to search for dashboards that will be impacted by saving the current library panel.
save-library-panel-modal.update-all_description: Button in the Save Library Panel modal: Confirms the action to update all dashboard instances that use the library panel being saved with the latest changes.

share.config-alert-description: Updating your settings will modify the default copy link to include these changes. Please note that these settings are saved within your current browser scope.
share.config-alert-title_description: Title in the 'Share link' dialog: This title identifies the section where users can adjust the parameters for sharing a link.
share.config-description: Create a personalized, direct link to share your dashboard within your organization, with the following customization settings:
share.copy-link-button_description: Button in the Share link dialog: This button allows users to copy the current dashboard's URL to their clipboard.
share.copy-to-clipboard_description: Confirmation message in the Share dialog: Notifies the user that the requested link, such as a dashboard or panel URL, has been successfully copied to their clipboard.
share.short-url-label_description: Label for a UI control in the link sharing interface: It allows users to shorten the URL that is being prepared for sharing.
share.time-range-description: Change the current relative time range to an absolute time range
share.time-range-description_description: Link in the Share dialog: This link, when activated, changes the current relative time range of the dashboard to an absolute time range. This ensures that the shared view displays data from a fixed time period, regardless of when it is accessed.
share.time-range-label_description: Label for a checkbox or toggle in the Share dialog: This control allows users to lock the currently selected time range when generating a shareable link for a dashboard. Locking the time range ensures that recipients view the dashboard with the exact time range set by the sharer.

share-panel.config-description: Create a personalized, direct link to share your panel within your organization, with the following customization settings:
share-panel.copy-image-link_description: Button in the Share panel: Enables users to copy a direct link to an image. This image typically represents a snapshot of the dashboard or a specific panel, facilitating its sharing.
share-panel.download-image_description: Button in the Share panel: Allows users to download an image representation of the current dashboard or panel.
share-panel.render-image_description: Button in Share Panel: This button allows users to generate or render the current dashboard or a specific visualization as an image, typically for sharing.

dashboard-changed-modal.continue-editing_description: Button in the 'Dashboard Changed' modal dialog: Allows the user to dismiss the modal and continue working on the dashboard without losing unsaved changes.
dashboard-changed-modal.description: The dashboard has been updated by another session. Do you want to continue editing or discard your local changes?
dashboard-changed-modal.discard-local-changes_description: Button in the "Dashboard Changed" modal: Allows the user to discard any unsaved modifications made to the dashboard, reverting it to its last saved state.
dashboard-changed-modal.title-dashboard-changed_description: Title in the 'Dashboard Changed' modal: Notifies the user that the dashboard they are currently viewing or working on has been modified, typically by another user or a background process.

live-channel-editor.description-grafana-live: This supports real-time event streams in Grafana core. This feature is under heavy development. Expect the interfaces and structures to change as this becomes more production ready.
live-channel-editor.namespace_description: Field label in Live Channel Editor: This field allows users to specify the namespace for a live channel, defining its logical grouping or scope within the system.
live-channel-editor.path_description: Field label in the Live Channel Editor: Specifies the file, URL, or resource path that the live channel is configured to monitor or stream from.
live-channel-editor.placeholder-select-watchable-resource_de: Placeholder text in the Live Channel Editor: To prompt the user to select an entity or item that can be monitored or viewed in the live channel. This text appears before a resource has been chosen.
live-channel-editor.scope_description: Label in the Live Channel Editor: This setting defines the parameters or boundaries for the data and events that the live channel will process or broadcast.
live-channel-editor.title-grafana-live_description: Title in the Live Channel Editor: Identifies the Grafana Live feature or service being configured or displayed.

live-panel.error_description: Text label in the Live Panel: Indicates that a general error has occurred during the display or fetching of live data within the panel.
live-panel.grafana-requires-feature: Grafana live requires a feature flag to run
live-panel.grafana-requires-feature_description: Informational text in the Live Panel: This message informs the user that the Grafana Live feature requires a specific feature flag to be enabled to function. It explains why the feature might not be available or running.
live-panel.panel-editor-channel: Use the panel editor to pick a channel
live-panel.panel-editor-channel_description: Instructional text in the Live Panel editor: Guides the user to select a channel for the Live Panel.
live-panel.title-grafana-live_description: Panel Title in a live panel within BMC Helix Dashboards: Identifies the panel as displaying content or information related to the Grafana Live feature, which handles real-time data streaming.
live-panel.waiting-for-data_description: Status message in Live Panel: Informs the user that the system is currently loading or streaming data and is waiting for it to be received before displaying it.

live-publish.publish_description: Button in the Live Publish section: Used to make changes or content live and available. This action finalizes and deploys data or updates in real-time.

lock-icon_description: Icon in the UI: Represents the visual graphic of a lock, typically indicating security, privacy, or a locked state for an item or feature.

divider.connecting-text_description: Conjunction text in the login screen: It separates alternative login methods or actions, typically appearing alongside a visual divider. The ellipsis in the Spanish translation suggests it may precede the second option.

error.blocked: You have exceeded the number of login attempts for this user. Please try again later.
error.invalid-user-or-password_description: Error message in Login screen: Informs the user that the entered username or password combination is incorrect. It appears when authentication fails.
error.title: Login failed
error.title_description: Heading text in the login error dialog or screen: Informs the user that their login attempt was unsuccessful.
error.unknown_description: Error message in the Login screen: Informs the user that an unspecified error occurred during their login attempt. This is a generic fallback message when a more specific error cannot be identified.

login.forgot-password_description: Link in the Login screen: Provides an option for users to initiate the password recovery process if they have forgotten their password.

form.confirmation-code_description: Validation message in the confirmation code field of the login form: Appears when the user attempts to submit the form without providing the required code. It explicitly states that the confirmation code is a mandatory input.
form.confirmation-code-label_description: Label for an input field in the login form: Prompts the user to enter a code for identity verification.
form.confirmation-code-placeholder_descriptio: Placeholder text in the login form: Instructs the user to enter a received confirmation code, typically for multi-factor authentication or account verification.
form.email-required_description: Error message in the login form: Displayed when a user attempts to submit the login form without entering an email address. It indicates that the email field is a mandatory requirement for login.
form.password-required_description: Validation message in the login form: Informs the user that the password field is mandatory and cannot be left empty during login.
form.submit-label_description: Button in the login form: Submits the user's entered credentials to log in to the application.
form.username-label_description: Label in the Login form: Prompts the user to enter their email address or username for authentication.
form.username-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Login form: Prompts the user to enter their email address or username for login.
form.username-required_description: Validation message in the login form: Informs the user that the "Email or username" input field is mandatory. This message appears when the field is left empty upon submission or focus-out.
form.verify-email-label_description: Button/Link in the Login form: Initiates the process of sending a verification email to the user. This is typically used for account verification or password reset scenarios.

layout.update-password_description: Heading in the login layout: This text serves as a title or label for the section or page where a user can update their password.

services.sing-in-with-prefix_description: **Button** on the login page, within the 'services' section: Allows users to authenticate via a specified external identity provider. The `{{serviceName}}` placeholder will be dynamically replaced with the name of the service (e.g., "Google", "Azure AD").

signup.button-label_description: Button in the Sign Up section of the login flow: Triggers the user registration process to create a new account.
signup.new-to-question_description: Question in the signup screen: Asks users if they are new to Grafana. This question likely helps tailor the onboarding experience or provides further introductory information.

logs.category-logs_description: Section Title in the Logs management interface: Designates the primary area for viewing, searching, and managing log data. This label serves as the main category for all log-related functionalities.

deduplication-options.label-exact_description: Label for an option in the log deduplication settings: This option allows users to select exact matching as the criterion for identifying and removing duplicate log entries.
deduplication-options.label-none_description: Option in Deduplication Options for Logs: Indicates that no deduplication method is applied to the logs.
deduplication-options.label-numbers_description: Option label in log deduplication settings: This option allows users to specify or configure how numerical values are considered during the log deduplication process.
deduplication-options.label-signature_description: Label in deduplication options for logs: This label refers to a method or field used for grouping similar log messages based on their unique signature. It helps in reducing the volume of displayed logs by showing only distinct log patterns.

logs.description-enable-infinite-scrolling: Experimental. Request more results by scrolling to the bottom of the logs list.
logs.description-enable-logs-highlighting: Use a predefined coloring scheme to highlight relevant parts of the log lines
logs.description-enable-logs-highlighting_des: Description in the Logs section: Explains a feature that applies a predefined coloring scheme to highlight relevant parts of log lines, aiding in quick identification of important information.
logs.description-show-controls: Display controls to jump to the last or first log line, and filters by log level
logs.description-show-controls_description: Descriptive text in the Log viewer: Informs users about the actions available when specific controls are displayed, such as navigating log lines and filtering by log level.
logs.description-show-log-attributes: Experimental. When OTel logs are displayed, add an extra displayed field with relevant key-value pairs from labels and metadata.

field-selector.aria-label-drag-and-drop-icon_descriptio: Icon in the field selector within the Logs section: An accessibility label for an icon that indicates an element can be dragged and dropped.
field-selector.clear-button_description: Button in the Field Selector of the Logs view: Clears any selected fields or applied filter criteria. This resets the field selection to its default state.
field-selector.collapse_description: Button in the Logs Field Selector: Collapses a sidebar, likely the field selector panel, to free up screen space. It helps optimize the display for viewing log data.
field-selector.expand_description: Button in the Field Selector section of the Logs view: Expands the sidebar to reveal field selection options.
field-selector.label-title_description: Label in Field Selector in Logs View: Displays the percentage of log lines that contain a specific field. This dynamically informs the user about the prevalence of a selected field across the logs.
field-selector.placeholder-search-fields-by-name_descri: Search input field placeholder in the Field Selector component within the Logs section: Guides users to search for log fields by entering their names.
field-selector.title-drag-and-drop-to-reorder_descripti: Title in the field selector area within the Logs section of BMC Helix Dashboards: This text instructs users that they can drag and drop fields to reorder them. It typically appears above a list of fields whose display order can be customized.

loki.indexed-label_one_description: Field label in the Loki log field type settings: This text identifies a log field as an "Indexed label," signifying that its values are stored in Loki's index for efficient and optimized querying.
loki.indexed-label_other_description: Indexed label option in Loki log field type settings: Specifies a type of log label that is indexed for efficient querying and filtering.
loki.parsedl-label_one_description: Parsed field in Loki log fields: Refers to a data field that has been extracted or processed from raw log entries originating from Loki. It allows users to filter and analyze specific information from their logs.
loki.parsedl-label_other_description: Label in Loki log fields: Identifies a field that has been extracted or structured from raw log data. This label is typically used for a generic parsed field or one that doesn't fit a more specific category.
loki.structured-metadata_one_description: Label in Loki log field type configuration: Identifies settings or options pertaining to structured data elements found within Loki logs.
loki.structured-metadata_other_description: A field type option in Loki log field type configuration: Specifies that a log field contains structured metadata from Loki. This helps in correctly parsing and querying structured log data.

font-size-options.label-default_description: Option label in the font size options for log display: Represents the standard, pre-defined font size for log entries.
font-size-options.label-small_description: Option in the font size settings within the Logs view: Allows users to select "Small" as the display font size for log entries.

text.labels_description: A field label or section heading within the text variable configuration for dataframe fields in the Logs feature, referring to descriptive tags or identifiers for log data.

infinite-scroll.end-of-range: End of the selected time range.
infinite-scroll.end-of-range_description: Status message in the Logs view's infinite scroll area: Informs the user that no more logs can be loaded because they have reached the end (oldest point) of the currently selected time range.
infinite-scroll.load-more_description: Instructional text in the Logs viewer with infinite scrolling: Prompts the user to scroll down to view additional log entries.
infinite-scroll.older-logs_description: Button or link label in the infinite scroll log viewer: Used to fetch and display previous, chronologically older log entries.

log-context-buttons.center-matched-line_description: Button in log context buttons: Centers a specific log line that has been identified or "matched" (e.g., through a search or filter) within the log viewer. This action helps users quickly navigate to and focus on relevant log entries.
log-context-buttons.label-wrap-lines_description: Label for a control in the log viewer's context buttons: This option allows users to toggle whether long log lines are automatically wrapped to fit the display area or require horizontal scrolling.

log-details.fields_description: Section Header in the log details view: This heading introduces the area displaying the structured attributes and key-value pairs of a selected log entry.
log-details.links_description: Label in the Log Details view: Indicates a section or feature where users can find related links or actions pertaining to the selected log entry.
log-details.log-line_description: Label in Log Details View: This string refers to an individual line of log data. It is used to display or identify a single record within a log entry.
log-details.no-details_description: Informational message in the Log Details section: Informs the user that specific details for a selected log entry or log context are not available for display. It indicates an absence of further granular information.

toggle-field-button.tooltip-hide-log-line_description: Button tooltip in the Log Details view: This text informs the user that clicking the associated button will hide the currently displayed log line.
toggle-field-button.tooltip-show-log-line_description: Tooltip for a button in the log details body section: Appears on hover to indicate that the associated button will show or expand a specific log line.

log-labels.collapse_description: Action Button in the log labels section: Collapses the currently displayed log labels, typically to save screen space or reduce visual clutter.
log-labels.expand_description: Button in the 'Log Labels' section within the Logs view: Allows users to expand and reveal hidden or truncated log labels, providing more details.

log-line.has-error_description: Filter label in the logs analysis interface: Allows users to filter and display only log lines that contain errors.
log-line.is-sampled_description: Label or status indicator in a log line within the logs view: Identifies whether the displayed log entry was included in a data sampling process.
log-line.show-less_description: Button in a log line within the logs viewer: Allows the user to collapse expanded details of a log entry, reverting to a more condensed view.
log-line.show-more_description: Link in a log line within the logs viewer: Allows users to expand a truncated log line to view its complete content.
log-line.tooltip-error_description: Error message in a tooltip on a log line within the Logs view: Informs the user about an error related to that specific log entry. The `{{errorMessage}}` placeholder will be dynamically populated with the actual error details.

log-line-context.center-matched-line_description: Button in the log line context view: This button centers the currently matched log line within the log viewer's display. This allows users to easily focus on the relevant log entry.
log-line-context.newer-logs_description: Button label in the log line context viewer: Indicates an option to display log entries that occurred more recently than the currently focused log line.
log-line-context.no-more-logs-available_description: Informational message in the log line context view: Informs the user that no additional log entries can be loaded or found beyond the currently displayed data. This appears when the end of available logs has been reached, often during scrolling or pagination.
log-line-context.older-logs_description: Button/Link text in the log line context view: Used to display log entries that occurred prior to the currently selected log line. This allows users to review the historical context.
log-line-context.open-in-split-view_description: Menu item in the context menu for a log line: Opens the selected log line's details or a related view in a split panel, enabling side-by-side analysis.
log-line-context.time-window-label_description: Label in the log line context view within the logs section: This label identifies the time range used to display surrounding log entries as context for a selected log line.
log-line-context.time-window-tooltip: Amount of time before and after the referenced log
log-line-context.time-window-tooltip_description: Tooltip for a time window setting in the Log Line Context section: Clarifies that the associated setting determines the time duration to be displayed both before and after a specific, referenced log entry.
log-line-context.title-log-context_description: Title in the Log Line Context view/panel: Identifies the section that displays contextual information for a specific log entry.
log-line-context.title-log-line_description: Title in the log line context view within the Logs section: Identifies the specific log line that is currently being referenced or viewed in detail.

log-line-details.clear-search_description: Button in the log-line-details view: Clears any search filters or terms that have been applied to narrow down the log view.
log-line-details.close_description: Button in the log line details view within the logs section: Closes the currently displayed detailed view of a specific log entry.
log-line-details.copy-shortlink_description: Button in the Log Line Details view: Allows users to quickly copy a direct, shareable link to the specific log line they are currently viewing. This shortlink can be used for easy sharing or quick navigation back to the particular log entry.
log-line-details.copy-to-clipboard_description: Button in the Log line details view: Allows the user to copy the selected log line content to their clipboard.
log-line-details.displayed-fields-section_description: Button in the displayed fields section of the log line details view: Allows users to customize the order and visibility of log fields for a log entry.

fields.adhoc-statistics_description: Label in the Log line details view, within the fields section: Refers to statistics that are generated dynamically or on-demand for specific fields of a log line. These statistics provide quick, temporary insights into the data without needing predefined reports.
fields.copy-value-to-clipboard_description: Action label in the fields section of a log line details view: Allows users to copy the displayed value of a specific log field to their clipboard.
fields.filter-out_description: Option in the fields section of log line details in the Logs viewer: Allows users to exclude the selected field value from the displayed logs.
fields.filter-out-query_description: Action link in Log Line Details for a field: Allows users to modify the current log query by excluding a specific field value from the search results. The `{{query}}` placeholder shows the query with the exclusion applied.

toggle-field-button.field-instead-message: Show this field instead of the message
toggle-field-button.field-instead-message_description: Menu item label in the log line details view, appearing as an option within or next to a field's toggle button. It enables the user to display the content of that specific field in the main log message column, replacing the default log message.
toggle-field-button.hide-this-field_description: Button text in the Log Line Details view: This text labels a button that allows users to hide a specific field from the displayed log line details.

log-line-details.fields-section_description: Section Heading in the Log Line Details view: Labels the dedicated area where users can examine the individual fields and their values extracted from a specific log line.
log-line-details.hide-log-line_description: Button/Link in the log line details view or context menu: Allows users to hide the currently selected log line from the displayed logs. This helps in temporary filtering of log entries.
log-line-details.inline-mode_description: Toggle option in the log line details view: Controls whether the log line details are expanded and displayed directly within the main log view, rather than in a separate pop-up or panel.
log-line-details.link-value-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the log line details view within the Logs section: This tooltip appears on hover over a clickable element. It indicates that the hovered item is a "Link value" that can be interacted with.
log-line-details.links-section_description: Section title in the Log Line Details view: Indicates a section containing links that provide additional context or actions related to the specific log entry.
log-line-details.log-attributes-field_description: Field label in the Log Line Details view: Identifies the section displaying OpenTelemetry (OTel) attributes associated with a specific log entry. These attributes provide additional contextual information.
log-line-details.log-line-field_description: Label in the Log Line Details section: Identifies the field or area displaying the complete text content of a selected log entry.
log-line-details.log-line-section_description: Section title in the log line details view: This title introduces the detailed information displayed for a specific log entry.
log-line-details.move-displayed-field-down_description: Button in the Log Line Details view: Moves a selected log field down one position in its display order. This allows users to customize the field arrangement within the log line.
log-line-details.move-displayed-field-up_description: Button in the Log Line Details view: Reorders the display of fields by moving a selected field to a higher position.
log-line-details.no-details_description: Informative message in the log line details view: Appears when a selected log line does not have any parseable or associated fields to display.
log-line-details.open-assistant_description: Button in Log Line Details screen: Allows users to send the selected log line to an AI Assistant for an explanation or further analysis.
log-line-details.pin-line_description: Button in the log line details view: Allows users to save or mark a specific log line for future reference or quick access.
log-line-details.remove-displayed-field_description: Button in the Log Line Details view: Allows users to remove a currently displayed field. This action helps customize the visible fields shown for a log entry.
log-line-details.remove-log_description: Button in the log line details view: Used to delete the specific log line currently being viewed.
log-line-details.scroll-to-logline_description: A button or link in the Log Line Details view: Allows the user to scroll the display directly to a specific log line, often when navigating from a different context to a particular log entry.

search.no-results_description: Message in the search results area of the log line details view: Informs the user that their performed search query did not return any matching logs or data.

log-line-details.search-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Log Line Details view: Informs users that they can search for both field names and their values within the log line details.
log-line-details.show-context_description: Button in the Log Line Details view: Displays additional log lines surrounding the selected log entry to provide more context.
log-line-details.show-log-line_description: Button or link in the log line details view: Displays the full content of a specific log line, potentially expanding a truncated view.
log-line-details.sidebar-mode_description: Option in the Log Line Details view's sidebar mode settings: Configures the position of the details sidebar. Selecting this option anchors the sidebar to the right side of the screen.

trace.error-message_description: Error message in the trace details section of a log line: Informs the user that the system was unable to retrieve the trace information associated with the selected log entry. This message is displayed when the trace data cannot be fetched or processed.
trace.error-tooltip: The trace could have been sampled or be temporarily unavailable.
trace.error-tooltip_description: Error Tooltip in the Trace section of the Log Line Details view: Informs the user why an expected trace might not be fully displayed or accessible. It indicates that the trace data could have been sampled or is temporarily unavailable.

log-line-details.trace-section_description: Section title in the log line details view: Displays distributed trace information related to the selected log line.
log-line-details.unpin-line_description: Action in the Log Line Details view: Removes the 'pinned' status from a specific log entry. This action typically unhighlights or unsticks a log line that was previously marked for special attention or persistent display.

log-line-menu.copy-link_description: Menu item in the log line context menu: Allows users to copy a direct link (URL) to a specific log entry for sharing or reference.
log-line-menu.copy-log_description: Menu item in the context menu for a log line: Allows users to copy the text content of that specific log line to their clipboard.
log-line-menu.hide-details_description: Menu item in a menu associated with a log line in the logs view: Allows the user to display additional, detailed information for a selected log entry. This option typically appears when the log details are currently collapsed or hidden.
log-line-menu.icon-label_description: Label for an icon on a log line within the logs viewer: It identifies the menu that provides actions or options related to that specific log entry.
log-line-menu.log-line_description: Label in the Log Line context menu: Identifies a single line of log data. This string indicates the specific log entry being focused on for actions or details.
log-line-menu.log-line-explainer: Explain log line in a concise way
log-line-menu.log-line-explainer_description: Menu item in the Log Line Context Menu: Allows users to get a concise explanation of a specific log line. This feature helps users quickly understand the meaning or context of a log entry.
log-line-menu.open-assistant_description: Menu item in Log line context menu: Allows users to send a specific log line to the AI-powered Assistant for explanation or analysis. This action facilitates deeper understanding of log events directly within the dashboard.
log-line-menu.pin-to-outline_description: Menu item in the log line context menu: Used to pin a specific log line to the log outline. This action makes the log entry easily accessible for later review or highlights it in a summary.
log-line-menu.show-context_description: Menu item in the log line context menu within the Logs viewer: Displays additional log entries surrounding a selected log line to provide more situational context, helping users understand related events.
log-line-menu.show-details_description: Menu item in the log line context menu: Used to hide the expanded details of a log entry. This option is displayed when log details are currently visible, allowing the user to collapse the detailed view.
log-line-menu.unpin-from-outline_description: Menu item in the context menu displayed for a log line within the logs view: Allows the user to remove a specific log entry that was previously 'pinned' or saved to an outline/summary.

log-list-search.close_description: Button in the log list search interface: Closes or dismisses the active search input field or results pane.
log-list-search.filter_description: Action text in the Log List Search view within the Logs section: This text indicates the action to apply filters to the displayed log list, showing only entries that match specific criteria.
log-list-search.info: Client-side search for strings within the displayed logs. Not to be confused with query filters. Use this component to search for specific strings in your log results.
log-list-search.input-placeholder_description: Input field placeholder in the log list search area: Instructs users to enter search queries to filter the displayed logs.
log-list-search.next_description: Button in the log list search interface: Allows users to navigate to the next matching log entry found during a search.
log-list-search.prev_description: Button in the log list search interface: Allows users to navigate to the previous search result among the logs displayed.

log-row-context-modal.error-loading-log-more-logs_description: Error Message in the log row context modal: Notifies the user that an attempt to load additional log entries, beyond those initially displayed, has failed.
log-row-context-modal.no-more-logs-available_description: Informational message in the Log Row Context Modal: Informs the user that there are no further log entries or context to display for the selected log row.
log-row-context-modal.open-in-split-view_description: Menu item in the context menu for a log row: Allows users to open the selected log row or its related data in a split view. This enables side-by-side comparison or detailed analysis.
log-row-context-modal.title-log-context_description: Title in the Log context modal: Introduces the content of a modal that displays contextual information for a selected log entry.

log-row-menu-cell.aria-label-copy-shortlink_description: Menu item in the log row menu cell: Allows users to copy a shortlink. This shortlink typically points to the specific log entry or a filtered view containing it.
log-row-menu-cell.aria-label-pin-line_description: Menu item in the context menu of a log row in the Logs viewer: This option allows users to "pin" a specific log line. This action typically highlights the log entry for easier reference or further investigation.
log-row-menu-cell.aria-label-show-context_description: Action item within the menu of a log row in the logs viewer: Allows users to view the surrounding log entries or related information for the selected log.
log-row-menu-cell.aria-label-unpin-line_description: Menu item in the log row context menu: Used to unpin a log line that was previously marked for special attention or persistent display.
log-row-menu-cell.tooltip-copy-shortlink_description: Tooltip text for an action in the contextual menu of a log row: Informs the user that clicking the associated element will copy a short URL. This shortlink typically points to the specific log entry for easy sharing.
log-row-menu-cell.tooltip-copy-to-clipboard_description: Tooltip in a log row menu cell: This text appears on hover to describe an action that copies log content to the user's clipboard.
log-row-menu-cell.tooltip-show-context_description: Menu item in the log row menu cell: Allows the user to display the surrounding context or related information for a specific log entry.
log-row-menu-cell.tooltip-unpin-line_description: Tooltip in the log row menu cell within the logs view: This tooltip appears when hovering over an action, indicating that clicking it will unpin a specific log line that was previously pinned or highlighted.

log-row-message.ellipsis_description: Ellipsis character ("…") in log message rows within the Logs view: Signifies that the displayed log message text is truncated. This indicates that more content is available, typically viewable upon expansion.
log-row-message.more_description: Link in a log row message within the Logs view: To expand and display the full, truncated content of a log message.
log-row-message.see-details_description: Link in a log row message: This link allows users to view more detailed information about a specific log entry.
log-row-message.tooltip-error_description: Error message tooltip in a log row message within the Logs section: This tooltip displays a specific error message, `{{errorMessage}}`, when a problem is encountered related to that log entry.

disable-popover.message: You are about to disable the logs filter menu. To re-enable it, select text in a log line while holding the alt key.
disable-popover.title: Disable menu
disable-popover.title_description: Title of a popover in the Logs view's log rows section: This title indicates that the popover's function is to disable a menu.

disable-popover-message.shortcut_description: Shortcut instruction in a message displayed when a popover related to log rows is disabled: It guides the user on how to re-enable the popover using the `Alt+select` keyboard shortcut.

label.total-bytes-processed_description: Label in a log processing metrics display: Indicates the total amount of log data that has been processed or ingested by the system.

timestamp-resolution.label-milliseconds_description: Label in the "Timestamp Resolution" setting within the Logs view: This option allows users to select millisecond precision for displaying timestamps.
timestamp-resolution.label-nanoseconds_description: Selectable option in the Timestamp Resolution settings within the Logs feature: Allows users to specify nanoseconds as the desired precision for log timestamps.

logs-controls.collapse_description: Button in the Logs controls section: Used to collapse a related UI element or panel, typically to save screen space or hide details within the Logs view.
logs-controls.deduplication_description: Control in the log controls section: Manages the deduplication of log entries. This setting allows users to display unique log events and consolidate identical ones.
logs-controls.disable-prettify-json_description: Control label in the Logs controls section: Allows users to collapse JSON log entries. This disables the prettified (expanded) view, making logs appear in a more compact format.
logs-controls.display-level-all_description: Option in the Logs Controls section: This option allows users to view log entries of all available severity levels, without applying any filtering by log type or severity.
logs-controls.download_description: Button in the Logs Controls section of the Logs view. This button allows users to download the logs currently displayed or selected for offline access.

download-logs.csv_description: File format option in the 'Download Logs' section: This option allows users to select CSV as the format for exporting log data. It represents the Comma Separated Values file format.
download-logs.json_description: Download Format Option in the "Download Logs" section: Enables users to select JSON as the format for downloading log data.
download-logs.txt_description: Format option in the 'Download Logs' section within the Logs Controls: Specifies the plain text (.txt) format for the log file download.

logs-controls.escape-newlines: Fix incorrectly escaped newline and tab sequences in log lines
logs-controls.escape-newlines_description: Checkbox in the Log Controls section within the Logs view: Enables or disables the automatic correction of improperly escaped newline and tab sequences. This feature helps improve the readability of log lines.
logs-controls.expand_description: Button in the Logs Controls section: Allows users to reveal additional details or options within the logs view. This action typically makes content visible that was previously hidden or collapsed.
logs-controls.filter-levels_description: Control in the Logs controls section: Allows users to filter displayed log entries by their severity levels (e.g., Info, Warning, Error).
logs-controls.font-large_description: Option in the Logs Controls section: Allows users to change the font size of the displayed logs to a larger setting. This control helps improve readability for users who prefer larger text.
logs-controls.font-small_description: Option in the Logs Controls section: Allows users to set the display font size of log entries to small.
logs-controls.hide-search_description: Button in the Logs section search controls: Hides or collapses the active search input field or panel within the logs view.
logs-controls.hide-timestamps_description: Checkbox in the log display controls: Allows users to hide the timestamps displayed alongside log entries. This helps simplify the log view.
logs-controls.hide-unique-labels_description: Option in the Logs controls section: Controls the visibility of unique labels in the log view. Activating it hides these labels to simplify the display.

label.collapse_description: Label in the Logs Controls section: This text indicates that an associated content section is currently expanded. It likely functions as a toggle to collapse the section.
label.disable-highlighting_description: Label in the Logs viewer control panel: This label is associated with a control (likely a checkbox or toggle) that enables or disables text highlighting in the displayed log entries. The key 'disable-highlighting' suggests it manages the state of this feature.
label.enable-highlighting_description: Option label in the log highlighting settings: Selects a display mode for logs where no special formatting or visual highlighting is applied. This option provides a raw text view, often as an alternative to other syntax or keyword highlighting modes.
label.escape-newlines_description: Label in the Logs Controls section: This option allows users to escape newline characters within log data for display or processing. This affects how multi-line log entries are rendered.
label.expand_description: Label for an expand/collapse control within the Logs Controls section: It indicates that the associated UI element is currently in a collapsed state, ready to be expanded.

labels.font-large_description: Option label in Logs controls: Indicates an option to set the display font size to large. This is typically found within text or display settings for log views.
labels.font-small_description: Label in Logs Controls: Specifies the option to use a small font size for displaying logs.
labels.hide-search_description: Button in the logs controls section: Used to hide the search input field or close the active search interface within the logs viewer.
labels.newest-first_description: Label for a sorting option in the Logs Controls section: Indicates that log entries will be displayed with the most recent logs appearing first. This allows users to prioritize current activity.
labels.oldest-first_description: Label in Logs controls: Indicates an option to sort log entries, displaying the oldest entries at the beginning of the list.
labels.show-search_description: Label for a control in the Logs section's controls: Used to initiate or display log search functionality.

line-wrapping.enable_description: Checkbox in Line Wrapping settings within Logs Controls: Enables or disables the wrapping of long lines in log entries.
line-wrapping.enable-prettify: Enable line wrapping and prettify JSON
line-wrapping.enable-prettify_description: Toggle in the Line Wrapping section of the Log Controls for logs: Enables line wrapping for log entries and prettifies JSON content, making complex log data easier to read.
line-wrapping.hide_description: Checkbox in the Line Wrapping section within the Logs controls: Allows users to disable the automatic wrapping of long log entries, forcing them to display on a single line.

state.hide_description: State label in the Line Wrapping controls within the Logs view: This text indicates that the line wrapping feature for log entries is currently disabled.
state.json_description: Toggle label in the log line wrapping controls: Specifies the option to enable or disable wrapping of JSON content within log entries.
state.wrap_description: Checkbox in Line Wrapping settings under Logs Controls: Toggles whether log lines wrap within the display area or extend horizontally.

line-wrapping.tooltip_description: Tooltip on the line wrapping control within the Logs controls section: Explains that this control enables or disables line wrapping for displayed log entries.

logs-controls.newest-first: Sorted by newest logs first - Click to show oldest first
logs-controls.newest-first_description: A clickable label in the logs controls section of the logs view: It indicates that logs are currently sorted by newest first and allows the user to switch to sorting by oldest first.
logs-controls.oldest-first: Sorted by oldest logs first - Click to show newest first
logs-controls.oldest-first_description: Sort order toggle in the logs controls section: This element displays the current sort order of log entries (oldest first) and allows the user to reverse the order to newest first by clicking it.
logs-controls.prettify-json_description: Button in Logs controls section: Toggles the display of JSON log entries between a compact and an expanded, formatted view, improving readability.
logs-controls.remove-escaping_description: Option in logs controls: Allows users to remove escape characters from log messages, typically to improve readability or display raw content.
logs-controls.resolution-ms_description: Unit label in the Logs Controls section: Specifies 'milliseconds' as the unit for a time resolution setting.
logs-controls.resolution-ns_description: Unit label in the resolution settings within Logs controls: Indicates nanoseconds as a time unit. This option allows users to define a very fine-grained time resolution for viewing or analyzing log data.
logs-controls.scroll-bottom_description: Button in the Logs Controls section: Allows users to quickly scroll to the bottom of the displayed log entries. This is useful for viewing the latest incoming logs or the end of a long log history.
logs-controls.scroll-top_description: Button in the Logs section: Allows users to quickly navigate back to the top of the displayed log entries. This control is typically found when viewing a long list of logs.
logs-controls.show-search_description: Heading in the Logs results display area: Labels the section or pane that presents the outcomes of a log search query.
logs-controls.show-timestamps_description: Option in the Logs controls section: Controls whether timestamps are displayed alongside log entries in the log viewer.
logs-controls.show-unique-labels_description: Button in the logs control section: Displays only the unique labels found in the log entries. This helps users quickly identify distinct metadata tags.

timestamp.hide_description: Control option in the timestamp controls section of the logs view: Allows users to hide the timestamps displayed alongside log entries.
timestamp.label: Log timestamps
timestamp.label_description: Label for Log timestamps in the Log Controls section, under timestamp settings: This identifies the UI component or settings that allow users to configure or view the timestamps associated with log entries.
timestamp.label-hide_description: Option label in the timestamp controls section of the logs view: Controls the visibility of timestamps displayed in the logs.
timestamp.label-ms_description: Label in the Timestamp settings under Logs Controls: Refers to an option for displaying milliseconds in log timestamps. This setting controls the precision of timestamp visualization.
timestamp.label-ns_description: Label in the timestamp settings within the Logs Controls section: Identifies an option to show or hide nanoseconds in the displayed log timestamps. This label is likely associated with a checkbox or toggle.
timestamp.milliseconds_description: UI control in the Timestamp settings within Logs Controls: This option allows users to enable or disable the display of millisecond precision in log entry timestamps.
timestamp.nanoseconds_description: Toggle switch in the Timestamp controls for Logs: Enables or disables the display of timestamps with nanosecond precision for log entries.
timestamp.tooltip_description: Tooltip for the timestamp control in the Logs Controls section: Explains that users can configure the display format for timestamps within logs.

tooltip.disable-highlighting_description: Tooltip text in the Logs Controls section: This text describes the action of disabling the highlighting feature for logs. It appears when hovering over the control that performs this action.
tooltip.download_description: Tooltip for a download button in the logs controls section of the Logs view: This tooltip, displaying 'Download', indicates that clicking the associated button will download the currently displayed log data.
tooltip.enable-highlighting_description: Tooltip text for a control in the Logs section: Provides a brief explanation for an option to enable highlighting functionality within log entries.
tooltip.filter-level_description: Tooltip in the Logs Controls section within Logs: This tooltip indicates that the associated control is used to filter log results by their severity level.

logs-controls.unwrap-lines_description: Control in Logs viewer controls: Toggles the display of long log messages, preventing them from wrapping to the next line and instead extending horizontally.
logs-controls.wrap-lines_description: Checkbox/Toggle in the logs viewer controls: This option allows users to wrap long log lines to fit the display area. This prevents horizontal scrolling and improves readability.

logs-navigation.newer-logs_description: Navigation item in the logs navigation section: Provides access to a view or filtered display showing the most recent log entries. This helps users monitor the latest system activities.
logs-navigation.older-logs_description: Link in the logs navigation menu: Navigates users to a section or view displaying log entries from an earlier time period.
logs-navigation.scroll-bottom_description: Button in the Logs view: Allows the user to quickly scroll to the end of the log display. This action typically brings the most recent log entries into view.
logs-navigation.scroll-top_description: Button in the Logs navigation pane: Allows users to quickly scroll to the top of the displayed log entries.
logs-navigation.start-of-range_description: Label in the logs navigation/filtering interface: Designates the start point of a selectable date or time range used for filtering or querying log entries. This defines the earliest moment for the data displayed.

render-common-labels.common-labels_description: Label in the Logs Panel: This heading introduces a list or display of labels that are common across the log entries being viewed. The colon implies it's followed by the actual common labels.

logs.name-common-labels_description: A heading in the Logs section: This identifies a group of frequently used or predefined labels associated with log entries. These labels help in organizing and filtering log data.
logs.name-deduplication_description: Label in Log Processing Configuration: Refers to the process or setting for identifying and removing redundant log entries to optimize data management.
logs.name-details-mode_description: Label in the Log details panel: Identifies the setting that controls the display mode of the log details panel.

name-details-options.label-inline_description: Option label in the name-details-options section for logs: This option allows users to choose how details are displayed, with "Inline" indicating they should appear directly within the main view.
name-details-options.label-sidebar_description: Label in the Logs Name Details Options section: This text identifies the collapsible sidebar panel, typically used for navigation, filters, or settings related to the displayed log information.

logs.name-enable-infinite-scrolling_descripti: Checkbox in the Logs interface: Allows users to enable or disable infinite scrolling for log entries. When enabled, more log data loads automatically as the user scrolls down the list.
logs.name-enable-log-details_description: Toggle switch in the Logs section: Allows users to enable or disable the display of additional detailed information for logs.
logs.name-enable-logs-highlighting_descriptio: Toggle switch in Logs display settings: Controls the functionality to highlight specific log entries or patterns. This feature improves readability and helps users quickly identify important information within the log data.
logs.name-font-size_description: Label in the log display settings: Allows users to adjust the font size for the names of log entries or fields.
logs.name-order_description: Label or option in logs sorting settings: Defines the sequential arrangement or direction for ordering log entries, particularly by their names. This element controls how log items are sorted.
logs.name-prettify-json_description: Button in the logs display area: Formats JSON data found in log entries. This makes unformatted JSON content more readable for users.
logs.name-show-controls_description: Button in the logs display area: Toggles the visibility of controls used to filter or customize the display of logs.
logs.name-time_description: Checkbox or toggle in the Logs view display settings: Controls whether timestamps are shown alongside individual log entries.
logs.name-unique-labels_description: Label in the Logs section: This text titles a section or list that displays or refers to distinct labels found within log entries.
logs.name-wrap-lines_description: Label for a checkbox/toggle in the Log Viewer Settings: Controls whether long lines of log data are automatically wrapped within the display area. This feature helps improve readability by preventing horizontal scrolling.

order-options.label-newest-first_description: Label in Logs order options: Specifies a sorting order for log entries. When selected, this option displays the most recent logs at the top.
order-options.label-oldest-first_description: Option label in the logs ordering options: Specifies a sorting preference for log entries. Selecting this option displays the oldest logs at the top of the list.

out-of-range-message.end-of-the-selected-time-range: End of the selected time range.
out-of-range-message.end-of-the-selected-time-range_descripti: Message in the Logs viewer: This text identifies the end boundary of the currently selected time range, typically displayed as part of a message indicating that log data extends beyond this point.

popover-menu.copy_description: Menu item in the Logs popover menu: Allows the user to copy the currently selected log content. This option is enabled when text or log entries are highlighted in the log viewer.
popover-menu.disable-menu_description: Menu item in a popover menu within the logs section: Allows users to disable a menu, either preventing its display or deactivating its functionality.
popover-menu.line-contains_description: Menu item in a popover menu within the Logs viewer: This action allows users to quickly add a new filter to the log display, which checks if a log line contains a specific string. It is typically activated by interacting with a log line or selected text to filter based on its content.
popover-menu.line-contains-not: Add as line does not contain filter
popover-menu.line-contains-not_description: Menu Item in the popover menu within the Logs view: Allows the user to add a filter to the current log query. This filter specifically excludes log lines that contain the selected text or pattern, refining the displayed log data.

logs.show-log-attributes: Display log attributes for OTel logs
logs.show-log-attributes_description: Action in the Logs interface: Enables users to display the detailed attributes specifically for OpenTelemetry (OTel) logs.
logs.timestamp-format_description: The 'Timestamp resolution' setting in the Logs section: Configures the precision or granularity for timestamps associated with log entries. This affects how timestamps are displayed or processed.

un-themed-log-details.aria-label-data-links_description: **Accessible label** in the **log details view**: This label identifies a section or interactive element containing links that provide navigation to related data or further context based on the log entry.
un-themed-log-details.aria-label-fields_description: Accessible label in the un-themed log details view: It identifies the section or panel where the attributes (fields) of a log entry are displayed.
un-themed-log-details.aria-label-line_description: ARIA label in the un-themed log details view: It provides an accessible name for a single line of log output, assisting screen readers.
un-themed-log-details.aria-label-log-level_description: ARIA label in the un-themed log details section: Identifies the severity or type (e.g., INFO, WARN, ERROR) of a specific log entry. This provides an accessible name for screen readers.
un-themed-log-details.aria-label-no-details_description: Informational message in the un-themed log details panel: Informs the user that no further details are available for the selected log entry or in the current view. It serves as an accessibility label for this state.

un-themed-log-details-row.filter-out_description: Action in an un-themed log details row within the Logs viewer: Excludes a specific selected value from the displayed log entries. This is used to narrow down the log view by removing entries containing that value.
un-themed-log-details-row.filter-out-query_description: Contextual action in an un-themed log details row: Allows the user to modify the current log query to exclude a specific value. This action dynamically updates the query to filter out logs containing the selected value.
un-themed-log-details-row.title-copy-value-to-clipboard_descriptio: Button in a log details row in the logs view: Allows users to copy a displayed value from that row to the clipboard.

toggle-field-button.tooltip-field-instead-message: Show this field instead of the message
toggle-field-button.tooltip-field-instead-message_descriptio: Tooltip on a button in an un-themed log details row: This tooltip indicates that clicking the button will replace the default log message display with the content of a specific log field.
toggle-field-button.tooltip-hide-this-field_description: Tooltip on a toggle field button within a log details row in the Logs section: Informs the user that clicking this button will hide the associated log field from the display.

un-themed-log-details-row.tooltip-adhoc-statistics_description: Tooltip in a log details row within the logs section: Indicates the availability or display of ad-hoc statistics for the selected log entry.
un-themed-log-details-row.tooltip-hide-adhoc-statistics_descriptio: Tooltip in a log details row within the logs view: This text describes an action to hide statistics that were dynamically generated or specifically requested for the selected log entry.

un-themed-log-label-stats.field-log-stats_description: Statistical label in the Logs section of BMC Helix Dashboards, within the log field statistics display: It indicates how many log rows contain a specific field out of the total number of rows being analyzed.
un-themed-log-label-stats.label-log-stats_description: Statistic Label in the Log Label Statistics section: Displays the count of log rows that have a specific label ({{label}}) out of the total rows ({{rowCount}}) being analyzed. It provides a quick summary of a label's frequency within log data.

import-dashboard-form.description-existing-library-panels: List of existing library panels. These panels are not affected by the import.

import-dashboard-form.change-uid_description: Button in the dashboard import form: Allows the user to change the Unique Identifier (UID) of the dashboard being imported. This option is useful for preventing conflicts with existing dashboards or assigning a custom UID.
import-dashboard-form.description-library-panels-imported: List of new library panels that will get imported.
import-dashboard-form.description-library-panels-imported_desc: Informational text in the 'Import Dashboard' form: Explains that the following list contains new library panels that will be imported into the system. This helps the user understand the scope of the import beyond just the dashboard itself.
import-dashboard-form.description-unique-identifier-uid: The unique identifier (UID) of a dashboard can be used for uniquely identify a dashboard between multiple Grafana installs. The UID allows having consistent URLs for accessing dashboards so changing the title of a dashboard will not break any bookmarked links to that dashboard.
import-dashboard-form.label-existing-library-panels_descriptio: Label in the "Import Dashboard" form within the "Manage Dashboards" section: This label introduces a section or field that pertains to library panels that already exist, likely offering options for handling them during the dashboard import process.
import-dashboard-form.label-folder_description: Label in the Import Dashboard Form within Manage Dashboards: This indicates the field where the user selects or specifies the destination folder for the dashboard being imported.
import-dashboard-form.label-new-library-panels_description: Label in the Import Dashboard form: Identifies a section or group of panels that are new to the library and are being imported as part of the dashboard.
import-dashboard-form.label-unique-identifier-uid_description: Label in the Import Dashboard form within the Manage Dashboards section: This text labels the input field where a user provides or views the unique identifier (UID) for a dashboard being imported.
import-dashboard-form.options_description: Section header in the Import Dashboard Form within the Manage Dashboards section: This element introduces a group of configurable settings or choices related to the dashboard import process.

import-dashboard-overview-un-connected.importing-from_description: Label in the dashboard import overview screen: It indicates the source from which a dashboard is being imported. The text within the `<2>` tag specifies the particular source, Grafana.com, in this instance.
import-dashboard-overview-un-connected.published-by_description: Label in the Import Dashboard Overview screen for 'un-connected' dashboards: It identifies the user or entity who originally published the imported dashboard.
import-dashboard-overview-un-connected.updated-on_description: Label in the 'Import Dashboard Overview' section for unconnected dashboards: This label precedes a date indicating when a specific imported, yet unconnected, dashboard was last updated.

snapshot-list-table.body-delete: Are you sure you want to delete '{{snapshotToRemove}}'?
snapshot-list-table.body-delete_description: Confirmation dialog message in the snapshot list table within the Manage Dashboards section: This message prompts the user to confirm the deletion of a specific dashboard snapshot. The placeholder `{{snapshotToRemove}}` is dynamically replaced with the name of the snapshot being deleted.

text.import-dashboard_description: Button in the Dashboard Import screen: Initiates the process of importing an external dashboard configuration into the system.

validation-srv.message-name-required_description: Validation message in a dashboard creation or editing form: This message indicates that the 'Name' field is mandatory and must be populated before saving or proceeding.
validation-srv.message-reserved-name: This is a reserved name and cannot be used for a folder.
validation-srv.message-reserved-name_description: Validation error message displayed when creating or renaming a folder in the "Manage Dashboards" interface: Informs the user that the entered name is a reserved name and cannot be used for a folder.
validation-srv.message-same-name: A dashboard or a folder with the same name already exists
validation-srv.message-same-name_description: Validation error message in the dashboard management interface: Informs the user that the chosen name for a new or renamed dashboard or folder is already taken. This message prevents duplicate names for dashboards or folders.
validation-srv.message-same-name-current-folder: A dashboard or a folder with the same name already exists in the current folder
validation-srv.message-same-name-current-folder_descrip: Validation message in Dashboard management: Informs the user that they cannot create or rename a dashboard or folder due to a naming conflict. An item with the same name already exists in the current directory.
validation-srv.message-same-name-general: A folder or dashboard with the same name already exists in the root folder
validation-srv.message-same-name-general_description: Validation message in the 'Manage Dashboards' section: Appears when a user attempts to create or rename a dashboard or folder with a name that already exists within the root folder, preventing duplicate entries.

metric-select.noOptionsMessage-no-options-found_descri: Message in the metric selection component: It is displayed when no selectable options are found for the user within a dropdown or list. This indicates that no items match the current criteria or are available.

build-snapshot.description: This tool can migrate some resources from this installation to your cloud stack. To get started, you'll need to create a snapshot of this installation. Creating a snapshot typically takes less than two minutes. The snapshot is stored alongside this Grafana installation.
build-snapshot.title: No snapshot exists
build-snapshot.title_description: Status message in the 'Build Snapshot' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Informs the user that no snapshot currently exists. This message likely appears when the user expects to view or interact with existing snapshots.
build-snapshot.when-complete: Once the snapshot is complete, you will be able to upload it to your cloud stack.
build-snapshot.when-complete_description: Informational message in the 'Build Snapshot' workflow: This message appears upon the successful completion of a snapshot build, instructing the user on the next available action, which is uploading it to their cloud stack.

building-snapshot.description: We're gathering your resources for migration to Grafana Cloud. This should only take a moment.
building-snapshot.description-eta: Creating a snapshot typically takes less than two minutes.
building-snapshot.description-eta_description: Informational text in the 'Building Snapshot' section within the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Informs the user about the estimated time required for the snapshot creation process. This helps set user expectations during the migration.
building-snapshot.title: Building snapshot
building-snapshot.title_description: Title on the 'Building snapshot' screen within the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Indicates that a snapshot is currently being created as an ongoing process.

can-i-move.body: You can use the migration assistant to migrate a large proportion of your Grafana resources.
can-i-move.body_description: Body text in the 'Can I move?' section of the Migrate to Cloud workflow: It describes the capability of the migration assistant for moving Grafana resources. It highlights that a large proportion of resources can be migrated.
can-i-move.link-title: Learn about migrating to Grafana Cloud
can-i-move.link-title_description: Link in the 'Can I Move?' section within the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Guides users to information about migrating to Grafana Cloud.
can-i-move.title: Where can I learn more about migrating to Grafana Cloud?
can-i-move.title_description: Title in the "Migrate to Cloud" feature, within a section addressing user inquiries or guiding them through the migration process. It serves as a heading or question to prompt users to seek further information about migrating to Grafana Cloud.

configure-snapshot.description: Select which resources you want to include in the snapshot to migrate.
configure-snapshot.description_description: Description in the Configure Snapshot screen during the cloud migration setup: It guides the user to select which resources will be included in the snapshot intended for migration. This snapshot is a crucial preparatory step before migrating to the cloud.
configure-snapshot.description-sub-line: Some resources may depend on others and will be automatically selected or deselected.
configure-snapshot.description-sub-line_description: Informative text in the 'Configure Snapshot' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Informs the user about resource dependencies during snapshot configuration. It clarifies that selecting or deselecting some resources may automatically affect others.
configure-snapshot.resource-include-all_description: Option in the 'Configure Snapshot' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Used to select all available resources to be included in the snapshot.
configure-snapshot.title: Configure snapshot
configure-snapshot.title_description: Title of the "Configure snapshot" page or section within the "Migrate to Cloud" feature: Informs the user they are defining the settings for a data snapshot.

connect-modal.body-cloud-stack: You'll also need a cloud stack. If you just signed up, we'll automatically create your first stack. If you have an account, you'll need to select or create a stack.
connect-modal.body-get-started: To get started, you'll need a Grafana.com account.
connect-modal.body-sign-up: Sign up for a Grafana.com account
connect-modal.body-sign-up_description: Instructional text in the Connect modal of the Migrate to Cloud section: It prompts users to sign up for a Grafana.com account. This is likely a prerequisite for connecting or migrating services.
connect-modal.body-token: Your self-managed Grafana installation needs special access to securely migrate content. You'll need to create a migration token on your chosen cloud stack.
connect-modal.body-token-field_description: Input field in the 'Connect' modal of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Users enter the migration token here. This token is necessary to establish a connection or authorize the migration process.
connect-modal.body-token-field-placeholder_description: Placeholder text for a token input field in the 'Connect' modal of the cloud migration feature: It prompts the user to paste a token required to establish a connection.
