connect-modal.body-token-instructions: Log into your cloud stack and navigate to Administration > General > Migrate to Grafana Cloud. Create a migration token on that screen and paste the token here.
connect-modal.body-view-stacks_description: Button in the 'Connect Modal' of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Allows users to view their existing cloud resource stacks. This is likely part of a setup or integration workflow.
connect-modal.connect_description: Button in the Connect to Stack modal within the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Allows the user to confirm and initiate the connection to a selected cloud stack.
connect-modal.title: Connect to a cloud stack
connect-modal.title_description: Modal Title in the Connect to Cloud Stack modal: This title indicates the primary action of the modal, which is to connect BMC Helix Dashboards to an external cloud stack.
connect-modal.token-error-title_description: Error title in the Connect Modal within the Migrate to Cloud section: This title informs the user that an error occurred while trying to save an authentication token.

token-errors.instance-request-error: An error occurred while attempting to verify the cloud instance's connectivity. Please check the network settings or cloud instance status.
token-errors.instance-unreachable: The cloud instance cannot be reached. Make sure the instance is running and try again.
token-errors.migration-disabled: Cloud migrations are disabled on this instance.
token-errors.migration-disabled_description: Error message in the Connect Modal within the Cloud Migration feature: Informs the user that cloud migrations are disabled on the current instance, which prevents cloud migration setup.
token-errors.session-creation-failure: There was an error creating the migration. Please try again.
token-errors.token-invalid: Token is not valid. Generate a new token on your cloud instance and try again.
token-errors.token-not-saved: There was an error saving the token. See the Grafana server logs for more details.
token-errors.token-request-error: An error occurred while validating the token. Please check the Grafana instance logs.
token-errors.token-validation-failure: Token is not valid. Please ensure the token matches the migration token on your cloud instance.

connect-modal.token-required-error_description: Error message in the Connect modal of the Migrate to Cloud feature: Informs the user that a migration token is mandatory to establish the connection. This error is displayed when the token is missing.

cta.button_description: Button in the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature's call-to-action (CTA) area: Initiates the process to move the current instance to the cloud.
cta.header: Let us manage your Grafana stack
cta.header_description: Call to Action Header in the 'Migrate to Cloud' section: This header promotes BMC's managed Grafana service, encouraging users to leverage it when migrating their Grafana stack to the cloud.

dashboard-info.dashboard_description: Dashboard label in the Dashboard Information section of the Migrate to Cloud feature: Identifies a specific dashboard, where `{{dashboardUID}}` is a placeholder for its unique ID.

delete-migration-token-confirm.body: If you've already used this token with a self-managed installation, that installation will no longer be able to upload content.
delete-migration-token-confirm.confirm-button_description: Button in the "Delete Migration Token" confirmation dialog within the Migrate to Cloud feature: This button confirms the user's decision to proceed with deleting a migration token.
delete-migration-token-confirm.error-title_description: Title in Delete Migration Token confirmation dialog: Indicates an error occurred when attempting to delete a migration token.
delete-migration-token-confirm.title: Delete migration token
delete-migration-token-confirm.title_description: Dialog title in the "Migrate to Cloud" feature: This title labels a confirmation dialog displayed to the user when they initiate the deletion of a migration token.

disconnect-modal.body: This will remove the migration token from this installation. If you wish to upload more resources in the future, you will need to enter a new migration token.
disconnect-modal.disconnect_description: Button in the 'Disconnect' modal within the Migrate to Cloud feature: This button allows the user to confirm and initiate the disconnection process.
disconnect-modal.error_description: Error message in the Migrate to Cloud Disconnect Modal: Informs the user that the attempt to disconnect a cloud resource or service connection has failed.
disconnect-modal.title: Disconnect from cloud stack
disconnect-modal.title_description: Title in a disconnect confirmation modal within the migrate-to-cloud section: It indicates the user is about to disconnect from a previously connected cloud environment or stack.

folder-info.folder_description: Label in the Folder Information section within the Migrate to Cloud feature: Displays the unique identifier for a specific folder. The `{{folderUid}}` is a dynamic placeholder for the folder's unique ID.
folder-info.unable-to-load-folder_description: Error message in the Folder Info screen within the Migrate to Cloud feature: Informs the user that the system was unable to load the details or contents of a folder.

get-started.body: The migration process must be started from your self-managed Grafana instance.
get-started.body_description: Body text in the "Get Started" page for the "Migrate to Cloud" feature: Informs the user about the necessary starting point for the migration process. It clarifies that the migration must be initiated from their self-managed Grafana instance.
get-started.configure-pdc-link_description: Link in the Get Started section of the Migrate to Cloud feature: This link guides users to configure a Private Cloud Deployment (PDC) for their current cloud migration stack.
get-started.link-title: Learn more about Private Data Source Connect
get-started.link-title_description: Link title in the 'Get Started' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: This link provides users with access to more information about Private Data Source Connect.
get-started.step-1: Log in to your self-managed instance and navigate to Administration > General > Migrate to Grafana Cloud.
get-started.step-2_description: Instruction text in Step 2 of the "Migrate to Cloud" 'Get Started' wizard: It prompts the user to select the "Migrate this instance to Cloud" option to initiate the migration process.
get-started.step-3: You'll be prompted for a migration token. Generate one from this screen. Enter your token and click "Connect to this stack".
get-started.step-4: After connecting to the cloud stack, click "Build snapshot" to create a snapshot of your self-managed instance.
get-started.step-5: After a snapshot is created, click "Upload snapshot" to copy the resources to your cloud stack.
get-started.step-5_description: Instructional text in Step 5 of the Migrate to Cloud 'Get Started' workflow: Guides the user to upload a previously created snapshot to their cloud stack. It instructs the user to click the "Upload snapshot" button to initiate the resource copy process.
get-started.step-6: If some of your data sources will not work over the public internet, you’ll need to install Private Data Source Connect in your self-managed environment.
get-started.title: Performing a migration
get-started.title_description: Title in the Get Started section of the Migrate to Cloud feature: Introduces the main topic or process of migrating to the cloud. It serves as a heading for the content describing how to perform a migration.

is-it-secure.body: Grafana Labs is committed to maintaining the highest standards of data privacy and security. By implementing industry-standard security technologies and procedures, we help protect our customers' data from unauthorized access, use, or disclosure.
is-it-secure.link-title_description: Link title in the 'Is it secure' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: This link navigates users to the external Grafana Labs Trust Center for security and compliance information.
is-it-secure.title: Is it secure?
is-it-secure.title_description: Section Title in the "Is it secure?" section of the "Migrate to Cloud" feature: This title poses a direct question to the user, introducing content that addresses security concerns related to cloud migration.

migrate-to-this-stack.body: You can securely migrate some resources from your self-managed Grafana installation to this cloud stack. To get started, you'll need to generate a migration token. Your self-managed instance will use the token to authenticate with this cloud stack.
migrate-to-this-stack.link-title_description: Link in the "Migrate to this Stack" section of the cloud migration workflow: Provides users with access to the comprehensive guide for migrating to the current stack.
migrate-to-this-stack.title: Let us help you migrate to this stack
migrate-to-this-stack.title_description: **Title** in the **'Migrate to this Stack' section**: This title introduces the user to a migration process for a specific stack. It sets a helpful tone, offering assistance to guide the user through this transition.

migration-token.delete-button_description: Button on the Migration Token management page: Deletes an existing migration token.
migration-token.delete-modal-body: If you've already used this token with a self-managed installation, that installation will no longer be able to upload content.
migration-token.delete-modal-title_description: Modal Title in the Cloud Migration Token management section: This title appears at the top of a confirmation modal, indicating the user is about to delete a migration token.
migration-token.error-body: Unable to generate a migration token. Please try again later.
migration-token.error-title_description: Error message title in the 'Migration Token' section within the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: This title indicates that an unexpected error occurred during an operation (e.g., generating, validating) related to a migration token. It typically appears above a more specific error description.
migration-token.generate-button_description: Button in the 'Migration Token' section within the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Initiates the generation of a new token required for cloud migration operations.
migration-token.modal-copy-and-close_description: Button in the migration token modal dialog of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Copies the displayed migration token to the clipboard and closes the modal dialog.
migration-token.modal-copy-button_description: Button in the migration token modal within the Migrate to Cloud section: Copies the displayed migration token to the user's clipboard.
migration-token.modal-field-description: Copy the token now, as you will not be able to see it again. Losing this token requires creating a new one.
migration-token.modal-field-label_description: Field label in a modal related to migration tokens within the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Prompts the user to enter an authentication or access token required for a cloud migration process.
migration-token.modal-title_description: Modal Title in a modal within the 'Migrate to Cloud > Migration Token' section: This title confirms the successful creation of a migration token. It indicates that the token generation process has been completed successfully.
migration-token.status_description: A display field in the 'Migration Token' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: It shows the current status of a migration token. The `<2></2>` placeholder will be dynamically replaced with the actual status value.

onprem.cancel-snapshot-error-title_description: Error message title in the On-premises migration section: This title is displayed when an attempt to cancel an ongoing snapshot creation process has failed. It indicates that the cancellation operation itself was unsuccessful.
onprem.create-snapshot-error-duplicate-resource: Duplicate resource type. See the Grafana server logs for more details

Error message in the snapshot creation process in the on-premise cloud migration workflow: Informs the user that a duplicate resource type was detected, which prevented the snapshot from being created. It advises checking Grafana server logs for details.
onprem.create-snapshot-error-empty-resource-typ: You need to provide at least one resource type for snapshot creation

Error message in the On-Premises section of the Migrate to Cloud feature: This message appears when a user attempts to create a snapshot of on-premise resources but has not selected any resource types. It instructs the user to provide at least one resource type to proceed with snapshot creation.
onprem.create-snapshot-error-missing-dependency: Missing dependency. See the Grafana server logs for more details

Error Message on the 'Create Snapshot' screen within the 'On-Premise Migration to Cloud' workflow: Informs the user that a snapshot could not be created due to a missing dependency. It directs the user to check Grafana server logs for troubleshooting details.
onprem.create-snapshot-error-title_description: Error title in the Migrate to Cloud > On-Premise section: Displayed when an attempt to create a snapshot for on-premise data migration fails.
onprem.create-snapshot-error-unknown-resource-t: Unknown resource type. See the Grafana server logs for more details

Error message in the On-Premises snapshot creation dialog of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: This message is displayed when the system encounters an unknown or unsupported resource type while attempting to create a snapshot from an on-premise environment. It advises the user to consult Grafana server logs for further diagnostic information.
onprem.disconnect-error-title_description: Error title in the On-premise section of the Migrate to Cloud feature: Displays when an on-premise component or connection fails to disconnect.
onprem.error-see-server-logs: See the Grafana server logs for more details
onprem.error-see-server-logs_description: Error message instruction in the On-premise Cloud Migration interface: Informs the user to consult Grafana server logs for more details when an error occurs during the migration process.
onprem.get-session-error-title_description: Error Title in the On-Premises Migration configuration view: Informs the user that the system failed to retrieve or load the necessary configuration settings for an on-premises migration.
onprem.get-snapshot-error-title_description: Error Title in the on-premises migration section of the Migrate to Cloud feature: This title is displayed when the system encounters an error while trying to load or retrieve a snapshot of the on-premises environment.
onprem.migration-finished-with-caveat-title_des: A title in the On-Premise to Cloud Migration feature: Informs the user that the resource migration from an on-premise environment to the cloud has been completed. This title is specifically used in scenarios where the migration finished with caveats, which would be detailed in accompanying status messages.
onprem.migration-finished-with-errors-body: The migration has completed, but some items could not be migrated to the cloud stack. Check the failed resources for more details.
onprem.migration-finished-with-warnings-body: The migration has completed with some warnings. Check individual resources for more details
onprem.migration-finished-with-warnings-body_de: Warning message body in the on-premise cloud migration results screen or a notification dialog: Informs the user that an on-premise migration has completed with warnings, prompting them to check individual resources for more details.
onprem.snapshot-error-status-body: There was an error creating the snapshot or starting the migration process. See the Grafana server logs for more details
onprem.snapshot-error-status-title_description: Title in the On-premise Migration status display: Indicates an error occurred during the migration of on-premise resources.
onprem.success-message: Successfully migrated {{successCount}} {{types, list}} to your Grafana Cloud instance.
onprem.success-message_description: Success message in the post-migration confirmation for an on-premise to Grafana Cloud migration: Informs the user that a specified number and type of elements have been successfully transferred to their Grafana Cloud instance.
onprem.success-message-generic: Successfully migrated {{successCount}} resources to your Grafana Cloud instance.
onprem.success-message-generic_description: Success message in the On-Premise Migration feature within the 'Migrate to Cloud' section: Confirms the successful migration of a specified number of resources to the user's Grafana Cloud instance. This is a generic confirmation displayed after the migration process completes.
onprem.success-title_description: Title on the 'On-Premises Cloud Migration' success screen: Confirms to the user that the migration process from an on-premises environment to the cloud has been completed successfully.
onprem.upload-snapshot-error-title_description: Error Title in the On-Premises Migration feature: This title is displayed when an attempt to upload a snapshot from an on-premise environment to the cloud fails. It informs the user about the error encountered during the snapshot upload process.

pdc.body: Exposing your data sources to the internet can raise security concerns. Private data source connect (PDC) allows Grafana Cloud to access your existing data sources over a secure network tunnel.
pdc.link-title_description: Link in the 'PDC' section within the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Allows users to learn more or get detailed information about PDC.
pdc.title: What if not all my data sources are on the public internet?
pdc.title_description: Question in the migrate-to-cloud PDC configuration section: This addresses a common user concern regarding data sources not accessible on the public internet during cloud migration. It likely introduces options or solutions for connecting private data sources.

pricing.body: Grafana Cloud has a generous free plan and a 14 day unlimited usage trial. After your trial expires, you'll be billed based on usage over the free plan limits.
pricing.link-title_description: Link in the 'Pricing' section under 'Migrate to Cloud': Navigates users to information about Grafana Cloud pricing. This link helps users understand the cost implications of Grafana Cloud.
pricing.title: How much does it cost?
pricing.title_description: Section Title in the 'Pricing' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: This title asks about the financial cost of migrating to the cloud. It introduces the pricing information for the service.

error-messages.alert-rules-group-quota-reached: Maximum number of alert rule groups reached: Delete some alert rule groups or upgrade your plan and try again.
error-messages.alert-rules-quota-reached: Maximum number of alert rules reached: Delete some alert rules or upgrade your plan and try again.
error-messages.alert-rules-quota-reached_description: Error message on a resource details page, likely within the "Migrate to Cloud" section: It informs the user that they have reached the maximum number of alert rules allowed by their current plan. The message prompts the user to either delete existing alert rules or upgrade their plan to resolve the issue.
error-messages.dashboard-already-managed: Dashboard is already provisioned and managed by Grafana in the cloud instance. We recommend using the provisioned dashboard going forward. If you still wish to copy the dashboard to the cloud instance, then change the dashboard ID in the dashboard JSON, save a new snapshot and upload again.
error-messages.datasource-already-managed: Data source is already provisioned and managed by Grafana in the cloud instance. If this is a different resource, set another UID and try again.
error-messages.datasource-invalid-url: There is a data source which has an invalid URL. Provide a valid URL and try again.
error-messages.datasource-name-conflict: There is a data source with the same name in the target instance. Rename one of them and try again.
error-messages.folder-name-conflict: There is a folder with the same name in the target instance. Rename one of them and try again.
error-messages.generic-error: There has been an error while migrating. Please check the cloud migration logs for more information.
error-messages.internal-service-error: There has been an error while migrating. Please check the Grafana server logs for more details.
error-messages.library-element-name-conflict: There is a library element with the same name in the target instance. Rename one of them and try again.
error-messages.resource-conflict: There is a resource conflict with the target instance. Please check the Grafana server logs for more details.
error-messages.unexpected-error: There has been an error while migrating. Please check the Grafana server logs for more details.
error-messages.unsupported-data-type: Migration of this data type is not currently supported.
error-messages.unsupported-data-type_description: Error message in the Migrate to Cloud > Resource Details section: Informs the user that the data type of a specific resource selected for migration is currently unsupported. This prevents the migration of that particular data type.

resource-details.error-title_description: Error message title in the Resource Details section of the Migrate to Cloud feature: Informs the user that a specific resource could not be migrated. It precedes further details about the migration failure.
resource-details.generic-title_description: Title in the Resource Details section within the Migrate to Cloud feature: It serves as a heading for a section that displays specific information about a resource's migration process.
resource-details.missing-message_description: Informational message in the 'Resource Details' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Indicates that a specific message or descriptive text for the resource was expected but is currently unavailable.
resource-details.resource-summary_description: Resource Summary Display in the Resource Details section within the Migrate to Cloud module: This display shows the unique reference ID and type of a specific resource. It provides a concise identifier for the item being detailed.
resource-details.title: Migration resource details
resource-details.title_description: Title on the Migration Resource Details page within the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: This title identifies the section displaying specific information about a selected migration resource.
resource-details.warning-title_description: Title in the Resource Details section of the Migrate to Cloud feature: It indicates a resource migration has completed with warnings. This title precedes the specific warning details.

resource-status.error-details-button_description: Button in the "Resource Status" section of the "Migrate to Cloud" feature: Allows users to view more information about a specific error or status issue.
resource-status.failed_description: Label in the resource status section of the Migrate to Cloud feature: Indicates that a specific resource failed or encountered an error during its migration to the cloud. It signifies a general failure state.
resource-status.migrated_description: Status label in the Resource Status section of the Migrate to Cloud feature: It indicates that a resource has been successfully uploaded or transferred to the cloud environment.
resource-status.not-migrated_description: Status label in the Resource Status section of the Migrate to Cloud feature: It indicates that a specific resource intended for cloud migration has not yet been uploaded or transferred to the target environment.
resource-status.unknown_description: Status label in the Resource Status section of the Migrate to Cloud feature: Indicates that the status of a specific resource is currently undetermined or could not be retrieved.
resource-status.warning_description: Status label in the 'Migrate to Cloud > Resource Status' section: This indicates that a resource upload operation completed, but with warnings. This suggests the uploaded resource might require attention due to minor issues.
resource-status.warning-details-button_description: Button in the Resource Status section of the Migrate to Cloud feature: This button allows users to view more specific information about a warning displayed for a resource.

resource-table.dashboard-load-error_description: Error message in the `resource-table` within the `migrate-to-cloud` section: Informs the user that a dashboard could not be loaded. This message is displayed when there is an issue retrieving or rendering the dashboard data.
resource-table.error-library-element-sub_description: Library Element identifier in the Resource Table within the Migrate to Cloud section: This string identifies a specific library element by its unique ID (`uid`) when an error occurs. It likely serves as a placeholder within a broader error message regarding resource migration issues.
resource-table.error-library-element-title_description: Error message title in the Resource Table within the Migrate to Cloud feature: Informs the user that a required library element could not be loaded. This prevents the proper display or functionality of a component within the table.
resource-table.unknown-datasource-title_description: Title in a resource table within the Migrate-to-Cloud feature: Identifies an unknown or unrecognized data source by its unique ID. It provides essential information when the data source's full details cannot be retrieved.
resource-table.unknown-datasource-type_description: Label in a resource table within the "Migrate to Cloud" feature: Informs the user that a data source associated with a listed resource is unrecognized or its type is unknown. This message typically indicates a configuration issue or an unsupported data source.

resource-type.alert_rule_description: Label in the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature, under resource types: It identifies 'Alert Rule' as a distinct category of resource that can be managed or migrated.
resource-type.alert_rule_group_description: Option in the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature, within the 'Resource Type' selection: This label represents 'Alert Rule Group' as a specific category of resources that can be chosen for migration to the cloud.
resource-type.contact_point_description: Label in the 'Resource Type' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: This string identifies a resource category named 'Contact Point.' It refers to an entity designated for communication or alerts regarding cloud migration resources.
resource-type.dashboard_description: Resource Type label in the 'Resource Type' selection within the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: This label identifies 'Dashboard' as an available resource type for selection or management during cloud migration.
resource-type.datasource_description: Resource type option in the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature's resource type configuration: Refers to a data source as a specific type of resource that can be selected or configured for migration.
resource-type.folder_description: Resource type label in the Resource Type selection within the Migrate to Cloud feature: Identifies 'Folder' as a classification for items that can be migrated.
resource-type.library_element_description: Library Element in resource type selection within the Migrate to Cloud feature: This option allows users to categorize a resource as a reusable component or item from a library.
resource-type.mute_timing_description: Setting in the resource type configuration within the Cloud Migration feature: This defines the schedule or duration for muting specific activities, alerts, or notifications for that resource type. It specifies the 'mute window' for the resource.
resource-type.notification_policy_description: Label in the 'Resource Type' section within the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: This refers to the configuration or definition of rules for notifications related to a specific resource type during cloud migration.
resource-type.notification_template_description: Label in the 'Resource Type' configuration within the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Identifies a customizable template used to generate notifications related to a specific resource type.
resource-type.plugin_description: Label in the 'Resource Type' selection within the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Identifies 'Plugin' as a specific type of resource that can be managed or migrated to the cloud.
resource-type.unknown_description: Value for a resource type in the Migrate to Cloud feature: Indicates that the specific type of a resource is unknown or could not be determined.

resource-types.alert_rule_description: Category Name in the 'Resource Types' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: It labels 'Alert Rules' as a specific type of resource available for management or selection. This refers to the configurations that define alert conditions.
resource-types.alert_rule_group_description: Resource type label in the Migrate to Cloud > Resource Types section: This label identifies 'Alert Rule Groups' as a category of resources that can be selected or managed for cloud migration. It refers to collections of alert rules.
resource-types.contact_point_description: Label for a resource type in the 'Resource Types' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Identifies categories of communication endpoints or notification channels.
resource-types.dashboard_description: Resource type label in the 'Resource Types' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Identifies 'Dashboards' as a selectable resource type for migration.
resource-types.datasource_description: Section Title in the 'Resource Types' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: This title indicates a section where users can view or manage various data sources relevant to cloud migration.
resource-types.folder_description: Resource Type option in the Migrate to Cloud > Resource Types section: Represents resources that are folders.
resource-types.library_element_description: Library Elements in the Resource Types section of the Migrate to Cloud feature: This refers to pre-defined, reusable components or configurations of resources that can be selected or managed for cloud migration.
resource-types.mute_timing_description: Configuration option in the 'Resource Types' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: This option allows users to define the schedules or durations for muting alerts or monitoring for specific resource types.
resource-types.notification_policy_description: Label in the 'Resource Types' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: This identifies the category for managing configurable rules governing how users are notified about cloud migration resources.
resource-types.notification_template_description: UI element in the 'Resource Types' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: This element represents a specific resource type for managing notification templates. These templates are used for configuring alerts and communications related to cloud migration processes.
resource-types.plugin_description: A category label under 'Resource Types' within the 'Migrate to Cloud' section: It identifies 'Plugins' as a specific type of resource managed or considered during cloud migration.

summary.cancel-snapshot_description: Button in the Summary section of the Migrate to Cloud feature: Allows users to cancel an ongoing or scheduled snapshot operation related to cloud migration.
summary.disconnect_description: Button in the `migrate-to-cloud > summary` view: Allows the user to disconnect a cloud account, data source, or other connection associated with a cloud migration summary.
summary.errored-resource-count_description: Metric label in the Cloud Migration Summary: Identifies the count of resources that encountered errors during the cloud migration process. This label is used to display the total number of errored resources.
summary.rebuild-snapshot_description: Button or link on the Migrate to Cloud Summary page: Used to reconfigure or rebuild a snapshot. This snapshot is typically related to the data or environment being migrated to the cloud.
summary.show-errors_description: Toggle on the Migrate to Cloud Summary page: Filters the displayed migration summary data to show only entries that are errors.
summary.snapshot-date_description: Label in the 'Migrate to Cloud' summary section: Displays the date and time when a system or data snapshot was captured, providing a reference point for migration planning or status.
summary.snapshot-not-created_description: Status label in the Migrate to Cloud Summary screen: This informs the user that a particular snapshot has not yet been generated. It signifies a pending or uninitiated state for snapshot creation.
summary.start-migration_description: Button in the 'Summary' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Initiates the creation of a snapshot of the environment. This snapshot is a crucial preparatory step before starting a cloud migration.
summary.successful-resource-count_description: Status label in the Migration to Cloud Summary section: Indicates the successful migration of resources. This label is typically displayed next to a count of successfully migrated resources.
summary.target-stack-title_description: Label in the 'Migrate to Cloud' summary screen: This label serves as a prefix for the name of the target cloud environment or stack to which data or configurations are being uploaded. It completes a dynamically generated title like 'Uploading to [Target Stack Name]'.
summary.total-resource-count_description: Label in the Cloud Migration Summary section: Displays the total number of resources identified or managed for cloud migration.
summary.upload-migration_description: Button on the 'Migrate to Cloud Summary' page: Initiates the process of uploading a system or application snapshot. This snapshot provides data relevant to cloud migration planning or execution.

support-types-disclosure.text: Resources are copied to your Grafana Cloud stack. <2>Learn more</2> about the full set of supported resources and migrating other settings.

token-status.active_description: Status message in the 'Token Status' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: It confirms that a required authentication token has been successfully generated and is currently active.
token-status.no-active_description: Status message in the Token Status section of the Migrate to Cloud UI: Informs the user that no active token is currently available or configured. This message indicates a missing prerequisite for cloud migration operations.
token-status.unknown_description: Status Label in the 'Token Status' section within the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: This indicates that the status of a specific token is currently unknown or could not be determined. It represents one of the possible states for a token.
token-status.unknown-error_description: Error message in the 'Token Status' section of the 'Migrate to Cloud' feature: Informs the user that the system encountered an unspecified error while attempting to retrieve an authentication token. This message is displayed when the specific reason for the token retrieval failure is unknown.

what-is-cloud.body: Grafana cloud is a fully managed cloud-hosted observability platform ideal for cloud native environments. It's everything you love about Grafana without the overhead of maintaining, upgrading, and supporting an installation.

users.title_description: Menu item in the navigation menu: Navigates to the section where administrators can view and manage user accounts.

breadcrumbs.aria-label_description: Accessible label in the navigation breadcrumbs: This string provides an accessible name for the breadcrumbs component, helping screen reader users understand it displays the current location in the navigation hierarchy.

button-tooltip.close_description: Tooltip for a close button in the extension sidebar: This tooltip informs the user that clicking the button will close the currently active extension or panel, whose name is represented by `{{title}}`.
button-tooltip.open_description: Tooltip in the navigation extension sidebar: This text appears when hovering over a button, indicating that clicking it will open the item specified by the `{{title}}` placeholder.
button-tooltip.open-all: Open AI assistants and sidebar apps
button-tooltip.open-all_description: Tooltip in the extension sidebar: Appears when hovering over a button, indicating its function to open various AI assistants and other sidebar applications.

help.aria-label_description: ARIA label for a Help button in the main navigation bar: This label provides an accessible name for the button that opens help documentation or support content. It is primarily used by screen readers.

interactive-learning.close-tooltip: Close interactive learning, help, and documentation
interactive-learning.close-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the Interactive Learning section within the Help menu: Describes the action of closing the interactive learning feature, including help content and documentation. This tooltip appears when hovering over the close control for the interactive learning panel.
interactive-learning.open-tooltip: Open interactive learning, help, and documentation
interactive-learning.open-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the Interactive Learning section (accessed via the Help menu in the main navigation): Describes the action of the associated UI element, which is to open interactive learning modules, help articles, and product documentation.

help.tooltip_description: Tooltip for the Help icon/button in the global navigation bar: Informs the user that clicking this element provides access to help resources and useful links.

invite-user.invite-button_description: Button in the 'Invite User' screen: Triggers the action to send an invitation to a new user.
invite-user.invite-new-user-button_description: Button in the Invite User section: This button is used to initiate the process of inviting a new user to the BMC Helix Dashboards system.
invite-user.invite-tooltip_description: Tooltip on an "Invite user" element within the navigation menu: It provides a brief explanation for the action of inviting a new user to the system.

item.add-bookmark_description: Menu item in the navigation menu or an item's context menu: Allows users to save the currently viewed dashboard or report to their personal bookmarks list for quick access.
item.remove-bookmark_description: Menu item on a bookmarked item within a navigation list or context menu: Allows the user to remove the item from their personal list of bookmarks.

kiosk.tv-alert: Press ESC to exit kiosk mode
kiosk.tv-alert_description: Instructional message in Kiosk Mode: Informs the user how to exit the full-screen Kiosk Mode display. This message provides the command to return to the standard dashboard interface.

megamenu.close_description: Button in the mega menu navigation: Used to dismiss the currently displayed mega menu.
megamenu.dock_description: Button in the navigation's mega menu: Allows the user to dock or pin the mega menu. This action keeps the menu persistently visible on the screen.
megamenu.list-label_description: Section Heading in the Mega Menu: This label identifies a major category for a list of navigation options within the global navigation. It groups related navigation links.
megamenu.open_description: Button in the main navigation area: Allows the user to open or expand the application's megamenu, typically revealing further navigation options.
megamenu.undock_description: Menu item in the navigation mega menu: Allows the user to undock the mega menu, detaching it from its currently fixed or docked position. This action typically transforms a persistently displayed menu into a floating or dismissible element.

megamenu-item.collapse-aria-label_description: ARIA label for a collapse button/toggle within a mega menu item: This label informs screen reader users that activating the button will collapse the specified `{{sectionName}}` section.
megamenu-item.expand-aria-label_description: Expand button's ARIA label in a navigation mega menu item: This label describes the action of expanding a specific collapsible section for accessibility. The `{{sectionName}}` placeholder dynamically provides the name of the section being expanded.

org-switcher.aria-label_description: Interactive control in the organization switcher within the main navigation: This `aria-label` describes a control that allows the user to switch to a different organization.

profile.aria-label_description: An `aria-label` for a link or button in the global navigation: This element allows users to access their personal profile settings and information.

navigation.rss-button_description: Link in the navigation: Directs users to the latest content from the product's blog.

news.category-news_description: Category label within the News section: Designates a section or page dedicated to general news, updates, and announcements.
news.description-show-image: Controls if the news item social (og:image) image is shown above text content
news.description-show-image_description: Setting in News item configuration: Controls whether the social (og:image) of a news item is shown. It determines if the image appears above the news item's text content.
news.description-url_description: Informational text in the News configuration settings: Informs the user that the system supports both RSS and Atom formats for news feeds. This text likely accompanies a URL input field or a section for managing news feed sources.
news.link-title_description: Link in the news section: Allows users to navigate to the Grafana Labs blog for further information or updates.
news.name-show-image_description: **Toggle switch or checkbox** in **News configuration**: **This option controls whether an image (e.g., a profile picture or icon) associated with a name or other entity is displayed within news items.**

news-panel.body-error-loading-rss-feed: Make sure that the feed URL is correct and that CORS is configured correctly on the server. See <2>News panel documentation.</2>
news-panel.title-error-loading-rss-feed_description: Error message title in the News panel: Informs the user when the RSS feed fails to load, preventing content from being displayed.

news.title: Latest from the blog
news.title_description: Heading in the News/Blog section: This title introduces a list of the most recent blog posts or articles from a blog feed.

node-graph.category-edges_description: A label or section header in a node-graph visualization: Refers to the connections between nodes (edges). This might be used in a section for managing or categorizing these connections.
node-graph.category-node-graph_description: Category Title in BMC Helix Dashboards: This string labels a category for 'Node graph' visualizations, representing interconnected entities and their relationships.
node-graph.category-nodes_description: Section label in a Node Graph interface: Identifies a category or grouping of the individual nodes displayed within the graph.

layout-algorithm-options.description-force_description: Option description in the Node Graph layout algorithm settings: Informs the user that this option utilizes a force-directed algorithm for arranging graph nodes.
layout-algorithm-options.description-grid_description: Description text in the Layout Algorithm Options section for a node graph: It describes the "Grid layout" option, which arranges the nodes in a grid pattern.
layout-algorithm-options.description-layered_description: Label in the layout algorithm options for a node-graph visualization: It describes an option to apply a layered layout to the graph, typically used for displaying hierarchical or directed relationships.
layout-algorithm-options.label-force_description: Label in the Layout Algorithm Options section of a Node Graph: This label identifies a parameter for configuring the 'force' aspect of a layout algorithm. It typically adjusts the strength of interaction or repulsion between nodes in a force-directed layout.
layout-algorithm-options.label-grid_description: Layout option name in Node Graph Layout Options: Refers to a layout algorithm that arranges nodes in a grid pattern.
layout-algorithm-options.label-layered_description: Option in the layout algorithm options for a node graph: This label represents a layout algorithm type that arranges nodes in distinct layers or levels.

node-graph.name-arc-sections_description: A label in the node graph UI: It refers to distinct segments or parts that comprise an arc (connection) between nodes.
node-graph.name-layout-algorithm_description: Setting in Node Graph configuration: This defines the specific algorithm used to automatically arrange and position the nodes and edges within the graph visualization. It controls the visual organization of the graph elements.
node-graph.name-main-stat-unit_description: Field label in the node graph configuration: Specifies the unit of measurement for the primary statistical value displayed on a node within the graph.
node-graph.name-secondary-stat-unit_description: Label in Node Graph configuration: Identifies the unit of measurement for a secondary statistic displayed within the node graph.
node-graph.name-zoom-mode_description: A label in a node graph visualization: It refers to the interactive method or current behavior for zooming within the graph.

zoom-mode-options.description-cooperative: Lets you scroll the page normally
zoom-mode-options.description-cooperative_description: Description text in the 'Zoom Mode Options' panel for a node graph: This text describes the 'Cooperative' zoom mode option, explaining that it allows users to scroll the page normally without affecting the graph's zoom.
zoom-mode-options.description-greedy_description: Description text for a zoom mode option within the Node Graph's 'Zoom Mode Options': This text explains a specific zoom behavior that is designed to react to all possible user zoom gestures, such as mouse wheel, pinch, or trackpad actions.
zoom-mode-options.label-cooperative_description: Label in the zoom mode options for a node graph: This option describes a "cooperative" zoom behavior, where zooming might be synchronized or affect related parts of the graph.
zoom-mode-options.label-greedy_description: Option label in Node Graph > Zoom Mode Options: Specifies a zoom behavior that aggressively fills the viewport or includes all available elements. This mode prioritizes displaying as much data as possible, potentially at the expense of precise scaling.

arc-options-editor.add-arc_description: Button in the Arc Options Editor within a Node Graph: Allows users to add a new arc (connection) between nodes in the graph.
arc-options-editor.title-remove-arc_description: Button label in the arc options editor within a node graph: This button allows users to remove a selected arc.

edge.aria-label-from-to_description: Edge in a node graph: This ARIA label describes the connection between two nodes, indicating the starting node (`{{from}}`) and the ending node (`{{to}}`). It is used to provide an accessible description for screen readers.

edge-header.label-source-target_description: Label in the header of an edge in a Node Graph: This label indicates the direction of a relationship or data flow, moving from a source node to a target node. The arrow symbol is crucial for conveying this direction.

marker.100-node-count_description: Label in a node graph marker: This label signifies that the entity associated with the marker contains more than 100 nodes. It helps categorize or filter elements on the node graph.
marker.aria-label-hidden-marker_description: Accessibility label for a marker in the Node Graph: This label describes a marker that represents hidden nodes. It is used for screen readers to convey the marker's meaning, where `{{marker}}` will be replaced by the specific marker value.
marker.node-count_one_description: Label on a node graph marker: Displays the number of nodes. Although the key `_one` typically indicates a singular count, this string provides the plural form.
marker.node-count_other_description: Count display in a marker within a node graph: Shows the quantity of nodes associated with that specific marker. This is the plural form used for displaying node counts.

node.aria-label-node-title_description: ARIA label for a node in a node graph: Provides an accessible name for a node, displaying its specific name. This text is primarily used by screen readers to identify the node.

node-graph.aria-label-layered-layout-performance-wa: Accessibility label for a warning in the Node Graph visualization: It alerts users to potential performance issues that may arise when a layered layout is selected or applied.
node-graph.aria-label-nodes-hidden-warning_descript: ARIA label for a warning message in the node graph view: This label informs users that some nodes within the graph are not currently displayed. It aids accessibility for situations where nodes are hidden due to filtering or view settings.
node-graph.computing-layout_description: Status message in the node graph view: This message indicates that the system is currently arranging the nodes and edges of the graph for optimal visual display. It appears temporarily while the graph's layout is being calculated.
node-graph.hidden-nodes_one: <0></0> {{count}} nodes are hidden for performance reasons.
node-graph.hidden-nodes_one_description: Informational text within the node graph visualization: This text indicates that a certain number of nodes have been hidden automatically due to performance considerations. It typically appears alongside an icon or a clickable element.
node-graph.hidden-nodes_other: <0></0> {{count}} nodes are hidden for performance reasons.
node-graph.hidden-nodes_other_description: Status message in the Node Graph view: Informs the user that a certain number of nodes have been automatically hidden to improve the performance of the graph visualization. This message appears when the graph reaches a complexity threshold.
node-graph.no-data_description: A message displayed in a Node Graph: It informs the user when there is no data to render or visualize within the graph component.
node-graph.processed-nodes_one: <0></0> Layered layout may be slow with {{count}} nodes.
node-graph.processed-nodes_one_description: Warning message in the Node Graph visualization: It informs the user about potential performance degradation when a layered layout is applied to a large number of nodes. The `<0></0>` placeholder is for embedded content, such as a link or text formatting.
node-graph.processed-nodes_other: <0></0> Layered layout may be slow with {{count}} nodes.
node-graph.processed-nodes_other_description: Warning Message in Node Graph: Informs the user that enabling a layered layout might result in slow performance when the node graph contains a high number of nodes. The message includes the count of nodes causing the potential issue.

node-graph-panel.no-data-found-in-response_description: Message in Node Graph panel: Informs the user when a data query performed for the Node Graph panel returns no results. This message is displayed in place of the graph when no data is available to render.

node-header.label-subtitle_description: Label in the node header within a node graph view: Displays secondary identifying or descriptive information for the node.

view-controls.hide-config_description: Button in the view controls section of a node graph visualization: Hides the configuration panel or settings related to the node graph's display.
view-controls.show-config_description: Button in the view controls section of the Node Graph: Toggles the visibility of the node graph's configuration settings.
view-controls.title-zoom-in_description: Button in the view controls of a node graph: Increases the magnification of the displayed node graph content. It typically appears as a label or tooltip for the zoom in action.
view-controls.title-zoom-out_description: Button or Tooltip in the Node Graph view controls: Decreases the magnification of the displayed node graph.

notifications.starred-dashboard_description: Notification message in the notifications system: This message confirms that a dashboard has been successfully marked as a favorite or "starred" by the user.

stored-notifications.dismiss-notifications_description: Button in the Stored Notifications section: Allows users to dismiss or clear stored notifications from the list.
stored-notifications.title-alert: This page displays past errors and warnings. Once dismissed, they cannot be retrieved.

notifications.unstarred-dashboard_description: Notification message in the system's notification display: Confirms to the user that a dashboard has been successfully unstarred (removed from their favorites).

form.server-discovery-action-button_descripti: Prompt for an input field in the OpenID Connect configuration section of the OAuth form: It directs the user to provide the discovery URL for an OpenID Connect provider. This URL is used to automatically fetch the provider's configuration details.
form.server-discovery-modal-submit_descriptio: Button in an OAuth form within a server discovery modal: Submits the configured server discovery settings. This action finalizes user input and initiates the server discovery process.

login.error_description: Error message on the OAuth login screen: Informs the user that the external identity provider (e.g., Google, Azure AD) denied their login request. This typically appears after the user has been redirected to the provider for authentication and the provider responds with a denial.

color.clear-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the color options section: It describes the action of clearing or resetting the current color settings to their default values.

field-color.color-by-label_description: Label in the field color configuration options: Prompts the user to choose the field or attribute that will determine how data series are colored in a visualization.

get-all-option-editors.description-time-zone_description: UI label in an option editor within the options user interface: It identifies the control or section used for selecting a time zone.
get-all-option-editors.name-time-zone_description: Setting name in an option editor interface: This setting allows users to specify the time zone for data interpretation and display within a dashboard or panel.

standard-field-configs.category_description: Category label in the 'Standard field configurations' section of the Options UI: This label identifies a grouping of default or common configuration settings for standard fields.
standard-field-configs.category-thresholds_description: Section header or label in the Standard Field Configurations: Allows users to configure threshold values for different data categories. These thresholds can define visual cues, color changes, or alert conditions based on data.
standard-field-configs.category-value-mappings_description: Label in the Standard Field Configurations section: This option allows users to define custom transformations or display rules for specific data values, particularly categories. It is used to map raw data values to more user-friendly representations.
standard-field-configs.description-display-name: Change the field or series name
standard-field-configs.description-display-name_description: Description in Standard field configuration settings: Instructs the user on how to modify the displayed name for a data field or data series within the dashboard.
standard-field-configs.description-field-min-max_description: A setting in the "Standard Field Configurations" section of the Options UI: Controls whether minimum and maximum values are calculated and displayed independently for each field.
standard-field-configs.description-max: Leave empty to calculate based on all values
standard-field-configs.description-max_description: Input field in the Standard Field Configuration settings for dashboards: This field allows the user to specify a maximum value for a visual representation. The instruction indicates that if left blank, the maximum will be automatically calculated based on all data values.
standard-field-configs.description-min: Leave empty to calculate based on all values
standard-field-configs.description-min_description: Instruction text in the Standard Field Configuration panel: This informs the user that if an input field for a minimum value is left empty, the system will automatically calculate that minimum based on all available data.
standard-field-configs.description-no-value: What to show when there is no value
standard-field-configs.description-no-value_description: Label in standard field configuration options: Prompts the user to specify the text or behavior that should be displayed when a data field has no value.
standard-field-configs.description-value-mappings: Modify the display text based on input value
standard-field-configs.description-value-mappings_description: Configuration setting in Standard Field Configurations within the Options UI: This setting allows users to define custom display text for specific input values of a field. It enables mapping raw data values to more descriptive or user-friendly labels.
standard-field-configs.name-actions_description: Column header in the standard field configurations table: This header identifies the column where various actions related to individual field configurations are displayed or accessible.
standard-field-configs.name-ad-hoc_description: Label for a configuration option in the `standard-field-configs` section: This label describes a setting that, when enabled, allows a specific data field to be used for ad-hoc or dynamic filtering in dashboards.
standard-field-configs.name-color-scheme_description: Setting in the standard field configuration options: Allows users to define or select the visual color scheme for data associated with standard fields.
standard-field-configs.name-data-links_description: Label for a configuration setting in Standard Field Configurations within the Options UI: This setting allows users to define interactive links associated with data field values. These links typically facilitate navigation to other dashboards or external URLs.
standard-field-configs.name-decimals_description: Label in the Standard Field Configurations section of the Options UI: This setting specifies the number of decimal places to display for numerical values in a field. It controls the precision of displayed numerical data.
standard-field-configs.name-display-name_description: Label in Standard Field Configuration settings: This specifies the user-facing name that will be displayed for a standard field. It provides a more descriptive or readable alternative to a technical field name.
standard-field-configs.name-field-min-max_description: Label in standard field configuration settings: This option allows users to define the minimum and maximum allowable values for a data field.
standard-field-configs.name-max_description: Label in Standard Field Configurations: This label identifies a configuration option to set the maximum allowed value or length for a standard field.
standard-field-configs.name-min_description: Label in a standard field configuration setting: Represents the minimum value or lower boundary for a configurable field. It is an abbreviation for 'Minimum'.
standard-field-configs.name-no-value_description: Text label in the Standard Field Configurations section within the Options UI: Used to indicate that a field or configuration property currently has no value defined or selected.
standard-field-configs.name-thresholds_description: Section label in Standard Field Configurations settings: Allows users to define limits or boundary values for data. These thresholds are typically used for conditional formatting, alerting, or visual indicators on dashboards.
standard-field-configs.name-unit_description: Label in the Standard Field Configurations section of the Options UI: Used to define or display the unit of measurement for a data field.
standard-field-configs.name-value-mappings_description: Setting label in the Standard Field Configurations section: Allows users to define how specific data values are mapped or transformed for display and interpretation within the dashboard.
standard-field-configs.placeholder-decimals_description: Placeholder value in standard field configurations within the options UI: This value, "auto", represents the default setting for how decimal places are handled in standard fields, meaning they are automatically determined.
standard-field-configs.placeholder-display-name_description: String value in Standard Field Configurations: This string represents the option to not use or display any placeholder name for a field.
standard-field-configs.placeholder-max_description: Configuration setting in Standard Field Configurations within the UI Options: It defines the maximum value or limit for a standard field. The 'auto' value indicates that this maximum is automatically determined by the system or context.
standard-field-configs.placeholder-min_description: Configuration setting in Standard Field Configurations: Specifies that the minimum value to be displayed as a placeholder for a field is automatically determined. This ensures dynamic and context-aware placeholder suggestions.
standard-field-configs.placeholder-unit_description: Value in Standard field configuration settings: Represents the option to display 'none' as the placeholder unit. This indicates that no unit will be shown by default for a field when one is not explicitly defined.

units.clear-tooltip_description: **Tooltip in the Units section of the Options UI:** This text describes an action to clear any previously chosen unit selection. It would appear on hover over a button or icon.

new-org-page.description: Each organization contains their own dashboards, data sources, and configuration, which cannot be shared shared between organizations. While users might belong to more than one organization, multiple organizations are most frequently used in multi-tenant deployments.
new-org-page.label-organization-name_description: Label on the 'New Organization' page: This string identifies the input field where users provide the name for a new organization being created.
new-org-page.placeholder-org-name_description: Placeholder text in an input field on the New Organization Page: Prompts the user to enter the desired name for the new organization.

title.confirm-preferences-update_description: Title in the Organization Details Page: This string serves as the title for a confirmation dialog or section, prompting the user to confirm changes made to their organizational preferences.

org-profile.label-organization-name_description: Label in the Organization Profile settings: Identifies the field for the organization's name. It typically precedes an input field where the name can be entered or viewed.
org-profile.label-organization-profile_description: Title in the Organization profile page: Identifies the section where users can view and manage details and settings for their organization.

select-org-page.description: You have been invited to another organization! Please select which organization that you want to use right now. You can change this later at any time.

user-invite-form.label-email-or-username_description: Label in the user invitation form: It prompts the user to enter either an email address or a username for the person being invited.
user-invite-form.label-send-invite-email_description: Button in the User Invite Form: Initiates the process of sending an invitation email to a user. This action is typically performed after user details have been entered into the form.
user-invite-form.placeholder-optional_description: Placeholder text in an input field on the user invite form: It indicates that the specific input field is optional and does not require user input.
user-invite-form.role_description: Label in User Invitation Form: Indicates the permission level or function assigned to the user being invited.
user-invite-form.submit_description: Button in the User Invitation Form: Used to finalize and send the invitation for a new user.
user-invite-form.tooltip: You can now select the "No basic role" option and add permissions to your custom needs. You can find more information in <1>our documentation</1> .

user-invite-page.sub-title: Send invitation or add existing Grafana user to the organization. <1> {{orgName}}</1>
user-invite-page.sub-title_description: Sub-title in the user invitation page within an organization's settings: It clarifies the methods available for adding users (inviting new or adding existing Grafana users) to the displayed organization.

text.invite-user_description: Button in User Invite Page: To initiate the process of inviting a new user.

org-picker.noOptionsMessage-no-organizations-found_: Message in the organization picker: This text is displayed when the list of organizations to choose from is empty. It informs the user that no organizations could be found or are available for selection.
org-picker.select-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Organization picker component: Prompts the user to select an organization from a dropdown or list.

editable-title.edit-tooltip_description: Tooltip in an editable title section of a page: Appears when hovering over an associated edit control, indicating that the title can be modified.

dashlist.empty-state-message_description: Empty state message in the Dashlist panel: Informs the user that no dashboard groups have been configured for display. It appears when the list is empty.
dashlist.recently-viewed-dashboards_description: Section label in the Dashlist component: Identifies the section displaying dashboards that have been recently viewed by the user.
dashlist.search_description: **Search input field** in **the dashboard list within a panel**: Enables users to filter and locate specific dashboards by entering keywords.
dashlist.starred-dashboards_description: Section label in the Dashlist panel: Indicates the section displaying dashboards that the user has marked as favorites for quick access.

label.calculation-name_description: Label in a configuration section for defining calculation variables within a dashboard panel: It prompts the user to enter or view the name of a calculation being configured.

label.time_description: Label in data link variable suggestions for a time-based value in a dashboard panel: This identifies a variable or field representing "Time" in the context of data link suggestions.

text.data_description: Text label in the field link configuration settings of a dashboard panel: Refers to data, likely as a category or option for defining field links.
text.field_description: Label in a dashboard panel's field links configuration section: It identifies a specific data field. This field is used to define or generate dynamic links based on the panel's data.
text.series_description: Label in a dashboard panel's field link configuration: Refers to a data series within a visualization. It is used when defining or accessing dynamic links related to data points or series.
text.value_description: Text label in the 'get-field-links-supplier' section of a dashboard panel: This label identifies a specific data value or parameter.

label.all-variables_description: Label in the variable suggestion dropdown for panel links: This option allows users to view or select all available variables when configuring a panel link.

header-menu.copy_description: Menu item in the header menu of a panel: Allows users to duplicate the current panel or its configuration.
header-menu.create-library-panel_description: Menu item in the header menu of a panel: Allows users to create a new library panel, a reusable component that can be shared across multiple dashboards.
header-menu.duplicate_description: Menu item in a panel's header menu: Allows users to create a duplicate copy of the current panel.
header-menu.explore_description: Menu item in the header menu of a panel: Initiates a deeper exploration or analysis of the panel's data or underlying metrics.
header-menu.get-help_description: Menu item in the header menu of a panel: Allows users to access help resources or documentation related to the panel.
header-menu.hide-legend_description: Menu item in the header menu of a dashboard panel: Used to hide the legend displayed within that panel.
header-menu.inspect_description: Menu Item in the panel's header menu: Allows users to examine the panel's underlying data, query, or configuration settings.
header-menu.inspect-data_description: Menu item in the panel's header menu: Allows users to inspect the raw data associated with the panel.
header-menu.inspect-json_description: Menu item in the header menu of a panel: Allows users to view or inspect the underlying JSON configuration for that specific panel.
header-menu.new-alert-rule_description: Menu item in a panel's header menu: For initiating the creation of a new alert rule.
header-menu.query_description: Menu item in the header menu of a dashboard panel: This item opens the query editor, enabling users to define and configure the data queries for the panel's visualization.
header-menu.replace-library-panel_description: Menu item in the header menu of a panel: Allows the user to replace the current panel with a panel chosen from the library.
header-menu.share_description: Menu Item in the header menu of a panel: Allows users to share the current panel or its content, often providing options like generating a link or exporting the view.
header-menu.show-legend_description: Menu item in the panel's header menu: This item controls the visibility of the data legend for the panel.
header-menu.time-settings_description: Menu item in the header menu of a dashboard panel: Used to access and configure the time-related display options or range for the panel.
header-menu.unlink-library-panel_description: Menu item in the header menu of a panel: Allows users to convert a panel that is currently linked to a library definition into a standalone, local panel. This action detaches the panel from its library source, enabling independent modification.
header-menu.view_description: Menu item in the header menu of a dashboard panel: Provides options to change or select different display configurations or perspectives for the panel.

missing-value.number: Data is missing a number field
missing-value.number_description: Error message in a panel's data error view: Informs the user that the data source for the panel lacks a necessary numeric field. This typically prevents the panel from rendering or functioning correctly.
missing-value.string: Data is missing a string field
missing-value.string_description: Error message in a panel's data error view: Informs the user that the data intended for display or processing in a dashboard panel is incomplete because a required string field is missing. This typically prevents the panel from rendering correctly.
missing-value.time: Data is missing a time field
missing-value.time_description: Error message in a dashboard panel's data error view: Informs the user that the data being displayed or processed in the panel is missing a required time field, which is essential for time-based visualizations or filtering.
missing-value.unknown_description: Error message in a dashboard panel's data error view: This message indicates that data cannot be displayed in the panel, often occurring when critical values are missing or an unknown visualization issue prevents rendering.

no-value.default_description: Status message in a dashboard panel's error view: It indicates that no data is available or could be retrieved for display within the panel.

panel-data-error-view.open-visualization-suggestions_descripti: Button in the panel data error view: This button opens a list of suggested visualizations. It is displayed when the panel encounters data-related errors, offering users options to troubleshoot or find alternative data representations.
panel-data-error-view.switch-to-table_description: Button in the panel's data error view: Allows the user to switch the data visualization to a tabular format. This option is typically presented when there is an error or issue with the default data display.

panel-plugin-error.text-load-error:
Check the server startup logs for more information.
If this plugin was loaded from Git, then make sure it was compiled.
panel-plugin-error.title-load-error_description: Error message within a panel's error display: Informs the user when a panel fails to load. It includes the ID of the specific panel that encountered the error.
panel-plugin-error.title-not-found_description: Error message title in a dashboard panel: This message is displayed when a required panel plugin cannot be found or loaded. It informs the user about the missing plugin, identified by `{{id}}`.

panel-renderer.failed-to-load-plugin_description: Error message in Panel Renderer: Informs the user when a plugin required for a dashboard panel fails to load, providing the specific error details.
panel-renderer.no-panel-component: Seems like the plugin you are trying to load does not have a panel component.
panel-renderer.no-panel-component_description: Error message in the panel renderer: Informs the user when a plugin selected for display does not contain a necessary panel component. This explains why a panel cannot be rendered.
panel-renderer.no-panel-data_description: Message in a dashboard panel's display area: Informs the user that the panel is currently empty because no data is available to be rendered or displayed within it.

panel-type-card.aria-label-delete-button-on-panel-type-c: Delete button on panel type card
Delete button in a panel type card: Used to delete the associated panel type card. This is an ARIA label for accessibility purposes.
panel-type-card.title-click-to-close: Click again to close this section
panel-type-card.title-click-to-close_description: Instructional text on the title bar of a panel-type card: It prompts the user to click the title a second time to close the currently open section within that card.

visualization-suggestions.based-on-current-data_description: Descriptive label in the Visualization Suggestions section of a panel: Informs the user that the suggested visualizations are derived from the data currently configured or displayed in the panel.
visualization-suggestions.no-results-matched-your-query_descriptio: Message in the visualization suggestions area of a dashboard panel: Informs the user that their current query or filter for visualization suggestions did not yield any results.

viz-type-picker.could-anything-matching-query: Could not find anything matching your query
viz-type-picker.could-anything-matching-query_descriptio: Message in the visualization type picker within a panel: This informs the user when a search for a specific visualization type within the picker yields no results. The query refers to the user's input to filter the available visualization options.

panel-type-filter.clear-button_description: Button in the panel type filter: Resets any selected or filtered panel types, returning the filter to its default state.
panel-type-filter.select-aria-label_description: ARIA label for a filter control in Dashboard filter options: This label identifies a control that allows users to filter the displayed panels by their type.
panel-type-filter.select-no-options_description: Message in Panel Type Filter: Informs the user that no panel types are available for selection in the filter dropdown or list. This typically appears when a search yields no results or no panel types are configured.
panel-type-filter.select-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a panel type filter component: This text prompts the user to select a type to filter the displayed items by. It appears in a dropdown or select input.

piechart.category-legend_description: Heading in a pie chart: For the legend section that explains the categories and their corresponding data representations.
piechart.category-pie-chart_description: Label in the chart selection or configuration interface: Identifies the standard pie chart visualization. This is the generic name for this chart type.
piechart.description-labels: Select the labels to be displayed in the pie chart
piechart.description-labels_description: Instructional text in the pie chart configuration panel: Guides the user to select which data labels (e.g., slice names, values, or percentages) should be visible on the pie chart. This control allows customizing the information displayed for each slice.
piechart.description-pie-chart-type: How the pie chart should be rendered
piechart.description-pie-chart-type_description: Descriptive text in the pie chart configuration panel: Explains the setting that determines how the pie chart visualization is rendered. This refers to the visual style or type of pie chart displayed.

labels-options.label-percent_description: Option in Pie Chart label options: Allows users to choose to display percentage values within the pie chart labels.
labels-options.label-value_description: Option in the Pie Chart visualization's label options: This setting controls the display of the raw numerical data value for each pie slice directly on the chart.

legend-values-options.label-percent_description: Label in Pie Chart legend value options: This label represents the option to display pie chart legend values as a percentage.
legend-values-options.label-value_description: Label in the legend values options of a pie chart: This label identifies the option to display the numerical or categorical value associated with each slice in the pie chart legend.

piechart.name-labels_description: Option labeled 'Labels' in the Pie Chart settings: This controls the display of text identifiers for the categories or names of the pie chart slices.
piechart.name-legend-values_description: Label in a pie chart's legend: This string refers to the numerical or categorical values displayed for each item within the chart's legend. It helps users interpret the data represented by each pie slice.
piechart.name-pie-chart-type_description: Label in the pie chart configuration panel: Describes the selection field for choosing the specific type or visual style of the pie chart, such as a standard pie or donut chart.

pie-chart-type-options.label-donut_description: Option label in the pie chart type selection: Identifies the "Donut" chart style, which is a circular chart with a hollow center.
pie-chart-type-options.label-pie_description: Option label in the pie chart type options: This label represents the standard circular pie chart type. It is used to select this specific chart style from available options.

text.new-playlist_description: Button label in the navigation area of the 'New Playlist' creation page: Initiates the process for creating a new playlist.

playlist-table-rows.aria-label-playlist-item_description: Accessibility label in the playlist table rows: Provides a descriptive name for a specific playlist entry, dynamically including its type and value for screen reader users.
playlist-table-rows.multiple-dashboards-found_description: Status message in the playlist table rows: Informs the user that multiple dashboards were found during a search or selection, with the `{{items}}` placeholder displaying the count or names of the found items.
playlist-table-rows.no-dashboards-found_description: Empty state message in a table or list displaying dashboards for a playlist: Informs the user when no dashboards are found.
