playlist-table-rows.not-found_description: Status message in the playlist table: Informs the user that specific playlist items could not be found. The `{{items}}` placeholder lists the names of the missing items.

start-modal.button-start_description: Button in the Start Modal within the Playlist feature: Initiates the playback or process of the item specified by the `{{title}}`. This button confirms the action to start a particular playlist or component.
start-modal.description-customize-dashboard-elements: Instruction text in the Playlist Start Modal: This text describes an option for users to customize the visibility of dashboard elements. It allows users to control which parts of the dashboard are shown during playlist playback.
start-modal.description-panel-heights-adjusted-scree: Panel heights will be adjusted to fit screen size

Informational text in the 'Start Playlist' modal dialog: This text informs the user that the heights of panels displayed as part of the playlist will automatically adjust to fit the current screen size.
start-modal.label-autofit_description: Option in the Playlist Start Modal: This option controls the automatic adjustment of content (e.g., dashboards, panels) to fit the display when the playlist starts.
start-modal.label-dashboard-links_description: Label in the start modal of a playlist: Identifies a section or field that displays or manages links to dashboards.
start-modal.label-display-dashboard-controls_descrip: Label in the "Start Playlist" modal: This setting controls whether the standard dashboard controls (e.g., time range, refresh, variables) are visible when a dashboard plays as part of a playlist.
start-modal.label-mode_description: Label in the playlist start modal: Indicates a selection option for the playback or presentation style of the playlist. This likely precedes a control for choosing how the playlist will run.
start-modal.label-time-and-refresh_description: Label in the start modal for a playlist: This label introduces a section where users can configure the time range and refresh frequency for the dashboards within the playlist.
start-modal.label-variables_description: Label in the 'Start' modal for a Playlist: Identifies the section or configuration options for dashboard variables.

label.kiosk_description: Label in the playlist start modal: Identifies a display mode named 'Kiosk' for running a playlist. This mode likely enables an automated, full-screen, and continuous presentation of dashboards.
label.normal_description: Label in the Start Playlist modal's modes selection: Represents the standard or default playback mode for a playlist.

start-modal.title-start-playlist_description: Modal Title in the Playlist start modal: This title is displayed at the top of the modal window that prompts users to start a playlist.

playlist-edit.error-prefix_description: Error message prefix in the Playlist Edit screen: Informs the user that an error occurred while attempting to load a playlist. This prefix is followed by more specific details about the error.

form.add-tag-label_description: Label or section heading in the playlist editing form: It identifies a method for adding items to the playlist based on their assigned tags.
form.add-tag-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the tag input or selection field within the playlist editing form: It guides the user to select or enter tags for the playlist.
form.add-title-label_description: Label in the Playlist Edit form: This label indicates a method for adding items to the playlist by searching or filtering them based on their title.
form.heading: Add dashboards
form.heading_description: Heading in the 'Add dashboards' form: This text introduces the section where users can select and add dashboards to a playlist within the playlist editing interface.
form.interval-label_description: Label in a form within the playlist editing section: This label identifies a field where users define the time duration for displaying each item in a playlist before advancing to the next.
form.interval-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the playlist edit form: Displays an example or default value for a time interval input field. It indicates a duration of 5 minutes.
form.interval-required_description: Validation message in a form on the playlist edit screen: Informs the user that the "Interval" field is mandatory and must be provided before proceeding.
form.name-required_description: Validation message in the Edit Playlist form: Informs the user that the 'Name' field is mandatory and must be provided before saving or proceeding.
form.table-delete_description: Button in the playlist editing form: Deletes an individual item from the playlist. This button typically appears next to an item in a list or table within the form.
form.table-drag_description: Instruction text in a form on the playlist edit page: Informs the user how to reorder items within a table or list using drag-and-drop functionality. This text guides the user on the interactive reordering method.
form.table-empty: Playlist is empty. Add dashboards below.
form.table-heading_description: Table heading in the Edit Playlist form: Labels the section or table where dashboards are listed or managed for the playlist.

playlist-edit.sub-title: A playlist rotates through a pre-selected list of dashboards. A playlist can be a great way to build situational awareness, or just show off your metrics to your team or visitors.
playlist-edit.title: Edit playlist
playlist-edit.title_description: Page title or dialog title in the 'Edit Playlist' section: Indicates to the user that they are currently editing an existing playlist.

card.delete_description: Button in a playlist card on the playlist page: Allows the user to delete the entire playlist associated with that card.
card.edit_description: Button or link in a playlist card on the playlist page: Allows the user to modify the selected playlist.
card.start_description: Button in a playlist card on the playlist page: Initiates the playback of the playlist. This button is displayed on a card that represents a specific playlist.
card.tooltip_description: Tooltip in a card on the playlist page: This text appears when hovering over an icon or button, indicating that clicking it will allow the user to share the playlist.

create-button.title: New playlist
create-button.title_description: Button label on the Playlist page for a 'create' button: Used to initiate the creation of a new playlist.

delete-modal.body: Are you sure you want to delete {{name}} playlist?
delete-modal.body_description: Confirmation message in Delete Confirmation Modal on the Playlist Page: Asks the user to confirm the deletion of a specific playlist. The `{{name}}` placeholder will be replaced with the actual playlist's name.

empty.button_description: Button on the empty state of the Playlists page: Allows the user to create their first playlist.
empty.pro-tip: You can use playlists to cycle dashboards on TVs without user control. <2>Learn more</2>
empty.pro-tip_description: Pro-tip message in the empty state of the Playlists page: Informs the user about the primary use case of playlists, which is to cycle dashboards on TVs without user control.
empty.title: There are no playlists created yet
empty.title_description: Title in the empty state of the Playlist page: Informs the user that no playlists have been created yet.

empty-state.message_description: Message in Empty state of the Playlists section: Informs the user that no playlists have been created or are available to display.

plugin-details-deprecated-warning.body-deprecated: This {{pluginType}} plugin is <4>deprecated</4> and has been removed from the catalog.
plugin-details-deprecated-warning.body-deprecated_description: Warning message in the Plugin Details section or a related dialog: Informs the user that a specific plugin type has been deprecated and removed from the catalog, indicating it's no longer supported or available.

access-denied.permission: You do not have permission to see this page.
access-denied.permission_description: Error message on the Access Denied page within a plugin's root application: Informs the user that they lack the required permissions to view the current page.
access-denied.title-access-denied_description: Title in the Access Denied screen of a plugin's root page: Informs the user they do not have permission to access the requested resource or page.

app-root-page.error-loading-plugin_description: Error message in the application's root page: Informs the user that a plugin failed to load. This indicates a technical issue preventing the plugin from functioning.
app-root-page.no-root-app-page-component-found: No root app page component found
app-root-page.no-root-app-page-component-found_descrip: **Error Message** in **the plugin's root application page**: Informs the user that a critical component required to render the main application page of a plugin could not be found. This indicates a configuration or installation issue preventing the plugin from loading correctly.

browse.aria-label-plugin-type-filter_descriptio: Filter control in the Plugin browse page: Allows users to narrow down the displayed list of plugins by selecting specific plugin types. This string functions as an ARIA label for accessibility.

label.all_description: Option label in the 'Filter by' options within the Plugins Browse section: Represents the choice to display all available items. It is used to clear or bypass any specific filter selections.
label.installed_description: Label in the Plugins Browse page, within the filter options: This label represents an option to filter the list of plugins to display only those that are currently installed.
label.new-updates_description: Label in the filter options section of the Plugins Browse page: Allows users to filter or sort plugins to display those that have received recent updates.

label.all_description: Label in the Plugins browse section: Represents an option to view all available plugins, categories, or to clear any active filters. This option typically ensures no filtering is applied.
label.applications_description: Label in the Plugins browsing interface: This label identifies a section or category where users can browse or manage applications. It is likely used as a heading or a menu item to navigate to a list of applications.
label.data-sources_description: Section title in the plugins browsing interface: Labels the area where users can view, configure, or manage connections to various data sources used within the dashboarding system.
label.panels_description: Label in the Plugins browser: This label denotes a category or section for managing 'Panels,' which are individual visual components used in dashboards.

browse.label-search_description: Placeholder text in the Plugins browse section: for a search input field to allow users to filter and find specific plugins.
browse.label-state_description: Label in the Plugins Browsing interface: It indicates the current operational state or status of a plugin.
browse.label-type_description: Label in the plugins browse interface: Identifies the category or classification of a plugin displayed in the list.
browse.tooltip-filter-disabled: This filter has been disabled because the Grafana server cannot access grafana.com
browse.tooltip-filter-disabled_description: Tooltip in the Plugins browse section: Explains why a filter is currently disabled. It informs the user that the Grafana server is unable to access grafana.com, which is preventing the filter from functioning.

catalog.no-updates-available_description: Informational text in the Plugins Catalog: Indicates that the system has checked for plugin updates and found none available.

update-all.all-plugins-updated_description: Confirmation message in the Plugins Catalog: Indicates that a bulk update operation for all plugins has completed successfully.
update-all.available-header_description: Header in the Plugin Catalog's "Update All" section: Labels a list or group of plugin updates that are currently available for installation.
update-all.button_description: Button in the Plugins Catalog: Triggers an update for all plugins available for update. The `{{length}}` placeholder shows the number of plugins to be updated.
update-all.cloud-update-message: * It may take a few minutes for the plugins to be available for usage.
update-all.cloud-update-message_description: Informational message on the Plugins Catalog page: This message informs the user that it may take a few minutes for plugins to be fully available after an 'Update All' operation. It is specifically related to cloud updates.
update-all.error_description: Error message in the Plugins Catalog's 'Update All' functionality: Appears when an attempt to update a plugin fails during the bulk update process. It includes a placeholder for the specific error details.
update-all.error-status-text_description: Status text in the Plugins Catalog update functionality: Indicates that the 'Update All' plugins operation failed. It directs the user to review specific error messages for more details.
update-all.header: The following plugins have update available
update-all.header_description: Header in the Plugin Catalog's 'Update All' section: Notifies the user that the following list contains plugins with available updates. This text appears when multiple plugins have updates.
update-all.installed-header_description: Header label in the Plugins Catalog 'Update All' section: It identifies a list or category of plugins that are currently installed.
update-all.modal-title_description: Modal title in the plugin catalog: This title labels a dialog where users are prompted to update plugins, likely in a bulk action.
update-all.update-status-text_description: Status message in the 'Update All' section of the Plugins Catalog: This message confirms that a batch update operation on plugins has been successfully completed. It indicates the current status after the update process.

versions.confirmation-text: Are you really sure you want to downgrade to version {{version}}? You should normally not be doing this
versions.downgrade-confirm_description: Button in a plugin version management confirmation dialog: Confirms the action of downgrading a selected plugin to an older version.
versions.downgrade-title_description: Title in the Plugins Catalog, within the plugin versions management interface: Identifies the action or dialog for reverting an installed plugin to an older, previously installed version.

connections-tab.description: You currently have the following data sources configured for {{pluginName}}, click a tile to view the configuration details. You can find all of your data source connections in <4><5>Connections</5> - <8>Data sources</8>.</4>

disabled-error.angular-deprecation-link_description: Link in a disabled plugin error message on the Plugin Details page: Allows users to read more about Angular deprecation, which is likely the reason for the plugin being disabled.
disabled-error.angular-error-text: This plugin has been disabled as Grafana no longer supports Angular based plugins. You can try updating the plugin to the latest version to resolve this issue. You should then test to confirm it works as expected.
disabled-error.angular-error-text-no-non-angular-versio: This plugin has been disabled as Grafana no longer supports Angular based plugins. Unfortunately, the latest version of this plugin still uses Angular so you need to wait for the plugin author to migrate to continue using this plugin.
disabled-error.contact-server-admin: Please contact your server administrator to get this resolved.
disabled-error.contact-server-admin_description: Error message text in the Plugin Details section: Informs the user that a plugin is disabled and they need to contact their server administrator to resolve the issue.
disabled-error.failed-backend-start-text: This plugin failed to start. Server logs can provide more information.
disabled-error.invalid-signature-text: Grafana Labs checks each plugin to verify that it has a valid digital signature. While doing this, we discovered that it was invalid. We can not guarantee the trustworthy of this plugin and have therefore disabled it. We recommend you to reinstall the plugin to make sure you are running a verified version of this plugin.
disabled-error.manage-plugins-link_description: Link in the plugin details disabled error screen: Guides users to documentation on how to manage plugins. This link is displayed when a plugin encounters a disabled error, offering a way to find more information or resolve the issue.
disabled-error.missing-signature-text: Grafana Labs checks each plugin to verify that it has a valid digital signature. While doing this, we discovered that there is no signature for this plugin. We can not guarantee the trustworthy of this plugin and have therefore disabled it. We recommend you to reinstall the plugin to make sure you are running a verified version of this plugin.
disabled-error.modified-signature-text: Grafana Labs checks each plugin to verify that it has a valid digital signature. While doing this, we discovered that the content of this plugin does not match its signature. We can not guarantee the trustworthy of this plugin and have therefore disabled it. We recommend you to reinstall the plugin to make sure you are running a verified version of this plugin.
disabled-error.title: Plugin disabled
disabled-error.title_description: Title in the plugin details view: Informs the user that the displayed plugin is currently disabled. This message is shown when the plugin is in a disabled error state.
disabled-error.unknown-error-text: We failed to run this plugin due to an unkown reason and have therefore disabled it. We recommend you to reinstall the plugin to make sure you are running a working version of this plugin.

labels.contactGrafanaLabs_description: Button text in the plugin details view: This text labels a button or link that allows users to contact Grafana Labs, typically for support or inquiries related to the plugin.
labels.customLinks_description: Label in Plugin Details: Indicates a section or feature dedicated to managing user-defined links. These links are typically external resources or custom navigation options associated with the plugin.
labels.customLinksTooltip: These links are provided by the plugin developer to offer additional, developer-specific resources and information
labels.dependencies_description: Label or Heading in the Plugin Details screen: Indicates a section or list where related components or other plugins that this plugin relies on, or that rely on this plugin, are displayed.
labels.documentation_description: Label on the plugin details page: This label identifies a link or section that provides access to the documentation for the selected plugin.
labels.downloads_description: Label in the plugin details section: Identifies a feature or area dedicated to downloads for that specific plugin. This could be a heading for a list of downloadable files, a count of downloads, or a button/link to initiate a download.
labels.from_description: Label in the plugin details section: Used to specify the beginning of a time or data range. This helps define the scope of information displayed by the plugin.
labels.installedVersion_description: Label in the details panel of a plugin: Displays the version number of a specific plugin that is currently installed on the system.
labels.latestReleaseDate_description: Label in the Plugin details section: Indicates the most recent date a plugin was released. It is typically followed by the actual release date.
labels.latestVersion_description: Label in Plugins Details: Indicates the most current release version of a plugin.
labels.license_description: Label in the plugin details section: Identifies information related to the licensing of the plugin.
labels.raiseAnIssue_description: Button label in the plugin details section: Text for a button that users click to report a problem or issue pertaining to the plugin being viewed.
labels.reportAbuse_description: Label in the Plugin Details section: This text provides an option for users to report concerns or inappropriate content related to the specific plugin being viewed.
labels.reportAbuseTooltip: Report issues related to malicious or harmful plugins directly to Grafana Labs.
labels.reportAbuseTooltip_description: Tooltip in the plugin details view: Informs users about the process for reporting malicious or harmful plugins. It directs users to report such issues directly to Grafana Labs.
labels.repository_description: Label in the Plugins Details section: Identifies the source repository or storage location of a plugin.
labels.signature_description: Label in the details view of a plugin: Identifies a field displaying information about the plugin's digital signature, often for authenticity and verification purposes.
labels.sponsorDeveloper_description: Button text in the Plugin Details section: Prompts the user to financially support or endorse the developer of the displayed plugin.
labels.status_description: Label in the plugin details view: It indicates the current operational state or condition of the plugin.

modal.copyEmail_description: Button in the plugin details modal: Allows the user to copy the support or contact email address for the plugin displayed in the modal.
modal.description: This feature is for reporting malicious or harmful behaviour within plugins. For plugin concerns, email us at:
modal.node: Note: For general plugin issues like bugs or feature requests, please contact the plugin author using the provided links.
modal.title: Report a plugin concern
modal.title_description: Modal Title in the plugin details view: This title indicates that the modal allows users to report an issue or concern about a specific plugin they are currently viewing.

empty-state.message_description: Message in Plugins section: Informs the user that no plugins have been found or installed. This message is displayed when the plugins list is empty.

extensions.extension-error-alert-description: Check the console for more details on the error.
extensions.extension-error-alert-description_descri: Error alert description in the Plugins > Extensions section: Advises the user to check the browser's developer console for more details when an error related to an extension occurs. This message helps users troubleshoot issues.
extensions.extension-error-alert-title_description: Alert title in the Extensions management section: Informs the user when a specific extension fails to load. The placeholders display the ID of the plugin and the title of the extension that encountered the error.

filter.disabled: This filter has been disabled because the Grafana server cannot access grafana.com
filter.disabled_description: Status message in the Grafana UI, specifically within the plugin management interface or when browsing available plugins: This message explains why a particular filter is disabled, indicating that the Grafana server's inability to access `grafana.com` prevents the filter from functioning.
filter.sort_description: Control in the filter options: Allows users to specify the order in which displayed data or available filter choices are presented.
filter.sort-list_description: Button in the Plugins > Filter section: Used to sort the displayed list of plugins.
filter.state_description: Filter label in the plugin filter section: Used to specify the operational status or condition of a plugin.

text.alpha_description: Text label in the 'Get Feature State Info' section within Plugins: It indicates that a feature or plugin is in its 'Alpha' development stage.
text.beta_description: A label in the feature state information for plugins: It indicates that a specific feature is currently in its beta phase.
text.deprecated_description: Status label in the plugin feature state information display: Indicates that a specific plugin feature is no longer recommended or supported.

get-started-with-app.disable_description: Button within the 'Get Started with App' plugin's configuration: Allows users to deactivate the plugin. Disabling it will stop its functionality.
get-started-with-app.enable_description: Button or action label in the 'Get Started with App' section of the Plugins area: Used to activate or enable a specific plugin or application as part of the initial setup process.

get-started-with-data-source.add-new-data-source_description: Button in the 'Get Started with Data Source' section of the Plugins interface: Initiates the workflow for adding a new data source to BMC Helix Dashboards.
get-started-with-data-source.title-button-disabled: The plugin isn't usable yet, it may take some time to complete the installation.
get-started-with-data-source.title-button-disabled_description: Informational message on the 'Get Started with Data Source' screen for a plugin. It explains that the plugin is not yet ready for use because its installation is still in progress.

install-controls.install_description: Button in the plugin installation controls section: Initiates the installation process for a selected plugin. It confirms the user's intent to start the installation.
install-controls.installing_description: Status label in the plugin installation controls: Indicates that a selected plugin is currently in the process of being installed.
install-controls.updating_description: Status message in the Plugins installation and control interface: Informs the user that a plugin is currently being updated.

install-controls-button.title-uninstall-modal_description: Modal Title in the Uninstall Confirmation Dialog: This title informs the user that they are about to uninstall a specific plugin. The `{{plugin}}` placeholder will be dynamically replaced with the name of the plugin being uninstalled.

uninstall-controls.body-uninstall-plugin: Are you sure you want to uninstall this plugin?
uninstall-controls.body-uninstall-plugin_description: Confirmation Message in a confirmation dialog: Asks the user to confirm their intention to uninstall a selected plugin. This message is typically shown after clicking an 'Uninstall' button for a plugin.

install-controls-warning.body-not-published: This plugin is not published to <2>grafana.com/plugins</2> and can't be managed via the catalog.
install-controls-warning.enterprise-plugin-info: This plugin is only available in Grafana Cloud and Grafana Enterprise.
install-controls-warning.enterprise-plugin-info_description: Warning Message in the Plugin Installation/Management screen: Informs the user that a specific plugin is only available for Grafana Cloud or Grafana Enterprise editions. This clarifies availability restrictions for plugins.
install-controls-warning.learn-more_description: Link in a warning message concerning plugin installation controls: Allows users to access more detailed information or documentation related to the warning.
install-controls-warning.title-dev-alert: This is a development build of the plugin and can't be uninstalled.
install-controls-warning.title-dev-alert_description: Alert title in the Plugin management interface: Informs the user that the displayed plugin is a development build and cannot be uninstalled, serving as a warning.
install-controls-warning.title-plugin-doesnt-support-version-graf: This plugin doesn't support your version of Grafana.
Warning Title in the Plugin installation UI: Informs the user that the plugin they are trying to install or manage is not compatible with their current Grafana version.
install-controls-warning.title-remote-plugins-unavailable: The install controls have been disabled because the Grafana server cannot access grafana.com.
install-controls-warning.title-renderer-plugins-cannot-managed-pl: Renderer plugins cannot be managed by the Plugin Catalog.

Title in a warning message within the plugin installation controls: Informs the user that renderer plugins are not manageable through the Plugin Catalog. This clarifies a limitation regarding specific plugin types.

log-view-filters.label-extension_description: Label in Log View Filters: This label identifies a filter option used to specify or display the file extension of log entries. It allows users to filter logs based on their file extension (e.g., .log, .txt).
log-view-filters.label-plugin-id_description: Label in Log View Filters: Identifies an input field or column where the unique identifier for a plugin can be entered or displayed. This field is used to filter log data associated with specific plugins.
log-view-filters.label-severity_description: Label in log view filters: This label identifies the control used to filter log entries based on their severity level.

no-updates-available.message: All plugins are up to date
no-updates-available.message_description: Status message in the Plugins management section: Informs the user that all installed plugins are currently updated to their latest versions, requiring no further action.

not-found-plugin.body-plugin-not-found:
That plugin cannot be found. Please check the url is correct or
go to the <3>plugin catalog.
not-found-plugin.title-plugin-not-found_description: Title in the 'Plugin not found' interface: Informs the user that a requested or expected plugin is missing. This serves as a prominent header for the associated error message.

plugin-actions.refresh-changes: Refresh the page to see the changes
plugin-actions.refresh-changes_description: Notification message in the context of plugin actions: It informs the user that changes made through a plugin action require a page refresh to become visible.

plugin-dashboards.dashboards-included-plugin: No dashboards are included with this plugin
plugin-dashboards.dashboards-included-plugin_description: Status message in the plugin details view: Informs the user that the currently selected or viewed plugin does not come with any pre-built dashboards. This message appears when a plugin that could potentially offer bundled dashboards is found to have none.

plugin-deprecated-badge.text-deprecated_description: Badge text on a plugin entry: Indicates that the plugin is deprecated. This informs users that the plugin is no longer recommended or supported.
plugin-deprecated-badge.tooltip-plugin-deprecated-longer-receive: This plugin is deprecated and no longer receives updates.
Tooltip on a deprecated plugin badge: Informs the user that the associated plugin is no longer maintained or receives updates. This appears when hovering over the deprecated badge.

plugin-details-body.needs-service-account: The {{pluginName}} plugin needs a service account to be able to query Grafana. The following list contains the permissions available to the service account:
plugin-details-body.page-not-found_description: Error message in a plugin's details page within BMC Helix Dashboards: Informs the user that the specific page or resource they attempted to access within the plugin's details could not be found.

plugin-details-deprecated-warning.title-deprecated_description: Title in the plugin details deprecated warning: Informs users that a specific plugin is no longer recommended for use or supported. It serves as a prominent label for this status.

plugin-details-header-dependencies.grafana-dependency_description: Label in the Plugin Details header's dependencies section: This string identifies a dependency related to Grafana. The `{{grafanaDependency}}` placeholder will display the specific Grafana version or type that the plugin depends on.

subTitle.requested-belong-plugin: The requested ID does not belong to any plugin
subTitle.requested-belong-plugin_description: Subtitle in a 'Plugin Not Found' modal on the Plugin Details page: Informs the user that the plugin ID they attempted to access or was referenced does not correspond to an existing plugin.

text.unknown-plugin_description: Error message in the 'Plugin Not Found' modal on the Plugin Details page: Informs the user that the requested plugin could not be found or identified.

plugin-details-signature.body-invalid-plugin-signature: Grafana Labs checks each plugin to verify that it has a valid digital signature. Plugin signature verification is part of our security measures to ensure plugins are safe and trustworthy. Grafana Labs can’t guarantee the integrity of this unsigned plugin. Ask the plugin author to request it to be signed.
plugin-details-signature.read-more-about-plugins-signing_descript: Link in the Plugin Details page's signature information section: Allows users to access additional documentation or information regarding plugin signing.
plugin-details-signature.title-invalid-plugin-signature_descripti: Title in Plugin Details Signature section: Informs the user that a plugin's digital signature is invalid. This message indicates a security or integrity issue with the plugin.

plugin-disabled-badge.text-disabled_description: Text label in a badge on a plugin listing or management page: Indicates that a specific plugin is currently disabled.

plugin-enterprise-badge.aria-label-enterprise_description: ARIA label for an Enterprise badge in a plugin: Provides an accessible name for a visual indicator signifying an "Enterprise" feature or status.
plugin-enterprise-badge.text-enterprise_description: Text label in an Enterprise badge: Identifies an associated plugin or feature as part of the Enterprise edition or offering enterprise-level functionality.
plugin-enterprise-badge.title-requires-a-grafana-enterprise-lice: Title in a plugin's Enterprise Badge: Informs the user that the associated plugin or feature requires a Grafana Enterprise license. This title likely appears as part of a visual indicator or informational pop-up for enterprise-specific plugins.

plugin-help.error: An error occurred when loading help.
plugin-help.error_description: Error message in the help section of a plugin: Informs the user that an attempt to load the help content for the plugin has failed.
plugin-help.not-found: No query help could be found.
plugin-help.not-found_description: Informational message in a plugin's help feature: Notifies the user when specific help documentation or guidance for a query cannot be located or displayed.

plugin-installed-badge.text-installed_description: A text label within a plugin installed badge in the Plugins section: It indicates that a specific plugin has been successfully installed.

plugin-list-item.label-author_description: Label in a plugin list item: This text identifies the author of a specific plugin. `{{author}}` is a placeholder that will be replaced with the actual author's name.
plugin-list-item.title-icon-plugin-type_description: Text label in a plugin list item on the Plugins management page: Displays the specific category or classification of a plugin. The `{{pluginType}}` placeholder will be replaced by the actual type, such as 'Data source' or 'Panel'.

plugin-signature-details-badge.signed-by_description: Text label in the plugin signature details badge: Identifies the organization that signed the plugin, confirming its origin and authenticity. The `{{signatureOrg}}` placeholder is replaced with the actual organization's name.

plugin-usage.body-missing-feature-toggle-panel-title-: Plugin usage requires the new search index to find usage across dashboards. Please enable the feature toggle
plugin-usage.num-usages_description: Display text in the Plugin Usage section: This text shows how many times a specific plugin has been used. The number of usages is highlighted for emphasis.
plugin-usage.title-missing-feature-toggle-panel-title: Panel Title in the Plugin Usage section: This title informs the user about a missing feature toggle, identified as `{{toggle}}`, which is preventing some plugin functionality. It appears at the top of a panel detailing this specific issue.
plugin-usage.title-not-used-yet: {{pluginName}} is not used in any dashboards yet
plugin-usage.title-not-used-yet_description: Status message in the Plugin Usage section: Informs the user that a specified plugin has not yet been incorporated into any dashboards.

plugins-errors-info.disabled-list: The following plugins are disabled and not shown in the list below:
plugins-errors-info.disabled-list_description: Informational text in the Plugins management section, displayed above a list of plugins: It notifies the user that specific plugins are disabled and consequently excluded from the displayed list. This clarifies why certain expected plugins might not be visible.
plugins-errors-info.read-more-about-plugin-signing_descripti: Link in the Plugins Errors Info section: Directs users to more information about plugin signing. This link is typically displayed in the context of errors or warnings related to plugin signing.
plugins-errors-info.title-unsigned-plugins: Unsigned plugins were found during plugin initialization. Grafana Labs cannot guarantee the integrity of these plugins. We recommend only using signed plugins.

search-field.placeholder-search-grafana-plugins_descr: Placeholder text in the search field of the Plugins section: Prompts users to search for Grafana plugins.

text.dashboards_description: Navigation item in the plugin details view: Represents a link or tab to access dashboards related to the current plugin. Users click this to view or manage relevant dashboards.

version-install-button.text-installed_description: Status label in the Plugins section, associated with a version installation button: Indicates that a specific plugin version has been successfully installed.

version-list.grafana-dependency_description: Label in the plugin version list: This field indicates the specific Grafana version or component that a plugin relies upon to function correctly. It helps users understand compatibility requirements.
version-list.installed-version_description: Display text in the plugin version list: Indicates the version number of a plugin that is currently installed. The `{{versionNumber}}` is a dynamic placeholder.
version-list.latest-compatible-version_description: Informational text in a list of plugin versions: It identifies a specific plugin version as the latest one that is compatible. This text is displayed alongside the actual version number.
version-list.latest-release-date_description: Label in the Plugin Version List: Displays the release date of the most recent available version for a specific plugin.
version-list.no-version-history-was-found_description: Informational message in the version history list of a plugin: This message is displayed when the system attempts to retrieve version history for a plugin or an item within a plugin, but no historical versions are found.
version-list.version_description: Label in a list or table displaying plugin versions: Identifies the specific version number of a plugin.

change-password.cannot-change-password-message_descripti: Informational message in the Change Password section of the user profile: Informs the user that their password cannot be changed directly within this application interface. This message appears when the password management is handled externally (e.g., by an identity provider) or in a different part of the system.
change-password.change-password-button_description: Button in the Change Password section of the user profile: Submits the user's new password.
change-password.confirm-password-label_description: Label in the 'Change Password' screen within the user's profile: Prompts the user to re-enter their new password for verification.
change-password.confirm-password-required_description: Validation message in the 'Change Password' screen: Notifies the user that the 'Confirm New Password' field is required and cannot be left empty when attempting to change their password.
change-password.ldap-auth-proxy-message: You cannot change password when signed in with LDAP or auth proxy.
change-password.ldap-auth-proxy-message_description: Informational message in the Change Password screen within the user profile: Informs the user that they cannot change their password when authenticated via LDAP or an authentication proxy.
change-password.new-password-label_description: Label in the Change Password screen within the Profile section: Prompts the user to enter their new desired password. It is typically associated with an input field where the user types this new password.
change-password.new-password-required_description: Error message in the 'Change Password' section of the user profile: Informs the user that the new password field cannot be left empty when attempting to change their password.
change-password.new-password-same-as-old: New password can't be the same as the old one.
change-password.new-password-same-as-old_description: Validation message in the 'Change Password' section of the user profile: Informs the user that the new password they entered is the same as their current password, which is not allowed. This message appears when the system validates the new password against the old one.
change-password.old-password-label_description: Label in the "Change Password" form within the user's "Profile" settings: Prompts the user to enter their current, existing password.
change-password.old-password-required_description: Validation message in the Change Password form within the User Profile settings: Informs the user that the "Old Password" field is mandatory and must be filled to proceed with changing the password.
change-password.passwords-must-match_description: Validation error message in the "Change Password" form within the user profile settings: Informs the user that the 'New password' and 'Confirm new password' fields do not contain identical values.
change-password.strong-password-validation-register: Password does not comply with the strong password policy
change-password.strong-password-validation-register_desc: Validation message in the "Change Password" section of the user profile: Informs the user that the new password they entered does not comply with the strong password policy requirements.

profile.change-theme_description: Link in the user's profile settings: Allows the user to modify the application's visual theme.
profile.enable-kiosk-mode_description: Setting in Profile Settings: Allows the user to activate Kiosk Mode for displaying dashboards. Kiosk mode typically hides navigation and other UI elements for a focused, full-screen view.

feature-toggle-page.profile-not-enabled_description: Informational message on the Feature Toggle page in the Profile settings: Informs the user that the profile feature or functionality is currently disabled.

feature-toggles-age.enable-in-config: Enable profile in the Grafana config file.
feature-toggles-age.enable-in-config_description: Configuration setting in the Grafana configuration file: Enables the main 'profile' functionality or feature within Grafana. This specific setting is located under the `feature-toggles-age` section of the profile configuration.

input-suffix.content-login-details-locked-because-man: Login details locked because they are managed in another system.

Informational text adjacent to login detail input fields in the user profile section: It explains that the user's login details are locked because they are managed by another, external system, preventing direct modification within the current system.

user-sessions.browser-details_description: Text string in the User Sessions section of the User Profile: Displays the browser and operating system information (browser type, OS name, and version) for a specific user session.
user-sessions.now_description: Time range option in the User Sessions section: Allows the user to view or filter for sessions that are active at the current moment.
user-sessions.sessions_description: Section title in the User Sessions section within a user's profile: Labels the content displaying information about a user's login sessions, such as active or past sessions.

user-teams.aria-label-user-teams-table_description: ARIA label for a table in the User Teams section of the user profile: This label provides an accessible name for the table displaying teams associated with the current user. It is used by screen readers to describe the table's content.
user-teams.members_description: Label in the User Teams section of the user's Profile: Identifies the list of individuals belonging to the user's teams.
user-teams.name: Name
user-teams.teams_description: Section title in the 'User Teams' section of a user's profile: It labels the area where a user's associated teams are displayed or managed.

save-or-delete-resource-shared-fields.comment-placeholder-describe-changes-opt: Add a note to describe your changes (optional)

Placeholder text in a comment field within a provisioned resource management form: It prompts the user to optionally describe the changes being made, whether saving modifications or initiating a deletion.
save-or-delete-resource-shared-fields.description-branch-name-in-git-hub_descr: Description text for an input field in the Provisioned Resource Form: Explains that the field is for specifying the GitHub branch name. This is part of the shared fields used when saving or deleting a resource.
save-or-delete-resource-shared-fields.description-file-path: File path inside the repository (.json or .yaml)
save-or-delete-resource-shared-fields.description-folder-path_description: Label for a text input field in the 'Save or Delete Resource Shared Fields' section of the Provisioned Resource form: This field prompts the user to enter the specific folder path where a resource is located within its repository.
save-or-delete-resource-shared-fields.description-inside-repository_descriptio: **Label** in **the 'Save or Delete Resource Shared Fields' section within the 'Provisioned Resource Form'**: This label identifies a text input field for entering or viewing the description of a provisioned resource, specifically concerning how it is represented or stored within a repository. It clarifies the resource's context from the repository's perspective.
save-or-delete-resource-shared-fields.label-branch_description: Label in the "Save or Delete Resource Shared Fields" section of the Provisioned Resource Form: It identifies an input field for specifying the version control branch (e.g., Git branch) associated with the provisioned resource.
save-or-delete-resource-shared-fields.label-comment_description: Label for a comment field in the 'Save or Delete Resource' section of the Provisioned Resource form: It prompts users to enter additional notes or remarks about the resource operation.
save-or-delete-resource-shared-fields.label-path_description: Label for an input field in the 'Save or Delete Resource' section of the 'Provisioned Resource' form. This field specifies the file system or logical path for the resource.
save-or-delete-resource-shared-fields.label-workflow_description: Label in the 'Save or Delete Resource Shared Fields' section of the 'Provisioned Resource Form': Identifies a field or section where users can define, select, or view the workflow associated with the provisioned resource. This workflow likely dictates processes related to managing the resource.
save-or-delete-resource-shared-fields.placeholder-branch_description: Placeholder text for an input field within the 'Save or Delete Resource Shared Fields' section on the 'Provisioned Resource' form: It prompts the user to select an existing branch or enter a new branch name to associate with the resource.
save-or-delete-resource-shared-fields.placeholder-new-branch_description: Placeholder text in an input field within the 'Save or Delete Resource Shared Fields' section of a provisioned resource form: It prompts the user to enter a name for a new branch. This is likely used when saving a new version or configuration of a resource.
save-or-delete-resource-shared-fields.suffix-configured-branch_description: Label in the 'Shared Fields' section of the 'Provisioned Resource' form: Indicates the specific branch (e.g., source code, deployment) that is configured for or associated with the provisioned resource.
save-or-delete-resource-shared-fields.suffix-last-used_description: Label in the "Save or Delete Resource Shared Fields" section of the Provisioned Resource Form: Refers to or identifies the last used branch for the resource being managed.
save-or-delete-resource-shared-fields.suffix-pr-branch_description: Field label in the 'Save or Delete Resource Shared Fields' section of the Provisioned Resource Form: Labels the input field where a user specifies the Git pull request branch. This branch likely serves as a suffix for naming or identifying the provisioned resource or its related deployment artifacts.

preview-banner.behind-branch-text: This resource is behind the branch in {{repoType}}.
preview-banner.behind-branch-text_description: Status message in the preview banner of a provisioned resource: Informs the user that the current resource is outdated compared to its source repository. It indicates that the resource is behind the latest version available in the specified repository type (e.g., Git).
preview-banner.branch-text_description: Label in a preview banner within the provisioned resource section: It identifies the specific branch or version of the resource that is currently being previewed.
preview-banner.not-saved: The rest of Grafana users in your organization will still see the current version saved to configured default branch until this branch is merged
preview-banner.open-in-repo-button_description: Button in the preview banner for a provisioned resource: Allows users to open the currently viewed resource in its associated repository, with `{{repoType}}` dynamically displaying the specific repository type.
preview-banner.open-pull-request-in-repo_description: Link label in a preview banner for a provisioned resource: This link enables users to open a pull request in the relevant code repository for the resource. The `{{repoType}}` placeholder will be replaced with the specific type of repository (e.g., GitHub).
preview-banner.view-in-repo-button: View it in {{repoType}} to see the latest changes.
preview-banner.view-in-repo-button_description: Button in the Provisioned Resource Preview Banner: This button allows users to navigate to the resource's external repository to view its latest changes. The `{{repoType}}` variable dynamically indicates the specific type of repository.
preview-banner.view-pull-request-in-repo_description: Button/Link in the preview banner of a provisioned resource: Navigates the user to view the associated pull request in the specified repository type. This provides a quick way to inspect changes related to the resource.

provisioned-resource-preview-banner.title-created-branch-in-repo: A new resource has been created in a branch in {{repoType}}.
provisioned-resource-preview-banner.title-created-branch-in-repo_description: Banner title in the Provisioned Resource Preview Banner: Informs the user that a new resource has been successfully created within a specific branch and repository type. The `{{repoType}}` placeholder will be replaced with the actual type of repository.
provisioned-resource-preview-banner.title-loaded-pull-request-in-repo: This resource is loaded from the branch you just created in {{repoType}} and it is only visible to you

provisioned-resource-request-handler_des: Success message in the resource management interface: Confirms that a resource has been successfully saved by the system after a user's save operation.
provisioned-resource-request-handler-das: Confirmation message in the Dashboard editor: Notifies the user that the current dashboard has been successfully saved. This message appears after saving changes to a dashboard, specifically one managed by the provisioned resource request handler.
provisioned-resource-request-handler-fol: Confirmation Message in the resource provisioning interface: Informs the user about the successful completion of a request to create a folder as a provisioned resource.

banner.message: This feature is currently under active development. For the best experience and latest improvements, we recommend using the <2>nightly build</2> of Grafana.

bitbucket.branch-description: The branch to use for provisioning
bitbucket.branch-description_description: Field in the Bitbucket configuration section for provisioning: Specifies the Git branch from which provisioning operations should retrieve configurations or data.
bitbucket.branch-label_description: A field label in the Bitbucket provisioning settings: It identifies an input field where the user specifies the name of a Git branch to use for provisioning.
bitbucket.branch-required_description: Validation message in the Bitbucket provisioning configuration: Notifies the user that a branch name is a mandatory field for the Bitbucket integration setup.
bitbucket.path-description: Optional subdirectory path within the repository
bitbucket.path-description_description: Description text in the Bitbucket provisioning settings: Explains an input field for an optional subdirectory path within a configured Bitbucket repository. This path specifies the location for dashboard provisioning files.
bitbucket.path-label_description: Label in Bitbucket Provisioning settings: Identifies an input field where the user specifies a path. This typically refers to a file, directory, or repository path within Bitbucket.

permissions.pull-requests-label_description: Label in the Bitbucket permissions configuration during provisioning: This label identifies the area or option for configuring access rights related to pull requests within Bitbucket.
permissions.pull-requests-read-write_description: **Permission option** in **Bitbucket provisioning permissions for pull requests**: This option grants both read and write access for managing pull requests.
permissions.repository-label_description: Label in the Bitbucket permissions provisioning screen: This identifies the section where users can view or manage Bitbucket repositories or their associated permissions.
permissions.repository-read-write-admin_description: Option in Bitbucket permissions settings: This option grants users the ability to read and write data. It is typically selected when configuring repository access during Bitbucket integration provisioning.
permissions.webhooks-label_description: Label in the Bitbucket permissions settings within the provisioning section: This identifies the area for managing Webhook configurations.
permissions.webhooks-read-write_description: Option label in Bitbucket provisioning permissions for webhooks: Indicates a permission level that grants both read and write access for webhooks. This is typically a selectable option for controlling webhook capabilities.

bitbucket.pr-workflow-description: Allows users to choose whether to open a pull request when saving changes. If the repository does not allow direct changes to the main branch, a pull request may still be required.
bitbucket.pr-workflow-label: Enable pull request option when saving
bitbucket.pr-workflow-label_description: Checkbox label in the Bitbucket integration settings within the provisioning section: This option allows users to enable or disable the functionality to create a pull request in Bitbucket when saving related configurations.
bitbucket.token-description: Bitbucket App Password with repository permissions
bitbucket.token-description_description: Helper text in the Bitbucket integration settings within the provisioning section: Informs the user that the Bitbucket App Password provided for integration must have repository permissions.
bitbucket.token-label_description: Label in the Bitbucket provisioning settings: Identifies the input field where the user must enter their Bitbucket App Password. This app password is required for BMC Helix Dashboards to authenticate and integrate with Bitbucket for provisioning purposes.
bitbucket.token-required_description: Validation Message in the Bitbucket configuration section within Provisioning settings: Notifies the user that a Bitbucket token must be provided to complete the Bitbucket integration or provisioning.
bitbucket.token-user-description: The username that will be used to access the repository with the app password
bitbucket.token-user-description_description: Descriptive text in the Bitbucket provisioning settings: Explains the purpose of the username required to access the Bitbucket repository when authenticating with an app password.
bitbucket.token-user-required_description: Validation message in the Bitbucket provisioning settings: Informs the user that a username is a mandatory field for configuring Bitbucket integration, particularly when setting up token-based authentication. It appears when the username field is left empty.
bitbucket.url-description_description: Description in the Bitbucket configuration section under Provisioning: Clarifies that the associated input field is for entering the URL of a Bitbucket repository.
bitbucket.url-label_description: Label on the Bitbucket provisioning configuration page: Prompts the user to enter the URL for the Bitbucket repository to be provisioned.
bitbucket.url-pattern: Must be a valid Bitbucket repository URL
bitbucket.url-pattern_description: **Validation message** in the **Bitbucket configuration settings**: This message appears when a user enters an invalid Bitbucket repository URL in the designated input field. It instructs them to provide a valid URL for successful integration.
bitbucket.url-required_description: Validation message in the Bitbucket provisioning configuration screen: Indicates that the 'Repository URL' field is mandatory and must be provided for Bitbucket integration.

bootstrap-step.dashboards-count_one_description: Count label in the Provisioning Bootstrap Step: Displays the number of dashboards. This string is used when the count is exactly one.
bootstrap-step.dashboards-count_other_description: Text label in a provisioning bootstrap step: Displays the total number of dashboards. This specific string (`_other`) is intended for the plural form or general case of the count of dashboards, typically when `count` is not 1.
bootstrap-step.description-clear-repository-connection: Add a clear name for this repository connection
bootstrap-step.description-clear-repository-connection_: Instruction in the Bootstrap step of the provisioning process: Prompts the user to enter a unique and easily identifiable name for the new repository connection being configured.
bootstrap-step.empty_description: Label or status indicator in a bootstrap step during provisioning: This signifies that a component, field, or selection is currently devoid of content. It can also be an option to start with a blank state.
bootstrap-step.error-field-required_description: Error message for a form field during a bootstrap step in the provisioning process: It informs the user that the field is mandatory and must be completed to proceed.
bootstrap-step.external-storage-label_description: Label in the 'Bootstrap step' of the 'Provisioning' process: This label identifies the configuration option or section for setting up or specifying external storage during the initial setup of BMC Helix Dashboards.
bootstrap-step.files-count_one_description: Text label in the provisioning bootstrap step interface: Displays the number of files processed or identified during an initial setup phase. This specific key is used for the singular count (e.g., "1 file").
bootstrap-step.files-count_other_description: Text label in the Provisioning Bootstrap Step: Displays the total number of files. This string is used for plural counts (i.e., when the file count is not one).
bootstrap-step.folders-count_one_description: Display text in the provisioning bootstrap step: Shows the singular count of folders. This string is used when indicating exactly one folder.
bootstrap-step.folders-count_other_description: Pluralized display text in the bootstrap step of the provisioning process: Indicates the total number of folders. This string is used for counts other than one.
bootstrap-step.label-display-name_description: Label in the Provisioning > Bootstrap Step screen: This prompts the user to enter a user-friendly name or alias for an entity being configured during the initial setup. This name is intended for display within the system rather than being a technical identifier.
bootstrap-step.placeholder-my-repository-connection_des: Placeholder text in the 'Bootstrap step' of the 'Provisioning' workflow: This placeholder guides the user to input or represents their specific repository connection details during the initial setup.
bootstrap-step.unmanaged-resources-label_description: Label in the provisioning bootstrap step: Denotes resources that are not yet under the system's management. These resources may need to be configured or brought under control during the initial provisioning process.

check-repository.check_description: Button in the 'Check Repository' section of Provisioning settings: Initiates the process to verify or check the status or configuration of a repository. This button performs an action to inspect the repository.

code-block.aria-label-copy_description: Button in a code block within the provisioning section: Copies the displayed code to the user's clipboard. This is an ARIA label providing an accessible description for the copy action.

config-form.alert-repository-settings-saved_descript: Success notification in the provisioning configuration form: Confirms to the user that their changes to the repository settings have been successfully stored.
config-form.alert-repository-settings-updated_descri: Alert message in the Provisioning configuration form: Confirms to the user that the repository settings have been successfully updated after a save action.
config-form.description-enabled: Once automatic pulling is enabled, the target cannot be changed.
config-form.description-enabled_description: Informational text in the Provisioning configuration form: Informs the user that enabling automatic pulling will make the configured target unchangeable. This serves as a warning before activation.
config-form.description-read-only: Resources can't be modified through Grafana.
config-form.description-read-only_description: Descriptive text in the provisioning configuration form: Informs the user that resources configured or displayed in this section are read-only and cannot be modified directly through the Grafana UI.
config-form.description-title: A human-readable name for the config
config-form.description-title_description: Label for a text input field in the configuration form within the provisioning section: It prompts the user to enter a clear, human-readable name for the configuration being created or edited.
config-form.error-fetch-branches_description: Error Message in the Provisioning Configuration Form: This message informs the user that the system encountered an error while attempting to retrieve or load branches, likely from a source control repository or a similar hierarchical data structure.
config-form.error-required_description: Error message in a configuration form within the Provisioning section: Informs the user that a mandatory input field must be filled out. This message is displayed when a required field is left empty.
config-form.error-save-repository_description: Error message on the Repository configuration form: Indicates that the system failed to save the user's updated repository settings.
config-form.label-automatic-pulling_description: Label in a configuration form in the provisioning section: This label identifies an option that controls the automatic retrieval or synchronization of data or configurations.
config-form.label-enabled_description: Label text in a provisioning configuration form: Indicates or allows the user to select whether a specific feature or setting is active.
config-form.label-interval-seconds_description: Label in a configuration form in the provisioning section: Specifies the duration, in seconds, for a recurring process or update.
config-form.label-repository-type_description: **Label** in a **configuration form within the provisioning section**: It identifies the input field or selection control where the user specifies the type of repository being configured.
config-form.label-target_description: Label in the provisioning configuration form: Identifies the target system or entity for the provisioning process or configuration. This could be the destination server, database, or other resource.
config-form.option-entire-instance_description: Option label in the Provisioning Configuration form: This option specifies that a configuration or action will apply to the entire BMC Helix Dashboards instance. It is used when defining the scope of a setting or operation.
config-form.option-managed-folder_description: Option label in the provisioning configuration form: This option relates to a system-managed folder, likely for storing configurations or resources created during provisioning.
config-form.placeholder-my-config_description: Placeholder text in a configuration form within the provisioning section: This text suggests to the user to enter a name or identifier for their personal configuration.

config-form-github-collapse.description-realtime-feedback: <0>Configure webhooks</0> to get instant updates in Grafana as soon as changes are committed. Review and approve changes using pull requests before they go live.
config-form-github-collapse.label-git-hub-features_description: Label in the GitHub configuration form: This label identifies a collapsible section that contains settings or options related to various GitHub features.
config-form-github-collapse.label-realtime-feedback_description: Label in the collapsible GitHub configuration form within the provisioning section: This label identifies a setting or feature related to displaying or receiving real-time feedback during configuration or operation.

subTitle.connect-external-storage-manage-resource: Connect to an external storage to manage your resources

Subtitle in the Connect Page of the Provisioning section: This text provides instructions or a brief explanation for users to connect their system to an external storage solution to manage their resources.

connect-repository-button.configure_description: Button in the Provisioning section: Allows users to configure settings for connecting a repository.
connect-repository-button.instance-fully-managed-tooltip: Configuration is disabled because this instance is fully managed
connect-repository-button.instance-fully-managed-tooltip_descripti: Tooltip on Connect Repository button in the Provisioning section: It explains that configuration functionality is disabled because the instance is fully managed, preventing user-initiated changes.
connect-repository-button.repository-limit-reached-tooltip_one_des: Tooltip on the 'Connect repository' button in the Provisioning section: It informs the user when the maximum allowed number of repositories has been reached. The `{{count}}` placeholder displays the specific repository limit.
connect-repository-button.repository-limit-reached-tooltip_other_d: Tooltip on the 'Connect Repository' button in the Provisioning section: This message indicates that the user has reached the maximum allowed number of repositories. The `{{count}}` placeholder displays the specific limit.

delete-repository-button.confirm-delete-keep-resources: Are you sure you want to delete the repository configuration but keep its resources?
delete-repository-button.confirm-delete-keep-resources_descriptio: Confirmation dialog message after clicking the 'Delete Repository' button in the provisioning section: It prompts the user to confirm the deletion of a repository's configuration while explicitly stating that its associated resources will be retained.
delete-repository-button.confirm-delete-with-resources: Are you sure you want to delete the repository configuration and all its resources?
delete-repository-button.confirm-delete-with-resources_descriptio: Confirmation prompt in the Provisioning section: It asks the user to confirm the deletion of a repository configuration. This message emphasizes that all associated resources will also be removed.
delete-repository-button.delete-and-keep-resources_description: Option in the delete repository action menu within the Provisioning section: This option allows the user to delete a repository but retain any associated resources (e.g., data sources, dashboards).
delete-repository-button.delete-and-remove-resources_description: Option in the 'Delete Repository' button's menu within the Provisioning section: This selection allows users to delete a repository and automatically remove all associated resources. It is the default action for repository deletion.
delete-repository-button.error-repository-delete_description: Error message in the Provisioning section: It informs the user that the attempt to delete a repository was unsuccessful. This message appears after clicking a "Delete repository" button when the operation fails.
delete-repository-button.success-repository-deleted_description: Success Message in the Provisioning section: Confirms to the user that the repository settings have been successfully queued for deletion. This indicates that the deletion process will occur asynchronously.
delete-repository-button.title-delete-repository-and-resources_de: Button label in the Provisioning section: This text indicates the action to completely remove a selected repository's configuration and all its associated data and resources. This is a permanent and destructive operation.
delete-repository-button.title-delete-repository-only_description: **Option label** in the **Provisioning section, within the repository deletion workflow**: This option allows the user to delete only the repository's configuration settings. It implies there are other deletion options that might include associated data.

edit-repository-page.back-to-repositories_description: Button on the 'Edit Repository' page: Allows the user to navigate back to the list of all repositories.
edit-repository-page.repository-config-exists-configuration: Make sure the repository config exists in the configuration file.
edit-repository-page.repository-config-exists-configuration_d: Instructional text on the Edit Repository page in the Provisioning section: Informs the user about a prerequisite for repository setup, stating that the corresponding configuration must exist in the system's external configuration file for proper functionality. This ensures the repository can be successfully saved or used.

subTitle.configure-repository-storing-resources: Configure a repository for storing your resources.
subTitle.configure-repository-storing-resources_d: Subtitle on the Edit Repository page within the Provisioning section: This subtitle instructs the user to set up or modify a repository that will be used to store various resources such as dashboards, data sources, or alerts.

text.configure-repository_description: Page title or section heading on the 'Edit Repository' page, located in the Provisioning section. It identifies the area where users can manage and configure a repository's settings.

enhanced-features.description: Get the most out of your GitHub integration with these optional add-ons
enhanced-features.description_description: Description in the Enhanced Features configuration section: It introduces and promotes optional add-ons designed to enhance the GitHub integration during the provisioning process.
enhanced-features.description-instant-updates: Get instant updates in Grafana as soon as changes are committed. Review and approve changes using pull requests before they go live.
enhanced-features.description-visual-previews-dashboard-up: See visual previews of dashboard updates directly in pull requests

Description in Enhanced Features section within Provisioning settings: Explains the functionality of viewing visual previews of dashboard updates directly in pull requests. This feature aids in efficient review and collaboration on dashboard changes.
enhanced-features.header_description: Header in the 'Enhanced Features' section within the Provisioning settings: Introduces options or configurations designed to improve the user's experience with GitHub integration.
enhanced-features.set-up-image-rendering_description: Call to action in the Enhanced Features section under Provisioning: Allows users to configure or enable the service responsible for rendering images. This setup is crucial for displaying dynamic or external images within dashboards.
enhanced-features.set-up-public-webhooks_description: Link or button in the Enhanced Features section of Provisioning settings: Allows users to configure and manage webhooks that are publicly accessible.
enhanced-features.title-instant-updates-requests-webhooks: Instant updates and pull requests with webhooks.
enhanced-features.title-instant-updates-requests-webhooks_: Feature Title in the Provisioning > Enhanced Features section: This title describes a capability that enables instant updates and handles pull requests using webhooks. This functionality offers advanced automation within the provisioning setup.
enhanced-features.title-visual-previews-in-pull-requests: Visual previews in pull requests with image rendering
enhanced-features.title-visual-previews-in-pull-requests_d: Feature Title in the Enhanced Features section under Provisioning: This title describes a capability that enables visual previews, including image rendering, directly within pull requests for enhanced review.

provisioning.error-title-default_description: Error Title in Provisioning section: Displayed as a generic title for an error message or dialog. This is the default title shown when an error occurs during a provisioning operation.

expanded-row.job-specification_description: Label in an expanded row within the provisioning section: This identifies the detailed requirements and configuration settings for a specific job or task being provisioned. It outlines the parameters and specifics of the job.
expanded-row.summary_description: Section title in an expanded row within the provisioning feature: It introduces a brief overview or recap of the item's details shown in that expanded row.

actions.set-up-required-feature-toggles_descript: A button or link in the 'Features List' section under 'Provisioning': This action is used to set up or configure required feature toggles. It likely leads to a separate screen where these feature states can be managed.

features-list.learn-more-documentation: Want to learn more? See our <2>documentation</2> .
features-list.learn-more-documentation_description: Call to action link in the Provisioning Features List: Prompts users to access product documentation for additional details about the listed features.
features-list.manage-dashboards-provision-updates-auto: Manage dashboards as code in Git and provision updates automatically

Feature description in the Provisioning section, within a list of features: This text describes an option to manage dashboards as code in Git, allowing for automatic provisioning of updates. It highlights a key automation capability for dashboard deployment.
features-list.manage-your-dashboards-with-remote-provi: Heading in the Provisioning > Features List: Introduces the Git Sync feature, which allows users to manage their dashboards through remote provisioning. It acts as a title for the section dedicated to this functionality.
features-list.store-dashboards-in-version-controlled-s: Store dashboards in version-controlled storage for better organization and history tracking

Feature description in the Provisioning > Features list: Describes an option to enable storing dashboards in a version-controlled system. This feature aims to improve dashboard organization and provide history tracking.

file-history-page.back-to-repositories_description: Link on the File History page within the Provisioning section: Allows the user to navigate back to the main list or overview of repositories from the current file history view.
file-history-page.history-not-supported: File history is not supported for this repository
file-history-page.history-not-supported_description: Informational message on the File History page within the Provisioning section: This message is displayed when a user attempts to view the file history for a repository that does not support this feature. It informs the user about this limitation for the current repository.
file-history-page.repository-config-exists-configuration: Make sure the repository config exists in the configuration file.
file-history-page.repository-config-exists-configuration_d: Instruction message on the File History page within the Provisioning section: Informs the user about a prerequisite, reminding them to ensure the repository configuration exists in the relevant configuration file for successful provisioning or file history operations.
file-history-page.repository-not-found_description: Error message in the File History Page in the Provisioning section: It indicates that a required repository could not be found. This message is displayed when the system attempts to retrieve file history but fails to locate the specified repository.

file-status-page.saving_description: Status message in the File Status Page within the Provisioning section: It indicates that a file or configuration is currently being saved or updated.
file-status-page.title-error-loading-file_description: Title for an error message on the file status page within the provisioning section. It informs the user that a file failed to load.

columns.history_description: Column header in the "Files View" table within the "Provisioning" section: Displays information related to the history or past changes of a provisioned file.
columns.view_description: Column header in the Files View table within the Provisioning section: It labels a column that provides options or links for viewing individual files.

files-view.placeholder-search_description: Placeholder text in a search input field in the Files view within the Provisioning section: Prompts the user to enter text to search for files.

finish-step.description-enable-previews: Adds an image preview of dashboard changes in pull requests. Images of your Grafana dashboards will be shared in your Git repository and visible to anyone with repository access.
finish-step.description-generate-dashboard-previews: Create preview links for pull requests
finish-step.description-generate-dashboard-previews_: Description in the Provisioning Finish Step: This text describes the functionality to generate preview links for dashboards, intended for integration with pull requests for review.
finish-step.description-image-rendering: Requires image rendering. <2>Set up image rendering</2>
finish-step.description-image-rendering_description: Informational text with an embedded link in the Provisioning Finish Step: Notifies the user that image rendering is required for certain dashboard functionalities (e.g., image exports or sharing) and provides a direct link to configure it.
finish-step.description-preview-requirements_descrip: Explanatory text in the finish step of the provisioning process, within the section for preview requirements: It specifies the system capabilities (image rendering and public access) needed for a successful preview. This text likely appears when a user is about to finish provisioning an item and wants to ensure the preview functionality works correctly.
finish-step.description-read-only: Resources can't be modified through Grafana.
