finish-step.description-read-only_description: Read-only description in the finish step of the provisioning process: Informs the user that the resources provisioned through this process are not editable directly within Grafana. It highlights a limitation regarding resource modification via the Grafana interface.
finish-step.description-sync-interval: How often to sync changes from the repository
finish-step.description-sync-interval_description: Description text in the "Finish" step of the provisioning process: Provides context for an input field where the user defines how frequently changes should be synchronized from an external repository.
finish-step.error-sync-interval-required_description: Error message in the finish step of the provisioning process: Informs the user that the 'Sync interval' setting is a mandatory field and must be provided.
finish-step.label-enable-previews: Enable dashboard previews in pull requests
finish-step.label-enable-previews_description: Checkbox label in the finish step of a provisioning workflow: This option allows users to enable a feature that displays previews of dashboards directly within pull requests. This helps developers or administrators review dashboard changes before they are merged.
finish-step.label-generate-dashboard-previews_descri: Label/Button text in the finish step of the provisioning process: This text prompts the user to generate and view previews of the dashboards they have just provisioned or configured.
finish-step.label-read-only_description: Label in the provisioning finish step: This label indicates that the displayed information or the resulting configuration is in a read-only state and cannot be modified.
finish-step.label-sync-interval_description: Label in the 'Finish Step' of the Provisioning wizard: This label describes a setting that determines the frequency (in seconds) for data synchronization. It indicates how often a provisioned item will synchronize its data or status.

folder-repository-list.all-resources-managed_one_description: Status message in the Folder Repository List within the Provisioning section: It informs the user that a single resource is currently being managed. This specific string is utilized when the `{{count}}` value is 1, adhering to pluralization rules.
folder-repository-list.all-resources-managed_other_description: Status message in Provisioning > Folder Repository List: Confirms that all resources within the folder repository list are currently managed. This message includes a count of all resources under management.
folder-repository-list.no-results-matching-your-query_descripti: Informational message in the folder/repository list within the provisioning section: Informs the user when a search or filter operation for folders or repositories yields no matching results.
folder-repository-list.partial-managed_description: Status text in the Folder Repository list within the Provisioning section: It indicates the proportion of resources within a folder repository that are actively managed by Git synchronization. This specific string is used when only some resources are managed.
folder-repository-list.placeholder-search_description: Placeholder text in a search input field on the folder repository list page: Guides users to type a query to search or filter the list of folders and repositories.
folder-repository-list.unmanaged-resources_one: {{count}} resource isn't managed by git sync.
folder-repository-list.unmanaged-resources_one_description: Informational text in the Provisioning > Folder Repository List: Indicates that a single resource is not managed by Git synchronization. This alerts users to unmanaged provisioning artifacts within their repositories.
folder-repository-list.unmanaged-resources_other: {{count}} resources aren't managed by git sync.
folder-repository-list.unmanaged-resources_other_description: Status message in the Provisioning > Folder-Repository List: It indicates the number of resources that are currently not being managed or tracked by Git synchronization.

title.repository_description: Title in the 'Get Default Values' screen within the Provisioning section: It labels a field or section related to specifying or displaying the source repository for default values.

getting-started.alert-temporary-outage: When you connect your whole instance, dashboards will be unavailable while running the migration. We recommend warning your users before starting the process.
getting-started.modal-description-public-access: Set up public access to your Grafana instance to enable GitHub integration
getting-started.modal-description-public-access_descript: Description in the Getting Started modal (Provisioning section): Informs the user that public access to their Grafana instance must be set up to enable GitHub integration. This text appears as a guiding instruction during an initial setup or onboarding process.
getting-started.modal-description-required-features: Enable required Grafana features for provisioning
getting-started.modal-description-required-features_desc: Modal Description in the "Getting Started" modal within the Provisioning section: It informs the user about enabling specific Grafana features that are required for the provisioning process.
getting-started.modal-title-set-up-public-access_descrip: Modal Title in the Getting Started section of Provisioning: Guides the user through configuring public access settings.
getting-started.modal-title-set-up-required-features_des: Modal Title in Provisioning > Getting Started section: Informs the user that essential features must be set up to proceed. This title appears on a modal that guides the user through the initial configuration of critical functionalities.
getting-started.step-description-copy-url: From the ngrok output, copy the https:// forwarding URL that looks like this:
getting-started.step-description-copy-url_description: Instructional text in the Getting Started section of the provisioning process: It guides the user to copy the https forwarding URL from the ngrok output. This is a step for configuring an integration or connection.
getting-started.step-description-enable-feature-toggles: Add these settings to your custom.ini file to enable necessary features:
getting-started.step-description-enable-feature-toggles_: Instructional text in a Getting Started section of the provisioning process: It guides users to add specific settings to their `custom.ini` file to enable necessary features. This is part of the initial setup instructions.
getting-started.step-description-start-ngrok: Run this command to create a secure tunnel to your local Grafana:
getting-started.step-description-start-ngrok_description: Instruction text in the Getting Started section of the Provisioning workflow: This text prompts the user to execute a command to establish a secure tunnel to their local Grafana instance via ngrok. It's a step within the initial setup process, likely for connecting the local environment to a remote service.
getting-started.step-description-update-grafana-config: Add this to your custom.ini file, replacing the URL with your actual ngrok URL:
getting-started.step-description-update-grafana-config_d: Step description in the "Getting Started" section of the provisioning process: Instructs the user to add a configuration snippet to the `custom.ini` file and replace a placeholder URL with their ngrok URL. This is part of configuring Grafana.
getting-started.step-title-copy-url_description: Step Title in the 'Getting Started' section of the Provisioning workflow: Instructs the user to copy their public URL. This is a step in the initial setup process, likely for accessing or sharing a newly provisioned dashboard or service.
getting-started.step-title-enable-feature-toggles_descri: Step title in the 'Getting Started' section of the provisioning process: It instructs users to activate essential feature toggles required for certain functionalities or setup steps to proceed successfully.
getting-started.step-title-start-ngrok: Start ngrok for temporary public access
getting-started.step-title-start-ngrok_description: Step Title in the Getting Started section of the Provisioning interface: Guides the user to initiate the ngrok service. This step enables temporary public access, likely for testing or demonstration purposes.
getting-started.step-title-update-grafana-config_descrip: Step title in the 'Getting Started' section of the 'Provisioning' feature: Instructs the user to update their Grafana configuration as a step in the initial setup or provisioning process.
getting-started.title-setting-connection-could-cause-tem: Setting up this connection could cause a temporary outage

Warning title in the Getting Started section of the Provisioning module: Informs the user about the potential for a temporary service outage when setting up a new connection. This prepares the user for possible disruption during the configuration process.

getting-started-page.header_description: Page Title in the Provisioning Getting Started page: It serves as the main heading for the section dedicated to guiding users through the provisioning process.
getting-started-page.subtitle-provisioning-feature: View and manage your provisioning connections
getting-started-page.subtitle-provisioning-feature_descriptio: Subtitle in the Provisioning Getting Started Page: Describes the functionality for viewing and managing provisioning connections within this section.

git.branch-description: The branch to use for provisioning
git.branch-description_description: Description in Git provisioning settings: This text specifies the Git branch from which provisioning configurations will be pulled.
git.branch-label_description: Input field label in the Git configuration settings for provisioning: Prompts the user to specify the Git branch to be used for content provisioning.
git.branch-required_description: Validation message in the Git provisioning configuration screen: Informs the user that a Git branch name is a mandatory input for setting up Git integration. This message appears when the corresponding input field is left empty.
git.path-description: Optional subdirectory path within the repository
git.path-description_description: Description text in Git provisioning settings: This text clarifies the purpose of an input field where users can specify an optional subdirectory path within a Git repository for provisioning files.
git.path-label_description: Label in Git provisioning settings: This label indicates the path to the Git repository or a specific subdirectory from which provisioning configurations are loaded.
git.pr-workflow-description: Allows users to choose whether to open a pull request when saving changes. If the repository does not allow direct changes to the main branch, a pull request may still be required.
git.pr-workflow-label: Enable pull request option when saving
git.pr-workflow-label_description: Checkbox label in the Git integration settings within the Provisioning UI: This option, when enabled, allows users to create a pull request when saving changes to the integrated Git repository.
git.token-description: Git repository access token or password
git.token-description_description: Description text in the Git provisioning settings: This text explains what credential (an access token or password) is needed for BMC Helix Dashboards to authenticate and access a Git repository during the provisioning process.
git.token-label_description: Label in the Git integration provisioning settings: Indicates where the user should enter an access token. This token allows BMC Helix Dashboards to authenticate and access a Git repository for provisioning.
git.token-required_description: Validation message in the Git provisioning settings: Informs the user that a Git token is a mandatory field required for Git integration or configuration.
git.token-user-description: The username that will be used to access the repository with the access token
git.token-user-description_description: Help text for an input field in Git Provisioning settings: This string describes the username that will be used in conjunction with an access token to authenticate and access a Git repository. It clarifies the specific purpose of the username when configuring token-based access.
git.token-user-required_description: Validation message in the Git provisioning configuration interface: Informs the user that the 'Username' field is mandatory and must be provided, likely when setting up token-based authentication for Git provisioning.
git.url-description_description: Description text on the Git provisioning configuration page: It clarifies the input field where users should enter the URL of their Git repository.
git.url-label_description: Label in Git provisioning settings: To identify the URL of the Git repository that is used for provisioning.
git.url-pattern: Must be a valid Git repository URL
git.url-pattern_description: Validation message in Git configuration settings within the Provisioning section: Ensures the user enters a correctly formatted Git repository URL required for provisioning.
git.url-required_description: Validation message in Git integration settings within the Provisioning section: Indicates that the 'Repository URL' field is a mandatory input for configuring Git integration.

github.branch-description: The branch to use for provisioning
github.branch-description_description: Description text in the GitHub configuration section of the Provisioning settings: Describes an input field where users define the specific Git branch from which provisioning configurations should be pulled.
github.branch-label_description: Label in GitHub provisioning settings: Identifies the input field where the user specifies the GitHub repository branch to be used for provisioning.
github.branch-required_description: Validation message in GitHub provisioning settings: Informs the user that a branch name is a mandatory input field.
github.path-description: Optional subdirectory path within the repository
github.path-description_description: Description text for an input field in GitHub Provisioning settings: Explains the option to specify an optional subdirectory path within the repository, likely to locate configuration files.
github.path-label_description: Label in GitHub provisioning settings: Refers to a file or directory path within a GitHub repository, typically for locating configuration files or other provisioning assets.
github.pr-workflow-description: Allows users to choose whether to open a pull request when saving changes. If the repository does not allow direct changes to the main branch, a pull request may still be required.
github.pr-workflow-label: Enable pull request option when saving
github.pr-workflow-label_description: Label for an option in the GitHub provisioning settings: This text describes a control that enables or disables the automatic creation of a pull request when configuration changes are saved.
github.token-description: GitHub Personal Access Token with repository permissions
github.token-description_description: Informational text in the GitHub provisioning configuration section: It describes the specific type of GitHub Personal Access Token required, emphasizing the necessity of repository permissions for successful integration.
github.token-label_description: Label in GitHub provisioning settings: Identifies the input field for a GitHub Personal Access Token. This token is required for authenticating with GitHub to enable provisioning.
github.token-required_description: Validation message in the GitHub Provisioning settings: Informs the user that a GitHub token must be provided to successfully configure the GitHub integration.
github.url-description_description: Label in the GitHub configuration section within the provisioning settings: Describes the input field for specifying the URL of a GitHub repository. This URL is used to retrieve provisioning configurations.
github.url-label_description: Label in GitHub provisioning settings: Identifies the input field where users enter the URL of a GitHub repository.
github.url-pattern: Must be a valid GitHub repository URL
github.url-pattern_description: Validation message in GitHub provisioning configuration: Informs the user that the input for a GitHub repository URL field must be a valid URL. This message appears when the entered value does not meet the required format or validity.
github.url-required_description: Validation message in the GitHub provisioning configuration settings: It informs the user that the 'Repository URL' field is mandatory and must be provided.

gitlab.branch-description: The branch to use for provisioning
gitlab.branch-description_description: Label for a field in GitLab provisioning settings: It clarifies which Git branch will be utilized for the provisioning process.
gitlab.branch-label_description: Label in GitLab provisioning settings: Identifies an input field where the user specifies the name of the Git branch to be used for provisioning.
gitlab.branch-required_description: Validation message in Gitlab provisioning settings: Informs the user that a branch name is a required input for the Gitlab integration. This message appears when the 'Branch' field is left empty during configuration.
gitlab.path-description: Optional subdirectory path within the repository
gitlab.path-description_description: Helper text in GitLab Provisioning Configuration: Explains an optional input field for specifying a subdirectory path within a GitLab repository. This path directs the system to provisioning configuration files located in that specific folder.
gitlab.path-label_description: Label 'Path' in the GitLab provisioning configuration: Prompts the user to enter a specific file system path or repository location. This path is essential for configuring the GitLab integration.

permissions.api-read-write_description: Permission level option in GitLab API permission settings during provisioning: This option grants both read and write access to the GitLab API.
permissions.repository-label_description: Label in GitLab Permissions settings within the Provisioning section: This identifies the specific Git repository for which access permissions are being defined or viewed.
permissions.repository-read-write_description: Permission level option in the GitLab permissions section of provisioning settings: This option grants both read and write access to a repository.
permissions.user-label_description: Label in GitLab permissions configuration: Identifies a field or entry for an individual user. This is used when assigning specific permissions within the GitLab provisioning setup.
permissions.user-read_description: Permission label in GitLab provisioning permissions settings: Indicates that the selected user or role will have read-only access to GitLab resources.

gitlab.pr-workflow-description: Allows users to choose whether to open a merge request when saving changes. If the repository does not allow direct changes to the main branch, a merge request may still be required.
gitlab.pr-workflow-label: Enable merge request option when saving
gitlab.pr-workflow-label_description: Option in the GitLab Provisioning settings: This controls whether a merge request is automatically generated as part of the workflow when saving changes related to the GitLab integration.
gitlab.token-description: GitLab Project Access Token with repository permissions
gitlab.token-description_description: Label/Description text in the GitLab provisioning settings: This text describes the specific type of GitLab Project Access Token that is required, emphasizing it must have repository permissions for integration.
gitlab.token-label_description: Label in the GitLab provisioning settings: This label identifies the input field for entering a Project Access Token. This token grants BMC Helix Dashboards access to specific GitLab projects for provisioning purposes.
gitlab.token-required_description: Validation message in the GitLab provisioning settings: Informs the user that a GitLab token is mandatory for setting up the integration.
gitlab.url-description_description: Description text in the GitLab configuration section of the provisioning settings: This text clarifies that the corresponding input field requires the URL of a GitLab repository.
gitlab.url-label_description: Label in the GitLab provisioning settings: This field label prompts the user to enter the URL of the GitLab repository that will be used for provisioning configurations or content.
gitlab.url-pattern: Must be a valid GitLab repository URL
gitlab.url-pattern_description: Validation message in GitLab provisioning settings: This message informs the user that the entered value for a GitLab repository URL is invalid and must conform to a valid URL pattern.
gitlab.url-required_description: Validation message in the GitLab provisioning settings form: This message appears when the user attempts to save or proceed without providing a value for the 'Repository URL' field, indicating it is a mandatory input.

history-view.not-found_description: Status message in the provisioning history view: It indicates that a specific history record or item being searched for or displayed could not be found.

home-page.button-delete-repositories_description: Button in the Provisioning Home Page: This button allows users to delete repositories that have been provisioned or are available for provisioning.
home-page.configured-repositories-while-running-le: Configured repositories will not work while running legacy storage.

Warning message in the Provisioning home page: Informs the user that configured repositories will not function due to the system running legacy storage. This message highlights an important incompatibility.
home-page.confirm-delete-repositories: Are you sure you want to delete all configured repositories? This action cannot be undone.
home-page.confirm-delete-repositories_description: Confirmation Dialog in Provisioning Home Page: This message prompts the user to confirm the deletion of all configured repositories. It explicitly warns that the action is irreversible.
home-page.error-delete-all-repositories_descriptio: Error message in the Provisioning home page: Informs the user that an attempt to delete all repositories was unsuccessful. This message typically appears after a user action has failed.
home-page.remove-all-configured-repositories_descr: Button on the Provisioning Home Page: Allows the user to remove all currently configured repositories from the system.
home-page.subtitle: View and manage your configured repositories
home-page.subtitle_description: Subtitle in the Provisioning home page: Informs users about the ability to view and manage configured repositories on this page.
home-page.success-all-repositories-deleted_descrip: Success message in the Provisioning home page: Informs the user that all configured data repositories have been successfully deleted after a user action.
home-page.tab-getting-started_description: Tab in the provisioning home page: Offers introductory information or guidance for users to begin using the provisioning features.
home-page.tab-getting-started-title_description: Tab title in the Provisioning home page: Labels a tab that provides introductory information or guidance for users to get started with the provisioning features.
home-page.tab-repositories_description: Tab in the Provisioning Home Page: This tab provides access to manage various repositories. These repositories likely store configurations, templates, or other assets used for provisioning.
home-page.tab-repositories-title_description: Tab Title in the Provisioning Home Page: Labels the section or tab dedicated to showing a list of repositories.
home-page.title-delete-all-configured-repositories: Button label or confirmation dialog title in the Provisioning home page: Allows the user to delete all currently configured repositories. This is a global action that will remove all repository settings.
home-page.title-legacy-storage-detected_descriptio: Status message on the Provisioning Home Page: Informs the user that an older, legacy storage solution has been detected for their environment. This often serves as a heads-up or a prompt to consider migrating to a more modern storage type.

http-utils.access-denied: Access denied. Please check your token permissions.
http-utils.authentication-failed: Authentication failed. Please check your access token.
http-utils.http-error_description: Error message in Provisioning HTTP Utilities: Displays a generic HTTP error, including the status code and descriptive text. It informs the user about issues encountered during HTTP requests within the provisioning process.
http-utils.request-failed_description: Error message in the HTTP Utility section of the Provisioning feature: Informs the user that an HTTP request, essential for a provisioning operation, has failed. This typically indicates a network issue or an unsuccessful server response during the provisioning process.
http-utils.resource-not-found: Resource not found. Please check the URL or repository.
http-utils.unsupported-repository-type_description: Error Message in the `provisioning > http-utils` section: This message informs the user that the specified repository type is not recognized or supported by the system when attempting to configure or provision a resource using HTTP utilities.

provisioning.inline-secure-values-warning: You need to save your access tokens again due to a system update
provisioning.inline-secure-values-warning_description: Warning message in Provisioning settings: Notifies the user that secure access tokens need to be re-saved following a system update. This action is crucial for maintaining the validity and security of these credentials.

job-status.label-view-details_description: Button in the Job Status page within the Provisioning section: Allows users to view detailed information about a specific job. This button typically navigates to a dedicated page or expands a section to display comprehensive job-related data.
job-status.no-job-found_description: Status message in the Provisioning Job Status view: Informs the user when no jobs are found or match the current criteria in the system.
job-status.no-job-found-message: The job may have been deleted or could not be retrieved. Cancel the current process and start again.

status.title-error-running-job_description: Message in the job status display in the provisioning section: Informs the user that an error occurred during job execution. This likely appears as a title or heading for a detailed error message.
status.title-success-running-job_description: Status message in the provisioning job status view: Informs the user that a job initiated during the provisioning process has successfully completed. This message indicates the successful conclusion of a background task.
status.title-warning-running-job_description: Status message in the Job Status screen within the Provisioning module: Informs the user that a background job has successfully completed its execution, but with warnings.

job-status.summary_description: Section Title in the Provisioning > Job Status screen: Indicates the section providing a high-level overview of all provisioning job statuses.

title.error-fetching-active-job_description: Error Message Title in the Job Status section of the Provisioning feature: It indicates that the system failed to retrieve or display information about a currently active provisioning job.

local.path-description: Local file system path to the repository
local.path-description_description: A label for an input field in the local provisioning settings: This text guides the user to enter the specific file system path where a repository is stored. It defines the location on the local machine for repository access.
local.path-label_description: Label in local provisioning settings: Identifies the input field where a user specifies the local file system path for the repository, used for storing dashboard configurations or other relevant files.
local.path-required_description: Validation message in Local Provisioning settings: Informs the user that the path to the local repository is a mandatory field. It is displayed when the field is left empty.

folder.description: After setup, a new Grafana folder will be created and synced with external storage. If any resources are present in external storage, they will be provisioned to this new folder. All new resources created in this folder will be stored and versioned in external storage.
folder.label: Sync external storage to a new Grafana folder
folder.label_description: Label in the Provisioning settings, within the "Mode Options" for folder management: This option allows users to synchronize externally provisioned content (e.g., dashboards, data sources) into a newly created Grafana folder.
folder.subtitle: Use this option to sync external resources into a new folder without affecting the rest of your instance. You can repeat this process for up to 10 folders.

instance.description: Resources will be synced with external storage and provisioned into this instance. Existing Grafana resources will be migrated and merged if needed. After setup, all new resources and changes will be saved to external storage and automatically provisioned back into the instance.
instance.label: Sync all resources with external storage
instance.label_description: **Option label** in the **Instance Provisioning Mode Options** section: This label describes a setting to synchronize all resources with an external storage system. It defines the storage and data management strategy for the provisioned instance.
instance.subtitle: Use this option if you want to sync and manage your entire Grafana instance through external storage.

title.repository-connection-failed_description: Title in the Provisioning Wizard, displayed after a submission attempt: This title indicates that an attempt to connect to a repository has failed.
title.repository-request-failed_description: Title in the Provisioning Wizard's 'On Submit' phase: This title is displayed to inform the user that a request to a repository, likely for data or configuration, has failed after they attempted to submit information in the wizard.

provisioning.read-only-local-tooltip: This resource is read-only and provisioned through file provisioning. To make any changes, update the connected repository. To modify the settings go to Administration > Provisioning > Repositories.
provisioning.read-only-remote-tooltip: This resource is read-only and provisioned through Git. To make any changes, update the connected repository. To modify the settings go to Administration > Provisioning > Repositories.

recent-jobs.active-jobs_description: Section heading in the 'Recent Jobs' section within the 'Provisioning' area: This title indicates the display of jobs that are currently active or in progress.
recent-jobs.column-action_description: Column header in the Recent Jobs table within the Provisioning section: Labels the column that provides actions relevant to each listed recent job.
recent-jobs.column-duration_description: Column header in the 'Recent Jobs' table within the Provisioning section: Displays the time taken for a job to complete.
recent-jobs.column-job-id_description: Table Column Header in the Recent Jobs table within the Provisioning section: Displays the unique identifier for each job. This allows users to quickly locate and reference specific job executions.
recent-jobs.column-message_description: Column header in the 'Recent Jobs' table within the 'Provisioning' section: It displays a descriptive message or status update for each job listed.
recent-jobs.column-started_description: Column header in the 'Recent Jobs' section of the 'Provisioning' area: Labels a column displaying the start time or initiation of a job.
recent-jobs.column-status_description: Column header in the Recent Jobs table within the Provisioning section: Displays the current status of each job, such as success, failure, or in-progress.
recent-jobs.error-loading_description: Error message in the Recent Jobs section of the Provisioning area: Informs the user that there was an issue loading a specific type of data or resource. The `{{type}}` placeholder indicates what failed to load.
recent-jobs.jobs_description: Label in Recent Jobs section within Provisioning: Refers to a list of recently performed tasks or operations.

repository-actions.settings_description: Link in Repository Actions within the Provisioning module: Used to access and configure settings related to repository management actions. This could include parameters for how repositories are provisioned or maintained.
repository-actions.source-code_description: Link in the 'Repository Actions' section of the Provisioning interface: Allows users to view or download the underlying source code for a selected repository artifact or configuration.

get-repository-meta.webhook_description: Label in the repository metadata section during provisioning: Refers to the webhook configuration or information associated with the repository, enabling automated integrations.

repository-card.read-only-badge_description: Badge on a repository card in the Provisioning section: This badge indicates that the associated repository is read-only and cannot be edited.
repository-card.settings_description: Button on a repository card in the provisioning section: To access and modify the configuration options for the repository displayed on that card.
repository-card.view_description: Button on a repository card in the Provisioning section: Allows the user to view the details or contents of that specific repository.

delete-or-move-job.compare-branch_description: Action button in the Delete or Move Job screen for Repository Links: Allows users to compare branches within a repository. This helps ensure the correct version or state of a job is being managed before a delete or move operation.
delete-or-move-job.open-pull-request_description: Button in the `Delete or Move Job` section within `Repository Link` provisioning: Opens a pull request. This action is used to formally submit changes (like deleting or moving a job) to the linked repository for review and integration.
delete-or-move-job.view-branch_description: Button in the 'Delete or Move Job' section, which is part of Repository Link management under Provisioning: This button allows the user to view the specific branch associated with the job being deleted or moved.
delete-or-move-job.view-repository_description: Button in a dialog for deleting or moving a job linked to a repository within the Provisioning section: Allows the user to view the details or contents of the associated repository.

repository-link.grafana-repository-synced: Your resources are now in your external storage and provisioned into your instance. From now on, your instance and the external storage will be synchronized.

sync-job.view-repository_description: Button in the 'Sync Job' management interface for a repository link within the Provisioning section: Allows users to view the details of the repository associated with the synchronization job.

repository-overview.checked_description: Label in the Repository Overview page within the Provisioning section: This label indicates the verification status or when a repository (or its contents) was last checked for integrity or compliance.
repository-overview.finished_description: Label in the Repository Overview page within the Provisioning section: It displays the timestamp of the last successful data pull or synchronization operation from a repository. This helps users quickly assess the freshness of the repository's data.
repository-overview.health_description: Label in the Repository Overview section within the Provisioning module: Displays the operational health or current status of a repository.
repository-overview.healthy_description: Status label in the Repository Overview section of the Provisioning dashboard: Indicates that a repository or its components are in a healthy, operational state.
repository-overview.job-id_description: Label in the 'Repository Overview' section under 'Provisioning': Identifies the unique identifier for a job or task related to repository operations.
repository-overview.last-ref_description: Label in the Repository Overview section of the provisioning feature: It displays the identifier (e.g., commit hash, branch name, tag) of the last synchronized or deployed reference from the repository.
repository-overview.messages_description: Label in the Repository Overview section of Provisioning: It identifies a display area for system messages, status updates, or other notifications related to the repositories.
repository-overview.not-available_description: Placeholder text in Repository Overview (within the Provisioning section): Used to indicate that a specific piece of information or a value for a repository is not available or not applicable.
repository-overview.pull-status_description: Label in the Repository Overview section within the Provisioning module: Indicates the current status of a data or configuration 'pull' operation from a repository, signifying its retrieval and synchronization.
repository-overview.resources_description: Label in the Repository Overview page within the Provisioning section: Identifies a collection of managed items such as dashboards, data sources, or alerts. It represents the entities available or provisioned within the repository.
repository-overview.status_description: Label in the Repository Overview section of the Provisioning area: Labels the current status of a repository.
repository-overview.unhealthy_description: Status label in the Repository Overview within Provisioning: Indicates that a repository is in an unhealthy or problematic state.
repository-overview.view-folder_description: Button in the Repository Overview screen within the Provisioning section: Allows users to navigate into and view the contents of a selected folder.
repository-overview.webhook_description: Label in the Repository Overview section of Provisioning settings: Identifies the dedicated area for managing webhook configurations. This enables automated notifications or actions related to repository events.
repository-overview.webhook-events_description: Label in the Webhook Events section of the Repository Overview: It introduces a display or list of events related to the configured webhook.
repository-overview.webhook-id_description: Label in the repository overview screen within the Provisioning section: It identifies a webhook's unique ID.
repository-overview.webhook-last-event_description: Label in the Webhook details section of the Repository Overview page: It indicates the timestamp or a brief description of the most recent activity or trigger for a specific webhook.
repository-overview.webhook-url_description: Button in the Repository Overview section: Allows users to view or navigate to the details of a configured webhook.

columns.history_description: Column header in the Repository Resources table under Provisioning: Displays the history or log of changes or activities related to a specific repository resource.
columns.view-dashboard_description: Action button or link in a column within the 'Repository Resources' table: Allows users to open and display a specific dashboard from the repository. This control is located in the provisioning management section.
columns.view-folder_description: Action link in a column of the repository resources table: Allows the user to open and view the contents of a specific repository folder.

repository-resources.placeholder-search_description: Placeholder text in the search input field on the Repository Resources screen within Provisioning: Prompts users to enter text to find repository resources.

repository-status-page.back-to-repositories_description: Button on the Repository Status Page within the Provisioning section: Navigates the user back to the main list or overview of repositories. It allows users to return from a detailed status view to the repository selection page.
repository-status-page.cleaning-up-resources_description: Status message in the Repository Status page within the Provisioning section: Informs the user that the system is currently performing cleanup operations on repository resources. This message indicates an ongoing, temporary process.
repository-status-page.legacy-storage-message: Instance is not yet running unified storage -- requires migration wizard
repository-status-page.legacy-storage-message_description: Informational message on the Repository Status page (Provisioning section): This message indicates that the current instance is not yet running unified storage and a migration wizard is required to proceed. It guides the user on the necessary action for storage modernization.
repository-status-page.not-found_description: Status message on the Repository Status page: Indicates that a specified repository or a related item could not be located or retrieved.
repository-status-page.not-found-message_description: Error message in the Repository Status page within Provisioning: Informs the user that the requested or specified repository could not be located. This message is displayed when a user attempts to access or view details of a non-existent repository.
repository-status-page.repository-config-exists-configuration: Make sure the repository config exists in the configuration file.
repository-status-page.repository-config-exists-configuration_d: **Message** in **Repository Status Page (Provisioning section)**: Instructs the user to confirm the presence of the repository configuration within the system's configuration file. This is a critical check for ensuring repository functionality or for troubleshooting issues.
repository-status-page.tab-files_description: Tab in the Repository Status Page within the Provisioning section: This tab displays a list or details of files associated with the repository.
repository-status-page.tab-files-title: The raw file list from the repository
repository-status-page.tab-files-title_description: Tab title in the "Repository Status" page within the "Provisioning" section: This title identifies a tab that displays a list of raw (unprocessed) files retrieved directly from the repository.
repository-status-page.tab-overview_description: Tab label in the Repository Status page within the Provisioning section: Displays general information or a summary of the repository's current status.
repository-status-page.tab-overview-title_description: Tab Title in the Repository Status page within the Provisioning section: This title labels a tab that provides an overview or summary of the repository's status.
repository-status-page.tab-resources_description: Tab on the Repository Status page in the Provisioning section: Displays information about the resources associated with or provisioned for the repository.
repository-status-page.tab-resources-title_description: Tab title in the Provisioning Repository Status page: This labels a tab listing resources (e.g., dashboards, data sources) that have been saved in the Grafana database through the provisioning process.
repository-status-page.title: Repository Status
repository-status-page.title_description: Page title in the Repository Status page: It serves as the main heading for the page that displays the current health and status information of a repository within the provisioning area.
repository-status-page.title-legacy-storage_description: Title in the repository status page within the provisioning section: This title identifies a section or status related to older, possibly deprecated, storage systems.
repository-status-page.title-queued-for-deletion_description: Status label in the Repository Status page within the Provisioning section: This label indicates that a repository or other provisioned item is marked for deletion and is awaiting the process to execute.

repository-type-cards.choose-provider_description: **Label** in **the 'Repository Type Cards' section during provisioning**: It prompts the user to select a specific data source or service provider from available options.
repository-type-cards.configure-file_description: **Action label** on a **card representing a file-based repository** in the **Provisioning section**: It initiates the configuration workflow for file-based provisioning.
repository-type-cards.configure-with-provider_description: Button on a repository type card in the provisioning section: This button allows users to initiate the configuration process for a specific repository integration. The `{{ provider }}` placeholder is dynamically replaced with the name of the integration provider.
repository-type-cards.provider-not-listed: If your provider is not listed:
repository-type-cards.provider-not-listed_description: Instructional text in the repository type selection cards during the provisioning process: It introduces subsequent instructions for users whose specific data provider is not explicitly listed among the available choices.

repository-types.bitbucket_description: Label for a repository type in the Provisioning > Repository Types section: Represents Bitbucket as a selectable option for integrating source code repositories.
repository-types.bitbucket-description_description: Descriptive text in the Provisioning > Repository Types section: Informs the user that they can connect to Bitbucket repositories. This text likely accompanies an option or field for configuring Bitbucket integration.
repository-types.github_description: Label in the Provisioning Repository Types section: Identifies GitHub as an available repository type for provisioning.
repository-types.github-description_description: Description in the 'Provisioning > Repository Types' section: It clarifies the purpose of the GitHub repository type option, which is to establish a connection with GitHub repositories.
repository-types.gitlab_description: Label in the repository types section within provisioning settings: Designates GitLab as a repository type option. This allows users to select GitLab for provisioning dashboards or other resources.
repository-types.gitlab-description_description: Descriptive text in the Provisioning > Repository Types section: It informs the user about the ability to connect to GitLab repositories as a supported type for integration or data source configuration.
repository-types.local_description: Selection option in Repository Type configuration: Used to designate a repository as being locally hosted or managed within the system.
repository-types.local-description_description: Description in the Provisioning > Repository Types section, specifically for the 'Local' repository type: This text explains that this repository type is used to configure dashboard and datasource provisioning via local files.
repository-types.pure-git_description: Repository type option in Provisioning > Repository Types: This option allows users to select a direct, generic Git repository as a source for provisioning configurations.
repository-types.pure-git-description_description: Description text in the repository type selection for provisioning: Describes the "Pure Git" option, highlighting its capability to connect to any generic Git repository.

resource-view.base_description: Label or option in the resource view within the provisioning section: This designates the fundamental, primary, or default configuration or state related to a resource.
resource-view.dashboard-preview_description: Title in the Resource View section of the Provisioning module: It identifies a feature or area dedicated to displaying a preliminary version of a dashboard for review.
resource-view.existing-dashboard_description: Label or option in the resource view of the provisioning section: Indicates or allows selection of a dashboard that has already been created. It differentiates from creating a new dashboard during the provisioning process.
resource-view.history_description: Button or tab label in the Resource View section of the Provisioning module: Allows users to view the chronological history of the selected resource, such as past configurations, events, or status changes.

message.removed-from-repo-test-ui_description: Status message in the Resource View within the Provisioning section: Informs the user that an item has been removed from the repository test user interface. This message confirms a successful deletion action.
message.updated-from-repo-test-ui_description: Status message in the resource view within the provisioning section: Informs the user that a resource or its configuration has been updated. It specifies that the update originated from a 'repository test UI'.

resource-view.read-only-repo-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the Resource View within the Provisioning feature: Informs the user that the displayed repository is read-only.
resource-view.repository_description: Label in the 'Resource View' section of the 'Provisioning' area: Identifies a storage location or source, such as a Git repository, where resources or configurations are managed for provisioning.

label.dry-run-result-after-apply_description: Label in the 'Information' tab of a resource provisioning view: It displays the anticipated outcome of a dry run, showing the effect of applying configuration changes without actually committing them.
label.existing-from-grafana_description: Label in the `tab-info` section of the resource view within the provisioning interface: This label identifies resources that are already present and have been imported or originated from Grafana.
label.file-from-repository_description: Label in the Resource View's information tab within Provisioning: This label identifies an option or input field for specifying a file that is sourced from a repository, distinguishing it from other file input methods.

resource-view.title-error-saving-file_description: Title in the Resource View within the Provisioning section: This title is displayed when an error occurs while attempting to save a file, such as a configuration or definition related to a resource.

setup-modal.done_description: Button in the provisioning setup modal: Indicates completion of a setup process and closes the modal.

progress-bar.aria-label_description: Progress Bar in Provisioning: This accessibility label identifies a UI element that visually tracks the progress of an operation during the provisioning of resources or services.

sidebar-item.label-completed-step_description: Label in the provisioning sidebar: Indicates that a particular step in a multi-step provisioning process has been successfully completed.
sidebar-item.label-current-step_description: Label in a sidebar item within the provisioning feature: Indicates the user's current position or progress within a multi-step provisioning process.
sidebar-item.label-pending-step_description: Label in the Provisioning sidebar: It identifies a step within a provisioning process that is currently awaiting completion.

status-badge.automatic-pulling-disabled_description: Status indicator in the provisioning status badge: Informs the user that automatic data pulling for provisioning is currently disabled. This status likely requires user action or configuration changes.
status-badge.deleting_description: Status label on a status badge within the provisioning interface: This text indicates that an item or resource is currently being deleted.
status-badge.error_description: Text on a status badge in the Provisioning section: Indicates an error status for a provisioned resource or process.
status-badge.pending_description: Status Label in Provisioning Status Badge: Represents a state where a provisioning operation is awaiting completion or further action.
status-badge.pulling_description: Status label in a status badge within the provisioning section: It indicates that a resource or configuration is actively being retrieved or downloaded, signifying an ongoing, in-progress operation.
status-badge.unknown_description: Status indicator text in the provisioning status badge: Displays when the status of a provisioning operation or item cannot be determined.
status-badge.up-to-date_description: Status indicator in a provisioning status badge: Signifies that the associated component, configuration, or agent is current and fully synchronized with its intended state.
status-badge.waiting-for-health-check: Waiting for health check to run
status-badge.waiting-for-health-check_description: Status message in the provisioning status badge: This indicates that a newly provisioned resource or service is in a temporary state, waiting for its initial health check to complete. It informs the user about the current status during the provisioning process.
status-badge.warning_description: Status Badge in Provisioning section: Indicates a warning state for a provisioning item or process.

provisioning.success-title-default_description: Title in the provisioning workflow: Indicates a successful completion of a provisioning operation. This serves as the default heading for general success messages.

sync-job.error-no-job-id_description: Error message in the Provisioning > Sync Job feature: This message indicates that an attempt to initiate a synchronization job was unsuccessful.
sync-job.error-no-repository-name_description: Error message in the Provisioning Sync Job feature: This message is displayed when a user attempts to create, configure, or run a sync job without specifying a required repository name. It indicates a mandatory field is missing for the sync job to proceed.
sync-job.error-starting-job_description: Error message in the Sync Job provisioning section: This message is displayed when the system encounters an error while attempting to initiate a synchronization job.

sync-repository.body-edit-configuration_description: Heading in the Provisioning > Sync Repository configuration section: Describes the interface where users can modify the settings for the synchronized repository.
sync-repository.error-pulling-resources_description: Error message in the Sync Repository feature within the Provisioning section: Informs the user that an attempt to retrieve or synchronize resources from a configured repository has failed. These resources typically refer to configuration items like dashboards, data sources, or alert rules.
sync-repository.pull_description: Action in the Sync Repository section under Provisioning: Triggers the retrieval and integration of updates from an external repository to synchronize local content.
sync-repository.success-pull-started_description: Status message in the Provisioning > Sync Repository section: Informs the user that a repository synchronization (pull) operation has successfully started. This operation likely involves fetching and merging content from the repository.
sync-repository.title-pull-not-enabled_description: Status message in the Provisioning > Sync Repository section: This message indicates that the 'pull' functionality, which fetches changes from the external repository, is currently disabled.
sync-repository.tooltip-unhealthy-repository: Unable to pull an unhealthy repository
sync-repository.tooltip-unhealthy-repository_description: Tooltip in the Sync Repository section within Provisioning: Informs the user that a repository cannot be pulled due to its unhealthy status.

synchronize-step.repository-error_description: Error message in the Synchronization step within the provisioning process: Informs the user that a problem occurred with the data repository.
synchronize-step.repository-error-message: Unable to check repository status. Please verify the repository configuration and try again.
synchronize-step.repository-unhealthy: The repository cannot be synchronized. Cancel provisioning and try again once the issue has been resolved. See details below.
synchronize-step.synchronization-description: Include commits for each historical value
synchronize-step.synchronization-description_description: Description text in Provisioning > Synchronization Step: Explains an option to include version control commits associated with each historical data value during the synchronization process. This enables detailed tracking of changes over time.
synchronize-step.synchronization-options_description: Section title in the synchronization step of the provisioning process: Introduces a set of configurable settings for a synchronization operation.

token-permissions-info.and-click_description: Instruction text fragment in the token permissions information section under provisioning settings: This phrase completes an instruction, guiding the user to perform a click action, typically on a button or link.

bitbucket.create-token-button_description: Button in the Bitbucket section of the token permissions information: Allows users to create new application passwords for integrating with Bitbucket.
bitbucket.token-text_description: Label in the Bitbucket token permissions configuration section: This text prompts the user to enter their Bitbucket Personal Access Token. This token is used to authenticate BMC Helix Dashboards with Bitbucket for provisioning purposes.

gitlab.create-token-button_description: Button in the GitLab token permissions information section within Provisioning: Allows users to initiate the process of creating a new authentication token for GitLab integration.
gitlab.token-text_description: Label in the GitLab configuration section of the provisioning settings: It identifies the input field or displayed text for a GitLab Personal Access Token, which is used for integration and authentication.

token-permissions-info.go-to_description: **Link or button** in **the 'Token Permissions Info' section of the 'Provisioning' feature**: **Used to navigate to a specific page or section for viewing or managing details related to token permissions.**
token-permissions-info.make-sure: Make sure to include these permissions
token-permissions-info.make-sure_description: Instructional text in the Token Permissions Information section during provisioning: This prompts the user to verify or select a specific list of necessary permissions to be included for the token.

provisioning.warning-title-default_description: Title in provisioning dialogs or sections: This is the default title for a warning message displayed to the user during the provisioning process, alerting them to potential issues or important information.

wizard.alert-point-1: Resources won't be able to be created, edited, or deleted during this process. In the last step, they will disappear.
wizard.alert-point-2: Once provisioning is complete, resources will reappear and be managed through external storage.
wizard.alert-point-2_description: Informational message in the Provisioning Wizard: Informs the user about the re-appearance and external management of resources once provisioning is successfully completed.
wizard.alert-point-3: The duration of this process depends on the number of resources involved.
wizard.alert-point-3_description: Informational tip in the Provisioning Wizard: Explains to the user that the duration of the current provisioning process is determined by the quantity of resources involved. It manages user expectations regarding wait times.
wizard.alert-point-4: Enterprise instance administrators can display an announcement banner to users. See <2>this guide</2> for step-by-step instructions.
wizard.alert-title: Important: No data or configuration will be lost, but dashboards will be temporarily unavailable for a few minutes.
wizard.button-next_description: Button in the provisioning wizard: This button allows the user to complete the wizard and finalize the provisioning process. It typically appears on the final step, replacing the standard 'Next' button.
wizard.button-start_description: Button in the Provisioning Wizard: Initiates the synchronization process.
wizard.check-status-button_description: Button in the Provisioning Wizard: Allows the user to check the status or availability of a repository during the provisioning process.
wizard.check-status-message: Repository connecting, synchronize will be ready soon.
wizard.check-status-message_description: Status message in Provisioning wizard: Informs the user that the system is establishing a repository connection and preparing for synchronization. This message provides an update during the setup process, indicating a brief wait.

discard-modal.body: This will delete the repository configuration and you will lose all progress. Are you sure you want to discard your changes?
discard-modal.confirm_description: Button text in the discard confirmation modal within the provisioning wizard: Confirms the user's decision to discard unsaved changes or cancel an ongoing operation.
discard-modal.dismiss_description: Button in the discard confirmation modal of the provisioning wizard: Allows the user to dismiss the modal and continue working without discarding current progress.
discard-modal.title: Discard repository setup?
discard-modal.title_description: Modal title in the provisioning wizard's discard confirmation modal: This title prompts the user to confirm if they wish to abandon the current repository setup process or any unsaved changes.

wizard.step-bootstrap_description: Instruction in the Provisioning Wizard's bootstrap step: Prompts the user to select the specific items or configurations that should be synchronized as part of the initial setup.
wizard.step-connect_description: Step label in the provisioning wizard: Identifies the stage where a user establishes a connection to a system or data source.
wizard.step-finish_description: Label in the provisioning wizard's final step: It guides the user to a section for configuring additional, often optional, settings. This step usually precedes the final completion of the provisioning.
wizard.step-synchronize_description: Label in the Provisioning Wizard: This element identifies a step or action that involves synchronizing configurations or data with an external storage system.
wizard.sync-description: Sync resources with external storage. After this one-time step, all future updates will be automatically saved to the repository and provisioned back into the instance.
wizard.sync-option-history_description: Option label in the provisioning wizard, related to synchronization: Refers to the log or record of past synchronization events or changes.
wizard.title-bootstrap_description: Wizard step title in the Provisioning Wizard: This title prompts the user to select the specific data or configurations they want to synchronize. It appears at the beginning of the synchronization setup process.
wizard.title-connect_description: Title in the provisioning wizard: It marks a step where users configure or establish a connection to an external storage system. This is crucial for setting up data storage within the dashboard.
wizard.title-finish_description: Title in the final step of the provisioning wizard: This title indicates the screen where users can select or configure any remaining optional settings before completing the provisioning process.
wizard.title-synchronize_description: Title in Provisioning wizard: This title identifies a step or the overall wizard focused on synchronizing with an external storage system. It guides the user through connecting and integrating with external data sources as part of a setup or configuration process.

wizard-content.error-instance-repository-exists_descrip: Error Message in a provisioning wizard: Displayed when a user attempts to create an instance repository, but one with the same identifier or configuration already exists in the system.
wizard-content.error-save-repository-setting_descriptio: Error message in the provisioning wizard: Informs the user that an attempt to save repository settings was unsuccessful during the provisioning process.

workflow-options-label.push-to-a-new-branch_description: Menu item in the Workflow Options menu within the Provisioning section: Allows users to push current dashboard or configuration changes to a newly created branch. This action is typically used for version control and collaborative development of provisioned assets.
workflow-options-label.push-to-existing-branch_description: Option in Workflow Options in the Provisioning section: Allows users to select pushing changes to an existing branch as part of a workflow.

acknowledgment-checkboxes.ack-title: Before you make the dashboard public, acknowledge the following:
acknowledgment-checkboxes.ack-title_description: Title in the acknowledgment checkboxes section for public dashboards: It introduces the items that users must acknowledge before making a dashboard publicly accessible.
acknowledgment-checkboxes.data-src-ack-desc: Publishing currently only works with a subset of data sources*
acknowledgment-checkboxes.data-src-ack-desc_description: Informational text in the acknowledgment checkboxes section of the public dashboard publishing feature: It serves as a disclaimer, notifying users that publishing is currently limited to only a subset of data sources. The asterisk likely indicates a link to more details or a footnote.
acknowledgment-checkboxes.data-src-ack-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the public dashboard, near acknowledgment checkboxes: Provides users with additional information or a link to learn more about public data sources. This tooltip likely appears when hovering over a related element that requires acknowledgment regarding data sources.
acknowledgment-checkboxes.public-ack-desc: Your entire dashboard will be public*
acknowledgment-checkboxes.public-ack-desc_description: Explanatory text in the Public Dashboard's acknowledgment checkboxes section: This text informs the user that making their dashboard public will expose its entire content, requiring their acknowledgment.
acknowledgment-checkboxes.public-ack-tooltip_description: Link text in a tooltip associated with an acknowledgment checkbox on the Public Dashboard configuration page: Provides a clickable link for users to learn more about public dashboards.
acknowledgment-checkboxes.usage-ack-desc: Making a dashboard public will cause queries to run each time it is viewed, which may increase costs*
acknowledgment-checkboxes.usage-ack-desc-tooltip_description: Tooltip text next to a usage acknowledgment checkbox on a public dashboard: Provides information or a link to learn more about query caching, likely relevant to the terms being acknowledged regarding dashboard usage.

config.can-view-dashboard-radio-button-label_de: Radio button label in the public dashboard configuration settings: Labels an option to enable or disable public viewing access for the dashboard.
config.copy-button_description: Button in Public Dashboard Configuration: Copies a shareable link or configuration details for the public dashboard.
config.dashboard-url-field-label_description: Label in the public dashboard configuration screen: Identifies the input field or display area for the URL of a public dashboard.
config.email-share-type-option-label_descriptio: Option label in the public dashboard configuration for email sharing: This option allows users to select a sharing type where the public dashboard is shared via email only with explicitly named recipients.
config.pause-sharing-dashboard-label_descriptio: Button label in the public dashboard's configuration settings: This allows users to temporarily suspend or halt the public sharing of a dashboard.
config.public-share-type-option-label_descripti: Option label in Public Dashboard configuration settings: This option describes a sharing permission where the public dashboard will be accessible to anyone who has the generated link, without requiring specific authentication.
config.revoke-public-URL-button_description: Button in the Public Dashboard Configuration section: Used to disable or revoke access to a previously generated public URL for the dashboard. This action makes the dashboard inaccessible via that public link.
config.revoke-public-URL-button-title_descripti: Button title in the configuration settings for a public dashboard: This button allows users to disable or remove public access to the dashboard by revoking its previously generated public URL.
config.settings-title_description: Section Title in the configuration area of a public dashboard: It indicates the section where users can view or modify various settings and options related to the public dashboard.

configuration.display-annotations-description_descript: Description text in Public Dashboard Configuration: Explains the functionality of an option to display annotations on the current dashboard. This text clarifies what enabling the annotation display feature does.
configuration.display-annotations-label_description: Label in the Configuration section of a Public Dashboard: It indicates a setting that allows users to control the visibility of annotations on the dashboard.
configuration.enable-time-range-description: Allow people to change time range
configuration.enable-time-range-description_descriptio: Checkbox label in Public Dashboard Configuration settings: This label describes a control (e.g., a checkbox or toggle) that, when enabled, allows viewers of the public dashboard to interact with and change the displayed time range.
configuration.enable-time-range-label_description: Checkbox/Toggle label in Public Dashboard Configuration settings: This control allows users to enable or disable the time range selection functionality for the public dashboard.
configuration.settings-label_description: Label for a UI element in the configuration section of a public dashboard: Indicates a section or button for accessing and managing the dashboard's settings and options.
configuration.success-pause: Your dashboard access has been paused
configuration.success-pause_description: Success message in public dashboard configuration settings: Confirms that access to a public dashboard has been successfully paused after a user action.
configuration.success-resume: Your dashboard access has been resumed
configuration.success-resume_description: Success notification in Public Dashboard configuration: Confirms that access to a public dashboard has been successfully restored or resumed. This appears after an action taken to re-enable access to the dashboard.
configuration.success-update_description: A success notification in the public dashboard's configuration section: Informs the user that their modifications to the dashboard settings have been successfully saved.
configuration.time-range-label_description: Label in the configuration section of a public dashboard: Identifies a setting or input field used to specify a time range for the dashboard.
configuration.time-range-tooltip: The shared dashboard uses the default time range settings of the dashboard
configuration.time-range-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the Public Dashboard configuration section: Explains that a shared dashboard will display using its predefined default time range settings. This clarifies that the shared view does not override or offer separate time range selections.

create-page.generate-public-url-button_description: Button on the public dashboard creation page: Used to generate a unique URL that can be shared to make the newly configured dashboard publicly accessible.
create-page.unsupported-features-desc: Currently, we don’t support template variables or frontend data sources
create-page.unsupported-features-desc_description: Informational message on the Public Dashboard creation page: Informs users about features that are not supported when creating a public dashboard. This clarifies that template variables and frontend data sources cannot be used in this context.
create-page.welcome-title_description: A title on the public dashboard creation page: Welcomes the user to the public dashboards feature. It appears at the top of the page when a user initiates the creation process.

email-sharing.accept-button_description: Button in the email sharing feature of a public dashboard: Used to accept or confirm the action of sharing the dashboard via email, or to agree to associated terms or disclaimers.
email-sharing.alert-text: Sharing dashboards by email is billed per user for the duration of the 30-day token, regardless of how many dashboards are shared. Billing stops after 30 days unless you renew the token.
email-sharing.bill-ack: I understand that adding users requires payment.*
email-sharing.bill-ack_description: Acknowledgment message in Public Dashboard's email sharing feature: Informs the user that adding recipients for email sharing requires payment, and their explicit understanding is needed to proceed. This serves as a billing acknowledgment.
email-sharing.input-invalid-email-text_description: Error message in the email sharing section of a Public Dashboard: Informs the user that the entered email address is not valid.
email-sharing.input-required-email-text_description: Validation message in the Email Sharing dialog for a public dashboard: Informs the user that an email address must be provided in the input field before sharing. This message appears when the email field is left empty.
email-sharing.invite-button_description: Button in the email sharing section of a public dashboard: Used to invite recipients to view the dashboard.
email-sharing.invite-field-desc_description: Description in the email sharing section of a public dashboard: This text serves as an instruction or label for the input field where users enter email addresses to invite others to view the dashboard.
email-sharing.invite-field-label_description: Label for an input field in the email sharing section of a public dashboard: It indicates where users should enter an email address to invite a recipient.
email-sharing.learn-more-button_description: Button in the email sharing section of a public dashboard: Allows users to access more information or details about the email sharing functionality.
email-sharing.recipient-invalid-email-text_description: Error message in the email recipient input field within the public dashboard email sharing dialog: Notifies the user when the entered email address is not in a valid format.
email-sharing.recipient-invitation-button_description: Button in the email sharing section of a public dashboard: Allows the user to invite new recipients to view the dashboard via email.
email-sharing.recipient-invitation-description_descrip: Prompt in the email sharing section of a public dashboard: Guides the user to invite a recipient by entering their email address. This typically appears as a header for an email input field.
email-sharing.recipient-invitation-tooltip: This dashboard contains sensitive data. By using this feature you will be sharing with external people.
email-sharing.recipient-list-description: Only people you've directly invited can access this dashboard
email-sharing.recipient-list-description_description: Description in the Email Sharing section for a Public Dashboard: This text clarifies the access rights for recipients, stating that only individuals explicitly invited can view the dashboard when it is shared via email. It emphasizes that access via this method is restricted, even for a public dashboard.
email-sharing.recipient-list-title_description: Title in Email Sharing settings for a Public Dashboard: This title labels the section displaying individuals who have existing access to the dashboard and are potential recipients of a shared email.
email-sharing.recipient-required-email-text_descriptio: Validation message in the email sharing dialog for a public dashboard: Appears when the recipient's email address field is left empty, indicating it is a mandatory input.
email-sharing.resend-button_description: Button in the Email Sharing section of a Public Dashboard: Used to resend an email notification or sharing link for the dashboard. This typically applies when an initial share attempt failed or the recipient needs another copy.
email-sharing.resend-button-title_description: Button label in the Email Sharing section of a Public Dashboard: Allows a user to resend an email sharing the dashboard.
email-sharing.resend-invite-label_description: Button label in the email sharing section for a public dashboard: Allows the user to resend an invitation to a recipient, typically when the original was not received or needs to be resent.
email-sharing.revoke-access-label_description: Button label in the email sharing settings for a public dashboard: Allows the user to remove a recipient's access to the shared public dashboard.
email-sharing.revoke-button_description: Button in the email sharing settings for a public dashboard: Allows users to revoke or cancel a previously generated email sharing link or access.
email-sharing.revoke-button-title_description: Button title in the email sharing settings of a public dashboard: Used to cancel or withdraw existing email sharing access for the public dashboard.
email-sharing.success-creation: Your dashboard is ready for external sharing
email-sharing.success-creation_description: Success notification in a confirmation dialog or banner: It informs the user that their public dashboard has been successfully configured and is ready for external sharing, appearing after an email sharing action.
email-sharing.success-share-type-change: Dashboard access updated: Only specific people can now access with the link
email-sharing.success-share-type-change_description: Success message in Public Dashboard sharing settings: This message informs the user that the dashboard's sharing permissions have been successfully updated to a more restricted access level. Only specific individuals can now access the dashboard via its link.

modal-alerts.save-dashboard-changes-alert-title: Please save your dashboard changes before updating the public configuration
modal-alerts.save-dashboard-changes-alert-title_descr: Alert Title in a modal alert for public dashboard configuration: It prompts the user to save their dashboard changes before proceeding to update its public sharing settings. This ensures the public configuration is applied to the most recent, saved version of the dashboard.
modal-alerts.unsupport-data-source-alert-readmore-lin: Read more about supported data sources

Link in an alert modal on a public dashboard: This link directs users to additional documentation or information regarding supported data sources, typically displayed when a dashboard is using or encountering an unsupported data source.
modal-alerts.unsupported-data-source-alert-title_desc: Title in a modal alert on a public dashboard: Serves as the heading for a message informing the user about unsupported data sources.
modal-alerts.unsupported-template-variable-alert-desc: This public dashboard may not work since it uses template variables

Alert description in a modal alert displayed when viewing a public dashboard: It warns the user that the dashboard may not function correctly due to its use of template variables, which might not be fully supported in a public context.
modal-alerts.unsupported-template-variable-alert-titl: Alert Title in a modal alert on a public dashboard: Informs the user that template variables are not supported in this specific public dashboard context. It is displayed when an action involving template variables is attempted.

public-sharing.accept-button_description: Button in the public sharing interface for a public dashboard: Used to confirm or accept the public sharing settings or a disclaimer related to public access.
public-sharing.alert-text: Sharing this dashboard externally makes it entirely accessible to anyone with the link.
