public-sharing.alert-text_description: Alert text in the Public Sharing settings within a Public Dashboard: Informs the user about the security implications of sharing the dashboard externally. It warns that the dashboard will become entirely accessible to anyone with the link.
public-sharing.learn-more-button_description: Button in the Public Sharing section of a public dashboard: Allows users to access more information about public sharing functionalities, policies, or related details.
public-sharing.public-ack: I understand that this entire dashboard will be public.*
public-sharing.public-ack_description: Confirmation text in the Public Sharing section of a dashboard: Prompts the user to explicitly acknowledge that the entire dashboard will be publicly accessible. This acts as a critical warning before enabling public sharing.
public-sharing.success-creation: Your dashboard is now publicly accessible
public-sharing.success-creation_description: Success message in Public Sharing settings for a dashboard: Informs the user that their dashboard is now publicly viewable. This message appears after a user successfully enables public sharing for a dashboard.
public-sharing.success-share-type-change: Dashboard access updated: Anyone with the link can now access
public-sharing.success-share-type-change_description: Success message in the public sharing settings of a dashboard: It confirms that the dashboard's public access has been successfully updated, allowing anyone with the link to view it.

settings-bar-header.collapse-settings-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the settings bar header of a public dashboard: This tooltip text describes the action of collapsing or hiding the settings panel.
settings-bar-header.expand-settings-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the settings bar header of a public dashboard: Indicates that clicking the associated icon or button will expand or reveal more settings options.

settings-configuration.default-time-range-label_description: Label in Public Dashboard settings configuration: This label identifies the setting where users can define or view the default time period for data displayed on the public dashboard.
settings-configuration.default-time-range-label-desc: The public dashboard uses the default time range settings of the dashboard
settings-configuration.default-time-range-label-desc_descriptio: Description label in the Public Dashboard's Settings Configuration section: Informs the user that the public dashboard inherits its time range settings from the main dashboard's default configuration.
settings-configuration.show-annotations-label_description: Checkbox label in the settings configuration section of a public dashboard: Controls whether annotations are displayed on the public dashboard.
settings-configuration.show-annotations-label-desc_description: Description for a setting in Public Dashboard Settings: This text describes an option to control whether annotations are visible on a public dashboard.
settings-configuration.time-range-picker-label_description: Label for a setting in the Public Dashboard configuration: This setting controls whether the time range picker component is enabled for viewers of the public dashboard.
settings-configuration.time-range-picker-label-desc: Allow viewers to change time range
settings-configuration.time-range-picker-label-desc_description: Description text for a setting in the Public Dashboard Settings Configuration: This setting controls whether viewers of a public dashboard are permitted to adjust the time range of the displayed data.

settings-summary.annotations-hide-text_description: Summary text in the Public Dashboard settings summary: This line displays the current configuration state for annotations, indicating that they are set to be hidden.
settings-summary.annotations-show-text_description: Text label in the public dashboard settings summary: It indicates that annotations are currently enabled or configured to be displayed.
settings-summary.time-range-picker-disabled-text_descript: Text label in the settings summary section of a public dashboard: Informs the user that the time range picker functionality for the dashboard is disabled. This text is part of a summary display of dashboard settings.
settings-summary.time-range-picker-enabled-text_descripti: Text label in the Settings Summary section of a Public Dashboard: Indicates whether the time range picker functionality is enabled for the public dashboard.
settings-summary.time-range-text_description: Text label in the settings summary section of a public dashboard: This label precedes the displayed time range, indicating the period of data shown on the dashboard.

share.success-delete: Your dashboard is no longer shareable
share.success-delete_description: Success notification in the Public Dashboard Sharing feature: Informs the user that their dashboard's public sharing link has been successfully deactivated. This confirms that the dashboard is no longer accessible to others via that link.

share-configuration.share-type-label_description: Label in the Share Configuration section of a Public Dashboard: Identifies a sharing option that grants access to the dashboard via a direct link.

share-externally.copy-link-button_description: Button in the "Share Externally" section of the Public Dashboard feature: Allows users to copy a public, shareable link for the dashboard.
share-externally.email-share-type-option-description: Only people with the link can access dashboard
share-externally.email-share-type-option-description_desc: Description for a sharing option in the 'Share Externally' section of a Public Dashboard: This text explains that access to the dashboard is restricted to only those who have the direct share link.
share-externally.email-share-type-option-label_descriptio: Radio button option in the 'Share Externally' settings for a public dashboard: This option allows users to restrict email sharing of the dashboard to only designated individuals.
share-externally.no-upsert-perm-alert-desc: Contact your admin to get permission to {{action}} shared dashboards
share-externally.no-upsert-perm-alert-desc_description: Alert message in the 'Share Externally' feature for Public Dashboards: Informs the user that they lack the necessary permissions to perform a specific action (e.g., create, update) on shared public dashboards. It instructs them to contact their administrator to obtain these permissions.
share-externally.no-upsert-perm-alert-title: You don’t have permission to {{ action }} a shared dashboard
share-externally.no-upsert-perm-alert-title_description: Alert title in the Share Externally section of a public dashboard: Informs the user they lack the necessary permissions to perform a specific action on an externally shared dashboard. The `{{ action }}` placeholder will be dynamically replaced with the attempted action (e.g., update, save, delete).
share-externally.pause-access-button_description: Button in the "Share Externally" section of a Public Dashboard: Temporarily suspends external access to the shared dashboard. This action is typically reversible.
share-externally.pause-access-tooltip: Pausing will temporarily disable access to this dashboard for all users
share-externally.pause-access-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the 'Share Externally' section of a Public Dashboard: Explains the consequence of pausing access to the dashboard, indicating it will temporarily disable access for all users.
share-externally.public-share-type-option-description: Anyone with the link can access dashboard
share-externally.public-share-type-option-description_des: Option Description in the "Share Externally" section within the Public Dashboard feature: It describes a public sharing type where access is granted to anyone possessing the generated link.
share-externally.public-share-type-option-label_descripti: Option label in the 'Share externally' settings for a public dashboard: This option defines an access level, allowing anyone with the shared link to view the dashboard without further authentication.
share-externally.resume-access-button_description: Button in the 'Share externally' section of a public dashboard: Restores external access to the dashboard after it was previously paused or revoked. This reactivates previously shared links.
share-externally.revoke-access-button_description: Button in the Public Dashboard's 'Share Externally' section: Used to revoke previously granted external access to the dashboard.
share-externally.revoke-access-description: Are you sure you want to revoke this access? The dashboard can no longer be shared.
share-externally.revoke-access-description_description: Confirmation message in the 'Share Externally' section of a public dashboard: It asks the user to confirm revoking external sharing access and clarifies that the dashboard will no longer be shareable.
share-externally.unsupported-data-source-alert-desc: There are data sources in this dashboard that are unsupported for shared dashboards. Panels that use these data sources may not function properly: {{unsupportedDataSources}}.

dashboard-modal.external-link_description: Label in a dashboard modal within the public dashboard users access list: This identifies a field or section for associating an external web address with the dashboard.
dashboard-modal.open-dashboard-list-text_description: Link text in a dashboard modal within the Public Dashboard Users Access List interface: This allows users to open and view a list of available dashboards, likely for selection in the context of access management.
dashboard-modal.sharing-setting_description: Heading in the dashboard sharing modal: It introduces the section where users can configure the sharing options and access permissions for a specific dashboard.

delete-user-modal.delete-user-button-text_description: Button in the delete user confirmation modal: Confirms the action to delete a selected user from the public dashboard access list.
delete-user-modal.delete-user-revoke-access-button_descrip: Button in the "Delete User" modal on the Public Dashboard Users Access List page: Confirms and performs the action of revoking a selected user's access to public dashboards.
delete-user-modal.revoke-access-title_description: Title in a modal dialog displayed within the 'Public Dashboard Users Access List' interface: It indicates that the purpose of the modal is to revoke (remove) a user's access rights to the public dashboard.
delete-user-modal.revoke-user-access-modal-desc-line1: Are you sure you want to revoke access for {{email}}?
delete-user-modal.revoke-user-access-modal-desc-line1_desc: Confirmation prompt in the 'Revoke User Access' modal: This message asks the user to confirm their action to revoke access for a specific user from the Public Dashboard Users Access List. The `{{email}}` placeholder will be replaced with the user's email address.

delete-user-shared-dashboards-modal.revoke-user-access-modal-desc-line2: This action will immediately revoke {{email}}'s access to all shared dashboards.
delete-user-shared-dashboards-modal.revoke-user-access-modal-desc-line2_desc: A confirmation message line in the 'Revoke User Access' modal (part of the 'Delete User Shared Dashboards' modal): It warns the administrator that confirming the action will immediately revoke the specified user's access to all shared dashboards.

modal.shared-dashboard-modal-title_description: Modal Title in a modal window within the public dashboard users access list section: This title identifies a modal that displays or manages information about shared dashboards.

table-body.dashboard-count_one_description: A label in a table row of the public dashboard users access list. It displays the number of dashboards a user has access to, specifically used when the dashboard count is one.
table-body.dashboard-count_other_description: Text label in the table body of the public dashboard users access list: Displays the total number of dashboards associated with an entry. This string includes a `{{count}}` placeholder and is used for plural forms (when the count is not one).

table-header.activated-label_description: Table column header in the user access list table for public dashboards: It indicates whether a user's access is currently active or not.
table-header.activated-tooltip: Earliest time user has been an active user to a dashboard
table-header.activated-tooltip_description: Tooltip in a table header in the public dashboard users access list: This tooltip clarifies that the corresponding column displays the earliest recorded time a user was active on a dashboard.
table-header.last-active-label_description: Table header label in the user access list table for public dashboards: It identifies the column displaying the last activity timestamp for each user.
table-header.origin-label_description: Table header label in the 'Public Dashboard Users Access List' table: Identifies the source or method by which a user obtained access to the public dashboard.
table-header.role-label_description: Table header label in the 'Users Access List' table on a public dashboard: It indicates the column that displays the role assigned to each user.

query-editor-row-header.hidden_description: Label in the query editor row header: Indicates that the data series generated by this query row is hidden from the dashboard visualization. This typically means the query runs but its output is not rendered on the chart.
query-editor-row-header.query-name-div-title-edit-query-name_des: Label in the query editor row header: Allows users to modify the name of a specific query.

query-error-alert.trace-id_description: Text indicating an identifier in a query error alert: Displays a unique trace ID that helps users and support staff troubleshoot issues with failed queries.

query-group.add-query_description: Button in the query group editor: Initiates the process of adding a new query definition to the current group of queries.
query-group.expression_description: Input field label in a query group configuration: Used to define a logical or mathematical expression for data manipulation, such as calculations, filters, or formulas, within the query.
query-group.title-data-source-help_description: Title in the Query Group configuration: This title introduces a section or provides a link to help documentation specifically about data sources.

query-group-options-editor.collapsed-interval_description: Collapsed state label in the Query Group Options Editor: This label summarizes the configured interval value when the corresponding editor section is collapsed. The `{{intervalDesc}}` placeholder will display the specific interval.
query-group-options-editor.collapsed-max-data-points_description: Label in the Query Group Options Editor: This label displays the abbreviated 'Max Data Points' (MD) setting and its current value, typically shown when the setting is collapsed or in a summary view.
query-group-options-editor.hide-time-info_description: Checkbox in the Query Group Options Editor: This option allows users to hide time-related information displayed for the query results or visualization.
query-group-options-editor.Query options-title-query-options_descri: Title in the Query Group Options Editor: Labels a section where users can configure specific parameters or settings for a query.
query-group-options-editor.relative-time_description: Setting label in the Query Group Options Editor: This label identifies a configuration option allowing users to define a time range relative to the current time for the query group.
query-group-options-editor.relative-time-tooltip: Overrides the relative time range for individual panels, which causes them to be different than what is selected in the dashboard time picker in the top-right corner of the dashboard. For example to configure the Last 5 minutes the Relative time should be <1>{{relativeFrom}}</1> and <4>{{relativeTo}}</4>, or variables like <6>{{variable}}</6>.

render-cache-timeout-option.cache-timeout_description: Option title in the Query Group Options Editor, for the render cache timeout setting: Configures or displays the duration that rendered query results are stored in the cache before being refreshed.

render-interval-option.interval_description: Label in the Render Interval Option of the Query Group Options Editor: This label refers to the configurable time or data interval used for rendering and grouping data. This setting dictates how data points are aggregated or displayed over time.
render-interval-option.interval-tooltip: The evaluated interval that is sent to data source and is used in <1>$__interval</1> and <4>$__interval_ms</4>. This value is not exactly equal to <6>Time range / max data points</6>, it will approximate a series of magic number.
render-interval-option.min-interval_description: Input Field Label in the Render Interval Option within the Query Group Options Editor: Specifies the minimum time interval allowed for data rendering or aggregation.
render-interval-option.min-interval-tooltip: A lower limit for the interval. Recommended to be set to write frequency, for example <1>1m</1> if your data is written every minute. Default value can be set in data source settings for most data sources.
render-interval-option.time-range-max-data-points_description: Setting label in the Render Interval Options section of the Query Group Options editor: This label refers to parameters that control how data is sampled or aggregated for rendering. It typically involves defining a time interval for aggregation and/or limiting the total number of data points displayed.

render-max-data-points-option.max-data-points_description: Label for an input field in the 'Render max data points' option within the query group options editor: Defines the maximum number of data points that will be displayed or processed for a query. This setting helps manage visualization performance and detail.
render-max-data-points-option.max-data-points-tooltip: The maximum data points per series. Used directly by some data sources and used in calculation of auto interval. With streaming data this value is used for the rolling buffer.
render-max-data-points-option.width-of-panel_description: Label in the Query Group Options Editor: This label identifies an input field for setting the width of a display panel. This setting is found within the options for rendering a maximum number of data points from a query.

render-query-caching-ttloption.cache-ttl_description: Setting title in the query caching options within the query group options editor: Specifies the Time To Live (TTL) for cached query results. This value determines how long the query data remains valid in the cache before needing to be refreshed.

query-group-options-editor.time-shift_description: Field label in the query group options editor: Defines the temporal offset to apply to the data in the query group. This allows users to compare data from different time periods by shifting the query's time range.
query-group-options-editor.time-shift-tooltip: Overrides the time range for individual panels by shifting its start and end relative to the time picker. For example to configure the Last 1h the Time shift should be <1>{{relativeFrom}}</1> and <4>{{relativeTo}}</4>, or variables like <6>{{variable}}</6>.

query-group-top-section.data-source_description: Label in the top section of a query configuration group: This identifies the field where users select the specific data source for the query.
query-group-top-section.query-inspector_description: A section header or button in the top section of a query configuration: It is used to access detailed information and debugging tools for the currently configured query.
query-group-top-section.query-tab-help-button-title-open-data-so: Tooltip for a help button in the top section of the Query tab: It describes the action of opening help documentation relevant to the currently selected data source.
query-group-top-section.title-data-source-help_description: Label in the query group top section of a query editor: This label is used for a link or button that provides access to help documentation or information about the data source configured for the query.

header.collapse-row_description: Button in the header of a query operation section: Allows users to collapse a single query row, minimizing its displayed content.
header.datasource-help_description: Button in the header of the query operation panel: Allows users to display help documentation or information relevant to the data source currently selected or configured for the query.
header.drag-and-drop_description: Instructional text in the header of a query operation section: Informs users that elements within that section can be reordered by dragging and dropping them.
header.duplicate-query_description: Action label in the header of the query operation section: Prompts the user to duplicate the current query. This action creates a new query based on the existing one's configuration.
header.expand-row_description: Interactive element in the header of a query operations area: Used to expand a particular query row. This reveals additional details or configuration options for that specific query.
header.hide-response_description: Button in the header of a query operation panel: Toggles the visibility of the executed query's response to save screen space or focus on the query definition.
header.remove-query_description: Button or menu item in the header of a query operations interface: Used to delete or remove an existing query.
header.show-response_description: Button in the header section of a query operation: Allows the user to view the detailed response of a performed query.

query-operation.query-editor-not-exported: Data source plugin does not export any Query Editor component
query-operation.query-editor-not-exported_description: Informational message in the Query Editor: This message explains that the currently active data source plugin does not provide or export a user interface component for editing queries. It appears when the system expects a query editor UI from the plugin.

config.bar-width_description: Bar width in Radial Bar chart configuration: This setting allows users to specify the visual thickness of the individual bars in the chart.

effects.bar-glow_description: Setting in Radial Bar configuration effects: This controls the visual glow effect applied to the bars within a radial bar chart. It enhances the visual appearance of the radial bar segments.
effects.center-glow_description: Setting in the 'Effects' section of the Radial Bar chart configuration: Configures a glow effect originating from the center of the radial bar visualization.
effects.rounded-bars_description: Setting 'Rounded bars' in the Effects configuration for Radial Bars: Determines if the bars in the radial bar visualization have rounded ends. This option enhances the visual styling of the chart.
effects.spotlight_description: Spotlight effect in radial bar chart configuration: Configures a visual effect that highlights specific sections or data points within the radial bar chart. This helps draw attention to key information.

config.gradient_description: Option in Radial Bar chart configuration: Specifies whether a color gradient should be applied to the radial bar segments for visual styling.
config.gradient-auto_description: Auto" option in Radial bar chart configuration: This setting controls whether the gradient colors for the radial bar are automatically determined. When selected, the system manages the gradient display.
config.gradient-none_description: Selection option in the Gradient settings for a Radial Bar chart: Allows users to choose not to apply any gradient effect to the chart's bars.
config.segment-count_description: Setting in Radial bar chart configuration: Defines the number of divisions or parts within the radial bar visualization. This allows users to control the granularity of the radial bar display.
config.segment-spacing_description: Setting label in Radial Bar Chart Configuration: This setting defines the visual spacing between the individual segments within a radial bar chart. It allows users to customize the chart's appearance and readability.
config.shape_description: Configuration setting in radial bar chart's configuration: Defines the visual form or geometry of the radial bar elements.
config.shape-circle_description: Option in Radial Bar Chart Configuration: This option allows users to select "Circle" as the shape for the segments of the radial bar.
config.shape-gauge_description: Shape Option "Gauge" in the configuration panel for Radial Bar charts: This option allows users to select a gauge-style visualization for their data within the radial bar chart.
config.sparkline_description: Option in the Radial Bar chart's configuration settings: Controls whether a sparkline is displayed within the chart. Sparklines offer a compact visualization of data trends or patterns.
config.threshold-labels_description: Checkbox in radial bar configuration settings: Controls the visibility of threshold labels displayed on the radial bar chart.
config.threshold-markers_description: Checkbox in the Radial Bar configuration panel: Enables or disables the visibility of threshold markers on the radial bar visualization.

buttons.restore_description: Button in the Recently Deleted section: Allows the user to restore a selected deleted item back to its original location or state.

page.no-deleted-dashboards: You haven't deleted any dashboards recently.
page.no-deleted-dashboards_description: Empty state message on the Recently Deleted Dashboards page: Informs the user that they have not deleted any dashboards recently. This message is displayed when the list of recently deleted dashboards is empty.
page.no-deleted-dashboards-text: When you delete a dashboard, it will appear here for 30 days before being permanently deleted. Your organization administrator can restore recently-deleted dashboards.
page.no-search-result: No results found for your query
page.no-search-result_description: Message on the 'Recently Deleted' page: Displayed when a user performs a search for recently deleted items and no results match their query.

restore-modal.folder-picker-text_one: Please choose a folder where your dashboard will be restored.
restore-modal.folder-picker-text_one_description: Instructional text in the Restore Dashboard modal: Guides the user to choose a destination folder for the recently deleted dashboard being restored.
restore-modal.folder-picker-text_other: Please choose a folder where your dashboards will be restored.
restore-modal.folder-picker-text_other_description: Instructional text in the Restore modal within the Recently Deleted section: Guides the user to select a destination folder for the dashboards they are restoring. This text prompts the user for input during the restoration process.
restore-modal.restore-button_description: Button in the Restore confirmation modal within the 'Recently Deleted' section: Confirms the action of restoring a previously deleted item.
restore-modal.text_one_description: Confirmation message text in the Restore Modal within the Recently Deleted section: Informs the user that a single selected dashboard will be restored. This text is displayed as part of a confirmation prompt before the restoration action is performed.
restore-modal.text_other_description: Explanatory text in the Restore Modal within the Recently Deleted section: Informs the user about the consequence of proceeding with the restore action, specifically how many dashboards will be restored. It uses a dynamic placeholder for the number of dashboards.
restore-modal.title: Restore Dashboards
restore-modal.title_description: Modal title in the 'Restore Dashboards' modal within the recently deleted section: This title informs the user that they are about to restore one or more previously deleted dashboards.

filter.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the filter section of the Recently Deleted dashboards view: Prompts the user to search for dashboards within the deleted items.

strictMode.description: When <1>Reduce Strict mode</1> is used, the <3>fill(null)</3> function (InfluxQL) will result in <6>NaN</6>. <9>See the documentation for more details.</9>
strictMode.title: Strict Mode Behaviour
strictMode.title_description: Title in the 'Strict Mode' settings section of the 'Reduce' feature: Describes or configures how 'Strict Mode' operates or behaves.

aria-label.choose-interval: Auto refresh turned off. Choose refresh time interval
aria-label.choose-interval_description: ARIA label in the refresh picker component: Informs users that auto-refresh is currently turned off and prompts them to select a refresh time interval.
aria-label.duration-selected: Choose refresh time interval with current interval {{durationAriaLabel}} selected
aria-label.duration-selected_description: Aria-label for a refresh interval selector (e.g., dropdown or button) in the refresh picker component: It describes the control allowing users to choose how often data is refreshed, and informs them of the currently selected interval for accessibility.

auto-option.aria-label_description: ARIA label for the 'Auto refresh' option in the Refresh Picker: This accessibility label provides descriptive text for screen readers when they encounter the automatic refresh option. The current empty value means this option is not properly labeled for accessibility.
auto-option.label_description: Label in the automatic refresh option within the refresh picker: This UI string is intended to provide text for the 'Auto' setting related to dashboard refresh intervals. The empty value suggests it might be dynamically generated or used as an accessibility label.

live-option.aria-label_description: Toggle switch in the refresh picker component: Allows users to activate live data streaming, enabling continuous, real-time updates.
live-option.label: Live
live-option.label_description: Label in the refresh rate picker: Represents the option to display dashboard data in real-time or near real-time.

off-option.aria-label_description: Option in the refresh picker component: Disables the automatic refreshing of data on the dashboard. This is an accessibility label for screen readers.
off-option.label: Off
off-option.label_description: Label in the refresh frequency picker: Indicates the option to turn off automatic data refreshing.

tooltip.interval-selected_description: Instructional text in a tooltip for the dashboard's refresh rate selector: This text prompts the user to set or choose the automatic data refresh interval.
tooltip.turned-off_description: Tooltip in Refresh picker: Indicates that the automatic refresh function is currently disabled.

button.label: Back to {{title}}
button.label_description: Button in a navigational context: Allows the user to return to the previous screen or view. The `{{title}}` placeholder dynamically displays the name of the destination.

built-in.basic-roles_description: Label in the "Built-in" section of a role picker: Identifies a category of fundamental, system-provided roles.

input.no-roles_description: Message in the role picker input field: This message is displayed when no roles have been assigned or selected within the role picker component. It informs the user about the current state of role assignment for the associated entity.
input.placeholder-select-role_description: Placeholder text in an input field within the role picker component: Prompts the user to select a role. It is displayed when no role has been selected.

menu.clear-button_description: Button in the role picker's menu: Clears all selections or applied filters within the role picker.
menu.tooltip: You can now select the "No basic role" option and add permissions to your custom needs. You can find more information in <1>our documentation</1> .

role-picker.menu-aria-label_description: Menu in the role picker component: Provides an accessible label for the menu that allows users to select roles.
role-picker.menu-group-option-aria-label_description: ARIA label for a menu option in the Role picker: Identifies a selectable option for role assignment within the role picker component.
role-picker.menu-option-aria-label_description: ARIA label for a selectable option in the role picker: This text provides an accessible name for an individual role choice within the role picker component. It helps screen readers identify the purpose of the option.

sub-menu.clear-button_description: Button in the role picker's sub-menu: Clears any selections, filters, or input made within that sub-menu.

role-picker.sub-menu-aria-label_description: Aria-label for a submenu in the role picker component: This label provides an accessible name for the submenu that appears when interacting with the role selection functionality.

title.description: Assign roles to users to ensure granular control over access to Grafana‘s features and resources. Find out more in our <2>documentation</2> .

basic-roles.label: Basic Roles
basic-roles.label_description: Section title in the role picker drawer: Labels a group of fundamental or standard user roles that can be selected.

role-picker-drawer.user-count_description: Number of users count in the role picker drawer: This string displays the number of users currently associated with a specific role. The plus sign serves as a visual prefix for the numerical count.

route-error.description: Grafana has likely been updated. Please try reloading the page.
route-error.error-title_description: Section title on an error page or panel: Labels the section that displays detailed information about an encountered error.
route-error.error-unexpected-title_description: Title in an Error Page: Displays a generic message informing the user that an unexpected error has occurred. This serves as the main heading for an unhandled error condition within a specific route.
route-error.reload-button_description: Button in an error message or page related to route failures: Allows the user to refresh the page or re-attempt loading the route after an error.
route-error.title: Unable to find application file
route-error.title_description: Title on a route error page or dialog: Informs the user that a necessary application file, required for the current route or application to function, could not be located.

message-length.info: The message is {{messageLength}} characters, which exceeds the maximum length of 500 characters. Please shorten it before saving.
message-length.title: Message too long
message-length.title_description: Error message in Save Dashboards functionality: Informs the user that text entered into a field (e.g., name, description) has exceeded the maximum allowed character limit when attempting to save a dashboard.

name-exists.message-info: A dashboard with the same name in the selected folder already exists, including recently deleted dashboards.
name-exists.message-suggestion: Please choose a different name or folder.
name-exists.message-suggestion_description: Message in the Save Dashboard dialog: This message is displayed when a user attempts to save a dashboard with a name that already exists within the current folder, suggesting a different name or destination.
name-exists.title: Dashboard name already exists
name-exists.title_description: Dialog title in the Save Dashboard workflow: Informs the user that the entered dashboard name is already in use when attempting to save.

dashboards.collapse_description: Button in the Dashboard interface: Used to minimize or hide a specific section, panel, or navigation element within the dashboard view. This action typically frees up screen space.
dashboards.expand_description: Button in a dashboard interface: Allows users to expand a collapsed element or section to reveal more content or a larger view.
dashboards.loading_description: Loading indicator text in the Dashboards section: Informs the user that the dashboards are actively being fetched or prepared for display.
dashboards.noResultsForFilter: No results found for your query
dashboards.noResultsForFilter_description: Status message in the Dashboard view: Informs the user when a search query or filter applied within a dashboard yields no matching data.
dashboards.noResultsForFilterClear_description: Button in the Dashboards section: Allows users to clear the currently applied search or filter criteria. This button is likely displayed when a search or filter operation results in no matching items.
dashboards.noResultsForScopes: No dashboards or links found for the selected scopes
dashboards.noResultsForScopes_description: Message in the Dashboards view: Displayed when no dashboards or links are found that match the user's selected "scopes." "Scopes" likely represent filters or organizational units within the system.
dashboards.noResultsNoScopes_description: Informational message in BMC Helix Dashboards: This text indicates that no data or results are displayed because no scopes have been selected.
dashboards.search_description: Search element label or placeholder in the Dashboards section: Enables users to find specific dashboards.

toggle.collapse_description: Toggle button in the Dashboards section: This control allows users to collapse (hide) the list of suggested dashboards, helping to save screen space.
toggle.disabled: Suggested dashboards list is disabled due to read only mode
toggle.disabled_description: Status message in the Suggested Dashboards section: Informs the user that the list of suggested dashboards is disabled. This occurs because the application is in read-only mode.
toggle.expand_description: Toggle button in the Dashboards section within Scopes: Used to expand and display the full list of suggested dashboards, which is likely collapsed by default.

selector.apply_description: Button in the Scope Selector: Confirms and applies the selected scopes.
selector.error-title_description: Error message title in the scope selector: Informs the user that an unexpected error occurred, likely preventing proper scope selection.

input.removeAll_description: Button in the scopes selector input field: Clears all currently selected or entered scopes from the input.

selector.title: Select scopes
selector.title_description: Title in a scope selection component: Prompts the user to choose one or more scopes to apply to the current view or configuration.

headline.noResults: No results found for your query
headline.noResults_description: Headline in the Scopes Tree View: Informs the user that their search or filter query within the tree did not yield any matching results.
headline.recommended_description: Headline in the scopes tree view: This labels a section within the tree structure dedicated to displaying recommended scopes or related items for selection.
headline.results_description: Headline in the Scopes tree view: Labels the section displaying search or filtering results.

tree.search_description: Search input field placeholder text in the scopes tree view: Guides users on what to search for within the tree. The `{{parentTitle}}` variable dynamically specifies the search scope.

action-row.panel-type_description: Label in a search action row: Displays the type of a dashboard panel. The `{{panel}}` placeholder is dynamically replaced with the specific panel type.

actions.include-panels_description: Menu item in the Search actions menu: Allows users to include dashboard panels within the scope of their search. This broadens the search results to encompass relevant panels.
actions.remove-datasource-filter_description: Filter label in the active filter display area of the search interface: Identifies a specific datasource filter that has been applied and can be removed by the user.
actions.sort-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the search actions section: Indicates where users can select or define a sorting order for search results.
actions.starred_description: Menu item in the Search actions list: This option allows users to access or filter for items that have been marked as favorites or important.
actions.view-as-folders_description: Option in the search actions menu: Allows users to display search results or other items in a hierarchical folder structure.
actions.view-as-list_description: Action button in the search actions menu: Allows users to switch the display format of search results or other displayed items to a list view.
actions.view-layout_description: Action in the Actions menu of the Search interface: Allows users to view or change the current display layout. This option helps in customizing the visual presentation of search results or dashboards.

label.alphabetically-az_description: Label in the search options: This option allows users to sort items or search results in ascending alphabetical order (A-Z).
label.alphabetically-za_description: Sorting option label in search options: This label allows users to sort search results or lists in reverse alphabetical order from Z to A.

dashboard-actions.import_description: Button in the Dashboard Actions menu: Allows users to import a dashboard configuration or definition. This action is typically found when managing or searching for dashboards.
dashboard-actions.new-dashboard_description: Menu item in the Dashboard actions menu: Initiates the process of creating a new dashboard.
dashboard-actions.new-folder_description: Menu item in the Dashboard Actions menu (e.g., within a dashboard search results page): Allows users to create a new folder for organizing dashboards.

generate-columns.score_description: Column header in the Generate Columns section within a search interface: Indicates a calculated relevance or performance score for displayed items or search results.

results-table.datasource-header_description: Column Header in the search results table: It labels the column that displays the data source for each search result.
results-table.deleted-less-than-1-min_description: Filter option in the search results table: Used to filter or indicate items that were deleted less than one minute ago.
results-table.deleted-remaining-header_description: Table header in the search results table: Displays the time remaining until a deleted item is permanently removed or its data retention period expires.
results-table.location-header_description: Column Header in the search results table: Displays the location information for each item listed in the search results.
results-table.tags-header_description: Column header in the search results table: Labels a column that displays the tags associated with each search result item.
results-table.type-dashboard_description: Label in a search results table: Identifies an entry as a 'Dashboard' type, helping users categorize or filter search results.
results-table.type-folder_description: Label in the search results table: Denotes that a search result item is of the type 'Folder'. This string helps categorize items found during a search.
results-table.type-header_description: Column header in a search results table: Used to identify the type of an item or entity listed in that column.

search-input.include-panels-placeholder: Search for dashboards, folders, and panels
search-input.include-panels-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the search input field: It informs the user about the types of content they can search for, specifically dashboards, folders, and panels.
search-input.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the search input field: Guides the user to search for available dashboards and folders within the application.

search-results-table.aria-label-search-results-table_descript: ARIA label for a table in the search results area: Provides an accessible name for the table that displays the results of a search query.
search-results-table.no-data_description: Text message in the search results table: Informs the user that no data values were found to display after a search operation.

label.alphabetically-az_description: Label in the Unified Searcher's options: This text represents a sorting option that arranges search results or items in ascending alphabetical order (A-Z).
label.alphabetically-za_description: Label in the sorting options of the unified searcher: Allows users to sort search results or items in descending alphabetical order.

select-menu.selected-count_description: Label in a select menu: Displays a count of selected items. It is typically part of a phrase like 'X Selected' visible when multiple options are chosen.

name.label: Display name
name.label_description: Label in the 'Name' section on the Service Account Create page: It prompts for the user-facing name used to identify the service account. This name will be displayed within the UI.
name.required-error_description: Error message in the Service Account Create page: This message is displayed when the 'Display name' input field is left empty, indicating that it is a required field for creating a service account.

page-nav.label: Create service account
page-nav.label_description: **Navigation link label** in the **page navigation section on the 'Create Service Account' page**: This label allows users to navigate to or identify the part of the page dedicated to creating a new service account, often as a tab or step in a workflow.

role.label: Role
role.label_description: Label on the Service Account Create page: Denotes the input field or selection for assigning a specific role or set of permissions to the new service account.

service-account-picker.noOptionsMessage-no-service-accounts-fou: Message in the service account picker: Informs the user when no service accounts are available to select.
service-account-picker.select-aria-label_description: Picker control in the Service Account Picker component: Allows users to select a service account. This string serves as an ARIA label for accessibility purposes.
service-account-picker.select-placeholder: Start typing to search for service accounts
service-account-picker.select-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a service account selection input field: Prompts the user to start typing to search for and select desired service accounts.

empty-state.button-title_description: Button in the empty state of the Service Accounts page: Prompts the user to add a new service account when none are currently defined.
empty-state.message_description: Message in the 'Service Accounts' empty state: Informs the user that no service accounts are currently found or configured. This text is displayed when the list of service accounts is empty.
empty-state.more-info: Remember, you can provide specific permissions for API access to other applications
empty-state.more-info_description: Informational message in the empty state section of the Service Accounts page: It reminds users about the core function of service accounts, which is to grant specific API access permissions to other applications. This message helps users understand the value of creating a service account when the list is empty.
empty-state.title: You haven't created any service accounts yet
empty-state.title_description: Title in the Service Accounts section's empty state: This title informs the user that no service accounts have been created yet. It is displayed when the list of service accounts is empty.

create-token-modal.copy-clipboard_description: Button in the Create Token modal within the Service Accounts section: To allow the user to easily copy a generated token or API key to their clipboard.
create-token-modal.copy-to-clipboard-and-close_description: Button in the Create Token Modal within the Service Accounts section: This button allows the user to copy the newly generated service account token to the clipboard and then dismiss the modal.
create-token-modal.description-name-to-easily-identify-the-: Name to easily identify the token

Label for a text input field in the 'Create Token' modal within the Service Accounts section: Prompts the user to enter a descriptive name for the new token being created. This name helps in easily identifying the token among others.
create-token-modal.description-token: Copy the token now as you will not be able to see it again. Losing a token requires creating a new one.
create-token-modal.generate-token_description: Button in the Create Token Modal for Service Accounts: Initiates the generation of a new service account token.
create-token-modal.label-display-name_description: Label in the "Create Token" modal within the Service Accounts section: Prompts the user to enter a user-friendly name for the service account token. This name serves as an alias for easy identification.
create-token-modal.label-expiration_description: Label in the Create Token Modal (Service Accounts): Specifies the expiration date or time for the new token. This field determines the token's validity period.
create-token-modal.label-expiration-date_description: Label in the Create Token modal within the Service Accounts section: Indicates the date when a new service account token will become invalid or expire.
create-token-modal.label-token_description: Label in the 'Create Token' modal within the Service Accounts section: Identifies the input field or display area for the new security token.

get-actions-cell.add-token_description: Button in the 'Actions' column on the Service Accounts management screen: This button allows users to generate and add a new API token for a specific service account, typically used for authentication or API access.
get-actions-cell.aria-label-delete-button_description: ARIA label for a delete button in the actions cell within the Service Accounts list/table: It provides an accessible description for screen readers, specifying which service account will be deleted when the button is activated.
get-actions-cell.disable_description: Action in the actions cell of the Service Accounts section: Used to deactivate a selected service account. This action typically makes the service account temporarily unusable without deleting it.
get-actions-cell.enable_description: Action button in Service Accounts actions cell: Allows users to enable a selected service account. This action changes the status of a service account to active.
get-actions-cell.tooltip-managed-service-account-cannot-m: This is a managed service account and cannot be modified

Tooltip in the actions column for a service account on the Service Accounts management page: Informs the user that the selected service account cannot be modified because it is a system-managed account.

get-role-cell.aria-label-role_description: Accessibility label in the Service Accounts section: Provides a descriptive label for the role displayed or selected for a service account. This label is primarily for accessibility, assisting screen reader users.

service-account-list-item.add-token_description: Button in a Service Account list item: Allows users to add a new authentication token for that specific service account.
service-account-list-item.aria-label-role_description: Label in a service account list item: This label identifies the security role assigned to the service account. It is an accessibility label.
service-account-list-item.disable_description: Button in a service account list item within the Service Accounts section: Used to deactivate the selected service account. This action prevents the service account from being used while retaining its configuration for potential re-enablement.
service-account-list-item.enable_description: Action button in a Service Account list item within the Service Accounts section: Activates the corresponding service account, making it available for use.
service-account-list-item.title-tokens_description: Title in a Service Account List Item: Identifies the section or tab dedicated to managing or viewing authentication tokens associated with that specific service account.
service-account-list-item.tooltip-delete-button_description: Tooltip for a delete button in a service account list item: This tooltip clarifies which specific service account will be deleted when the user activates the delete button. The placeholder `{{serviceAccountName}}` will be dynamically replaced with the actual name of the service account.
service-account-list-item.tooltip-managed-service-account-cannot-m: This is a managed service account and cannot be modified

Tooltip in a service account list item: Informs the user that a specific service account is managed and therefore cannot be modified. It appears when hovering over an unmodifiable service account in the list.

service-account-page-unconnected.add-service-account-token_description: Button or link in the Service Account page for unconnected accounts: Enables users to add an authentication token to a service account. This action is used to establish or complete the connection for a service account.
service-account-page-unconnected.body-delete-service-account: Are you sure you want to delete this service account?
service-account-page-unconnected.body-delete-service-account_description: Confirmation message in the Service Account Management page for 'unconnected' accounts: Prompts the user to confirm the deletion of a service account. This message is displayed in a modal dialog before the permanent removal of the account.
service-account-page-unconnected.body-disable-service-account: Are you sure you want to disable this service account?
service-account-page-unconnected.body-disable-service-account_description: Confirmation message in the Unconnected Service Account Page: Asks the user to confirm their decision before disabling a specific service account.
service-account-page-unconnected.confirmText-delete-service-account_descr: Confirmation dialog title on the Service Accounts page: Prompts the user to confirm the deletion of an unconnected service account. This text is displayed at the top of a dialog box before the service account is permanently removed.
service-account-page-unconnected.confirmText-disable-service-account_desc: Confirmation text in a dialog on the Unconnected Service Accounts page: Prompts the user to confirm the action of disabling a selected service account.
service-account-page-unconnected.delete-service-account_description: Button label on the Service Account management page for unconnected accounts: Initiates the process to delete a service account. This action is specific to accounts that are not currently connected or fully configured.
service-account-page-unconnected.disable-service-account_description: Action button on the Unconnected Service Account page: Disables a service account that is not currently connected. This action prevents the service account from being used or connected.
service-account-page-unconnected.enable-service-account_description: Button on the 'Unconnected Service Account' page: Allows the user to activate a specific service account that is currently inactive or newly configured, making it operational.

subTitle.manage-settings-individual-service-accou: Manage settings for an individual service account.

Sub-title in the header of the Service Account Management page: This text serves as a contextual indicator, informing the user that they are currently managing the settings for one specific service account.

service-account-page-unconnected.title-delete-service-account_description: Title of a confirmation dialog in the 'Unconnected Service Account' page: Prompts the user to confirm the deletion of a service account.
service-account-page-unconnected.title-disable-service-account_descriptio: **Dialog Title** on the **Service Account Details page for an unconnected service account**: This title appears in a confirmation dialog when a user attempts to disable a service account that is not currently connected or in use.
service-account-page-unconnected.tokens_description: Section label in the Unconnected Service Account details page: This label designates the section where users can manage authentication tokens for API access or external integration of the service account.
service-account-page-unconnected.tooltip-managed-service-account-cannot-m: This is a managed service account and cannot be modified
Tooltip in the Service Account Details page (unconnected view): Explains that the displayed service account is managed externally and therefore cannot be modified.

service-account-profile.information_description: Section title in the Service Account Profile: This title indicates the section containing general information and core properties of the service account.
service-account-profile.label-creation-date_description: Label in the Service Account Profile page: Indicates the date when the service account was created.
service-account-profile.label-numerical-identifier_description: Label in the Service Account Profile page: Represents a unique, system-generated numerical identifier assigned to the service account. This identifier is used for internal system recognition and referencing.
service-account-profile.label-roles_description: Label in the Service Account Profile page: This label identifies the section or field that displays the roles assigned to a particular service account.
service-account-profile.used-by_description: Label in Service Account Profile page: Introduces a section or list displaying which entities (e.g., dashboards, monitors, users) are currently utilizing the service account. This helps administrators understand the service account's dependencies and potential impact.

service-account-role-row.aria-label-role_description: **Accessibility label** in **a service account's role row in the Service Accounts section**: It identifies the assigned **role** of a service account. This string is used as an ARIA label for accessibility purposes.

service-account-tokens-table.aria-label-delete-button_description: Accessible label for a Delete button in the Service Account Tokens table: This label provides an accessible, descriptive name for a button used to delete a specific service account token. The `{{key}}` placeholder indicates that the token's unique identifier will be included in the label for clarity.
service-account-tokens-table.created_description: Table header in the Service Account Tokens table: Labels a column indicating the date and time when a service account token was created.
service-account-tokens-table.expires_description: Table header in the Service Account Tokens table: Indicates the expiration date or time for a service account token.
service-account-tokens-table.last-used-at_description: Column header in the Service Account Tokens table within the Service Accounts section: It indicates the timestamp when a service account token was last used.
service-account-tokens-table.name: Name

service-accounts-list-page-unconnected.add-service-account_description: Button in the Service Accounts List page (unconnected view): This button allows users to initiate the process of adding a new service account.
service-accounts-list-page-unconnected.body-delete_one: Are you sure you want to delete {{serviceAccountName}} and {{count}} accompanying tokens?
service-accounts-list-page-unconnected.body-delete_one_description: Confirmation dialog message in the Service Accounts List Page: Prompts the user to confirm the deletion of a specific service account and its accompanying tokens. This message is displayed when deleting a single service account from the unconnected list.
service-accounts-list-page-unconnected.body-delete_other: Are you sure you want to delete {{serviceAccountName}} and {{count}} accompanying tokens?
service-accounts-list-page-unconnected.body-delete_other_description: Confirmation dialog message on the Service Accounts list page for unconnected service accounts: This message asks the user to confirm the deletion of a specific service account along with its associated tokens.
service-accounts-list-page-unconnected.body-delete-with-tokens: Are you sure you want to delete {{serviceAccountName}}?
service-accounts-list-page-unconnected.body-delete-with-tokens_description: Confirmation dialog message in the Service Accounts List Page (unconnected): Asks the user to confirm the deletion of a specified service account. This message is used when the service account potentially has associated tokens.
service-accounts-list-page-unconnected.body-disable-service-account: Are you sure you want to disable '{{accountToDisable}}'?
service-accounts-list-page-unconnected.body-disable-service-account_description: Confirmation message in the Unconnected Service Accounts list page: Prompts the user to confirm they want to disable a specific service account. This text appears within a confirmation dialog.
service-accounts-list-page-unconnected.confirmText-disable-service-account_desc: Confirmation dialog title in the Service Accounts List Page (unconnected): Prompts the user to confirm the action of disabling a service account.
service-accounts-list-page-unconnected.placeholder-search-service-account-by-na: Placeholder text for a search input field on the Service Accounts list page for unconnected accounts: It guides users to enter a service account's name to filter the displayed list.
service-accounts-list-page-unconnected.sub-title: Service accounts and their tokens can be used to authenticate against the Grafana API. Find out more in our <2>documentation.</2>
service-accounts-list-page-unconnected.title-delete-service-account_description: Title in the Service Accounts List (Unconnected) page: This heading is displayed for a dialog or section where a user confirms or initiates the deletion of a service account.
service-accounts-list-page-unconnected.title-disable-service-account_descriptio: Title on the 'Service Accounts List (Unconnected)' page: This title describes the action to disable a service account. It is likely used for service accounts that are not currently connected or in use.

token-expiration.content-this-token-has-expired_descripti: Message in the Service Accounts > Token Expiration section: Informs the user that a token they are attempting to use or view has expired.
token-expiration.expired-label_description: Status label in the Service Accounts token expiration display: Indicates that a service account token has passed its validity period and is no longer active.
token-expiration.never_description: Option in Token expiration settings for service accounts: This option indicates that the service account token will never expire. It allows for an indefinite token lifespan.

token-revoked.content-token-publicly-exposed-please-ro: This token has been publicly exposed. Please rotate this token

Security warning message displayed within the Service Accounts management area for a token that has been revoked. It informs the user that the token was publicly exposed and advises immediate rotation for security.
token-revoked.revoked-label_description: Label in the Service Accounts management interface, specifically within a section dealing with revoked tokens: It indicates the status of a service account token that has been cancelled or invalidated.

menu.export-image-title_description: Menu item in the 'Share Dashboard' menu: Allows users to export the current dashboard view as an image file.
menu.export-json-title_description: Menu item in the Share Dashboard menu: Allows users to export the current dashboard's definition or data in JSON format.
menu.share-externally-title_description: Menu item in the dashboard's "Share" menu: This option allows users to initiate the process of sharing the dashboard with recipients or platforms external to the BMC Helix environment.
menu.share-internally-title_description: Menu item in the dashboard sharing menu: This option allows users to share the current dashboard exclusively with other internal users or groups within the system.
menu.share-snapshot-title_description: Menu item title in the "Share dashboard" menu: This title initiates the process for users to share a static snapshot of the current dashboard.

share-dashboard.share-button_description: Button in the Share Dashboard dialog/panel: Initiates the process of sharing the current dashboard.
share-dashboard.share-button-tooltip_description: Tooltip for the Share button in the Share Dashboard feature: Appears on hover to indicate that clicking the button will copy the dashboard's link to the clipboard.

confirm-action.back-arrow-button_description: Button in the confirmation action screen within the Share drawer: Allows the user to navigate back to the previous view or cancel the current action.

dashboard.title: Share
dashboard.title_description: Title in the Share modal for a dashboard: Indicates the purpose of the modal is to facilitate sharing of the dashboard.

embed.copy_description: Button in the Embed section of the Share modal: This button allows users to copy the generated embed code for a dashboard to their clipboard. It facilitates sharing the dashboard content on other platforms.
embed.html_description: Title in the 'Embed' section of the 'Share' modal: Indicates the option or section where users can generate HTML code to embed the dashboard content into external applications or websites.
embed.html-description: The HTML code below can be pasted and included in another web page. Unless anonymous access is enabled, the users viewing that page need to be signed into BMC Helix Dashboards for the graph to load.
embed.info: Generate HTML for embedding an iframe with this panel
embed.info_description: Informational text in the "Embed" section of the "Share" modal: Describes the functionality to generate HTML code for embedding the current dashboard panel as an iframe into another web page.
embed.time-range_description: Toggle in the 'Embed' section of the 'Share' modal: Controls whether the time range for the embedded dashboard or panel is locked. When enabled, viewers of the embedded content cannot change the displayed time range.

export.back-button_description: Button in the export configuration screen of the share modal: Returns the user to the main export configuration settings screen.
export.info-text_description: Informational text in the 'Export' section of the 'Share' modal: This text introduces or describes the primary action of exporting the current dashboard from this section.
export.save-button_description: Button in the export section of the Share modal: Initiates saving the exported content (e.g., dashboard, panel data) to a local file.
export.share-externally-label_description: Label in the Export section of the Share Modal: This label indicates an option to export content (e.g., a dashboard or panel) specifically for sharing or distribution outside of the BMC Helix Dashboards environment. It distinguishes this action from internal sharing mechanisms.
export.view-button_description: Button in the Share Modal's Export section: Allows users to view the underlying configuration or data of the shared dashboard in JSON format.
export.view-button-yaml_description: Button in the Export section of the Share modal: Allows the user to view the YAML representation of the dashboard or configuration being shared/exported.

image.actions_description: Heading in the Image export section of the Share modal: This heading labels the various options or controls available for exporting an image.
image.download-button_description: Button in the Image section of the Share modal: Allows the user to download the current dashboard or panel as an image file.
image.error-title_description: Error title in the Share modal's image generation feature: Informs the user that the system failed to generate an image, likely for export or sharing.
image.generate-button_description: Button in the Share Modal's 'Image' section: Initiates the process of generating an image (e.g., of the current dashboard or panel) for sharing.
image.info-text: Save this dashboard as an image
image.info-text_description: Instructional text in the 'Image' section of the Share modal: It informs the user about the functionality to save the current dashboard as an image file.
image.preview_description: Button or text label in the image sharing section within the Share modal: This element allows users to view a preview of the image being prepared for sharing.
image.preview-aria_description: ARIA label for the image preview area in the Share modal: Describes the dynamically generated image content that will be shared.
image.preview-region_description: Label for a region in the 'Image' tab of the 'Share modal': Indicates the area where a visual representation of the image being shared or exported is displayed. This allows the user to review the output.
image.title: Export as image
image.title_description: Button label in the Share modal, within the image export options: This button initiates the export of the current dashboard or visualization as an image file.

library.info_description: Button in the 'Library' section of the 'Share' modal: Allows the user to initiate the creation of a new reusable library panel.

link.copy-link-button_description: Button in Link section within the share modal: Allows the user to copy the shareable link to their clipboard.
link.info-text: Create a direct link to this dashboard or panel, customized with the options below.
link.info-text_description: Informational text in the 'Link' section of the Share modal: This text explains how to create a direct link to the current dashboard or panel, noting that customization options are available below this message.
link.link-url_description: Label in the "Link" section of the "Share" modal: This identifies the URL of the dashboard or panel that can be shared or copied.
link.render-alert_description: Error message in the Share modal's link generation section: This message informs the user that the required image renderer plugin is not installed, preventing the creation of a shareable image or an image link.
link.render-instructions: To render an image, you must install the <2>Grafana image renderer plugin</2> . Please contact your Grafana administrator to install the plugin.
link.rendered-image_description: **Option label** in the **Link section of the Share modal**: This label identifies an option to generate a direct link to a static, rendered image of the current dashboard or panel for sharing purposes.
link.save-alert_description: Alert message in the Share modal, specifically when attempting to generate a shareable link: It notifies the user that the dashboard has unsaved changes and must be saved before a shareable link can be generated.
link.save-dashboard: To render a panel image, you must save the dashboard first.
link.save-dashboard_description: Notification message in the Share Modal, under link sharing options: It informs the user that the dashboard must be saved before a panel image can be rendered. This message appears when attempting to generate a shareable link or embed an image of a panel.
link.shorten-url_description: Button in the Share modal's Link section: Used to generate a shortened version of the current dashboard's URL for sharing.
link.time-range-description: Transforms the current relative time range to an absolute time range
link.time-range-description_description: Description text in the Share Modal, associated with an option for sharing a link: It explains that enabling this option converts the dashboard's current relative time range (e.g., "last 5 minutes") into an absolute time range (e.g., "10:00 AM - 10:05 AM"). This ensures the shared link displays data from a fixed, precise time period.
link.time-range-label_description: Checkbox label in the share modal's link sharing options: Allows the user to fix the current dashboard time range when generating a shareable link. This ensures the recipient views the content with the exact same time range as the sender.

panel.title: Share Panel
panel.title_description: Title in the Share modal: Identifies the section or modal used for sharing a specific dashboard panel.

snapshot.copy-link-button_description: Button in the Snapshot section of the Share modal: Copies a link to the generated dashboard snapshot.
snapshot.delete-button_description: Button in the 'Snapshot' section of the 'Share' modal: Allows the user to delete a previously created snapshot.
snapshot.deleted-message: The snapshot has been deleted. If you have already accessed it once, then it might take up to an hour before before it is removed from browser caches or CDN caches.
snapshot.expire_description: Label in the Share modal's Snapshot section: This string labels a setting or input field related to the expiration of a shared snapshot. It defines how long the snapshot will remain accessible.
snapshot.expire-day_description: Expiration duration option in Share Snapshot modal: Specifies that the shared dashboard snapshot will expire after one day. This is a selectable option to define the snapshot's availability period.
snapshot.expire-hour_description: Selectable option in the 'Share Snapshot' modal: Specifies a 1-hour expiration period for the shared snapshot link.
snapshot.expire-never_description: Option in Share Snapshot Modal: Indicates that the shared snapshot will never expire.
snapshot.expire-week_description: Selectable option in the Share modal's Snapshot section: Indicates a one-week expiry period for the shared snapshot.
snapshot.info-text-1: A snapshot is an instant way to share an interactive dashboard publicly. When created, we strip sensitive data like queries (metric, template, and annotation) and panel links, leaving only the visible metric data and series names embedded in your dashboard.
snapshot.info-text-2: Keep in mind, your snapshot <1>can be viewed by anyone</1> that has the link and can access the URL. Share wisely.
snapshot.local-button_description: Button in the Snapshot section of the Share modal: Publishes (generates and saves) a snapshot of the current dashboard data, likely making it available for local access or sharing.
snapshot.mistake-message_description: Confirmation prompt in the Snapshot section of the Share Modal: This message asks the user to review their snapshot for any errors before proceeding with sharing.
snapshot.name: Snapshot name
snapshot.name_description: Label for an input field in the 'Snapshot' functionality within the 'Share' modal: Prompts the user to enter a name for the snapshot being created.
snapshot.timeout_description: Label for an input field in the Share modal, within the Snapshot settings: Specifies the maximum duration, in seconds, for generating or processing a snapshot before the operation times out.
snapshot.timeout-description: You might need to configure the timeout value if it takes a long time to collect your dashboard metrics.
snapshot.url-label_description: Label in the Share modal, within the Snapshot section: It identifies the URL that users can copy to share a static, point-in-time view of the dashboard.

tab-title.embed_description: Tab Title in Share Modal: This tab allows users to generate code for embedding the dashboard into other applications or websites.
tab-title.export_description: Tab title in the Share modal: Identifies the section where users can export the dashboard or its data.
tab-title.library-panel_description: Tab title in the Share modal: This tab provides access to options for sharing or managing a reusable dashboard panel.
tab-title.link_description: Tab in the Share Modal: Allows users to access options for sharing a dashboard or panel via a direct link.
tab-title.public-dashboard-title_description: A tab title in the Share modal: This labels the tab dedicated to configuring or viewing options for publicly sharing a dashboard.
tab-title.snapshot_description: Tab title in the Share modal: This tab allows users to share a static image or a point-in-time view of the dashboard.

theme-picker.current_description: Option in the theme picker within the share modal: Refers to the theme that is currently active or selected for the shared content.
theme-picker.dark_description: Option label in the theme picker within the Share modal: Identifies the "Dark" theme as a selection for how the shared content will appear.
theme-picker.field-name_description: Label in the theme picker section of the share modal: This label identifies the input field or control used to select a visual theme for the content being shared.
theme-picker.light_description: Theme option in the Theme picker within the Share modal: Allows the user to select the light color scheme for the dashboard or content being shared.

view-json.copy-button_description: Button in the 'View JSON' section of the 'Share' modal: Copies the displayed JSON content to the user's clipboard.

drawer.new-library-panel-title_description: Title in the share panel drawer: This title identifies the section or form for creating a new reusable library panel.
drawer.share-embed-title_description: Title in the Share panel drawer: This title introduces the options for generating and sharing embed code or a link to integrate a dashboard or panel elsewhere.
drawer.share-link-title_description: Heading in the Share panel's drawer: This title introduces the section where users can configure settings for the shareable link.

menu.new-library-panel-title_description: Menu item in the share panel menu: This option allows users to create a new library panel. A library panel is a reusable component for dashboards.
menu.share-embed-title_description: Menu item in the 'Share' menu: This option allows users to generate an embed code to integrate a dashboard or panel into an external application or webpage.
menu.share-link-title_description: Menu item in the Share panel's menu: Allows users to generate and share a link to the current dashboard, panel, or visualization.
menu.share-snapshot-title_description: Title in the Share panel menu: This title introduces the options for sharing a static, point-in-time view of a dashboard or report.

new-library-panel.create-button_description: Button in the 'New Library Panel' section within the 'Share Panel': Initiates the creation of a new, reusable dashboard panel that can be saved to the library.

share-panel-image.disabled-help: Save the dashboard to enable image generation
share-panel-image.disabled-help_description: Help message in the Share panel's image generation section: Informs the user that image generation is currently disabled and instructs them to save the dashboard to enable this feature.
share-panel-image.download-disabled-help: Generate an image first to enable download
share-panel-image.download-disabled-help_description: Helper text in the Share panel, associated with image export: Explains why the image download option is disabled and instructs the user to generate an image first to enable the download.
share-panel-image.error-title_description: Error message title in the image sharing panel: Informs the user that the image generation process failed. This title is displayed as part of an error dialog or notification.

form.label: Panel image settings
form.label_description: Label in the 'Share Panel Image' form: This is the title for the section where users can configure various settings related to how a dashboard panel is presented as an image.

preview.title: Panel preview
preview.title_description: Title in the Share Panel Image feature, within the preview section: This title labels the area where users can see a preview of how the panel will appear when shared as an image.

settings.height-label_description: Label in the image settings section of the Share Panel: Prompts the user to specify or view the height of the image being shared.
settings.height-min: Height must be equal or greater than 1
