settings.height-min_description: Validation message for a numeric input field in the Share panel image settings: This message informs the user that the specified height value for the shared image must be at least 1.
settings.height-required_description: Validation message in the Share Image panel settings: Informs the user that a value for the image height field is mandatory when configuring the shared image properties.
settings.max-warning: Setting maximums are limited by the image renderer service
settings.max-warning_description: Warning message in the image settings of the Share panel: Informs the user that certain maximum settings for image generation or rendering are limited by the capabilities of the backend image renderer service.
settings.scale-factor-label_description: Label in the Image Settings section of the Share Panel dialog: This setting allows users to specify the scaling factor for an image generated when sharing a dashboard panel. A higher scale factor will result in a larger, higher-resolution image.
settings.scale-factor-min: Scale factor must be equal or greater than 1
settings.scale-factor-min_description: Validation message in the image sharing settings panel: This string informs the user that the 'Scale factor' for exported or shared images must be a value equal to or greater than 1. It enforces a minimum scale to prevent excessively small or low-resolution outputs.
settings.scale-factor-required_description: Validation message in the Share Panel's image settings: This message appears when the 'Scale Factor' field is left empty, indicating that a value is required to proceed with image sharing or save the settings.
settings.title: Image settings
settings.title_description: Section title in the image settings section within the share panel: This title labels the configurable options for an image being shared.
settings.width-label_description: Label in the share image settings panel: This label identifies the setting for the width dimension of the image.
settings.width-min: Width must be equal or greater than 1
settings.width-min_description: Validation message in the Image settings within the Share Panel: Specifies the minimum allowed value for the image's width. This message appears when a user enters a width value less than 1.
settings.width-required_description: Validation message in 'Share Panel Image' settings: Appears when the user attempts to save or share an image without specifying a mandatory width value. It prompts the user to enter the required width.

share-playlist.checkbox-description: Panel heights will be adjusted to fit screen size
share-playlist.checkbox-description_description: Description text for a checkbox in the "Share Playlist" dialog. It explains that enabling the associated checkbox will automatically adjust the height of dashboard panels in the shared playlist to fit the recipient's screen size.
share-playlist.checkbox-label_description: Checkbox label in the Share Playlist dialog: Controls whether the dashboard panels or content within the shared playlist automatically adjust their size to fit the viewer's display area.
share-playlist.copy-link-button_description: Button in the Share Playlist dialog: Used to copy the generated share link for a playlist.
share-playlist.link-url-label_description: Label in the Share Playlist dialog: This label identifies the URL provided for sharing the playlist, typically for copying.
share-playlist.mode_description: Label in the Share Playlist dialog: Allows users to select the viewing or playback mode for the shared playlist.
share-playlist.mode-kiosk_description: Option in Share Playlist settings: Select this to enable a full-screen, display-only Kiosk mode for the dashboard playlist, typically used for public displays.
share-playlist.mode-normal_description: Mode Option in Share Playlist settings: Denotes the standard or default viewing mode for a shared playlist. This option presents the playlist without specialized display enhancements or restrictions.
share-playlist.title: Share playlist
share-playlist.title_description: Title in the Share Playlist dialog: This title introduces the functionality for sharing a playlist.

theme.dark-label_description: Option label in Theme preferences: Identifies the option to set the application's visual theme to dark mode.
theme.light-label_description: Label in Theme Preferences: Used to identify and select the "Light" visual theme for the dashboard. This option sets the overall application appearance to a brighter color scheme.
theme.system-label_description: Option label in Theme preferences screen: This setting allows the dashboard theme to automatically match the operating system's light or dark mode preference.

delete-modal.revoke-body-text: Are you sure you want to revoke this access? The dashboard can no longer be shared.
delete-modal.revoke-body-text_description: Confirmation message body text in the 'Revoke Shared Access' modal: It prompts the user to confirm revoking access for a shared dashboard and informs them that the dashboard will no longer be shared.
delete-modal.revoke-title_description: Modal Title in a delete modal for shared dashboards: It indicates that the user is about to revoke someone's access to a shared dashboard.

fields.timezone-label_description: Label in shared dashboard settings: Indicates the timezone setting for the dashboard.

button.config-button-tooltip_description: Tooltip on a button in the Shared Dashboard List: This tooltip appears on hover, informing the user that the associated button is used to configure a shared dashboard.
button.revoke-button-text_description: Button in the Shared Dashboard List: Used to revoke a user's or group's access permissions to a shared dashboard.
button.revoke-button-tooltip_description: Tooltip in a button within the shared dashboard list: Explains the action of revoking a user's access to a shared dashboard.
button.view-button-tooltip_description: Tooltip for a button in the Shared Dashboard List: Informs the user that clicking the button will display a shared dashboard.

empty-state.message: You haven't created any shared dashboards yet
empty-state.message_description: Message in the Shared Dashboards List empty state: Informs the user that no shared dashboards have been created by them yet.
empty-state.more-info: Create a shared dashboard from any existing dashboard through the <1>Share</1> modal. <4>Learn more</4>

toggle.pause-sharing-toggle-text_description: Text label for a toggle in the shared dashboard list: This label indicates the action to temporarily pause access to a shared dashboard, implying that access can be resumed later.

fields.home-dashboard-label_description: Label in Shared Preferences fields: Identifies the main or default dashboard for the user or system. This label is used to refer to the primary dashboard view.
fields.home-dashboard-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Shared Preferences > Fields section: This text is used within a selection component for the home dashboard, representing the system's default option or indicating that the default is currently active.
fields.language-preference-label_description: Label in the Shared Preferences section: Identifies the setting where users can select their preferred language for the application UI.
fields.language-preference-placeholder_descript: Placeholder text in a language selection field within the Shared Preferences section: Prompts the user to choose their preferred display language.
fields.locale-preference-description: Choose your region to see the corresponding date, time, and number format
fields.locale-preference-description_descriptio: Description text in the Shared Preferences > Fields section: Informs the user that selecting a region will determine the display format for dates, times, and numbers throughout the dashboard. This text typically accompanies a locale selection control.
fields.locale-preference-label_description: Label in Shared preferences, within the fields section: This preference allows users to specify the display format for region-specific data (e.g., dates, numbers, currency), which is distinct from the interface language.
fields.locale-preference-placeholder_descriptio: Placeholder text for a region selection field in the Shared Preferences or Settings section. It prompts the user to select their preferred geographical region or locale.
fields.theme-description: Enjoying the experimental themes? Tell us what you'd like to see <2>here.</2>
fields.theme-description_description: Descriptive Text in the 'Shared Preferences' section, within the theme settings: This text encourages users to provide feedback on experimental themes. It contains a clickable link to facilitate submitting their suggestions.
fields.theme-label_description: Label in the Shared Preferences section: This label identifies the setting that allows users to choose the visual theme for the user interface.
fields.week-start-label_description: Label in the Shared Preferences 'Fields' section: This label identifies the setting for determining which day the week starts on. Users interact with an associated control to select the desired start day for the week.

shared-preferences.save_description: Button in Shared Preferences section: Allows the user to save their current preferences or settings.

theme.default-label_description: Option in the theme selection settings within Shared Preferences: This label represents the standard theme option. It allows users to revert to or select the system's default theme.
theme.experimental_description: Label in Shared Preferences > Theme settings: This indicates a setting, option, or section related to experimental themes or features that are under development.

shared-preferences.title: Preferences
shared-preferences.title_description: Title in the Shared Preferences section: Labels the area where users can view and modify their personal settings and preferences.

sign-up.back-button_description: Button in the Sign-up page: Allows users to navigate back to the login page.
sign-up.confirm-password-label_description: Label in Sign-up form: Prompts the user to re-enter their chosen password for verification. This ensures accuracy and prevents typos during account creation.
sign-up.org-name-label_description: Label in the Sign-up form: Prompts the user to enter the name of their organization. It is an abbreviation for 'Organization name'.
sign-up.submit-button_description: Button in the Sign-up form: Submits the user's registration information to complete the sign-up process.
sign-up.user-name-label_description: Label in the sign-up form: Prompts the user to enter their name during the account creation process.
sign-up.user-name-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the User Name input field on the sign-up form: It indicates that providing a user name is optional.
sign-up.verification-code-label: Email verification code (sent to your email)
sign-up.verification-code-label_description: Label in the sign-up form: Prompts the user to enter the email verification code. This code has been sent to their email address to complete the account verification process.

verify.back-button_description: Button in the Sign-up verification screen: Allows the user to navigate back to the login page instead of completing the verification process.
verify.complete-button_description: Button in the sign-up verification step: Finalizes the user's account creation after a successful verification process. This typically follows an email or phone number confirmation.
verify.email-description: Enter your email address to get a verification link sent to you
verify.email-description_description: Description in the sign-up verification screen: Guides the user to enter their email address to receive a verification link. This text prompts the user for the necessary action to proceed with verification.
verify.header_description: Header in the email verification screen during the sign-up process: This title informs the user that they need to verify their email address to complete their registration.
verify.info: An email with a verification link has been sent to the email address. You should receive it shortly.
verify.send-button_description: Button in the verification screen within the sign-up flow: Allows the user to send a verification email to confirm their account or email address.

empty-state.button-title_description: Button in the empty state for the Silences feature: Allows users to create their first silence. This button is displayed when no silences are currently configured.
empty-state.title: You haven't created any silences yet
empty-state.title_description: Title in the Silences screen's empty state: Informs the user that no silences have been created yet. This message prompts the user to create their first silence.

table.add-silence-button_description: Button in the table on the Silences page: Allows users to create a new silence. This initiates the process of temporarily suppressing alerts for specific conditions or durations.
table.expired-silences: Expired silences are automatically deleted after 5 days.
table.expired-silences_description: Informational message in the silences table: Informs users that expired silences are automatically deleted after 5 days. This clarifies the data retention policy for silences.
table.no-matching-silences_description: Message in Silences table: Informs the user that no silences match the current search or filter criteria.
table.noConfig: Create a new contact point to create a configuration using the default values or contact your administrator to set up the Alertmanager.
table.recreate-button_description: Button in the Silences table: This button allows users to create a new silence entry using the parameters of an existing silence.
table.unsilence-button_description: Button in the table displaying silences: This button allows users to end an active silence, causing previously suppressed alerts to be triggered again.

header.alert-name_description: Column header in the Silences table: Displays the name of the alert that is being silenced.
header.state_description: Column header in the header of the 'Silences' table: Indicates the current status or condition of a silence entry in the table.

empty-state.message: You haven't created any snapshots yet
empty-state.message_description: Message in the empty state of the Snapshots section: Informs the user that they have not created any snapshots yet.
empty-state.more-info: You can create a snapshot of any dashboard through the <1>Share</1> modal. <4>Learn more</4>
empty-state.more-info_description: Instructional text in the Snapshots empty state: This message provides guidance to users on how to create their first dashboard snapshot via the Share modal, and includes a "Learn more" link for additional information.

snapshot.external-badge_description: Badge in Snapshot view: Labels a snapshot as external, typically indicating it is publicly accessible or shared outside the immediate system.

share.copy-link-button_description: Button in the snapshot share dialog: Copies the sharable link for the current snapshot to the user's clipboard.
share.delete-button_description: Button in the snapshot sharing interface: Allows users to delete the selected snapshot.
share.delete-description: Are you sure you want to delete this snapshot?
share.delete-description_description: Confirmation message in the Snapshot sharing management interface: Asks the user to confirm their intent to delete a selected snapshot. This message appears before the irreversible deletion action is performed.
share.delete-permission-tooltip: You don't have permission to delete snapshots
share.delete-permission-tooltip_description: Tooltip message in the snapshot sharing or management interface: Informs the user that they lack the necessary permissions to delete snapshots. This tooltip likely appears when hovering over a disabled delete option.
share.delete-title_description: Confirmation dialog title in the snapshot sharing interface: Confirms the user's intent to delete a snapshot.
share.deleted-alert: Snapshot deleted. It could take an hour to be cleared from CDN caches.
share.expiration-label_description: Label in the Share Snapshot dialog: Precedes an input field or dropdown where the user sets the expiration duration for a shared snapshot. This string introduces the expiration setting.
share.info-alert: A dashboard snapshot publicly shares a dashboard while removing sensitive data such as queries and panel links, leaving only visible metrics and series names. Anyone with the link can access the snapshot.
share.local-button_description: Button in the snapshot sharing interface: Publishes the current snapshot, making it viewable or accessible to others.
share.name-label_description: Label in the Snapshot sharing dialog: Prompts the user to enter a name for the snapshot they are sharing. This name will be used to identify the specific dashboard view or state being shared.
share.new-snapshot-button_description: Button in the Snapshot Share section: Allows users to initiate the creation of a new snapshot.
share.success-creation_description: Success message in the Snapshot sharing feature: Confirms to the user that a snapshot they initiated has been successfully created.
share.success-delete_description: Success message in the Snapshot Sharing management interface: Confirms to the user that a snapshot has been successfully deleted.
share.view-all-button_description: Button in the Snapshot Sharing interface: Allows users to navigate to a page that lists or displays all available snapshots.

share-panel.info-alert: A panel snapshot publicly shares a panel while removing sensitive data such as queries and panel links, leaving only visible metrics and series names. Anyone with the link can access the snapshot.

snapshot.url-column-header_description: Column header in a table or list displaying snapshot details: Identifies the column that shows the URL for a particular snapshot.
snapshot.view-button_description: Button in the Snapshot section: Used to open or display the content or details of a specific snapshot.

sort-picker.select-aria-label_description: ARIA label for a dropdown or selection control in a sort picker: It describes the action of sorting for accessibility purposes.

sql-expressions.add-query-tooltip: Add at least one data query to generate SQL suggestions
sql-expressions.add-query-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the SQL expressions editor: Explains why SQL suggestions are not currently available and guides the user to add a data query first.
sql-expressions.ai-explain-title_description: Title in the SQL expressions feature: This heading introduces a section or functionality that provides AI-powered explanations for SQL expressions.
sql-expressions.ai-suggestions-title_description: Title in the SQL expression editor: Labels the section or feature offering AI-powered suggestions to assist users with SQL expressions.
sql-expressions.apply_description: Button in the SQL Expression editor/configuration: Used to apply or save the configured SQL expressions.
sql-expressions.code-label_description: Label in SQL Expressions editor: Identifies the query language currently being used for the code expression. The actual language name (e.g., SQL, PromQL) will be dynamically displayed.
sql-expressions.copy_description: Button in the SQL Expressions editor: Allows users to copy the currently displayed or selected SQL expression. This is typically used to duplicate an expression for reuse.
sql-expressions.explain-empty-query-tooltip: Enter a SQL expression to get an explanation
sql-expressions.explain-empty-query-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the SQL expression explanation feature: It prompts the user to enter a SQL expression to receive an explanation. This message is displayed when the expression input field is empty.
sql-expressions.explain-query_description: Button in a SQL Query Editor or Panel: Triggers the display of the execution plan for the current SQL query. This feature is used for performance analysis and optimization.
sql-expressions.explanation-modal-title_description: Modal Title in the SQL Expressions feature: This labels a pop-up window that provides an explanation or details for an SQL query.
sql-expressions.sql-ai-interaction_description: AI interaction text in the SQL expressions editor: This displays dynamic content, such as AI-generated SQL suggestions or explanations, to assist users in crafting or understanding SQL expressions.
sql-expressions.sql-suggestion-history_description: Section title in the SQL expression editor or a related panel: This string labels a section that displays a history of SQL suggestions. It allows users to review or reuse previous suggestions.
sql-expressions.suggestions_description: Label in the SQL expression editor: Denotes a section that provides autocompletion suggestions or recommended SQL elements. This assists users in constructing SQL queries.
sql-expressions.view-explanation_description: Button in the SQL expressions view: Displays a detailed explanation. This explanation likely clarifies the syntax, behavior, or results associated with SQL expressions.

stars.mark-as-starred_description: Menu item in a dashboard or asset list: Provides an action to mark the selected item as a favorite. The `{{title}}` placeholder will be dynamically replaced by the item's name.
stars.unmark-as-starred_description: Menu item or button in the 'Stars' or 'Favorites' section: Allows the user to remove the specified item (e.g., dashboard or report) from their list of favorite content.

add-orientation-option.description-orientation_description: A label in the Stat panel's configuration for adding orientation options: It identifies the setting where users can choose the layout's arrangement direction (e.g., horizontal or vertical).
add-orientation-option.name-orientation_description: Label in the 'Add Orientation Option' settings: This string is the name for the configuration option that controls the display orientation of a statistical component within a dashboard or report.

orientation-options.label-auto_description: Option label in the orientation settings for a Stat panel: Allows the user to select an automatic orientation for the visualization.
orientation-options.label-horizontal_description: Label in the orientation options for a statistic display: Identifies the choice to set the display layout as horizontal. This option determines the arrangement of elements within the statistic visualization.
orientation-options.label-vertical_description: Label in the orientation options for a stat panel: This option allows users to select a vertical layout or display direction for the stat.

add-standard-data-reduce-options.category-value-options_description: Group Label in the 'Add Standard Data Reduce Options' section of a Statistics configuration: It introduces a set of configurable parameters that define how data values are processed or displayed when data is reduced.
add-standard-data-reduce-options.description-calculation_description: Instruction text in the "Add Standard Data Reduce Options" section: Prompts the user to select a function that aggregates or calculates data values. This guides the user in configuring how data points are combined or transformed.
add-standard-data-reduce-options.description-fields: Select the fields that should be included in the panel
add-standard-data-reduce-options.description-fields_description: Instruction text in the 'Add Standard Data Reduce Options' section of a Stat Panel: Guides the user to select specific data fields to be displayed or used in the panel's visualization.
add-standard-data-reduce-options.description-limit: Max number of rows to display
add-standard-data-reduce-options.description-limit_description: Label in the data reduction options for a Stat panel: Specifies the maximum number of data rows that will be rendered or displayed.
add-standard-data-reduce-options.description-show: Calculate a single value per column or series or show each row
add-standard-data-reduce-options.description-show_description: Description text in the data reduction/transformation options of a statistics panel or data visualization: Explains whether to aggregate data into a single value per column/series or to display each individual row. This clarifies how the data will be processed and presented.

fields-options.label-all-fields_description: Option label in the data reduction options for fields within the Statistics feature. This label designates the selection to include all available data fields for data processing or analysis.
fields-options.label-numeric-fields_description: Label in the "Fields Options" section within the "Add Standard Data Reduce Options" configuration: This text identifies a category or selection for data fields that are numeric. It guides the user to choose or configure numeric fields for data reduction operations.

add-standard-data-reduce-options.name-calculation_description: Label in Data Reduction Options: This identifies the setting for defining a calculation method for data aggregation within a statistics panel.
add-standard-data-reduce-options.name-fields_description: Label in the 'Add Standard Data Reduce Options' section for statistics configuration: Identifies the area where users select or define the data fields relevant for data reduction operations.
add-standard-data-reduce-options.name-limit_description: Control Label in the data reduction configuration for statistics: Specifies a maximum limit for the data displayed or processed. This often refers to the number of items.
add-standard-data-reduce-options.name-show_description: Checkbox label in the 'Add Standard Data Reduce Options' section of a statistics panel's configuration: Controls whether a name or label associated with a data reduction option is displayed.

show-options.label-all-values_description: Option label in the 'Show options' section under 'Add Standard Data Reduce Options' for statistics: It represents the choice to include or display all data values, without applying any reduction, filtering, or aggregation.
show-options.label-calculate_description: Button label in the standard data reduction options: Initiates the calculation or application of data reduction based on the selected options.

stat.category-stat-styles_description: Section title in the Stat widget configuration panel: Groups options for customizing the visual appearance and styling of the Stat widget.

color-mode-options.label-background-gradient_description: Label in Color Mode Options for a Stat panel: Identifies an option to apply a gradient effect to the background of the statistic. This allows users to choose a gradient style for visual presentation.
color-mode-options.label-background-solid_description: Label in the Color Mode Options section of the Stat visualization settings: Identifies the option to use a solid color for the background. This choice contrasts with other potential background coloring modes.
color-mode-options.label-none_description: Option in the color mode settings for a Stat visualization: Represents the choice to apply no specific color mode.
color-mode-options.label-value_description: Label for an option in the 'Color Mode Options' within a Stat panel: This indicates that the color of the displayed statistic should be determined by its actual numerical value.

stat.description-graph-mode_description: Display mode setting in Stat panel: Allows users to choose between graph and sparkline visualizations for displaying data trends within the Stat panel.
stat.description-text-mode: Control if name and value is displayed or just name
stat.description-text-mode_description: Display option in Stat panel configuration: This setting controls whether the name and value of a statistic are both shown, or if only the name is displayed within the panel.

options.label-area_description: Label in graph mode options: Refers to the 'Area' display style for a graph, which fills the space beneath the data line. This option allows users to visualize data as an area chart.
options.label-none_description: Option in the graph mode display settings for a statistic panel: Represents the choice to display no label for a data point or element.

name-color-modcolor-mode-options.label: Color mode
name-color-modcolor-mode-options.label_description: Label in a Stat panel's color options: Defines how colors are applied to or interpreted for the displayed statistical value.

stat.name-graph-mode_description: **Label/Option** in the **display or configuration settings for a statistical graph**: This term indicates the current visual mode of the graph or allows the user to select a different display type (e.g., line, bar, area).
stat.name-show-percent-change_description: Checkbox or toggle in a Stat panel's display settings: Enables or disables the visibility of the percentage change for the primary statistical value displayed.
stat.name-text-alignment_description: Control in the Stat panel's text display settings: Configures the horizontal alignment for the stat's name or label displayed on the panel.
stat.name-text-mode_description: Option label in a Stat panel's display settings: It identifies a display mode where the statistical data is rendered as plain text. This is an alternative to graphical or other visual representations.
stat.name-wide-layout_description: Option label in Stat panel display settings: This allows users to configure the layout of the stat visualization to a wider format.
stat.percent-change-color-mode_description: Setting in Stat visualization configuration: This option defines the color mode used for displaying percentage changes. It allows users to control how percentage change values are colored within the stat panel.

percent-change-color-mode-options.label-inverted_description: Label for an option within the percentage change color mode settings of a Stat panel: This option allows users to reverse the standard color scheme used to indicate positive and negative percentage changes.
percent-change-color-mode-options.label-same-as-value_description: Label in Stat panel's Percent Change Color Mode Options: This option allows users to set the color of the percentage change value to be identical to the color of the main statistic displayed.
percent-change-color-mode-options.label-standard_description: Option label in the configuration settings for a Stat panel's 'Percentage Change Color Mode': This option allows users to select a standard, default color scheme for displaying percentage changes.

text-alignment-options.label-auto_description: Label for an option in Stat panel text alignment settings: Indicates that the text alignment for the Stat panel should be automatically determined by the system.
text-alignment-options.label-center_description: Option in Text Alignment Options for a Stat panel: This option allows users to center the text, such as a label or value, displayed within the panel.

text-mode-options.label-auto_description: Option text in Text Mode Options within the Stat configuration: This text labels an option to enable automatic behavior for the stat's text display, such such as automatic sizing or formatting.
text-mode-options.label-none_description: Selection option in the 'Text mode options' for a statistic panel: This choice allows users to specify that no label should be applied or displayed.
text-mode-options.label-value_description: Label in the text mode options for a stat panel: Refers to the primary data value displayed by the stat. This element is used to configure or display the actual data value.
text-mode-options.label-value-and-name_description: Label for a display option in the Text mode options section of a Stat panel. This option allows users to configure the display to show both the numerical value and its corresponding name.

wide-layout-options.label-off_description: Option label in the wide layout settings for a stat panel: Represents the 'off' state for a configurable display element, such as a label.
wide-layout-options.label-on_description: Label for the 'On' state of a toggle or switch in the Wide Layout Options section of a statistics panel or dashboard widget: It indicates that a specific wide layout feature is enabled.

align-values-options.label-center_description: Option label in the align values options section of a state timeline visualization: Allows users to horizontally center the values displayed within the state timeline.
align-values-options.label-left_description: Option label in the alignment options for a state timeline panel: This text represents the 'Left' alignment choice, allowing users to align data values or labels within the timeline display.
align-values-options.label-right_description: Option label in the 'Align values' options for a State Timeline visualization: Used to select right alignment for the displayed values.

state-timeline.category-state-timeline_description: Panel type label in BMC Helix Dashboards' panel creation interface: This label identifies a visualization designed to display the historical state or status of an entity over a period. It allows users to track changes in status chronologically.
state-timeline.name-align-values_description: Option in the State Timeline panel: Controls the alignment of the displayed values or labels within the timeline visualization.
state-timeline.name-connect-null-values_description: Option in the settings for a State Timeline panel: This checkbox or toggle controls whether null data points are visually connected. When enabled, it draws lines across gaps in the timeline where no state value is reported.
state-timeline.name-disconnect-values_description: Option in the State Timeline panel: Allows users to separate or unlink values displayed within the timeline visualization.
state-timeline.name-fill-opacity_description: Label in State timeline visualization settings: Allows users to adjust the transparency of the filled regions displayed in the state timeline.
state-timeline.name-line-width_description: A setting in the State Timeline panel configuration: Controls the visual thickness of lines displayed in the state timeline visualization.
state-timeline.name-merge-equal-consecutive-values_desc: Option label in the State Timeline visualization configuration: This setting allows users to merge consecutive data points that have the same value. This simplifies the visual representation of state changes over time.
state-timeline.name-page-size_description: Setting label in the State Timeline panel: Defines the maximum number of items to display on a single page. This setting is used to configure or enable pagination for the timeline data.
state-timeline.name-row-height_description: Setting in the State Timeline visualization: This allows users to adjust the vertical height of individual data rows within the timeline. This impacts the display density and readability of the visualization.
state-timeline.name-show-values_description: Toggle switch in State timeline visualization settings: Controls the visibility of data values displayed directly on the timeline.

show-values-options.label-always_description: Selection option in the 'Show Values' options for a State Timeline: Configures the timeline to always display values on the visualization.
show-values-options.label-auto_description: Option in the State Timeline panel's 'Show Values' settings: Configures the automatic labeling of states or values on the timeline. Selecting this allows the system to optimally determine and display the labels.
show-values-options.label-never_description: Option label in the 'Show Values' settings for the State Timeline visualization: This option, when selected, ensures that no values or markers are displayed on the timeline.

status-history.category-status-history_description: Label in the Status History section: Identifies a category or section dedicated to displaying past status changes or events.
status-history.name-column-width_description: Label for a setting in the Status History configuration: Used to define the visual width of a column within the status history display.
status-history.name-fill-opacity_description: Label in Status History visualization settings: For a control that adjusts the transparency of filled visual elements. This setting determines how opaque or translucent the colored regions representing status appear in the graph.
status-history.name-line-width_description: Control in the Status History visualization settings: Allows the user to adjust the thickness of the lines displayed on a status history chart.
status-history.name-page-size_description: Setting in the Status History section: This control allows users to define the number of items displayed per page and enable pagination for the status history data.
status-history.name-row-height_description: A label in the Status History configuration: Refers to the setting that allows users to adjust the height of rows displayed in the status history.
status-history.name-show-values_description: Checkbox in the Status History view: Controls the visibility of specific data values or labels within the status history display, such as on a chart or table.

show-values-options.label-always_description: Option label in the "Show Values Options" for a Status History visualization: Configures a display setting to always show specific values or details. This option determines that values will consistently be visible, rather than conditionally.
show-values-options.label-auto_description: Option in the 'Show Values Options' section of Status History: This setting enables the automatic determination of how data values are displayed or aggregated. It allows the system to apply default or smart settings for value representation.
show-values-options.label-never_description: Option label in the 'Show values' options for Status History: This choice indicates that associated data or values should never be displayed.

status-history-panel.too-many-points_one: Too many points to visualize properly. <1></1>Update the query to return fewer points. <3></3>({{count}} points received)
status-history-panel.too-many-points_other: Too many points to visualize properly. <1></1>Update the query to return fewer points. <3></3>({{count}} points received)

new-bundle-button.new-support-bundle_description: Button label in the Support Bundles section: Used to initiate the creation of a new support bundle.

support-bundles-create-unconnected.sub-title: Choose the components for the support bundle. The support bundle will be available for 3 days after creation.

text.create-support-bundle_description: Button label in the Support Bundles creation page for unconnected systems: Initiates the process of generating a diagnostic support bundle. This action is likely used when the system requiring the bundle is not actively connected or managed.

support-bundles-unconnected.created-on_description: Column header in a table listing unconnected support bundles: Displays the date when each support bundle was created.
support-bundles-unconnected.download_description: Button in the 'Unconnected Support Bundles' section: Allows users to download a support bundle that is not directly associated with a connected system or agent.
support-bundles-unconnected.expires_description: Label in the 'Unconnected Support Bundles' section: Indicates the expiration date or time for a support bundle. This tells the user when the bundle will no longer be valid or accessible.
support-bundles-unconnected.requested-by_description: Label in the 'Unconnected Support Bundles' section: This label identifies the user or system that initiated the request for a specific support bundle.
support-bundles-unconnected.sub-title: Support bundles allow you to easily collect and share Grafana logs, configuration, and data with the Grafana Labs team.
support-bundles-unconnected.tooltip-remove-bundle_description: Tooltip in the Unconnected Support Bundles section: Indicates the action to remove a specific support bundle. This tooltip appears when hovering over an icon or button that initiates the removal process for an unconnected support bundle.

swagger.login_description: Button in the Swagger UI: Enables users to log in and authenticate themselves to interact with and authorize API requests.

bar-gauge-cell-options-editor.label-gauge-display-mode_description: Label in the Bar Gauge Cell Options Editor: This label introduces a setting for configuring how a bar gauge is visually displayed within a table cell.
bar-gauge-cell-options-editor.label-value-display_description: An option in the bar gauge cell options editor within a table: This setting controls how the numerical value associated with the bar gauge is displayed (e.g., shown or hidden) within the cell.

table.category-cell-options_description: Label or menu item in the table configuration interface: Used to access or define settings that customize the appearance, data display, or behavior of individual cells or groups of cells, particularly those within specific categories.
table.category-table_description: Label for a table category or panel type: This string identifies the 'Table' panel type in dashboard creation or a general category for table-related features and settings.
table.category-table-footer_description: Footer in a table: Labels or represents the footer section at the bottom of a table. This area often contains summary information, pagination, or actions related to the table content.

cell-height-options.label-large_description: Option label in table cell height options: Indicates a large size setting for table cell height. This is one of the choices a user can select to adjust row height.
cell-height-options.label-medium_description: Option label in the Cell Height options for a table: This label represents a 'Medium' height setting for cells within a table, usually one of several predefined choices (e.g., Small, Medium, Large).
cell-height-options.label-small_description: Option label in the table's cell height settings: This allows the user to select 'Small' as the cell height.

cell-types.actions_description: Label in a table column or cell: Identifies an area where users can perform operations (e.g., edit, delete, view details) on the corresponding row's data.
cell-types.auto_description: Option in table cell type settings: Allows the system to automatically determine the rendering or formatting of a table cell's content.
cell-types.color-background_description: Setting label in the cell type configuration for a table: This option allows users to enable or configure a colored background for table cells.
cell-types.color-text_description: Cell type option in table column cell type configuration: This option allows users to display data within a table cell as text formatted with custom colors.
cell-types.data-links_description: This setting in the table cell type configuration: Allows users to define interactive links that can be associated with data displayed in table cells. These links typically enable navigation to other dashboards or external URLs when clicked.
cell-types.gauge_description: Gauge as a cell type in a table: Represents a visual component for displaying a single metric or data point within a table cell.
cell-types.image_description: The "Image" cell type option in table cell type configuration: This option allows users to configure a table cell to display an image.
cell-types.json_description: Label in the Table cell types configuration: This option allows users to display the content of a table cell in a raw JSON format.
cell-types.markdown_description: Cell type option in table settings: Specifies that content within a table cell will be rendered using a combination of Markdown and HTML formatting.
cell-types.pill_description: A cell type in a table cell: Refers to a distinct visual element, typically a rounded label or tag, used to display short data, status, or categorization.
cell-types.sparkline_description: Option for a table cell type: Refers to a miniature graph displayed within a table cell to visualize data trends.

color-background-cell-options-editor.description-apply-to-entire-row: If selected the entire row will be colored as this cell would be.
color-background-cell-options-editor.description-apply-to-entire-row_descript: Help text for an option in the cell background color options editor for a table: It explains that if the associated option is selected, the background color meant for a single cell will be applied to the entire row.
color-background-cell-options-editor.label-apply-to-entire-row_description: Label in the 'Color background cell options editor' for a table visualization: This option allows users to specify that a configured cell background color should be applied to the entire row of the table.
color-background-cell-options-editor.label-background-display-mode_descriptio: Option label in the cell background color options editor within a table visualization: Defines how the background color of table cells is presented or rendered. This setting allows users to choose the visual mode for cell backgrounds.

column-alignment-options.label-auto_description: Label for an alignment option in a table's column settings: This option allows the system to automatically determine the alignment for the column's content.
column-alignment-options.label-center_description: Label in column alignment options for a table: This option allows users to center the text or content within a specific table column.
column-alignment-options.label-left_description: A label in the column alignment options for a table: Indicates that the content within a table column should be aligned to the left.
column-alignment-options.label-right_description: Option in column alignment settings for a table: This option allows users to align the content of a table column to the right.

container.content: Showing too many columns in a single table may impact performance and make data harder to read. Consider refining your queries.
container.show-all-series_description: A button or toggle in a table container: This control reveals all available columns within the table, typically when some are hidden by default. The key path includes "series", but the string and its translations specifically refer to "columns".
container.show-only-series_description: Status message in a table container: Informs the user that the displayed table is currently showing a limited number of columns. This message appears when the table is configured or constrained to display only a subset of all available columns.

table.description-calculation_description: Instructional text / label for a selection control in the table configuration interface: Prompts the user to select an aggregation or calculation function to be applied to the table's data.
table.description-cell-value-inspect: Enable cell value inspection in a modal window
table.description-cell-value-inspect_descripti: Setting description in Table visualization options: This text describes the functionality that allows users to view the full value of a table cell in a dedicated modal window.
table.description-column-filter_description: Description in a table: This text describes the functionality that allows users to enable or disable filters for individual fields (columns) within the table.
table.description-frozen-columns: Columns are frozen from the left side of the table
table.description-frozen-columns_description: Description in Table UI: Informs the user about the behavior of frozen columns. It specifies that columns are frozen from the left side of the table.
table.description-min-column-width: The minimum width for column auto resizing
table.description-min-column-width_description: Description in Table settings: Explains the minimum width a table column will maintain when its auto-resizing feature is enabled. This prevents columns from becoming excessively narrow.
table.description-styling-from-field: A field containing JSON objects with CSS properties
table.description-styling-from-field_descripti: Input field in table styling options: This field specifies a data source column whose content, consisting of JSON objects with CSS properties, will be used to dynamically style elements within the table.
table.description-tooltip-from-field: Render a cell from a field (hidden or visible) in a tooltip
table.description-tooltip-from-field_descripti: Option description in Table Panel Settings: Explains the functionality to render data from a specific table field, regardless of its visibility, within a tooltip.

image-cell-options-editor.description-alt-text: Alternative text that will be displayed if an image can't be displayed or for users who use a screen reader
image-cell-options-editor.description-title-text: Text that will be displayed when the image is hovered by a cursor
image-cell-options-editor.description-title-text_description: Label for an input field in the Image Cell Options Editor: Guides the user to enter the text that will be displayed as a tooltip when a cursor hovers over an image within a table cell.
image-cell-options-editor.label-alt-text_description: Label in the image cell options editor for a table: Identifies the input field where users can provide alternative text for an image. This text enhances accessibility by describing the image for screen readers or when the image fails to load.
image-cell-options-editor.label-title-text_description: Label in the Image Cell Options Editor within a table: This label identifies the input field where users can define the title text to be displayed for an image cell.

link-wrapper.menu_description: Menu option in a table's link wrapper menu: This option allows users to view and interact with data links and associated actions for the table's content.

markdown-cell-options-editor.description-dynamic-height: We recommend enabling pagination with this option to avoid performance issues.
markdown-cell-options-editor.description-dynamic-height_description: Description text in the Markdown Cell Options Editor for a Table panel: It recommends enabling pagination when using the dynamic height option to avoid performance issues.

label.text-alpha_description: Label in the Markdown Cell Options editor within a table: This label identifies a setting to adjust the transparency (alpha channel) of text.

markdown-cell-options-editor.label-dynamic-height_description: Label in the Markdown cell options editor for a table: This option allows users to configure if the Markdown cell's height should automatically adjust based on its content. It ensures the cell's height is dynamic rather than fixed.

table.name-calculation_description: Field label in Table configuration: This label indicates the input field where a user can specify the name for a custom calculation applied to the table.
table.name-cell-height_description: Setting in table visualization configuration: Allows users to define the height of cells displayed in a table. This option controls the vertical spacing of rows in the table.
table.name-cell-type_description: Label in table configuration: Specifies the display format or data type for individual cells within a table.
table.name-cell-value-inspect_description: Action in a table's cell context menu: Allows users to view detailed information or metadata associated with the value in a selected table cell.
table.name-column-alignment_description: A setting in the table column properties: Configures the horizontal alignment of the content displayed within that specific column.
table.name-column-filter_description: Filter control in a data table: Allows users to filter the displayed data based on the values within a specific column.
table.name-column-width_description: Setting label in table visualization settings: This label identifies the control or input field used to configure the display width of a column within a table.
table.name-enable-pagination_description: Toggle switch in table settings: Controls whether the table displays its data across multiple pages. This feature helps improve performance and readability for large datasets.
table.name-frozen-columns_description: Setting in Table visualization configuration: This option allows users to specify which columns in a table should remain fixed and visible when horizontally scrolling. It enhances readability for wide tables.
table.name-hide-in-table_description: Option in table configuration: This control allows users to hide the display name of an element, such as a column header or field label, within a table visualization.
table.name-max-height_description: Setting label in table configuration: Specifies the maximum height for rows in the table. This helps manage table layout and readability.
table.name-min-column-width_description: Setting in Table panel configuration: Defines the minimum allowed width for columns within the table. This ensures columns do not become too narrow, maintaining readability.
table.name-show-table-header_description: Control in table display settings: Used to toggle the visibility of the header row in a table visualization.
table.name-styling-from-field_description: Setting in Table panel configuration: This option allows users to define how the visual styling of table elements (like cells or rows) should be dynamically determined by the values of a specific data field. It enables conditional formatting based on data.
table.name-tooltip-from-field_description: Configuration Option in Table Panel Settings: This allows users to define a data field whose value will be used to generate tooltips for elements within the table visualization.
table.name-tooltip-placement_description: Option in table configuration: Specifies the visual position of a tooltip displayed for an element's name within the table. This allows users to control where the tooltip appears relative to the hovered item.
table.name-wrap-header-text_description: Setting in Table configuration: This option controls whether the text within the table headers should wrap to multiple lines when it's too long to fit on a single line.
table.name-wrap-text_description: Toggle in table visualization settings: Controls whether text within table cells or headers wraps onto multiple lines. This setting helps improve readability for long text entries in a table.
table.placeholder-column-width_description: Placeholder value for column width in table settings: It signifies the automatic adjustment of a column's width, often used as a default or suggested behavior.
table.placeholder-max-height_description: Table content maximum height setting in Table visualization: This option controls the maximum vertical space available for the table's content. When set to 'none', the table will expand vertically as needed without a height constraint.

tooltip-placement-options.label-auto_description: Option label in table tooltip placement settings: This value allows the system to automatically determine the optimal display position for tooltips associated with a table.
tooltip-placement-options.label-bottom_description: Option in Tooltip placement settings for a table: This option allows users to specify that tooltips should be displayed below the element they are associated with.
tooltip-placement-options.label-left_description: Label in table tooltip placement options: This label represents the choice to position a tooltip to the left of its associated element within a table.
tooltip-placement-options.label-right_description: Label in table tooltip placement options: Indicates the option to position a tooltip to the right of its target. This is one of the choices for setting tooltip direction.
tooltip-placement-options.label-top_description: Option label in table tooltip placement options: Specifies the 'Top' position for a tooltip. This setting determines that the tooltip will appear above its associated data point in a table.

tag-filter.clear-button_description: Button in the Tag Filter component: Used to clear all selected or applied tags.
tag-filter.no-tags_description: Status message in the tag filter component: Informs the user that no tags were found or are available for selection/display.
tag-filter.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a tag filter input field: It prompts the user to enter a tag name to filter the displayed list or results.
tag-filter.select-aria-label_description: ARIA label for a tag selection control in a dashboard's filtering options: Provides an accessible name for the control used to select tags for filtering content.

team-picker.noOptionsMessage-no-teams-found_descript: Message in the team picker component: Informs the user that no teams were found, typically after a search or when the list of available teams is empty.
team-picker.select-aria-label_description: Aria label for a selection control in the Team Picker component: This label provides an accessible name for the interactive element used to select one or more teams.
team-picker.select-placeholder_description: Placeholder text for a team selection field in the team picker component: Guides the user to select a team.

subTitle.manage-members-and-settings_description: Navigation Link/Subtitle in the Teams section's navigation menu: Directs users to the dedicated page or interface for managing the members and settings of a team.

text.settings_description: Menu item in the Teams navigation menu: This allows users to access and configure settings for the Teams feature.

text.external-group-sync_description: Label in Team Management settings: This refers to the feature or process of synchronizing groups from an external directory or identity provider with teams in BMC Helix Dashboards.

text.members_description: Navigation label in Teams management interface: Identifies the section or view dedicated to managing or listing the individuals belonging to a specific team.

create-team.description-email: This is optional and is primarily used for allowing custom team avatars
create-team.description-email_description: Helper text in the Create Team page/modal: Describes an optional input field, likely for an email address, whose primary purpose is to enable custom team avatars.
create-team.label-role_description: Label in the 'Create Team' screen: Indicates the input field or selection where the user defines the role for the new team being created.

empty-state.button-title_description: Button in the Teams empty state: This button allows users to create a new team when no teams have been defined yet.
empty-state.message_description: Message in the empty state of the Teams management section: Indicates to the user that no teams are currently registered or found within the system. This message appears when the list of teams is empty.
empty-state.pro-tip: Assign folder and dashboard permissions to teams instead of users to ease administration. <2>Learn more</2>
empty-state.title: You haven't created any teams yet
empty-state.title_description: Title in the Teams empty state: Informs the user that they haven't created any teams yet, prompting them to create one.

team-group-sync.add-group_description: Button in the Team Group Sync section of Teams settings: Used to add a new group for synchronization with BMC Helix Dashboards teams. This typically refers to external groups (e.g., from LDAP or Active Directory) that can be integrated.
team-group-sync.add-group-button_description: Button in the 'Team Group Sync' section within the Teams management area: Allows users to add a new group to be synchronized or managed. This button likely triggers a form or dialog to configure the new group.
team-group-sync.aria-label-remove_description: aria-label` for a button or icon in the Team Group Synchronization section within Teams: It describes the action of removing a specific group from the synchronization configuration. This label is used for accessibility purposes.
team-group-sync.external-group-id_description: Field label or column header in the Team Group Synchronization settings: This element identifies a group originating from an external system, used for mapping or synchronizing with teams in BMC Helix Dashboards.
team-group-sync.external-group-sync_description: Label for a setting or feature in the Team Group Sync section: This element identifies the functionality for synchronizing user groups from external identity providers (e.g., LDAP, SAML) with internal teams.
team-group-sync.label-add-external-group_description: Button in the Team Group Synchronization settings within the Teams management area: Initiates the process of adding and synchronizing a group from an external identity provider to BMC Helix Dashboards.
team-group-sync.team-sync-upgrade: Add a group to enable team sync for free during your trial of Grafana Pro
team-group-sync.team-sync-upgrade_description: Prompt in the Team Group Sync configuration area: This message encourages users to add a group to enable team synchronization, highlighting that this feature is free during their Grafana Pro trial.

text.add-group_description: Button or link in the Team Group Sync management interface: Used to add a group to be included in the synchronization process or associated with a team.

team-group-sync.title-there-external-groups: There are no external groups to sync with
team-group-sync.title-there-external-groups_description: Informational message in the Team Group Sync section: Informs the user that no external groups are currently configured or available to be synchronized with BMC Helix Dashboards teams. This indicates that there's nothing for the user to select or manage in this particular context.
team-group-sync.tooltip-add-external-group_description: Tooltip in the 'Team Group Sync' section: This tooltip provides an example format for an LDAP group, guiding users when adding external groups. The `{{example}}` placeholder will be dynamically populated with a specific example (e.g., "cn=groupname,ou=groups,dc=example,dc=com").

columns.aria-label-delete-button_description: ARIA label for a button in the team list column: Describes the action of deleting a specific team. The `{{teamName}}` placeholder dynamically indicates which team will be deleted, enhancing accessibility for users.
columns.aria-label-edit-team_description: ARIA label for an Edit button or icon in a column of a team list on the Teams page. It provides an accessible name for screen readers, indicating the button's action to edit the specified `{{teamName}}`.
columns.title-edit-team_description: Column title in the 'Team List' table: Labels the column that provides actions or links to edit existing teams.
columns.tooltip-edit-team_description: Tooltip in a column within the 'Team List' table: Indicates the function of an 'Edit' icon or button to modify a team.

team-list.new-team_description: Button label in the Team List view: Initiates the process of creating a new team.
team-list.placeholder-search-teams_description: Placeholder text for a search input field in the Teams list page: Guides users to enter text to filter or find teams within the list.

team-settings.description-email: This is optional and is primarily used to set the team profile avatar (via the Gravatar service)
team-settings.description-email_description: Description for the Email field in Team Settings page: Informs users that this optional email address is primarily used to set the team profile avatar through the Gravatar service.
team-settings.label-numerical-identifier_description: Label in Team Settings: This labels a field or value that displays a unique numerical identifier associated with a team or a specific team setting.
team-settings.label-role_description: Label in the Team Settings screen: Designates the function or permissions assigned to a team member.
team-settings.label-team-details_description: Label in Team settings page: This label introduces a section or form where users can view and edit information specific to a team.
team-settings.save_description: Button in Team Settings: Saves the configured details for a team.

team-sync-upgrade-content.description: Team Sync makes it easier for you to manage users' access in Grafana, by immediately updating each user's Grafana teams and permissions based on their single sign-on group membership, instead of when users sign in

text.category-text_description: Category label in a dashboard element selection menu: Identifies the category for options or components related to displaying or inputting text.

mode-options.label-code_description: Label in Mode Options: Identifies an option to switch to or display a 'Code' view or mode. This allows users to view or edit underlying code, such as queries, scripts, or data definitions.
mode-options.label-markdown_description: Option label in the mode options section: It identifies the choice to enable or use Markdown for text formatting.

text.name-content_description: Label for a section or field in the UI: Refers to the actual information, data, or media displayed or stored within that element. This distinguishes the substantive data from its metadata or properties.
text.name-language_description: Label in UI: Identifies the language setting or a selection option for the application's interface.
text.name-mode_description: Label for a field, dropdown, or column header in a form, table, or settings panel: Specifies or indicates a particular operational or display mode for an element, component, or system.
text.name-show-line-numbers_description: Option in a text or code display component: Toggles the visibility of line numbers next to each line of text or code. This helps users navigate and reference specific lines.
text.name-show-mini-map_description: Checkbox or menu item in the display options: This UI element controls the visibility of a mini map. The mini map offers an overview for navigating large dashboards or visualizations.

theme-playground.label-base-theme_description: Label in the Theme Playground: Identifies the foundational or default theme option that can be selected or configured.
theme-playground.title: Theme playground
theme-playground.title_description: Page title in the Theme Playground: Identifies the section where users can experiment with different UI themes.

breadcrumbs.dashboards_description: Link in breadcrumb navigation within the theme preview: Identifies and provides navigation to the Dashboards section or page.
breadcrumbs.home_description: Breadcrumb link in the theme preview's breadcrumbs navigation: This link allows users to navigate back to the main dashboard or application home page.

panel.form-label_description: Setting label in a panel within the theme preview: This label identifies a configuration option for customizing the appearance or behavior of form labels in the theme preview.
panel.title: Panel
panel.title_description: Title of a panel in the theme preview section: It labels a panel component that is being displayed or configured within the theme preview interface.

time-period.1_day_description: Selection option in a time period selector: Allows users to specify a 1-day duration for the dashboard's data display or analysis.
time-period.1_hour_description: Time duration option in a Time Period selector: Allows users to quickly set the data displayed on a dashboard to cover the last 1 hour.
time-period.12_hours_description: Time period option in a time range selection component: Allows users to select a predefined duration of the last 12 hours for data display.
time-period.30_days_description: Time period option in a time range selector: Represents a 30-day duration. It allows users to view or filter data over the last 30 days.
time-period.6_hours_description: Time Range Option in Time Period Selector: Allows users to select a 6-hour duration. This option is used to display or filter dashboard data for the last six hours.
time-period.7_days_description: A time duration option in a time period selection component: Allows users to select a 7-day relative time range for data visualization or filtering.

absolute.recent-title_description: Title in the Absolute time range selection section of the time picker: This title labels a list of recently used custom absolute time ranges, allowing users to quickly re-select them.
absolute.title: Absolute time range
absolute.title_description: Title in the absolute time range section of a time picker: It labels the input fields or selection for defining a specific, fixed start and end date/time. This option allows users to specify an exact time window, rather than a relative one.

calendar.apply-button_description: Button in the Calendar within the time picker: This button confirms and applies the selected date and time range to update the dashboard display.
calendar.close_description: Button in the calendar component of a time picker: Allows the user to close or dismiss the calendar view.
calendar.next-month_description: Button in the time picker calendar: Advances the calendar display to show the next month.
calendar.previous-month_description: Button in a calendar display within a time-picker: Allows the user to navigate to the previous month.
calendar.select-time_description: Label in the calendar component of the time picker: Prompts the user to define or select a specific time period. This is used to filter data or set the display interval.

content.empty-recent-list-docs: <0><0>Read the documentation</0><1> to find out more about how to enter custom time ranges.</1></0>
content.empty-recent-list-docs_description: Text message with a link in the time picker component: This message is displayed when the list of recent time range selections is empty, guiding users to documentation on how to enter custom time ranges.
content.empty-recent-list-info: It looks like you haven't used this time picker before. As soon as you enter some time intervals, recently used intervals will appear here.
content.filter-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a search input field within the time picker's content area: Prompts the user to search for predefined "quick" time range options. It guides users on what they can filter within the time picker.

copy-paste.aria-label-copy_description: ARIA label in the Time Picker's copy/paste options: Describes an interactive control that allows users to copy the currently selected time range to their clipboard.
copy-paste.aria-label-paste_description: ARIA label in the Time Picker's copy-paste feature: Describes the action to paste a previously copied time range.
copy-paste.copy-success-message_description: A success message displayed near the Time Picker: It confirms that the selected time range has been successfully copied to the user's clipboard. This message appears briefly after a user initiates the copy action.
copy-paste.default-error-message: {{error}} is not a valid time range
copy-paste.default-error-message_description: Error message in the copy-paste functionality of the time picker: Notifies the user that the value they attempted to paste is not a valid time range. The `{{error}}` placeholder will be replaced with specific details about the validation failure.
copy-paste.default-error-title_description: Error message title in the time picker component: This title is displayed when a user attempts to copy and paste a time range that is invalid or cannot be parsed. It serves as a generic error indication for unsuccessful time range inputs via copy-paste.
copy-paste.tooltip-copy_description: Tooltip on a 'copy' icon in the time picker's copy-paste section: Informs the user that clicking it will copy the displayed time range to the clipboard.
copy-paste.tooltip-paste_description: Tooltip on a paste option within the time picker component: Informs the user that this action will paste a previously copied time range.

footer.change-settings-button_description: Button in the footer of the time picker component: Allows users to modify settings related to time selection or display.
footer.fiscal-year-option_description: Option in the footer of the time picker: This option allows users to quickly select the current fiscal year as the time range for data display.
footer.fiscal-year-start_description: A label for a configuration option in the footer of the time picker: This allows users to specify the starting month for their fiscal year. This setting influences how data is aggregated or displayed according to an organization's fiscal calendar.
footer.time-zone-option_description: Label in the time picker footer: This label is for an option that allows users to select or view the time zone. It affects how time-related data is interpreted and displayed within the dashboard.
footer.time-zone-selection_description: Label in the time picker's footer: This text identifies the control or section used to select the time zone. It ensures that data timestamps are displayed according to the user's chosen time zone.

range-content.apply-button_description: Button in the time range selection area of the time picker: This button is used to confirm and apply the currently selected time range to the dashboard or data view.
range-content.default-error: Please enter a past date or "{{now}}"
range-content.default-error_description: Error message in the Time Range Content of a Time Picker: This message instructs the user to enter a valid past date or the `{{now}}` keyword if they have input an invalid or future date.
range-content.fiscal-year_description: Display text in the time picker's range content area: Shows the selected fiscal year range. The `{{from}}` and `{{to}}` placeholders represent the start and end dates of the fiscal year.
range-content.from-input_description: Label in the time range content of a time picker: Specifies the starting point for a custom time range. This label is typically associated with an input field where the user can enter or select the start date/time.
range-content.open-input-calendar_description: Button in the time picker's range content area: Opens a calendar interface for selecting specific dates to define a time range.
range-content.range-error_description: Error message in the time range selection area of a time picker: Informs the user that the specified 'From' date or time cannot be chronologically after the 'To' date or time, indicating an invalid range selection.
range-content.to-input_description: Label in the time range selector: Indicates the end date or time for a selected time range.

range-picker.backwards-time-aria-label_description: Interactive control in the time range picker within the time picker component: Shifts the currently displayed time range backwards in time, effectively moving to an earlier period. This label is used for accessibility purposes.
range-picker.current-time-selected_description: Text label in the time range picker component: Informs the user about the currently active time range that has been selected. This string includes a placeholder for the actual time range value.
range-picker.forwards-time-aria-label_description: ARIA label for a button in the time range picker: Describes the action of moving the currently selected time range forwards. This label is primarily for accessibility (screen readers).
range-picker.to_description: Label in the Time Range Picker: This word indicates the end point of a user-selected time range. It typically appears between the start and end date/time values or labels the end date/time input field.
range-picker.zoom-out-button_description: Button in the range picker component of a time picker: Allows users to expand the currently displayed time range to show a wider time period.
range-picker.zoom-out-tooltip_description: Tooltip in the Range Picker within the Time Picker: Informs users about the "zoom out" action for the currently selected time range. It also specifies the keyboard shortcut CTRL+Z for this action.

time-range.apply_description: Button within the time range selection of the time picker: Used to confirm and apply the currently selected time range to the dashboard or visualization.
time-range.aria-role_description: Accessibility label for the time range selector within the Time Picker component: This text describes the element's function, indicating that it allows users to select a specific time range.
time-range.default-title_description: Section title in the time range selection area within the time picker: Labels the collection of available time range options that users can choose from.
time-range.example-title_description: Title in the time range selection area: Introduces a list of predefined or example time range options that users can select from.
time-range.from-label_description: Label in the time range selector within the time picker: Identifies the field or control used to select the beginning of a custom time range.
time-range.from-to_description: Time range display in the time picker component: It shows the beginning and end of the currently selected custom time period. The placeholders represent the actual 'from' and 'to' time values.
time-range.specify_description: Option in the time range picker: This allows the user to define a custom start and end date/time for the dashboard data.
time-range.submit-button-label_description: Submit button in the time range selection area of the TimePicker component: Confirms and applies the selected time range.
time-range.to-label_description: Label in the time range selector within the time picker: Indicates the end point (e.g., date or time) of the selected time range.

zone.select-aria-label_description: Accessible label for a selector in the time zone section of the time picker: It identifies the interactive element that allows users to choose their preferred time zone.
zone.select-search-input_description: Search input field in the time zone selection within the time picker: Allows users to search for a time zone by typing a country, city, or abbreviation.

missing-field.all_description: Informational message in the Timeline component: Informs the user that no suitable data fields are available to generate a graph or timeline visualization. This message is displayed when the system cannot find any fields that can be plotted.
missing-field.time: Data does not have a time field
missing-field.time_description: Error Message in Timeline Visualization: Informs the user that the data selected for display on the timeline does not contain a necessary time field. This message appears when the data structure lacks a column that can be interpreted as a time dimension, which is essential for rendering the timeline.

annotation-editor2.add-annotation_description: Button in the timeseries annotation editor: Initiates the process of adding a new annotation to the timeseries display. This button likely opens a form or a dialog for entering annotation details.
annotation-editor2.edit-annotation_description: Action in the timeseries annotation editor: This enables users to modify the details of an existing annotation.
annotation-editor2.label-tags_description: Label in the Annotation Editor for Timeseries: This label marks the input field where users can add or view tags for an annotation. Tags are used to categorize and organize annotations.
annotation-editor2.placeholder-add-tags_description: Placeholder text in the annotation editor of a timeseries dashboard: It prompts the user to input tags for an annotation.
annotation-editor2.saving_description: Status message in the Timeseries Annotation Editor: Indicates that the changes made to an annotation are currently being saved. This text typically appears while the system processes the save request.
