timeseries.category-axis_description: Text in category axis settings within timeseries visualizations: This is the title for the section or control where users configure the properties of the category axis, often the X-axis representing time or distinct series.

get-graph-field-config.aria-label-bar-width-factor_description: Label for a configuration setting in the graph field configuration panel for a Time Series visualization: Defines a factor that controls the visual width of bars within the graph.
get-graph-field-config.aria-label-fill-opacity_description: Accessibility label for a UI control in the time series graph field configuration: This label describes a setting that allows users to adjust the transparency of filled regions in a graph visualization.
get-graph-field-config.aria-label-line-width_description: Accessibility label in the timeseries graph configuration settings: It describes a control used to adjust the visual thickness of lines within the graph.
get-graph-field-config.aria-label-point-size_description: Label for a setting in the Time series graph configuration panel: Describes an input or control that allows users to define the visual size of data points on the graph.
get-graph-field-config.category-graph-styles_description: Section title in the timeseries graph configuration panel: Groups settings for customizing the visual appearance and display styles of the graph.
get-graph-field-config.category-thresholds_description: Label in time series graph field configuration: Identifies the section for defining and managing thresholds. These thresholds are applied to specific data categories within the graph to trigger visual indicators.
get-graph-field-config.name-bar-alignment_description: Setting name in the time series graph configuration: Defines the horizontal alignment of bars in a time series visualization. This determines their positioning relative to time intervals.
get-graph-field-config.name-bar-width-factor_description: Configuration setting in the Graph Field configuration section of a Time Series panel: It allows users to adjust the relative width of bars displayed in the time series graph. This value acts as a multiplier.
get-graph-field-config.name-connect-nulls_description: Setting in Timeseries Graph Configuration: Controls how null data points are rendered in a time series graph, specifically whether the line should connect across these null values.
get-graph-field-config.name-disconnect-values_description: Checkbox in the graph field configuration settings for a Time Series panel: This option, when enabled, ensures that lines on the graph will break at null or missing data points, rather than connecting across them.
get-graph-field-config.name-fill-below-to_description: Label in the timeseries graph field configuration: This label introduces a setting for defining the threshold or condition up to which the area below a data series line should be filled.
get-graph-field-config.name-fill-opacity_description: Setting in time-series graph field configuration: Controls the transparency of filled areas or regions within a time-series graph visualization.
get-graph-field-config.name-gradient-mode_description: Option label in Timeseries graph field configuration: Configures whether a color gradient is applied to visualize data. This setting determines how visual intensity or range is displayed.
get-graph-field-config.name-line-interpolation_description: Label in Timeseries graph configuration: This setting defines the method for drawing lines between data points on a timeseries graph, influencing their visual style.
get-graph-field-config.name-line-style_description: Configuration option in the time series panel's graph field configuration: Defines the visual style of lines (e.g., solid, dashed) used to represent data fields in the graph.
get-graph-field-config.name-line-width_description: Setting in timeseries graph field configuration: This control allows users to define the visual thickness of a line displayed in a timeseries graph. It is part of the field-specific display options for timeseries visualizations.
get-graph-field-config.name-point-size_description: Setting in the time series graph field configuration: This option allows users to control the visual size of data points displayed on the graph for a specific field.
get-graph-field-config.name-show-points_description: Option label in the Time Series Graph Field Configuration: Controls whether individual data points are displayed on the time series graph.
get-graph-field-config.name-show-thresholds_description: Checkbox label in Time series graph configuration settings: This option allows users to display or hide predefined thresholds on the time series graph.
get-graph-field-config.name-show-values_description: Label in the Time Series graph field configuration: This option controls the visibility of data values on the graph for the selected field.
get-graph-field-config.name-style_description: Label in Time series graph field configuration: This label identifies the setting for configuring the visual appearance or presentation style of a specific data field within a time series graph. It allows users to customize how individual data series are displayed.
get-graph-field-config.name-transform_description: Option in Timeseries graph field configuration: Used to apply a transformation to the display name of a graph field. This customizes how field names appear on the dashboard.

transform-options.description-constant: The first value will be shown as a constant line
transform-options.description-constant_description: Description in "Transform Options" within a Timeseries graph's field configuration: This text explains that when a specific display option is chosen, the first value from the data series will be used to draw a constant horizontal line on the graph.
transform-options.description-style: Flip the results to negative values on the y axis
transform-options.description-style_description: Description text for a Y-axis transformation style option in the Time Series graph configuration: It informs the user that enabling this setting will invert the sign of the displayed values on the Y-axis.
transform-options.label-constant_description: Option label in Timeseries graph transform options: Identifies a configuration setting for applying a constant value or displaying a constant line on the graph.
transform-options.label-style_description: Configuration option label in Transform Options within the Timeseries graph field configuration: This option controls the display of the Y-axis, allowing it to represent negative values or be inverted.

disconnect-options.label-never_description: Option in the Disconnect Options of the Timeseries Nulls Editor: Specifies that lines or data points in a timeseries visualization should never be disconnected, even when null values are present.
disconnect-options.label-threshold_description: Label in the 'Disconnect options' section of the 'Insert nulls editor': It identifies a setting where users define a threshold value. This threshold determines when timeseries lines should be disconnected or broken, often based on gaps or null data.

line-fill-options.label-dash_description: Option label in the Timeseries Line Style Editor: This option allows users to select a dashed line style for data visualization. It renders lines as a series of short, separated segments.
line-fill-options.label-dots_description: Option label in the line fill options of the timeseries line style editor: This option allows users to display data points on the line chart as dots.
line-fill-options.label-solid_description: Option in Line Fill Options within the Line Style Editor for Time Series visualizations: Specifies a solid color fill for the area below a line in a time series chart.

line-style-editor.title-the-input-expects-a-segment-list: The input expects a segment list
line-style-editor.title-the-input-expects-a-segment-list_d: Helper text for an input field in the Line Style Editor within the Timeseries panel: This text informs the user that the input field requires data in the specific format of a segment list. It guides the user on the expected pattern for defining line styles.
line-style-editor.tooltip-help_description: Tooltip text in the Line Style Editor for Timeseries panels: This text appears as the content of a tooltip, indicating that the associated UI element provides help or further information.

timeseries.name-time-zone_description: Field label in Time series configuration settings: Identifies the control or input field used to specify the time zone for a time series visualization or its data.

nulls-threshold-input.placeholder-never_description: Placeholder text in an input field for a nulls threshold in time series settings: It indicates that no specific threshold for null values will be applied if the field is left empty.

outside-range-plugin.data-outside-time-range_description: Informational text in a time series visualization: Informs the user that some data points exist but are not displayed because they fall outside the currently selected time range. This message, typically part of an 'outside range' plugin, clarifies why data might appear missing from the graph.
outside-range-plugin.zoom-to-data_description: Button in the `outside-range-plugin` for a time series chart: Allows users to automatically adjust the displayed time range to encompass all available data points. This is typically used when data falls outside the currently visible time window.

gaps-options.label-always_description: Option label in the Gaps Options section of the Time Series Nulls Editor: This label represents the choice to consistently apply a configured behavior for gaps or null values in time series data. It ensures the specified handling is always active.
gaps-options.label-never_description: Option label in the Gaps options section of the Span Nulls Editor: It allows users to configure that a specific behavior for handling gaps in time series data should never be applied.
gaps-options.label-threshold_description: Label in the Gaps Options section of the Span Nulls Editor for a Time Series panel: This label identifies a setting where a user can define a threshold value. This threshold likely controls how null or missing data points are processed or displayed as gaps in the time series.

timezones-editor.tooltip-add-timezone_description: Button in the Timezones editor within the Timeseries section: Allows the user to add a new timezone to the displayed list or configuration.
timezones-editor.tooltip-remove-timezone_description: Tooltip in the Timezones Editor within the Timeseries feature: Appears when hovering over an element that removes a selected timezone from the list or configuration.

traces.category-span-filters_description: Filter controls in the Traces analysis view: These are used to refine and narrow down the list of individual spans displayed within a trace, helping users focus on specific operations or characteristics.
traces.name-critical-path-only_description: Checkbox in the Traces view: Controls whether only the critical path of a trace is displayed. This option helps users focus on performance-critical operations.
traces.name-find-in-trace_description: Button or menu item in the Trace detail view: Allows users to search for specific text or attributes within the currently displayed trace.
traces.name-max-duration_description: Label/Input field in the Traces section: Specifies the maximum allowed or displayed duration for a trace. This is typically used as a filter criterion or configuration setting.
traces.name-min-duration_description: Label or input field in the Traces view or filtering options: Allows users to set or indicates the minimum duration for traces. This is typically used for filtering traces.
traces.name-service-name_description: Label in the traces section: Used to identify or input the name of a service associated with a trace.
traces.name-service-name-operator_description: Dropdown label in the Traces filtering interface: Used to select the comparison operator (e.g., 'equals', 'contains') for filtering traces by service name. This operator defines how service names are matched.
traces.name-show-matches-only_description: Checkbox label in the traces filtering interface for trace names: When enabled, it restricts the displayed traces to only those whose names match the current search or filter criteria.
traces.name-span-name_description: Label or column header in the Traces view: Identifies the name of a specific span, which represents a single operation or unit of work within a distributed trace. This string is used to label fields, columns, or display values related to span names.
traces.name-span-name-operator_description: Label in the Traces feature: This field label identifies a control for selecting an operator to filter or search by span name. It determines how span names are matched in trace queries.
traces.name-tags_description: Label in the Traces view: Identifies metadata or attributes associated with trace data, used for categorization, filtering, or search.

traces-panel.no-data-found-in-response_description: Informational message in Traces panel: Informs the user that a query or operation for traces did not return any data, indicating an empty result set from the backend.

empty.add-transformation-body:
Transformations allow data to be changed in various ways before your visualization is shown.
This includes joining data together, renaming fields, making calculations, formatting data for display, and more.
empty.add-transformation-header_description: Header in the empty state of the Transformations panel: It encourages users to begin adding transformations to their data.

description.split-the-buckets-based-on-size: Split the buckets based on size
description.split-the-buckets-based-on-size_descript: Description text in the Axis Editor's Log Mode Options: This explains a setting for how data buckets are divided or grouped based on their size when a log scale is applied to an axis.

label.split_description: Label in the log mode options for the axis editor: This option allows for dividing or segmenting the axis scale or its labels when a logarithmic mode is applied.

description.split-the-buckets-based-on-count: Split the buckets based on count
description.split-the-buckets-based-on-count_descrip: Description text in the 'Mode Options' section of the Axis Editor: Explains an option to configure how data buckets (e.g., in a histogram or grouped visualization) are divided or segmented, using the count of items within each bucket as the criterion.
description.split-the-buckets-based-on-size: Split the buckets based on size
description.split-the-buckets-based-on-size_descript: Option description in the Axis Editor's mode options for data transformers: This setting explains how to segment data into groups ('buckets') based on their quantitative size or value range, influencing data aggregation on a chart axis.

label.count_description: Option label in the Axis Editor's mode options for labels: This option allows users to configure axis labels to display the count of data.
label.size_description: Label in the axis label settings within the Axis Editor's mode options: Denotes the control for adjusting the size of an axis label.

axis-editor.placeholder-auto_description: Placeholder text in the Axis Editor: It indicates that an axis setting will be automatically determined by the system if the input field is left blank. This placeholder guides the user to the default "auto" behavior.

basic-matcher-editor.placeholder-value-or-variable_descriptio: Placeholder text in an input field within the Basic Matcher Editor: Guides the user to enter either a specific value or a variable name for a matching criterion.

binary-operation-options-editor.label-operation_description: Label in the binary operation options editor: Identifies the field where the user selects the specific binary operation to be applied.
binary-operation-options-editor.placeholder-field-or-number_description: Placeholder text in the Binary Operation Options Editor (within the transformers section): Guides the user to input either a data field name or a numerical value as an operand for a binary operation.
binary-operation-options-editor.placeholder-fields-or-number_description: Placeholder text in the Binary Operation Options Editor: This text guides the user to enter either one or more data fields or a numerical value as an input for a binary operation.

label.binary-operation_description: Label in the 'Calculation Modes' section of the Calculate Field Transformer editor: It identifies a calculation mode that performs an operation on two input values.
label.reduce-row_description: Label in the Calculation Modes section of the Calculate Field Transformer editor: It identifies an option for performing calculations that consolidate or aggregate data across multiple rows into a single output row.
label.row-index_description: Label in the Calculate Field transformer editor, within the calculation modes section: This label identifies a calculation method that utilizes the row number of the data.
label.unary-operation_description: Label in the 'Calculation Modes' section of the 'Calculate Field Transformer Editor': This label identifies a calculation mode that applies an operation to a single input value or field.

label.cumulative-functions_description: Label in the Calculate Field Transformer Editor: It identifies a category of functions that perform cumulative calculations. This grouping helps users select functions that aggregate data over a range or sequence.
label.window-functions_description: Label in the Calculate Field Transformer Editor: Identifies a category of advanced data transformation functions that operate over a defined "window" of rows. Users select these functions to calculate new fields within their dashboard data.

calculate-field-transformer-editor.label-alias_description: Label for an input field in the Calculate Field Transformer Editor: This allows users to provide a custom display name (alias) for a field being calculated or transformed, improving readability in the dashboard.
calculate-field-transformer-editor.label-mode_description: Label in the Calculate field transformer editor: Specifies the operational mode or method for calculating new fields. This setting determines how the transformer performs its operation.
calculate-field-transformer-editor.label-replace-all-fields_description: Label in the Calculate Field Transformer Editor: This option controls whether the transformer replaces all existing fields with the newly calculated value instead of adding a new field.

add-heatmap-calculation-options.name-x-bucket_description: Label for an input field in the "Add Heatmap Calculation Options" panel of the "Calculate Heatmap" transformer: This field allows users to define or name the bucket (grouping) for data along the X-axis of a heatmap calculation.
add-heatmap-calculation-options.name-y-bucket_description: Option label in the 'Add Heatmap Calculation Options' section of the 'Calculate Heatmap' transformer: This option defines the grouping or interval for data points along the Y-axis in the heatmap calculation.
add-heatmap-calculation-options.name-y-bucket-scale_description: Label in the 'Add Heatmap Calculation Options' section: This identifies the setting for configuring the scale used to group data into Y-axis buckets for a heatmap calculation.

concatenate-transformer-editor.label-label_description: A label for an input field in the concatenate transformer editor: Users provide a descriptive name for the concatenated output or an element within the transformation.

label.copy-frame-name-to-field: Copy frame name to field name
label.copy-frame-name-to-field_description: Option label in the Concatenate Transformer editor's name modes settings: This option specifies that the name of an input data frame or element should be used as the field name in the concatenated output.
label.ignore-the-frame-name_description: Label for a UI option in the 'Name Modes' settings of the concatenate transformer editor: This option determines if the original 'frame name' (e.g., a data series or element name) should be ignored during data concatenation. This allows users to control the naming convention of the resulting output data.
label.label-frame: Add a label with the frame name
label.label-frame_description: **Option label** in the **Concatenate Transformer Editor's 'Name Modes' settings**: This setting allows users to include the name of the source data frame as a label in the transformed output.

concatenate-transformer-editor.placeholder-frame_description: UI Label in the 'Concatenate Transformer Editor': It identifies an input or selection field where users specify a data frame to be used in the data concatenation process.

config-from-query-transformer-editor.label-apply-to_description: Label in the Config From Query Transformer Editor: This label introduces a selection mechanism where the user specifies to which part or element the transformer's configuration or action should be applied.
config-from-query-transformer-editor.label-apply-to-options_description: Label in the 'Config from Query Transformer Editor': This text identifies a setting or action related to applying the transformer's configuration or output to a specific set of options. These options likely pertain to how the transformed data is utilized.
config-from-query-transformer-editor.label-config-query_description: Label in the 'Config from Query Transformer Editor': This label identifies the input field where users enter the query used to configure the transformer.

convert-field-type-transformer-editor.aria-label-add-a-convert-field-type-tran: Add a convert field type transformer

Button in the Convert Field Type Transformer editor: Allows users to add a new transformer configuration that converts the data type of a field. This is an accessibility label for an interactive element, such as an 'Add' icon or button.
convert-field-type-transformer-editor.aria-label-remove-convert-field-type-tra: ARIA label for a button in the Convert Field Type Transformer editor: This button allows users to remove the configured 'Convert Field Type Transformer'.
convert-field-type-transformer-editor.convert-field-type_description: Transformer Name in the 'Convert field type' transformer editor: This string labels the data transformation used to change a field's data type.

description.convert-to-specified-field-type: Convert a field to a specified field type.
description.convert-to-specified-field-type_descript: Description in the Convert Field Type Transformer Editor: Describes the main action performed by this transformer. It indicates that the transformer converts a field to a user-specified data type.

label.browser_description: Label in the Convert Field Type Transformer editor: This label represents 'Browser' as a field type or an option for transforming data related to web browsers.

convert-field-type-transformer-editor.label-as_description: Keyword in a label in the Convert Field Type Transformer Editor: This string acts as a connector, specifying the target type for a field conversion. It typically appears in a phrase like "Convert [field] as [type]".
convert-field-type-transformer-editor.label-date-format_description: Label in the Convert Field Type Transformer Editor: This label prompts the user to specify the desired format for date values when transforming or configuring a field's type.
convert-field-type-transformer-editor.label-field_description: Label in the Convert Field Type Transformer Editor: This labels an input or selector used to specify a data field for type conversion.
convert-field-type-transformer-editor.label-input-format_description: Label in the 'Convert field type' transformer editor: This label prompts the user to define the existing format of the data in the field that is being converted. It helps the transformer understand the original data structure before applying a type conversion.
convert-field-type-transformer-editor.label-join-with_description: Label in the Convert Field Type Transformer editor: This label prompts the user to specify a character or string to use when joining or concatenating multiple values into a single field, typically as a delimiter.
convert-field-type-transformer-editor.label-set-timezone_description: Label in the Convert Field Type Transformer Editor: This label introduces a setting or option for specifying the timezone to be applied during a field type conversion. It is typically used for date/time field conversions.

name.convert-field-type_description: Label in the 'Convert Field Type Transformer' editor: Identifies the transformer or action that changes the data type of a field. This option allows users to modify how data fields are interpreted.

convert-field-type-transformer-editor.placeholder-type_description: Placeholder text in the Convert Field Type Transformer Editor: Prompts the user to input or select a data type for field conversion.

tags.format-field_description: Option label in the 'Convert Field Type' transformer editor's 'Tags' configuration section: This option allows users to define or apply display formatting for a data field.

convert-field-type-transformer-editor.tooltip-explicit-separator-joining-array: Use an explicit separator when joining array values

Tooltip in the Convert Field Type Transformer editor: Explains the function of an option that allows users to define a specific separator for joining array values. This provides control over how array elements are concatenated into a single string.
convert-field-type-transformer-editor.tooltip-input-format: Specify the format of the input field so Grafana can parse the date string correctly.
convert-field-type-transformer-editor.tooltip-input-format_description: Tooltip in Convert Field Type Transformer editor: This tooltip provides guidance on specifying the format of an input field, primarily for date strings, to ensure Grafana can parse them correctly.
convert-field-type-transformer-editor.tooltip-specify-the-output-format_descri: Tooltip in the Convert Field Type Transformer editor: Instructs the user to define the desired output format for the transformed field.
convert-field-type-transformer-editor.tooltip-timezone-manually: Set the timezone of the date manually
convert-field-type-transformer-editor.tooltip-timezone-manually_description: Tooltip in the Convert Field Type Transformer Editor: Explains that the user can manually set the timezone for a date field. This option allows overriding default timezone settings for the converted data.

cumulative-options-editor.label-calculation_description: Label in the Cumulative Options Editor: This text identifies a setting or section related to defining the calculation method for cumulative values.
cumulative-options-editor.label-field_description: Label in the Cumulative Options Editor: This identifies a selectable field or column for configuring cumulative data transformations.
cumulative-options-editor.placeholder-field_description: Placeholder text in the Cumulative Options Editor within the Transformers section: Prompts the user to enter or select a data field on which a cumulative transformation will be applied.

draggable-field-name.rename-placeholder_description: Label in the data transformation editor or field configuration interface: It guides the user to rename a specific draggable field by displaying its current name dynamically.
draggable-field-name.title-drag-and-drop-to-reorder_descripti: Tooltip title for a reorderable list of field names within a data transformer configuration: It instructs users to reorder the fields by dragging and dropping them.
draggable-field-name.tooltip-disable_description: Button in a tooltip associated with a draggable field name within the Transformers configuration: Disables the tooltip for that specific field.
draggable-field-name.tooltip-enable_description: Label for a toggle control or checkbox in the 'Transformers' settings for 'draggable field names': This enables the display of tooltips for these fields.

draggable-sort-order.off_description: Toggle state in Draggable Sort Order settings (under the Transformers section): Indicates that the draggable sort order functionality is disabled.

description.combine-all-fields: Combine all fields into a single frame.
description.combine-all-fields_description: Description text in the Concatenate Transformer Editor: This text describes the functionality of combining all available data fields into a single output frame when the concatenate transformer is applied.

name.concatenate-fields_description: Label in the Concatenate Transformer editor: This identifies the functionality or section used to combine values from multiple data fields into a single field.

tags.combine_description: Button in the Concatenate Transformer Editor, within the tags section: Allows users to combine tags or tag-related data. This action is performed as part of a data transformation process.

enum-mapping-editor.add-enum-value_description: Button in the Enum Mapping Editor: Allows users to add a new enumeration value to a list or mapping being configured within the data transformation process.
enum-mapping-editor.generate-enum-values-from-data_descripti: Action button in the Enum Mapping Editor: Allows users to automatically generate a list of enumeration values by extracting unique values directly from the provided data.

enum-mapping-row.click-to-edit_description: Instruction text in an enum mapping row within the data transformer configuration: Informs the user that clicking on the row or element will activate editing for its contents.
enum-mapping-row.remove-enum-row-aria-label-delete-enum-r: ARIA label in the Enum Mapping row of a transformer configuration: Allows users to delete a specific enum mapping row. This label describes the action for accessibility purposes.

description.parse-fields-from-content: Parse fields from content (JSON, labels, etc).
description.parse-fields-from-content_description: Description text in the Extract Fields Transformer editor: This text describes the transformer's capability to parse and extract structured fields from raw content, such as JSON or labels.

placeholderText.select-field_description: Placeholder text in a field name picker component within the 'Extract Fields Transformer' editor: It instructs the user to select a field.

extract-fields-transformer-editor.label-delimiter_description: Label in the Extract Fields Transformer Editor: Specifies the character or string used to separate fields when extracting data from a source.
extract-fields-transformer-editor.label-format_description: Label in the Extract Fields Transformer Editor: This label identifies a setting where the user can specify the desired output format for the extracted fields.
extract-fields-transformer-editor.label-keep-time_description: Checkbox label in the Extract Fields Transformer editor: This option allows users to specify whether to retain the original timestamp (time field) of the data being processed.
extract-fields-transformer-editor.label-replace-all-fields_description: Option label in the "Extract Fields" transformer editor: This setting determines if the fields extracted by the transformer should completely replace all existing fields in the data. When active, it discards old fields in favor of the new ones.
extract-fields-transformer-editor.label-source_description: Label in the Extract Fields Transformer editor: Identifies the origin or input data from which fields will be extracted.

name.extract-fields_description: **Transformer name** in the **Extract Fields transformer editor**: This label identifies a transformer used to extract specific data fields from incoming data.

extract-fields-transformer-editor.placeholder-auto_description: Placeholder text in the Extract Fields Transformer editor: This value indicates that an automatic setting or detection will be used if the user does not provide a specific input for a field.
extract-fields-transformer-editor.tooltip-regexp_description: Tooltip in the 'Extract fields transformer' editor: Provides an example to guide the user on the expected regular expression format for an input field. The `{{regexExample}}` placeholder will be dynamically replaced with a specific regex example.

field-config-mapping-handler-arguments-e.aria-label-threshold-color_description: Label in the Field Config Mapping Handler Arguments Editor within the Transformers section: Identifies the control used to select or display the color for a defined threshold. This ARIA label enhances accessibility for screen reader users.

description.lookup-additional-fields-external-source: Use a field value to lookup countries, states, or airports.

Description text in the Field Lookup Transformer editor: This text explains how users can leverage a field's value to search for and retrieve additional related data, such as countries, states, or airports, from an external data source.

noFieldsMessage.no-text-fields-found_description: Informational message in the field selection settings of the Field Lookup Transformer editor: Informs the user when no text fields are found in the selected data source or available for selection.

placeholderText.select-text-field_description: Placeholder text in the Field Lookup Transformer Editor's field name picker settings: It instructs the user to select a field that contains text data for a lookup operation.

field-lookup-transformer-editor.label-field_description: Label in the Field Lookup Transformer Editor: Identifies the input where the user specifies a data field to be used in the lookup transformation.
field-lookup-transformer-editor.label-lookup_description: Label in the Field Lookup Transformer Editor: Refers to the functionality for performing a data lookup. This allows users to find and retrieve values to transform fields.

name.lookup-fields-from-resource_description: **Label** in the **Field Lookup Transformer Editor**: This string describes an action or option to retrieve fields from a specified resource during a data transformation process. It enables enriching the dashboard data by integrating information from an external source.

field-to-config-mapping-editor.additional-settings_description: Section heading in the Field-to-Config Mapping Editor: This heading introduces a section containing advanced or secondary options for customizing how data fields are mapped to configuration parameters. Users can access these settings to fine-tune the mapping process beyond basic configurations.
field-to-config-mapping-editor.field_description: Label for a data field in the Field-to-Config Mapping Editor: Identifies a data field or column within the transformer's mapping interface that users select or configure for specific settings.
field-to-config-mapping-editor.select_description: Button in the Field-to-Config Mapping Editor: Used to initiate a selection process or to confirm a user's choice. It is part of defining how fields are mapped to configurations.
field-to-config-mapping-editor.use-as_description: Label in the field-to-config mapping editor: Prompts the user to select the specific role or purpose a data field will serve when being mapped to a configuration setting.

description.remove-part-query-results-regex-pattern: Remove parts of the query results using a regex pattern.
description.remove-part-query-results-regex-pattern_: Explanatory text in the 'Filter by Name Transformer' editor: It describes a feature allowing users to remove specific parts of query results using a regular expression pattern.

filter-by-name-transformer-editor.label-from-variable_description: Label in the Filter by Name Transformer Editor: This label indicates an option to derive the filter value from an existing variable. It distinguishes the source of the filter value.
filter-by-name-transformer-editor.label-identifier_description: Label in the Filter by Name Transformer Editor: This label designates a field where a user can specify an identifier to filter data.
filter-by-name-transformer-editor.label-use-variable_description: Label in the Filter by Name Transformer Editor: This option allows users to specify that the filter criterion should be based on a dynamic variable rather than a static value.
filter-by-name-transformer-editor.label-variable_description: Label in the "Filter by Name" Transformer Editor: This identifies a field where a variable can be selected or defined for use within the transformer's filtering logic.

name.filter-fields-by-name_description: Label in the 'Filter by Name' transformer editor: This indicates an option or input field for enabling or configuring the filtering of data fields based on their names.

filter-by-name-transformer-editor.placeholder-regular-expression-pattern_d: Placeholder text for an input field in the 'Filter by Name Transformer' editor: Guides the user to enter a regular expression pattern. This pattern will be used to filter items by their name.

description.filter-data-by-query-useful-sharing-resu: Remove rows from the data based on origin query
A description in the Filter by Ref ID Transformer Editor: It clarifies that this transformer removes data rows based on their originating query, which is useful for refining shared results.

name.filter-data-by-query_description: Label in the Filter by Ref ID Transformer Editor: This names a setting or option that allows users to filter the data displayed in a dashboard panel by applying a specified query. This is a data manipulation step within the transformer configuration.

filter-by-value-filter-editor.aria-label-remove-filter_description: Button/Icon in the filter by value editor: Removes a configured filter from the list. This label provides an accessible name for screen readers.
filter-by-value-filter-editor.label-field_description: Label in the Filter by Value transformer editor: It identifies the specific data field (column) that the user wants to apply the filter to.
filter-by-value-filter-editor.label-match_description: Label in the "Filter by Value" filter editor within the Transformers section: This label introduces the criteria or options for defining how values should be matched when applying the filter.
filter-by-value-filter-editor.label-value_description: Label in the filter-by-value filter editor in the Transformers section: Prompts the user to enter the specific value to be used as a filtering criterion. This typically refers to the data point or text value that the filter will match against.
filter-by-value-filter-editor.placeholder-field-name_description: Placeholder text in the Filter by Value transformation editor: It prompts the user to enter or select the name of the field to be filtered.
filter-by-value-filter-editor.placeholder-select-test_description: Placeholder text in the Filter by Value editor: It prompts the user to select a specific test from available options to apply the filter.

filter-by-value-transformer-editor.add-condition_description: Button in the 'Filter by Value' transformer editor: Allows users to add a new filtering condition to the data transformation. This is used to define more rules for filtering data.

description.remove-rows-query-results-user-defined-f: Remove rows from the query results using user-defined filters.
Description text in the Filter by Value Transformer editor: Explains the transformer's function of removing rows from query results. This is achieved by applying user-defined filters.

label.match-all_description: Option label in the Filter by Value transformer editor: Specifies that all defined filter conditions must be met for data to be included. This is one of the options for configuring how filter criteria are applied.
label.match-any_description: Label in the Filter by Value Transformer editor, within the 'Filter Match' section. This option allows users to configure the filter to include data that satisfies at least one of the defined conditions.

label.exclude_description: Label for a filter type option in the Filter by Value Transformer Editor: This option enables users to define a filter that excludes data records matching the specified criteria.
label.include_description: Label for a filter type option in the 'Filter by Value' transformer editor: It signifies a filtering behavior where only data matching the selected values will be included in the results.

filter-by-value-transformer-editor.label-conditions_description: Label in the Filter by Value Transformer editor: Introduces the section where users define the criteria or rules for filtering data based on specific values.
filter-by-value-transformer-editor.label-filter-type_description: Label in the Filter by Value Transformer editor: Prompts the user to select the specific method or category for filtering data within the transformer configuration.

name.filter-data-by-values_description: Title in the Filter by Value Transformer Editor: Identifies the option or feature that allows users to filter data based on specific values.

noFieldsMessage.no-text-fields-found_description: Status message in the field name picker within the 'Format string' transformer editor settings: Informs the user that no text fields are available for selection. This indicates that the current data context lacks suitable text-type fields for string formatting.

placeholderText.select-text-field_description: Placeholder text in the Field Name Picker settings of the Format String Transformer Editor: Prompts the user to select a text field. This field will be used as input for the string formatting transformer.

format-string-transfomer-editor.label-field_description: Label in the Format String Transformer Editor: Identifies the input field where the user selects or defines the data field to be transformed or used in string formatting.
format-string-transfomer-editor.label-format_description: Label in Format String Transformer editor: This label identifies the field where users define or select the desired string format.
format-string-transfomer-editor.label-substring-range_description: Label in the Format String Transformer editor: This label identifies a field where users define the start and end positions for extracting a specific part of a string. It specifies the character range for substring operations.

description.manipulate-string-fields-formatting_desc: Description in the Format String Transformer editor: Explains the primary function of this transformer, which is to modify the presentation of string fields.

name.format-string_description: Setting or Input Field Label in the Format String Transformer Editor: This identifies where users define a format string. The format string specifies the desired pattern for data presentation.

format-time-transfomer-editor.label-format_description: Label in the Format Time Transformer editor: It identifies the field or setting where the user specifies how time data should be formatted.
format-time-transfomer-editor.label-set-timezone_description: Label in the Format Time Transformer editor: This label indicates an option or field where users can specify the timezone for the time data being transformed.
format-time-transfomer-editor.label-time-field_description: Label in the Format Time Transformer editor: It identifies the specific data field that contains time information for transformation or formatting.
format-time-transfomer-editor.tooltip-format: The output format for the field specified as a <2>Moment.js format string</2> .
format-time-transfomer-editor.tooltip-timezone-manually: Set the timezone of the date manually
format-time-transfomer-editor.tooltip-timezone-manually_description: Tooltip in the format time transformer editor: Guides the user to set the timezone for a date manually. This tooltip likely appears next to an input field or a selection control for timezone settings.

description.set-based-on-time: Set the output format of a time field
description.set-based-on-time_description: Description text in the Format Time Transformer Editor: Describes the action of setting the output format for a time field.

name.format-time_description: Name in the Format Time Transformer Editor: This string serves as the title or label for the time formatting transformer. It identifies the function of transforming time values for display.

description.values-calculate-field: Use the row values to calculate a new field.
description.values-calculate-field_description: Description text in the 'Calculate Field' data transform: Explains that this transform uses existing row values to generate a new field.

name.add-field-from-calculation_description: Button label in the Calculate Field transformation settings: Allows users to add a new data field whose values are computed based on a user-defined calculation.

description.set-unit-min-max-and-more: Set unit, min, max and more.
description.set-unit-min-max-and-more_description: Description in the configuration panel of a data transformer in BMC Helix Dashboards: This text indicates that the user can set display characteristics for data, such as its unit, minimum value, maximum value, and other related formatting options.

name.config-from-query-results_description: Option Name in Data Transformer configuration: Specifies that the transformer's configuration should be derived directly from the results of a data query.

description.generate-heatmap-data-from-source: Generate heatmap data from source data.
description.generate-heatmap-data-from-source_descri: Description text in the Heatmap Transformer configuration: It explains the transformer's function to generate heatmap data directly from the available source data.

name.create-heatmap_description: Button or menu item in the Heatmap Transformer configuration: Allows the user to initiate the creation of a new heatmap visualization or data transformation.

description.flatten-labeled-results-table-joined-lab: Flatten labeled results into a table joined by labels.

Description text in the 'Get Join by Labels Transformer' configuration interface: This string describes the functionality of the transformer. It explains that the transformer takes labeled results and flattens them into a single table by joining data based on its labels.

name.join-by-labels_description: Name in Data Transformer Selection: This identifies a specific data transformer that merges data series or results based on common labels. It is presented as an option for users to select this transformation.

description.split-oneframe-dataset-multiple-series: Split a one-frame dataset into multiple series.
description.split-oneframe-dataset-multiple-series_d: Description string in the 'Partition by values' transformer configuration: This explains the transformer's core functionality, which is to separate a single data frame into multiple individual series.

name.partition-by-values_description: Label in the Data Transformation section: Identifies a transformer that divides data into groups based on unique field values.

description.convert-field-dynamic-config: Convert each row into a field with dynamic config.
description.convert-field-dynamic-config_description: Description in the Get Rows to Fields Transformer: This describes a feature that converts each row of input data into a field, allowing its properties to be dynamically configured.

name.rows-to-fields_description: Name of a data transformer in the transformation configuration options: This transformer allows users to pivot data by converting rows into distinct fields or columns.

description.apply-spatial-operations-to-query-result: Apply spatial operations to query results.
Description text for a Spatial Transformer: It clarifies that this transformer allows users to apply spatial operations to data query results as part of a data transformation pipeline.

name.spatial-operations_description: Label in the Spatial Transformer configuration: It identifies a section or option that allows users to perform functions on spatial data.

label.browser_description: Selection option label in the timezone settings: Represents the option to use the timezone detected by the user's web browser for data display or transformation.

get-tooltips.description: A valid path of an json object.
get-tooltips.description_description: Description in the 'Get Tooltips' transformer configuration: It clarifies the type of input expected for a setting, specifically a valid path to a value within a JSON object. This path is used to extract the relevant data for displaying tooltips.
get-tooltips.json-value_description: Label in the 'Get Tooltips' section of Transformers: This identifies a field where a JSON value is specified or displayed, likely as part of configuring the content for a tooltip.

description.extract-fields-from-object_description: Description of an operation in the data transformation configuration for mapping valid paths to generate tooltips: It specifies the action of extracting fields from an object. The "=>" prefix indicates it is a step or option in a sequence of data manipulation.
description.extract-value_description: Description text in the Tooltip Transformer configuration for mapping valid paths: Explains the action of extracting a specific value. This text likely appears as a rule or step in a transformation definition.
description.extract-value-first-element_description: Option description in a tooltip data transformer configuration: Describes the action of extracting the first element from a specified data value. This is typically used to process array-like or multi-valued fields for display in a tooltip.
description.extract-value-second-element_description: Transformation rule description in the tooltip data mapping configuration: This string indicates that the second element should be extracted from the specified value. It's used to define how a data field is processed for display in a tooltip.

get-tooltips.valid-paths_description: Label in the 'Get Tooltips' configuration section of a data transformer: It introduces a field or section for defining or displaying data paths that are considered valid for generating tooltips or for the data transformation process.

title.root_description: Title Label in the Transformer Option Pane: This string identifies the top-level or base element of a hierarchical structure, such as a data path or configuration group, within the transformer settings.

label.calculate_description: An option label in the Group By Field Configuration options: This allows users to perform or enable a calculation on the grouped data.
label.group-by_description: Label in Group by field configuration options: This string labels the control or section where users define how data should be grouped. It typically precedes options for selecting fields to group by.

group-by-field-configuration.placeholder-ignored_description: Option in the Group by field configuration: This option allows users to specify that a particular field should be ignored when performing a group-by operation.
group-by-field-configuration.placeholder-select-stats_description: Placeholder text in the Group by field configuration within data transformers: Prompts the user to select statistical functions to be applied to the grouped data.

description.group-series-by-field-calculate-stats: Group data by a field value and create aggregate data.
description.group-series-by-field-calculate-stats_de: Description in the Group By Transformer editor: This text describes the functionality of the transformer, which groups data by a specified field value to create aggregated results. It clarifies this data transformation capability within BMC Helix Dashboards.

name.group-by_description: Label in the Group by transformer editor: This string identifies the functionality or a field used to configure how data is grouped for display in a dashboard.

group-by-transformer-editor.title-calc-alert: Calculations will not have an effect if no fields are being grouped on
group-by-transformer-editor.title-calc-alert_description: Alert message in the Group By Transformer editor: Informs the user that calculations will not be effective unless fields are selected for grouping. This guides the user to configure data aggregation correctly.

description.group-by-field-value: Group data by a field value and create nested tables with the grouped data.
description.group-by-field-value_description: Description in the Group to Nested Table Transformer editor: Describes the primary function of this transformer, which is to group data based on a specified field's value to create nested tables. This string provides a high-level overview of the transformer's behavior.

group-to-nested-table-transformer-editor.description-show-field-names: If enabled nested tables will show field names as a table header
group-to-nested-table-transformer-editor.description-show-field-names_description: Description for a setting in the Group to Nested Table Transformer editor: This text explains that if the associated option is enabled, nested tables will display field names as their column headers. It clarifies the display behavior for nested table headers generated by this transformer.
group-to-nested-table-transformer-editor.label-show-field-names-in-nested-tables: Show field names in nested tables
group-to-nested-table-transformer-editor.label-show-field-names-in-nested-tables_: Label in the Group to Nested Table Transformer Editor: This setting controls whether the names of fields (columns) are displayed within the nested table structures generated by the transformer.

name.group-to-nested-tables_description: Title in the Group to Nested Table Transformer editor: It identifies a data transformation option. This transformer converts flat data structures into hierarchical nested tables.

group-to-nested-table-transformer-editor.title-calc-alert: Calculations will not have an effect if no fields are being grouped on.
group-to-nested-table-transformer-editor.title-calc-alert_description: **Warning text** in the **Group to Nested Table Transformer editor**: This message informs the user that calculations defined within this transformer require fields to be grouped to have any effect. It serves as an alert that calculations are dependent on proper grouping configuration.

description.summarize-and-reorganize-data: Summarize and reorganize data based on three fields.
description.summarize-and-reorganize-data_descriptio: Description in the Grouping to Matrix Transformer Editor: Explains the functionality of this data transformer. It summarizes and reorganizes data specifically using three chosen fields.

grouping-to-matrix-transformer-editor.label-cell-value_description: Label in the Grouping to Matrix Transformer editor: This label identifies the setting for defining the data that populates each cell of the resulting matrix. It specifies the value to be displayed at each row/column intersection after the data transformation.
grouping-to-matrix-transformer-editor.label-column_description: Label in the Grouping to Matrix Transformer Editor: Prompts the user to specify a column from the input data. This column is used as a key for grouping or as a dimension in the matrix transformation.
grouping-to-matrix-transformer-editor.label-empty-value_description: Label in the Grouping-to-Matrix Transformer Editor: This label represents a null or empty value. It is used to identify options or settings related to handling missing data during the data transformation process.
grouping-to-matrix-transformer-editor.label-row_description: Label for a selection field in the Grouping to Matrix Transformer editor. This field allows users to specify which data dimension will form the rows of the transformed matrix.

name.grouping-to-matrix_description: Label in the Grouping to Matrix Transformer Editor: Identifies a data transformer feature that converts grouped data into a matrix format.

description.calculate-histogram-from-input-data: Calculate a histogram from input data.
description.calculate-histogram-from-input-data_desc: Description in the Histogram Transformer Editor: This text describes the core functionality of the transformer. It indicates that the transformer is used to calculate a histogram based on the data it receives.

name.histogram_description: Name in Histogram Transformer Editor: This is the display name for the 'Histogram' data transformer. It identifies the type of data transformation available within the editor.

histogram-transformer-editor.placeholder-auto_description: Placeholder text in the Histogram Transformer Editor: Indicates that a setting, such as the number of bins or range for the histogram, will be automatically calculated or applied. It suggests the system's default behavior when no explicit value is entered.
histogram-transformer-editor.placeholder-default_description: Placeholder text within an input field in the Histogram Transformer Editor: It indicates the default value for a setting (e.g., number of bins or interval) that will be applied if the user does not enter a custom value.
histogram-transformer-editor.placeholder-none_description: Selectable option in the histogram transformer editor: This option signifies no selection, an empty state, or disabling a specific setting, possibly related to placeholder functionality or default values.

index-options-editor.label-as-percentile_description: Option label in the Index Options Editor of a transformer: This setting allows users to specify that a particular value or index should be calculated or displayed as a percentile.
index-options-editor.tooltip-transform-index-percentile: Transform the row index as a percentile.
index-options-editor.tooltip-transform-index-percentile_descr: Tooltip in the Index Options Editor: Explains the function of an option that transforms the row index of data into a percentile.

description.combine-rows-from-2-tables: Combine rows from 2+ tables, based on a related field.
description.combine-rows-from-2-tables_description: Description in the 'Join by field' transformer editor: This text explains the primary function of the 'Join by field' data transformation, which is to combine rows from multiple tables or data sources based on a common, related field.

name.join-by-field_description: Label in the Join by field transformer editor: Identifies a data transformation operation that combines data based on a specified field.

join-by-labels-transformer-editor.error-required_description: Error message in the Join by Labels Transformer editor: Informs the user that a specific input field is mandatory. This message is displayed when a user attempts to proceed without providing a value for a required input.

info.input-contain-labels: The input does not contain any labels
info.input-contain-labels_description: Informational message in the "Join by Labels" transformer editor: This message notifies the user when the input data for the transformer does not contain any labels. This is crucial as labels are required for the join operation.
info.no-input-or-labels-found_description: Informational message in the 'Join by labels' transformer editor: This message is displayed when the transformer detects that no input data or labels are available for processing.
info.title-no-input-found_description: Title for a status message in the Join by Labels Transformer editor: Informs the user that the transformer is not detecting any input data. This typically appears when the transformer is configured without a connected data source or if the upstream data flow is empty.
info.title-no-labels-found_description: Informational message in the Join by labels transformer editor: This message informs the user that no labels were found or are available for the data transformation operation. It indicates an absence of relevant labels needed for the 'Join by labels' function.

join-by-labels-transformer-editor.label-join_description: Button or action label in the Join by Labels Transformer editor: Represents the action to combine data series based on their labels.
join-by-labels-transformer-editor.label-value_description: Label in the Join by Labels Transformer Editor: This identifies an input field for specifying a value, typically as part of a label-value pair, during data transformation configuration.
join-by-labels-transformer-editor.tooltip-select-label-indicating-values: Select the label indicating the values name
join-by-labels-transformer-editor.tooltip-select-label-indicating-values_d: Tooltip in the Join by Labels Transformer editor: This tooltip instructs the user to select a data label that will be used to name or identify the values resulting from the transformation.

jsonpath-editor.add-path_description: Button in the JSONPath editor within the transformer configuration UI: Used to add a new JSONPath expression to the transformer.
jsonpath-editor.label-alias_description: Label in JSONPath Editor: Used to provide an alternative, more readable name for the output of a JSONPath expression or a transformed data field.
jsonpath-editor.label-field_description: Label in the JSONPath Editor (within the Transformers section): This label identifies an input or selection field where the user specifies a data field to be targeted or configured using JSONPath.
jsonpath-editor.placeholder-valid-objectvalue: A valid json path, e.g. "object.value1" or "object.value2[0]"
jsonpath-editor.tooltip-remove-path_description: Tooltip in the JSONPath editor for BMC Helix Dashboards transformers: Appears when hovering over an interactive element, indicating that clicking it will remove a configured JSON path.

labels-as-fields-transformer-editor.label-labels_description: Label in the Labels as Fields Transformer editor: This identifies the data 'labels' that are being configured to be transformed into fields. It likely serves as a section heading or a column header for these labels.
labels-as-fields-transformer-editor.label-mode_description: Label for a selection field in the Labels as Fields Transformer editor: Specifies the operational mode or method for how labels are processed and converted into fields.
labels-as-fields-transformer-editor.label-value-field-name_description: Input Field Label in the Labels as Fields Transformer editor: It prompts the user to define the name for the new field that will store the extracted value from a label.

label.columns_description: Section header or label in the 'label' mode of the Labels as Fields Transformer Editor: This refers to the configuration or display of data columns relevant to transforming labels into fields.
label.rows_description: Label in the Labels as Fields Transformer editor, within the 'Label' processing modes: This refers to the data rows that are being processed or configured by the transformer when converting labels into fields.

labels-as-fields-transformer-editor.placeholder-optional-select-label_descri: Placeholder text in the Labels as Fields Transformer editor: It prompts the user to optionally select a label that will be used or processed by the transformer.
labels-as-fields-transformer-editor.tooltip-replace-value-field-label: Replace the value field name with a label
labels-as-fields-transformer-editor.tooltip-replace-value-field-label_descri: Tooltip in the Labels as Fields Transformer editor: Explains the function of an option that allows replacing the value field's name with a specified label. This is used when configuring how labels are converted into distinct fields.

description.groups-series-time-return-labels-tags-fi: Group series by time and return labels or tags as fields.
Description text in the Labels to Fields transformer editor: It explains how this transformer groups data series by time and converts their labels or tags into new fields.

name.labels-to-fields_description: Name in the 'Labels to fields' transformer editor: This string identifies a data transformation option that converts existing labels into structured fields. It allows users to select this specific transformation type.

description.limit-number-items-displayed: Limit the number of items displayed.
description.limit-number-items-displayed_description: Description text in the 'Limit Transformer' editor: This text describes the primary function of the transformer, which is to restrict the total number of data items that will be visible in the dashboard visualization.

name.limit_description: Label in the Limit Transformer Editor: Refers to the specific transformer type that restricts the number of data points or rows.

limit-transformer-editor.placeholder-value-or-variable_descriptio: Placeholder text in an input field within the Limit transformer editor: This text guides the user to enter either a specific value or a variable name to define the limit.

long-info.ascending-order-duplicates: Time in ascending order, but may have duplicates
long-info.ascending-order-duplicates_description: Information text in the data transformation configuration: Describes a data ordering behavior where time is sorted in ascending order, but duplicate time entries are not removed. This clarifies what users can expect from this specific sorting option.
long-info.multiple-value-fields-may-exist_descript: Informational message in the data transformation settings: It informs the user that the data being processed might contain multiple fields identified as 'value' fields. This could be a heads-up for how the transformer will handle the data.
long-info.single-frame_description: Option in the 'long-info' settings for data transformers: Configures the transformer to process or output data as a single, consolidated frame, often for display or further processing.
long-info.st-field-is-time_description: Informational message within a data transformer's detailed configuration in BMC Helix Dashboards: It explains that the first field of the input data is expected to be a time field. This is often a prerequisite for time-series data transformations.
long-info.string-values-separate: String values are represented as separate fields rather than as labels
long-info.string-values-separate_description: Description in Data Transformer settings: Explains how string values are represented, specifically as separate fields rather than labels. This informs users about the chosen data output format.

description.merge-multiple-series: Merge multiple series. Values will be combined into one row.

merge-transformer-editor.merge-effect-applied-single-frame: Merge has no effect when applied on a single frame.
merge-transformer-editor.merge-effect-applied-single-frame_descri: Informational message in the Merge Transformer editor: Informs the user that the merge operation requires multiple frames of data to be effective. This message appears when the merge transformer is applied to a single data frame.

name.merge_description: Name in the Merge Transformer Editor: Identifies the purpose of the transformer, which is to combine multiple data series or tables.

multi-info.all-values-are-numeric_description: Informational message in the Multi-info transformer settings: Confirms that all data values processed or displayed in this context are numeric. This could be a result of a data type check or a prerequisite for further operations.
multi-info.frame-fields-value_description: Label in the Multi-Info Transformer configuration: This text informs the user that each data frame processed or configured within this transformer will utilize two specific fields. The placeholders {{field1}} and {{field2}} will be dynamically replaced with the actual names of these fields.
multi-info.multiple-frames_description: Option in multi-info transformer settings: This option determines how the transformer processes or presents data when the input contains multiple distinct frames or data series.
multi-info.string-values-are-represented-as-labels: String values are represented as labels
multi-info.string-values-are-represented-as-labels_: Informational text in the Multi-info transformer settings: This statement clarifies that string data values will be displayed as visual labels.
multi-info.time-in-ascending-order_description: Option in multi-info panel transformer settings: Specifies that time-based data within the panel should be sorted in ascending order.

description.reorder-hide-or-rename-fields_descriptio: Description text in the Organize Fields Transformer Editor: This string describes the functionality of the transformer, which allows users to re-order, hide, or rename data fields presented in a dashboard.

organize-fields-transformer-editor.field-order_description: Label in the Organize Fields Transformer Editor: This element refers to the sequence or arrangement of data fields within the transformer. It is used to define or display the desired order of fields.
organize-fields-transformer-editor.field-order-auto_description: Option "Auto" in the Organize Fields Transformer editor: Specifies that fields should be automatically ordered. This option is used when configuring how data fields are arranged within a dashboard transformer.
organize-fields-transformer-editor.field-order-manual_description: Label in the Organize Fields Transformer Editor: This option, "Manual," allows users to manually specify the order of fields. It represents a user-controlled method for field arrangement within the data transformer.
organize-fields-transformer-editor.first-frame-warning: Organize fields only works with a single frame. Consider applying a join transformation or filtering the input first.

name.organize-fields_description: Label or option in the Organize Fields Transformer editor: This text describes an option to organize (e.g., sort or group) fields by their names.

partion-by-values-editor.tooltip-naming: Sets how the names of the selected fields are displayed. As frame name is usually better for tabular data

partition-by-values-editor.label-field_description: Label in the partition-by-values editor: Refers to the input or selection where the user specifies the data field to be used for partitioning values.
partition-by-values-editor.label-keep-fields_description: Option label in the 'Partition by values' transformer editor: This option allows users to specify whether certain input fields, potentially including those used for partitioning, should be retained in the transformed data output.
partition-by-values-editor.label-naming_description: Section heading in the 'Partition By Values' editor: This section allows users to define the naming conventions for the resulting partitions or their labels generated by the transformer.
partition-by-values-editor.label-select-field_description: Label in the Partition by values editor: Instructs the user to select a data field. This label is associated with a control where the user chooses a specific field from the dataset for partitioning.

label.as-frame-name_description: Option label in the 'Partition by values' transformer editor's 'Naming modes' options: This choice allows users to name transformed data elements (e.g., series, fields) using the name of the data frame they belong to.
label.as-label_description: Option in the 'Naming Modes Options' section of the 'Partition by Values Editor': This setting allows users to specify that the values from a selected field should be used directly as the labels for the resulting data partitions.

partition-by-values-editor.partition-values-works-single-frame: Partition by values only works with a single frame.
partition-by-values-editor.partition-values-works-single-frame_desc: Informational message in the Partition by Values Editor within the Transformers section: Informs the user about a limitation of the "Partition by values" transformer, stating it requires a single data frame to function.
partition-by-values-editor.tooltip-keeps-partition-fields-frames: Keeps the partition fields in the frames
partition-by-values-editor.tooltip-keeps-partition-fields-frames_de: Tooltip in the Partition by Values editor (within data transformers): Explains an option that ensures the fields used for partitioning data are retained in the resulting data frames. This clarifies the behavior of a data transformation setting.

description.stretch-data-frames: Stretch data frames from the wide format into the long format.
description.stretch-data-frames_description: Description in the 'Prepare time series' transformer settings: This text explains the function of reshaping data frames from a wide format into a long format. This transformation is commonly used to normalize time series data for processing.

name.prepare-time-series_description: Name of a data transformer in the transformer configuration: This label identifies the 'Prepare Time Series' data transformation, which processes and prepares time series data for further use or visualization within BMC Helix Dashboards.

prepare-time-series-editor.label-format_description: Label in the Prepare Time Series Editor: Specifies options or a field for defining the display format of time series data or elements within the editor.
prepare-time-series-editor.label-info_description: Label in the Prepare Time Series Editor: Identifies a section or field containing relevant information. This could include details about the time series data, the transformation process, or specific parameters.

description.convert-each-frame-to-long-format: Convert each frame to long format
description.convert-each-frame-to-long-format_descri: Description in the Prepare Time-Series Editor: It describes an option to convert individual data frames of time-series data into a 'long' format. This transformation is often crucial for structuring data for analysis and visualization.

label.long-time-series_description: Label in the Prepare Time Series Editor: This label identifies or categorizes time series data that is considered 'long', likely for display within an informational or descriptive section.

description.creates-frame-timenumber: Creates a new frame for each time/number pair
description.creates-frame-timenumber_description: Description in the Prepare Time Series Editor's Multi-info section: Explains a data transformation option that structures time series data. It specifies that a new data frame is generated for each unique time/number pair found in the input.

label.multiframe-time-series_description: Label in the Time Series Preparation Editor: This text identifies a time series that aggregates or presents data from multiple frames. It is used in a section where multiple data series or information types are managed for transformation and display.

description.creates-single-frame-joined: Creates a single frame joined by time
description.creates-single-frame-joined_description: Description string in the 'Prepare Time Series' editor: This string describes a data transformation option that merges multiple time series into a single data frame, aligning them by time. It is likely found under a "Wide Info" setting within the transformation configuration.

label.wide-time-series_description: Label in the 'Wide Info' section of the Prepare Time Series Editor: It identifies an option or displays information related to a "wide" format for time series data, where different data points or series are typically represented in separate columns. This label helps users understand or configure how time series data is structured.

transformers.query-validation-message: One or more queries failed to return fields. This transformation can only reference fields from queries with a successful and visible result.

range-matcher-editor.and_description: Text label in the Range Matcher editor: It serves as a logical connector to combine multiple conditions when defining a range.
range-matcher-editor.placeholder-from_description: Placeholder text in the Range Matcher Editor within data transformers: Prompts the user to enter the starting value for a defined range.
range-matcher-editor.placeholder-to_description: Placeholder text in the Range Matcher Editor: This word marks the upper bound or end value of a range definition. It prompts the user to input the 'to' value for a specified range.

reduce-row-options-editor.label-calculation_description: Label in the Reduce Row Options editor: It identifies an option or field for defining or selecting a method to calculate aggregated data.
reduce-row-options-editor.label-operation_description: Label in the data transformer's "Reduce row options editor": This identifies the field where users select or define the aggregation or reduction operation to apply to data rows.

description.reduce-to-single-value: Reduce all rows or data points to a single value (ex. max, mean).

reduce-transformer-editor.label-calculations_description: Label in the Reduce Transformer editor: Indicates the section where users can define calculations or aggregations for data transformation.
reduce-transformer-editor.label-include-time_description: Label in the Reduce transformer editor: This UI element allows users to specify whether time values should be included during data transformation by the reduce transformer. It likely functions as a checkbox or toggle.
reduce-transformer-editor.label-labels-to-fields_description: Setting label in the Reduce Transformer Editor: This option configures a data transformation that converts existing labels (metadata or tags) into distinct data fields or columns.
reduce-transformer-editor.label-mode_description: Label in Reduce Transformer Editor: This label introduces a setting where users select the specific method or type of reduction (e.g., sum, average, count) to be applied by the transformer. It defines how data will be aggregated.

description.collapse-field-single-value: Collapse each field into a single value
description.collapse-field-single-value_description: **Description for a mode option** in **the Reduce transformer editor**: It explains a function that collapses all values within each data field into a single aggregated value. This is typically used for data aggregation or simplification.
description.create-table-series-value: Create a table with one row for each series value
description.create-table-series-value_description: Description in the "Reduce" transformer editor modes: This text explains a transformation mode that creates a table where each unique series value from the input data occupies its own row. This is an option for how the transformer processes and displays data.

label.reduce-fields_description: Label in the Reduce Transformer editor: This label identifies a mode or option for configuring the transformer to reduce the number of fields (columns) in the dataset.
label.series-to-rows_description: Label in the Reduce Transformer Editor's mode selection: Describes a data transformation option that reshapes data by converting data series into individual rows. This is used to normalize data for different visualization or analysis needs.

name.reduce_description: Transformer Name in the Reduce Transformer Editor: Identifies the data transformation operation that aggregates multiple data points into a single result.

reduce-transformer-editor.placeholder-choose-stat_description: Placeholder text in a field within the Reduce Transformer Editor: Prompts the user to select a statistic. This is for configuring a data transformation or aggregation process.

regex-matcher-editor.placeholder-value-or-variable_descriptio: Placeholder text in the Regex Matcher editor: This field expects either a static value or the name of a variable to be used in the regex operation.
regex-matcher-editor.variable-regex-documentation: Formats multi-value variable into a regex string
