regex-matcher-editor.variable-regex-documentation_description: Documentation text in the Regex Matcher Editor (within the Transformers section): Describes the functionality of formatting a multi-value variable into a regular expression string for matching purposes.

description.create-new-data-frame: Create a new data frame containing values predicted by a statistical model.
description.create-new-data-frame_description: Description in Regression transformer configuration: This text explains an option to create a new data frame. The new data frame contains values predicted by a statistical model, typically for visualizing or analyzing the model's output.

name.trendline_description: **Label** in **regression transformer settings**: **Identifies 'Trendline' as a type or name for a regression analysis output or component.**

label.cubic_description: Label for a selection option in the Regression Transformer Editor: This text identifies the 'Cubic' method as a choice for regression or interpolation analysis.
label.decic_description: Label in Regression Transformer Editor: This string identifies an option or setting for a 'decic' transformation or polynomial. It refers to a transformation or polynomial of the tenth degree.
label.nonic_description: Label in Regression Transformer Editor: Represents an option for selecting a polynomial degree, specifically degree 9. This value defines the complexity for a regression model.
label.octic_description: Label in the regression transformer editor: This label represents an option for selecting an "Octic" or "8th-degree" polynomial regression model.
label.quadratic_description: Label in the Regression Transformer Editor: Represents the "Quadratic" type of regression or a quadratic term available for selection or configuration.
label.quartic_description: Option label in the Regression Transformer Editor: This label represents the "Quartic" option for a polynomial regression type, indicating a fourth-degree polynomial model.
label.quintic_description: Label in Regression Transformer Editor: This option allows users to select the "Quintic" (fifth-degree polynomial) regression type for data transformation.
label.septic_description: Label in the Regression Transformer Editor: This label represents a specific classification or state within the data transformation or regression settings. The Spanish translation 'De grado 7' suggests it refers to a particular grade or level, possibly 'Grade 7'.
label.sextic_description: Label in the Regression Transformer Editor: This label identifies a "Sextic" polynomial regression option, referring to a polynomial of degree 6.

regression-transformer-editor.label-degree_description: Label in the Regression Transformer Editor: Specifies the 'degree' of the polynomial to be used in the regression model. This label is typically associated with an input field.
regression-transformer-editor.label-model-type_description: Label in Regression Transformer Editor: Identifies or allows selection of the specific model type to be used for regression analysis.
regression-transformer-editor.label-predicted-points_description: Label in Regression Transformer Editor: Identifies data points that have been predicted or generated by the regression transformer.
regression-transformer-editor.label-x-field_description: Label for an input field in the Regression Transformer Editor: It indicates where users select the independent variable (X-axis data) for performing regression analysis.
regression-transformer-editor.label-y-field_description: Label in the Regression Transformer Editor: Identifies the data field that represents the dependent variable (Y-axis) for the regression analysis.

label.linear_description: Option label in the Regression Transformer Editor's 'Model Type Options': This label identifies "Linear" as a selectable model type for regression analysis.
label.polynomial_description: Option label in the Regression Transformer Editor: Identifies 'Polynomial' as a selectable model type for regression analysis within the transformer configuration.

regression-transformer-editor.regression_description: Label in the Regression Transformer Editor: Identifies the statistical method of 'regression' as a configurable option or section. It refers to the technique used for modeling relationships between variables.

tags.regression-analysis_description: Tag option in the Regression Transformer Editor: Allows users to select or apply 'Regression analysis' as a specific data transformation method. It categorizes a type of statistical analysis.

regression-transformer-editor.tooltip-high-degree-polynomial: Higher-degree polynomials (e.g., degree 4 or higher) can result in misleading trends and unstable fits. Proceed with caution.
regression-transformer-editor.tooltip-number-of-xy-points-to-predict: Number of X,Y points to predict
regression-transformer-editor.tooltip-number-of-xy-points-to-predict_d: Tooltip in the Regression Transformer Editor: Explains a field where users specify the number of future X,Y data points the regression model should predict. This setting controls the length of the predicted output.

description.rename-parts-using-regex: Rename parts of the query results using a regular expression and replacement pattern.
description.rename-parts-using-regex_description: Description in the Rename by Regex data transformer configuration: Explains the transformer's function to rename parts of query results using a regular expression and replacement pattern.

name.rename-fields-by-regex_description: Label in the Transformers configuration: This string identifies a data transformation option that allows users to rename fields using regular expressions.

rename-by-regex-transformer-editor.label-match_description: Label in the 'Rename by Regex Transformer' editor: Refers to the input field where the user defines the regular expression pattern to match against existing text. This pattern identifies the elements (e.g., field names) that will be targeted for renaming.
rename-by-regex-transformer-editor.label-replace_description: Label in the "Rename by Regex" transformer editor: Marks the input field where the user specifies the string to replace patterns found by the regular expression.
rename-by-regex-transformer-editor.placeholder-regular-expression-pattern_d: Placeholder text in the 'Rename by Regex Transformer' editor: It prompts the user to enter a regular expression pattern used for renaming data fields.
rename-by-regex-transformer-editor.placeholder-replacement-pattern_descript: Label for an input field in the Rename by Regex Transformer Editor: This field is where users define the pattern used to replace matched text when renaming items with a regular expression. It determines how the new names are constructed.

series-to-fields-transformer-editor.label-field_description: Label in the Series to Fields Transformer Editor: Prompts the user to select or specify a data field. This field is crucial for defining how the series data is transformed into individual fields.
series-to-fields-transformer-editor.label-mode_description: Label in Series to Fields transformer editor: This label introduces a selection field that determines the method or type of transformation to apply when converting time series data into fields.

description.combine-data-from-two-tables: Combine data from two tables whenever there are matching values in a fields common to both tables.
description.combine-data-from-two-tables_description: Description for a mode in the Series to Fields transformer editor: This text explains the functionality of a transformer mode that combines data from two tables. It specifies that the combination occurs when there are matching values in fields common to both tables.
description.join-on-a-field: Join on a field value with duplicated values. Non performant outer join best used for tabular(SQL like) data.
description.keep-all-rows: Keep all rows from any table with a value. Join on distinct field values. Performant and best used for time series.

label.inner_description: Label in the Series to Fields Transformer editor: This option designates an "Inner" mode for data transformation, likely referring to a strategy for combining or processing data points where only matching or intersecting data is retained.
label.outer-tabular_description: Label in the Series to Fields Transformer editor modes section: This option defines a data transformation mode that structures converted series data into an outer tabular format.
label.outer-time-series_description: Label for a mode in the Series to Fields Transformer editor: This option specifies an "Outer" processing method for handling time series data during the transformation into fields. It likely defines how data points are aligned or aggregated across different series.

description.merge-multiple-series: Merge multiple series. Return time, metric and values as a row.

name.series-to-rows_description: Label in the "Series to rows" transformer editor: This text identifies a data transformation that reshapes data from a series-oriented structure into a row-oriented one.

description.sort-fields_description: Description in the Sort by Transformer editor: This text explains the functionality of sorting fields within a data frame. A "frame" refers to a data frame, which is a structured dataset.

sort-by-transformer-editor.label-field_description: Label in the Sort by Transformer editor: Labels the input for selecting the data field or column by which to sort the data.
sort-by-transformer-editor.label-reverse_description: Checkbox/Toggle in the Sort by Transformer editor: Allows users to reverse the current sorting order, typically from ascending to descending or vice-versa.

name.sort-by_description: Text label in the Sort by Transformer editor: This text introduces the selection or input field where users define the criteria for sorting data.

sort-by-transformer-editor.placeholder-select-field_description: Placeholder text in the Sort by Transformer Editor: Prompts the user to select a data field from a dropdown list to configure how data will be sorted.

description.apply-spatial-operations-to-the-geometry: Apply spatial operations to the geometry

Description text for an option or setting in the `Transformers > Supplier` configuration: This text clarifies that the associated feature allows for the application of spatial operations to geometric data, enabling manipulation of geographical or spatial information.
description.create-a-line-between-two-points: Create a line between two points
description.create-a-line-between-two-points_descrip: Description text in the data transformation interface, specifically within the 'supplier' section: It defines a function or tool used to create a line connecting two specified data points, likely for data generation or manipulation.
description.geometry-define-field-headingdistanceare: Use the geometry to define a new field (heading/distance/area)

Description text in the configuration panel for a geometry-based "Supplier" transformer: This text explains that the transformer utilizes geometric data to create new fields representing properties such as heading, distance, or area.

label.area_description: Label in data transformation settings for a supplier: This field specifies a geographical or logical 'Area' relevant to the data provided by the supplier, often used for filtering or categorization.
label.as-line_description: Label in data transformation settings: Indicates an option to display data or a data series as a line.
label.calculate-value_description: Label for an action in the Transformer Supplier configuration section: Initiates the calculation of a specific value.
label.distance_description: Label for a field in the Supplier section of the Transformers module: Identifies a measurement of spatial separation.
label.heading: Heading
label.heading_description: Label in the supplier configuration within the Transformers feature: This label designates an input field for defining a section or component heading. It is used to provide a title for a specific part of the supplier settings.
label.line-builder_description: Label for the 'Line builder' feature in the 'Supplier' section of data transformers: This feature allows users to construct or define lines, likely referring to data transformation logic, expressions, or connections within a workflow.
label.prepare-spatial-field_description: Label in the data transformer's supplier configuration: This refers to an action or option to configure a data field specifically for spatial (geographical) information, preparing it for use.
label.transform_description: Button text in the data transformation feature, related to a supplier. It initiates the action of applying a transformation to data.

description.convert-to-table-rows: Convert time series data to table rows so that they can be viewed in tabular or sparkline format.
description.convert-to-table-rows_description: Description in the 'Convert to Table Rows' transform option for the Time Series Table Transformer: Explains that this option restructures time series data into table rows, allowing it to be viewed in tabular or sparkline formats.

is-applicable-description.requires-time-series-frame: The Time series to table transformation requires at least one time series frame to function. You currently have none.

name.time-series-to-table_description: Name for a data transformer in the 'Transformers' section: This name indicates that the transformer converts time series data into a tabular format.

time-series-table-transform-editor.label-stat_description: Label in the Time-series Table Transform Editor: This label refers to a specific statistical aggregation or value. It allows users to select or define a statistic to apply to time-series data.
time-series-table-transform-editor.label-time-field_description: Label in Time Series Table Transform Editor: This label indicates the setting where users specify which data field (column) in their data source represents time. It is crucial for correctly processing and displaying time-series data in the table.
time-series-table-transform-editor.tooltip-statistic-should-calculated-seri: The statistic that should be calculated for this time series.
Tooltip in the Time Series Table Transform Editor: Explains the purpose of a setting or field where the user configures which statistic should be calculated for a particular time series.
time-series-table-transform-editor.tooltip-time-field: The time field that will be used for the time series. If not selected the first found will be used.

transpose-transfomer-editor.label-first-field-name_description: Label in the Transpose Transformer Editor: Prompts the user to specify the name of the initial field used in the data transposition. This field often serves as a key or identifier for restructuring the data.
transpose-transfomer-editor.label-remaining-fields-name_description: Label in the Transpose Transformer Editor: Specifies the name for the output field that will contain data from any fields not explicitly defined for the transpose operation.
transpose-transfomer-editor.placeholder-field_description: Placeholder text in the Transpose Transformer editor: Guides the user to enter or select a data field relevant to the transpose operation.
transpose-transfomer-editor.placeholder-value_description: Placeholder text in the Transpose Transformer editor: It indicates the expected input is a value for a specific field or setting.
transpose-transfomer-editor.tooltip-name-for-value-fields_descriptio: **Tooltip** in **Transpose Transformer editor**: This tooltip provides clarification or a prompt for the user to define how the new value fields, created after the transpose operation, should be named.

description.transpose-data-frame_description: Description in the Transpose Transformer editor: Describes the primary action performed by this transformer, which is to transpose data frames.

name.transpose_description: Transformer name in the Transpose Transformer Editor: This identifies the data transformation operation that swaps rows and columns.

tags.pivot_description: Option in the Transpose transformer editor: Represents the action of pivoting data. This transformation reorganizes data by turning rows into columns or vice versa, often used for creating pivot tables.
tags.transform_description: Button in the Transpose Transformer editor's tags section: Initiates a transformation process, likely for the selected tags or data within this specific editor.
tags.translate_description: Button in the Transpose Transformer Editor, within the 'Tags' section: Allows the user to apply a data translation or remapping operation to tags.

unary-operation-editor.label-operation_description: Label in the Unary Operation Editor (within the Transformers section): It prompts the user to select or define the specific unary operation to be applied.
unary-operation-editor.placeholder-field_description: Placeholder text in the unary operation editor: It prompts the user to select or enter a data field on which to perform the unary operation.

get-categories-labels.calculate-new-fields_description: Option in the 'get-categories-labels' utility within the Transformers section: Enables users to define and compute new data fields. This is used for data transformation, potentially for categorization or labeling.
get-categories-labels.combine_description: Action in the data transformation utilities for category and label management: Used to combine selected categories or labels.
get-categories-labels.create-new-visualization_description: Button/Link in the BMC Helix Dashboards interface: Allows users to begin creating a new data visualization. This action typically navigates to a builder or configuration screen for defining the new visualization.
get-categories-labels.filter_description: Button in the utility component for retrieving or displaying categories and labels: Applies filtering criteria to the categories or labels shown.
get-categories-labels.perform-spatial-operations_description: **Action** in **the `get-categories-labels` utility within the data transformers**: This initiates the processing or analysis of spatial (geographical) data. It applies geographical computations to data categories or labels, potentially influencing their generation or modification.
get-categories-labels.reformat_description: **Action button/link** in a data transformation utility for categories and labels: Allows users to reformat the associated data, altering its structure or presentation.
get-categories-labels.reorder-and-rename_description: Menu item or button in the data transformation utility for configuring categories and labels: This option enables users to rearrange and change the names of defined categories and their labels.

description.boolean-false_description: Description for an option in the Special Value Options selection of the Transformers utility: It defines the literal 'false' boolean value available for selection in data transformations.
description.boolean-true_description: Description text in the 'Special Value Options' settings of the 'Transformers' utility: It specifies the value that represents a boolean 'true' within data transformations.
description.empty-string_description: Option label in the Special Value Options section (within data transformers utilities): Allows users to select an empty string as a special value.
description.null-value_description: Option label in the 'Special Value Options' for data transformers: This label refers to a null or undefined value, used for configuring how such values are handled or displayed.
description.number-value_description: Description text for a special value option in the Transformers utility: It represents the numerical value 0. This option is likely used when configuring how data transformations handle or display the number zero.

label.boolean-false_description: Label in special value options within data transformation utilities: Represents the boolean value 'False'. This string is used to display or select the 'False' option for a boolean field.
label.boolean-true_description: Label in the special value options for data transformers: Represents the boolean 'true' value, likely used as an option or indicator.
label.empty-string_description: Option in special value settings within data transformation utilities: Allows users to select 'Empty' as a placeholder or specific value. This indicates an empty string or null content for a configuration parameter or data output.
label.null-value_description: Option label in the special value settings of data transformers: Identifies the 'Null' value as a configurable option for handling null data.
label.number-value_description: Label in Special Value Options: This string represents the numerical value 'Zero' as a selectable option or setting within data transformation utilities. It is used for special handling of number fields.

wide-info.multiple-value-fields-of-any-type: Multiple value fields of any type
wide-info.multiple-value-fields-of-any-type_descri: Setting description in the 'Wide to Info' transformer settings: This text indicates the ability to select or process multiple fields containing values, irrespective of their data type.
wide-info.single-frame_description: Configuration option in data transformer settings for detailed information: Specifies that the transformed data should be presented within a single display frame or panel.
wide-info.st-field-is-shared-time_description: Informational message in the wide-info data transformer settings: It indicates that the first field is identified as a shared time field. This designation typically affects how time values are synchronized or displayed across multiple data series.
wide-info.time-in-ascending-order_description: Label in the 'Wide Info' data transformer configuration: Specifies the sorting preference for time-based data, ensuring it is ordered from earliest to latest.

window-options-editor.label-calculation_description: Label in the window options editor: Identifies the section or a setting for configuring calculations applied to data within a defined window. These calculations are part of data transformation operations.
window-options-editor.label-field_description: Label in the transformer's window options editor: This label prompts the user to select a data field that the window transformation will apply to or base its operations on.
window-options-editor.label-type_description: Label in the window options editor within a data transformer: This label indicates the field for selecting the type of window function or operation to be applied.
window-options-editor.label-window-size_description: Label in the Window Options Editor within the Transformers section: It identifies the setting where users specify the size of a data window, typically used for aggregation or transformation operations.
window-options-editor.label-window-size-mode_description: Label in the window options editor for a data transformer: This label indicates the setting used to define how the size of a data processing window is determined.
window-options-editor.label-window-size-percent_description: Label in the Window Options Editor within a data transformer configuration: Specifies the size of the "window" for a window function or rolling calculation, expressed as a percentage of the total data or a grouping.
window-options-editor.placeholder-auto_description: Placeholder text in the Window Options Editor within the Transformers section: It represents the 'Auto' selection, signifying that a particular setting or value will be automatically determined by the system.
window-options-editor.placeholder-field_description: Placeholder text in the Window Options Editor: This text appears within an input field, prompting the user to enter or select a data field or column name for a transformation or configuration.
window-options-editor.tooltip-window-size_description: **Tooltip label** in the **Window Options Editor**: This describes the **size of a window**, typically referring to a data aggregation or time window configured for data transformations.
window-options-editor.tooltip-window-size-percent: Set the window size as a percentage of the total data
window-options-editor.tooltip-window-size-percent_description: Tooltip in the Window Options Editor for data transformers: Describes how to set the size of a data processing window as a percentage of the total dataset.

label.centered_description: Option in Window Options Editor: This option allows users to select "Centered" as an alignment setting for a label.
label.trailing_description: Label in Window Options Editor, under Type Options: This label designates a window type or option that refers to the end or a backward-looking segment of data, often implying a "trailing" or "sliding" window behavior.

label.fixed_description: **Label** in the **window size mode options within the data transformer editor**: This indicates a setting where the data window will have a fixed or constant size.
label.percentage_description: Option label in the Window Size Mode options editor: This label indicates a method for defining the size of a data window or time window as a percentage of the total range.

trend.category-x-axis_description: Configuration option in trend visualization settings: Used to label or configure the horizontal axis (X-axis) of a trend chart.
trend.description-x-field_description: Description text within trend analysis settings for a data field: It specifies that the data values of the field are numeric and show an increasing pattern.
trend.name-x-field_description: Label in trend configuration settings: This string identifies the data field to be plotted on the X-axis of a trend chart.
trend.placeholder-x-field_description: Placeholder text in Trend visualization settings: Guides the user to select or indicates the primary numeric data field to be used for the trend line or axis.

upgrade-box.discovery-text_description: Informational text in an upgrade box: Notifies the user that they have discovered a feature available only in a 'Pro' subscription. This message likely appears when a user encounters a feature that requires a plan upgrade.
upgrade-box.discovery-text-continued: Get the Grafana Pro plan to access {{featureName}}.
upgrade-box.discovery-text-continued_description: Text label in an upgrade box: This message informs the user that a particular feature, specified by `{{featureName}}`, is only available with the Grafana Pro plan. It encourages users to upgrade their subscription to access premium functionality.
upgrade-box.get-started_description: Call to action button in an upgrade or new feature notification area: Prompts users to begin exploring or using a specific new feature. This appears after a system upgrade or feature rollout.
upgrade-box.learn-more_description: Link in an upgrade box: Directs users to additional information about a product upgrade.
upgrade-box.upgrade-button_description: Button in the upgrade box: Initiates the upgrade process for BMC Helix Dashboards or a specific component within it.

user-orgs.current-org-button_description: Button label in the user organizations interface: Identifies the currently active or selected organization.
user-orgs.select-org-button_description: Button in the User Organizations screen: Allows the user to select one of their associated organizations.
user-orgs.title: Organizations
user-orgs.title_description: Title in the Organizations management page: Identifies the section where a user can view and manage their associated organizations.

user-picker.noOptionsMessage-no-users-found_descript: Message in a user picker component: Displayed when no users match the search criteria or are available for selection.
user-picker.select-aria-label_description: Label for the user picker component: This text identifies the control responsible for selecting users within the interface.
user-picker.select-placeholder: Start typing to search for user
user-picker.select-placeholder_description: Input field placeholder in a user picker component: This text guides users to type into the field to search for and select a user.

fields.email-error_description: Error message in User Profile email field: Indicates that the email address is a mandatory field and must be provided. This message appears when a user attempts to save or submit without entering an email address.
fields.name-error_description: Error message in User Profile fields: Indicates that the 'Name' input field is mandatory and must be filled out before saving or updating the user profile.

tabs.general_description: Tab label in User Profile settings: This tab displays general or basic information or settings related to the user profile.

user-session.auth-module-column_description: Column header in the User Sessions table: Displays the name of the identity provider (e.g., SAML, LDAP) that authenticated the user for a specific session.
user-session.browser-column_description: Column header in a table displaying user session details: Identifies the web browser and operating system used during a user's session.
user-session.created-at-column_description: Column header in a table or list of user sessions: Indicates the date and time when a user logged on to create that session.
user-session.identity-provider-column_description: Column header in a table displaying user session details: It identifies the authentication service or system (e.g., SAML, LDAP) used for a particular user session.
user-session.ip-column_description: Column header in the User Session table: Displays the IP address associated with a user's session.
user-session.revoke_description: Button in User Session Management: This button allows an administrator to terminate an active user's session. It is typically used for security purposes or to force a remote log out.
user-session.seen-at-column_description: Column header in a table or list displaying user sessions: Indicates the timestamp when a user's session was last active, seen, or updated.

empty-state.message_description: Message in the Users management page: Informs the user when no user accounts are found, either because none exist or no users match the current search or filter criteria.

token-revoked-modal.auto-revoked: Your session token was automatically revoked because you have reached the maximum number of {{numSessions}} concurrent sessions for your account.
token-revoked-modal.resume-message: To resume your session, sign in again. Contact your administrator or visit the license page to review your quota if you are repeatedly signed out automatically.
token-revoked-modal.sign-in_description: Button in the token revoked modal: This button prompts the user to sign in again after their authentication token has been revoked, typically due to session expiration or security reasons.
token-revoked-modal.title-you-have-been-automatically-signed: You have been automatically signed out

Title in the automatic sign-out modal: Informs the user that their session has been automatically terminated. This message appears when a user's authentication token has been revoked or expired, requiring them to log in again.

label.users_description: Option label in the Users action bar: This label identifies a choice or section related to viewing or managing user accounts.

users-action-bar-unconnected.placeholder-search-login-email: Search user by login, email or name
users-action-bar-unconnected.placeholder-search-login-email_descripti: Placeholder text in a search input field located within the Users section's action bar: It prompts users to search for users by their login, email, or name.

tabs.shared-dashboard-users-tab-title_descrip: Tab Title in the Users Access List section: This tab displays a list of users who have access to shared dashboards.

variable-usages-button.usage-title_description: Title in the variable usages view: This heading indicates which specific variable's usages are currently being displayed. The `{{variableId}}` placeholder will be replaced with the actual variable's identifier.

adhoc.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in an input field for an ad-hoc variable: Prompts the user to select or enter a value for the variable.

dropdown.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a variable dropdown field: This text prompts the user to enter or select a value for the variable.

picker.link-all_description: Selection option in a variable picker: Allows users to select all possible values for the associated variable, acting as a global filter or selection.
picker.option-all_description: Selection option in a variable picker: Allows users to select all available values for the associated variable.
picker.option-selected-values_description: Option label in a variable picker component: Displays the total count of items that are currently selected. This provides a summary within a multi-select dropdown or similar interface.
picker.option-tooltip_description: Option in the variable picker: Allows the user to clear all currently selected values for the variable.

textbox.placeholder_description: Placeholder text in a textbox for defining or setting a variable's value: It instructs the user to input the desired value for the variable.

plus-segment.aria-label-add-filter_description: ARIA label for an 'Add Filter' button in the Ad-hoc Filters section under Variables: Enables users to add a new ad-hoc filter to the current configuration. This label is likely for a '+' icon or similar control.

confirm-delete-modal.body-delete-variable: Are you sure you want to delete variable "{{variableToDelete}}"?
confirm-delete-modal.body-delete-variable_description: Confirmation message in the Delete Variable confirmation modal: Prompts the user to confirm the deletion of a specific variable. The `{{variableToDelete}}` placeholder displays the name of the variable being deleted.
confirm-delete-modal.title-delete-variable_description: Title of a confirmation modal: This string serves as the title for the modal displayed when a user is prompted to confirm the deletion of a variable.

description.add-keyvalue-filters-on-the-fly: Add key/value filters on the fly.
description.add-keyvalue-filters-on-the-fly_descript: Description text in the "Create Ad-Hoc Variable Adapter" configuration: This describes a feature that allows users to add key/value filters dynamically. It informs users about the flexible, on-the-fly filtering capability offered by the ad-hoc variable adapter.

description.define-variable-values-manually_descript: Instructional text in the custom variable adapter creation screen: This describes an option for the user to manually enter specific values for the variable being configured, as opposed to fetching them automatically.

description.define-timespan-interval_description: Instructional text in the Create Interval Variable Adapter screen: This guides the user to define a timespan interval for the new variable. It includes a placeholder for showing examples of valid time interval formats.

description.variable-values-fetched-datasource-query: Variable values are fetched from a datasource query
Description text in the 'Create Query Variable' configuration: Clarifies that the variable's values will be populated by executing a query against a selected data source.

text.default_description: **Option label** in **Data Source Variable configuration**: This label identifies a selectable 'default' option or value for a data source variable. It allows users to revert to or select the system's predefined default data source.
text.no-data-sources-found_description: Status message in the Data Source Variable selection component: Informs the user that no data sources are currently available or configured for selection as a variable.

empty-state.button-title_description: Button in the 'Variables' empty state view: This button allows users to add their first variable when no variables have been defined yet for a dashboard.
empty-state.info-box-content: Variables enable more interactive and dynamic dashboards. Instead of hard-coding things like server or sensor names in your metric queries you can use variables in their place. Variables are shown as list boxes at the top of the dashboard. These drop-down lists make it easy to change the data being displayed in your dashboard.
empty-state.info-box-content-2: Check out the <2>Templates and variables documentation</2> for more information.
empty-state.info-box-content-2_description: Informational text with a link in the empty state of the Variables section: Guides users to the documentation for templates and variables. This text is displayed when there are no variables configured or available yet, offering help on how to proceed.
empty-state.title: There are no variables added yet
empty-state.title_description: Title in the empty state for the variables management section: Informs the user that no variables have been defined yet, indicating that the user needs to add variables to proceed.

legacy-variable-query-editor.placeholder-metric-name-or-tags-query_de: Placeholder text in the legacy variable query editor: This text prompts the user to enter either a metric name or a tags query to define a dashboard variable.

no-unknowns.no-renamed-or-missing-variables-found: No renamed or missing variables found.
no-unknowns.no-renamed-or-missing-variables-found_de: Informational message in the variable validation status display: It confirms that a check for renamed or missing variables found no issues, indicating all variables are correctly identified.

query-variable-refresh-select.description-update-values-variable: When to update the values of this variable
query-variable-refresh-select.description-update-values-variable_descr: Label in Query Variable Refresh settings: This text prompts the user to select when the variable's values should be updated. It introduces the options for defining the refresh frequency or trigger.
query-variable-refresh-select.label-refresh_description: Option in a dropdown for query variable refresh configuration: Specifies the action to update or reload the data for a query variable.

query-variable-sort-select.description-values-variable: How to sort the values of this variable
query-variable-sort-select.description-values-variable_description: Descriptive text in the Query Variable Sort Selection settings: It explains the various options available for sorting the values of the selected query variable.
query-variable-sort-select.name-sort_description: Option label in the query variable sort selection menu: Allows users to sort query variables by their name.

label.alphabetical-asc_description: Label for a sorting option in the query variable sort selection: Users choose this option to sort query variable values alphabetically in ascending order.
label.alphabetical-caseinsensitive-asc_descrip: Label in the query variable sort selection menu: This option sorts query variables alphabetically, ignoring case, in ascending order.
label.alphabetical-caseinsensitive-desc_descri: Label for a sort option in the Query Variable Sort Selection menu: This option sorts variable values alphabetically in descending order, disregarding case.
label.alphabetical-desc_description: Label for a sort option within the query variable configuration. It specifies that the variable values will be sorted alphabetically in descending order.
label.disabled_description: Label in a query variable sort selection component: This label indicates that a specific sort option is disabled or unavailable.
label.natural-asc_description: Label in the Query Variable Sort Options: This option allows users to sort the values of a query variable in natural ascending order.
label.natural-desc_description: Sorting option label in the query variable sort selection: This option allows users to sort query variable values in a natural descending order, where "natural" implies a human-friendly alphanumeric sort.
label.numerical-asc_description: Selection option in the sort options dropdown for a query variable: This option allows users to sort the listed variable values numerically from smallest to largest.
label.numerical-desc_description: Label for an option in the query variable sort selection control: It allows users to choose to sort query variable results numerically in descending order.

query-variable-static-options.description: Add custom options in addition to query results
query-variable-static-options.description_description: Description text in the Query Variable static options section: Informs the user that they can add custom, manually defined options to the variable's dropdown list, in addition to those populated from a database query.

query-variable-static-options-sort-selec.description-values-variable: How to sort static options with query results
query-variable-static-options-sort-selec.description-values-variable_description: Help text for a sorting option within variable configuration: This string describes the process of sorting static options that are combined with dynamic query results for a dashboard variable.

static-options.add-option-button-label_description: Button in the Static Options section of Variable settings: Allows users to add a new, predefined choice to a variable's list of options. This is typically used when configuring variables that present static selection choices.
static-options.drag-and-drop_description: Instruction in the list of static options for a dashboard variable: This text guides users on how to visually reorder the items in the list by dragging and dropping them.
static-options.label-header_description: Label in the static options configuration for variables: This label identifies the input field where users define the textual content to be displayed.
static-options.label-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the static options configuration for variables: This informs the user that if no custom label is entered for a static option, its value will be used as the default display label.
static-options.remove-option-button-label_description: Button label in the static options configuration for a variable: Used to remove an existing static option from the list.
static-options.value-header_description: Column header in the 'Static options' configuration for a variable: This labels the input field or column where the static values for the variable are defined. It typically accompanies a 'Label' field to define key-value pairs for options.
static-options.value-placeholder_description: Placeholder text in the Static Options section for a variable: It indicates where to input a specific value when defining a static option for a variable.

text-box-variable-editor.name-default-value_description: Label in the Text Box Variable Editor: For specifying the initial value that a text box variable will hold when the dashboard loads. This value will be pre-filled in the text input field.
text-box-variable-editor.placeholder-default-value-if-any_descrip: Placeholder text in the text box variable editor: It suggests where the user can optionally enter a default value for the text box variable.
text-box-variable-editor.text-options_description: Section header in the Text Box Variable Editor: This indicates a group of settings for configuring the text-related properties of a text box variable.

label.dashboard_description: Text label in the result configuration for the 'transform-usages-to-network' variable: This label identifies a specific entity or node as a 'dashboard' within the network transformation's output.

unknown-table.no-unknowns: No renamed or missing variables found.
unknown-table.no-unknowns_description: Informational message in the 'Unknown Variables' table within the Variables section: Informs the user that no variables were found that have been renamed or are currently missing. This indicates a clean state with no variable definition issues to display in this table.
unknown-table.renamed-or-missing-variables_description: Status message in the 'Unknown Table' section of the Variables management interface: This message indicates that variables expected to be associated with an unrecognized or unknown table are either missing or have been renamed. It alerts the user to a potential configuration or data retrieval problem.
unknown-table.tooltip-renamed-or-missing-variables: Click to expand a list with all variable references that have been renamed or are missing from the dashboard.
unknown-table.variable_description: Label for a Variable field in the variable configuration section: This text identifies a customizable parameter. It is likely used when configuring variables before a specific data table has been selected or identified (unknown-table).

variable-check-indicator.title-variable-referenced-dashboard: This variable is not referenced by any variable or dashboard.
variable-check-indicator.title-variable-referenced-dashboard_desc: Informational message in the variable management interface, associated with a variable check indicator: It notifies the user that the selected variable is not referenced by any other variables or dashboards. This helps users identify and manage unused variables.
variable-check-indicator.title-variable-referenced-other-variable: This variable is referenced by other variables or dashboard.
Tooltip in the variable management section: Informs the user that a specific variable is referenced by other variables or dashboards, indicating its dependencies.

variable-editor-editor-un-connected.apply_description: Button in the variable editor for 'unconnected' variables: To apply or save the changes made to the variable configuration.
variable-editor-editor-un-connected.aria-label-variable-editor-form_descript: Aria-label for a form in the Variable Editor: Describes the interface for defining and configuring variables, specifically when the editor or variable is in an "unconnected" state.
variable-editor-editor-un-connected.description-optional-display-name_descri: Label in the variable editor for unconnected variables: This text labels an input field where users can optionally provide an alternative, more user-friendly name for a variable.
variable-editor-editor-un-connected.description-template-variable-characters: The name of the template variable. (Max. 50 characters)
variable-editor-editor-un-connected.general_description: Section label in the variable editor's settings pane for an un-connected variable: Designates the area for configuring basic or common properties of the variable.
variable-editor-editor-un-connected.name-label_description: Label for a text input field in the Variable Editor for an unconnected variable: It identifies the field where users define the variable's display name.
variable-editor-editor-un-connected.placeholder-descriptive-text_description: Text field placeholder in the variable editor for an unconnected variable: It prompts the user to enter a descriptive text for the variable.
variable-editor-editor-un-connected.placeholder-label-name_description: Placeholder text in the Variable Editor for unconnected variables: Prompts the user to enter a name for a label. This label identifies a variable being defined or edited.
variable-editor-editor-un-connected.placeholder-variable-name_description: Placeholder text in the variable name input field within the unconnected variable editor: Prompts the user to enter a name for the variable being defined or edited.
variable-editor-editor-un-connected.run-query_description: Button in the variable editor for an unconnected variable: Executes the configured query. This is typically used for previewing results or validating the query during setup.

variable-editor-list.definition_description: Label in the variable editor list: Refers to the field or section where the variable's definition (e.g., its query, value, or expression) is specified.
variable-editor-list.new-variable_description: Button in the variable editor list: Initiates the process of creating a new variable.
variable-editor-list.variable_description: Label in the variable editor list: Identifies an individual variable within the list or serves as a label for a field related to a single variable definition. It represents a generic reference to a variable instance.

variable-editor-list-row.tooltip-duplicate-variable_description: Tooltip in a variable editor list row: Appears when hovering over an icon or button, indicating the option to duplicate the variable in that row.
variable-editor-list-row.tooltip-remove-variable_description: Tooltip text in an action icon/button within a variable list row of the variable editor: It describes the action of removing the specific variable from the list.

variable-options.aria-label-toggle-all-values_description: Aria-label for a toggle control in the variable options section. This control allows users to select or deselect all available values for a dashboard variable, primarily for accessibility.

variable-type-select.name-select-variable-type_description: Label in the variable type selection area within the Variables management section: Prompts the user to choose the type of variable (e.g., query, custom, textbox) they want to create or edit.

variable-usages-button.tooltip-show-usages_description: Tooltip on the 'Variable Usages' button in the Variables section: Informs the user that clicking this button will display where the associated variable is used.

variables-dependencies-button.show-dependencies_description: Button label in the Variables section: Clicking this button will display the dependencies associated with variables.
variables-dependencies-button.title-dependencies_description: Label for a button in the 'Variables' management section: Clicking this button will display or allow management of variable dependencies within BMC Helix Dashboards.

variables-unknown-button.usage-title_description: Title in the usage details view for dashboard variables: It serves as a header, indicating that the displayed content lists all occurrences or references of the specified dashboard variable (`{{variableId}}`). This view is typically accessed after interacting with a button related to variables.
variables-unknown-button.VariablesUnknownButton-tooltip-show-usag: Tooltip for a button related to unknown variables in the Variables section: This tooltip indicates that clicking the button will display the locations or contexts where these unknown variables are used.

variables-unknown-table.collapse-label_description: Collapse label in the 'variables-unknown-table' section: This label titles a collapsible section that highlights variables which have been renamed or are no longer available. It helps users identify and address broken variable references within the system.
variables-unknown-table.collapse-tooltip: Click to expand a list with all variable references that have been renamed or are missing from the dashboard.

data-hover-view.link_description: Clickable text in the data hover view of a visualization: This allows users to navigate to additional details or a related view from the hovered data point.

welcome-banner.need-help_description: Link in the welcome banner: Offers users, particularly new ones, a way to access help or support resources.
welcome-banner.welcome-to-grafana_description: Heading in the welcome banner: Greets the user to Grafana when they first access the application.

xychart.category-xychart_description: Chart type label in a dashboard's chart selection: Identifies the XY Chart as an available visualization option.
xychart.name-fill-opacity_description: Property in XY Chart configuration: Adjusts the transparency of the filled areas for chart elements, such as the area under a line or the bars in a bar chart.
xychart.name-line-style_description: Option in the XY chart customization panel: Used to configure the visual style (e.g., solid, dashed, dotted) of lines displayed on an XY chart.
xychart.name-line-width_description: Setting in XY chart properties: Configures the thickness of lines displayed in the chart.
xychart.name-max-point-size_description: Setting in XY Chart configuration: Defines the maximum visual size for data points displayed on an XY chart.
xychart.name-min-point-size_description: Label in XY chart settings: Defines the minimum visual size for data points displayed on the chart.
xychart.name-point-shape_description: An option in the XY Chart configuration settings: Allows users to select the visual shape for individual data points displayed on the chart.
xychart.name-point-size_description: Setting label in XY chart visualization settings: This label allows users to configure the visual size of data points displayed on an XY chart.
xychart.name-point-stroke-width_description: A control for point stroke width in an XY chart: This setting determines the thickness of the outline (stroke) of data points displayed on the chart.
xychart.name-series-mapping_description: Setting label in the XY Chart configuration: Allows users to define how data series are mapped or assigned to the chart's visual representation.
xychart.name-show_description: Checkbox label in XY chart settings: Controls the display of the chart's name or a related textual identifier.

point-shape-options.label-circle_description: Option label in the point shape options for an XY chart: This identifies 'Circle' as a choice for the visual representation of data points.
point-shape-options.label-square_description: Option Label in XY Chart Point Shape Options: This label represents "Square" as one of the selectable shapes for data points displayed in an XY chart.

series-editor.add-series_description: Button in the series editor for an XY chart: Allows users to add a new data series to the chart visualization.
series-editor.aria-label-select-series_description: Accessible label for a series option in the XY Chart's Series Editor: Identifies a specific data series for selection within the editor. This label is primarily for accessibility, providing context for screen readers.
series-editor.label-color-field_description: A selection field in the series editor of an XY chart: Used to specify a data field whose values will determine the color assignments within the series.
series-editor.label-frame_description: Setting in the XY Chart Series Editor: This setting controls the appearance of a visual frame or border around the labels of a chart series.
series-editor.label-size-field_description: Label for a field selector in the XY chart series editor: This allows users to choose a data field whose values will determine the visual size (e.g., bubble size, marker size) of elements within the chart series.
series-editor.label-x-field_description: Label in the XY Chart Series Editor: This label identifies the input field where users select the data source or column to be plotted on the chart's X-axis for a specific series.
series-editor.label-y-field_description: Label in the Series editor of an XY chart configuration: It identifies the data field to be used for the Y-axis of the chart series.
series-editor.placeholder-all-frames_description: Placeholder text in the series editor of an XY chart: This text appears in a selection field, indicating that a setting or action applies to, or includes, all available data frames by default. 'Frames' refers to individual data series or data sets within the chart.
series-editor.placeholder-select-frame_description: **Placeholder text or label** in the **XY chart series editor**: Prompts the user to select a specific data frame or visual frame for configuring the chart series.
series-editor.placeholder-x-field: First number or time field in each frame
series-editor.placeholder-x-field_description: Placeholder text in the X-axis field selection within the Series Editor of an XY Chart: It describes the default field that will be used for the X-axis, which is the first number or time field found in each data frame.
series-editor.placeholder-y-field: Remaining number fields in each frame
series-editor.placeholder-y-field_description: Placeholder text in the Y-axis field selection within the XY Chart Series Editor: It prompts the user to select available numeric data fields for the Y-axis, specifically those remaining within each data frame. It serves as a dynamic hint for what can be chosen.
series-editor.tooltip-delete-series_description: Tooltip in the XY Chart Series Editor: Appears when hovering over an option or button to remove a data series from the chart. It describes the action of deleting a series.

series-mapping-options.label-auto_description: The "Auto" option in the series mapping options of an XY chart: This setting enables the system to automatically generate or infer the label for a data series.
series-mapping-options.label-manual_description: Label for an option in the Series Mapping Options section of an XY Chart: This option allows users to manually configure data series labels.

show-options.label-both_description: Option in the XY chart's 'Show Options' settings: This selection allows the user to display labels for 'both' relevant chart components, such as both axes or both data series.
show-options.label-lines_description: Label for a UI element in the 'Show Options' section of an XY chart: This element controls the visibility or display of lines on the chart.
show-options.label-points_description: Checkbox label in the 'Show options' section of an XY chart: Controls the visibility of individual data points on the chart.
